raw_text: ABBOTT, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1893; ed. Blundell's; entd. Crown Agents' office after compt. exam., Oct., 1912; astt. head, finance dept., 1929; dep. head, 1933; astt. sec., E. African currency bd., 1932; sec., 1930.
ABBOTT, CHARLES, Barrister-at-law (Gray's Inn).—Res. Mag., Kenya, Feb., 1930; crown coun., Oct., 1930; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Sept., 1932.

ABRAHAM, HENRY CHARLES, F.L.S.—B. 1887; ed. Taunton's Schl., Southampton and Southampton Univ. Coll.; survr. prob., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; survr., grade II, topo. br., Apr., 1912; 2nd heut., R.G.A. (S.R.), Sept., 1915; heut., July, 1917; astt. supt., topo. br., Jan., 1919; rel. comm., Mar., 1920; ag. supt., topo. br., June, 1927; ag. supt., surveys, Penang Feb., 1928; Empire conf., survey offrs., Southampton and London, July, 1928; sen. astt. suptd., topo. br., Jan., 1929; Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. Medal, Nov., 1929; senr. astt. supt., topo. br., May to Dec., 1930; do., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., Feb., 1932; supt., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1933.

ABRAHAM, JOHN CONRAD, M.A. (Oxon.), M.B.E. (1918).—B.1889; ed. Keswick and Wadham Coll., Oxford; astt. res., Nyasaland, 1911; O.C. Details, Fort Johnston; attd. 1st K.A.R., 1914; sp. serv., carrier transport, N.F.F., 1915-18; men in desps., three times; admin. offrs., lat gde., 1923; ag. astt. ch. sec., Nov., 1926; N. Nyassa native reserves comm., 1928; sp. duty, Tanganyika, Nov., 1929; astt. sec., native affrs., Nyasaland, Jan., 1931; astt. ch. sec., July, 1931; chmn. native languages bd., 1931; supt., census, 1931; ag. ch. sec., May-Nov., 1932; gov.'s dep., July, 1932; senr. prov. commr., Nov., 1933.

ABRAHAMS, SIDNEY SOLOMON, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Modern Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; Midland circuit; town mag., Zanzibar, June, 1915; crown counsel, Kenya, Nov., 1915, but did not proceed; seconded for serv. with Mesopotamia civil admntr., Jan., 1920; advoc. gen. and regist. of companies, Baghdad, Apr., 1920; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, Jan., 1921; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, June, 1922; atty.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1923; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1928; ch. just., Uganda, Feb., 1933; do., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1934; joint edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1868-1918; sole edr., Zanzibar law repts., 1918-22; 2nd ola. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1924.

ABRAMSON, MAJOR ALBERT, C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.E.F.; O.B.E., 1918; twice ment. in desps.; mily. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comm., and dir., land dept., 1920; ch. Br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov., southern dist., 1922; dist. commr., northern dist., 1925; commr., lands, 1927.

ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1895; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in desps., Croix-de-Guerre; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte. on the development of scientific training and research 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte. on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-secr., 3rd Oct., 1923; ag. prin., Jan., 1926.

ADAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, D.Sc. (N.Z.), A.I.A. (Lond.), F.R.A.S.—B. 1870; ed. Christchurch Boys' High Sch., Wellington Coll., Otago Boys' High Sch., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; ent. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1900; ch. trigonometrical computer and dir., tidal survey some years; govt. astronomer and seismologist since 1911; Martin Kellogg fellow in astronomy, Univ. of California, 1915, and assoc. in astronomy, Yale Univ., 1916.
ADAMS, C. H.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. comsrr., Mar., 1917.
ADAMS, CYRIL OCTAVIUS.—B. 1907; ed. Sherborne; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., 1930; 3rd cls. inspr., 1932.

ADAMS, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1924), F.C.I.S. (Eng.) F.A.A. (Australia).—B. 1884; ed. Royal Orphanage, Wolverhampton; company sec., Sydney, N.S.W., 1908-1914; office of agt. gen. for N.S.W., London, 1915; liquidator of enemy firms, E.A. Prot., 1915; custodian of enemy property, occupied territory of German E. Africa, 1916 and Tanganyika territory, 1919; comprtr. of cust., and custodian of enemy property, Tanganyika, Jan., 1923; chmn., Tanganyika trade and infrn. local advisory committee.
ADAMS, F.V., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1904; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928.

ADAMS, G. H., A.S.A.—B. 1891; C.A. office, Nov., 1911; temp. acctnt., Nyasaland, July, 1915; cashier, 1915; asst. comsrr., income tax, 1921; senr. asst. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas., in 1921, 1925 and 1927; dep. treas., Oct., 1929; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1930; dep. treas., Palestine, 1933.

ADAMS, JAMES WILMOT, F.R.C.S. (Eng.), B.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab.).—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; med. offr., S.S., Feb., 1913; surg., Tan Tock Seng's hosp. and gen. hosp., 1915 to June, 1917; surg., Penang, Aug., 1926; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, June, 1929 to Nov., 1929.

ADAMS, THEODORE SAMUEL.—B. 1885; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; asst. dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., K Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1914; supr., lands, Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st asst. sec., 1919; ag. 1st asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1920; ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Larut and registr. titles, Perak, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. II., chmn., savn. bd., K. Lumpur, June, 1928; mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; ag. under-secr. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; Br. rep., Selangor, Dec., 1933.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots' Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Lt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; exam. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; ref. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Confes. of Great London, 1913-1914; prep'd. off. reps. on housing and town planning investigtns. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first big planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; org. cheap cottage exhibts., Letchworth, 1913; asst. Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Govt. since 1914; mem. Instn. of Mun. and Cnty. Engrs., Eng.; mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvement; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914; and mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of exec. American City Planning Inst., 1913-1918; chair., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMS, MAJOR WILFRID CARNE, M.C.—B. 1894; supr., constab., N. Borneo, Dec., 1914; adjt., Apr., 1922; ag. offr. commg., constab. and offr. mem., leg. coun., May, 1927; commit. and offr. mem., leg. coun., Apr., 1931.

ADAMS, WILFRID GEORGE, C.M.G. (1926), B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Shrewsbury and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsrr., Uganda, Dec., 1914; dist. comsrr., Apr., 1919; dep. prov. comsrr., Oct., 1928; prov. comsrr., 2nd grade, Sept., 1929; 1st grade, Oct., 1930.

ADAMSON, ST JOHN ERNEST, KT. BARC (1924), C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1867; ed. in London; Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Norza Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educ., Transvaal, 1905; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educ., Transvaal Prov.; vice-chan., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925-31; vice-pres., educati. sec., Br. Assn. (centenary meeting).

ADDERLEY, CHARLES J. H., B.A., R.A.I., A.Inst.C.E., A.M.Inst.C.E.I.—B. 1884; Chinese govt. rly., 1898-16; war serv., R.G.A., France, 1916-19; road surv., Nigeria, 1920; exec. engnr., 1923; senr. exec. engnr., 1926; ag. ass't dir., in 1927-30; ag. D.P.W., in 1927-34; ag. D.P.W. in 1927 and 1929; asst. D.P.W., 1934.

ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed. Rutgers and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; B.A. thoms., 1923; asst. dist. comsrr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. ass't sec., clk. of couns., edir., offl. gazette, Mar., 1929 to June, 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. regist., Pemba in 1931 and 1932; dist. offr., Pemba, Apr., 1934.

ADDISON, D'ARCY WESTWORTH, C.M.G. (1928), M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian prov. serv., 1st Dec., 1887; clk. of exec. coun., 1902; sec. to premier since 1902; ch. electoral offr., 1914; mag., 1918; admstr. of charitable funds, 1914-19; sec. for neglected children, 1914-19; state organiser, visit of H.R.H. Prince of Wales, 1920 and the Duke and Duchess of York, 1927; under sec. for Tasmania, 1928; ag. gen. in England for Tasmania, 1930.
ADJUO, J. A., B.A.—B. 1903; 4th cls. teacher, Gold Coast, 1922; 2nd divn. teacher, 1923; cmtd. inspr., schls., 1927; inspr., schls., 1928.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARCHBISHOP NUTTER, THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1892; rector of Guisborough, Yorks, 1901-1906; bish. of Adelaide since 1906.

AGAR, ARTHUR KIRWAN.—B. 1877; ed. at Brighton Coll., called to the bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1915; ag.-capt. R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb.-Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, regiar. of sup. ct., escheator gen., provost marshal, regiar. gen., regiar. of titles and legal adviser to govt. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. comsnr., Belize, Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; ag.-atty-gen., Dec., 1923 to Apr., 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Oct., 1925.

AHEARNE, CHRISTOPHER DOMINIC.—B. 1886; ed. Christian Schls., Cork, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, scholar 1907; senr. moderator, B.A., 1909; cadet, S.S., 1910; India, studying Tamil, 1911; asst. supt., Indian immigrants, Selangor, 1912; 1st asst. contrlr., lab., Penang, 1913; dep. contrlr., lab., Klang, 1914; dep. regiar. sup. ct., Penang, 1915; dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1916; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, 1917; asst. regiar., impts. and expts. (war dept.), 1918; offr., cls. iv., Jan., 1919; asst. advr. to govt. of Johore, Batu Pahat, 1919-21; 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1923; dep. contr. lab., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1923; dep. treas., S.S. (cls. Ia), 1928; contr., lab., Malaya, 1930; leader S.S. deleg., Simla shipping confce., July, 1931; chmn., S.S. retrenchment comtce., Feb., 1932; leader Malaysian deleg., to govt. of India, New Delhi, as to Indian immigr. to Br. Malaya, Oct., 1933.

AINSLIE, JAMES ROBERT, B.Sc. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry (Oxford), F.L.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. at Graeme Walker's (Aberdeen); Aberdeen Univ. and Oxford; probationary asst. cons., forests, Imp. Indian Forest Serv. (Oxford), Aug., 1907; asst. cons., forests, Ceylon, Nov., 1910; on active serv., 1914-17 (Egypt and France); attd. to W.O., timber supplies depot. (XI Forest Divn. staff), Aug., 1917; dep. cons., forests, Ceylon, May, 1919; senr. cons., forests, Nigeria, Feb., 1922; dep. dir., forests, Nov., 1928; dir., forests, May, 1929; author of "The Physiography of Southern Nigeria and its effect on the Forest Flora of the Country" and "Timber Resources of Nigeria."

AITKEN, ERIC COLQUHOUN.—B. 1884; secr. gen. regis. office, Jamaica, 1902; asst. treas., 1907; ditto, secretariat, 1911; 2nd cls. clk., 1913; 1st ditto, 1919; conf. clk., 1920; prin. clk., 1921; av. col. sec. on several occasions; audr.-gen., 1932.

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., L.L.B. (1906).—B.1885; ed. Giggleswick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; asst. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1915; crown counsel, Gold Coast, 3rd Nov., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921, July to Sept. and Oct. to Nov., 1921 and June to July, 1922; ag. puiane judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1925; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1929; puiane judge, Gold Coast, 1933.

AITKEN, JOHN BANKS.—B. 1903; ed. Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1925; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1926; asst. conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1928; utilization offr., May, 1934.
AITKEN, W. J., M.B., Ch.B. (Glass.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926.

AKBAR, MAAN THAJOON.—K.C., B.A., L.L.B. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1880; ag. crown coun., Ceylon, Aug., 1907; crown coun., June, 1909; ag. 2nd addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, and as solr.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Oct., 1924; ag. puiane just., Nov. to Dec., 1925; ag.-atty-gen., Dec., 1927; puiane judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1929.

ALABASTER, CHALONER GRENVILLE, O.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1922).—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1904; ag. atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1911, 1912, 1928, 1930; confirmed in appt., 1931; unoffl. mem., leg. coun., 1919, 1924, 1925; edr. of "Laws of Hong Kong 1841-1912."
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island comsnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 4th grade comsnr., 1912; 3rd grade, Jan., 1918; 2nd grade, 1926; 1st grade, Mar., 1931.

ALBURY, WILTON GRANT, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-apptd., 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915; ag. S. and C. mag., June to July and from July, 1925.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM D. E.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl. and Birmingham Univ.; B.A., 1913 (1st clas., two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, and Western Pacific civ. serv., 1915-20; asst. polit. offr., 2nd grade, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 1927; seconded as lab. offr., 1929; dist. offr., July, 1931.
ALEXANDER, JOHN EDWARD — Asst. land ranger, E.A.P., Oct. 1912; lands offr., Nyassaland, July, 1922.

ALLAN, ALBERT, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1893; war serv., 1914-19; station supt. and traffic inspr., F.M.S. rly., Feb., 1920; asst. sec., G.M.R. office in 1921, 1923, 1925 and 1929; asst. sec., lab. in 1924, 1927 and 1930; divnl. supt. (W), K. Lumpur, Aug., 1932.

ALLAN, PATRICK.—B. 1886; ed. George Heriot's Schl., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Hons.), 1910; D.P.H., 1912; M.D., 1920; R.A.M.C., France, 1917-18; capt., R.A.M.C.; med. inspr., Union of S. Africa health dept., 1920; major, S.A.M.C., 1921; med. supt., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1923; seconded to tuberculosis research comtce., 1927-30; reassumed M.S., Nelspoort sanatorium, 1930.

ALLARD, JULES.—B. 1859; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; apptd. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1905; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916; prothonotory of the superior ct., Montreal, 1919.

ALLDER, WM. HOWELL.—B.1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit offr., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. coun. and comtce., as acctnt. in P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine comsnr., 1903, to West Indian quarantine confce., 1904, and to confce. on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910; sec., Barbados civ. serv. comsnr., 1920.

ALLEN, ARTHUR AUGUSTUS.—B.1890; ed. Christ Hospital; examr. Exchequer and Audit dept., Nov., 1911; asst. audr., Nigeria, July, 1914; seconded, rly. constrn., Oct., 1922-June, 1929; senr. asst. audr., Nigeria, Nov., 1930.

ALLEN, BERTRAM WEDGWOOD.—B. 1888; ed. Repton and King's Coll., Lond.; pol. probationer, S.S., Nov., 1909; asst. supt. pol., Aug., 1914; dep. comsnr., pol., Muar, Johore, June, 1918; asst. supt., pol., Penang, June, 1920; asst. comsnr., pol., Kedah, Jan., 1923; supt., pol., S.S., July, 1928; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Oct., 1929; comdt., pol., depot, Singapore, Dec., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Penang, 1932.
ALLEN, CONWAY PHILLIPS, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Liverpool)—B.—1901; anaesthetist, F.M.S., July, 1927; med. offr. in ch., offrs., in addn., Aug., 1932.
ALLEN, E. M.—Ed. St. Etheldreda Coll., Norwich; temp. mistress, kindergarten schl., Tamale, Gold Coast, 1927; mistress, 1928; inspr., schl., 1933.

ALLEN, EDWARD CUNNINGTON.—B.—1902; clk., secretariat and reporter, legis. coun., Nyasaland, 1925; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1930; ag. finan. asst., provi. admstn., Feb. to June, 1933 and July, 1934.

ALLEN, FREDERICK HAROLD, M.I.Mech.E., A.M.Inst.C.E.—B.—1890; engnrng. asst., Port of London Authority, 1910-19; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1919; dist. engnr., Ilorin, Nigeria, 1923; exec. engr., 1924; native admstn. engr., 1925; mech. engr., Br. Guiana, 1926; exec. engr., P.W.D., Jan., 1930; ag. D.P.W. and M.L.C., May to Nov., 1934.

ALLEN, GEORGE VANCE, M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Hons), D.T.M. and H.—B.—1894; asst. bacteriologist, Kenya, Feb., 1921; senr. bacteriologist, Sept., 1925; bacteriologist, F.M.S., Nov., 1927; ag. prin., med. coll., Singapore, June, 1929; prin., Mar., 1930; pres., Raffles Coll. in addn., Apr., 1932.

ALLEN, H.—Cashier and acct., E.A.P., July, 1914; on active serv., E. Africa, 1914-16 and 1917-18; tempy. asst. dist. commt., E.A.P., Apr., 1919; asst. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920; asst. treas., Zanzibar, Dec., 1930; ag. treas., in 1932, 1933 and 1934; dep. treas., Uganda, 1935.

ALLEN, H. T.—B.—1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898; conf. clk., Dec., 1907; also clk. to concessions and finance comtees., Sept., 1910 to June, 1916; promoted, under order-in-council of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th June, 1916; asst. sec. to trade relations comte., Aug., 1917; sec., Dependencies of Falkland Islands comte., Jan., 1918; sec., raw materials board, Dec., 1918; ass't priv. sec. to sec. of state (Lord Milner), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 15th Aug., 1931.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B.—1855; entd. New Zealand Parlmt., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Div., New Zealand Garrison Art., 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educn., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919-20; high commr. in England, 1920-26; M.L.C., 1927.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B.—1901; entered C.O., 1917; apptd., after exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.

ALLEN, LUCIEN ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1931)—B.—1888; ed., Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; I.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Kualah, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. res., Brunei, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jan., 1924; dep. pub. pros., N. Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1929; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1931; offr., cls. I.B., 1932; do., Kinta, Mar., 1933; commr., lands and mines, Johore, Mar., 1934.
ALLEN, PRACY DE VERRE.—B.—1891; ed Melbourne, Australia; on mily. serv., E. Africa, Egypt and France, 1914-19; lab. inspr., Ken., Apr., 1925; lab. offr., Jan., 1929.

ALLEN, COL. SIR STEPHEN SHEPHERD, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), with (1918)—Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1904 and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with 6th bft. forements N.Z. Exped. Force, with rank major, 1915; lieut.-col., Jan., 1917; in comm. 2nd batt., Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to Jan., 1919; comdr., Auckland Infy. Brig. (T.F.), Feb., 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1920; in comm. Hauraki Regt., Sept., 1926 to Apr., 1928; R.O., Apr., 1928; admstr., W. Samoa, 1928; chinm., transport co-ordination bd., 1934; times ment. in densp.; hon. A.D.C. to gov.-gen. N.Z., Jan., 1925 to May, 1928.
ALLEN, W.R., B.A.—B.—1895; inspr., estbl., Gold Coast, Jan., 1929.

ALLEYNE, HERBERT MASSY.—B.—1899; ed., Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; com. Connaught Rangers, Dec., 1918; transfd., K. R. Own Royal Regt., 1922; ret., 1929; admstr., offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; dist. dist. offr., Aug., 1931.

ALLEYNE, JAMES DOUGLAS, M.B.E. (1917), M.D. (Tuft's Univ., U.S.A.)—Ag. pol. med. offr., A., Barbados and visiting physician, leprosy asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1919; supt., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B.—1891; apptd. asst. clk., Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv., to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.
ALLISON, J. S.—B.—1879; ed. Glenalmond and Edinburgh Univ.; M.A. (Edin.)—L.L.B. (Cape); Cape law dep., 1903; Union dep. of nativ. affrs., 1918; under sec. for nativ. affrs., 1929.

ALLISON, WILLIAM FRANCIS DUDLEY, A.M., Mech.E., M.I.Loco. E.M.Inst.T.—B.—1882; ed., Glasgow High Schl. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; N.B.R. Co. and L. and S.W.R., 1906; mech. engr., crown agts., 1908; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1911; seconded as loco. supt., mly. rlys., Togoland, 1916; asst. loco. supt., Uganda rly., 1918; dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1922; ch. mech. engr., July, 1930.

ALLNUTT, RICHARD BUSWELL, B.Sc. (Agric.)—B.—1907; ed Malvern Coll. and S. Eastern Agric. Coll., Wye (B.Sc. (agric.) (Lond.) and dipl., agr., 1928; crop. observn. offr., Norfolk agric. sta., 1928-30; dist. agric. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.
ALLSOP, HERBERT HENRY.—B.—1885; Germs East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade adminstr. offr., Aug., 1922.

ALUWIHARE, RICHARD.—B.—1895; on mily. serv., Oct., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; acc. to Jaffna kach., Oct., 1920; office asst. to dir. of agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamewa Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924; 2nd landing surr., cust., 1926; consmr. requests, Kandy, Nov., 1928; asst. sttlmt. offr., Oct., 1929; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1931; contr., finance and suppr., Mar., 1934.

ALVAPILLAI, KOVINAPILLAI.—B.—1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; apptd., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1929; attd., Matara kach., Sept., 1929; attd. office asst., Puttalam kach., Oct., 1930; office asst., Galle kach., Mar., 1931.

AMBLEDER, Capt., Goodman, M.C., V.D., F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. Steyning Grammar Schl., Brighton Grammar Schl. and St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; asst. mast., Victoria Inst., K. Lumpur, F.M.S., Jan., 1910; war serv., France and Gallipoli, 1914-19; rejoined Victoria Inst., 1919; sec., Selangor cadets with rank of capt., Mar., 1924; Col. Aux. Forces Serv. Med., Mar., 1924; headmast., Outram schl., S'pore, Apr., 1926; lieut., S.V.C., May, 1926; capt., Aug., 1930; transf'd to S.S.V.F., R. of O.; senr. normal instr., S'pore, Dec., 1931.

AMBLEDER, Lloyd Edward.—B. 1894; clk., postal dept., Fiji, June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., lands dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., K.R.R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. (commd., Oct., 1917); Baluchistan, 1917-19; 3rd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; attd., lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd cls. clk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., 1920; ag. sub-inpr. constab. (during Indian strike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; regy. clk., Western Pacific high comm., May, 1921; ag. ch. clk., Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1927; ag. ch. clk. and acct., on various occasions, 1923-31; ag. sec., Western Pacific high comm., June to Aug., 1931; ch. clk. and acct., Jan., 1932.

AMERASINGHE, Samson Felix, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1926; attd., Kalutara cach., Aug., 1926; office astt. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 1928; office astt. to govt. agt., Uva, June, 1930; office astt. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., June, 1932; pol. mag., Kandy, Nov., 1932.

AMERY, Lieut.-Col., L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late Fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar of Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1899-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Imp. War Coun. at Versailles, and as astt. sec. to the War Cabinet and Imp. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Mar., 1920; First Lord of the Admir., Oct., 1922; S. of S. for the Cols., 7th Nov., 1924; also S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925 to June, 1929; capt. 14th B'th' Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1917; M.P. (U) for Birmingham, May, 1911; ditto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Times History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, M.P.), 1901; "Union and Strength," 1912.

AMERY, William Bankes, C.B.E. (1920).—B.1883; ed. Christ's Hosp.; ent. cust. and excise dept., 1901; astt. sec. (temp.) war trade dept., 1916; estab. offr., ministry of transport, 1919; finance offr. (with rank of prim.) overseas settlement dept., D.O., 1922; rep. of the U.K. govt. in the Commonwealth of Australia under the Empire Settlement Act, 1925; prin., O.S.D., 1929; temp. astt., D.O., 1932.

AMES, Cecil Geraint.—B. 1897; ed. Elstree Prep. Schl. and Dover Coll.; (schol.); 5th Somerset Light Infv., 1915-19; Law Soc.'s final exam. (hons.), 1921; Nigerian admnstr. serv., 1922; ag. ch. registr., sup. ct., Nov. 1932-July, 1933; admitted, solr., Nov., 1933; attd. judl. dept., Jan.—Mar., 1934; astt. judge, high ct., Apr., 1934.

ANDERSON, Adam, B.E., B.A. (R.U.I.)—B. 1898; ed. Foyle Coll., Londonderry and Queen's Coll., Belfast; astt. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; astt. engrnr., 1919; dep. port engrnr., port development dept., 1924; exec. engrnr., 1926.

ANDERSON, Admiral Sir David Murray, K.C.B. (1930), C.M.G. (1918), M.V.O. (1910).—B. 1874; ed. Stubbington and H.M.S. "Britannia"; served with Brass river expndn. and M'Wele expdn., 1906; Ashanti, 1906; Great War, 1914-19, including opera. in Rufiji river, 1915, and with Grand Fleet, 1918; capt. supt., Pembroke dockyard, and senr. naval offr., Milford Haven, 1920-22; rear-adm'l., 1922; naval A.D.C. to the King, 1921-22; senr. naval offr., Yangtse river, 1923-25; C. in C., China station, Apr.-June, 1925; Africa station, 1927-29; vice-adm'l., 1927; high comm., S. Africa, June-Dec., 1928; adm'l., 1931; adm'y. rep., L. of N. perm't. advisory comm., 1929-31; gov. and comdr., in-ch., Newfoundland, 1932.
ANDERSON, E. L. B.—Astt. dist. comsmr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1925.

ANDERSON, James Henry (Lieut. R.A.R.O. Seaforth Hrs.)—B. 1895; 2nd cls. clk., treas., Nyassaland, 1920; trans'd. to secretariat, 1922; senr. clk., 1924; ag. ch. clk., secretariat and edr., Govt. Gazette, 1927; astt. treas., Gold Coast, Aug., 1928.
ANDERSON, Thomas John, M.A., B.Sc., (Hons. Edin.) F.Z.S., F.S.E.—Entomologist, agric. dept., Kenya, May, 1908.

ANDERSON, William John.—B. 1895; ed. Kilmarnock Acad. and Tech. Coll.; active serv. (France) 1914-19; dep. storekeeper, Hongkong, Feb., 1925; ag. supt., accts. and stores, P.W.D., 1926; supt., accts. and stores, Jan., 1928.
ANDRE, Wilfrid Joseph.—B. 1890; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; mag., St. Andrew, &c., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; mag., Caroni, Dec., 1924.
ANDREWES, Lancelot Ruggles.—Ed. Fort Street, Sydney; war serv., 1915-18; admitted solr., sup. ct., London, 1924; astt. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1928; J.P., 1931; ag. dep. registr., 1932.

ANDREWS, Norman Frederick Stewart.—B. 1902; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Oxford; schol., Brasenose Coll., 1919; Bridgemarke Prize, 1923, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1924, B.A., 1924; M.A., 1928; cadet, adminstr., Uganda, 1925; astt. dist. offr., 1927; temp. serving in C.O., 1927-28; seconded to secretariat, Uganda, 1929; sec., cotton enquiry comm., 1929.

ANNECKE, D. H. Siegfried.—B. 1895; ed. Grey Univ. Coll. Bloemfontein; B.A. (lit. and phil.), 1914; Univ. C.T., M.A. (physiology), 1918; Guy's Hosp., Lond., 1919-23, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1921; all house appts., Guy's, 1921; M.B., B.S., Univ. Durham, 1924; D.P.H. and B. Hy., Univ. Durham, 1925; research schl. and astt. dept. protozoology, Lond. Sch. Hwy. Trop. Med., 1925-28; D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), 1927; joint educ. grant, govt. S.A. and Health Section L. of N. for study of anti-malarial methods in Italy, 1927; senr. govt. pathologist, Union health dept., Durban, 1928; med. inspr. (malaria), 1931.

ANSDELL, George Arthur Reks Wettenhall.—B. 1894; ed. Lisard High Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; war serv., Egypt, 1916; Turkey in Asia, 1919; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1921; passed lower Swahili, 1922; admin. offr., 2nd gde., 1924; passed law, 1925; ag. dist. offr., 1925; seconded as lab. offr., 1928; A.D.C. and P.S. to gov., Apr. to Nov., 1927 and Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Sch.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Rly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust.; Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine rly., 1925; ag. ch. engr. (design), C.A., Nov., 1925 to Dec., 1926.

ANTROBUS, MAURICE E., B.A.—B. 1895; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhib., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps, Aug., 1914; lieut. June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. trip. pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. trip. pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Aug., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ass't. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; ass't. pvtc. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1925; seconded as pvtc. sec. to gov., Ceylon, July, 1927; prin., D.O., 23rd July, 1930.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1898).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical moda., 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; app'td., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the G.O., May, 1877; ass't. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, Dec., 1880 to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, Dec., 1882 to June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), June, 1885 to Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, Feb. to Aug., 1886; administ'd. the govt. of St. Helena, Nov., 1889 to June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Menie, Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; princ. clk., 1896; ass't. under-secr. of state, Oct., 1898; crown agent for the cols., 1909; ret., 1918.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; ass't. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ass't. col. sec., May to Sept., 1915; travelling commnr., Nsawam, Oct., 1915, to May, 1916, and from Apr., 1917; ag. dist. commnr., Kwahu, 1919; dep. prov. commnr., 1929; prov. commnr., 1932.

APTHORPE, MARMAUCHE GWYN, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Malvern and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; clk., atty-gen.'s office, Cape Town, Sept., 1900; clk., survr.-gen.'s office, Feb., 1905; clk., treas. and pvtc. sec. to prime min. Apr., 1909; ag. prin. clk., P.W.D., May, 1910; prin. clk., native affrs. dept., June, 1910; on active serv. with E. African expedy. forces, 1916-17; ag. clk. ch. and ag. under sec. for native affrs., 1917; mag. Lady Frere, May, 1918; do., Kentani, June, 1923; ch. clk., ch. mag.'s office, Umtata, and sec. and treas., Transkeian Territories and Pondoland gen. couns., Dec., 1925; ch. nativ. commnr., Ciskei, Oct., 1928.

ARCHER, SIR GEOFFREY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1920), C.M.G. (1913).—Asst. collr., East Africa Prot., Dec., 1902; attached secretariat, June, 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. commnr., Apr., 1907; spec. serv., Northern Frontier, East Africa, 1909; offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Cuthbert Peak Grant by the R.G.S., 1918; for survey of E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinian triangulation with the triangulation of E. Afr. transit. to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admin. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June to Oct., 1913; dep. commnr., Mar., 1913; commr. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., May, 1914; assumed govt., May, 1914; present at operations against the Dervishes at Shimbiris, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Med. and clasp); member of British Mission on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st clas. gov., Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1919; directed operations against the Mullah, 1920, resulting in complete destruction of Dervish power in Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., Sept., 1922; gov., Sudan, 1924-26.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1892; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman in Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1909-12; sub-lieut. (six 1st cls. certif.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, special course, as torpedo lieut., 1916; served with Russian Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1915-17 (Order of St. Stanislas, 3rd cls.); passed exam. as Russian interpreter, 1918; intell. divn. adm'y., H.M.S. Louvencourt in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; app'td. under reconstruction scheme ass't. prin., mar. of lab., Sept., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., Dec., 1921; pvtc. sec. to perm't. under sec. for Dom. affrs., Sept., 1927; seconded as sec. to Br. econol. mission to Australia and visited Australia, N.Z. and Canada, 1928-29; pvtc. sec. to partly. under sec. for Dom. affrs., June, 1929; prin., D.O., 4th Sept., 1929; seconded as sec., office of chief commnr. in Canada for H.M.G. in U.K., 1st Apr., 1932.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1885; recd. prnv. educn. at Yarmouth pub. sch. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1907; also grad. from the Nova Scotia hort. schl. in the same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and experimt. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Experimt. Farm, Ottawa; dir. of Experimt. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1919.
ARDRON, HAROLD GRAHAM.—B. 1866; family serv., 1916-21; educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1922; transf'd. polit. serv. as ass't. dist. commnr., 1923; asst. col. sec., 1930.

ARGYLE, SIR STANLEY SKYMEUR, K.B.E. (1930), M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1867; ed. Trinity Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut.-col., A.A.M.C., A.I.F. in European war; M.L.A., Victoria since 1929; ch. m. in four ministries, 1923-29; premier and treas., Victoria, May, 1932.
ARMSTRONG, ANDREW CLARENCE FRANCIS—B. 1907; ed. St. Edmunds Schl., Ware and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Gren. and Ellice Is., 1929; dep. commnr., 1931; admin. offr., 1932.

ARMSTRONG, ARTHUR LEOPOLD.—B. 1888; cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1915; asst. sec. for native affrs., Jan., 1920; resigned, Oct., 1920; dist. commr. and prov. commr., Dec., 1921; under sec. for native affrs., Oct., 1922; asst. sec., do., Jan.,

ASHMORF, JACK HALIBURTON.—B. 1900; 2nd lieut., The King's Own Royal Lancaster Regt., Apr., 1919; col. pol. offrs. course, R.I.C., May, 1920; sub-inspr., pol., British Guiana, Oct., 1920; dist. inspr., July, 1923; travelling mag. and warden, Nos. 3 and 4 mining dists., Aug., 1923 to Apr., 1924; passed colloquial Hindi exam., 1926; county inspr., 1927; ag. detective inspr., for various periods in 1926-28 and 1930-32; local commdt., pol. in ch. crim. invesgn. dept., Cyprus, Dec., 1932; ag. ch. commdt. pol. and inspr., prisons in 1933.

ASHTON, DANIEL ASPINALL.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owen Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ.; civ. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expdn., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obubura.
ASHTON, HUGH.—B. 1878; served in S. African War; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1906; inspr., 1914; served in European War with native labour contingent; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1923.

ASHWORTH, HENRY TWEDDALE, M.B.E. (1934), M.M.—B. 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., 1902; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt (Canal Defences) and France; passed supplementary clk. exam., Jan., 1921; services loaned to C.O., 1st May, 1922; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to the C.O., 1st Sept., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1927.
ASKEW, G. G.—B. 1895; ed. St. Bees Schl., Cumberland; on mily. serv., 1914-19; "1914" Star, ment. in despr.; dep. colliery man., Nigeria, 1924.

ASPINALL, SIR ALGERNON EDWARD., K.T. BACH. (1928), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comtee. since 1898 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; pres. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comtee. on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian shipping comtee., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agr. Coll. comtee., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comtee., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comtee., 1923.

ASSER, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH JOHN, K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), C.B. (1915), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1867; served with Nile expdn., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expdn., Sudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1907-16; served in European War, 1914-19; base commdt., 1914-16; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdg. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed govt., 9th Aug., 1922; ret., 1927.

ASTON, ARTHUR VINCENT.—B. 1896; ed. King's Schl., Chester, and Queen's Schl., Oxford (open maths. schol.); R.F.A., 1915-18 (M.C.), 1917; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; collr., land rev., July, 1922; sec. to Br. advr., Keiulah, Feb., 1930; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Nov., 1930; attd., land office, Penang, Nov., 1931; collr., land rev., Penang, July, 1932.

ATHILL, SAMUEL LAUCHLAND, JUNR.—B.1883; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry comsnr., Montserrat, 1911; asst. regis., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dista. "A" and "B," Antigua, 6th Oct., 1917; mag. dist. B., Antigua, 1st July, 1924; city comsnr., St. Johns, Antigua, Nov., 1925.

ATHLONE, FIRST EARL OF (created 1917), ALEXANDER CAMBRIDGE, K.G.(1928), G.C.B.(1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commodr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923-30; chan. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George (1934); Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.

ATKINSON, VIVIAN BUCHANAN—B. 1886; N. Staffs. Rly. Co., 1907; A.M.I.C.E., 1916; ast. engrnr., Uganda Rly., Jan., 1912; dist. engrnr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1920; ast. ch. engrnr., Kenya and Uganda rlys. and harb., 1930.
ATTERBY, H. W.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.O., 14th Oct., 1921.
ATTERBY, WILLIAM ALBERT.—Brit. post office, 1910-19; war serv., 1916-19; sub-tel. engrnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; tel. engrnr., 1926.

AUBER, EGERTON LANCELOT.—B. 1889; ed. Wesleyan High Sch., Freetown, Sierra Leone; supernum. clk., cust. (indoor), Gambia, Feb., 1908; 3rd clk., treas., Nov., 1908; 2nd clk., Nov., 1911; regraded 4th grade clk., 1915; 3rd grade clk., July, 1916; 2nd grade clk., Dec., 1918; ch. clk., treas., Oct., 1920; seconded for duty as marina acctnt., Feb., 1927 to July, 1929.

AUCHINLECK, GILBERT GRAHAME.—B.Sc. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls. hons. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-08; science and 2nd mast., St. Kitts-Nevia gram. schl., 1908-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-14; ast. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric., and registr., C.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan.-May, 1917; reported on Is. of Rodrigues to the Mauritius govt., 1919; Mauritius del. to Imp. Entom. Conf., London, 1920; divnl., agric. offr., Ceylon, 1920; seconded to Seychelles to report on the possibilities of agric. development, 1921-22; dep. dir., agric., G. Coast, Aug., 1925; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to rept. on palm oil industry, Apr. 1926 to Jan., 1927; del. to W. African agric. conf., Nigeria, Mar., 1927; author of papers on land attlit. and various trop. agric. subjects; dir., agric., Dec., 1928.

AUCHINLECK, MAURICK M.—B. 1888; treas. and cust., Antigua, June, 1906; col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1912; clk. to commr. and educat. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1915; ag. postmr. and harbmr., Antigua, 1919-20; ch. clk., treas. and cust., Antigua, 1921; ag. treas., Dominica, in 1925; treas. and compr., cust., Antigua, and Fedl. treas., Leeward Is., local chin., income tax commrs., Antigua; mem., exec. and leg. couns., Antigua, and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., 1926-30; served on hurricane relief comtee., 1928; J.P. and visiting just. of prison; mem., city commrs. and road bd., Antigua, 1930; senr. ast. recr. gen., Gambia, 1931; recr. gen., 1933.

AUDEN, FRANCIS THOMAS.—B. 1869; ed. Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1903; M.B., 1891; civil surg., S. African war, 1900-1901 (medal and clasp); dist. commr. and govt. med. offr., Turks and Caicos Islands, Dec., 1914; med. offr., Jamaica, 1917; Kenya, 1919; Bahamas, 1924; Cayman Is., 1925; St. Vincent, 1926; Seychelles, 1927.
AUDETTE, HON. LOUIS ARTHUR, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1856; apptd. regir. of exchequer et al. of Canada, 1887; ast. judge, exchequer et al., 1912; puisne judge, 1919.
AUSTEN, HAROLD CHOLMLEY MANSFIELD, C.B.E. (1935).—B. 1878; tempy. harbr. engnr., Mauritius, 1923; gen. man., rly. and harbr. engnr., May, 1931.
AUSTIN, T. A., L.R.C.P. and S.I.L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. and H. (Liverpool).—B. 1892; ed. Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Dublin; East Afr. med. serv. (Zanzibar), May, 1924.

AVICE, TRISTAN, O.B.E. (1929).—Ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; matric., Lond. Univ., 1898; ast. civ. serv. Mauritius, 1893; 6th cls. clk., 1894; ditto, Apr., 1899; 4th ditto, Nov., 1899; 2nd ditto, 1903; ch. clk., 1905; ast. regir.-gen., 1912; regir.-gen., recr. of registr. dues, comr., mortg. gages, registr. of companies, 1920; nom. min. coun. of govt., May, 1926.

AYLESWORTH, HON. SIR ALLEN BRISTOL, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C., B.A. (1874), N.A. (1875).—B. 1854; educ. at Newburgh h't. school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales prize-man; bencher of law soc. of U.K.; Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); one of H.M.'s commrs. for settlmt. of Alaska bound., 1903; elected to the H. of C. for North York, 1904; postmr.-gen. of Canada, 1905; min. just., 1906; British ag. internat. fisheries arbitr. at the Hague, 1910; resigned portfo., and retired, 1911; mem., privy coun. of Canada; called to the Senate, 1923.

AYRE, CLAUDE FREDERICK CHARLES—B. 1881; ast. mast., Raffles Inst., Singapore, Oct., 1907; lent to F.M.S. govt., inspr., schla., Jan., 1912; grade II, Jan., 1913; sr. mast., Raffles Inst., Oct., 1915; grade I, and prin. High Sch., Malacca; pol. mag., Aug., 1919; census off., Mar., 1921; prin., Anderson Sch., Ipoh, May, 1922; sr. normal instr., Nov., 1924.

AYTON, A. L.—B. 1878; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. Jul., 1899; clk. in charge of accs., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1913; ag. sec., W.P.H.C., 2nd Mar., 1915; returned to C.O. as staff clk. in accs. dept., 29th Jan., 1920; ag. ast. prin., tech Adv. 1920; sec. to "Discovery" comtee., 1925; prin. 1st June, 1929.

BABER, EDWARD ALFRED.—B. 1888; comp. survey dept., B. Honduras, 1905; appntd. ditto, 1906; passed exam. in theory of land surveying; 2nd cls. clk., Aug., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1911; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Nov., 1913; clk., roads and rivers bd., 1914; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of sc. and clk. to bd. of educn., Nov., 1915; senr. ast. col. sec.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. ch. clk., etc., for various periods, 1917-21; ag. ast. col. sec., Nov., 1921; ag. priv. sec. to Sir Eyre Hutson May-Aug., 1922; ch. clk., Apr., 1921; ast. ast. sec., June, 1923.

BACKWELL, HENRY FLEMING, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. scholarships), and at King's Coll., Camb., exhib., ast. mast., Portsmouth gram. sch., 1907-8; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1908; ast. dist. offr., Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls. Aug., 1917; res., July, 1925.

BADDELEY, SIR FRANK MORRISH, K.C.E. (1930), KT. BACH (1928), C.M.G. (1925).—M.A. Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Mat. tripus, 1896; cadet F.M.S., 1897; called to bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; recd. thesis of govt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, Feb., 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1904; ag. offl. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1907; apptd. a currency comsrr., May, 1907; dist. btrf., Kuantan, June, 1908; supt. govt. monopolies dept., S. Sttlmrs., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. Sttlmrs., Feb., 1916; food controller, Oct., 1920; under-sec., S. Sttlmrs., July, 1921; ag. col. sec., S. Sttlmrs., Apr.-Sept., 1922, May-Oct., 1923 and from Sept.-Nov., 1924; ch. sec., Nigeria, Nov., 1924; O.A.G., Nigeria, in 1925, 1927 and 1928-29; govts. dep. for various periods in 1926, 1928 and 1927; ret., 1930.
BAKER, H.E., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law, dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; sec. expenditure advisory comte., 1932-33; ag. clk., counra., 1933; seconded to C.O. as ast. prin., Jan., 1934.

BAEZA, JOSHUA ISADORE, M.B., B.Ch. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Oxon.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1890; jr. res. surgn., Barbados gen. hosp., Oct., 1913; med. offr., W. Africa, May, 1914; war serv., 1914-16; med. offr. and ag. bacteriologist, Trinidad, 1919-22; ag. sr. health offr., Penang, June, 1923; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1924; health offr., S'pore, Mar., 1924; sr. health offr., Kedah, June, 1929; ag. ch. health offr., S'pore, June, 1933.

BAGOT, EDWARD.—B. 1892; pol. probnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1912; ast. comnrr., pol., Oct., 1916; ch. pol. offr., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1918; adjt. p.l. p't., June, 1920; 1st ast. comnrr., Selangor, June, 1922; ast. comnrr., pol., Ipoh, Feb., 1925; offr. i/c detectives, Perak, Mar., 1925; ast. comnrr., pol., fed. hqrs., Dec., 1929; ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Jan., 1930; ch. pol. offr., Perak, Apr., 1930; dep. comnrr., pol., F.M.S., July, 1930; comnrr., pol., Trengganu, Nov., 1930; do., Johore, Jan., 1933.
BAILWARD, A.N.—Ast. dist. comnrr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; junr. sec. native affrs. dept., 1926; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1927.

BAIN, ARTHUR HERBERT.—Ck., Sept., 1895; examr. deeds, April, 1912; 1st gr. examr. deeds, Oct., 1919; ast. regtr., deeds, King Williamstown, Nov., 1919; ast. regtr., deeds, Natal, Jan., 1926; ast. regtr., deeds, Cape Town, Oct., 1928; regtr., deeds, Natal, Jan., 1932.

BAIN, JAMES, M.C., M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1892; ed. Univ., European mast., S.S. Raffles Inst., S'pore, Jan., 1921; headmast., Geelong English Sch., Jan., 1923; ag. inspr., schls., 1923-28; ag. supt., educn., Kedah, Apr., 1930; senr. educn. offr. (B), Jan., 1932; ag. headmast., Penang Free Sch., Oct., 1933.

BAKER, ALAN CUSTANCE, M.C., B.A., Oxon.—B. 1885; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1908; attchd. to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909; officiating ast. supt., monopolies, Penang, June, 1919; officiating ast. offl. assignee and dep. regsr., sup. et., Penang, Aug., 1919; capt., Penang volunteers, Nov., 1919; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1920; collr., land rev. and ast. treas., Malacca, Mar., 1923; comnrr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1927; cls. IB: ag. Br. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1932; Br. advr., Kelantan, 1935.

BAKER, ERNEST LAIDLAW.—B. 1880; ed. Australia; ck., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec. Albert Park Bl. (joint apptnt.), 1908; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. to legis. coun., 1909; clk., exec. coun. (joint apptnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., 1913; dist. comnrr., Colo East, 1914; ditto, Ra., 1917; ditto, Savu Savu, 1918; ag. ast. col. sec., 1920; dist. comnrr., Lautoka, 1920; ditto, Macuata, 1923; ditto, Rewa, 1923.

BAKER, FRANCIS HORACE.—B. 1887; ed. Stratford-on-Avon Schl.; admitted solr., sup. et., England, 1910; practised in England until 1913 and in Hong Kong, Jan., 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; comnrr., R. Artillery, 1915; staff capt., 1919; mily. mag., Nablus dist., Apr., 1919; mily. mag., Jerusalem dist., May, 1919; pres., Jerusalem dist. ot., Aug., 1919; also pres., Beerseba dist. ot.

BAKER, HERBERT ARTHUR, D.Sc., D.I.O., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Univ. Coll. and London Day Training Coll., Univ. of London, 1906-08; B.Sc. hon.; B. of E. teacher's certif., 1908; M.Sc., London, 1918; D.Sc., London, 1920; dipl. of membership, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology (D.I.C.), 1920; R.F.C., France, 1915-16; warrant offr., R.N., 1916-19; govt. geologist, Falkland Is., 1920-22; headmaster, Grenada boys secondary schl., 1924.

BAKER, JOHN ERNEST.—B. 1896; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; junr. draughtsman, off. supdt. civ. eng., H.M. dykd., Roysth, 1914-15; R.F.C., France, Mesopotamia, Persia, 1916-20; studied Seale-Hayne Agr. Coll., 1920-24; agrl. offr., Zanzibar, 1925; del., Imp. Agr. Res. Confe., 1927.
BALDWIN, JOSEPH MASON, M.A., D.Sc.—B. 1878; ag. govt. astronomer, Victoria, 1915; govt. astronomer, July, 1919.
BALDWIN, THOMAS HAYWARD, B.A.—B. 1896; ed., Stamford Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Prov., Nigeria, Oct., 1921; ag. prin., higher coll., Katzina, 1930 and 1932.

BALE, EDGAR GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. King Edward's Schl.; Imp. cust. and excise serv., 1912-21; ast. to comnrr. of cust., Kenya and Uganda, Mar., 1921; dep. comnrr., cust., April, 1923; ag. comnrr., cust., for various periods, 1924-29.
BALFE, R. S.—B. 1892; cler.-ast., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; inspr., 1927; dep. ast. comnrr., 1930.

BALFOUR, DOUGLAS HASTINGS.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1911; attd. to Jaffna Kach., Feb., 1911; ag. pol. mag., P. Pedro, May, 1911; extra office ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1911; attd. to Trincomalee Kach., May, 1912; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; ast. comnrr. excise, June, 1917; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1923; ag. ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Apr., 1926; dist. judge, Negombo, June, 1925; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, July, 1927; ast. judge, Anuradhapura, Oct., 1929; ag. dist. judge, Jaffna, Sept., 1930; dist. judge, Negombo, Oct., 1933.
BALFOUR, STEPHEN FRANCIS, B.A.—B. 1905; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1930; 1st clk., mag., Kowloon, 1932; ast. head, army, dept., Feb., 1933; 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1933.
BALMER, F. E.—Dep. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ch. acctnt., G.P.O., Kenya, Oct., 1919; ch. acctnt., Kenya-Uganda-Tanganvika post. and tel. service, 1933.

RAMFORD, LIEUT.-COLONEL H.W.M., C.B.E., (1933), O.B.E. (1918); M.C. (1916).—B. 1882; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1900-1902; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff, N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjt., Natal Light Horse X S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F. Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. African bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde.-major, 90th infy. bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infy., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in France includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defence of Hazebrouk, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches; O.B.E., M.C., Croix de Guerre avec palme; commr. of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, July, 1919; commdt., constab., N. Territories, G. Coast, July, 1922; inspr.-gen., pol., G. Coast, Aug., 1924.
BANCROFT, EDWARD NATHANIEL, M.C., M.Am.Soc.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; lieut., R.E., 1916; France, 1917-18; survey engr., Nigerian rly., July, 1927; survr., gen., Jamaica, May, 1929.

BANCROFT, OSWALD LAWRENCE.—B. 1888; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1909; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; admitted bar, Barbados, 1910; barrister-at-law and solr., sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1911-24; pol. mag., dist. A, Barbados, 1925; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, 1928; pol. mag. and coroner, Barbados, 1931.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUSE, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and advoc., Jan., 1914; practiced before sup. ct., G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct., last Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-1931; circuit judge, Ashanti and N.T., Gold Coast, Sept., 1934.
BANTING, WILLIAM VILLIERS.—B. 1903; ed. Haileybury Coll.; chartered acctnt. (England and Wales), 1928; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1824), RIGHT REV. DAVID WILLIAMS BENTLEY.—Ed. Durham Univ.; B.A. (1910), M.A. (1913), D.D. (1919); asst. curate, St. John's, Barrow-in-Furness, 1906-10; do., St. James-the-Less, Bethnal Green, 1910-14; vicar, St. Mary's, Plaistow, 1914-16; warden, St. Peter's Coll., Jamaica and examining chaplain to bishop, 1917; canon missioner, 1918; asst. bishop of Jamaica, 1919; bishop of Barbados, 1927.

BARKER, H. P., D.S.C. (1917).—B. 1883; master mariner, 1919; asst. marine supt., mercantile marine; ch. instr., Dublin Sch. of Navig. and Seamanship, 1918; port off., Tanganyika, Oct., 1919; marine supt., 1931.

BARIAS, WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. Pitlochry High Sch. and Thomson's Acad., Dundee; travelling teacher, Falkland Is., Feb., 1908; ag. govt. schoolmr., June, 1913; asst. teacher, govt. schl., July, 1914; mag., &c., S. Shetlands, Nov., 1914; junr. clk., C.S.O., Apl., 1915; mag., &c., New Is., Mar., 1916; mag., &c., S. Georgia, Apl., 1918; dep. mag., S. Georgia, Jan., 1920; ag. mag. S. Georgia, on various occasions, 1921-27, ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., May, 1922-Feb., 1923; ag. col. postmtr. and local audr., June, 1926-Feb., 1927; mag., S. Georgia, Sept., 1928.

BARLEY, JACK CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A., 2nd cls. honors; cadet, W. Pacific ev. ser., Dec., 1911; attd., res. comntr.'s off., Apl., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, Nov., 1912; ag. treas. and colls. cust., Apl., 1913; passed cadet, Apl., 1914; dep. comntr., July, 1914; dist. offr., Gus., 1915; ag. res. comntr., Apl., 1917; dep. comntr., Fiji, Mar., 1924; judl. comntr. (temp. as provisional), Feb., 1928; 1st dist. offr., Apr., 1928; chmn., lab. comntr., 1928; res. comntr., Gilbert and Ellioe Is., Col., Oct., 1934.

BARNARD, SIR FRANK STILLMAN, K.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1856; entd. ser. of B. Columbia Express Co. in 1879; subsequently became gen. man. and pres. of the company; promoter, and dir. for many years of the B. Columbia Electric Rly.; identified with many other important interests; mem. of H. of C., Canada, 1879-1896; alderman of Victoria, B.C., 1886 and 1887; mayor, 1896; ret. from active business life in 1906; lieut.-gov. of B. Columbia, 1914-1919.

BARNES, ARTHUR CHAPMAN, B.Sc. (Hons.), F.I.C., A.M.I.C.H.—B. 1891; ed. Deacon Schl., Peterborough, Munic. Coll. of Tech. and Victoria Univ., Manchester (schol.); comp. survey dept., E.A.P., Oct., 1914; asst. anal., Apr., 1916; chem. offr., Kenya, 1919; ag. dir. chem. research, May to Aug., 1920; agr. chem. Nigeria, Apr., 1923; asst. dir., agr., Zanzibar, June, 1927; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Sept., 1929; prov. offl. mem., leg. coun.; dir., agr., Fiji, Jan., 1929; offl. mem., leg. coun.; chmn., coconuts comte., chmn., livestock record assn.; dir. agr., 1930; dir., agr. and island chemist, Jamaica, 1933.
BARNES, HAROLD CHARLES VERNON PLACE.—B. 1905; ed. Uppingham Schl.; asst. aud., Kenya, July, 1928.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1884; c. and pay and qtrmr., 1900; sub-inspr. for constab., 1906; attached to R.I.C. for training, 1908; inspr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher grade, 1914; asst. adjutant to local forces, 1914; c. Fiji Def. Forces, 1914; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1916; J.P. for the county, 1916; prov. censor, 1917; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, 1917 and Sept., 1920; chief inspr., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1918; dist. comntr., 1921; ag. dist. comntr. and prov. comntr., Ba, May, 1921-June, 1922; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., Jan., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1925.

BARNHILL, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A. Hon. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comntr., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1926; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1926; seconded to Gold Coast as asst. col. sec., 1934.

BARRETT, ARTHUR LUTLEY.—B. 1882; nat. affrs. dept., Cape, Feb., 1901; clk., nat. appl. ct., sec. to several deptl. comtees, and pvt. sec. to Sir Walter Stanford; prot., nat. Kimberley, 1913; prin. clk., ch. nat. comntr., Natal, 1920; mag., Nqamakwe, 1925; ch. clk., office of ch. mag., Umtata and sec. and treas. Transkeian Territories gen. coun., 1928; ch. clk., dept. nat. affrs., Pretoria, 1929; seconded as comntr. of conciliations, 1930; dir., nat. lab. (Union) and ch. nat. comntr., Witwatersrand, 1932.

BARRETT-LENNARD, SIR FIRMINES CECIL ARTHUR, KT., B.A. (1926)—Served in Anglo-Egyptian War, but invalided through wounds received in action, 1899-1900; judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, and ex officio of sup. ct., Nigeria, Sept., 1915; served with local mil. forces and as judge, court of appeal for eastern Africa and the high ct. of Uganda, Feb., 1915 to Nov., 1916; judge, sup. ct., S. Stilimts, and F.M.S., Mar., 1920; judge.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BAOH. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1895; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Tem., 1900; registr. and prin. reg. of documents, E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1902; Crown advoo., Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comnr., Oct., 1904; judge, high ct., E. Africa Prot., April, 1905; atty-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918; ch. just., Kenya, Aug., 1920; ret., 1934.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MURRAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1923; war serv., Aug., 1914—Jan., 1920; admnstr. offr. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; asst. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; asst. admnstr. gen. and asst. reg. of companies, June, 1924 and Nov., 1925 to Jan., 1926; rea. mag., Oct. 1925; ag. admnstr. gen., ag. reg. gen., births, deaths and marriages, ag. reg., companies, ag. liquidator German savings bank, ag. reg., trade marks, Jan. to Aug., 1926; asst. judge, high ct., Nigeria, 1934.

BARTLEY, WM., M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1885; B.A., Dublin; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1908; passed cadet, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., Jan., 1912; passed in Javanese, Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindinga, May, 1912; asst. reg., impts. and expts., S.S., Dec., 1915; sec., food control comte., June, 1917; mem. shipping comte., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrl's comte., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services recog. by S. of S., Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering comnr., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comte. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Apr., 1922; sec. to rem., Selangor, Aug., 1926; offr. cls. IV. comnr., lands, S.S., Dec., 1926; cls. IA; under-secr. S.S., 1930; ag. col. sec., S.S., May to June and Aug. to Sept., 1930; pres., mun. comnr., Singapore, Mar., 1931; supernmy. offr., cls. IA, S.S., 1932.

BARTOLO, PROF. SIR AUGUSTO, KT. BACH. (1929), LL.D., B. LIT., F.R.H.S.—B. 1883; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; B.Lit., 1904; called to bar, 1907; edr. for many years of "Daily Malta Chronicle"; prof., hist. of legislation and examr. in law and lit., Malta Univ.; chmn., Malta branch, Empire Parly. Assocn.; Malta Univ. del. at congress of Univ. of Empire in Gt. Britain, 1921; el. for 2nd divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for educn. and migrn., Aug., 1927; judge, 1934; author of several works on Maltese hist. and Imp. affrs.

BARTON, A. B.V., O.B.E. (1933).—Imp. cust. serv., 1912; cust. ast., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. of cust., Apr., 1923; comptr., cust., B. Guiana; collr., gen., Jamaica, 1927; collr., cust. and excise, Trinidad, 1930.

BARTON, C. J. J. T., O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law triposea; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; asst. dist. comnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comnr., 1917, 1919, 1921; asst. for native affrs., 1923; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1924-26; ag. ast. col. sec., 1927; ag. prin. ast. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun. on various occasions, 1928-33; senr. ast. col. sec., 1932; ag. dep. col. sec., 1934; author various papers on Kenya tribes and local history.

BARTON, CECIL MOLYNEUX, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Dublin (hons., classics, law); barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1906; ast. dist. comnr., E.A.P., 1913; rea. mag., 1914-21; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1921 to Oct., 1922; legal adviser, Gambia, Dec., 1922; comsnr. to enquire into double inoculation of cattle, 1918; chmn. of coman. to enquire into inoculation of cattle for rinderpest by vety. dept., 1919; ag. judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1923-28; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; circuit judge, Gold Coast, 1929; puisne judge, 1934.

BARTON, GEORGE SAMUEL HORACE, B.S.A., D.Sc.A.—B. 1883; ed. Vankleek Hill High Schl., Ontario Agr. Coll., Toronto Univ. (B.S.A.), Laval Univ. (D.Sc.A.); asst., animal husbandry, 1907; lecturer, 1910; prof., 1911; dean, faculty of agr., Macdonald Coll., McGill Univ., 1925; comdr., Agricole Merite, Quebec govt., 1929; dep. min., agr., Canada, 1932.

BARTON, MAJOR P. F., V.D.—Asst. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; addl. col. sec.'s office, Oct.-Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1908; ch. asst. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in desps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1922; treas., Dominica, 1932; treas. and collr., cust., St. Helena, Mar., 1933.
BARTON, T., M.A.—B. 1904; qualified teacher Scottish educn. dept.; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Oct., 1928.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HENRY NEWMAN, K.C.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; atty.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and atty.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. Senator, 1925; agt. gen. for S. Australia, 1928.

BASSETT, JOHN HAROLD, Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple.—B. 1896; ed. Wallacey and Glasgow Univ.; war serv., 1914-19; priv. sec., dep. contrlr. (organisation), Min. of Lab., 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1921; mag., Klang, Jan., 1922; town planning admtr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Mar., 1927; offr., cls IV, Oct., 1927; regsr., sup. ct., Penang, Oct., 1931; offr., cls III, Oct., 1932; dep. pub. pros., S'pore, Apr., 1933; ag. solr.-gen., S.S., Mar., 1934.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; addl. to Ratnapura Kuch., Jan., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1922; asst. sttlmt. offr., July, 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1930; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1933.
BATEMAN, G. S., L.D.S., R.S.C., Eng.—Serv. with Forces in E. Africa 1915 and 1916; dental surg., Uganda, May, 1912.

BATES, ISAAC GRAINGER.—B. 1886; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1909; equity bar, 1909-13; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., with jurisdiction as a juill. comsnr. for the Western Pacific, Nov., 1913; ag. res. comsnr. in addn. during 1919; seconded for serv. as ch. pol. mag., Fiji, May, 1920 to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., for various periods, 1924-33; ag. solr.-gen., July to Oct., 1927; puisne judge, Apr., 1934.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control and audit office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tender bd., 1901; transf. Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1902; prin. clk., July 1910; prin. clk., Union prisons dept., Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; asst. dir. prisons, Apr., 1926; is also comsnr. of oaths for Union; sp. grade supt., Pretoria cent. prison and Pretoria gaol and outstations and warden, Baviaanspoort farm col. and inebriate reformatory, 1930; do., Johannesburg gaol and outstations and warden, Leeuwkop farm col., Jan., 1934.

BATTERBEE, SIR HARRY F., K.C.M.G. (1921), K.C.V.O. (1927), C.M.G. (1918), C.V.O. (1915), B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Schls., Faversham and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1898-1904; 1st cls. class. moda., 1901; 1st cls. math. moda., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., C.C., May, 1906; ag. 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; 1st cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. Afr. comm. lands comtec., 1912; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lew., (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Islington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st Mar., 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; priv., 1st Apr. 1921; asst. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Admincmdg. sp. serv. squadron during Empire cruze, 1923-24; asst. sec., 12th Dec., 1924; polit. sec. to H.R.H. the Duke of York during his visit to Australia and N.Z., 1927; asst. under sec. of state for dom. affrs. 25th Jan., 1930; dep. sec. Imp. Conf., 1930; regiar., Order of St. Michael and St. George since 1930.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS.—B. 1865; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet Ceylon, Apr., 1920; addl. to Colombo cust., May, 1923; addl. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1921; office ass't. Badulla kach., Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Panatana Oct., 1922; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1925; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1925; 4th asst. col. sec., Mar., 1925; 3rd asst. col. sec., May, 1926; 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun. May, 1926; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, 1926; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1929-32; col. sec., Cyprus 1934.

BATTISCOMBE, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Haileybury Coll.; schol., Lincoln coll., Oxford; B.A., (hons.) 1914; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1914; served with R. Berks. Regt., Grenadier Guards during European War; apptd. in col. serv. cancelled on acct. of wounds, 1st re-enl. col. serv. as headmnr., Machakos misc. schl. and inspr. of schls., Ukamba prov., K. Africa, 1921; tutor to Prince Seyyid Abduilla, son of the sultan of Zanzibar, 1922; sec. to sultan 1923; admtr. offr., Zanzibar, 1923; Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 4th cls., 1924; addl. as ptve. sec. to suite of Sultan on state visit to England, 1929; clk., couns., June, 1930; sec. offr. gaz., July, 1930 and July, 1933; agt. prin., D.O., 18th Mar., 1932; 1st amt. sec. secretariat, Nov., 1932.

BAVERSTOCK, WILLIAM CHARLES BETR., R. of O., K.R.R.C.—Ed. Wimborne Grammar Schl. and Milton Abbas Grammar Schl., Earlsford; overseer, Pacific Phosphate Co. F. Phosphate Coman.), 1905; served, Great War, Queen's Own Dorset Yeomanry, Dorset Regt., K.R.R.C., Aug., 1914 - July, 1919; min. of munitions, 1919; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellerton Col., Jan., 1920; dep. commr., W. P. legs. comm., 1920.

BAVIN, HON. SIR THOMAS RAINFORD, K.C.M.G. (1933), K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Auckland, N.Z. and at Sydney Univ.; called to bar, 1897; prof. of law, Univ. of Tasmania 1900; sec. to prime min. C. of A., 1901-4; M.I.A., N.S.W., from 1917; premier and co-treasurer, N.S.W., 1927-30.

BAXTER, JOHN BABINGTON MACAULEY, K.C., D.C.L.—Elected to New Brunswick Legislature, 1911; atty.-gen., 1914; leader of provincial opposition, 1920; min. of int. in Dom. govt., 1921; prime min., New Brunswick, 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.B., 1931.

BAYLES, HERBERT LAURENCE.—B. 1886; ed. Dulwich Coll.; clk., C.A.'s office, 1905; olk., const. serv., Nyassaland, 1909; P.W.D., 1910; Nyassaland Field Force, 1914; ast. finan. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1916-17; ast. treas., Zanzibar, 1921; dep. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1924; treas., Trinidad and chmn., agrl. bank, May, 1931; treas. and currency offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933.

BAYNES, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1880; clk., G.P.O., Antigua, Jan., 1899; olk. to comntr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1901; priv. sec. and olk. to adminstr. Dominica, Aug., 1909; transf'd, to Impl. civ. ser. and apptd. 2nd cl. clk., National Health Insurance Comm. (England), June, 1912; 1st cl. clk., May, 1913; priv. sec. to sec., ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917; sec., National Health Insurance Joint Comttee., Oct., 1919; priv. sec. to parly. sec., ministry of shipping, Apr., 1920 to Mar., 1921; suptg. clk., Min. of Health, Oct., 1920; sec., "Discovery" comttee., C.O., July, 1923; col. sec., Leeward Is., Oct., 1925; admtd. govt. of Antigua on numerous occasions; ag. gov., Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1930-34.

BAYNES, THOMAS EDWIN PERCIVAL, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1884; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl. and Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; clerical astt., col. sec.'s off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th clk., C.S.O., 1903; 5th clk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st clk., post office, Antigua, 1907; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1911; ag. olk. to adminstr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., clk. of exec. and legis. councils, Antigua, and supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; clk. to adminstr., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Dominica, 1919; offr. in ch. (later, warden), N. dist., 1924; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, in Mar., 1926 and Apr., 1928; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dominica in 1927 and 1928; ag. astt. col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr., Sept., 1927; treas., Dominica, June, 1928; offl. mem., exec. coun., Dominica, June, 1928; O.A.G., Montserrat, June-Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Dominica on three occasions in 1929, 1930 and 1931; comntr., Montserrat, 1932.

BAZELL, C.—B. 1885; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar 1904-08); B.A. 1908 (2nd hon. moda., 2nd lit. hum.); astt. mast., Raffles Inst., Spore, 1914-17 and 1922; headmast., Malay Coll., K. Kangsar, 1923.

BEAL, KEAR-ADML. ALISTER FRANCIS, C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), R.N.—B. 1875; ed. H.M.S. "Britannia," Dartmouth; comdr., H.M.S. "Princess Royal," 1912-15; capt., 1915; served on Admty. war staff, 1915-18; in command H.M.S. "Weymouth," 1918-19 and 1920-21; dep. dir., operations divn., Admty. naval staff, 1921-23; commodore commdg. N.Z. squadron and naval adviser to N.Z. Govt., 1923-26; offr., Legion of Honour, 1919; Italian Silver Med. for mily. valour, 1918.

BEARDMORE, ERNEST.—B. 1882; ed. Manchester and Capetown; B.A. (1907); LL.B. (1911); clk., Cape civ. serv., 1900; prof. astt. and ch. clk., solr.-gen., Cape, 1920; solr.-gen., Cape, 1931; crown pros., Witwatersrand, 1933.

BEATLEY, FRANK EDWARD MAMERTUS.—B. 1882; ed. Lucton Grammar Schl.; traffic sup., Mauritius govt. rly., 1914; dep. traffic man., Sierra Leone rly., Jan., 1925; traffic man., 1930; gen. man. and traffic man., 1933.

BEATTIE, Lieut.-Col. ALEXANDER ELDER, C.M.G. (1933), C.B.E. (1920), M.C. (1915), ret. pay (res. of offrs.).—Entd. Army (Argyll and Sutherland Highlands), 1906; transfd. on promotion to the Queen's (now The Queen's Royal) Regt., 1908; served in Aden and India; seconded for serv. with W.A.F.F., 1911; astt. adjutant, Southern Nigeria Regt., 1912; ag. regtl. adjutant, S.N. Regt., 1913; adjutant, 4th Bn. Nigeria Regt., 1914; capt., 1914; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914; staff offr. to various field columns, 1914-15; ment. in despe., 1914; M.C., 1915; severely wounded, 1915; astt. staff offr., W.A.F.F., Col. Office, 1915; staff offr. (gen. staff offr.), W.A.F.F., 1916; temp. major, 1916; offr. staff offr., K.A.R., 1916; promoted major by brevet, 1918; tempy. lieut. col., 1918; C.B.E., 1920; accompanied inspr.-gen., W.A.F.F., on tour of inspection, Nigeria, Gold Coast, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 1920; C.O. rep. on various inter-depl. comtees, 1918-21; ret. with rank of lieut.-col., Sept., 1922; reed. thanks of S. of S. for services under the C.O.; astt. sec., Gibraltar, Sept., 1922; res. inspr. of schls., Oct., 1922; clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1922; sec. bd. of educn. (ex officio), 1922; local secy., Cambridge Univ. exams. (ex officio), 1922; ag. D.P.W. on two occasions; col. sec., Falkland Is., 8th Oct., 1925; ag. gov. from May, 1926 to Apr., 1927; ch. astt. col. sec., Cyprus, 8th Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1927-29; col. sec., Gibraltar, Jan., 1930.
BEATTIE, JAMES, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1891; European War, 1914-18; Somaliland Camel Corps, 1918-20; M.C., 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. comntr., 1922; comdt., pol., Oct., 1928.

BEATTIE, Sir JOHN CARRUTHERS, K.T. (1920), F.R.S.E. and S.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Ed. and Wits).—B. 1866; ed. Moray House, Edinburgh Univ.; Vans Dunlop schl. in Physics, 1861 Exhibn. Schi., Univ. of Munich, Vienna, Berlin and Glasgow; prof. of physics, S. African Coll., 1897; medallist S. African Assoctn. for Advancement of Science, 1910; crossed Africa from Cape Town to Cairo with Prof. J. T. Morrison on magnetic survey; chmn., survey comen., 1921; pres., S. African Assoctn. for Advancement of Science, 1928; prin., S. African Coll., 1917-18; prin. and vice-chane., Univ. of Cape Town since 1918; prin. publns.: rept. of a Magnetic Survey of S. Africa, Cambridge, 1909, and (in conjunction with Prof. J. T. Morrison) "On Magnetic Work in Southern and Central Africa," Washington, 1913.

BEATTY, Sir KENNETH JAMES, K.T. BACH. (1926).—B. 1878; ed. Univ. High Schl. and Melbourne Univ.; qualified Victorian bar, 1900; Transvaal law certif. (including Roman Dutch law), 1904; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1908; served in S. African war as lieut., V.M.I., wounded in action (Queen's med. and 4 clasps); apptd. pub. pros., Transvaal, Nov., 1902; addtnl. astt. res. mag., Mar., 1903; astt. res. mag., Dec., 1905; astt., Natal Carbineers, Natal rebellion, 1906; reed. thanks of Natal govt., med. and clasp; pol. mag., master of sup. ct. and registr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1908; has acted as ch. just., circuit judge and atty.-gen., Sierra Leone; on mily. serv., 1915-18; puisme judge, Gold Coast, 1921; ch. just., Bermuda, 1924; ch. just., Bahamas, 1927; dep. gov. in, 1928.; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1931.

BEAUBRUN, IGNATIUS CECIL.—B. 1889; treasy. clk., St. Lucia, 1906; astt. audit clk., 1907; rev. offr., St. Vincent, 1912; ag. audit clk., 1910 and 1912; rev. offr., Grenada, 1915; 3rd treas. clk., 1916; ch. clk., treas. and commr. of income tax, 1922; ast. treas., 1929; col. treas., collr. of cust., supt. of inld. rev., ch. of excise, Grenada, June, 1930; ag. col. sec. and regisr.-gen., June, 1932.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th Earl (creat. 1815), Visct. Elmley (1815). Baron Beauchamp (creat. 1806, U.K.); K.G.; K.C.M.G. (1899); William Lygon; B. 1872; succeeded his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1895-6; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900; capt. of hon. corps of Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1906; first commr. of works, 1911-14; lord-pres. of the coun., 1910-15.

BEAUCHAMP, ARTHUR KENNETH.—B. 1888; ed. Worcester Cath., King's Schol. and Christ Church, Oxford, B.A., 3rd cls. math. hons., 1910; headmr., gram. schl. and sub-inspr., schls., Dominica, Sept., 1912; ag. inspr., schls., Leeward Is., July-Oct., 1916; inspr., schls., St. Lucia, Jan., 1921; ast. educn., S. Provin., Nigeria, 1926.

BEAUCHESENE, ARTHUR, M.A., K.C., LL.D., Litt.D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1876; ed. St. Joseph's (class. course), Memramcook, N.B.; grad. as B.A., M.A. and Litt.D. valedictorian, 1895; studied law, Laval, Montreal; admitted to bar, Quebec, 1904; K.C., 1914; legal adviser, justice dept., 1913-16; clk. astt., House of Commons, Canada, and commr. to admstr. oath of allegiance to M. of P.; olk., House of Commons, 1925; author of "Beauchesne's Parliamentary Rules and Forms," "Ecrivains d'Autrefois," etc.

BECK, DAVID MACDONALD HERBERT, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; temp. lieut., W.A.F.F., Feb., 1917; admstr. offr., Nigeria, Jan., 1919; dep. res., Apr., 1933; admstr. offr., cls. I, 1935.

BECK, EWALL EGERTON HERBERT.—B. 1894; ed. Weymouth Coll., Dorset; pol. probtnr., F.M.S., Oct., 1914; ast. astnt. commr. pol., Selangor, Feb., 1923; suplt., convict estabtn., Taiping and inspr., prisons, F.M.S., Sept., 1923; offr. i/o, pol. dist., Kuantan, Aug., 1927; comdt. pol., depot, K. Lumpur, Mar., 1929; suplt., pol. S.S., Oct., 1931; ch. pol. offr., N. Semban, Feb., 1932.

BECKETT, HAROLD.—B. 1891; ed. at Monmouth Grammar Schol. and Oxford; exhibtioner at Wadham Coll., 1910-1914; also Symons exhibitioner, 1912-1914; 1st cls. Class. Moda., 1912; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum., 1914; B.A., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 30th Nov., 1914; ast. priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 14th Jan. to 16th Mar., 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., 17th July, 1919; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir G. Fiddes), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; mem. W. Indies currency comte., 1923; sent to Mauritius as spec. commr. to enquire into and rept. to S. of S. on questions of currency and exchange, Apr., 1923; returned to C.O., Oct., 1925; ag. astnt. sec., 28th Sept., 1931.

BECKETT, HERBERT JAMES RUST, A.C.A.—B. 1885; treas., N. Borneo, Feb., 1911; dist. treas., Apr., 1911; mag., 1st cls., Apr., 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1916-19; dist. offr., Aug., 1919; ast. commr., cust. and excise, Mar., 1921; ast. commr., excise, Feb., 1926; auditor, Mar., 1929; finance contr., Dec., 1930.

BEDINGTON, GUY CYRIL, A.M.I.C.E., F.S.I., M.I.M. & Cy. E., M.R.San.l., Chtd. Civ. Engrnr. and Chtd. Survr.—B. 1889; ed. Univ. of Bristol; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., July, 1913; exec. engrnr., Ipoh, 1922; ag. senr. exec. engrnr., Kuantan, 1927; attd. ch. hydrlc. engrnr.'s office, K. Lumpur, Nov., 1929; ag. senr. exec. engrnr. waterworks, K. Lumpur, Oct., 1930; ar. exec. engrnr., F.M.S. & S.S., Klang, 1932; sr. exec. engrnr., wks. and bldgs., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1935.

BEECHING, HERBERT.—Seconded from Basutoland service; apptd. ag. sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., Dec., 1920; ag. astnt. res. mag., 1921; ag. res. mag., 1923; passed 1st grade Secwana exam., 1926; sub. inspr., Bec. Prot. pol., July, 1927; hon. rank inspr., Nov., 1927.
BEBGIE, RALPH STOCKMAN, M.D., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.).—Health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; ag. malarialogist, 1931; ast. bacteriologist, 1932; ag. bacteriologist, 1933.

BELAND, HON. HENRI SEVRIN.—B. 1865; ed. Three Rivers (Que.), Coll. (B.A.), Laval Univ. (M.D.); physician; mayor of St. Joseph, 1910; el. to leg. ass., Quebec, 1897 and 1901; el.
