COCHRANE, CAPT. BASIL WILLIAM DUNSTONALD.—B. 1891; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1916; served S.W. Africa campaign and Me-opotamia (R. of O.); "Dunsterforce" (Azerbaijan), North Persia, 1918-19; South Persia Riders, Kerman Brig., cant. and adjt., 1st Cavalry, 1919-20; "1914-15" star, Br. War, Victory and L.G.S. medal (bar, S. Persia); constable, Kenya pol., 1921; asst. supplt., pol., 1925.

COCHRANE, EDGAR, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow, 1925), D.P.H. (1931).—B. 1897; on war serv., 1915-19; ag. dist. surg., Jesselton, Br. N. Borneo, 1925-26; asst. gov't. med. offr. of health and deport health offr., Br. Guiana, 1932.
COCHRANE, KENNETH ALEXANDER BASIL—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; ag. res., 1932 and 1933-34; admtr. ser., clis. II, 1933.

COCKEY, C. E. E.—Ed. St. Edwards Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; M.A.; served in France and Salonica; 3rd Devons, atchd. 2nd Glouc. Regt., May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; ag. capt.; mentd. in desps.; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, June, 1919.

COCKMAN, HERBERT JAMES, D.F.C.—B. 1898; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Merton Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1921; attd., Fedl. secrt., Jan., 1922; offr. cls. V, Dec., 1924; offr. cls. IV, dist. offr., Pekan, Dec., 1928; offr. cls. III, dist. offr., Lipis, Dec., 1933.

COCKRAM, BEN.—B. 1903; ed. Victoria, Taunton's Schl. and Oxford; hist. schol., Queen's Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls., mod. hist., 1925; Goldsmith's sen. student, 1926; 1sts cls., med. and mod. hist., London, 1923; fellow, Michigan, 1925-26; Ph.D., 1926; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin., Dominions Office, 4th Nov., 1928; pte. sec. to the party, under sec. for dom. affrs. (Mr. Lunn), 1st Oct., 1930; prte. sec. to perm. under sec. of state for Dominion affrs., 22nd June, 1931.

COCKS, HUGH CECIL, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd. Colombo kach., Jan., 1928; attd. secretariat, May, 1928; office ass't, Nuwara Eliya kach., Jan., 1929; office ass't to gov't. agt., Uva, May, 1930; asst. attlit. offr., June, 1930.
CODD, LESLIE EDWARD WASTELL, M.Sc.—B. 1908; ed. Natal Univ. Coll., S.A.; plant breeder, Br. Guiana, 1931.

CODERRE, HON. LOUIS, K.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1865; ed. primary schl., St. Ours, Hyacinthe and Montreal Colleges and Laval Univ.; called to the bar, 1892; head of the firm Coderre and Coderre, advocates, Montreal; city attorney, St. Henri, 1896-1905; syndic of the Montreal bar, 1904-1909; unsuccessful cand. for Quebec legislature, 1908, and for H. of C. in same year; elec. to H. of C. for Hochelaga, Sept., 1911; sworn of the P.C. for Canada and sec. of state, Oct., 1912; also min. of mines, 1913; judge, super. ct., Quebec, 1915.

COE, THOMAS PEROWNE, M.C.—B. 1887; ed. King Edward VI Schl., Norwich and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; (schol.) 2nd cls. class. moda., 1908, 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, Dec., 1912; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1913; on war serv. with Norfolk Regt., 1915-18; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; demob., Feb., 1919; 2nd ass't, sec., Fed. secretariat, Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec. in add., Nov., 1919 to June, 1920; 2nd-lieut. M.S.V.R., Apr., 1921; ag. capt., Nov., 1921; capt., Aug., 1922; supernumy. offr., cls. III, Nov., 1922; major, M.S.V.R., Jan., 1923; dist. offr., July, 1924; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; sec. to res., Perak; asst. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1928; ag. gen. advr., Johore, Mar., 1930; offr. cls. I.B; asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Feb., 1930; ag. dir. gen., posts. and tels., Malaya, Mar., 1933 and Jan., 1934.
COGHLAN, B. A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1899; I.F.S., A.M.C., 1922-23; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1926.

COHEN, ANDREW BENJAMIN.—B. 1909; ed. Malvern and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., 1st pt., class. tripes, 1st cls., 2nd pt., class. tripes; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., inland rev., Oct., 1932; transf'd to C.O., 2nd Oct., 1933.

COHEN, EZCHIEL, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1882; clk., Cape govt. rly., May, 1900; transf'd to Imperial Mil. Rly., Nov., 1900; transf'd to Bech. Prot. service and attached to office of high commr. for S. Africa, Aug., 1901; asst. Imp. acctnt., Apl., 1918; prin. clk., Nov., 1923.

COLE, ARNOLD KIRTLAND.—B. 1884; sec. to bd. of educn., Bahamas, 1907; ag. res. justice, Rum Cay, 1908; ag. inspr. and genl. supt. of schools, 1910, 1911, 1913; 3rd clk., treasy. dept., 1913; 1st clk., P.O., 1914; ch. clk. and asst. examr. offr., cust. dept., Aug., 1921; ag. contrlr., cust., in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; asst. contr., and examining offr., Dec., 1923; survr., shipping, Mar., 1925; ag. contr., cust. and regurl., shipping from Apr., 1926; ag. harbour., Mar. to Apr., 1927; contrlr., cust., Sept., 1928; M.L.C., Dec., 1931; del., W. Indies cust. confee., Bermuda, 1934.

COLEBATCH, HON. SIR HAL PATESHALL, KT. BACH. (1927).—B. 1872; ed. Goolwa, S. Australia; M.L.C. for E. Prov., W. Australia, May, 1912; col. sec., min. for educn., min. of pub. health and leader of legis. coun., 1916-19; premier, W. Australia for short period, 1919; min. of educn. and pub. health, 17th May, 1919; agt.-gen. for W. Australia, 1923-27; mem., Australian constitution royal comm., 1927-29; C'wealth senator for W. Australia since 1929.

COLEMAN, EPHERAIM HERBERT, K.C.—B. 1890; ed. Pub. and High Schls., Arnprior and Carleton Place, Ont., and Univ. of Manitoba; sec.-treas., Canadian Bar Assoc., 1919-33; dean, Man. Law Schl., 1929-33; bencher, Law Soc. of Man., 1928-33; under-secv. of state, Canada, 1933.

COLEY, CAPT. C. F. (Reg. Army, R. of O.)—B. 1897; ed. Oxford Univ. and Université de Paris; served with Duke of Cornwall's Light Inf'y., Devon Regt., and R. Corps of Signals in European War, 1914-18; diplomatic serv., 1918; cadet, Nigerian admstive. serv., Mar., 1926; ag. asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1926.

COLLENS, ARCHIBALD EDGAR, F.I.C., F.C.S.—Lab. asst., and chem. demstr. to the prof. of chem'y., Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1900-08; lect., agrl. chem'y., govt. training schle., 1902-05; asst. analyst, govt. lab., Trinidad, 1908-16; offr. in charge, rubber tapping experiments, dept. of agr., 1911-17; scientific asst., bd. of agric., 1908-10; science master, Queen's Royal and St. Mary's Colls., Trinidad, 1906-08, 1910, and 1914; ag. prin. asst. anal., govt. lab., 1914-15; supt., field experiments, 1915-16; examr. in pharm. chem'y. to med. coun., Trinidad, 1908-17; examr. in agr., bd. of educn., 1908-17; govt. chem. and supt. of agr., Edward Is.; chmn., granary bd., Antigua; mem., food control prices bd., Antigua, 1917; mem., Barbuda bd., pub. lib. trustees; mem., Fed. exec. and leg. couns. and leg. coun., Antigua; gov. and on exec. coun., Imp. Coll. of Trop. Agr., 1921.
COLLETT, J. W., M.R.C.P., M.R.C.S.—Med. offr., S. Nigeria, 1901; author of "Text Book of Physiology and Hygiene."

COLLIER, C. W. H.—B. 1892; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; cler. asst., immigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1909; sec. to various col. comites. and gradually promoted to 1st cls. offr., 1920; immigrn. agt., 1926; sec., colonization (500 family scheme) comtee., 1927; senr. immigrn. agt. and immigrn. agt., Demerara county, 1928; ag. immigrn. agt., 1929; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1930; prin. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1932; asst. col. sec., 1934.

COLLIER, HON. PHILIP.—B. 1873; M.L.A. for Boulder, W. Australia, since 1905; min. for mines and rlys. in Scaddan govt., 1911-14, and of mines and water supply, 1914-16; leader, party labour party, May, 1917; premier, W. Australia, Apr., 1924 to Apr., 1930.
COLLIER, REGINALD.—B. 1880; stock inspr.; Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1915; inspr., 1924; Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Great War, 1917-19.

COLLINS, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Lond.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1910; attd. Karunegala kachi, Dec., 1910; office astt., Kandy Kachcheri, July, 1913; astt. land settm. offr., May, 1915; astt. land settm. offr., Sept., 1915; addtl. astt. govt. agt., ditto, Sept., 1916; office astt. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1916; 2nd astt. col. sec., Oct., 1917; 3rd astt. col. sec., Sept., 1920; 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1922; prin. astt. col. sec., Feb., 1924; addnl. astt. col. sec., Oct., 1925; dep. collr., cust., Dec., 1925; ag. dep. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Feb., 1933; dep. finan. secy., Feb., 1934.

COLLINS, W. H., Ph. D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Toronto, 1904; Heidelberg Univ., 1907; Chicago Univ., 1909; Univ. of Wisconsin, 1911; astt. geologist, geol. surv. of Can., 1905; jun. geol., 1912; geol., 1914; fellow, Royal Soc. of Can., 1918; dir. geol. surv. of Can., 1920; ex-off. mem., Can. Inst. Min. Met.; mem., Geol. Soc. of America, 1920; has pub. numerous papers and memoirs on pre-cambrian geol. and petrology.

COLLISSON, PERCIVAL LORIMER, O.B.E., Mil., 1919.—B. 1883; ed. at Westminster and King's Coll., London; examr., E. and A. dept., 1901; col. aud. branch, Sept., 1905; examr. of accts., O.R.C., Jan., 1906; rejoined col. aud. branch, Jan., 1908; astt. auditor, N. Nigeria, Sept., 1908; 2nd cls. clk., col. audit., 1914; mil. audit., E. Africa Exped. Force, 1916 (twice ment. in despatches); senr. astt. auditor, E.A.P., Apr., 1919; ag. audr., E.A.P., 1920; audr., Fiji and W. Pacific, 1922; audr., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1924; audr., Uganda, Sept., 1927; audr., Hong Kong, Aug., 1930; J.P., 1932.

COLLYMORE, ERNEST ALLAN, M.A. (Oxon.), K.C. (1933)—B. 1893; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Merton Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. hons. final schl. of jurisprudence, 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1920; serv. in E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-19; capt., Oct., 1915; capt., Barbados Vols. and O.C., cadet corps of the island, 1921-26; sen. mag., Bridgetown and dist. A., June, 1925; recvr. of wrecks, Aug., 1925; ag. judge, astt. ct. of appeal in 1925 and 1926; stip. and circuit n.g., Bahamas and coroner for New Providence, July, 1927; ag. atty.-gen., in 1927 and 1928; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1928.

COMBE, SIR RALPH MOLYNEUX, KT. BACH. (1920).—Ed. at Haileybury Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1897; Crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 4th Aug., 1905; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1907; atty.-gen., E.A.P., 1912; atty.-gen., Nigeria, 1913; ch. just., Nigeria, 1918; ret., Mar., 1929.

COMMISSIONG, TEKENER BERTRAND.—B. 1883; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Grenada, Apr., 1909; 2nd clk., gov.'s office, June, 1915; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1919; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., med. regia., and man. govt. printing sub-dept., Feb.-Sept., 1920; seconded for active serv., 1915 to Oct., 1919; awarded meritorious serv. med. in German E. African campaign, June, 1916; apptd. J.P., Grenada, in recog. of war serv., July, 1919; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. to exec. coun., med. regia., and man., govt. printing dept., May, 1921; astt. col. sec., Grenada, Jan., 1928.
CONNELL, W. K., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1893; mily. serv., R.A.M.C., 1916-20; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1924; junr. surg. specialist, 1934.

CONNOLLY, HON. SIR JAMES DANIEL, Kt. BACH. (1920).—B. 1870; ed. Warwick Pub. Sch.; Catholic Schl.; M.L.C., Western Australia, 1902; col. sec. and min. of com. and lab., 1916; col. sec. and min. of pub. health, 1910; leader of leg. coun. from 1906 to 1911; M.L.A. for Perth, 1914; agt. gen. in London for Western Australia, 1917-23; agt. gen. in London for Malta, 1930.

CONRADIE, DAVID GIDEON, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Hamlet and Ceres Schls., Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Dublin; sen. mod. and gold medal, LL.D., 1906; advr., Cape Town and Bloemfontein, 1907-09; sec., educ. dept., O.F.S., 1909-15; atty., Reitz and Bloemfontein, 1915-33; mem., prov. and prov. exec., 1920-26; M.P., Bethlehem and Lindley, 1926-33; admstr., S.W. Africa, 1933.

CONRADIE, JOHANNES HENDRIK.—B. 1872; ed. Calitzdorp, Robertson, pvt. study, later Stellenbosch; schlmaster, Christiana, 1889; Boshof, 1890-95 and Grey Coll. Schl., 1896-1900; admitted as atty.-at-law, 1905; finan. sec., Dutch Reformed Ch., Cape Prov., 1905-12; supr. Kakamas lab. col., 1912-1920; organising secy., Nationalist Party, Cape Prov., 1921-29; mem., leg. asscm. for Gordonia, 1924-29; admstr., Cape Prov., Sept., 1929.
CONSTANT, CHARLES FREDERICK, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1888; radiologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1924; do., col. hosp., Port-of-Spain, Trinidad, Jan., 1934.

CONWAY, WILLIAM EWAN.—B. 1893; ed. High Schl., Dundee; govt. travelling schl. master, 10th Dec., 1913; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1914; ag. clk. and priv. sec. to govt. of Falkland Is., June, 1916 to Apr., 1917 and Sept., 1917 to Apr., 1920; collr., cust., G. Coast, Oct., 1920; secr. colr., cust., June, 1928.

COOK, MAJOR DUDLEY STAFFORD.—B. 1899; ed. Elston Schl., Bedford; solr., June, 1913; astt. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; served in Cameroons, 1914; G.E.A., 1917-18; major, 1918; ag. c.msnr., lands, Nigeria, 1924-27; dist. offr., 1924; astt. c.msnr., lands, S. Provs., 1927-30; dep. rep., 1933; ag. c.msnr., Lagos, 1934.

COOK, RT. HON. SIR JOSEPH P. (C1914), G.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1860; mem. of H. of E. Commonwealth of Australia, since 1901; postm. gen., N.S. Wales, 1894-1898; min. for mines and agric., 1898-9; min. of defence, C. of A., June, 1909; prime minister, C. of A., June, 1913 Sept., 1914; min. for the Navy, 1917; ditto, from 1923 Jan., 1918; high commr.in London for C. of A., Nov., 1921; ret., 1927.

COOKE, EDWARD ROBERT CECIL, M.C., R.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1889; ed. Cambridge Univ. and Univ. Coll. Hosp., London; temp. commn., R.A.M.C., 2nd Sept., 1914; med. offr., 2nd Scottish Rifles, France; ag. matron, R.A.M.C. and divnl. anti-malaria offr., 1918; health offr., F.M.S.R., Jan., 1922; health offr., Klang and Port Swettenham, Mar., 1927; specl. specialist venereal disease, F.M.S., June, 1928; ch. med. offr., soc. hygiene, F.M.S., sept., 1929.
COOKE, ELEANOR MARGARET.—B. 1891; matron, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, May, 1928.

COOKE, S. V.—Astt. dist. commn., E.A.P., Jan., 1917; accompanied S. Aulhan (Sooma) patrol, 1917-18; polit. offr., Turkana, 1921; A.G.S. med. and clasp.; dist. commn., Kenya 1928; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1931.

COOKE, WILLIAM ERNEST, M.A., F.R.A.S.—Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel Univ., 1883; ast. astronomer, Adel. obser., 1884; M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astronomer, W.A., 1896; govt. astronomer, New South Wales, 1912-26; prof. of astronomy at Sydney Univ.

COOLS-LARTIGUE, THOMAS.—B. 1867; ed. at Mount Saint Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1889; admitted to the bar, St. Lucia, 1889; ditto, Leeward Is., 1894; ag. regis., St. Lucia, 1891; ag. mag. in 1903-4; ag. regis., St. Lucia, 1906-7; ag. mag., 1911; elective mem., Castries town bd., 1903-1912; regis. and prov. marshal, Dominica, and also supplementary mag., Roseau, Dominica, Feb., 1912; offl. mem. leg. coun., Dominica, visiting just. of Dominica prison and J.F., 1912; govt. mem., Roseau town bd., 1914; valuation commnr. for assessing of taxes, 1916; ag. crown atty. and mem. exec. coun., 1916-19; crown atty. and regis., sup. ct., Oct. 1919; ret., 1929; re-apptd. ag. addnl. mag., dist. E, 1930.

COOMARASWAMY, CHINNAPPAN.—B. 1887; ed. Hindu Coll., Jaffna, and Royal Coll., Colombo; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. serv., April, 1910; extra office asst. to govt. agt., Batticaloa, Apr., 1910; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1913; pol. mag. and office asst. to govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1913; pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Point Pedro, June, 1915; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1918; ditto, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1920; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, in addn., Aug., 1920; commnr. of requests, Colombo, May, 1922; dep. commnr., stamps, May, 1925; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1926; dep. commnr., stamps, May, 1926; regis., motor cars, Jan., 1928; regis.-gen., Sept., 1931; dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1932; do., Jaffna, Oct., 1933.

COOMARASWAMY, VALUPPILLI.—B. 1892; Cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1913; attached to Puttalam Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; Anuradhapura Kach., June, 1913; ag. addnl. office asst., Anuradhapura Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, Feb., 1914; extra office asst. to asst. govt. agent, Puttalam and Chilaw, in addition to his own duties, Mar., 1914; pol. mag., Negombo, July, 1917; ditto, Dambagamuwa, July, 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1919; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1920; dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1924; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, July, 1927; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Jan., 1931; clk., state coun. and sec. bd. of ministers, July, 1932; ag. govt. agt., Batticaloa, June, 1933.

COOMBES, H. P.—B. 1894; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland, May, 1917; senr. clk., Aug., 1923; ag. asst. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1926 to Mar., 1927; ag. senr. asst. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. office supt., secretariat and clk., exec. and leg. couns., in 1929 and 1930; office supt., secretariat, Apr., 1931; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1931 to Dec., 1933.

COOPE, ARTHUR EGERTON.—B. 1888; ed. Fonthill Sch., St. Edward's Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; passed cadet, June, 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., May, 1915; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Apr., 1919; dist. offr., Mar., 1921; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1923; offr. assist. advr., lands, Kedah, May, 1924; town planning admr. and state valuer, Ipoh, Oct., 1928; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1928; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, in addn., Apr. to May, 1929; ag. mag., Seremban, Sept., 1929; ag. chmn., sany. bd. in addn., asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Mar., 1930; mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1931; offr., cls. I B, Nov., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judi. commnr., Kelantan, Mar., 1933.

COOPER, BERTRAND, M.N.Z.I.S.—B. 1882; mounted infy., S. Africa, 1901-02; licensed survr., N.Z., 1906-07; survr., Pahang, Apr., 1921; asst. supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S., Jan., 1926; asst. supt. (field), Kinta, Aug., 1929; ag. sr. asst. supt., rev. surveys, Kinta, June, 1930 to Feb., 1931.

COOPER, EVANS GUSTAVUS.—B. 1875; asst. clk. of courts, Jamaica, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; clerk of ots., Hanover, Dec., 1912; J.P., 1913; ag. res. mag. on various occasions; supernum. res. mag., June, 1923; res. mag., June, 1924.

COOPER, COMDR. GUY EDWARD, R.N. (ret.).—B. 1891; ed. R.N.; Great War, ment. in desps.; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1923; legal adviser, Seychelles, Apr., 1929; ag. ch. just., May to Nov., 1928 and Nov., 1930 to May, 1932; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Nov., 1932.
COOPER, MAJOR NELSON GEORGE, R.A.M.C., M.D., C.M. (Univ. of Manitoba).—B. 1874; health offr., S.S. Nov., 1920.

COOPER, WILLIAM GERALD GROVES, Ph.D. (Lond.), B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.S., F.G.S., A.Inst. M.M.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Sch., Crediton, Devon, Battersea Polytechnic Inst. and Imp. Coll. of Sc. and Technology, 1911-14; res. engr. on location and constrn. of Canadian Northern Ontario Rly., 1911-14; war serv., 1914-18; geologist, Gold Coast geol. survey, Nov., 1919.
COORAY, EDMUND JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1931; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1931; office asst., Ratnapura kach., Mar., 1934.

CORBETT, ALAN FREDERICK.—B. 1878; ed. K.H.S., Coventry, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1902; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), 1903; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1904; sup. ct. (T.P.D.) Union of S.A., 1919; clk., res. mag., Worcester, July, 1903; clk., mast. sup. ct., Cape, Jan., 1905; clk., res. mag., Beaufort West, Dec., 1905; clk., civil. commnr., Kimberley, May, 1906; clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1911; ag. recr. of rev., Germiston, Oct., 1915; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Jan., 1921; asst. commnr. for inland rev., Apr., 1929; commnr. for inland rev., Sept., 1929.

CORBETT, GEORGE HAMBLIN, B.Sc., F.E.S.—B. 1887; ed. King Edwards Sch., Bath and Edin. Univ.; govt. entomologist, F.M.S., Apr., 1920; deleg. of F.M.S. and S.S. govts. at 2nd Imp. Entomological Conf., London, June, 1925; consltg. entomologist, Inst., Med. Resch., and Rubber Resch. Inst., in addn., June, 1930 and 1931.

CORDEAUX, MAJOR SIR HARRY EDWARD SPILLER, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.B. (1904), C.M.G. (1902).—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; minor scholarship, 1889; class. tripus, 1892; passed in Hindustani and Persian, 1895; Somali, 1899; B.A., 1892; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, Bombay polit. dept. as 4th asst. polit. res., Aden, Apr., 1898; asst. res., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admtd. govt. of Somaliland Prot., in 1900, 1902, 1903 and 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in despa., medal and 3 clasps); dep. commnr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admtd. govt., June, 1905;

CORY, SIR GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1862; ed. at St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, Mechanics Inst., Woolwich Arsenal and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (nat. sci. tripods), 1888; M.A. 1891; D. Litt., 1921; demonstr. of chem., Univ. of Cambridge, 1888; vice-prin., pub. schl., Grahamstown, 1891; lecturer in physics and chem., St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, 1894; prof., chem., Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown, 1904; hon. archivist to Union Govt., 1925; author "Rise of South Africa."

COSGRAVE, ALEXANDER KIRKPATRICK, M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Shrewsbury and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1909, D.P.H., 1918; L.M., Rot. Hosp., 1920; grad. with dist., L.S.T.M., 1920; D.T.M. & H. Lon., 1920; col. med. serv., 1910; med. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1911; on active serv., 1914-19; B.E.F. with 34th div., 1916; D.A.D.M.S., 16th (Irish) div., 1917-18; demob. with hon. rank of maj.; med. offr., Klang, ag. H.O., Coast and in charge, quarantine camp Port Swettenham, 1920; H.O., Negeri Sembilan, 1921; sen. med. offr., Selangor, 1922; S.M.O., Selangor, Jan., 1926; offl. mem., advisory coun. to L. of N. eastern health bureau, Singapore, Jan., 1927; senr. health offr., Selangor and Pahang, and ag. ch. health offr., F.M.S. in admn., Feb. to Oct., 1927 and Nov., 1927 to Mar., 1928; ag. ch. health offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1928 and Sept., 1931; major, M.S.V.R., Feb., 1930; state med. and health offr., Pahang, Oct., 1932.

COSSER, S. C. A.—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge; clk., treas., Transvaal, 8th May, 1901; ass't acctnt., treas., 1st Dec., 1905; ass't acctnt., treas., Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1916; provincial acctnt., Transvaal Prov., 21st Nov., 1910; served in European War, 1917-19 (Gen. Serv., Victory and Milly. Meda.)

COSTAR, NORMAN EDGAR.—B. 1909; ed. Battersea Grammar Schol. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; hist. schol., 1928-32; cls. I divn. 1, h. tripods, pts. I and II; B.A., 1931; Kellen prize man, 1931; apptd. after compet. exam., ass't prin., C.O., Apr., 1932.

COTCHING, HOUGHTON ALDGETT, A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1877; serv. in mech. dept., N.E. Ry. Cov., 1903-15; on mil. serv., 1915; seconded for duty with R.E. (inland waterways and docks), 1917; major, D.A.D.R.T. and ente Chemin de Fer du Hedjaz as loco. supt., 1919; ch. mech. engrnr., Palestine rly., 1920.

COULSON, NORMAN.—B. 1891; ed. Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; (M.A., hon. in cls. and mod. hist.); 2nd lieut., 10th Res. Reg., Car., Sept., 1914; attd. 8th (K.R.L.) Hussars, Sept., 1915; (France and army of occupation); cadet F.M.S., Apr., 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; attd. to treas., Penang on sp. duty, Sep., 1922; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1923; dist. offr., Raub, Feb., 1925; seconded for serv. in Kelantan, Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1928 supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1928; n.c. Seremban, Mar., 1930; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1931; offr., cls. II, Feb., 1933; ass't treas., Penang, Apr., 1934.

COURT, PHILIP FRANCIS SHIRLEY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.),—B. 1900; med. offr., mel. dept., Hong Kong; med. offr., ch., Kowloon and New Territories, Oct., 1930; ditto, Victoria Hosp., Mar., 1932; ass't med. offr., Victoria Hosp., May, 1932; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon Hosp., Mar., 1933.

COUTTS, JAMES MAGNUS.—B. 1886; ed. Heriot's Schol., Schol. of Medicine and Univ., Edinburgh; L.D.S., R.C.S.E., 1924; served great war with Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; M.M., 1916; dental surg., Falkland Is., Oct., 1925; ag. loc. audr., Apr., 1926-Apr., 1927; lieut., Falkland Is. Def. Force, 1927; capt., 1929; ag. O.C., May to Nov., 1928; 2nd in commd., 1927-31; dental offr., Straits Settlements, Aug., 1931.
COWAN, JOHN ARCHER, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1890; bacteriologist, S. Stltlnts., Apr., 1925; pathologist, S'pore, Aug., 1926.

COWAP, JOHN CHESTER, B.Sc. (Vict.), F.I.C.—B. 1884; chem. astt., govt. lab'y., London, Sept., 1904; astt. govt. analyst, S'pore, Apr., 1909; dep. govt. analyst, Penang, Oct., 1909; govt. analyst, S.S., June, 1924; distt. and astt. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, Jan., 1929.

COWELL, HUBERT RUSSELL, B.A., C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1877; ed. Malvern and Cambridge; scholar of Clare, 1896-9; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., inland revenue, Oct., 1901; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 27th Mar., 1902; sec. to inter-deptm'tl. comte., on oil fields of W. Indies, 1904; sec. to Roy. Coman. on trade relations between Canada and W. Indies, 1909-1910; priv. sec. to Sir J. Anderson, G.C.M.G., 26th June, 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emmott, 9th Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Islington, 8th Aug., 1914; ag. 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 10th Mar., 1916; ag. prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; astt. sec., 1st Apr., 1920.

COWGILL, JOHN VINCENT.—B. 1888; ed. Durham Schl. and All Souls Coll., Oxford (bible-clerk, 1907-11), 3rd cls. moda., 3rd cls. lit. hum., B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; studied Chinese, 1912-14; on mily. serv., 1915-19; M.C., 1917; ment. in desps., 1919; dist. offr., Dec., 1919; astt. advr., Trengganu, Nov., 1923; town planning admtr. and state valuer, Ipoh, June, 1926; collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and registr., titles, Selangor, Nov., 1927; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Kinta and dist. supt., prisons, July, 1930; mem., Perak coun. of state, Jan., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Mar., 1932; offr., cls. I.B., 1934; chmn., navy bd., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1934.

COWPER, HERBERT WELLENLEY.—B. 1883; ed. at St. Paul's School, London, Hertford Coll.; Oxford, and in Germany, France and Italv, astt. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; ag. res., 1916; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Dec., 1918.

COX, ARTHUR HENRY.—B. 1888; ed. Felsted and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; B.A. 1910; astt. dist. comsnr., Uganda, Nov., 1911; A.D.C. to gov., 1918-19; 1st gr. admtr. offr., Jan., 1922; dep. prov. comsnr., Dec., 1922; ag. prov. comsnr., July, 1929 and June, 1932; astt. ch. sec., Uganda, Mar., 1930; prov., comsnr., Dec., 1932.

COX, CHARLES EDWARD LANTON.—B. 1889; entd. civ. serv., B. Guiana, 1907; 2nd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office; called to the bar, 1918; stip. mag. for the colony; revising barrister, 1919; served in France as lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.) ; injured at Loos, Sept., 1916; invalided out of army, Nov., 1917; served under Admiralty as A.T.S.O., mining depot, Grangemouth, Mar. to Dec., 1918; extra A.D.C. to Sir W. Egerton, K.C.M.G., 1913-18; lieut., artillery cov., B. Guiana Militia, 1908; capt., 1919; pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1920-23; ag. atty.-gen. and ch. just., St. Vincent; crown atty. and registr., sup. ct., Dominica, 1923-25; mem., exec. coun., income tax comsnr., offl. recr., visiting just., prison, Dominica; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1925; ag. guiana judge, sup. ct., July, Oct. and Nov., 1929, and June-Oct., 1932.

COX, Lieut.-Col. C. H. F., C.M.G. (1927), D.S.O. (1917).—Ed. Rugby and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; comand. R.H. and R.F.A., 6th Jan., 1900; served S. African War; served in India, 1903-08; seconded Sudan civ. serv., 1913-15; served in Great War, 1915-18; four times ment. in desps., D.S.O., 1917, bar, 1918; brevet. lieut.-col., 1st Jan., 1919; astt. admtr. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1919; mil. gov., Nablus, Mar., 1920; ditto, Galilee, Aug., 1920; dist. gov., Samar, July, 1922; retd. from Army with rank of lieut.-col., Jan., 1923; ch. Br. res. Trans-Jordan, Apr., 1924.

COX, CHARLES LESLIE, M.Inst.C.E., F.R.S.I.—B. 1886; astt. engnr., Colombomun., 1906; city sanitation engnr., 1910; lieut., R.E. (B.E.F.), 1915; capt., 1917; dep. dir., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1921; ag. D.P.W., 1922 and 1923; D.P.W., 1923.

COX, HERBERT CHARLES FAHIE.—Br. Guiana pol., 1913-19; called to bar, 1915; on war serv., France, 1917-19; astt. to atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1919; ag. mag. on various occasions; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1923; M.L.C., 1926; ag. ch. just., May-Sept., 1926, Mar.-Oct., 1927 and from June to Sept., 1929; chmn., bd. of health, 1926-27; chmn., water and sewage bd., 1928-29; dep. gov., Mar., 1929; dep. admtr., June and July, 1929; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Dec., 1929; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1933; ag. atty.-gen., Mar.-Aug., 1934.

COX, MAJOR-GEN. SIR PERCY ZACHARIAH, G.C.M.G. (1922), G.C.I.E. (1917), K.C.S.I. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1920), K.C.I.E. (1911), C.S.I. (1909), C.I.E. (1902), F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—R. 1864; ed. Harrow and Sandhurst; joined army 1884; served with 2nd batt. Cameronians till 1889; joined Indian Staff Corps, 1889; Indian polit. dep., 1889; vice-consul, Zaila, Somal Coast, 1893; Berbera, 1894-5; consul and polit. agt., Muscat, 1899-1904; consul-gen., Bushire, 1904; polit. res., Persian Gulf, 1909; sec., foreign dept., govt. of India, 1914; ch. polit. offr., Indian Expdy. Force "D," 1914; ment. in desps., 1916; ag. British min. to Persia, 1919-20; high commr., Mesopotamia, 1920, ret., 1923.

CRABB, RICHARD BRUCE—B.1891; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; astt. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1915; served European War, East Surrey Regt., 1916-18; dist. comsnr., 1922; astt. col. sec., 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1926; ag. clk., couns. in 1927 and 1928; ag. prim. astt. col. sec., Apr. to Sept., 1932; senr. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1932.
CRAGG, WILLIAM GILLIAT, D.S.O. (1918), Major, R. of O. Loyal Regt.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and R.M.C., Sandhurst; astt. dist. offr., N. Nigeria, 25th Aug., 1920.
CRAIG, ERNEST DUNCAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.Z.S.—B. 1888; ed. Aberdeen Univ. and at Exeter Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, Jan., 1920.
CRAIG, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1928), LL.D. (N.Z.)—B. 1873; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1891; ch. clk., 1910; astt. comptr., 1913; comptr., 1923.

CRAIG, JOHN.—B. 1884; ed. at Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1st cls. hona., Classics; univ. prize in Latin, 1906; and at Christ Church, Oxford; B.A., 2nd cls. hona., lit. hum., 1908; after exam., exam'r., E. and A. dept., Jan., 1909; astt. auditor, S. Nigeria, May, 1909; temporarily transf'd to N. Nigeria, Aug., 1909; joined col. audit dept., Jan., 1910; aud. Br. Honduras, Nov., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., Sir W. Collett, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1914; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov. R. Walter, C.M.G., gov. W.Hart-Bennett, C.M.G., and gov. Eyre-Hutson, C.M.G., 1915-19; lieut., B. Honduras Territorial Force; audr., Mauritius, Apr., 1920; col. audit dept., 1st Apr., 1924; dep. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1926; ag. audr., Mar.-Nov., 1927; treas., Fiji, Apr., 1930; chmn., economy comtee., 1931; chmn., tariff revision comtee., 1932.

CRAIG, MAJOR JOHN CORMACK, D.S.O. (1918), M.E.I.C.—Res. eng., P.W.D., Straits Settlements, 1905-13; asst. P.G.E.Rly., Vancouver, Br. Columbia, 1914; lieut., Can. Engrs., 1914; seconded to 1st Can. Pioneers for serv. in France, Oct., 1915; promoted major, July, 1916; ch. engrnr. and 2nd in commd., 9th Can. Rly. Troops, Dec., 1916; asst. dir. constrn. (ag. lt. col.) G.H.Q., France, Sept., 1918; demob., Canada, Apr., 1919; R. of O., desps. 3 times; engrnr.-in-ch., soundings and borings, Vancouver harbour, 1919; asst. ch. engrnr., Gold Coast harbour, 1921; ch. engrnr., 1924; P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

CRAIG, RUPERT BERCHMANS, A.M.Inst.C.E., M.R.San.I.—B. 1897; apprentice, P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1913; served with H.M. Forces, 1915-19; ment. in desps.; ag. asst. engrnr. and ch. draughtsman, Oct., 1921-July, 1922; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jan., 1924; seconded to consultants, Georgetown improvement schemes, June-July, 1923; seconded to harbour, as engrnr.-in-ch., Berbice River training wks., Jan. to Oct., 1925; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., 1926; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., 1929.

CRAIG-MCFEELEY, J. J.—B. 1891; ed. Catholic Univ. Sch. (Dublin), St. Francis Xavier's (Liverpool) and Stonyhurst Colls., Univ. of Liverpool; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Jan., 1923; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914-Dec., 1919; 2nd lieut., Kent Cyclist Batt. (T.F.), Aug., 1915; seconded for duty with M.G.C., Nov., 1916 to Dec., 1919; lieut. (ag. capt.), B.E.F., Mar. to Oct., 1917; asst. dist. offr. and mag., Nigeria (S. Provs.), 1920-21; asst. res. and mag., Nyasaland Prot., Apr., 1923 to July, 1926; dep. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1926; dep. regisr., titles, Apr., 1928; ag. land offr., and regisr., titles in 1928, 1931 and 1933; ag. regisr.-gen., docs., in 1931 and 1933; ag. regisr., co-op socs., July, 1933.

CRAGIE-HALKETT, MONTAGU C., O.B.E. (1934)—Ag. priv. sec. to gov. Falkland Islds., May, 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1897; priv. sec. to admnr., Apr., 1897; clk., col. sec.'s off., Oct., 1897; treas. clk., 1899; J.P., Nov., 1909; postmr., man., savings bank, regisr.-gen., and regisr. of sup. ct., and offr. admnr., 1911; temp. mem., leg. coun., 1911 and 1914; supt., census, 1911 and 1921; local audr., 1916-26; regisr.-gen. and mag., 1926; temp. mem., exec. coun., 1923; treas. and collr., cust., Aug., 1926; has acted as mag. and regisr., sup. ct., and as col. postmr. on various occasions; dep. for gov., Jan. and Dec., 1932 and May, 1933; ag. col. sec., Feb. to Mar., 1932; ag. gov., July, 1933; col. sec., 1934.

CRANSTON, T. D.—M.A. (Glasgow); asst. mast., training instn., for teachers, Accra, G. Coast, Apr., 1912; asst. censor, in addn., Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. qtrmtr., Gold Coast Volunteers, Nov., 1916; lieut., ditto, Apr., 1917; ag. prin., Accra Training Coll. on several occasions between 1917 and 1927; ag. prov. inspr., schls., Apr., 1926; asst. dir. educn., Nov., 1926; ag. dir., educn., in 1930, 1932, 1933 and 1934.

CRAZY, WILLIAM RAYMOND DUNSTAS, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1900; ed. Gresham's Sch., Holt and London Univ.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Apr., 1926; ag. dist. engrnr., in 1930, 1931, 1933 and 1934; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1936.

CRAWFORD, JOHN GERALD.—B. 1894; ed. King's Schl., Chester and Merton Coll., Oxford, M.A., 1920; lieut., Cheshire regt., T.F., Nov., 1915; capt., June, 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; attd. to dist. office, K. Selangor, Apr., 1921; attd. to office of conserv., foresta, Jan., 1922; passed cadet, Feb., 1923; supernumerary offr., cl. V, Feb., 1922; asst. dist. offr., B. Padang, Nov., 1923; ditto, Kroh, May, 1924; offr., cl. IV, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, June, 1927; attd. to co-op. socs. dept., Oct., 1931; offr., cl. III, Nov., 1931.

CRAWFORD, MATTHEW, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1901; asst. vety. surg., Ceylon, 1924; ag. sov't. vety. surg., in 1924 and 1931; attended Imp. Agr. Research Conf., as Ceylon del., 1927; gov't. vety. surg., Nov., 1931.
CRAWSHAW, EDWARD DANIEL WESTON.—B. 1903; ed. St. Bees and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1926; admitted, solr., Mar., 1929; N. Rhodesia, 1930; asst. adminstr. gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1933.

CRAN, B. A.—Barrister-at-law; dep. recvr. of titles, Kenya, Oct., 1920; res. mag., Apr., 1921; puisne judge, sup. ct., Cyprus, Apr., 1930; ag. puisne, dist. ct., June to Sept., 1930; ag. ch. just.; ch. just., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1934.

CREASY, GERALD HALLEN.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby; schol., King's Coll., Camb., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1916; lieut., May, 1918; capt. and adjutant, Nov., 1918; app'td. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., C.O., 26th July, 1920; pvt. sec. to parl. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th Aug., 1925; dep. sec., C.O. coll., 1927; prin. (ag'l.), 17th Sept., 1928.
CREED, HENRY.—B. 1871; app'td., after exam., 3rd class messenger in C.O., July, 1900; 2nd class, 1st Feb., 1904; King's mess., Feb. Jan., 1911; office keeper, 1924; med., Order of Br. Empire, 1931.

CREMER, ARTHUR STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Durham schl.; col. audit dept., 23rd Jan., 1911; asst. audr., G. Coast, 1911-13; asst. audr., Nyasaland, 1913-16; asst. audr., Uganda, 1916-18; asst. audr., provisional admintr., G.E.A., 1919; senr. asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; audr., Mauritius, July, 1924; audr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1933.

CREMER, JOHN AWDRY.—B. 1881; B.A. (Oxon.), 1903; clk., col. audit branch, R. and A.D., 1903; asst. auditor, Lagos, 1903; asst. auditor, Nyasaland, 1908; 2nd cls. cik., cent. office, 1913; auditor, Nyasaland, 1914; audr., Cyprus, Aug., 1925.

CRESSALL, PAUL EWART FRANCIS, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana, 1911; cler. ast., secretariat and auth. inspr., Br. Guiana pol., 1912; ag. dist. inspr., 1914-15; 2nd lieut., Br. Guiana Militia Inf., 1914; served in B.W.I. Regt. as capt., E. Africa and Palestine, 1915-19; dist. inspr., Br. Guiana pol., 1920; J.P. and coroner, 1920; ag. county inspr., 1920; pas'ed final law exam., 1921; cik., Br. Guiana local forces, 1922; ag. staff offr., Br. Guiana local forces, 1922; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1923; county inspr., pol., 1923; commr. for oaths and revising barrister, 1924; stip. mag., Apr., 1924; ag. stip. mag., Georgetown, Aug., 1924 to Dec., 1924 and July to Dec., 1926; chmn., comsn. of enquiry into Onderneeming indus., schl., Sept., 1925; ag. chmn., soldiers' pensions bd., 1925; chmn., ditto, Nov., 1926; senr. stip. mag., Nov., 1930; senr. Br. mag., Palestine, June, 1931; ag. pres., Nablus dist. ct., May, 1933; do., Jerusalem dist. ct., June, 1933; chmn., legal bd., June, 1933; relieving pres., dist. cts., Aug., 1934.

CRESWELL, COL. THE HON. FREDERIC HUGH PAGE, D.S.O.—B. 1866; ed. Bruce Castle, Derby Schl., and R. Schl. of Mines (grad. 1st cls.); arrived in S. Africa, May, 1893; mining in Rhodesia, 1893-94; man., Durban Roodpoort Deep Mine, 1895-99; served in Imp. Light Horse during Anglo-Boer war; man., Village Main Reef, 1902-03; supported agitation for expulsion of Chinese labour; elec. to first Union parl., 1910, and led Labour Party; 2nd in commd., Rand Rifles, S.W. Africa, 1914; 2nd in commd., subsequently commdgs., 8th S.A. Infy. Brig., German E. Africa campaign; mem. for Denver; min. of defence and labour, 1st and 2nd Hertzog min., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; M.P. for Belleville, May, 1933.

CREWE, 1ST MARQUESS OF (cr. 1911); ROBERT OFFLEY ASHBURTON CREWE-MILNES, K.G., P.C., M.A., F.S.A.—B. 1858; Baron Houghton of Great Houghton in the county of York, 1863; Earl of Crewe (cr. 1895); Earl of Madeley (cr. 1911); ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; astt. priv. sec. to sec. for foreign affairs (Earl Granville), 1883-84; lord-in-waiting to Queen Victoria, 1886; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-95; lord pres. of the coun., 1908-08; S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr., 1908; Lord Privy Seal (in conjunction with Secretaryship of State), 1908; S. of S. for India, 1910-15; accompanied H.M. King George V. on the occasion of the Royal visit to India, 1911; lord pres. of the coun., 1915.
CRICHLLOW, NATHANIEL.—M.B., Ch.B.; med. offr., Solomon Islands, Oct., 1914; ag. senr. med. offr. on several occasions; travelling med. offr., July, 1927.

CRITIEN, ATTILIO, O.B.E. (1927), M.B.E. (1919), M.A., M.D., Malta Univ., D.P.H., D.T.M. (Liverpool), 1906—R. 1873; demonstr., schl. of anatomy, 1899 to 1901; civil surgeon attchd. R.A.M.C., 1901 to 1904; med. offr. of hith., Malta, 1906 to 1917; chf. govt. med. offr. and supt. of pub. hth., with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1917-21.

CROCKER, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1890; served in R.A.S.C. (M.T.) in Mesopotamia, Persia and India; astt. govt. printer, Gold Coast, 1921; ag. govt. printer, 1925, 1927, 1928, 1930 and 1932; supt., govt. press, 1928.

CROCKETT, HON. OSWALD SMITH, LL.D.—B. 1868; ed. pub. and high schls., Fredericton, N.B., Canada, and Univ. of New Brunswick; B.A., 1886; attty., sup. ct., N.B., 1891; called to bar, 1892; M.P. (Can.) for York County, N.B., 1904-11; judge, king's bench div., sup. ct., N.B., 1913; judge, sup. ct., Canada, 1932.

CRONEEN, BRIAN.—Ed. Dulwich Coll.; served B.S.A. pol. and Bechuana land Prot. pol., 1911; 2nd grade clk., 1921; ag. sub-inspr., 1922; ag. astt. res. mak., 1926; passed 2nd grade Secwana exam., 1923; ag. res. mag., 1927; 1st grade clk., Apr., 1928; sub-inspr., Bechuana land Prot. pol., 1929.

CROOK, WALTER VERNON.—B. 1900; ed. Marlborough Coll., and R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; command, Reg. Army, East Surrey Regt., Dec., 1918; served N. Russia, 1919; seconded under C.O., Mar., 1926; cadet, Uganda, 1926; astt. dist. offr., Uganda, June, 1928, and placed on Reg. Army, R. of O.

CROSBIE, HON. SIR JOHN C., K.B.E. (1919).—M.H.A., Bay de Verde dist., Newfoundland, 1908; M.E.C., 1909; mem. defence comte., 1915; chmn., tonnage comte., 1917; min. of militia, 1917; min. of shipping, 1918.

CROSBIE, ROBERT EDWARD HAROLD, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1886; ed. Cheltenham and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A. (class. tripes, hon. cls. II), 1908; Egyptian civ. serv., 1910-19; seconded for mily. serv. with E.F.F., 1915; apptd. to O.E.T.A. (S.), 1918; dep. mily. gov., Brian, 1919; dep. mily. gov., Tiberias, 1920; dep. dist. gov., Jenin, 1920; astt. gov. (continuing as astt. dist. comsrr.), S. dist., 1922; passed advanced Arabic exam., 1924; dist. comsrr., Southern dist., 1931.

CROSS, GRAHAM.—Clk., native ct., Johannesburg, May, 1902; astt. res. mag. and astt. mag., native ct., Witwatersrand dist., Aug., 1908; mag., Nov., 1915; under sec. for lands, Sept., 1923; sec. for lands, Aug., 1932; chmn., pub. serv. comsrr., Jan., 1934.

CROSS, MATTHEW ARNOLD.—B. 1881; clk., deeds, Transvaal, Mar., 1902; examr., deeds, July, 1904; 2nd grade examr., deeds, Pretoria, Oct., 1919; 1st gr. ditto, Nov., 1919; ch. examr., July, 1922; astt. regis., deeds, Bloemfontein, Nov., 1927; ditto, Pretoria, Oct., 1928; regis., deeds, Bloemfontein, Apr., 1933; do., Pretoria, Feb., 1934.

CROSSE-CROSSE, WILLIAM WICKHAM REDDER.—B. 1899; ed. Blundells schl.; 2nd lieut., Welsh Guards, 1918; lt., 1920; served with 1st and 2nd K.A.R., in Tanganyika Territory, 1922-24; R. of O., (Welsh Guards), 1924; cadet, Ugan- da Prot., 1924; astt. dist. offr., 1926; ag. lab. comsrr., 1929; temp. astt. prin., C.O., 1930.
CROUCHER, HERBERT HARRY, B.S.C. (Lond.), A.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Reading Univ.; lect. in chem. and soil sci., Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; agrl. chemist, Jamaica, 1931.

CRUICKSHANK, ARTHUR HOWARD, M.I.Mar. E.—B. 1884; jnr. mech. engrn. and river survr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. mech. engrn., 1921; transf. as engrn., transport offr., to motor transport dept., Aug., 1922; ag. ch. mech. transport offr., 1924; ag. ch. transport offr. for various periods, 1926-34.

CRUICKSHANK, JOHN MERRILL, M.D., C.M., McGill Univ. Canada 1925, Licentiate Med. Coun. of Canada, 1925.—Med. dir., Alexander sanitorium (mental diseases) San Francisco, 1927-28; med. offr., Bahamas, 1928; res. surg., 1929; ag. dir., med. services in 1930; ch. med. offr. and res. surg., 1931.

CRUTCHLEY, ERNEST TRISTRAM, C.M.G. (1932). C.B.E. (civ.), 1926, O.B.E. (mily.), 1918.—B. 1878; lieut.-col., R.E., T.F. Res.; astt. survr., G.P.O., 1908; dep. dir., Army Postal serv. (T.F.), 1914-19; staff offr., grade I, miny. of transport, 1919; ag. prin., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin, 1921; Irish office and mem., Irish grants comte., 1922; C.O., 1923; ag. finance offr., overseas settlement dept., 1925; Br. govt. rep. for migr. in the C'wealth. of Australia, 1928; rep. in the C'wealth. of Australia of H.M.'s Govt. in the U.K., 1931.

CRUX, RICHARD MILLETT.—B. 1850; ed. in India; dept. of Transvaal prisons, dir. prisons office, Jan., 1904; astt. warden, Houtpoort Reformatory, Apr., 1916; prison supt., 1923; supt. prisons, Pretoria commd., 1934; war serv., Anglo-Boer War, 1900-02; Great War, G.E.A., 1916-17.

CUBITT, MAJOR-GEN. SIR THOMAS ASTLEY, K.C.B. (1931), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1903)—B. 1871; ed. Haileybury and B.M.C., Woolwich; ent. army, 1891; capt., 1900; served W. Africa, 1898 (med. and clasp); staff offr., W. Africa (despa., brevet-major, med. with clasp) 1901; W. Africa, (despa., clasp), 1902; Kanosokoto campaign, 1903 (despa., clasp, D.S.O.); European war in commd., troops, Somaliland, 1914-15; in commd., divn., France, with rank of major-gen., 1918; G.O.C., Presidency and Assam dist., 1920-24; 53rd Welsh Divn., T.A., Shrewsbury, 1927; G.O.C., 2nd Divn., 1928; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Bermuda, 1931.

CUFF, CYRIL CHARLES HERBERT, M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1888; ed. Guy's and Durham; lieut., R.N.V.R., 1914; passed Bd. of Trade exam. for master, 1916; transf'd to R.A.M.C., 1918; house surg. and bacteriologist, St. Lucia, 1921-23; resigned; re-ent. col. serv. and apptd. to Seychelles as sen. asst. med. offr., res. surg. and visiting mag., outlying islds., 1924; ag. ch. med. offr., June to Nov., 1924; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Apr., 1926; surg. specialist, Dec., 1928; ag. dir., health on various occasions in 1928 and 1932-33; ag. senr. health offr., 1934.
CULLEN, JAMES REYNOLDS, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1901; ed. Tonbridge and Balliol Coll., Oxford; Hertford schol., 1919; 1st hons. mods., 1920; 2nd lit. hum., 1922; dir., educn., Cyprus, Sept., 1930.

CULLEN, HON. SIR WM. PORTUS, K.C.M.G. (1912), KT, BACH. (1911), K.C., M.A., LL.D.—Barrister-at-law; M.L.A., New South Wales, July, 1891; M.L.C., Aug., 1895; fellow of Senate of Sydney Univ. since 1896; vice-chancellor, 1908; resigned from legis. coun. and apptd. chief just. of N.S.W., Jan., 1910; lieut. gov., N.S.W., 30th Mar., 1910; ret., 1925; chancellor of Sydney Univ. from Sept. 1914.

CUMARASWAMY, SUBRAMANIAN DAVIDS.—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; attd., Badulla kach., Feb., 1929; attd. Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1930; office asst. to asst. gov't. asst., Matale, Mar., 1930; attd., Kandy kach., Sept., 1933; office asst., Kandy kach., Feb., 1934.

CUNLIFFE-LISTER, Rt. Hon. Sir Philip, G.B.E. (1929), K.B.E. (1920), P.C. (1922), M.C., M.P. (Hendon).—B.1884; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; called to bar, 1908; served in army, 1914-17; joint sec. of Miny. of National Serv., 1917-18; chmn., perm. lab. comttee. of War Cabinet war priorities comttee, 1918; mem. select comttee. on nat. expend. and select comttee. on high prices and profits, 1919; parly. sec. to B. of T., 1920-21; sec., D.O.T., 1921-22; chmn., Imp. Economic Conf., 1923; pres., B. of T., 1922-23, 1924-29 and Aug.-Nov., 1931; S. of the Cola., 9th Nov., 1931.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STRYPHEN, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; advoc., high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; counsel for Imp. govt. in arbitn. with late S.A.R. re Asiatic question, 1895; ag. judge, high ct. (late S.A.R.), Apr., 1899 to Jan., 1900; pres. crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, Nov., 1899 to Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, Sept., 1903; puisne judge, sup. ct., S. Africa (Transvaal Prov. Div.), May, 1910; judge pres., July, 1924; judge of appeal, 1927; O.A.G., Union of S. Africa during absence of Lord Clarendon, 1933.

CURMI, CAPT. HENRY C.—B. 1890; ed. Malta Lyceum, Univ. and pte., tuition; matric. (Italian, French and Spanish), 1904; 1st place Malta civ. serv. exam., 1908; served in cust. lieut. gov't., treas., educn. and charitable instn. depts.; 2nd lieut., K.O. Malta Regt., 1910; mobilised 1914 and transf'd. to R. Malta Art., 1915; with Med. Expedy. Force at Mudros and Anzac from Sept., 1915 to evacuation; mem. in desps., Egyptian Expedy. Force, 1916-19; demob., Apr., 1920; served shipping office, cust. sec., charitable institutions comttee., 1920; sec. to min. for posts and agr. and in charge of lab. bureau, 1921; sec., emigrn. comttee., 1922; sec. and offr.-in-charge, tourist comttee., 1923-24; comntr. for lab., Apr., 1928; comntr. for Malta in Australia, Jan., 1929.

CURRY, H. D.—B. 1893; ed. Dulwich coll.; served in Fiji, 1911-15; ag. 2nd cls. clk., C.S.O., 1915; cadet, Solomon Islands, 1915; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, 1915; on mily. serv., 1917-19; 2nd lieut., 1919; priv. sec. to gov. of B. Honduras, 1919; resumed duty Solomon Is., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, 1920; ag. sec., 1920; admn. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923; seconded as lab. offr., 1926; offr. in ch., lab. affrs., N. prov., 1933.

CURRY, WILLIAM CLIVE, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. City of London Schl.; mily. serv., 1916-18; M.C., 1917; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1922; ast. land offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927; ast. admnstr.-gen., 1928; Br. mag., Palestine, 1932.

CURTIS, RICHARD JOHN FROUDE.—B. 1867; ed. Blundell's Schl. and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1917; lieut., R.O. of O., 1920; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; offr., cl. V, Nov., 1923; dist. offr., N. Telah, Dec., 1925; offr., cl. IV, Nov., 1927; Br. res., Brunel, Dec., 1928; res., Labuan, Aug., 1929; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1931; A.D.C. to gov., June, 1931; offr., cl. III, Nov., 1932; priv. sec. to gov., Dec., 1932; 2nd mag., Spore, Aug., 1933; 3rd mag., Sept., 1933.

CUSACK, CAPT. HENRY VERNON (Sec. of Offrs.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1895; ed. Aravon Schl. and Royal Coll. of Sci., Ireland; served in European War, Flanders and N. Russia, 1915-19; ast. audr., Sierra Leone, July, 1920; ag. audr., Oct.-Nov., 1921; ast. audr., Nigeria, Oct., 1922; senr. ast. audr., Nyasaland, May, 1928; ag. audr., Oct., 1928 to Oct., 1929 and Apr.-Nov., 1932; ast. dir., col. audit dept., July, 1933.
CUSACK, JOHN RALPH.—B. 1902; ed. Royal Schl., Armagh; apptd. to col. audit dept., as ast. audr., Nigeria, June, 1929.

CUSSEN, RICHARD CECIL.—B. 1897; R.A., Dublin; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Jebelu, Oct., 1912; passed cadet, June, 1914; offr. cl. V, Dec., 1914; ast. dist. offr., B. Padang, Feb., 1915; offr. cl. IV, Jan., 1919; max., Seremban, July, 1920; offr., cl. II, D.P.P., Perak, Nov., 1923; offr. cl. II, Nov., 1928; dep. legal advr., F.M.S., offr., cl. IB, Apr., 1929; ag. legal advr., F.M.S., July, 1934.

CUTHBERT, MORTON.—Ed. Edin. Insttn. and Glasgow and West of Scot. Tech. Coll.; apprent. engr. to Messrs. Dick Kerr and Co., Ltd., London, 1895; electrn. with Glasgow corptn. elec. dept., 1899; ast. engr., Govan corptn. elec. dept., 1901; chief ast. engr., Oban corptn. elec. dept., 1904; electrn. engr., B. Honduras, 1905; elec. and electrn. engr., P.W.D., B. Honduras, 1912; lieut., B.H.T.F., 1917; ag. adjtnt., B.H.T.F., Aug., 1917 to Jan., 1918; hon. A.D.C. to gov., B. Honduras, 1919.

CUTTERIDGE, JOSEPH OLIVER, M.B.E. (1930), A.C.P., F.R.G.S.—B. 1887; ed. Borough Road Coll., Isleworth, London, 1906-08; teacher's certif. (with 4 distinctions) Bd. of Educ., Lond.; A.C.T. cert., Bd. of Educ., Lond.; staff-capt., H.M. Forces; inspr. Q.M.G. services; math. and drawing master, Junr. Tech. Schl., Liverpool, 1919; prin., govt. training coll., Trinidad, Jan., 1921; mem., Inter-Col. Educn. Confce, 1921; mem. bd. indust. training; supt., tech. classes, 1922; ag. senr. inspr. schls., Feb., 1923; senr. inspr. schls., Trinidad, 1st July, 1923; ag. dir., educn., July, 1925 to Mar., 1926; asst. dir., educn. and senr. inspr., schls., June, 1926; asst. dir., educn. and ch. inspr., schls., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., educn., May to Nov., 1929; dir., educn., 1934; author "West Indian Readers" etc.

CUZEN, ALLAN LECKIE.—Served in R.E., 1901-2; S. African War (medal and five clasps); Cape police, 1902-6; Bech. Prot. police, 1906-14; passed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam., 1914; 2nd clk. to ast. commnr., N. Dist., Bech. Prot., 1914; ag. ast. res. mag., ditto, 1915; passed 2nd grade Seawana exam., 1916; 1st grade (B), ditto, 1927; ag. res. mag., 1917; ag. ast. res. mag., 1919; res. mag., 1921.

DABNER, CHARLES J.—B. 1876; on mily. serv., France and Flanders, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; clk., Army pay office, R. Engnr., Mar. to Oct., 1919; clk., Army pay office, command paymr., Overseas Bases, Oct., 1919 to July, 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 10th July, 1922.

DAKERS, COLIN HUGH, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Manch. Gram. Schl.; war serv., 1915-19; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1923; inspr., liquor shops, F.M.S., Sept., 1925; 2nd ast. dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S., Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1927; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1932; ast. dir., educn., Chinese schls., F.M.S., Dec., 1932; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Apr., 1933; do., Penang, Aug., 1934.

DALLARD, BERKELEY LIONEL.—B. 1889; ed. Rangiora High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. gov't. serv., 1907; stamp duties dept., 1907-13; audit office, 1913-18; advisory acctnt., bd. of trade, 1919-23; inspr., pub. serv. comsrr.'s office, 1924-26; contr.-gen., prisons, 1926; also ast. pub. serv. comsrr., 1929; and under see., justice and regist.-gen., births, deaths, marriages, 1933.

DALLIN, THOMAS.—Late lieut. 3rd Batt. Essex Regt.; served in S. African War (medal and two clasps); hon. 2nd lieut. in the army, Oct., 1902; joined col. audit dept., Feb., 1904; examr., exchequer and audit dept., July, 1906; ast. audr., S. Nigeria, Oct., 1906 to Feb., 1910; reverted to ex. and audit dept., Feb., 1910; ast. audr., Hong Kong, July, 1910; ag. audr. in 1911, 1912, 1913 and 1914; ast. cable censor, Aug.-Oct., 1914; on mil. serv., Mar., 1915 to July, 1916; ag. audr., Aug., 1916 to Dec., 1917; J.P., 1918; attd. to finan. branch, W.O., Sept., 1918 to Mar., 1919; ag. audr. on various occasions in 1920, 1922 and 1924-30.
DALTON, ALFRED, M.A.Inst.T.—B. 1892; G.S. and W.R., Ireland, 1909; ast. traffic supt., Tanganyika rly., Nov., 1929; traffic supt., 1930.

DALTON, LLEWELYN CHISHOLM.—B. 1879; ed. Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1900 (Hist. Tripos); M.A., 1905; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1901; legal asts., land settm. bd., O.R.C., Oct., 1901; ast. res. mag. and J.P., June, 1902 to Nov., 1910; advoc., sup. ct., 1904; registr., B. Guiana, Nov., 1910; judge advoc., local forces, 1914; ag. puisne judge, Nov., 1912 to Feb., 1913; Nov., 1914 to Jan., 1915; July, 1916, to June, 1917; July to Sept., 1918; Mar. to June, 1919; ag. solr.-gen., May to Aug., 1914; Sept., 1914 to Jan., 1915; ag. atty.-gen., Aug., Sept., 1914; Feb.-Apl., 1918; ag. chief just., July-Sep., 1917: June-Oct., 1919; puisne judge, B. Guiana, June, 1919; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Feb., 1923; puisne judge, Ceylon, Mar., 1925; compiled and edited "Statutory Rules and Orders (B. Guiana)," to 1914, 2 vols.; editor, B. Guiana Law Reports, 9 vols.; an ast. editor "Burge on Colonial and Foreign Laws," 2nd edit., vol. iv.; compiled Digest of Case Law of British Guiana, 1910-1920; author of "Civil Law of British Guiana."

D'ANDRADE, WILLIAM ALBERT.—B. 1884; cler. ast., surg. genl.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1903; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1903; 5th ditto, 1906; 4th ditto, 1913; 3rd ditto, 1921; 2nd ditto, 1925; ob. clk., cust., 1925; compt'r., cust., 1927.

DANEEL, JOSIAS, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1884; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Edin. Univ.; admitted F.R.C.S., Edin., 1922; med. supt., Rietfontein hosp., Union of S. Africa, 1927.

DANIEL, FRANCIS DE FOREST.—B.1886; ed. Marlborough Coll.; admitted solr., 1910; ast. acctnt., Lagos rly., July, 1912; transf., cust. dept., May, 1913; ast. dist. offr., N. Prov., Nigeria, July, 1914; res., Mar., 1930.

DANIEL, REGINALD YOUNG, M.A. (Oxon).—B.1894; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Galle kach., Oct., 1920; ag. office ast. to ast. gov't. agt., Matara, Sept., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Jan., 1922; ag. office ast. to ast. gov't. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; ditto to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1922; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1925; pol. mag., Kalutara, Dec., 1926; pol. mag., Kandy, Apr., 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1930; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1930; do., Ratnapura, Oct., 1931; ast. gov't. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1933.
DANNATT, ROBERT MALCOLM, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—B. 1891; med. offr., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; surgn., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; do., Selangor, Nov., 1933.

DARLING, JOHN SINGLETON.—B. 1898; ed. Marlborough Coll.; served in Indian Army, 1917-22; war serv., Palestine, 1918; Waziristan, 1921; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1929; ast. dist. offr., Aug., 1931.

DARRELL, ARTHUR LLEWELLYN.—B. 1883; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ast. clk. to regsr., St. Vincent, 1900; 3rd clk. treasy., 1901; 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is. (Grenada), 1909; pvt. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., Windward Is., 1913; treas. ast., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; treas. ast., 2nd grade, 1919; senr. ast. treas., 1921; ag. treas. in 1928 and in 1933; dep. treas., 1934.

DASH, PROF. JOHN SYDNEY, B.Sc. (Agr.) McGill Univ.—B. 1887; various posts, dept. of agr., Barbados, 1905-09; plant pathologist, Br. Columbia, 1913; ast. supt., agr., Barbados, 1914-18; dir., station agronomique, Guadeloupe, F.W.I., 1918-21; tobacco specialist and botanist, seed br., dept. of agr., Canada, 1921-22; prof. of agr., Impl. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1922-27; dir. agr., Br. Guiana, 1927; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1928; rep. col. at various col. agrl. confcees., 1930-33.
DAVENPORT, CECIL THORNHILL, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Malvern and Oriel Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. comsrr., Kenya, Jan., 1920.

DAVIDSON, EDWARD ERNEST.—B. 1878; extra clk., C.S.O., Ceylon, July, 1898; inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1900; office ast. to dir. of public instruction, Jan., 1906; ag. inspr. of schls., Apr., 1911, and May, 1912; apptd. to ols. V. of civ. serv., Jan., 1913; ag. ast. dir. of educn., Apr., 1920; addnl. ast. dir. of educn., Oct., 1920; office ast. to dir. educn., Nov., 1922.

DAVIDSON, GERALD LARCOM DEAN.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Sept., 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1921; attd. Badulla kach., Jan., 1922; office ast., Ratnapura kach., Oct., 1924; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Oct., 1926; pol. mag. Gampola, Sept., 1927; ast. dir. statistics, Jan., 1929; ast. commr. lands, Oct., 1931.

DAVIDSON, WALKER WHEATLEY, M.C., M.A.(Cantab), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I. Mech. E.—B. 1886; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; exec. engrnr., July, 1923; senr. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., S.S. and F.M.S., 1934.

DAVIES, D.E.—B. 1889; ed. at Llandovery and Worcester Coll., Oxon.; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ast. col. sec., May-July, 1915; ast. dist. commr., Adidah, July, 1915 to May, 1916; ast. dist. commr., Accra, Sep., 1916 to Sep., 1917.

DAVIES, DAVID, A.R. San. I.—B. 1887; sany. dept., Hong Kong, 1914; seconded for mily. serv., 1917-20; storekeeper, sany. dept., 1931; 1st. clk. and ast. sec., sany. bd., 1923; sec., sany. bd., 1927; ch. clk., senr. cler. and astg. staff, col. secretariat, 1928; J.P., 1930.
DAVIES, EBENEZER DYER.—Clk., educn. dept., Natal, 1902; senr. clk., 1913; prin. clk., 1919; sec., educn. dept., Natal, 1922; ch. clk., admstr.'s dept., Natal, June, 1932.

DAVIES, EDWARD HAROLD.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Kandy kach., Nov., 1920; ag. pol. mag. Matara, Jan., 1922; ditto Panadura, May, 1922; office ast. to gov't. agt., Prov. of Uva, Oct., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Feb., 1924; ast. dir. statistics, Mar., 1924; dep. fiscal, Colombo in admn., May, 1924; off. ast., Kandy kach., Oct., 1925; ast. dir. statistics, Feb., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; ast. gov't. agt., Colombo, May, 1928; ast. land commr., July, 1931; sec., Colombo port commr., Nov., 1931.

DAVIES, EDWARD JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Llandovery Coll. and Univ. of Wales, B.A., LL.B. (Wales); served with Artists' Rifles and West Yorks. Regt., 1916-18; France, 1917-18; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1922; crown coun., Kenya, May, 1927; legal mem. of land bank bd., Dec., 1931-July, 1932; crown coun., Gold Coast, Feb., 1933; ag. solr.-genl. in 1933 and 1934.

DAVIES, GEORGE FREDERICK, M.B.E., M.I.E.E.—B. 1889; imp. serv., 1908-13; on mily. serv. (R.E.) 1914-19; seconded to Kenya, 1913-19; ch. tel. engrnr., Tanganyika, 1919; divnl. engrnr., Nigeria, 1925; ast. engrnr. in ch., 1930; engrnr. in-ch., posts and tels. dept., 1933.

DAVIES, HENRY NOEL, O.B.E. (1933), M.Inst.T.—B. 1881; ast. audr., S. Indian rly., 1900-17; major, Rly. Corps, German E. Africa, 1917-19; twice ment. in desps.; ch. acct., Tanganyika rly., Apr., 1919; ag. gen. man. for various periods, 1928-32; dep. gen. man., July, 1930.

DAVIES, RICHARD MORGAN.—B. 1892; on mily. serv., Dec., 1914; cadet, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; attd. to Puttalam Kacheheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Colombo Kach., June, 1921; Colombo Port Coman., Oct., 1921; ag. office ast. to ast. gov't. ast., Kegalla, Jan., 1922; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1923; dep. rubber contr., May, 1924; ast. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1926; ast. sttlmt. offr., Dec., 1926; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Nov., 1928; ast. govt. agt., Manar, May, 1930; pol. mag., Matale, Nov., 1931; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Mar., 1932; ast. sttlmt. offr., July, 1932.

DAVIES, RUPERT MEREDITH, B.Sc., B.Sc. (Agr.).—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl.; mil. serv., Lon. Scot. & R.E.s, 1917-19; Aberdeen Univ., 1919-24; Cambridge Univ., 1924, post grad.; asst. agr., Nigeria, 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1930.

DAVIES, THOMAS, WALTON.—B. 1907; ed. Tonbridge and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd cl. mod. hist., 1929; apptd. after compet. exam. ass't prin., Miny. of Transport, 1930; transfd. to C.O., 1st Oct., 1931; called to bar, Inner Temp., Nov., 1934.

DAVIES, TUDOR THOMAS.—B. 1887; ast. govt. printer, Nyasaland Prot., 1916; ag. gr. printer and compt. stationery, May to July, 1917; July, 1919 to Mar., 1920 and Apr. to Dec., 1925; govt. printer, Dec., 1923.

DAVIES, WILLIAM ALFORD NOEL.—B. 1883; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl.; admitted solr. of sup. ct., England, Apr., 1906; private practice in England, 1906-1907; private practice in E. Africa, 1907-1908; ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Dec., 1908; passed lower standard Merdi exam., June, 1911; ast. dist. commr., Dec., 1911, to June, 1912; ag. pol. mag., June to Dec., 1912; acted as crown prosecutor on several occasions; ast. dist. commr., 1913-14; master and regisr., sup. ct., S. Leone, July, 1914; curator of intestate estates, Oct., 1914; regisr., pr. court, 1914; 2nd lieut., general list, Mar., 1916; mentioned in desps., 1916; attached S. Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., May, 1916; served in S. Leone, 1916-17; transfd. to G. Coast Regt., 1917; served in Port. E. Africa, 1918; staff capt. with Col. Rose's column, Apr. to June, 1918; staff quartermr., 2nd W. African Service Brz., Oct., 1918; demob., Dec., 1918; resumed duties as regisr., sup. ct., Sierra Leone, May, 1919; regisr., sup. ct., S.S., Nov., 1920.

DAVIS, SIR CHARLES THOMAS, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G.(1923), C.M.G.(1915).—B. 1873; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and Oxford; classical scholar, Balliol Coll., 1892-6; lat. class classemoda, 1894; Jenkyns exhib., 1896; 1st clas hum., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 1st cl. clk., acct.gen.'s dept., admiralty, Oct. 5th, 1896; transfd. to sec.'s dept., inland rev., Mar., 1897; 2nd cl. clk., col. office, Apr. 15th, 1897; went to Gibraltar as sec. to Ht. Hon. Sir C. R. Thompson on a mission to inquire into the currency question, May and June, 1898; sec. Pacif. cable comtece., 1900; priv. sec. to Sir M. Ommannay, June, 1902; 1st cls.clk., 1st Apr., 1905; principal clk., 4th June, 1916; sec. ast. sec. to Imperial Conference; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; ast. under-sec. of state, 1st Dec., 1921; perm. under-sec. of state for dominion affrs., 1st July, 1925; ret., 1930.

DAVIS, RICHARD PRITCHARD, M.I.M. & C.F., M.T.P.I., M.R.S.I.—B. 1888; town planning supt., Selangor, F.M.S., Dec., 1928; town planner, Selangor, July, 1932; do., Perak, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Oct., 1933.

DAVIS, SIR STRUART SPENCER, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G.(1919)—B. 1875; rev.offr. St. Kitts, 1893; 1st clk., treasy. and cust., 1898; lieut. St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1898; ag. adjutant, 1899; att.col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., July.

DAWSON, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); 2nd lieut., 5th Batt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; served India (N. W. Frontier), Mesopotamia; demobilised with rank of capt., 1919; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; 3rd mag., S'pore, July, 1920; 2nd mag. S'pore, Oct., 1922; 2nd asst. advr., B. Pahat, Nov., 1923; 1st mag. and regt., sup. ct. Johore, Mar., 1924; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Feb., 1925; offr., cls IV, Mar., 1927; called to bar (Grays Inn), June, 1929; dist. judge, Malacca, Sept., 1929; 2nd asst. sec., S.S., Jan., 1930; 1st asst. sec., S.S., Mar., 1932; ag. legal advr., Kedah, July, 1933.
DAWSON, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1886; B.A. (engg.) Cambridge with 2nd cl. hons., A.M.I.C.E.; Canadian N. rly., 1910; asst. engnr. Tanganyika rly., Jan., 1921; dist. engnr., Nov., 1928.
DAY, SIR ALBERT CECIL, KT. BACH. (1932), C.M.G. (1925), C.B.E. (1919).—Asst. priv. sec. to gov. of New Zealand, 1910-1912; offl. sec. to gov.-gen. since 1913.

DAY, EDWARD VICTOR GRACE.—B. 1896; ed. Christ's Coll., Christchurch and Timaru High Schl., New Zealand; cadet, S.S., Sept., 1921; attd., col. sec. office, Nov., 1921; asst. sec., Malaya Borneo Exhibn., Dec., 1921; A.D.C. to O.A.G., June, 1922; sec., many bd., A. Star in addn., Feb., 1923; offr., cls V, Apr., 1924; offr., cls IV, Apr., 1928; offr., cls III, Apr., 1933; ag. 1st asst. sec., S.S., Nov., 1933.

DEAKIN, CHARLES SIDNEY, B.A.—B. 1897; ed. at Orme Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne, Christ's Hosp. and Queen's Coll., Camb., schol., Hughes Eng. essay prize, 1st cl. mechan. tripes, engnr., 1923; research grant, bridge oscillations, 1923-24; junr. engnr dept., science and indust. research, bridge stress coms., 1924-25; John Wimbolt prize, engnr. research, 1925; R.A.S.C. (M.T.), 1916; Iraq, 1917; attd., polit. dept. as Q.M.S., Native Militia and hd. elk., Zubair, 1919; demob., 1920; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

DEAN, H. E.—S. African constab., 1902-0; asst. fencing dept., E.A.P., May, 1908; transport offr., Nairobi, 1908-11; office supt., P.W.D., B.E.A., 1911-16; acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1916-21; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1921-25; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1925; treas., Dominica, 1932.

DEANE, SIR GEORGE CAMPBELL, KT. BACH. (1930).—B. 1873; ed. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; M.A., Oxon (honours in jurisprudence); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1898; practised at the bar, B. Guiana, 1898-1903; ditto, Trinidad, 1903-1910; stip. mag., Arima, Trinidad, Sept., 1910; ag. S.J.P., Port of Spain, Trinidad, May to Dec., 1911, and from May to Oct., 1912; stip. mag., Port of Spain, 1913; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions, 1914-17; twice apptd. comanr. under ordce, 1919; capt. and adjutant, Trinidad Light Horse; 2nd puisne Judge Apr., 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Jan., 1923; puisne judge, S. Sttlments. and judl. comanr., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1929; pres., W.A. ct. of appl., 1930.

DEANE, LIRUT.—COL. ROBERT, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1879; S.A.War, 1900-02; S.A. Constab., 1901; Transvaal pol., 1908; S.A. Permit Force (Staff), 1912; German S.W.A. (staff capt. and brig. machine gun offr.), 1914; W.F.F. Egypt (brig. machine gun offr.), 1st S.A. Inf. Brigade, 1915; France, 1916; ch. of pol., St. Lucia, 1919; dep. inspr. gen. pol., Mauritius, 1922; inspr.-gen. pol. and supt., prisons, 1926; M.I.C., 1926.

De BOER, Henry Spelderwindt, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.).—B.1889; ed. privately and at London Hosp.; served with R.A.M.C., 1915-20 (Gallipoli, Sinai, Palestine and Syria, ment. in desps.); med. offr., Kenya, May, 1920; senr. health offr., E. African med. serv., Jan., 1926; dep. dir., san. services, N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1931; do., med. services, 1933; dep. dir., med. services, Uganda, 1933.

DE CARTERET, Valentine Edward Ross.—B. 1883; ast. commr., pol., Gold Coast, May, 1918; commr., Jan., 1922; col. pol. offrs' course, R.I.C., 1915, and senr. pol. offrs' course at New Scotland Yard, 1927; senr. commr., pol., 1929.

DE CHAIR, VICE-ADMIRAL Sir Dudley Rawson Stratford, K.C.B. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1933), M.V.O. (1908).—B. 1864; mid. of the "Alexandra," at bombardment of Alexandria, 1882; landed with naval brig., naval attache to courts of the Maritime Powers, 1902; and to N. and S. America, 1903-4; A.D.C. to the King, 1911-12; naval sec. to 1st lord of the adm'y., 1912-14; adml. of training service, 1914; comdg. tenth cruiser squadron, 1914-16; naval adviser to F.O., 1916; vice-adml. comdg. battle squadron, 1917-18; vice-adml., 1917; adml. comdg. coast guard and reserve, 1919; gov., New S. Wales, Oct., 1923; ret., 1930.

DECK, S. F.—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.F., 23rd Aug., 1907; dist. commr., May, 1913; offr. in charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1914-15; prin. inspr. of labour, Dec., 1920; supt., native lab., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Oct., 1925; ag. senr. commr., 1928; 1st grade prov. commr., 1931.

DE COMARMOND, Henri Maximilien.—B.1899; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1922; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for Mauritius and Rodrigues, Aug., 1925; ag. addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., on various occasions, 1925-28; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Jan., 1927; 2nd addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Jan., 1928; subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Dec., 1932; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., May, 1933; mem., exec. coun. and coun. of govt.

DE FREITAS, Sir Anthony, Kt. Bach. (1927), O.B.E. (1918), B.A. (Clare Coll., Camb.).—B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. chief just., St. Vincent, May-Oct., 1904; stip. mag., 1st Dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904; ag. atty.-gen., St. Lucia, July, 1906; atty.-gen., St. Lucia-Apl., 1907; chief just., St. Vincent, May, 1915; chief just., St. Lucia, Sept., 1919; ag. adminstr.; St. Vincent, 1909, 1915, and Mar., 1917, to Dec., 1918; ag. col. sec., St. Lucia, on seven occasions; ag. admin-str., St. Lucia, on 15 occasions, including July, 1914, to May, 1915; puisne judge, Jamaica, Aug., 1921; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, July, 1922 to Sent., 1923 and Jan., 1925 to Feb., 1926; ch. just., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1927; ret., 1934.

DE GLANVILLE, R. J. A. P. G.—B. 1885; called to the bar, Bahamas, 1914; notary pub., 1914; priv. sec. to H. E. Wm. Hart Bennett, Esq., C.M.G., admistr. of Bahamas, 1909; also to H. E. Sir W. Grey-Wilson, K.C.M.G., gov., 1912; ag. coroner for New Providence, 1911 and 1913; confirmed, 1914; 2nd clk., H. of A., 1912; chief clk., 1913; ag. regisr., sup. ct. from 1913; ag. stip. and circuit mag. and coroner on various occasions, 1921-31; ag. atty.-gen., advoc. gen. and admv. advoc., contr., local clearing office and temp. mem., exec. coun. on various occasions in 1923, 1924 and 1925; regisr.-gen., Apr., 1923; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1922-32.

DE HAVILLAND, Thomas Littleton, C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1874; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and R. Naval Schl., London; Bechuanaland campaign, 1897; Anglo-Boer war, 1894-1902; France and Belgium, 1915-19; serjeant-at-arms, H. of A., Union of S. Africa.

DE HEVENINGHAM, N. W. P.—Ed. King Coll., London and Worcester Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple); ent. home civ. serv. (open compet.), 1901; served H.O.L.G. Bd. army of munitions; practised at bar, 1918-24; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is.; mem., advisory coun. and judg. comm. for W. Pacific, 1924; dep. regist., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1928; ag. regist., May-Dec., 1931; res. mag., 1932.
DE JAGER, Samuel Jacobus, K.C., B.A., LL.B., Camb.—B. 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1902; admitted advocate, O.R.C., 1903; atty.-gen., O.F.S., 1911; ditto, Cape Prov., 1926.

DE KOCK, Gilles Van De Wall.—B. 1889; ed. Stellenbosch, Pretoria, Capetown and London; M.R.C.V.S., Lond. (1913); vety. research offr., Ondersteepoort, 1914; prof. of anatomy, Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1920; ar. vety. research offr., Ondersteepoort, 1920; sub-dir., vety. services, Ondersteepoort, 1927; D.Sc., Witwatersrand Univ., 1928; dep. dir., divn. of vety. services and animal husbandry, 1931.

DE KOCK, Michiel Hendrik, B.A. (Cape), M.A. and Ph. D. (Harvard).—B. 1878; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Harvard Univ., U.S.A.; sen. lecturer, economics, Univ. of Cape Town, 1923-24; mem., bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924-29; mem., diamond control advisory comm., 1930; S. African Reserve Bank, 1931; dep. gov., S. African Res. Bank, 1932; author of "Finance of the Union of South Africa," "Government ownership in South Africa," and "Economic History of South Africa."

DE LA HARPE, Peter Henry.—B. 1877; apptd. to cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1921; extra offr., asts., Galle Kach., June, 1923; ditto to col. sec., Aug., 1928; office asts. to col. sec., Sep., 1929.

DENHAM, Sir Edward Brandis, G.C.M.G. (1935), K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1927), C.M.G. (1922).—B. 1876; ed. at Malvern and Methe Coll., Oxford (exhibtn.) B.A., 1899; cadet, Col. Nov., 1899; ast. gov't. ast., Mannar, Feb., 1902; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., May, 1904, Mar., 1905; ast. gov't. ast. N. Prov. for pearl fishery work, Mar., 1905; 2nd ast. col. sec. and sec. agric. bd., May, 1905; priv. sec. to Sir A. Askmore, O.A.G., Sept., 1905; organising sec., agric. bd., Dec., 1905; landing surrv., cust., Colombo, June, 1906; dist. judge, Negombo, Sept., 1906; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1909; and priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1909; seconded as astp. of census, Aug., 1910; priv. sec. to Sir H. Clifford, O.A.G., June, 1911; attached to Col., 1st Jan. to 13th Oct., 1913; prin. ast. col. sec., Ceylon, 26th Apr., 1914; chief censor, in addition to his own duties, Aug., 1914; capt., administrative section, town guard, Colombo, 1915; ag. ast. agt., E. Prov., Sept., 1915; offr. of cls. I., grade II. on appt. as dir. of educn., Aug., 1916; dir. food production, Mar.-Dec., 1920; col. sec., Mauritius, 14th Dec., 1920; admstl. gov't. in 1921, 1922 and 1923; col. sec., Kenya Col. and Prot., Aug., 1923; admstl. gov't., Feb. to Oct., 1925 and from Jan. to Aug., 1927; gov., Gambia, 1928; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1930; capt.-gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1934; joint author of "Rubber in the East."

DENMAN, LORD.—3rd Baron (cr. 1834), Rt. Hon. Sir Thomas Denman, P.C. (1907); G.C.M.G. (1911): K.C.V.O. (1909); b. 1874; educ. at Sandhurst; lieut., reserve of offrs.; late lieut., Royal Scots, and major, Middlesex I.Y.; served in S. Africa war (wounded, Queen's medal, 3 clasps); lord-in-waiting in ordinary, 1905-7; capt. Lon. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1907-11; J.P., Sussex; gov. gen. and commr.-in-chief, Commonwealth of Australia, 1911-1914.

DENNIS, OWEN GREGORY.—B. 1894; ent. Army, King's Own Scottish Borderers, 1915; war serv. in Egypt and France, 1915-18; seconded serv., W.A.F.F., 1919; asst. commr., pol., Nigeria, 1922; resgd. Army commn., 1923; invalided from col. serv., Nov., 1923; re-apptd. as local commdt., pol. and asst. commr., Cyprus, Mar., 1928; commr. and local commdt., pol., Dec., 1929.

DE NOBRIGA, John.—B. 1884; 3rd clk., P.W.D., Trinidad, Apr., 1901; asst. clk., water and sewerage dept., Aug., 1903; storekeeper and clk., engnr.'s office, Apr., 1903; clk., mines dept., Sept., 1908; ch. clk., ditto, Dec., 1910; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1921; ch. clk. and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1925; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1930; ag. 1st asst. col. sec., Feb., 1931; ag. dep. col. sec., May-Aug., 1934.

DE NORMAN, ALBERT WILFRED NOEL.—B. 1891; ed. United Services Coll. and Chateau du Rosey, Switzerland; Canadian Expedy. Force, 1914; lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1915; West African Forces, 1916-20; survr., Nigeria survey, 1921; asst. survr.-gen., 1930; ag. survr.-gen., Apr., Sept., 1933.

DE PASS, HAROLD S.—B. 1881; sub.-inspr. of constab., Trinidad, July, 1904; inspr., Apr., 1912; has acted as senr. inspr. and detective inspr. on many occasions; ag. deputy inspr. gen. of constab. and supt. of fire brigade, 1918; senr. inspr., constab., June, 1920.

DE PINTO, CYRIL ERNEST.—B. 1892; cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; ag. office asst., Matara Kachcheri, July, 1915; ditto, Kalutara Kachcheri, Nov., 1915; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Apr., 1916; office asst., Batticaloa Kach., Nov., 1917; pol. mag., Arisawella, Nov., 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1925; ditto, Galle, Mar., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, June, 1933; do., Matara, May, 1934.

DE ROME, F. J., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.B.E.—B. 1886; B.Sc. Lond. Univ., 1908; Jena Univ. (Germany), 1908-1909; asst. mast., Queen's Coll. Hong Kong, 1910; asst. postal censor, 1914-19; asst. mast., cls. II., 1919; asst. mast., cls. I., 1920; ag. headmr., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1923; ag. headmr., Wansatl schl., 1923; headmr., Ellis Kadoorie schl., 1925; headmr., Queen's Coll., Mar., 1930.
DERRIMAN, FRANCIS PHILIP LOUIS.—B. 1901; ed. Ratcliffe Coll.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1924; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1925.
DE SANTOS, ERROL LIONEL.—B. 1890; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., Apr., 1912; asst. treas., Jan., 1929; treas. and chinm., agrl. bank, 1933.

DE SILVA, JOSEPH MARTIN.—B. 1880; apptd. to cls. V, Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1926; extra off. ast., Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1928; ditto, Galle kach., Oct., 1929; ag. office asst., Colombo kach., Feb., 1932.

DE SILVA, LUCIAN MACULL DOMINIC, B.A. (Cantab), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn.—B. 1893; commr., requests, Colombo, 1925; dep. solr.-gen., Ceylon, Apr., 1929; ag. atty.-gen. in 1930 and 1931; solr.-gen., 1932.

DE SILVA, WALWIN ARNOLD, B.Soc.(Lond.)—B. 1905; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Kegalle kach., Jan. and Sept., 1928; attd., Jaffna kach., June, 1928; attd., Kegalle kach., Sept., 1928; attd., Matara kach., Nov., 1928; office asst., Galle kach., Oct., 1932.

DE SOYZA, GUNASENA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1926; attd., Matara kach., Sept., 1926; attd. to Jaffna kach., June, 1927; office asst., Kegalle kach., Mar., 1928; 2nd landing survr., cust., Nov., 1928; office asst., Badulla kach., Oct., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Badulla, Aug., 1932; do., Mullaitivu, June, 1933.

DESPICHT, STANLEY MONK.—B. 1890; ed. Boston Gram. Schl.; passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi; war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. col. sec., 1919; dist. commr., July, 1923.
DES ROCHERS, FELIX.—B. 1886; ed. St. Hyacinthe Seminary, Quebec; B.A. (1905); LL.L. (1908); called to bar, 1908; ch. librnr., iby., Montreal, 1930; gen. librnr., liby. of parlmt., Canada, 1933.

DE VAUX, JUSTIN LOUIS.—B. 1884; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1906 (cert. of hon.); revised laws of St. Lucia, 1916; ag. mag., 1st dist., St. Lucia, Sept., 1917 to Sept., 1918; asst. legal adviser and pol. mag., Seychelles, Mar., 1919; ag. ch. just., Seychelles, Nov., 1919; crown pros., legal adviser, etc., Seychelles, June, 1920; ag. ch. just., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; ch. just., Apr., 1924; gov.'s dept., July-Aug., 1923; O.A.G. in 1925, 1927 and 1928; res. mag., Jamaica, 1929; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1931; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1931-Oct., 1932.

DE VERE, ROBERT STEPHEN, J.P., B.A., LL.B., barrister.—B. 1872; ed. Charterhouse and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (B.A., LL.B.); called to bar, 1898; serv. in Paget's Horse, S. African war (Queen's med.); dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1903-1905; leg. advsr. and ag. ch. just., Seychelles, 1906-08; recd. comm. in 5th Batt. R.
