Sussex Regt., Feb., 1915; mil. censor, B.E.F., Aug., 1915; Br. judge, Anglo-French condominium, New Hebrides, 1922; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Feb., 1926; ch. just., Grenada, 1931.
DE VERTEUIL, ARTHUR.—B. 1878; ent. Trinidad civ. serv., July, 1899; warden, county of Coronii, July, 1929; do., eastern counties, Mar., 1933.
DE VERTEUIL, ERIC J., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H. (Lond.).—B. 1881; asst. med. inspr. of health, Trinidad, Oct., 1930; ag. dep. surg.-gen., May, 1933.
DE VERTEUIL, JOSEPH, F.C.S.—B. 1874; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, Dec., 1899; supt., field experiments, dept. of agr., Apr., 1913; agrl. chem., dept. of agr., Sept., 1920; agronomist, 1931.
DE VERTEUIL, LEON EUGENE.—B. 1877; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Trinidad, June, 1900; 2nd grade dist. offr., Oct., 1903; 1st grade dist. offr., Feb., 1911.

DE VERTEUIL, ST. YVES DANIEL, M.B.E. (1932).—B. 1875; clk. of works, waterworks, Trinidad, Dec., 1900; head overseer, Arima, Jan., 1903; dist. engrnr., 2nd grade, Aug., 1916; dist. engrnr., 1st grade, Sept., 1916; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, Dec., 1925; warden, St. George, prot. of immigrts. and dir., lab. exchange, Mar., 1933.

DE VILLIERS, ISAAC PIERRE, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. S. African Coll.; atty. and notary, served S.W. Africa, in 1st Eastern Rifles, 1914-15; France and Palestine, in R.F.A., 1916-19; dep. commr. (headqrs.) S.A. pol., Feb., 1923; commr., S. African pol., Dec., 1928.

DE VILLIERS, SIR JEAN ETIENNE REENEN, M.A., LL.D., Kt.Bach. (1923).—B. 1875; ed. S. African Coll. and Cambridge; B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1897; LL.D., 1902; fellow of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1901; judge of water cts. from 1913; ag. judge, sup. ct., Cape Town, 1915, 1916, 1918, 1919; judge pres., O.F.S., 1920; judge of appl., 1933.

DEVINE, A. SIDNEY.—B. 1892; ed. Fran. Coll., Oxon; enlisted Middlesex Regt., Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., R. Warwickshire Regt., Oct., 1915; lieut., 1917; served in France and Italy; wounded; served with disposal bd. in Italy, 1919-20; disposal and liquidation commn., 1920-23; cler. offr., C.O., 19th Mar., 1923.
DEVONSHIRE, 9TH DUKE OF (creat. 1694).

VICTOR CHRISTIAN WILLIAM CAVENDISH, K.G., 1916; P.C.; G.C.M.G.; G.C.V.O.; LL.D.; BARON CAVENDISH, 1605; EARL OF DEVONSHIRE, 1618; MARQUESS OF HARTINGTON, 1694; EARL OF BURLINGTON, 1831; BARON CAVENDISH (U.K.), 1831.—B. 1863; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.P. (L.U.) Derbyshire W., 1891-1908; treas. of H.M. Household, 1900-1903; financial sec. to treasy., 1903-1905; lord-lieut. of Derbyshire; president territorial forces, Derby; chancellor of Leeds Univ.; civil lord of the Admiralty, 1915; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1916-21; S. of S. for the Colonies, Oct., 1922 to Jan., 1924.

DE WAAL, HON. DANIEL, D.S.O., K.C.—B. 1873; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; B.A., LL. B. (Cantab. and Cape); admitted, advoc., Cape Town, 1897; practised, Pretoria; German S.W. Africa campaign, 1915; K.C., 1919; ag. judge, Griqualand West Local Divn., Oct., 1919; judge, sup. ct., T.P.D., Aug., 1920; judge pres., T.P.D., 1927.

DE WAAL, HON. JAN HENDRIK HOFMEYR, M.P.—B. 1871; ed. Normal and S.A. Colls., Cape Town; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1897; advoc., Cape bar, 1897; mem., Union H. of A. for Piquetburg since 1915; whip to Nationalist Party, 1915-24; chmn. of comtees and dep. speaker, 1924-29; speaker, 1929-33; el. pres., Afrikaanse Taal Vereniging, 1910; formerly chmn., Regs Kommissie and chmn., head comtee., Nationalist Party, Cape; author of "Afrikaner Grammar," and of various works of fiction in Afrikaans; edr. "De Goede Hoop," 1903-14.

DE WAAL, COL. PIETER.—B. 1899; ed. Zeerust Pub. Schl., and Transvaal Univ. Coll. (now Univ. of Pretoria); lieut., perm. force, Union Defence Forces, 1922; S.A. Perm. Garrison Arty., 1923; attd. Coast Arty. Schl., Shoeburynews, Aug.-Dec., 1923; attd. R.A., S. Area, England, 1924-25; capt., S.A.P.G.A., 1925; seconded to S.A. Staff Corps as staff offr., gunnery, Cape Peninsula, 1925; ag. A.D.C. to gov.-genl., 1925-28; grad. Staff Coll., Camberley, 1930; A.D.C. to C.G.S., U.D.F., Impl. Confcof, London, 1930; attd., W.O., 1930-31; O.C., S.A.P.G.A. and C.G.A., Cape Peninsula, and promoted major, 1931; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Capetown, with temp. rank of lieut. col., 1932; maj. (temp. lieut.-col.), S.A. Staff Corps, 1933; lieut. col., S.A. Staff Corps, 1933; O.C., Sp Serv. Batt., 1933-34; O.C., Roberts' Heights commd. and comdt., S.A. Milv. Coll., 1933; dist. mily. operatns. and training, defence hdqrs., Pretoria, 1934, and temp. col., 1934.
DEWAR, H. J.—B. 1878; clk., repatriation dept., O.R.C., 1902; clk., Br. refugee aid, 1903; clk., treas., O.F.S., 1904; clk., treas., Unions of S. Africa, 1910; acctnt., 1916; ch. acctnt., 1921.

DEWAR, JOHN.—B. 1883; ed. Otago Univ. Coll.; cadet, lands and survey dept., New Zealand, 1901; survr., F.M.S., 1906; ast. supt., surveys, F.M.S., 1911; supt. of surveys, Kodah, Jan., 1920; supt., surveys, F.M.S., Oct., 1924; ast. surrur.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1927; dep. surrur.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931; surrur.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June, 1933.
DE WET, JOHANNES HENDRIK, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Univ. of Pretoria; educ. dept., 1911-13; Union pub. serv. from 1918; ag. under sec., dept. of interior, 1931; under sec., do., 1932.
DE WET, THE HON. N. J., K.C.—Min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1913-24; puisne judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1932.

DICKENS, K. J., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Christ's Hosp.; schol. and exhib.; open classical scholarship, Keble Coll., Oxford, 1925; final honours schol., English language and lit., 1929; inspr., schm., Gold Coast, 1929.

DICKINSON, ARTHUR HAROLD.—B. 1892; ed. Bromsgrove Schl.; pol. probrr., S.S., Nov., 1912; ast. supt., pol., Jly., 1916; ast. pol., May, 1920; supt., pol., Jan., 1928; K.P., Pol. Med., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., crim. intell. sec., and in addn. ag. dir., pol. intell. bureau, Dec., 1928; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Dec., 1929; cr. crim. intell., S.S., Jan., 1932; ch. pol. aff., Penang, Nov., 1933.

DICKINSON, BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Giggleswick and Caius Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1904; dist. commr., G. Coast, 1909-16; pol. mag., Accra, 1916-17; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1918; puisne judge, 1926; sec. puisne judge, 1927; ag. ch. just., six times; puisne judge, Kenya, 1930.

DICKINSON, JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—Ed. Liverpool Coll.; solr., 1920; 2nd lieut., The King's Own (R. Lancaster Regt.), 1914; lieut., July, 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; France, 1915-18 (M.C. and Bar); ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1921.

DICKINSON, THE HON. RICHARD SEBASTIAN WILLOUGHBY, D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1897; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1922; priv. sec. to Sir Hugh Clifford, 1922-23; seconded as priv. sec. to Sir H. Stanley, gov. of N. Rhodesia, 1923-26; ast. sec. to N. Rhodesia, 1926; ag. prim. ast. ch. on various occasions, 1929-32; ag. ch. sec., 1933.
DICKSON, T., ASWORTH, C.M.G. (1914), M.C. (1916).—B. 1881; cust. ast., E.A.P., 1926; ast. dist. commr., 1931; dist. commr., Kera, Jan., 1919; res. commr., Swaziland, 1928.

DICKSON, WILLIAM MURRAY, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. St. Mary's Schl. and Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; B.A., 1897; priv. sec. to several genls. and mins. of just. of Canada, 1914-25; ast. chf., remission dept., dept. of just., 1925-30; priv. sec., Hon. G. D. Robertson, and later, Hon. W. A. Gordon, mins. of lab., 1930-33; dep. min. of lab., 1934.

DIEMONT, J. F. W.—B. 1884; ed. Govt. Coll., Bloemfontein; ast., secretarial trans. posts and tels., O.R.C., 1903; clk., prov. sec. office, O.F.S., 1910; admstve. inspr., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1922-29; prin. elk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1929.

DILLON, ALBERT BARROW, I.S.O. (1932).—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; elk. to inspr. of schls., B. Hond., 1893; ag. elk. to audr. and in treasry. and cust., 1893; elk. to col. engr., 1895; ag.-insp. of schls., 1895-8; 2nd elk., treasry. and cust., 1897; insp. of schls., June, 1898; ag. supt. bot. station, Sept. to Dec., 1904; ag. ch. elk., C.S.O., and elk. of couns., June, 1905 to Jan., 1906; lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, Apr., 1918; ag. audr., May to Aug., 1918 and from Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1920.

DIMES, CAPT. DOUGLAS HENRY, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Kingsbridge Gram. Schl. and Schl. of Agr., S. Devon; R. Vety. Coll., London; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1913; war serv. in France and Belgium, 1914-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1923.

DINGLE, PERSIVAL ALFRED, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.S.M., certif. T.M.H., L.S.T.M.—B. 1881; dist. surg., N. Borneo, Dec., 1911; dep. health offr., July, 1915; civil res. surg., No. 1 London genl. base hosp., Jan., 1917 to May, 1918; P.M.O. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Apr., 1918; deleg. to 5th cong., Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Sept., 1923; deleg. to L. of N. Health Confce, S'pore, Feb., 1925; deleg. to meeting of advisory coun., eastern bureau, L. of N. health organisation, S'pore, Jan., 1926.

DISCOMBE, JOHN.—Apptd., after compet. exam., 3rd class elk., sup. ct., Gibraltar, 1892; 2nd class elk., 1899; asst. regiar. and dep. elk. of arraigns, 1900; called to the bar, Gray's Inn; regiar. and elk. of arraigns, admstr.-gen., ex officio J.P., and mem. of cemetery comte., 1911; marshal, ex officio, 1913; regiar. and marshal, prize court, 1914; custodian of enemy property, 1916; during the war rendered legal assistance to the atty.-gen., in addition to other duties, July, 1916, to Feb., 1919; regiar. under Deeds of Arrangement Orde., 1917; regiar. of business names, 1918; contrlr. local clearing office for enemy debts, 1920; ag. pol. mag. and coroner on several occasions.

DIVE, Major HUBERT ROY, M.C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), D.O.M.S. (Lond.), graduate Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med., certif. Royal Land. Opthalmic Hosp. (Moorfields).—B. 1887; great war, Gallipoli; Egypt, Palestine and France, 1915-19; 1st med. offr. i/c war pensioners., St. Barth., Hosp., Lond., 1919-20; student and house surgn. L.S.T.M., and Albert Dock Hosp. Feb.-Aug., 1920; med. offr., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1927; assisting in office of prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Aug., 1930; ch. med. offr., Penang, June, 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1933.
DIX, C. C., C.M.G., D.S.O., R.N., (ret.)—Ed. R.N. Schl., Eltham and H.M.S. "Britannia"; R.N., 1898-1919; harbrmr., Barbados, 1926; harbrmr., King-ton, Jamaica, 1927.
DIXEY, FRANK, O.B.E. (1929), D. Sc., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; govt. geologist, Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918 to Dec., 1921; dir., geological surveys, Nyasaland, Dec., 1921.

DIXON, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1932), O.B.E. (1924), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. mods., 1909; 1st cl. Lit.Hum., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1911; priv. sec. to Sir G. Fiddes, 8th July, 1917; 1st cls. elk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., D.C., 4th Sept., 1929.

DIXON, CAPT. HAROLD PARSON.—B. 1888; ed. Dover Coll. and Merchant Taylors Schl.; mily. serv., 1914-20; served in Salonika, Caucasus and Anatolia; mentd. in desps.; capt., 5th Durham L.I., T.A. (Res.), 1921; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1921.
DIXON, OWEN, K.C., M.A., LL.B. (Victoria).—B. 1886; ed. Melbourne Univ.; called to bar, 1910; ag. just., sup. ct., Victoria, July-Dec., 1926; a just. of high ct., O'wealth of Australia, 1929.
DIXON, CAPT. OSWALD, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, Dec., 1912; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1920; asst. ch. vety. offr., 1932.

DOBBS, CECIL MOORE, C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E. (1928).—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trim Coll., Dublin; B.A. Classics (honours); asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 25th Oct., 1906; dist. comsnr., 1910; senr. comsnr. (2nd cls.), Jan., 1925; ditto, 1st cls., Sept., 1927; ag. ch. native comsnr., Nov. 1928.
DOBSON, KENNETH BLAIR AUSTIN.—B. 1907; ed. Clifton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1931; asst. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.
DODDS, E. W.—B. 1881; elk., treasry., Natal, 1899; elk., treasry., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treasry., 1920; acctnt., treasry., 1928; acct. dept. of posts and tel., Dec., 1931.

DONALDSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM HUNTER, O.B.E., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.). M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif. (with distinc.) L.S.T.M., D.P.H. (Lond.).—B. 1881; ed. St. Thomas Hosp., London; joined R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915; B.E.F. in France as surg. specialist, 18th Casualty Clearing Station, 1915-19; ag. major, R.A.M.C., mentd. in desps.; served in Somaliland in operations against the Dervishes in 1920; med. offr., Somaliland, Nov., 1920; med. offr. in ch., June, 1933.

DONOVAN, THOMAS.—B. 1885; ed. Christian Brothers' Coll., Cork; Mediterranean Expedy. Force, Apr., 1915-Jan., 1916; B.E.F., Apr., 1916-June, 1919; Q.M.S., Somaliland pol., Oct., 1919 to Mar., 1923; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Apr., 1923.

DONOVAN, WILLIAM, M.S.C. (N.Z.), F.I.C.—B. 1879; ed. Thames (N.Z.) High Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Otago Univ. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; joined N.Z. govt. serv., 1905; asst. dom. analyst, 1919; dom. analyst, 1930.

DOOLE, EDGAR (CAPT. R. OF O.), M.I.A.Mech.E., M.I.A.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; A.S.C. (mech. transp.), 1914; engnr. offr., Tank Corpsa, 1917; R. of O., 1921; asst. mech. engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; transf'd., motor transp. dept., Aug., 1922; engnr. transp. offr., Nov., 1922.

DOORLY, A. N., B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister at Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1887; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; res. mag., Kenya, 1919; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1923; ag. judge, high ct., in 1923, 1924 and 1925; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1925; ag. ch. just. in 1926, 1928 and 1929; atty.-gen., 1929.
DOORLY, REV. CHARLES STOKLEY, B.A. Selwyn Coll. Camb.—B. 1882; 4th ast. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, July, 1911; 3rd ast. mast., July, 1914; 2nd ast. mast., July, 1920.

DOORLY, SIR CHARLES W., KT. BACH (1935), C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1876; 3rd cls. elk., imgrnr. dept., Jamaica, 1892; 2nd cls. elk., 1894; Hindustani interpreter, 1897; imprir. of imgrnr., 1903; protector of imigrts., 1909; govt. emigrn. agt., Madras, 1913; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, 1922; admstr., St. Lucia, Feb., 1928; ag. gov., Windward Is. in 1930, 1931 and 1933.

DORAN, CHARLES FREDERICK GABRIEL, B.A., B.L.—Ed. Royal Belfast Academical Inst. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd Lieut., R.A.F., 1916-19; served in France, Nov., 1916 till wounded in 1917; called to Irish Bar (King's Inn) 1913; crown counsel, Kenya, Dec., 1922; ag. solr.-gen. and M.L.C., Mar.-June, 1930 and Dec., 1931-July, 1932; ag. solr.-gen., Sept., 1933-Jan., 1934, res. mag., 1934.

DORION, HON. CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1860; ed. Quebec Seminary; Laval Univ. (L.L.L., 1884); admitted to bar of Prov. of Queb., 1884; practised law in Queb. City until 1911; LL.D., Laval Univ., 1894; prof. of laws, Laval Univ., 1898; and since, prof. of civ. code; judge, superior et., Prov. of Queb., 1911; judge, ct. of king's bench, 1921.
D'ORNELLAS, GUSTAVE.—B. 1885; 6th cls. clk., reg. office, Br. Guiana, 1905; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1906; 5th ditto, 1910; 4th ditto, 1915; 3rd ditto, 1923; 2nd ditto, 1925; asst. survr., cust., 1927.

DOS SANTOS, ERROL LIONEL.—B. 1890; clk., recr.-gen.'s office, Trinidad, Apr., 1912; asst. acctnt., ditto, 1921; acctnt., ditto, Jan., 1923; dep. treas., 1929; dep. treas., comsnr., income tax and dep. comsnr., estate duties, 1931; treas., comsnr., income tax and comsnr., estate duties, Oct., 1933.

DOUGHTY, ARTHUR GEORGE, C.M.G. (1905), M.A., Litt.D.—B. 1860; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., Quebec, Canada, 1896; librarian, Quebec, 1900; Dominion archivist, Canada, 1904; mem. of comsnr. on War Records and Trophies, Dec., 1918; joint del. to internat. congress of the history of America at Rio-de Janeiro, Sept., 1922.

DOUGHTY, LESLIE ROY, B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A.—B. 1902; ed. Derby Munie. Secondary Schl., Midland Agril. Coll. and Schl. of Agr., Cambridge; geneticist, East African agril. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

DOUGLAS, CUTHBERT SOBOLTO.—B. 1887; temp. survr., irrigtn. dept., Union of S.A., May, 1917; temp. asst. engrnr., Dec., 1919; 2nd gr. prof. asst., Dec., 1927; prof. asst., Nov., 1929; survr.-gen., Bloemfontein, May, 1934.

DOUGLAS-JONES, SIR CRAWFORD DOUGLAS, K.T. BACH. (1934), C.M.G. (1920).—Ed. Harrow; priv. sec. to res. comsnr., S. Rhodesia, Aug., 1898; lieut., S. Rhodesia volrs., July, 1908; capt., May, 1910; musketry instr.'s certif., Hythe, 1908; sec. to res. comsnr., Rhodesia, Apr., 1911; ag. res. comsnr., S. and N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1918; res. comsnr., July, 1918 to Sept., 1923; res. comsnr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1923; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1924; admstr., govt., Sept., 1924 to April, 1925 and from Apr. to July, 1926; col. sec., Br. Guiana, 1926; O.A.G. on various occasions, 1927-28.

DOVEY, JOHN EDWARD, M.B., Ch.B., D.T.M. & H. (Edin.).—Med. offr., Hong Kong; do., Kowloon, 1927-28; med. offr. in ch., subord. staff and families, med. offr. in ch. venereal disease clinic and asst., Victoria Hosp., 1929; M.O. in ch., Victoria Hosp., May-Sept., 1932 and 1934; M.O. in ch., civil and ment. hosp., 1933.

DOWBIGGIN, SIR HERBERT LAYARD, K.T. Bach., C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1890; inspr. of pol., Ceylon, Jan., 1901; asst. supt. of pol., Apr., 1901; supt. of pol., Feb., 1905; inspr.-gen. of pol., Nov., 1918; on sp. duty, Cyprus, Nov., 1926; resumed duties as inspr.-gen., pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1926.

DOWNEY, CHARLES EDWIN JAMES.—B. 1889; ed. Macandrew Road Schl., normal schl. and Otago Boys' High Schl., Dunedin, N.Z.; ent. N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1937; dep. comsnr., taxes, 1928; comsnr., taxes, 1932.

DOWNIE, H. F., O.B.E. (1934), B.A. (1912), M.A. (1914).—B. 1889; ed. at Christ's Hosp., Oxford; Lodge exhibnr. (class.) Univ. Coll., 1908-1912; 1st class moda., 1910; 2nd class Lit. Hum., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Dec., 1912; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug. to 19th Oct., 1914, and from 10th June, 1915, to 7th Apr., 1918; ag. 1st cls. clk., 3rd Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; visited Sudan, Uganda, Kenya, Tanganyika, Nyasaland, N. and S. Rhodesia and S. Africa as sec., East Africa comes closer union, Dec., 1927-May, 1928.

DOWNING, CAPT. GEOFFREY GEORGE DAVID, J.P.—Ed. Corrig Schl., Ireland, Royal Irish Univ. and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Ireland; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, 1883; Cape pol. (Grumland West), 1888; Mashonaland exped. (Pioneers), 1890 (med. and clasp); Dominica defence res., 1914; lieut., Nov., 1914; ag. sub-inspr., Leeward Is. pol., O.C., Montserrat defence force, O.C., local forces, Montserrat, Dec., 1916; ag. sub-inspr., Leeward Is. pol., and offr. instr. local forces, Antigua, July, 1917; O.C., Marine Batt., Antigua, Oct., 1917; O.C., flashlight service, Antigua-Montserrat, Jan., 1919; cap.t., Dominica defence force, 1919; O.C., Dominica defence force, Nov., 1919; manager, Barbuda and receiver of wrecks, Mar., 1920; mag. dist. L. (Barbuda), Jan., 1921; ag. warden, N. dist., Dominica and c. mem. legis. coun., Mar. to Sept., 1927; ag. warden, Nevis, and mem. legis. coun., St. Kitts Nevis, Mar. to Apr., 1928; warden, Barbuda, 1929.

DOWSE, WILLIAM ROBERT, A.I.E.E.—B. 1891; Br. P.O. engrnr. dept., G. Britain, Jan., 1912; Dub. Univ. ex-graduates, O.T.C., Aug., 1914; comsnr., R.E., Jan., 1915; Galipoli and France, May, 1915; asst. telegr. engrnr., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., June, 1919; demob. reg. army R. of O., June, 1919; engrnr., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrnr., ditto, Mar., 1928; senr. engrnr., P. and T. Selancor and Pahang, Mar., 1932; asst. engrnr.-in-ch., post and tels., Malaya, 1933.
DOWSETT, FRANCIS DUDLEY.—R. 1908; ed. Bristol Grammar Schl. and Hertford Col., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1931.

DOYLE, THOMAS FRANCIS.—B. 1891; ed. St Patrick's Acad., Dungannon; offr. in Imp. cust. and excise serv., Aug., 1912; inspr., cust., Palestine, July, 1926; ag. dep. dir., cust., excise and trade, periods in 1929 and 1930; dep. comsnr. cust. and excise comsnr., Mauritius, Feb., 1932; ag. collr., cust. and harmr., June, 1932.

DRAKE, HAROLD WILLIAM.—B. 1889; ed. City of London Schl.; entd. Col. Audit Dept., July, 1904; seconded to W.A.F.F., Oct., 1914 (Nigeria and Cameroons); paymr. Overseas Contingent, W.A.F.F. (G.E.A.); Col. Audt. Dept., Aug., 1918; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Mar., 1919; senr. asst. audr., Dec., 1921; ag. audr., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and Apr. to Oct., 1925; audr. Br. Guiana, Nov., 1927; asst. dir., col. audt. dept., Dec., 1931; audr., Nigeria, 1933.
DRAKE, THOMAS NORMAN.—B. 1896; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast; pilot, R.A.F., Great
WAR, 1917-19; cadet, R.I.C. and dist. inspr., Ulster spl. constab., 1922; Air Force Offrs. Reserve, 1923; 3rd cls. inspr., Jamaica constab., 1924.

DRAYTON, BERNARD MORGAN.—B. 1886; service in post office, pub. wks. and treasy. depta., Grenada, 1902-14; treasy. astt., 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1914; astt. treas., civ. admstrn., German E. Africa (Occupied Territory), 1917; astt. treas., Nigeria, 1920; senr. astt. treas., Nigeria, 1928.

DRAYTON, HON. SIR HENRY L., KT. BACH. (1915), K.C.—B. 1869; ed. in England and Canada; called to the bar, Ontario, 1891; counsel, railway comte., Ontario legislature, 1902; counsel for City of Toronto, 1910; Ontario gov't. comnr., on Toronto Power Comnr., 1911; ch. comnr., bd. of railway comnr., Canada, 1912; resig. to assume portfolio of min. of finance in Union Gov't., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Kingston, Oct., 1919; re-el. for York West, g.e., Dec., 1921; resig. portfolio on defeat of admtn., Dec., 1921; chmn., bd. of liquor comnr., Ontario, 1928.

DRAYTON, ROBERT HARRY, LL.B. (hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; solr. (Eng.); lieut., Devonshire Regt. and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-1918; serv. in France, Jan., 1916 to Sept., 1917; miny. of reconstr. and Lord Chancellor's dept., 1919; treas. solr.'s office, 1919-20; astt. legal sec., gov't. of Palestine (continuing as astt. atty.-gen.), Nov., 1920; ag. legal sec., Aug., 1921 to Oct., 1921; mem. of advisory and exec. couns., Apr. to July, 1923 and July to Sept., 1924; title changed to solr-gen., 1925; rep. Palestine at internat. arbitrn. on partition of Ottoman pub. debt, Geneva, 1925; mem., finan. comnr., orthodox patriarchate of Jerusalem, 1924-28; one of counsel rep. gov't. in parly. comnr. re disturbances of 1929; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions, 1922-31; legal draftman, Jan., 1932; called to bar, 1934; comnr. for revision of laws of Palestine, Sept., 1934; atty.-gen., Tanzania-Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1934.
DRAYTON, VIVIAN FORRESTE.—B. 1883; ent. civ. serv., Grenada, 1900; ch. clk., gov't. office, Aug., 1918; commr., Carriacou, Jan., 1932.

DRENNAN, JOHN MATTHEW, D.C.M. (1918).—B. 1891; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin.; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. serv. and assigned to B. of T., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 3rd cls., Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1912; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (France, Salonica, Palestine); astt. head, appts. dept., 1919; stores dept., 1922; shipping dept., 1930; dep. head, gen. dept., Jan., 1933; shipping dept., May, 1933.
DREW, DESMOND, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (T.C.D.). Med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921.

DREW, RINGOER CHARLES WILLINGTON, B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Monkton Combe Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1912; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1921; ag. state engrnr., Kelantan, Apr., 1928; senr. exec. engrnr., Apr., 1930; state engrnr., Kelantan, June, 1931.
DRIBERG, ALLAN, K.O.—B. 1873; ag. puisne just., Ceylon, Jan., 1927; puisne just., Sept., 1927.

DROWER, E. M., C.B.E. (1923).—B. 1880; admitted solr., 1904; practised, consular ct., Cairo, 1904-6; Sudan bar, 1906-14; served European War, 1914-18; pres. of first instance, Hillah, 1918; Mosul, 1918-19; Basrah, 1919; astt. judicial adviser, Iraq, 1921; tempy. judicial adviser, 1921; govt. counsellor, 1921; adviser to miny. of just., 1922.

DRUMMOND, DAVID B.—B. 1898; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., 1925; astt. dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, July, 1923; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1925; ag. dist. comnr., on five occasions, 1924-30; pvt. sec. to gov. and ag. gov. on five occasions, 1924-29; ag. senr. astt. col. sec., Aug.-Sept., 1930; ag. ch. astt. col. sec. on several occasions; edr. "Sierra Leone Studies."

DRYBURGH, ALEXANDER MITCHELL, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1895; ed. George Heriot Schl., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., 1919; govt. rice agt., Tapah, Mar., 1920; 1st astt. dist. offr., B. Padang, Sept., 1920; attd. Fedl. sect., Nov., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. V., 1922; cls. IV., 1926; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1926; dist. offr., Raub, 1928; sec., res., Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1930; offr., cls. III., 1931; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, June, 1933; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, 1934.

DU BOULAY, GEORGE CORNIBERT, C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1883; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. audit clk., St. Leia, Apr., 1899; 3rd clk., govt. office, Aug., 1899; 4th clk., treasy. and customs, Mar., 1903; ag. 2nd clk., treasy., from Mar. to Nov., 1903; chief clk., post office, Jan., 1905; ag. harbr. master, St. Lucia, Sept., 1909; priv. sec. and clk. to gov. of Seychelles, clk. to councils and aupt. of printing, Nov., 1912; ag. auditor, May, 1916; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., Mar. to May, 1919; astt. sec., col. sec.'s off., Gibraltar, Dec., 1919; senr. astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. and gov's dep. on various occasions, 1924-25; ch. astt. col. sec., Gold Coast, Jan., 1926; ag. col. sec., on various occasions; gov's dep. on various occasions; ag. gov., Apr. to Sept., 1930 and Dec., 1931.

DU CANE, GEN. ST JOHN, G.C.B. (1928), K.C.B. (1916), C.B. (1910).—B. 1865; lieut., R.A., 1884; capt., 1893; major, 1900; lieut.-col., 1902; col., 1908; major-gen., 1915; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen., 1926; D.A.A.G., Staff Coll., 1906-07; G.S.O., 1st grade, hqdrs. of Army, 1908-10; C.R.A., 3rd divn., 1911-12; S.O. to I.G., Home Forces, 1913-14; served, S. Africa, 1899-02 (desps., brevet lieut.-col., Queen's med., 5 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); European war, brig.-gen., III. Corps, 1914; maj.-gen., R.A., G.H.Q., 1915; sp. appt., min. of munitions, 1916; commanded XV. Corps.; Br. rep. with Marshal Foch, 1918 (7 time ment. in desps.); master-gen., ordnance, 1920-23; G.O.C.-in-ch., Western command, 1923-24; G.O.C.-in-ch., Br. Army of Rhine, 1924-27; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Malta, 1927; resigned, 1931.

DUCKWORTH, EDWARD HARLAND.—B.1893; ed. Cheltenham Coll., City and Guilds Engng. Coll., Finsbury, and R. Coll. of Science; B.Sc. Lond., A. R. C. Sc.; war serv. with London Elec. Engrs., R.E. (T.), 1916-18; France, 1916-18; instr., educn., S. Prov., Nigeria, Oct., 1930; author of "The Laboratory Workshop" and various articles in the "School Science Review."

DUFF, DAVID, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. Dubl.), D.P.H. (Irel.).—B. 1853; ed. Portora, Enniskillen and Trin. Coll., Dubl.; sch. in classics; B.A. (nat. sci.) with 1st cls. hon's and gold med.; med. offr., W.A.M.S. and posted to Gold Coast, June, 1910; senr. med. offr., 1925; astt. dir., med. serv., Jan., 1927; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; mem. bd. of examrs. in native languages, Apr., 1928; dep. dir., med. serv., Aug., 1931; dir., med. and sanry. serv., Feb., 1932.

DUFF, SIR H. L., K.R.E. (1918); C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1872; apptd. by F.O., asst. res. B.C. Africa, 1897; resig. from serv., 1903; re-apptd. Apr., 1904; res., 1906; seconded, 1909-10; admitted mem. of Inner Temple, Jan., 1909; 1st cls., hon. constitutional law, 1909; ag. dep. gov., July, 1910; and Apr. to July, 1911; 1st grade res., July, 1911; sec. to the admntr., 1912; ch. sec. to gov't. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1914; served with Nyasaland Field Force, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1915, mentd. in despatches; served against rebels in Shire Highlands, 1915 (medal and clasp); ch. polit. offr. to Brig.-Gen. Northey, C.B., in operations against G.E. Africa, July, 1916; ag. gov't., Nyasaland Prot., 1st Oct., 1918; ret., 1920; author of "Nyasaland under the F.O.", 2nd ed.; and a history of Nyasaland in the native dialect, published by the Prot. gov't.

DUFF, Rt. Hon. Sir Lyman Poore, G.C.M.G. (1934), P.C. (1919), B.A., LL.B. Toronto Univ.—B. 1865; apptd. judge, sup. ct., British Columbia, 1904; judge, sup. ct. of Canada, 1906; cent. appeal judge, Mil. Serv. Act, 1917; dep. of gov't. gen., 1926; ch. just., Canada, 1933.

DUFF, STEUART MURRAY, B.A.—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1928; attd., Kandy kach., Jan., 1929; attd. Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1930; office asst., Nuwara Eliya kach., May, 1930; pol. mag., Gampola, Oct., 1931; office asst., Jaffna kach., Feb., 1934.

DUFFY, Hon. Sir Frank Gavan, K.C.M.G. (1929), K.C., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1852; ed. at Stonyhurst Coll., and Melbourne Univ.; called to the bar, 1874; judge of the high ct. of Australia, Feb., 1913; ch. just., high ct., C'wealth of Australia, 1931.

DUGAN, Maj.-Gen. Sir Winston, K.C.M.G., C.B., D.S.O.—B. 1877; 2nd lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1900; served, S. Africa, 1899-1902 (Queen's med., 3 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); adjt., 1901-4; garrison adjt., Irish commd., 1910-14; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., E. Anglian divn., 1914; asst. prov. marshal, 1914; commd. 2nd R. Irish Regt., France, 1915-16; asst. inspr.-gen., training, B.E.F., 1918; brig.-commdr. (temp. brig.-gen.), 1916-18; commd. 10th Infy. Brig., 1919-23; A.A.G., Southern Commd., 1926-30; A.D.C. to King, 1928-30; commdr., 56th (1st Lond.) divn., T.A., 1931; gov't., S. Australia, 1934.

DUKE, EDGAR MORTIMER, LL.B.—B. 1895; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; Br. Guiana open schol. in maths., 1911; Andrews' entrance schol. in science, 1913; and Joseph Hume schol. in jurisprudence, 1914, Univ. Coll., London; Campbell Foster Prizeman of Middle Temple; sp. prizeman in crim. law and procedure, 1915; LL.B. (Lond.), 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; admitted to practise Br. Guiana, 1917 and in Trinidad and Tobago, 1921; ag. regar., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Br. Guiana, in 1929, 1930 and 1932; dep. regar., deeds, sup. ct., etc., Jan., 1930; stip. mag. and mem., dist. licensing bds., Demerara and Essequibo, Sept., 1931; regar., deeds, 1933; author, "Law of Immovable Property in British Guiana."
DUKE, ORKILST. A., M.B.E. (1932)—B. 1896; ent. gov't. serv., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1913; serv. European War, 1915-19; inspr., Leeward Is. pol., Sept., 1927.
DUMANOIRE, JOSEPH WENCKSLAS.—Asst. trigonometrical survey, Trinidad, 1902; 2nd asst., survey office, 1903; 1st asst., ditto, 1913; dep. surv.-gen., 1930; ditto and sub. intendant, 1933.

DUMBRELL, Henry James Edward, B.A.—B. 1885; asst. mast., Heaton Lodge Schl., nr. Huddersfield; mast., St. Bedes Coll., Estcourt, Natal; headmr., Ompumulo Native Training Coll.; inspr., schls., Natal, 1921; inspr., educn., Bechuanaland Prot. and Swaziland, 1928.

DUNCAN, CLAUDE WOODBUFF, C.B.E. (1930).—Clt., gov't. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, May, 1899, to Feb., 1901; sub-inspr., B. Guiana police, 1st Mar., 1901; passed school of musketry, Hythe, 1901; passed school of instruction, Chelsea Barracks, 1905; dist. inspr. and J.P., May, 1907; ag. country inspr., Jan., 1908 to June, 1909; passed in Hindi, 1909; in command of special expeditions to Venezuela frontier, Wena-mu, Oct. to Dec., 1910, and May to June, 1911; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Mauritius, Sept., 1912; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., and supt. of prisons Nov., 1914 to June, 1915; commr. of police, Malta, July, 1916; also inspr. of prisons, Oct., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1919; inspr.-gen. of police and director of prisons, Southern Provinces, Nigeria, June, 1919; relinquished appt. dir. prisons, S. Provs., Nov., 1921; inspr.-gen., N. Provs., in addn. to substantive post, Oct., 1929.

DUNCAN, HAROLD HANDASYDE.—B. 1885; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; hona. schl. mod. hist.; M.A.; qualified in compet. exam. for appt as junr. clk., 1st cls., F.O. (placed 4th in 1908 and 5th in 1909); conf. sec. to London commdr., Imp. Ottoman Bank for work relating to Turkish govt. finance and indu. enterprise in Near East from Dec., 1909; on sp. mission to Constantinople, 1912; served in army, Aug., 1914 to Jan., 1919; France and Italy; liaison offr. (intelligence) between Br. G.H.Q., Italy and Br. Mily. Mission Comando Supremo from Aug., 1918; capt., No. field Yeomanry; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1919; S. Eastern circuit; temp. legal ass., Miny. of Health, 25th Apr., 1927; est., 15th Mar., 1928; 2nd asst. legal adviser, D.O. and C.O., Nat. Mar., 1930; asst. legal adviser, do., 1st Apr., 1931; ag. legal adviser, Dec., 1931 to Mar., 1932 and Feb., 1933 to July, 1933.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904). K.C., LL.D. (Edinburgh).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford school. Ball. Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. schol., 1893; Craven scholar, p., 1890; clk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prim. clk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906; barrister, Middle Temple and advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa; M.L.A., for Yeoville; min. of interior, pub. health and educn., 1921-24; min. of mines, Apr., 1933.

DUNCAN, WILLIAM McFARLANE.—B. 1896; ed. Craigie Pub. Schl. and Acad.; certifa. in building constr., mechanics, mathematics, etc.; Caledonian rly., Perth, 1911; asst. engr., Tanganyika rly., Feb., 1921.

DUNCOMBE, F. A. C.—B. 1880; 4th clk., P.O., Bahamas, 1903; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1914; clk., record office, 1909; commr., 4th div., 1912; commr., grade III., June, 1920; clk., grade III., regar.'s office, July, 1927; ag. asst. regar.-gen., 1928 and from June, 1929.

DUNCOMBE, WALTER KRISALL.—Bahamas treasy. and cust., 1895-1900; Out Island commr., 1900-5; transfld. Lagos Col. as supervisor, cust., Jan., 1905; collr., cust., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; senr. collr. cust., Nigeria, Jan., 1920; dep. contr. cust., Dec., 1922; contr., Dec., 1929; mem. ex. and leg. couns.
DUNDAS, Hon. C. C. F., C.M.G. (1934). O.B.E. (1923).—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Sept., 1908; dist. commr., Aug., 1914; dist. polit. edf.
DUNN, C. de S.—Served in South Africa, 1901-1902 (medal and five clasps); S.A.C., 1903-1906; camel constab., Somaliland, 1912.
DUNN, J. S., B.Sc.—B. 1902; ed. Secondary Schl., Blyth, Northumberland and Univ. of Reading; B.Sc. (Hons.), London and Reading; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Sept., 1926.

DUNSTAN, Sir WYNAND ROWLAND, K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—M.A. (Oxon.); LL.D. (Aberdeen); F.R.S.; ed. Bedford Coll.; lec. and demonstr. of chem. Oxford, 1884-86; prof. of chem., Pharm. Soc., 1886-96; lec. on chem., St. Thomas Hosp., 1892-1900; sec., chem. soc., 1903-5; vice-pres., 1903-6; pres. section of chem. and agric. science, Brit. Assoc., 1906; mem. of coun., Royal Soc., 1904-7; mem. of advsy. comte., on trop. agric., C.O., 1908, vice-pres., Internat. assoc. of trop. agric., 1907; pres., 1910; pres., Internat. Congress trop. agric., London, 1914; mem. commil. intell. comte., Bd. of T., 1900-6; dir., scientific and tech. dept., Impl. Inst., 1896-1903; dir., Impl. Inst., from 1903 to 1923; reported on agric. resources of Cyprus for C.O. in 1904, and of Asia Minor in 1907 (reports presented to parlimt.); visited Ceylon at request of colonial govt., 1910, and again in 1913; visited Newfoundland at request of colonial govt., 1914; arranged and supervised govt. min. surveys in Ceylon, N. and S. Nigeria and Nyassaland (reports presented to parlimt.); author of numerous scientific papers in the Phil. Trans. and Proceedings of Royal Soc., chem. soc., etc., and of tech. reports relating to the utilisation of the resources of the colonies, including "Cotton Cultivation in the Brit. Empire and in Egypt," 1904, "Brit. Cotton Cultivation," 1908; edited "Tech. Reports and Scientific Papers," Impl. Inst., 1903; "Selected Reports," Impl. Inst., 1909 (presented to Parlimt.); "Reports on work of Impl. Inst., 1906-14" (presented to Parlimt.); Report on present position of Cotton Cultivation," 1910; editor, Impl. Inst. Handbooks on Commercial Resources of Tropics; author of article, "Rubber" in last edition "Ency. Brit."

DU PLESSIS, STEPHANUS HENDRIK.—B. 1885; mast., high ct., Windhoek (S.W.A.), Feb., 1920; asst. mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), July, 1926; ditto, Capetown (C.P.), Dec., 1926; mast., sup. ct., Bloemfontein (O.F.S.), Oct., 1931.

DUPRE, Hon. MAURICE, K.C., LL.L., M.P.—B. 1888; ed. at Levin Coll.; called to bar, 1911; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; solr.-gen. in Bennett adminstr., Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.

DURANLEAU, Hon. ALFRED, P.C. (Can.), LL.L., K.C., M.P.—B. 1871; ed. at Ste. Marie de Monnier; Univ. Laval; mem., Laurier divn., Montreal, 1923-27; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1930; minister of marine in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.

DUSSEK, ORMONDE THOMAS, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and Univ. of London; B.A., London, 1909; mem. of convocation; senr. asst. mast., Alleyne's Grammar Schl., Uxeter, 1909-11; senr. asst. mast., High Schl., Malacca, 1912-14; prin., Malay Training Coll., Malacca, 1914-19; prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., F.M.S., 1920; in addn., asst. dir. of educn. (Malay), and examr. in Malay, Mar., 1924.

DU TOIT, PETRUS JOHANN, Ph.D. (Zurich), Dr. Met. Vet. (Berlin), B.A. (Cape), F.R.S. (S. Africa).—Senr. vety. offr., vety. research divn., Vryburg, Dec., 1919; sub-dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1920; dep. dir., vety. educn. and research, Pretoria, July, 1921; dir., vety. services, Pretoria, Apr., 1927; dean of faculty and prof., trop. diseases and protozoology, Univ. of S. Africa, 1927; visited Nigeria at request of C.O. to rept. on stock problems, 1927; del. to Imp. Agr. Research Conf., London, 1927; chmn., 6th Pan-African Vety. Conf., Pretoria, 1929; dir., vety. services and animal industry, 1929.
DU TOIT, STEPHANUS FRANCOIS, LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Univ. of C.T.; translator, Union senate, 1920; genl. usher of black rod, 1926; clk. ast., 1930.

DUTTON, ERIC ALDELM TORLOGH, O.B.E. (1930), M.A.—Ed., Hurstpierpoint, and Keble Coll., Oxford; major, W. Yorks Regt.; served in Gallipoli (ment. in despatch); temp. asst., Basutoland adminstr., 1918-19; priv. sec. to gov. of Uganda, 1920-22; priv. sec. to gov., Kenya, 1922-30; sec., high comntr., Zanzibar, 1922-25; priv. sec. to high comntr., transport, Kenya and Uganda, 1925; prin. asst. ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. ch. sec. in 1931, 1932 and 1934; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1932-34; chmn., finance com-n., 1932.

DUTTON, ERNEST GODFRY.—B. 1886; clk., Impl. mil. rly., Apr., 1902; clk., high comntr. for S. Africa, Apr., 1903 to May, 1916; Swaziland service, Apr., 1907; clk. to ast. comntr., Maseru, Basutoland, June, 1916; ch. clk., master of ct. and regis., July, 1919; ag. govt. sec., June to Dec., 1923 and Feb. to Oct., 1924; ast. comntr., 1924.
DUTTON, STEPHEN BERNARD.—B. 1881; served in stores dept., C.S.A.R., 1902-3; served in Zulu land rebellion, 1906; comptroller of stores, Basutoland, Oct., 1909.

DYE, W. H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.D.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. S.H. and T.M. (Distinc.).—B. 1889; R.A.M.C., 1914-25; surg. specialist, 1918-20; N. Persian forces memorial med., for research, 1924 and 1927; seconded, Nyassaland Aug., 1921-Apr., 1925; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

DYER-MELVILLE, SYDNEY WHITMORE, M.I.E.E., S.A.—B. 1885; ed. City of London schl.; ast. dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; ag. dir., Apr. to Nov., 1913, Oct., 1915 to Apr., 1916 and from Dec., 1919 to Feb., 1925; immigr. offr., Sept. to Dec., 1917; ag. director, Jan. to Dec., 1919; ag. dir., rly. elec. and wireless tel. depts. for various periods, 1925-32.

DYKE, HAMILTON WILLIAM, M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1905.—B. 1881; ed. South African Coll., Cape Town and (Glasgow Univ.; house phys., Glasgow Royal Infirmary, house surg., Glasgow Western Infirmary, Glasgow Ophthalmic Inst. and Glasgow Royal Hosp. for sick children; med. offr., Maseru, Basutoland, 1913; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1915-19; served in France, 1915-19; S.M.O., S.A. Native Lab., Cont. Rouen area, 1919; surg. spec., govt. hosp., Jerusalem and govt. of Palestine, 1927; prin. med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1929; author of "An Outbreak of Scurvy among South African Natives in Rouen," Lancet, 1917; "A case of Madura Foot in Basutoland," S. African Med. Jnl.
DYKE, KENNETH H., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1892; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

DYSON, EDWARD TREVOR.—B. 1886; ed. Ruthin Gram.Schl., U.C.W., Aberystwyth(B.A.), and Jesus Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; ag. pol. mag., Trincomalee, May, 1911; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, May, 1912; office astt. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1912; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Aug., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, Apr., 1916; addtnl. astt. col. sec., Mar., 1918; 3rd astt. col. sec., May, 1918; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1920; astt. govt. agt., N. Eliya, Feb., 1921; ditto, Jaffna, Dec., 1922; dist. judge, Matara, Mar., 1926; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1926; ag. govt. agt., Anuradhapura, Mar., 1928; do., Jaffna, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. I., grade II, 1932; govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1934.

EAGLESOME, Sir JOHN EGAN, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1908).—M. Inst. C.E.; served in P.W.D., rly., India, 1890; recd. thanks of Indian govt., Khojak tunnel, 1892, Godavari bridge, 1900; services lent to C.O., 1st Sept., 1900, as D.P.W., Northern Nigeria; in charge of Baro-Kano rly. from 1907; dir. of rlys. and works, Nigeria, 1912; ret. 1919.

EALLES, SHIRLEY, C.M.G. (1935); C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1883; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; 2nd cls. clk., govt.'s office, Transvaal and O.R.C., July, 1902; 1st cls. clk., July, 1904; transf. to Basutoland service and att. to office of high commr. for S. Africa, July, 1908; prin. clk., Dec., 1911, and astt. Impl. acctnt., Apr., 1915; chief clk., Apr., 1919; mem. of comm. to enquire into establishments, etc., in Bechuanaland Prot., Basutoland and Swaziland, 1921-22; ag. astt. Imp. sec., May-Nov., 1921 and Mar.-Sept., 1922; astt. Imp. sec., 1923; ag. Imp. sec., in 1925, 1927, 1929 and 1931-2; title changed to admstive. sec. to high commr., 1933.

EARLE, HUGH J. M.—B. 1896; ed. Cheltenham; on war service, 1914-19; capt., 4th Batt. Essex Regt., 1918; ent. dept. of lands and mines, B. Guiana, 2nd Aug., 1921; 6th cls. clk., secretariat, 1st Mar., 1922; sec. for sp. duty in connexion with Georgetown disturbances, 1924; ag. A.D.C. to O.A.G., May-Nov., 1925; cadet, admstive serv., Nigeria, 1927.

EARLE, SIR LIONEL, K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1911); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1866; ed. Marl., Univa. of Gottingen and Paris, and Merton Coll., Oxford; astt. sec. to royal comm. on Paris exhibn., 1898-1900; ag. 2nd sec. of embassy, 1900; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1902-1903; priv. sec. to lord pres. of the coun. (Earl of Crewe), 1907; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Earl of Crewe), Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to S. of S. for the colonies (Mr. Lewis Harcourt), Nov., 1910; sec. to H.M. Office of Works, 1912; ret.

EASTER, B. H. M., M.B.E., B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Coll., Finchley; served R.N. Dm., 1917; Scot's Guards (lieut.), 1918; Ampleforth Coll., O.T.C., 1919-23; headmstr., St. Mary's Coll., St. Lucia, 1923; dir., educn. and headmstr., Grenada, 1928; ag. col. sec. and adminr., Grenada, Dec., 1930-Aug., 1931; local adviser, educn. comtee., 1931; dir., educn., Jamaica, Apr., 1932.

EASTWOOD, CHRISTOPHER GILBERT.—B. 1905; scholar of Eton, 1917 and of Trinity Col., Oxford, 1923; apptd. to C.O. as astt. prin. after compet. exam., 2nd Nov., 1927; seconded for astt. as prte. sec. to high commr., Palestine, 11th Feb., 1932.

EATON, BERTIE JAMES, F.I.C., F.C.S., F.I.R.L. O.B.E. (Mily. Div., 1919).—B. 1883; ed. Dorchester Gram. Schl., Finsbury Tech. Coll. (City and Guilds of London Inst.) and Birbeck Inst.; astt. research chem., Impl. Inst., 1904-06; gov't. chem., F.M.S., 1906-10; gov't. agrl. chem., F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; do. to Internat. Rubber Congress and Exhib., Batavia, 1914; recvd. thanks of the S. of S. and the Gov't., F.M.S., for re-searches on rubber, 1918; 2nd lieut., gen. list, adjutant, Civ. Guard Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1916; lieut., M.V.L., O.C., M.V.I., Selangor, 1917; major, M.V.L., Jan., 1923; del. of F.M.S. and S.S. at 2nd Pacif. Service Congress, Australia, July to Oct., 1923; Br. Empire Exhibn., Apr., 1924; O.C., Selangor Volunteer Corps, Jan.-Dec., 1926; mem., co-op. oper. bd., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; seconded for serv., Rubber Research Inst., Nov., 1926; del., rubber confere., and 4th Pacif. serv. conqg., Java, May, 1929; mem., agrl. adv. comtee., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1930; dir., Rubber Research Inst., Malaya, Nov., 1930; author of "The Preparation and Vulcanisation of Plantain Rubber," "The Cultivation and Preparation of Camphor in F.M.S.," contribn. on rubber re-searches to the Society of Chemical Industry, numerous articles on tropical agr., industries, etc., to the Agrl. Bulletin, F.M.S. and other journals.

EAVES, LIEUT.-COLONEL FREDERICK, D.S.O., T.A. (Res.), Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1881; admitted solic. (Eng.), 1908; 2nd-lieut., 5th King's Own Royal Regt.(T.F.), 1908; served in France, 1915-19; commd. 5th King's Own at battles of "Second Ypres" and "Loos" 1915; 1/5th Loyal N. Lancs. at "Messines," 1917 and "Cambrai," 1918; 1st Cameronians at Beaute, 1918; 8th Scottish Rifles in Rhine Army and Ireland 1919; C.M.G., Northern Ireland, 1921-22; D.S.O., 1916; met in desps. three times; astt. land offr., Hong Kong, 1923; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1923, J.P., Hong Kong, 1923; ag. land offr., 1924 and 1927-32; ag. land offr., regnr., marriages (in addn. temp.), 1st pol. mag., registr., trade marks and patents, offr. recvr., 1928.

EBDEN, WILLIAM SYDENHAM.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; class. tripes, 1909; cadet, S.S., 1911; 2nd lieut., Jan., 1915; lieut., Salonika Front; wounded astp. of w., Bulgaria; dist. offr., F.M.S., 1920; dep. contr., rubber expts., mag., Johore, and recvr., Alasgoff concession, in addn., July, 1924; regnr., sup. et., Johore and 1st mag., Johore Bhar., Dec., 1925; astt. adviser, Segamat, Jan., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1928; astt. treas., Penang, Dec., 1928; dist. offr., Upper Perak, Nov., 1930; offr., cls. I.B., Apr., 1931; comsmr., lands, S.S., Mar., 1932.

ECCLIES, LAUNCHLOT WILLIAM GREGORY, M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Michaelhouse Sch., Natal and Worcester Coll., Oxford; astt. survr., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1912; on war serv., 1914-20; astt. examr., diagrams, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. survr. from Dec., 1922; dep. dir., surveys and examr., diagrams and surveys, Oct., 1924; ag. dir., survr., in 1925, 1928 and 1931; comsmr., lands, mines and local govt. and M.L.C., 1933.
ECCLIES, WILLIAM HUNTER.—Ed. St. Paul's Schl., London; clk., educn. office, Trinidad, 19

EDWARDS, CAPT. DAVID, M.A. (1913), LL.B. (Aberdeen), 1914.—Temp. offr., R. Arty., 1915-20; served at home, N.W. Frontier of India, German E. Africa and Mesopotamia; Scottish solr., 1921; aast. dist. comnrs., Kenya, 1921; ag. regis., sup. ct., Kenya, 1921; regis., sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; called to Scottish bar, 1924; res. mag., Kenya, 1928.
EDWARDS, ERNEST JAMES.—B. 1886; aast. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; head mast., Wantse Eng. schl., 1928; senr. mast., educn. dept., 1934.

EDWARDS, WILFRED HENRY, D.I.C., F.E.S.—B. 1897; lect., zoology and entomology, Coll. of Agr., Mauritius, 1924; ag. entomologist, botanist and mycologist, dept. of agr., Mauritius, 1928; govt. entomologist, Jamaica, May, 1920.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM STUART, K.C.—B. 1880; called to the bar, Ontario, Sept., 1909; legal offr., dept. of justice, Canada, Nov., 1910; sec. of dept. of justice, 1913; aast. dep. min., of justice, 1914; dep. min. of justice, 1924.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S. Stltlms., Oct., 1880; aast. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); a J.P. and coroner for S. Stltlms., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungei Ujong, July, 1893; offl. assignee and regis. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Stltlms., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. comnrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high comnrs., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, July, 1904; govr. and comdr-in-chief of the amalgamated colony and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1st Mar., 1906; represented W. African Colonies and Prots. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; gov., B. Guiana, Mar., 1912; ret., Mar., 1917.

EGG, RICHARD TYLER.—B. 1886; called to bar, Inner Temple, June, 1913; admitted to practise before sup. ct., Br. Guiana, 1913; ag. mag., Oct., 1919; stip. mag., 2nd grade, 1921; ditto, 1st grade, 1924; ag. puisne judge, Sept., to Dec., 1925, May, 1926 to Feb., 1927 and Apr. to Nov., 1928; pol. mag., Gold Coast, May, 1929.

ELDER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, O.B.E. (1925).—B.-1881; ed. Reading and Edinburgh; graduated M.R.C.V.S., 1902; F.R.C.V.S., 1914; served in S. African war as civ. vet. surg. with army vet. serv., 1902-3; vet. offr., Swaziland admnstr., May 1903; prin. vet. offr., 1919; agrl. offr., 1928.

ELIAS, DAVID HENRY, M.C. — B. 1882; G.W.R., Eng., May, 1899-May, 1902; S. African Rlys., May, 1902-Sept., 1914; S. African Mounted Rifles, German S.W. Africa, S. African Infy. Brig., France, M.C., Sept., 1914-July, 1919; S. African Rlys., July, 1919: supl. line, Kenya and Uganda Rlys., Sept., 1922; ditto, Nigerian Rlys., Sept., 1925; dep. gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1931; gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., May, 1933.

ELKINS, MAJOR WALTER HENRY, O.B.E. (Mily.).—B. 1883; ed. Cirencester Grammar Schl.; S. African and Rhodesia Rlys.; Mesopotamia Mil. Rlys.; reorganisation rlye, Trans Caucasus; dep. dir., rly. transport, S. Russia, 1919; dep. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., 1928; ag. traffic man., Dec. 1928 to Aug. 1929; divnl. supt., F.M.S. rlys., Sept., 1932.
ELLENBERGER, VIVIAN FREDERIC.—Ck. to res. comntr., Bech. Prot., 1915; passed 2nd grade Secwana exam., 1919; sub-instr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1920; 1st grade ck., Apr., 1928; res. mag., May, 1934.

ELLIOT, JOHN AUGUSTUS GILBERT.—B. 1881; Lieut. reserve of officers; ed. privately and at Edin. Univ.; enlisted in C.I.V. (M.I.) for S. African war, Jan., 1900; 2nd lieut. R. Scots Fus., Aug., 1900; dangerously wounded at battle of Frederikstad, Oct., 1900; 1st lieut., May, 1903; served with 8th and 4th regts. of M.I., S. Africa, 1903-4; intell. dept., S. Africa, 1904-1906; rejoined 2nd R. Scots Fus., Salisbury Plain, June, 1906; M.I. certif.; musk. certif., Hythe, 1907; pub. health certif., Edin. Univ., 1908; asst. dist. comntr., R. Africa Prot., 14th Apr., 1909; dist. comntr., Oct., 1917.

ELLIOTT, COLIN FRASER (Fraser), K.C., B.A., So., C.E.—Solr., income tax br., dept. of finance, Canada, July, 1919; asst. comntr., income tax, dept. of national rev., May, 1926; comntr., income tax, July, 1932.

ELLIOTT, FRANK FIELD.—Tempy., asst. regiry., survr., O.F.S., Mar., 1903; asst. regiry., survr., May, 1903; chief draughtsman and compt., Apr., 1912; add. prof. ass., Apr., 1914; prof. ass., Sept., 1917; survr.-gen., O.F.S., Nov., 1930; do., Cape Prov., Dec., 1931.
ELLIOTT, PHILIP JOHN.—B. 1892; entd. crown agents' office, Dec., 1911; asst. head, dept., July, 1927; dep. head, stores dept., Nov., 1933.

ELLIS, CAPT. FREDERICK ROBBINS, M.C., Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1924.—B. 1891; ed. Blundell's Schl., Tiverton; 2nd lieut., 4th batt., Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy.; M.C.; served in Mesopotamia with 2nd Dorset Regt., 1915-16; prisoner of war on capitulation of Kut-el-Amarah, 1916; escaped from custody, 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; Siberia, 1919; passed solrs. final exam.; junr. admin. offr., S. Leone, 1920; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Jan. to Apr., 1921; J.P., 1921; ag. asst. col. sec., 1921; ag. comntr., pol., 1921; ag. dist. comntr., headqrs. dist., 1921-23; dist. comntr., Aug., 1928.

ELLIS, JOHN MEDICOTT, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1895; ed., Haileybury and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schl.); served, Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914-19; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, June, 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek, March, 1920 and Turkish, July, 1921; ch. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1923-25; ag. col. sec., Falkland Is., Sept., 1926 to Aug., 1927; col. sec., Aug., 1927; ag. gov., in 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1931; local audr. from May, 1930 to Feb., 1931; regist.-gen. and mag. from July, 1930 to Jan., 1931; col. sec. and regist.-gen., Grenada, Oct., 1934.
ELLISON, F. O'BRIEN, M.D., B.S., B.A.O.—B. 1879; prof. of physiology and regiar., Ceylon Med. Coll., Oct., 1923.
ELPHINSTONE, SIR H. G., BART.—Asst. diet. comntr., Kenya, June, 1921.

ELPHINSTONE, SIR LANCELOT HENRY, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's inn, 1904; attv.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; solr.-gen., Trinidad, 1919; attv.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; puisne judge, designate S. Stthlas., 1924; ag. solr. gen., Ceylon, June, 1924; attv.-gen., Oct., 1924; ch. just., F.M.S., Apr., 1929; ret., 1932.

EMBURY, BRIG.-GEN. HON. JOHN FLETCHER LEOPOLD, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; served European War, 25th Can. Inf. 13th Inf. Bde., 2nd Inf. Bde., Can. Contingent; Br. G.H.Q., France (Canadian sec.), 1914-19; judge of King's Bench Saskatchewan, 1918.
EMBURY, W. E.—B. 1898; on mil. serv., Oct., 1916 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Dec., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. ord., 8th Nov., 1921.
EMERSON, HON. CHARLES H., K.C.—B. 1894; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Burgeo and La Poile, 1900, Fortune Bay, 1908 and 1917; mem. exec. coun. without portfolio, 1919-20; regar. supreme ct., 1917.

EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; depl. ast., 26th Jan., 1922; promoted ag. asst. prin. under clause 51, 4th Sept., 1929.

KMPSON, CHARLES.—B. 1898; ed. Harrow and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; R.F.A. (S.R.); joined Iraq civil admin., Aug., 1930; adm. under sec. to high commr., Sept., 1931; under sec. (consular), July, 1922; H.M.'s consul, Bagdad, Dec., 1924; consular sec., July, 1927.

ENGLAND, WILLIAM WENTWORTH.—Edt. govt. ser., Antigua, Feb., 1907; clk. to regist. wr. of births and deaths; ag. treas. offr., Montserrat, Jan. to Dec., 1908; ag. clk. to comntr., Virginia, Mar. to Aug., 1909.
ENGLISH, JOSEPH SANDERS, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M. (Rotunda Hosp., Dub.), F.C.O.G.—B. 1890; prof. of midwifery and gynaecology, King Edward VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Jan., 1922.

ENGLISH, PATRICK THOMAS.—B. 1902; clk., treas., Montserrat, 1921; clk. to mag. and regir., 1924; ag. offr. in ch., prison, 1924; sec. clk., treas., 1925; ag. regir., sup. ct., 1925; pr. clk. col., sec.'s office, Antigua, 1927; ag. clk. couns., 1928 and 1930; ag. clk. to admin. m. clk. of couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1930; ag. m. col. sec., Leeward Is., and clk., fed. couns., 1930; clk. and cust. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Co., 1931; ag. acct., 1931-33; ag. sec. to govt., Jan., Aug., 1933.
ENGLISH, ROBERT.—B. 1888; asst. printer, Tanganyika Territory, 1919; secr. ast. to govt. govt. printer, Sierra Leone, 1930; do., Nigeria, 1932.

EPHRAIM, HERBERT COLEMAN, K.C., LL.B. (Manitoba).—B. 1890; ed. public and high schl. Ampryor and Carleton Place, Ont., and Univ. of Manitoba; barrister-at-law, Manitoba, 1922; Kt. (Dom.), 1929; sec.-treas., Canadian Bar Assn., 1919-33; dean, Manitoba Law Schl., 1925-28; bencher, Law Soc. of Manitoba, 1928-33; mem. sec. of state and dep. regir.-gen., Canada, 1930.

ERAUT, WALTER, C.B.E. (1935), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1874; ed. Aske's Haberdashers' Company Schl. and Imperial Coll. of Science; engnr. ast., Crown Agents, Mar., 1901; ast. b. engrng. contracts dept., 1913; ch. engr., contracts, 1922.

ERNST, NOEL EDWARD.—B. 1891; ed. local division, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1912; attld., Ratnapura kach., May, 1912; Tracayu kach., Mar., 1913; office ast. to govt. ast., Prov., July, 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kandy Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Avisawella, June, 1915; ast. comntr., excise, Nov., 1916; pol. ma... Gampola, Mar., 1918; office astt. to gov't. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1918; pol. mag., Galle, Oct., 1924; dep. comsnr., stamps, Feb., 1928; comanr., do., July, 1931; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Mar., 1933; astt. gov't. agt., Kurunegala, May, 1934.

ESPEUT, CLAUDE VIVIAN ARMIT., M.Inst., C.E.—B. 1875; ed. St. Paul's Schil., Lond. and Crystal Palace Schl. of Engnr.; served, P.W.D., Jamaica; astt. engr., Lagos, govt. rly., 1900-01; dist. engr., Gold Coast Govt. rly., 1901; exec. engr., P.W.D. E. Africa Prot., June, 1905; ag. astt. D.P.W., 1908 and 1909; D.P.W., Uganda Prot., 1911; Uganda Volunteers, 1914-19; hon. lt.-col., 1916; miny of munitions while on leave, 1917; D.P.W., Jamaica, Aug., 1921; mem., leg. coun., D.P.W., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; mem., fed. coun.; chm. electrical bd. and others; mem., rly. bd., immigr. comm. and others; ret. July 1928; re-employed as eng. in ch., colonial development fund wks., St. Lucia, Oct., 1930.

ESPITALIER-NOEL, JOSEPH GEORGES.—B. 1894; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, May, 1916; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, 1919; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Sept., 1921; addnl. subst. procureur and advoc. gen., Dec., 1922; ag. subst. procureur and advoc. gen. on various occasions, 1924-29; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., Mar., 1929; ag. mast. and regisr., supt. ot., Aug., 1929 to Feb., 1932 and Sept., 1932; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct. In 1932 and 1933.

EVANS, ARTHUR ANTHONY, O.B.E., M.C., M.L.E.E., Lieut., Col., R. Corps of Signals (Res.)—Ed. Brodribb Acad., Carmarthen and Univ. of Wales, Cardiff; served in S. African War (Queen's med., 4 clasps); engnr. branch, G.P.O., 1898-1914; R.E., signals, Gallipoli, Egypt; astt. dir., Army signals and ch. signal offr., Palestine and Syria, 1914-20; four ment. in desps., Order of White Eagle, 5th cls. with swords, O.B.E., M.C., Br. and Allies war meda., "1914-15" Star; ch. engnr., Palestine posts and tels., 1920.

EVANS, BENJAMIN DAVIES, F.R.A.S.—B. 1887; computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1902; junior astt., Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1909; 1st astt., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1912; on military service, 1917 to 1919; ag. ch. astt. for various periods in 1915, 1916, 1920, 1921, 1925, 1926 and 1929; astt. dir., 1932.

EVANS, EDWARD WALTER, B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1890; ed. Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class mode., 1st class lit. hum.; astt. dist. comanr., Zanzibar Prot., Feb., 1915; seconded for mil. ser., June, 1917; temp. comanr. in K.A.R.; ag. 1st astt. sec.; Zanzibar, 1919-20; astt. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1921; senr. astt. sec., June, 1923; ag. astt. ch. sec., July, 1924; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1926; ag. col. sec., Jan. to Mar., 1927; col. sec., Br. Honduras, June, 1927; admstd. gov't., on two occasions; col. sec., Bermuda, 1929; col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1932; admstd. gov't., Aug., 1932 to Apr., 1933.

EVANS, FRANK DUDLEY, M.Inst., C.E.—B. 1883; astt. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S. 1907; malaria adv. bd., 1911; exec. engr., grade III, 1913; ag. regtl. sergt. maj., M.S.V.R., 1917; 2nd lieut. ad., 1917; engnr. malaria adv. bd., 1919; ment. in desps., 1919; lieut., M.S.V.R., 1919; anti-malaria engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1921; awarded Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., 1921; ag. astt. D.P.W., 1924; ag. state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, 1924; ag. capt., M.S.V.R., 1924; visited anti-malaria wks., Palestine and Italy, 1926; D.D.P.W., Nigeria, 1926; ag. D.P.W., and M.L.C., Nigeria on various occasions 1926-33; malarial comanr., health sect., L. of N., Geneva, 1928.

EVANS, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.V.O. (1912), C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903), C.B.E. (1918); Comdr. of Order of Isabel la Catolica (Spanish), 1912; Knight Comdr., ditto, 1914.—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., adm'y., Somerset house, Dec., 1867; transf'd to sec.'s dept at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; astt. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1879 to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; astt. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr., cust. and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883 and on other occasions; clk. legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov', Lagos, July to Aug., 1883 and May, 1885 to Jan., 1886; dep. gov', G. Coast, Sept., 1883 and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. admnstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1895; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901; ag. gov', on seven occasions; ret., 1914; ag. col. sec., Gibraltar, in 1915 and 1917.
EVANS, H. G.—Astt. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Apr., 1913; 1st grade admstve. offr., 1925.

EVANS, HON. SIR JOHN WM., KT. BACH. (1926), C.M.G. (1906)—B. 1856; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1896; warden and master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educn., Tasmania, July, 1904 to Oct., 1905; premier and treas., Oct., 1905, to May, 1906; premier and ch. sec., May, 1906 to June, 1909; speaker.

EVANS, LEONARD WINNIE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., B.Sc., certif. L.S.T.M. (with distinc.).—B. 1891; med. offr., gen. hosp., S'pore, Jan., 1922; med. and health offr., P.W., north and central, June, 1923; ch. med. offr., Kelantan, Jan., 1929.

EVANS, SAMUEL, Hon. LL.D., Witwatersrand Univ.—B. 1859; pvt. sec. to finan. adviser, Egyptian govt., 1883; ch. contr., Egyptian coast guard serv., 1886; inspr.-gen., Imp. Ottoman Bank, Constantinople, 1889; man., Turkish Tobacco Regie, 1892; during Anglo-Boer War served on staff of Lord Roberts from Kroonstad to Johannesburg, and on staff of mil. gov. of Johannesburg as civ. comanr.; medal, 3 bars; pub. debt comanr., Union of S. Africa.

EVANS, SAMUEL ALAN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1899; ed. Mountjoy Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., R.A.F., cadet, 1918; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1921; astt. vety. pathologist, May, 1926.

EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to regisr., St. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apr., 1903; govt. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1905; clk., regisr.'s off., May, 1906; regisr. of births and deaths, St. George's parish; ag. ch. clk., regisr.'s offr., Antigua, Dec., 1910, to July, 1911; ag. regisr. and provost marshal for several periods since 1913; regisr. and provost marshal and regisr. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1922; prov. offr. mem., leg. coun., Oct., 1923.

EVELYN, EDWARD A. (jr.)—B. 1904; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl.; supernymy.rev.offr., 1922; ch. clk., post office, 1927; dep. habmr. and cashier, treas., 1928; govt. offr., Eastern Dist., Dominica, 1930; ag. mag. dist. F, also addnl. mag., dist. E, 1933; J.P., 1934.

EVELYN, MERRIT.—2nd clk. to pres., St. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treas., Nevis, 1881; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in charge, Nevis treas., 1883; prot. of imgrts., Nevis, 1883; land and house tax commnr., 1892; ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1895-6 and 1912; water commnr., Nevis, 1901; chmn., quarantine bd., Nevis, 1911.

EWART, F. K.—B. 1902; ed. Botelor Schl., Warrington and Rossall Schl., Williams Mem. Exh.; schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920; pres., O.U.S.C., 1923; B.A., 1923 (mem. pt. 1); F.C.S., 1923; 2nd chem., pt. 2, 1924; mem., Inner Temple, 1925; asst. mast., King's Coll., Hongkong, 1926; M.A., 1927.

BYBERS, GEORG VON WELFING.—B. 1887; ed. Boy's High Schl., Beaufort West, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Univ. of Amsterdam and King's Coll., London; B.A. (Cape); M.A., D. Litt. (Lond.); prin., Graaf Reinet Coll. and Hoët Volkakool, 1919-26; mem., Cape provin. educn. commn., 1926-27; inspr., schls., 1927; under sec. educn., Aug., 1927; prof. asst., Union educ. dept., July, 1934; author, various works on S. African hist. and travel.

EYRE-SMITH, CAPT. ST. JOHN ROBERT, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. St. Edmunds Coll., Ware; served with R.A.S.C., France and Germany, 1915-19; Turkey, 1919-20; ment. in despe.; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commnr., 1925; asst. sec., native affrs., 1931.

EZCHIEL, PERCY HUBERT, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1876; B.A. Bombay, 1889 (ct. 14); B.A., London, 1894; major schrl., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., O.C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; sec. to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; mem. of Fair Wages Advisory Comtee., 1909-1920; sec. to Colonial Economic Development Comtee., 1919-20; sec. and mem., E. African Currency bd., 1919 (resigned secretarieship 1921); crown agt. for the cols., Dec., 1920; dir. of colonial scholars, 1921; rep. of C.O. on exec. coun. of Br. Empire Exhibn., 1921-25; chmn., Palestine currency bd., 1926; mem., Hong Kong currency comm., 1931.

FAHEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1878; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, Canada; printer; arrived S. Africa, 1898; mem., prov. coun., Natal, 1920-23; prov. exec., 1924; joined bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1924; re-apptd., Apr., 1930; chmn., Mar., 1932.
FAIR, HON. ARTHUR, LL.B. (N.Z.), K.C.—B. 1885; ed. state schls., Nelson Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; crown solr., 1921; prin. law offr., 1923; solr.-gen., 1925; judge, sup. ct., N.Z., 1934.

FAIR, LIEUT.-COL. J. G., D.S.O.—B. 1864; entd. Army (21st Hussars—now Lancers) 1886; capt., 1892; major, 1900; staff offr. to Gen. Gatacre during Athbara campaign, 1898 (desps., Khedive's medal and clasp); with 21st Lancers at Omdurman (Queen's medal, clasp, 4th cls. Medjidieh); adjut., cavalry depot, Canterbury, 1899-1900; S. African war, 1901-2 (deaps., D.S.O., Queen's medal, 5 claps); commanded div. S.A.C., Eastern Transvaal, 1901-4; ditto, O.R.C., 1904-8; nom. mem. of I.C.C., 1906; res. commnr. and comdt.-gen., Southern Rhodesia, 1908-1911; dep. ast. dir. of remounts since 1911.

FAIRBAIRN, SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1926).—B. 1855; ed. Geelong Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; M.L.A. for Toorak, Victoria, Oct., 1903 to Sept., 1906; M.H.R. for Fawkner, 1906-13; Commonwealth Senator for Victoria, May, 1917; ast.-gen. for Victoria, 1924-27.
FAIRBAIRN, H., M.D., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1898; mily. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1923.

FAIRBURN, HAROLD, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1884; pol. prob., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; ast. commnr., pol., F.M.S., June, 1908; registr. crimin., F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1913, Oct., 1913 and Jly., 1915; ast. commnr., pol., ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Aug., 1917; dep. commnr., pol., F.M.S., Jan., 1921; in charge, intell. branch, F.M.S., Apr., 1921 and Feb., 1923; inspr.-gen., pol., S.S., Jly., 1925.

FAIRLEY, WILLIAM, B.A., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.) B.A., (mech. science tripos) 1913; 6th Bn. E. Surrey Regt., 1914; Ist K.A.R., 1917-19; ast. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Dec., 1919; ag. exec. engnr., Mar. to Dec., 1920; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Feb., 1922; ag. ast. D.P.W., Mar. to Oct., 1923 and 14th Mar., 1924; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Aug., 1925.
FAIRWEATHER, C.—Apptd. gov't. printer, Gold Coast, 19th Oct., 1907.

FAIRWEATHER, EUGENE ERNEST, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. at King's and Acadia Unive. and Dalhousie Law Schl., N.S.; ast. solr., dept. of rlys. and canals, 1912; solr., 1914; counsel, 1919; ast. gen. counsel, Can. Nat. rlys., 1922.

FAIRWEATHER, WILLIAM GEMMEL, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. at Univ. of St. Andrews (B.Sc.), A.M.I.C.E.; ast. survr., N.W. Rhodesia, Sept., 1910; ch. ast. survr. and examiner of diagrams, Nov., 1917; ch. survr. on various occasions, 1916 to 1920; ch. survr., 1920; dir., surveys, Oct., 1924; mem., leg. coun., 1929-32, 1933.

FALCONER, JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., Aberdeen (1st cls. hon.); cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; ast. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, June, 1924; dep. regiar., titles, Selangor, June, 1924; second ast. advr., Muar, Feb., 1926; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Aug., 1930; do., Raub, July, 1931; offr., cls. III., Apr., 1933; ast. advr., B. Pahat, June, 1934.

FALK, EDWARD MORRIS, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Bradford Grammar Schl. on the Continent and at Victoria Univ.; ast. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1907; West African G.S. Medal, 1909; dist. commnr., 2nd grade, 1913; interned as prisoner of war in Germany, 1914; escaped and retld. to England, 1915; resumed duty, Nigeria, 1915; res., 1923; staff grade, 1929; ast. admtr. of col., 1931.

FARFAN, JOSÉ MANUEL—B. 1876; 6th clk., secretariat, Trinidad, Apr., 1895; 5th clk., Aug., 1897; 4th clk., Feb., 1902; 3rd clk. and sec. to cen. road., Feb., 1906; 2nd and conf. clk., secretariat, Aug., 1915; 2nd ast. col. sec. and supr. of govt. printing, Apr., 1920; ag. 1st ast. col. sec. on several occasions since 1920; 1st ast. col. sec., Jan., 1930; ag. dep. col. sec., in 1931, 1932 and 1933.

FARMER, FRANK.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Dec., 1914; ast. clk., Apr., 1917; on mily. serv., MAR., 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted, under order in council of 1916, clerk of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Dec., 1924; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.
FARQUHAR, EDWARD ALLAN.—Commr., S. Aust. harbours bd., 1914; chmn., 1924.

FARQUHAR, GEORGE NEIL, M.C.—B. 1896; mili. serv., Dec., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to secretariat, Apr., 1920; ditto, Puttalam Kach, June, 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., 1925; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1926; sec., land commn., Aug., 1928; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1930; clk. to leg. comm., Mar., 1930; clk. to state comm., July, 1931; contr. establishments, July, 1932; contr. treas., Sierra Leone, 1934.

FARQUHARSON, ROBERT ALEXANDER, M.A. (Oxon.), M.S., F.G.S., A.O.S.M.—B. 1883; ed. Boys' High Sch., Otago Univ., N.Z.; St. John's Coll., Oxford; senr. schol., N.Z. Univ., 1905; dipl. in mining engrg., 1905; 1st cls., hona., geology and 2nd cls., physical science, N.Z., 1906; Rhodes schol., N.Z., 1906; B.A., Oxford, hona. in geology and mineralogy, 1908; M.A., 1912; govt. petrolist, mines dept., W. Australia, 1911-22; lent at request of C.O. to Somaliland govt. to furnish rept. on geological and mineral resources of Prot., 1923; govt. geologist, Somaliland, 1923-26; reed. thanks of S. of S. for geol. map of Somaliland; dir., agr. and geologist, Somaliland, 1926; geol. advr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bdry. commn., 1932-33; in ch., water boring scheme under Col. Development Act, 1932-33.
FARR, FRANK JAMES, M.R., Ch.B., L.D.S., D.M.R. & E.—B. 1900; radiologist, med. dept., Hong Kong, 1928.

FARRELL, CAPT. WILFRID JEROME, M.C., M.A., R. of O.—B. 1882; ed. Ushaw; Hymers; Jesus Coll., Cambridge; fellow of Jesus Coll., 1906; student, Br. Schl. of Archaeology, Athens, 1906; Munich Univ., 1907; on staff of Rugby Schl. and Haileybury Coll., 1911-19; served in European war, 1914-19; civ. educn. offr., Iraq, Sept., 1919; officiating dir. and adviser, min. of educn., Iraq, 1921; senr. educn. offr., Palestine, Apr., 1923; asst. dir. educn., Apr., 1927; dep. dir., Oct., 1929; ag. dir., June to Aug., 1927 and July, 1928 to Jan., 1929; Br. mag. for riot cases, Sept. to Oct., 1929.

FAULKNER, ODIN TOM, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1890; ed. St. Albans and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Schol.), B.A. (1st cls., nat. sci. tripods, 1911); dipl., agr., 1912; mycologist and asvl. expert to Rubber Growers Assocn., Malaya, 1912-14; dep. dir., agr. to govt. of Punjab 1914-21; dir., agr., Nigeria, 1921.
FAULKNER, S.N., O.B.E. (1918)—Ast. auditor R.A.P., Jan., 1909; senr. ast. auditor, Apr., 1915; dep. ch. acctnt., Uganda Rly., 1917.
FAWCETT, J. F. ST. A.—Deputy regstr., high et., E.A.P., 1910 regstr., high court, Feb., 1919; mast. and regstr., sup. ct., Sierra Leone, July, 1921; ch. regstr. and sheriff, Gold Coast, May, 1924.
FAZAN, S. H., C.B.E. (1934), O.B.E. (1930). Ast. dist. commn., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. commn., Kenya, Feb., 1920; senr. dist. commn., 1934.

FEARON, FRANK ST. CLAIR.—Ed. Charterhouse (schol.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A. (litt. hum.), 1902; clk. law dept., Cape Colony, 1903; ast. regstr., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; 1st ast. regstr. and taxing offr., 1923; regstr. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Johannesburg, 1927; ditto, Grahamstown, 1930.

FEASEY, GILBERT GEORGE.—B. 1891; ed. Westminster; served with H.A.C. and Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F.; active commn. Cameroons; ast. dist. offr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; dep. res., Apr., 1933; adm. offr., cls. I, 1935.

FEETHAM, RICHARD, C.M.G.—B. 1874; ed. Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; mem., Inner Temple; called to bar, 1899; deputy town clk., Johannesburg, 1902-3; town clk., Johannesburg, 1903-5; admitted advoc., Transvaal, 1903; legal adviser to high commn. for S. Africa, 1907-10 and 1912-13; K.C., 1919; mem., leg. comm., Transvaal, 1907-10; mem., H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1915-23; lieut., 1st battn. Cape Corps, 1916-19; mem., Southborough comte., on Indian Reforms and chmn. functions comte., 1918-19; mem., India Office comte., on Govt. of India Bill, 1919; ag. prea., sup. ct. of Swaziland, 1922-23; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal Prov. Divn., Aug., 1923; judge pres., sup. ct., Natal prov. divn., May, 1930.

FEHILY, JOSEPH PATRICK, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. N.U.I., F.A.C.S., D.P.H., Queen's Univ., Belfast.—B. 1892; ed. Blackrock Coll. and Univ. Colls., Cork and Dublin; late temp. surg., R.N. and capt., R.A.M.C.; res. med. offr., Br. Hosp., Buenos Aires, 1919; Hong Kong govt. med. serv., 1924; health offr. of port and impr., immigrts., 1931.
FELIX-JONES, IVOR, B.A.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, Mar., 1922.
FELTHAM, STANLEY CRATHERN, A.R.I.B.A., chtd. architect.—B. 1896; ed. Northern Polytechnic, London; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

FENN, EGERTON DUDLEY.—B. 1893; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Diocesan and St. Andrew's Colls., S. Africa; olk., staff of gov.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 1912-28; served, S. A. Army and lieut., R.A.F., 1915-19; sub-inspr., Swaziland pol., 1928; inspr., 1931; ch., pol., 1933.

FENTON, HON. JAMES EDWARD, M.H.R. (1934)—B. 1891; ed.; Dundee High Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. (Edin.); war service, Mesopotamia 1917-19; passed progress exam. in Mendi; ast. dist. commn., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1915; dist. commn., Apr., 1925.
FERGUSON, SIR DAVID GILBERT, KT, B.A. (1934)—B. 1861; called to the bar, 1890; judge of sup. ct., New S. Wales, Mar., 1912.

FERGUSON, HON. GEORGE HOWARD, P.C. (Can.), 1931, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1870; ed. Kempsville, Ont., High Schl., Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall; called to bar, 1894; el. to Ont. legis., g.e., 1905; re-el. g.e., 1908, 1911, 1914, 1919 and 1923; leader of conservative party since 1920; premier of Ont., 1923; high commn. for Canada in London, Nov., 1930.

FERGUSSON, GEN. SIR CHARLES, BART., G.C.B. (1932); G.C.M.G. (1924); K.C.B. (1915); K.C.M.G. (1918); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1906); D.S.O. (1898), D.L., J.P.—B. 1865; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; joined Grenadier Guards, 1883; adjutant, 1890-94; joined Egyptian Army, 1895; Sudanese campaigns, 1896-98; severely wounded, Rossires (med. with eight clasps, ment. five times in desps.); commanded 15th Sudanese, 1899 (2nd cls., Medjidie); garrison and dist., Omdurman, 1900; adjt.-gen., Egyptian Army, 1901-3; commanded 3rd Batt. Grenadier Guards, 1904-7; brig.-gen., gen. staff, Irish commd., 1907-8; inspr., infy., 1909-13; served European War, 1914-18; in command, 5th divn. and subsequently of 2nd and 17th Army Corps; mily. gov., occupied German territory, 1918-19 (seven times ment. in desps.); gov. and commdr-in-ch., New Zealand, Oct., 1924; assumed govt., Dec., 1924; ret., 1930; chmn., W. Indian Closer Union Comsn., 1932.

FERNANDO, SIR HILARIO MARCUS, KT. BACH. (1923), M.D., B.Sc. (Lond.), Fellow of Univ. Coll. (London); J.P.—B. 1864; ed. at the Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. Scholar and Gilchrist Scholar for the East, 1882-83; 2nd medical entrance scholar, Univ. Coll., 1884; Atchison scholar, 1888, 1st cls. hons. and gold medal, physiology, medicine, and forensic medicine (Univ. of London); asst. col. surg. and regir., Ceylon med. coll., 1890; 1st physician, general hosp., and lecturer in medicine, Ceylon med. coll., 1898; reported on outbreak of malaria in Galle, 1894, and plague in Bombay, 1897, (rec. thanks of govt.); ret. from govt. serv., 1909; served on Colombo lake development, railway traffic, education, opium, prevention of tuberculosis, and several other commissions and comtees of enquiry (1909-1918); nominated mem. of legis. coun. of Ceylon as the first low-country Sinhalese representative, Jan., 1917; nom. unoff. mem. leg. coun., 1921-1924; ditto, exec. coun. from 1921.

FERNANDO, PETER OLIVER.—B. 1898; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. to Kurunegalla kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Feb., 1922; ditto, Jaffna kach., Mar., 1923; pol. mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1924; attd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1925; office asst., ditto, Mar., 1926; pol. mag., Panadura, Dec., 1926; office asst., Mannar kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1929; asst. attlmt. offr., Jan., 1930 and May, 1934; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1933.

FERNANDO, SELLAPRUMAGA SHELTON CLARENCE, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1907; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1931; stdt. ch. sec's office, Dec., 1931; attd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1932; office asst., Matale kach., Mar., 1933.

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, KT. BACH. (1930).—Ed. Bedford Schl.; called to Irish bar, 1903; crown coun., Ireland, 1912-21; mem. coun. of bar of Ireland and of standing comte.; K.C., 1919; coun. to mily. during Sinn Fein rebellion, 1916; coun. to atty.-gen., 1919; coun. to crown, 1920; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1922; judge, high ct., Punjab, 1922-31; pres., special cts., Swaziland and Bechuanaland Prot., jud. commr., Basutoland, and legal advr. to high commr. for S. Africa, July, 1934.

FIDDIES, SIR GEORGE VANDELEUR, G.C.M.G. (1917), K.C.M.G. (1912), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905). B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888 to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and accntnt. to Sir A. Milner, high commr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; politica sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.; accounting offr., 1907; asst. under-sec. of state, 20th June, 1909; chmn., West African currency board, 1912-16; perm. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 10th Mar., 1916; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; ret., 1921.

FIDDIAN, ALEXANDER.—B. 1875; ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1893; 1st class classical honours, 1895; Goldsmiths' co. exhib., 1895; 1st class lit. hum., 1897; B.A. 1897; apptd., after compet. exam. 2nd class clk. C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; asst. priv. sec. to Rt. Hon.
