Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apr., 1905; ditto to Earl of Elgin, Dec., 1905; in cls. clk., Jan., 1907; mem. of deptm't. comte. of W. African med. staff, 1909; principal clk., Nov., 1917; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; estab. offr. for C.O. and Dominions office, 15th July, 1925.

FIELD, EDGAR JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls. and Christian Brothers' Coll., Adelaide; ent. survey dept., S. Australia, Jan., 1891; clk. office of commr. of crown lands, 1892; ch. clk. and acct., 1909; ch. immgr. offr., 1911; sec. for land chmn. of advances to settlers bd. and recr. of rents, 2nd Apr., 1917; chmn., irrigtn. commn., S. Aust., 1923; dir., lands, 1931.

FIELD, HENRY TREVOR CROMWELL.—B. 1891; ed. St. John's Schl., Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); on war serv., 1914-18; demob. (capt.) 1919; inspr. and schoolmast., S.P. Nigeria, 1922; supt., educn., 1926; prin., govt. coll., Ibadan, 1932.

FIELDGATE, ALAN FREDERIC EDMUND, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1889; ed. Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast (Colony), Apr., 1910; ag. dist. commr., on various occasions, 1918-21; dist. commr., Aug., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Mar. to June, 1925; prov. commr., 1934.

FIENNES, LIEUT.-COL. THE HON. SIR EUSTACE EDWARD TWISLETON-WYKHAM, B.A.—B. 1864; Lieut.-Col., Oxfords Imp. Y.C.; served Riel rebellion, 1885 (medal with clasp); Exp. 1887 and 1888 (medal with clasp, bronze star; pioneer expdn. to Mashonaland, 1890; Sout Africa, 1900 (medal with three clasps; twice ment. in desps.); European war, 1914 (France, Belgium, Dardanelles and Home); M.P. (L) Banbury div. of Oxfordshire, 1906-1910, and 1910-1918; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Seychelles, 6th July, 1918; assumed govt., 8th Oct., 1918; gov., Leeward Is., 29th Oct., 1921; assumed govt., 4th Dec., 1921; ret., 1929.
FINCH, A.—Asst. supt., pol. Kenya, Dec., 1922; supt., pol. 1929.

FINCH, FRANK GEORGE.—B. 1882; ast engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1907; ast. ast engrnr., Kedah, 1910-15; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., 1913; state engrnr., Pahang, 1923; state engrnr., Selangor, Mar., 1927; dep. dir., pub. wo. F.M.S., July, 1928; dir., advr., drainage ac. irrign., S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

FINDLAY, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1886; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl., Aberdeen Univ.; North of Scotland Coll. of Agr.; M.A., B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A., N.D.D.; ast. agr., Nigeria, 1912; senr. supt., 1922; ast. dir., 1927; dep. dir., 1930; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1931; M.L.C., 1932.

FINDLAY, GEORGE HUGO, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1888; ed. Repton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; ast dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; asst. sec., S. Prova., 1922; cls. I., grade I., admistr. serv., 1928; ag. admistr. of colony, Lagos, 1930; ag. prim. asst. sec., 1930; ag. sec., S. Prova., 1931; staff grade, 1932; ag. commnr., col. and lands, 1934; ag. ch. sec., 1934.
FINLAYSON, D. A.—B. 1892; home cust. and excise serv., 1912-24; war serv. in R.N., 1918; collr., cust., Sierra Leone, 1924; dep. dir., dept. of cust., excise and trade, Palestine, 1928.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE DANIEL.—B. 1882; ed. Pictou Acad. and Dalhousie Univ.; B.A., 1907; apptd. to dept. of insurance, Canada, 1907; associate of Inst. of Actuaries of Great Britain, 1911; supt. of insurance, Canada, 1914.
FIRR, TOM FREEMAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Crystal Palace Engng. Schl.; 1st asst. survr., Nyamaland Prot., June, 1903; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1909.

FIRTH, OWEN WATTS.—B. 1884; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; asst. dist. commnr., Nigeria, 1911; polit. offr., Udi-Okiwag Patrol, 1915; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1919; cls. I., grade I., admistr. serv., 1927; ag. prim. asst. sec., S. Prova., 1928; staff grade, 1932.
FISCHER, PERCY U., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1878; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontien, S.A. Coll., Cape Town, Trinity Hall, Camb.; judge, sup. ct., South Africa, 1929.

FISET, MAJOR-GEN. SIR EUGENE, KT. BACH (1917), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O., G.G.H.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1874; staff, adjt. med. corps., Canada, 1902; dir.-gen., med. services, 1903; dep. min., militia and defence, vice-pres., the militia coun. since 1906; recd. D.S.O. for service in S. Africa; mentioned in despatches; ret., 1924; mem. for county of Rimouski.

FISHER, BERTIE ST. JULIAN.—B. 1885; Fiji serv., 1909; dist. commnr., 1918; legal dept., 1920; ag. dep. regr.-genl. and regir., sup. ct., 1921; ag. regir., sup. ct., curator of intestate and vacant estates, pub. trustee and offl. recr. in bankruptcy, 1923-27; regir.-gen. and regir., titles, 1929; regir., sup. ct., pub. trustee, offl. recr. in addn., Jan., 1931.

FISHER, HARRY CAMPBELL, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1880; ed. at Oakham Schl. and Sid. Suss. Coll., Cambridge; clk., coll. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., July, 1902; asst. auditor, Lagos, Sept., 1903; detached for duty in N. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; asst. aud., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1905; local audr., July, 1909; examnr., coll. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Feb., 1910; 1st div. clk. (2nd cls.), central office, May, 1910; aud., Malta, Mch., 1913; mem. of control board and hd. of food and commerce control office, Malta, 1917; senr. clk., cent. office, May, 1918; asst. dir., coll. audit, Apr., 1928; deputy dir., Oct., 1929.
FISHER, HAROLD MAYSTON, L.D.S., R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1893; mil. serv., 1914-19; dental surg., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; senr. dental surg., 1930.

FISHER, REV. ROBERT, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. Cambridge; asst. mast., educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1921; house mast., 1922; mast., Ashimota Coll., 1926; supt., educn., S. Prova., Nigeria, 1928; prin., govt. coll., Umunia, 1928.

FISHER, SIR STANLEY, KT. BACH (1922).—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford. M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); supp. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrena, Cyprus, May, 1902; puisane judge, Cyprus, 1911; ch. just., Cyprus, 1919; on sp. mission to Cairo under F.O. 24th Jan. to 14th Mar., 1924; ch. just., Trinidad, 1924; ch. just., Ceylon, 1926; ret., Aug., 1930; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec. 1903 and in Turkish, 1918; joint commnr. for compiling revised edition of Statute Laws of Cyprus (published 1907), for which recd. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.; joint commnr. under reprint of statutes law, 1913; edition published, Feb., 1914; commnr. for laws, under reprint of statutes law, 1922; edition published, 1924; compiler of "The Mukhtar's Handbook," 1911; published "Ottoman Land Laws," 1919.
FISHER, V. M.—Medical offr., E.A.P., Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1926; prin. lab. inspr., Feb., 1928.

FITCHETT, WILLIAM HERBERT.—C.I.K., agric. dept., Cape Colony, Feb., 1895; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Wodehouse, Sept., 1898; Port Elizabeth, Oct., 1899; Colesberg, Mar., 1900; Britstown, Aug., 1901; acted as C.C. and R.M., Brittstown, 1902; asst. regir., high ct., and chief clk. to the sheriff of the O.R.C., July, 1902; acted as regir. and sheriff in 1903 and 1904; regir. and sheriff, O.F.S., Aug., 1921.

FITZGERALD, R. DESMOND, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin), D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.), certif. (hons.), L.S.T.M., M.C.—B. 1885; ed., Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.O., Penang, July, 1912; do., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1912; health offr., Malacca, Sept., 1914; med. offr., gen. hosp., Spore, Sept., 1914; served in European war, 1914-19 (ment. in desps., M.C.); med. offr., Tan Took Seng hosp. and surg., native hosp., Spore, Dec., 1919; M.O., gen. hosp., Spore, Apr., 1920; med. offr. in ch., officials, pol. surg. and prison surg., Jan., 1921; prin. med. offr., Johore, June, 1923; offl. del., Far Eastern Assn. Trop. Med., Calcutta, Dec., 1927; dep. dir. med. and health services, S.S., Feb., in 1932 and 1933; dir. med. and health services, S.S. and advr., med. and health services, F.M.S., 1934.
FITZGERALD, THOMAS, O. O.B.E. (1927).—F.M.S. postal service; dep. P.M.G., Kenya, July, 1920; P.M.G., Jan., 1922; P.M.G., Kenya-Uganda-Tanganyika Territory postal and tel. service, 1933.

FITZGERALD, WILLIAM JAMES.—ed. Blackrock Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; M.C. and Croix de Guerre (French); admistr. serv., Nigeria, 1919; crown coun., Nigeria, 1924; solr.-gen., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932; atty.-gen., July, 1933; ag. judge, 1934.

FITZGIBBON, HENRY ELLIOT.—B. 1889; ed. St. Stephen's Green Schl., Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., B.A.I., Assoc. M. Inst. C.E., M. Inst. M. & Cy. E., M.T.P.I.; inspr., municipalities, Egypt, 1913; war serv. with E.E.F. (ment. in desps.); dir., irrig. and mech. dept., min. of wafks., Egypt, 1919; Order of the Nile, 4th cls.; munici. and town planning engr., Kenya, 1928; Pickering prize and gold medal., Inst M. and Cy. E., 1931.

FITZ-GIBBON, WILLIAM GUERRIN.—B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland; B. 1885; rly. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st clk., col. secretariat, Hong Kong, 1917; ag. ch. clk., col. secretariat, 1921 and 1924; J.P., 1924; sec., P.W.D., 1927.

FITZ-HENRY, WILLIAM COOPER, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Dulwich Coll.; asst. engr., Nigerian rly., 1908; lieut. R.E., 1915; capt., R.E., 1916; served with miny. of munitions and admly., 1918; in India on rly. work, 1920-27; road engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927; ch. road engr., N. Rhodesia, July, 1928; ag. D.P.W., Aug., 1930.
FITZMAURICE, ALEXANDER LINDSAY—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (1900); M.B., B.S. (Lond.), 1913; late out-patient offr., asst. house surg. and house physician, Guy's Hosp.; med. offr. Somaliland Prot., 1914.

FITZPATRICK, RIGHT HON. SIR CHARLES, P.C. (1908); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1907).—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl., 1896, again in 1900 and 1904; solr.-gen., Canada, 1896; min. of just., 1902; ch. just. of Canada, 1906-1918; mem. of Hague tribunal of arbitration, 1908; lieut.-gov. of Quebec, 1918 to 1923.

FITZPATRICK, JAMES PATRICK, L.R.C.P. and S. (Ireland).—B.1885; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; ag. med. supt., Central Mental Hosp., T. Rambutan, May-Dec., 1929; med. offr., Seremban, Jan., 1931; do., L. Perak, Jan., 1932; do., Larut, Sept., 1932.
FLANAGAN, MAURICE JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab).—Asst. audr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; audr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; audr., Windward Is., June, 1919; audr., Nyasaland, Oct., 1925.

FLAVIN, FRANK.—B. 1888; Imp. posts and tel., July, 1905; postmstr., Nyasaland, 26th Sept., 1919; ag. asst. postmstr.-gen., Mar., 1922 to Feb., 1923; asst. postmstr.-gen., 3rd Feb., 1923; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1923 to June, 1924, Feb., 1927 and June 1929 to Apr., 1930.

FLECK, FRANK.—B. 1875; clk., educ. dept., O.F.S., July, 1897; clk., orphan mast., O.F.S., Oct., 1899; clk. (first), educ. dept., O.F.S., Feb., 1901; clk., atty.-gen.'s office, O.F.S., Sept., 1901; clk., master's office, high ct., Bloemfontein, 1902; asst. res. mag., Vredefont dist. (Parys), June, 1902; asst. res. mag., Fauresmith dist., Aug., 1903; asst. res. mag., Viertfontein, Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag., Bothaville, Feb., 1910; asst. res. mag., Reitz, Apr., 1912; detached asst. mag., Dewetsdorp, Nov., 1913; detached asst. mag., Zastron, Feb., 1919; mag., Zastron, Oct., 1919; pub. serv. inspr., Pretoria, May, 1928; dir., prisons, Pretoria, Nov., 1929; mag., Pretoria, Sept., 1932.

FLECKER, ORIEL JOYCE, B.Sc.—Ed. Cheltenham Ladies' Coll.; assoc. of Cheltenham Ladies' Coll., 1908; gold med., Société Nationale des Professeurs de Français en Angleterre, 1909; B. Sc., Lond., 1912; Somerville Coll., Oxford, 1924-5; headmistress, girls' schl., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925; author of "A School Chemistry," 1923.

FLEMING, EDWARD DONOVAN.—B. 1998; ed. Bradford Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Sept., 1917; Salonika, Dec., 1917-Feb., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921, attd., Chinese prot., K. Lumpur; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. prot., Chinese, Taiping, May, 1930 and supt., convict estabmt., in addn., Nov., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933; prot., Chinese, July, 1934.

FLEMING, ERNEST.—B. 1891; 5th class clk., crown agts. office, Oct., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd division of the civ. serv. and temporarily employed in the Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1912; assigned to C.O., April, 1912; on military service from 25th May, 1915 to 9th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; 2nd asst. acctnt., 22nd Oct., 1930.

FLEMING, COL. JOHN GIBSON, C.B.E. (1919), D.S.O. (1916), late R.E.—B. 1880; ed. Haileybury Coll. and R. Milly Acad., Woolwich; joined R.E.'s 1888; served S. African War, 1901-2 (Queen's med. 5 clasps); European War, 1914-18 (desp., D.S.O., bt. lt.-col., C.B.E.); ret. with rank of col., 1924; apptd. fourth Crown Agent and engrn.-in-ch., 1st June, 1932.
FLEMING, W. P.—B. 1887; apptd. after compet. exam., S. and T. elk., G.P.O.; lent to C.O., 9th May, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Sept., 1921; on mil. serv., 4th Apr., 1916 to 4th May, 1917.

FLETCHER, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE MURCHIES, K.C.M.G. (1930), KT. BAOF (1929), C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhib.), cadet, Hong Kong, 1910; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, May-June, 1903; ag. asst. regar. gen., 1903; J.P., 1914; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1905-8-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. recvr., 1909; ass. to atty.-gen., 1909; regar., trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911; ag. registrar, 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. recvr., 1912; ag. ass. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1912; ag. treasur., 1913; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G. and to gov., Aug.-Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns. in 1916, 1917, 1918, 1919, 1920 and 1922; chmn., shipping control comittee., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1923 and 1925; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1926; O.A.G., May-Aug., 1927; gov., Fiji and high commr., W. Pacific, 1929.

FLETCHER COOKE, JOHN.—B. 1911; Barham schol., Malvern Coll., 1926; senr. exhib., St. Edmund Hall, Oxford, 1929; Kitchener schol.; 1st cls. philosophy, politics, economics, 1932; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., 1934.
FLETCHER, REGINALD H.—Joined Jamaica oiv. serv., post office dept., by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. elk., 1908; ch. elk., 1915; asst. postmstr., 1921; postmstr., Jamaica, 1925.

FLEURET, ARTHUR ISADORE.—B. 1874; mesr., C.S.O., Falkland Is., Dec., 1912; ag. jun. elk., Jan., 1914 to Sept., 1919; clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1919; elk., C.S.O., Jan., 1924; ch. elk. and elk. of couns., Jan., 1928; offr.-in-ch., secretariat, Oct., 12th to Jan., 1929 and Feb. to June, 1931; 2nd lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, Jun., 1931.

FLEURY, CAPT. ARTHUR MARLEY, O.R.E. (1932).—Second lieut., 3rd Royal Irish Fusilers, May, 1900; capt., May, 1905; lieut., W.A.F.F., July, 1900; Ashanti campaign (medal), 1900; dist. commr., N. Territories, G. Comst., Jan., 1907; local commr., Cyprus mil. police, Feb., 1910; commr., Nov., 1919; ag. local commr., pol., Apr., 1930.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.R.E. (1919).—B. 1895; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal Irish Regt. and staff, 1914-19; admtn. offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, May, 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to H. Comst., 14th Apr., 1921; ag. elk., exec. coun., Feb.-Mar., 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Apr., 1922; ag. asst. sec., July, 1922; ag. commr., Sept., 1922; ag. local commr. police, Sept., 1922; ag. asst. sec., June to July, 1923; ag. commr., Sept., 1923; soc. societies, June, 1923; regar., co-op. societies July, 1923; ag. regar., co-op. credit soc., June, 1923 to Apr., 1924 and from Oct., 1924; ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1924-27; ch. sec. in 1926 and 1927; trade commr. for Cyprus in London, July, 1927.

FLOOD, J. E. W., C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1886; ed. at Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A. (scholar univ. student), apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 10th Oct., 1910; sec. edible nuts comittee., 1916 1st cls. olk., 15th Oct., 1918; on military service from 16th Apr., 1917 to 7th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ast. sec., Feb., 1926; vice-chmn., col. advisory med. and sany. comtee., 1924-26; mem. W. Afr. Currency Board, 1928.

FLOUD, SIR FRANCIS LEWIS CASTLE, K.C.B. (1921), C.B. (1918)—B. 1875; ed. Cranleigh Schl. and King's Coll., London; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn); ent. bd. of agr., 1894; prte. sec. to Sir Thomas Elliott (1904-5), Mr. Ailwyn Fellowes (Lord Ailwyn) (1905) and Lord Carrington (Marquess of Lincolnshire) (1906-7) as presids. of bd. of agr.; head, land branch, 1907-14; ast. sec., 1914-19; perm. sec., min. of agr. and fisheries, 1920; chmn., bd. of cust. and excise, 1927; perm. sec., min. of lab., 1930; high commr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1934.
FLYNN, J. B., B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Drogheda Grammar Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, 1928.

FOLEY, GERALD ROBERT EDWARD, O.B.E., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Tipperary Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. and senr. moderator in maths., Dublin Univ.; Royal Irish Constab., 1911-22; war serv. with R. Irish Regt. in France, Palestine and Salonica (rank major); twice ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E. (mil.); ast. comm., Br. gendarmerie, Palestine, 1922; comm., Police Training Schl., 1926; dist. sup't., N. dist., 1927.

FOLLIT, HAROLD HARRY BAILEY.—B. 1883; ed. Bedford, Heidelberg and Cambridge; St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; European War, 1914-18; Egypt, Gallipoli and France; med offr., Nyasaland, 1921; senr. health offr., Tanganyika, 1928; senr. health offr., Nyasaland, 1931; ag. D.M. and S.S., May, 1933 to Feb., 1934.

FOLLOWS, CHARLES GEOFFREY SHIELD.—B. 1896; ed. Wellington; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; served in France, 1915-18, latterly as adjt.; on staff, 1918 and 1919, attached to H.Q., Southern dist., Ireland, and H.Q., Aldershot command; priv. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and elk. to couns., 1920; attd. C.O., May-Oct., 1925; ast. sec., Gibraltar, 1925; ch. ast. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. on various occasions.

FOORD, FRANCIS LATTON, C.B.E. (1934).—B. 1874; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, S. African War, 1899-1902; lieut., lst S. Staffs. regt.; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1902; inspr., 1913; ast. commr., 1914; accompanied Basuto delegations to England in 1909 and 1919; mem., poor white comm., 1918; gov't. sec., 1930; dep. res. commr. and gov't. sec., 1931; ag. res. commr., Mar. to Oct., 1932.
FORAN, WILLIAM—B. 1871; entered civ. serv., Canada, 1890; 2nd cl. clk., 1894; sec., bd. of civ. ser. examiners, 1895; 1st cl. clk., 1906; sec. of civ. ser. comm., 1908.

FORBES, RT. HON. GEORGE WILLIAM, P.C. (1930).—B. 1869; ed. Roy's High Schl., Christchurch; mem., N.Z. parl., since 1908; senr. whip, Liberal party, 1912-22; leader of opposition, 1925; leader of National party, 1925-28; min., lands and agr., 1928-30; prime min., min. of finance, external affrs., cust., and stamp duties, 1930; prime min., min. of rlys. and min. of external affrs. in coalition min., 1931; atty.-gen., 1933; rep. N.Z., Imp. Confce., 1930 and World Economic Confce., 1933.

FORBES, HARRY.—B. 1873; cust. dept., Fiji, 1911; clk. of peace, Rewa, Fiji, 1914-18; J.P., 1915; cashier, treas., 1918; ag. acct., Gilbert & Ellice Col., June, 1921; ag. treas., ch. postmr. and collr. of cust., June-Dec., 1922; acct., Br. residency, New Hebrides, Aug., 1923.

FORBES, LESLIE.—B. 1889; B.A., Oxon. (lit. hum.), 1913; cadet, F.M.S., 1913; lent to Kedah govt., 1914; sec. to adviser, Kolah, 1916; dist. offr., Dec., 1920-22; offr. dist. offr., Bentong, Feb., 1925; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; collr., land rev., Seremban, Feb., 1931; dist. offr., Klang, June, 1933.
FORBES, PAUL HENRY.—B. 1892; admiralty (war staff), Aug.-Oct., 1914; B.E.F., 1914-19; ast. ch. acct., Palestine rly., Aug., 1921; ch. acct., Aug., 1923; ch. acct., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1926.

FORDE, JOHN REID (late Capt., R.A.M.C.), M.B., B.Ch.—On mily. serv., 1915-18; apptd. to W. African med. staff (Gold Coast), 1918; senr. med. offr., 1932; transf., Gambia, 1934; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

FORRER, HENRY AUGUSTUS, M.A. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple.—B. 1886; cadet S. S. Oct., 1909; passed cadet, Aug., 1913; supernm. offr. of cls. V., Mar., 1914; attd. to censor's office, May 1915; services lent to the F.M.S., Jan., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, July, 1921; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, Dec., 1923; dep. registr., and ast. offi. assignee, Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1926; registr., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1927; registr., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1929; offr., cls. IB, Apr., 1930; dist. judge and 1st mag., S'pore, Jan., 1933.

FORREST, ARTHUR ERIC.—B. 1892; ast. audr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1915; on active service, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1916; ast. audr. in charge, Somaliland, 1917; ag. senr. ast. audr., E. Africa Prot., in 1917, 1919 and 1920; ast. audr. in charge Zanzibar, Feb., 1920; senr. ast. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1922; senr. ast. treas., Nov., 1925; treas. acct., Jan., 1926; mem. and sec., Kenya cost of living comm., 1926; ag. dep. treas., June, 1926 to Nov., 1927 and Aug., 1929 to Apr., 1930; title of subst. post changed to prin. ast. treas., 1928; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1930; ag. treas. in 1930, 1932 and 1934; treas., Oct., 1934.

FORREST, ROBERT ANDREW DERMON—B. 1893; ed. Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., 1914; calet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1919; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Jan., 1922; attd., educn. dept., Jan., 1922; ag. 2nd pol. mag., May, 1925; ast. head, sany. dept., Nov., 1925; ch. ast. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1928; ast. treas. and dep. estate duty commr., 1931; head, sany. dept., 1934.

FORSTER, BARON (cr. 1919), Rt. Hon. HENRY WILLIAM, P.C. (1917), G.C.M.G. (1920), D.L.—B. 1866; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford; M.P., Sevenoaks, Kent, 1892-1919; a lord commr. of the treasy., 1902-05; finan. sec. to the W.O., 1915-19; mem. of army council; gov'-gen. and commr.in chief, Commonwealth of Aust., 1920-25.
FORSYTH, L. E. W.—B. 1877; ag. commr., Mangrove Cay, Bahamas, 1922; Out Island commr., 4th grade, 1927; 2nd grade, July, 1927.
FOSBROOKE, HENRY ALBERT, B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Pocklington Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1931; ast. dist. offr., Aug., 1933.
FOSDIKE, ALFRED ERNEST.—Served in Bechuana land Prot. pol., 1907-1917; contrlr. of stores, 1917.
FOSTER, LIONEL HUBERT LISLE.—B. 1889; ed. Bradfield and Christ Ch. Oxford (B.A. 1912); min. of educn., Egypt, Jan., 1913; ast. res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1915; res., 1st grade, Apr., 1925;

FOSTER, ROBERT SPENCE, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1891; ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl. and St. Edmund's Hall, Oxford; 2nd lieut., K.S.L.I.; 1915; K.A.R., 1917-18; head mast., educn. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. dir., educn., Aug. to Oct., 1924; Swahili examn.; dep. dir., educn., Uganda, 1930; ag. dir., educn., in 1931 and 1934.

FOSTER, Hon. WALTER EDWARD.—B. 1874; ed. St. John Pub. and Grammar Schls.; chosen leader of opposition liberal party, New Brunswick, 1916; premier, N.B., Apr., 1917; sec. of state of Canada, Sept., 1925; senator, Dec., 1928.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, M.B.E.—B. 1880; served in S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., 3 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; Br. post office, 1897-1920; serv. in Gt. War, 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major, 1920; dep. postmtr.-gen., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. postmtr.-gen., on several occasions.

FOULGER, HARRY CECIL, M.A. (1928).—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; capt., R. Fusiliers; asst. res., Nyassaland Prot., Aug., 1914; attd., K.A.R., ag. pvt., sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1919; res., 1st grade, Jan., 1924.

FOUND, WILLIAM AMBROSE, D.Sc.—B. 1873; ed pub. schls. and Prince of Wales Coll., Prince Ed. Island; dept. marine and fisheries, Canada, 1898; supt., fisheries in combined dept., 1911; advr. to Br. plenipotentiaries when pelagic sealing treaty negotiated, 1911; mem., Canadian-American Fisheries Comsn., 1918; dep. min., fisheries, 1928.

FOURIE, THE HON. A. P. J.—Formerly mem., Cape prov. coun. and subsequently mem., H. of A. for Somerset; M.H.A., Gordonia; admstr., Prov. of the Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1926; min. of mines and industries, Aug., 1929; del., Imp. Conf., London, 1930 and del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; min., lab., commerce and industries, Apr., 1933.

FOURIE, LOUIS, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Edin. Univ.; Berlin; M.B., Ch.B. (Hons) (Conan Doyle Prizeman); D.P.H., F.Z.S., F.R.A.I., Fell. Soc. M.O.H., M.R. San. Inst., mem., Amer. Anthropol. Assocn., res. med. offr., R. Infirmary, 1905; city fever hosp., 1906; roy. hosp. sick. child., Edin., 1906-7; h.surg., Paddington Green Child. hosp., Lond., 1907; act. serv., Boer Forces, 1899-1900; D.S. Taunton, 1913-15; capt., S.A.M.C., 1916-19; M.O. to admstr., S.W.A., 1920-29; asst. health offr., Union Health Dep., 1930.
FOWLIS, HENRY GEORGE.—Entd. judl. dept., Gambia, 1890; gov'r's off., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-05; ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOX, HAROLD ROBERT LESLIE, B.Sc (Mass.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1889; Institute of Technology; served in Great War, 1915-19; ment. in desps., demob. with rank of capt., 1919; ch. engr., Jamaica govt. rly., 1926; ag. dir., June-Dec., 1931.

FOXLEE, RICHARD WILLIAM, M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1885; ed. Westminster Schl.; served in engnr. dept., G.N.R., 1903-09; G.C.R., 1909-15; P.L.A., 1915-21; dep. head, engnr., design dept., C.A., 1921; dep. ch. engr. (civ.), 1928.

FRAMPTON, ALGERNON DEKEWER, C.D.A.—B. 1904; ed. Christ's Hosp., Oxford Schl. of Economics and at Wye Agr. Coll.; impl. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; agrl. offr., dist. agr., Nigeria, 1927; agrl. supt., Br. Guiana, 1929.

FRANCE, HENRY DUNLOP.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; gov't. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treas., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nera, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904; 1st cls. supervisor of cust., Aug., 1909; ag. insp., cust. preven. serv., Mar.-Oct., 1911; ag. insp., maritime cust., Oct., 1916 to Jan., 1917; prov. collr. cust., Jan., 1921.

FRANCIS, BERTRAM ALEXANDER, O.R.E. (1928)—B. 1878; ed. St. Edmunds Schl., Canterbury, and St. Catherine's Coll., Camb.; B.A. (class. tripods), 1900; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1901; lieut., 1902; inspr. of immigrts., Mauritius, 1906; ag. auditor-gen., Jan. to June, 1908; prot. of immigrts., and poor law comsnr., 1912; temp. capt., R.G.A., Jan., 1915 to 1918; ag. recr. gen., Feb. to Nov., 1919, Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1922 and from Nov., 1922 to Aug., 1923; ch. profiteering comte., May, 1920; collr. of cust. and harbr., Aug., 1924; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1927 to May, 1928 and Dec.-Aug., 1930; ag. ass't col. sec., Aug., 1929 to Dec., 1930.

FRANCIS, CLIFFORD CLAUDE, K.C., Barrister-at-Law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Rochester, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; service in Fiji, 1908-12; cadet W. Pacific ser. (Br. Solomon Is. Prot.), June, 1912; passed cadet May, 1914; dep. comsnr., high comsnr.'s ct., Dec., 1914; lieut., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; capt., Apr., 1916; served in France and Flanders, 1917-18; dist. offr., Gizo, Br. Solomon Is., Oct., 1919; ag. res. comsnr. of Prot., Oct., 1922 to Mar., 1923; rep. natives of Gizo dist. in claims before lands comsnr., 1923; judl. comsnr. for W. Pacific in New Hebrides and Br. judge, joint ct., New Hebrides condominium, Aug., 1925; pol. mag., Nigeria, 1930; puisne judge, Uganda, July, 1934; ag. ch. just., Aug., 1934.

FRANCIS, CYRIL GERARD BROOKE.—Agrd. Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieut., armed nativ constab., 1901; certif., musketry and macross gun, Hythe, 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., 1907; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1908; A.D.C. to ag. gov., for various periods, 1908-10; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., May, 1912; 2nd in com. local forces with rank of major, Aug., 1914; mer. by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; ooy. comdr., K.A.R., E Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. comsnr., F. Jan., 1916; registr. gen., registr. sup. ct., com. stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; cust. enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji and W. Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1920 to May, 1921; comsnr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., R. Honduras, July, 1921; ag. ch. just., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; chmn., comsnr. to inquire into outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; chmn., retiremnt comte., 1922; chmn., franchise comte., 1923; revd. and consol. laws, B. Hond., 1924.
FRANCIS, JOSEPH.—Served European war with A.I.F., 1916-19; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia since 1922; hon. min., Lyons' ministry, 1932.
FRANKLIN, B. J. F.—Aast. jun. staff surr., E. Africa Prot., 1914; ast. dist. comsr., May, 1915; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade admstv.-off., Apr., 1925.
FRANKS, JOHN WILLIAM.—B. 1879; prison comm., H.C., June, 1899; ast. supt., Victoria Gaol, Hong Kong, Nov., 1913; ast. supt. pol. res., Feb., 1919; supt., prisons, Dec., 1920.
FRASER, ALASTAIR GARDEN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 3rd cls. hons., phil., polit., and econ.; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, C.B.E. (1930), M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, Edin., Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; deacon, 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1900-03; prin., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1904; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; chaplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Schl., Inveraray; George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; ast. conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

FRASER, HUGH, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford(schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 1913; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., Apr., 1914-Feb., 1915; ag. supervisor of cust., Port Dickson, May, 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1916; 3rd ast. sec., Fed. sect., Aug., 1917, priv. sec. to ch. sec. in addn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jeddu, Feb., 1918; ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1924; offr., class III., Nov., 1925; ag. ast. advtr., Kedah, Mar., 1927; ag. temp. legal adviser, Kedah in admin., Apr. to Dec., 1927; ag. ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, June, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; dist. offr., Larut and regis., titles, Perak, Nov., 1930; offr. mag., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1932; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Mar., 1933; offr., cls. I.B., 1934.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, Leith, and Edin. Univ.; on mily. serv., 1915-19; cadet, Hong Kong, 1919; astd. to sany. dept., 1921; 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; 2nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr. (North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; ast. head, sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bd., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1924; ast. supt., impts., and expts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., North and South Sept., 1928; called to bar, Middle Temple, June, 1931; pol. mag., Kowloon, July, 1931; ag. ast. atty.-gen., Oct., 1932.

FRASER, SIR JOHN GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1864; cadet Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, &c., Badulla, Feb., 1894; ast. govt. agt., Chilaw, Dec., 1899; land settm. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller of revenue, in 1911, 1912, 1913, and 1916; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; ret., 1924.

FRASER, MALCOLM, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1873; ed. at Inverness; entd. N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1893; ast. pub. serv. superannuation fund, 1908; govt. statistician 1911-32, also comnr. of unemployment, 1930-32; admstd. enrolment and balloting, Exidy. Force Reserv., 1916-18; N.Z. rep. at British Empire Conf. of Statisticians, 1920; under sec., internal affrs., 1932; mem., Internat. Statistical Inst.
FRASER, WINFORD BLACHE.—B. 1868; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; mag., St. Andrew, Trinidad, Jan., 1923; mag., Caroni, Nov., 1923; mag., St. George East, Dec., 1924.

FRAZER, HON. FRANCIS VERNON, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Nelson Coll., Otago Univ. Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; barrister and solr., stip. mag., 1911; chmn., N.Z. pub. serv. appeal bd., 1918; ast. pub. serv. comnr., 1920; judge, et. of arbitn., 1921; chmn., dairy industry comnr., 1934.

FREEMAN, BENJAMIN JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp., London, and Horsham; br. P.O., Oct., 1903-Dec., 1913; jur. postal clk. and telegraphist, Kenya, Jan., 1913; 2nd cls. postmr., Apr., 1915; ast. ch. acctnt., Apr., 1918; 1st cls. postmr., Apr., 1920; dep. ch. acctnt., June, 1924; ast. P.M.G., July, 1931; snr. acctnt., S.S., Aug., 1932; ag. contrlr., P. and T., Selangor and Pahang, Apr., 1933.

FREEMAN, GROPPERT JAMES.—B. 1889; ed. privately and in Clongoweswood Coll., Ireland; served in Royal Dublin Fusiliers and Royal Munster Fusiliers in the Great War, 1914-21; collr., cust., Gold Coast, 1922; senr. do., Feb., 1932.

FREESTON, LESLIE BRIAN, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. at Willaston Schl., Nantwich and Oxford; scholar New Coll., 1911-1919; 2nd cls. hons. mod., 1913; B.A. (war), 1919; on mil. ser., 17th Sept., 1914 to 8th Mar., 1919; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Mar. to 15th July, 1919; appt. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th July, 1919; ast. priv. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sec., 18th June, 1920; sent to Ceylon for temp. serv. as sec., salaries comnr., 1921; act. prin., 6th Dec., 1922; prin., 1st April, 1924; visited Bahamas and Br. Hond. on duty, 1927; seconded for serv. with Col. Development Advisory Comte., 1st Feb., 1930.

FRECHER, LEWIS, L.C.S. (Ret.).—Ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; served as colonisation offr., Chenal Canal, Punjab, afterwards as dep. comnr. and joint ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, 1896-1921; dir., development, Palestine, 1931.

FRERE, SIR BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse schl. and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres. dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902; atty.-gen., 1911; ch. just., Apr., 1914; judge, prize ct., Aug., 1914; cable censor during European War; ret., Aug., 1922.

FRERE, NORL GRAY, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schl., N.Z., and Cranleigh Schl.; ast. dist. comnr., S. Leone, July, 1909; dist. comnr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comnr., N. Prov., in 1921, 1923, 1926 and 1928; prov. comnr., Jan., 1929.

FRETTZ, WILMOT THEODORE STUART.—B.1889; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Rhodesian admidstr., R.S.A.P., N. and S. Rhodesia, 1913-19; Transvaal Border Patrol, 1918; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1921; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1923; chmn., agric. comnr., 1924; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, July, 1925; res. mag., Jamaica, Apr., 1927; ast. judge, Zanzibar, Aug., 1934.

FREYBERG, CAPT. GEOFFREY HERBERT, R.N. (Retd.), O.B.E. (Mil.)—B. 1881; served in R. Navy, 1895-1926; China med., 1900; ment. in desps. for services at Battle of Jutland when comdr., H.M.S. "Valiant"; awarded O.B.E. (Mil.) for services afloat during the Great War; King's harbr., Plymouth, from 1919; elec. a younger brother, Trinity House Corps., 1921; served at admry., 1922; master attendant, S. Stilts., Apr., 1926.

FRITH, GEOFFREY HAMMOND.—Served in the Great War; just. of peace, Turks and Caicos Is., 1919; ch. clk. to commr.; clk. to leg. bd.; sec. to bd. of educn., 1920; sub-inspr., pol., 1922; asst. treas., collr. of cust., postmr., gen. man. of savings bank, regiar. of shipping, 1923; ag. ast. commr., mag., etc., 1927; ag. commr. and judge, grand ct., Cayman Islands, 1929; sub. treas., etc., Tobago, 1931; treas., St. Lucia, 1933; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1933.

FUAD, MUSTAFA.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; ed. Turkish Schls., St. Paul's Schl., and English Schl., Nicosia, Cyprus; olk., treas., Cyprus, 1905; inspr., pol. and dep. coroner, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Limassol, 1913; judge, dist. ct., Nicosia, 1916; mem., legal bd., 1916; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; pres., sheri. ct. of appeal, 1927.
FUGILES, NORMAN RALPH.—B. 1907; ed. Sir Roger Manwood's Sch.; B.Sc., Wye (Lond. Univ.), dipl. agr., Cantab and A.I.C.T.A., Trinidad; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1930.

FULLERTON, HON. SIR GEORGE WARBURTON, K.C.M.G. (1919).—Elected to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of home affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Apr., 1910; col. sec., N. S. Wales, Nov., 1916-20; premier, 1922-25; agt.-gen., N.S.W., 1926-31.

FULLERTON, HON. C. P., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. Dalhousie Univ., Dalhousie, Nova Scotia, Canada; admitted to bar, 1896; judge, ct. of appl., Manitoba, 1917-31; ch. commr., bd. of rly. commrs., for Canada, 1931; chmn., Canadian National Rly. trustees, 1933.
FULTON, ADAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; lieut., May, 1915; capt., 1916; on active serv., Gallipoli, Egypt and Suez, France and Italy, 1915-18; demob., Jan., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1926.

FURNESS, SIR ROBERT HOWARD, Kt. Bach. (1919).—B. 1880; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man; solr. (hons.), 1902; practised at bar of B. Honduras, 1906-13; regiar.-gen., B. Honduras, 1913; served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1920; senr. mag., Apr., 1921; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ch. just., Barbados, Oct., 1926.

FURNNESS-SMITH, CECIL, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, Apr., 1914; dist. commr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; crown coun., Mar., 1925; ag. solr.-gen., May-Oct., 1927; ag. circuit judge, 1929; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1932; ag. atty.-gen. in 1933.

FURSE, MAJOR R. D., D.S.O.—B. 1880; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis (now Lord Harcourt), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on active service in 1st King Edward's Horse, K.O.D.R., 5th Aug., 1914 to 26th Nov., 1918; served in France and Italy, Apr., 1915 to 26th Nov., 1918; promoted capt., 1911; major, 1916; wounded, 1917; D.S.O., July, 1918; Bar to D.S.O.-gazetted, Mar., 1919; twice ment. in desps., ast. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state (Lord Milner), May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. (apptmts.) to sec. of state, Mr. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, 8th June, 1929; visited Canada on C.O. business, 1922, and again in 1923 as rep. at Imp. Forestry Confce.; mem., bd. of govt., Imp. Forestry Inst.; ast. sec., 1st Oct., 1930.

GALEA, ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1930)—B. 1872; ed. Malta Lyceum; entr. Malta civ. serv., 1891; 3rd cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1895; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1901; ast. regiar. ct. of judicial pol., Dec., 1913; regiar., Jan., 1914; transf. secretariat, May, 1918; ch. clk., lient.-gov.'s office, Sept., 1921; ch. clk. to legislature, Dec., 1921; compt. charitable institutions, Apr., 1923; offi. sec. to head of ministry, Mar., 1927; ast. sec. to gott., Apr., 1934.

GALEA, PROF. ROBERT V., L.S. & A.—B. 1882; matric. (with hon.), Malta Univ., 1904; graduated as land survr. and architect, 1903; lect. on engnr. and architecture, Malta Univ., 1906; prof. ditto, 1915; ag. rector of univ., 1923; el. for 1st divn. in leg. assem., June, 1924; re-el., Aug., 1927; min. for pub. health and charitable institutions, Aug., 1927 and June, 1932, also min. for treas., Aug., 1929; dist. leader constitutional party, 1932; leader opposition in L.A., 1932-33; rector, Malta Univ., 1934.

GALIZIA, HON. MAJOR JAMES A., C.B.E. (1933), O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1876; clk., Malta m. ser., 1893; ch. clk., P.W.D., 1906; man., water wks. and elec. light dept., 1913; supt., pub. water and man., water wks. and elec. light dept., with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1921; treas., 1930.

GALLOWAY, WILLIAM CHARLES GOMBLETON.—B. 1892; Lond. Institution of City and Guilds bronze medalist, telephony, A.I.E.E.; Br. P.O. engnr. dept., Jan., 1912; on active serv., R.E. (Signals), Nov., 1915; ast. telegr. engnr., P. & I. dept., F.M.S., June, 1920; engnr., Oct., 1920; asr. engnr., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1928, and Feb-May, 1929; asr. engnr., Dec., 1930.

GALT, HON. ALEXANDER CASIMIR.—B. 1883; ed. Hellmuth Coll., Toronto Univ.; pracised law in Toronto, 1876-1896; Rosalind, B.C., 1896-1905; Winnipeg, 1906-1912; judge of the c. king's bench, Manitoba, 1912; contrib. to Can. Law Journal and Can. Law Times.

GALWAY, 8TH VISCOUNT (CR. 1727) GROVE VEER ARNDELL MONCKTON-ARNDELL, G.C.M.G. (1935), D.S.O. (1917), O.B.E. (1919)—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; Sherwood Rangers Yeomanry, 1900-04; 1st Life Guards, 1904; adjt., 1908; capt., 1911; staff capt., 4th Cavalry Brig., 1914; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., 2nd Cavalry Divn., 1915; A.A. and Q.M.G., 1917; brevet lieut.-col., 1919; contr. Life Guards, 1925-29; col. commdr., Hon. Art Coy., 1933; gov.-gen. and commdr.-in-ch., New Zealand, 1935.

GALWAY, LT.-COL. SIR HENRY LEONARD, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1898), B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted hest., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-ras., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt. col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. commr. and vice-consul Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. commr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and div.

GALWAY, JOHN PATRICK FRANCIS.—Ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Liverpool; clk., vet., pathological lab., Kabete, Aug., 1910; seconded, mily. serv., 1915; corp., E. African Vty. Corps, 1st June, 1916; lieut., ditto, 17th Oct., 1917; lieut., 3rd K.A.R.; "1914-15" Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory med.; labour inspnr., off., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919; office supt., P.W.D., Dec., 1922.
GAMBLE, F. C.—Asst. dist. commsr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; res. mag., July, 1923.

GAMBLE, JOSEPH SAMUEL.—Ag. sub-inspr. pol., B. Guiana, 1905; sub-inspr. pol., 1906; Hythe musketry certif., 1908; dist. inspr. pol., 1912; county inspr., 1920; ag. dep. inspr.-gen. and supt., fire brigade for various periods in 1920, 1922, 1924 and 1926; inspr.-gen., pol., Fiji, and commdt., Fiji defence force, Oct., 1927; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1928.
GAME, FREDERICK HABOLD ROBERTS, A.C.G.I. (1921), A.M.I.C.E. (1925).—B. 1900; ed. Taunton Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology, S. Kensington; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Uganda, Sept., 1925.

GAME, AIR VICE-MARSHALL SIR PHILIP WOOLCOCK, G.B.E. (1929), K.C.B. (1924), C.B. (1919), D.S.O.(1915).—B. 1876; ed. Charterhouse; ent. R.A., 1895; capt., 1901; adjt., 1902; major, 1912; air vice-marshal, 1922; G.S.O., 3rd and 2nd grade, W.O., 1910-14; served S. Afric., 1901-02 (desps., Queen's Med. five clasps); European war, 1914-18; (desps. six times); dir., training and organization, Air Ministry, 1919-22; commanded R.A.F., India, 1922-23; air. mem. for personnel on air coun., 1923-28; ret. list, 1929; gov., New South Wales, 1930-35.

GAMMANS, LEONARD DAVID.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1915; B.E.F., France, 1916; lieut. and capt., 1917; seconded for serv. as instr. in gunnery and equitation, R. Mily. Coll., Canada, May, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; ag. asst. dist. offr., K. Kubu, Apr., 1922; asst. supervar., cu.t., P. Dickson, Oct., 1922; served at Malayan pavilion, Br. Empire Exhib., Mar., 1925; offr., cls. IV., June, 1927; attd. co-op. socs. dept., F.M.S., Oct., 1927; offr., cls. III., June, 1932.

GANADO, ROBERTO F., LL.D.—B. 1875; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1891; LL.D., Malta, 1898; bar, Malta, 1899; advoc. for poor, 1905; mag., judl. pol., 1918; examt. laws, Malta Univ., 1919; chmn., licensing bd., 1920; visitor, notarial acts Nov., 1921; ag. crown advoc., June, 1922; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., July to Oct., 1922; chmn., bd. of prison visitors, 1924; ag. judge, supr. cts., June to Sept., 1924; mem., gen. coun. of Univ. of Malta, 1924; judge, supr. cts., Sept., 1925.

GARDNER, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1874; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, and Keble Coll., Oxford; P.A., Cape and Oxford; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1896; admitted to Cape bar, 1897; ag. puisne judge, Natal, 1907; atty.-gen., Cape Province, 1910; puisne judge of sup. ct. of S. Africa (Cape Prov. Div.), 16th Oct., 1914; judge pres., Cape Prov. divn., 1926.
GARDNER, H. M.—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, Dec., 1923; ag. conservator, 1925 and 1927; conservator, 1928.
GARDNER, JOHN HYSLOP, B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. Kelvinside Acad. and Univ., Glasgow; asst. engrn., Basutoland, 1909; govt. engrn., Swaziland, 1919; served with R.E., Flanders, 1916-18.

GARDNER, JOSEPH HENRY.—B. 1882; active serv. with Imp. Yeomanry, S. African War; mil. serv., E. African Exped. Force attd. Rly. Corps, 1916-18; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika Rly., Mar., 1918; dist. loco. supt., 1930; loco. running supt., 1933.
GARLAND, T. V., B.A., B.A.I. (T.C.D.).—B. 1906; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1930; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1934.

GARLICK, GEORGE HERBERT, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.M.R.E. (Camb.).—B. 1886; med. offr., Johore, 1917; Malayan med. serv., 1923; ag. prin. med. offr., Johore, 1924, 1931 and 1933; physician and radiologist, Johore, 1927; prin. med. offr., 1934.

GARNER, JOSEPH JOHN SAVILLE.—B. 1908; ed. Highgate Schl. (senr. foundation schl., 1923-26) and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (mod. languages schol., 1926-29); mod. and med. languages tripso Pt. I, cls. 2, div. 1; ditto, Pt. II, cls. 1; B.A., 1930; asst. mod. lang. mast., Haileybury Coll., 1929-30; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1930.

GARNETT, CHRISTOPHER BRADSHAW, M.A. (Agr.).—B. 1903; ed. Clayesmore Schl., Winchester and Peterhouse, Cambridge; B.A. (Agr.), 1924; M.A. (Cantab), 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1926.

GARNETT, W. J., B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1889; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1907; seconded for service in Oversea Stntln. Office, Jan., 1919; promoted asst. prin. under clause 51, 1924; asst. to the rep. in the C'wealth of Australia of H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1931.

GARRAN, SIR ROBERT RANDOLPH, K.C.M.G. (1920), KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1867; barrister-at-law; sec. to drafting comte. of Australian Fed. Convention, 1897-8; sec. to atty.-gen.'s dept., C. of A., Jan., 1901, and partly, draftaman; solr.-gen., 1916-32; chmn., Canberra univ.; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint author (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "The Annotated Constitution of the Aust. Commonwealth," 1901.

GARROOD, HARRY ST. CLAIR.—B. 1882; ed. St. Polycarp's Schl., Farnham; articled architect; Sudan civ. serv., 1906; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1917; capt., 1918; served with Hedjaz Expedy. Force; Br. agt., Jeddah, 1919 with rank of major; M.C., 3rd cls. Order of Nahda from King Hussein; ment. in desps.; demob., 1920; gen. headqrs., Egypt to 1928; crown survr. and engrn., Gibraltar, 1929.

GARSON, ALEXANDER DENIS.—B. 1904; ed. Epsom Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. house, hist. tripso; M.A.; cadet, Nigeria, 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; seconded to C.O. as asst. prin., Oct., 1933.

GARTON, HARRY.—B. 1889; served in R.F.A., France, Salonica, Egypt and Palestine, 4th Aug., 1914 to Apr., 1919; clk. to court martials offr., R.A.F., May to Sept., 1919; Admiralty, 21st Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1922; served on Naval Inter-Allied Coman. of Control in Germany, May, 1921 to Oct., 1922; cler. offr.; C.O., 21st Oct., 1922.

GARVEY, FRANCOIS WILLOOCS.—B. 1882; served in S. Africa, first in the ranks and afterwards as transport offr.; served Transvaal town pol., 1904-08; asst. comntr., pol., S. Nigeria, 1909; comntr., pol., 1918; recd. thanks of govt. for investigation and suppression of human sacrifices at Agbara Juju at Awka, 1921; comntr., pol., Lagos, 1922; ag. dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Feb., 1925 to Mar., 1925; dir., prisons, S. Provs. and Colony, Mar., 1925.

GARVEY, RONALD HERBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), F.R.A.I.—B. 1903; ed. Trent Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji and Western Pacific services, Nov., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Mala, Apr., 1929; dist. offr., Oct., 1930; asst. sec., W. Pacific high comm., Aug., 1932; ag. sec. in 1932, 1933 and 1934.

GARVIN, THOMAS FORREST, K.C.—B. 1881; Crown counsel, Ceylon, Mar., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1913; ag. addtl. dist. judge, Colombo, Feb., 1914; solr.-gen., 1915; ag. puisne just., Nov., 1921 to Jan., 1922; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1922 to Dec., 1922; ag. puisne just., sup. ct., Jan., 1923; puisne just., June, 1924; ag. ch. just., July to Sept., 1926.
GASH, VINCENT GORDON.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1911-15; sub-inspr., 1915; passed 3rd grade Sewana exam., 1923; impr., 1929.

GATER, BOSSLEY ALAN REX, M.A., Dip. Agric. (Cantab.), D.I.C., F.R.M.S., F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1896; war serv., 1914-19; asst. entomologist, dept. of agric., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; malaria resch. offr., Nov., 1926; entomologist, Inst. Med. Resch., 1927; prof., biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Aug., 1930; del. to 8th cong. of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Dec., 1930.

GATFIEKLD, WILLIAM HUBERT, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Streatham Gram. Schl. and St. George's Coll., London; apptd. to the 2nd divn. of the Imp. civ. serv., 1914; joined Queen's Westminster Rifles, 1915; 2nd lieut., 1916; served in France, and demob. with rank of lieut., 1919; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1920; proceeded to China to study Chinese, July, 1921; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1922; 2nd asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1931; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, May, 1934.
GAUNT, A. E.—B. 1897; entd. C.O., Aug., 1913; apptd. asst. clk., Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from Dec., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 28th Dec., 1928.

GAUNTLETT, HUGH GAUNTLETT.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1919; ent. civ. serv., Jamaica by compet. exam. and apptd. asst. clk. of cts., Mar., 1894; dep. ditto, Apr., 1903; ag. clk. of cts., on various occasions between June, 1907 and June, 1912; clk. of cts., Feb., 1915; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, Aug., 1926 and from Sept., 1926; res. mag., 1929.

GEERING, SIDNEY.—Temp. asst. to irrig. engr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1st Aug., 1916 to Oct., 1918; ag. dist. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; 1st cls. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of works, Gambia, 20th Aug., 1920.

GELDENHUIYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; B.A., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Univ. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of natr. study and agric. science, Grey Coll. Schl., 1915; edr., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of econ. and marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1923; under sec. for agr., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931; comm. coun., Rome, Oct., 1934.

GENEVE, FREDERIC PIERRE, K.C. (1929).—B. 1888; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1909; ag. dist. am. stip. mag. for varying periods; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, July, 1921; admr. substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1922; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1923; ag. ditto, Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925 and Apr., 1925 to Jan., 1927; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1927; ag. col. sec., Apr.-Sept., 1928, Mar.-Dec., 1929 and Apr.-Sept., 1930; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1931.

GENT, GERARD EDWARD JAMES, D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1895; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury; Trinity Coll., Oxford; served with Duke of York's Light Infy., 1914-19; asst. prin., C.O., 1920; sec., rubber restriction comte., 1922; sec. prin., 1923; pvt. sec. to partly. under sec., 1924; prin., 1927; dep. sec., C.O. confce., 1927; sec. ex. services comte., 1929; seconded to India Office as a sec. of Indian Round Table Confce., 1930; accompd. Permt. Under-Sec. of State to Ceylon, Malaya and Hongkong, 1932.

GENTLE, GEORGE EDWIN PALMER, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1850; ed. Denstone Coll.; clk. gen.'s office, Grenada, 1898; warden, P.W.D., St. Vincent, 1901; road survr., Grenada, 1906; asupt., pub. wks., 1914, 1916, 1918, 1921, 1922; asst. asupt., pub. wks., 1916; asupt., pub. wks., 1922.

GEORGE, HENRY BERNARD.—B. 1880; imp. P.O., 1897; clk., G.P.O., Cape, 1900; ed. P.W.D., 1903; clk., C. and A.G.'s office, 1904; clk., audit office, Cape admin., 1912; served Great War, France, with S.A.O.E.F. and R.A.F. offr. in ch., local gov't. audt., 1920; prov. ass't Natal Prov., 1923; prov. audt., Transval Prov. 1933; author of "Standard Electricity Acco. in S.A."

GEORGE, REGINALD, R.D., N.R.N.—B. 1887; ed. H.M. Schl. Ship "Conway"; seconded to serv., Lake Nyasa, 1915; commodr., marine transport, Sept., 1918; ag. marine asupt. in 1922, 1928 and 1931; marine asupt., Oct., 1932.
GEORGE, NANCY MARGARET FANNY.—B. 1896; ed. St. Catherine's High Schl., Malta; Devonport Secondary Schl., Frobel Training Coll.; headmistress, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Jan., 1923.

GERAHTY, CHARLES CYRIL, K.C. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Trent Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1909; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt. Lancs. Regt., 1910; lieut., 1911; capt., 1914; asst. res. and comntr., prov. cts., N. Nigeria, 1911-14; war serv. in France, Belgium and W. Africa, 1914-18; restored to civil establt., N. Provs., Nigeria, Nov., 1918; legal asst., law directorate, W.O., Sept., 1919 to Sept., 1923; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1920-26; ag. atty.-gen. and puisne judge on various occasions; law compiler, 2nd vol. stat. law of Cyprus, 1923; atty.-gen., Dec., 1926; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1929; puisne judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Apr., 1932.

GERMAN, RALPH LIONEL.—B. 1892; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (class. sch.); B.A., 1914; served with Hampshire Regt., 1915-19; Tank Corps, 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Perak, Feb., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923. offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Mar., 1924; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1924; dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1926; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1929; supt., marine and cuit., chandu., posts and tels., audr. and pub. pros., Kelantan, Jan., 1931; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Jan., 1933; regiar., titles, Selangor in addn., July, 1933.
GERMAN, WALTER.—B. 1885; bldg. inspr., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1913; ag. ch. sany. inspr. in addn., Aug., 1919 to May, 1920.
GERRAND, JOHN KENNEDY.—B. 1899; asst. engnr., Basutoland, 1929.

GETHIN, PERCY EDWARD LOVELL, A.F.C.—B. 1884; ed. Radley Coll.; R. Geographical Soc.'s dipl. in surveying, 1908; explorn. in S. Arabia, 1909; asst. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1910; in charge, topographical surveys in the field, 1912; on mily. serv., 1914-20; major, R.F.C., 1916; asst. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, 1923; ag. reg.-gen., 1923; dir., surveys, Tanganyika Territory, 1929; tech. adviser, air bd., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; dir., civ. aviation, 1930.

GETLEY, ALFRED CURPHEY.—B. 1897; ed. Roborough Schl., Eastbourne; accnt. branch, Royal Navy, 1915-22; paymr.-lieut., Jan., 1920; ret. at own request, 1922; seconded as pilot to R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-20; ent. col. audit dept., Apr., 1923; asst. audr., Uganda, June, 1923; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions; asst. audr., Kenya, 1929; asst. audr., col. audit dept., 1933.
GIBB, JOHN.—B. 1881; supervisor of warehouses, gvt. stores, Ceylon, Oct., 1914; col. storekeeper, Sept., 1916.

GIBBINGS, ALBERT VICTOR, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Mar., 1913; Kedah, Apr., 1913; ag. exec. engnr., Kedah, Jan., 1917; exec. engnr., Kedah, Mar., 1922; exec. engnr., Johore, Sept., 1925; ag. senr. exec. engnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1927; senr. exec. engnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1929; water wks. engnr., Seremban, June, 1930; senr. exec. engnr., Klang, Apr., 1931; ag. state engnr., Pahang, June, 1931; senr. exec. engnr., Coast, Kuantan, Jan., 1932; do., Seremban, Dec., 1933; ag. state engnr., Kedah, June, 1934.
GIBBON, GEOFFREY MCKAY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1896; ed. Elgin acad., Gordon Coll., Aberdeen and Univ. of Edin.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Oct., 1933.
GIBBON, JOHN FRANK, M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1869; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, Jan., 1899; dist. med. offr., Jan., 1916.
GIBBS, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1889; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920.

GIBSON, H. D.—B. 1886; ed. Bedale and Lincoln Gram. Schls.; junr. traffic asst., Nigerian rly., 1910; asst. traffic supt., 1913; dist. ditto, 1920; traffic supt., 1922; divnl. supt., 1927; supt. of line, 1932.

GIBSON, LESLIE BERTRAM.—B. 1896; ed. King Henry VIII. Schl., Coventry; hon. LL.B. (War), London; flight sub-lieut., R.N.A.S.; attd. Grand Fleet; lieut., R.A.F.; cadet, S.S., Mar., 1920; attd., atty.-gen.'s office, S'pore, Apr., 1920; attd., food control office, Penang, May, 1920; dep. dir. food prodn., Apr., 1921; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; dep. regir. and asst. offr., assignee, Penang, June, 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1925; asst. regist., sup. ct., Ipoh, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; ag. dep. pub. pros., Penang, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; ag. legal advr., Kedah, Aug., 1932; attd. M.L.O., Mar., 1934; ag. dist. offr., P. Wellesley, May, 1934.

GIBSON, ROY ALFRED.—B. 1885; ed. Brandon High Schl. and Brandon Coll.; ent fed. gvt. serv. at Regina, 1908; transf. to Ottawa, and apptd. ch. clk. to dep. min., 1908; asst. dep. min., June, 1919; commr., Yukon and N.W. Territories, 1923.

GIBSON, WILLIAM SUMNER, C.M.G. (1934), B.A., Oxon., Barrister-at-Law (Lincoln's Inn).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Keble Coll., Oxford (schol.), B.A. (1st cls. mod. hist.), 1899; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1899; services lent to Johore gvt. July-Nov., 1904; asst. man., monopolies dept., Penang, Oct., 1909; called to the bar, June, 1913; legal adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1916; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1916; judge of high ct., Kedah (in addn.), July, 1916; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; pres., comm. to enquire into organisation and expend. of gvt. depts., F.M.S., Feb., 1922; offr., cls. Ia., Dec., 1922; legal advr., F.M.S., Dec., 1922; M.F.C.; pres. S.S. trade comm., Mar., 1933.

GIE, S. F. N., Ph.D.—B. 1885; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Amsterdam and Berlin Univ.; teacher and inspr. schls., Cape Prov.; prof. hist. and chmn., senate, Stellenbosch Univ., 1918; under-sec., educn., Union of S. Africa, 1926; sec., ditto, 1927; min., plenipotentiary, Berlin, Oct., 1934; publications include a history of S. Africa in Afrikaans in two vols.

GILBERT, HUMPHREY RALEIGH.—B. 1899; ent. R. Navy, 1912; served, Great War 1914-18; ret., Jan., 1923; lieut. commdr. (ret'd.). Feb., 1928; admtn. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1927; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1933.

GILBERT, SAMUEL MOORE, B.Sc. (Lond.), N.D.A. Dip. Agr. (Wye).—B. 1892; ed. City of Norwich Schl., Wye Coll. and London Univ.; commr., The 1st King's Regt.; served in War, 1914-18; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1923; asst. dir., agr., Trinidad, 1929; ag. dir., 1929-30; M.L.O., 1933; ch. scientific offr., coffee research and experimental station, Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1934.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1879; ed. at Brunswick House private schl., B. Guiana; entd. H.M. Customs, B. Guiana, Dec., 1899; copyist, regir.'s office, Oct., 1900; 6th cls. clk., ditto, Mar., 1902; 5th cls. clk., cust., 1903; asst. commissary, Sept., 1905; clk. to atty.-gen., May, 1906; stip. mag., J.P., coroner, and commr. of oaths, 1910; chmn., Rose Hall village partition of lands comm., 1911; called to the bar, Grav's Inn, 1914; ag. asst. gvt. sec., Mar., to Sep., 1914; censor (war) under defence scheme, 1914-20; recd. thanks of S. of S.; practised at English bar, 1920-21; commr. to enquire into disturbance, H.M. penal attlt., B. Guiana, 1922; ag. junr. puisne judge, in 1923 and 1925-6; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923, 1924 and 1925; ag. puisne judge in 1925, 1926 and 1927; puisne judge, Sept., 1927; 1st puisne judge, Trinidad, 1931.

GILKS, J. L., C.M.G. (1933), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Medical offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1904; prin. med. offr., Kenya, Feb., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. councils; dir. med. and sanv. services, 1926.
GILL, EDWIN.—B. 1896; on naval serv., 1915-19; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 12th July, 1926.

GILL, EDWIN LEONARD, D.Sc.—B. 1877; asst., Manchester Museum, June, 1900; curator, Hancock Museum, Newcastle-on-Tyne, July, 1901; asst. R. Scottish Museum, July, 1922; dir., S. African Museum, Cape Town, Jan., 1925.

GILL, Henry Sewell Currie, M.A.—B. 1903; ed. Edin. R. High Sch., Edin. Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford (coll. exhib.) (1st cls. hons. classics); cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1927; aast. dist. offr., 1929; aast. sec., 1933; ag. aast. ch. sec., 1934.

GILL, Ronald Goddard.—B. 1896; ed. Haberdashers' Askes Sch. (Hampstead); on mily. serv. overseas, 1917-19; commissioned, Nov., 1917; temp. clk., Miny. of Lab., 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., Mar., 1923.

GILLESPIE, James David, B.Sc.—B. 1905; ed. Musselburgh Gram. Sch., George Watson's Boys' Sch. and Edin. Univ.; three years land reclamation and cotton growing Nile Delta; agril. supt., Br. Guiana, 1930.

GILLMAN, Clement, F.G.S., M.S.A.S.C.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1882; ed. Swiss Federal tech. high schl., Zurich; on rly. constrn. and surveys in E. Africa, 1905-14; served E.A.P. Force, 1916-19; capt., rly. corps; exec. engr., E. African mily. rly. (occupied territory), 1916-18; ch. engr., ditto, 1918-19; dist. engrnr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1919; senr. dist. engrnr., Apr., 1925; ch. engrnr., Apr., 1928.

GILMOUR, Andrew.—B. 1898; ed. Royal High Schl., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; served with 14th A. & S.H., B.E.F., 1916-17; M.A. hons. (Edin.), 1920; cadet, S.S., Apr., 1921; attch. to res. councillors' office, Penang, May, 1921; offr., cls. V, ast. contrlr., lab., Penang and ast. prot., lab., Kedah, Apr., 1924; ast. contrlr., lab., Malacca, Feb., 1926; collr., land rev., S'pore, Jan., 1927; ast. supt., govt. monops., S'pore, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; res., Labuan, June, 1928; dist. offr., Jasin, Sept., 1929; ditto, U. Kelantan, Dec., 1930; offr., cls. III, Apr., 1933.

GIMSON, Franklin Charles.—B. 1890; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1914; attchd. to office of naval intell. offr., in add. to his own duties; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, in add. to his own duties; on mil. duty, 1918; addtnl. ast. col. sec., July, 1919; office ast. to govt. agt., N. C. Prov., Sept., 1919; addnl. ast. col. sec., Mar., 1920; 4th ast. ditto, Sept., 1920; ag. ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1922; landing survr. cust., Feb., 1924; ag. dep. collr. cust., Mar. to May, 1928; addnl. ast. dir., educn., Mar., 1929; sec. to min. educn., July, 1931; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Feb., 1932; do., Kegalle, Dec., 1933.

GLEN, James Alexander, M.A.—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Dec., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Nov., 1918; served in France and Palestine; demob., Jan., 1919; ast. dir. of edn., Iraq, Apr., 1920; ag. adviser, Min. of educn., Apr., 1922.

GLENWAY, Vincent G., O.B.E. (1929), M.A. Oxon. (hons. nat. sci.), F.G.S., Diploma, Forestry.—Ast. dist. consnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. consnr., N. Frontier, Jan., 1914 to June, 1919; offr. in charge, Somali disarmament, N. Frontier dist., Mar., 1919 (A.G.S. Med.); seconded as dist. consnr., Br. Somaliland, Mar., Sept., 1920; ast. dist. consnr., Oct., 1922; ag. dist. consnr., Sept., 1922; dist. consnr., 1926; Abyssinian mission, 1927-28; ag. prov. consnr., 1930-31, and from Mar., 1933; senr. dist. consnr., 1934.

GODBOLT, S. B.—B. 1884; clk., treas., Cape 1901; Transvaal, 1905; Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treas., Union of S. Africa, 1920; clk., grade II, 1924; ditto, grade I, 1928; comnr. pensions, July, 1932.
GODDARD, Harry Frederick.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

GODDARD, Thomas Nelson, M.R.E. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., Falkland Is. and clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Apr., 1914; lieut., Falkland Is. volc. force, Aug., 1914; capt., 1917; O.C., 1919; postal censor, 1914; chief clk., coll. sec.'s office, Feb., 1915; ag. comnr. of currency, 1915; ag. local audr., 1920; ast. col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1920; clk., exec. coun., 1921; A.D.C. to ast. gov., 1921 and to gov., 1922-23; census offr., 1921; senr. ast. col. sec. for various periods between 1920 and 1930; ag. col. sec. for various periods 1926-34; ch. ast. col. sec., 1930; gov. a dep. on many occasions, 1930-34; ag. gov. on three occasions, 1934; chmn., Wilberforce Centenary commtee., 1933; compiler, Sierra Leone Handbook, 1924.

GODDEN, Henry James, M.B.E. (1929)—B. 1877; telegraphist, Basutoland, 1902; postm., 1907; clk. to ast. comnr., 1917; ag. ast. comnr., 1922; passed 1st grade Senate, 1922; ch. clk., mast. of ct. and regis., 1924.

GODFREY, George Crosbie.—B. 1880; ed. Waimataitai Schi., Timaru, N.Z.; N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1897; priv. sec. to min. of pub. wks., 1901; ast. ch. clk., pub. wks. dept., 1908; ch. clk., 1914; ast. under-secr., 1929; marine, 1923; sec. of lab. and comnr. of unemploy., 1932.

GODFREY, Sir Joseph Ed., K.T. Bach. (1841)—B. 1858; M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of erect hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regis. of birth deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn. exec bd. of health, 1902; acted on sew. occasions; surg.-gen., regis.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regis.-gen. of h. m. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and off. mzn. of ct. of policy, and comb. ct., July, 1904; act. chmn. of bd. of agric., 1905; chmn. of ast. ast. comnr., 1906; del. for B. Guiana and India to Internat. Leprosy Confere., Bergen, 1915.

GODLEY, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Alexander Edward K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1886; adjutant, 1889; capt., 1896; major, 1897; colonel, 1905; apptd. to New Zealand Forces, 1910, with local rank of major-general; comdg. New Zealand expeditionary force in Egypt and at Dardanelles, 1914-15; gen. major-general, 1915; temp. lieut.-gen. in command of an Anzac army corps, France, 1918; lieut.-gen., 1918; gov. and comnr. in charge Gibraltar, 1928; foreign decorations: Legion Honour, Croix de Commandeur (1917); Order of the Crown, Grand Officer (Belgian) 1917; Order of the White Eagle (Serbian), 1917; Croix de Guerre (Belgian), 1918.

GODSALL, Walter Douglas, B.A. (Oxon.)—Cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; N'Eliya kach., Jan., 1924, ditto, Badulla kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Mar., 1925; office ast., Ratnapura kach., Dec., 1925; Galle kach., Mar., 1929; ag. ast. dist. ast., Kandy, Jan., 1930; do., Galle, May, 1944.
GODWIN-AUSTEN, Robert Amos, F.R.G.S.—B. 1885; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate; employed on rly. and land survey Canada, 1910-14; served with line and road survey...

GOLDBURG, JOSEPH.—B. 1886; entd. crown agents' office, 1902; ast. head, gen. dept., 1919; ast. head, finance dept., 1922; ast. head, shipping dept., 1925; ast. head, finance dept., 1930; dep. head, finance dept., May, 1933; accntn., W. African Currency bd., 1912-33.

GOLLAN, SIR HENRY COWPER, KT. BACH. (1921), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, comsrr. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., Jan.-Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admstr., June-Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1900-Apr., 1901; ch. just., Nov., 1901; prepared criminal code for N. Nigeria, 1904; compiled pros. of N. Nigeria, 1905; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904; prepared Cts. Consolidating Act, Bermuda, Dec., 1905; chmn. of produce comsrr., 1905 and of aliens comttee, 1906; chmn. of bd. of educn., Bermuda, July, 1907; attv.-gen., Trinidad, 1911; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 1918; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1924; chmn., salaries comsrr., 1928-29; ret., Aug., 1930.
GONTHER, GEORGE, C.A., L.I.A.—B. 1869; ed. Commissioner's Schl., Montreal and priv. tuition; audr.-gen., Dominion of Canada, Jan., 1924.

GOOCH, STANLEY JOHN WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., chtd. civ. engrnr.—B. 1889; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1912; exec. engrnr., Johore, May, 1922; sr. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., June, 1927; sr. exec. engrnr., hydraulics, Selangor, Mar., 1929; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, 1930 and 1932; res. engrnr. civ. aerodrome, S'pore, Jan., 1933; ag. state engrnr., Perak in 1933 and 1934.
GOOD, CECIL ALBERT.—B. 1892; called to bar, 1913; served in Army, lieut. and capt., 1915-20; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 1921.

GOODALL, EDWARD BASIL HERBERT, C.B.E. (1931), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Loughborough Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; ast. native comsrr., N. Rhodesia, Mar., 1909; native comsrr., Sept., 1911; ast. max., Nov., 1921; ag. dist. comsrr., Apr., 1928; dist. comsrr., Feb., 1929; prov. comsrr., Apr., 1929; ag. sec., native affrs., Sept.-Oct., 1931; mem., leg coun.

GOODMAN, ARTHUR MITCHELL.—B. 1886; ed. New Coll., Oxon.; B.A. 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; supernumerary mag., Ipoh, June, 1915; pres., rent assessment bd. for Penang, July, 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; supernumy. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1921; offr., cls. II.; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., S.S., Nov., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1927; offr., cls. Ia, mem., exec. coun., S.S., July, 1931; res. coun., Penang, Apr., 1933.
GOODMAN, REGINALD ERNEST.—B. 1886; clk., Basutoland, 1913; accntg. clk., 1916; fin. sec., mast. and regisr., deeds, Bechuanaland Prot., Oct., 1927.

GOODWIN, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR THOMAS HERBERT JOHN CLAYTON, K.C.B. (1919), K.C.M.G. (1932), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1898), hon. D.Sc. (Oxon.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.)—B. 1871; ed. Newton Coll., Devon and St. Mary's Hosp., London; comdnl., Army Med. Serv., 1893; staff surg., Mohmand Field Force, N.W. Frontier, 1897 (desps., med. and clasp); European War, 1914-18 (3 times ment. in desps., Mons Star, 1914, Allied and Victorymeds.); A.D.M.S. with Mr. Balfour's mission to U.S.A., 1917; dir.-gen., Army Med. Serv., 1918-23; retired, 1923; comdr., Legion of Honour; comdr., Belgian Order of Leopold; comdr., Italian Order of the Crown; Belgian Croix de Guerre; American D.S.M., Knight of Grace, Order of St. John of Jerusalem; gov., Queensland, 1927-32.

GOONETILLEKE, OLIVER ERNEST, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1892; ast. audr., rly., Ceylon, Aug., 1921; ag. ast. col. audr., Feb. to Dee., 1923; ast. col. audr., Feb., 1925; ag. col. audr., Feb. to Aug., 1927; audr.-gen., July, 1931.

GOPPSILL, WALTER LEONARD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Ed. Alley's Schl., Dulwich, King's Coll. and St. Thomas's Hosp.; R.F.C. (2nd lieut.), 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918-20; med. offr., Zanzibar, 1926.
GORDON, HON. SIR ALEXANDER, KT. BACH. (1930).—Judge of sup. ct., New South Wales, 1910-28.

GORDON, CHARLES AUGUSTINE.—B. 1882; ed. Stonyhurst Coll., St. Edmund's Coll., Herts. and Edinburgh Univ. (faculty of law); writer to the signet (W.S.); war serv., France and Flanders, 1915-19 (The Royal Scots); ast. admstr. gen., Zanzibar, 1925; offr. admstr. and pub. trustee, Nigeria, 1929.
GORDON, SIR DAVID JOHN, KT. BACH. (1925).—B. 1865; M.L.C. of S. Australia since 1913; pres., leg. coun. since July, 1932.

GORDON, GUY MONTGOMERY, B.A., B.A.I., T.C.D., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1885; mily. serv., R.E. (S.R.), Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine and France, 1915-20 (ment. in desps.); ast. dist. wks. offr., Iraq, 1920; dist. wks. offr., 1921; ag.-dep. dir., wks., Baghdad, Aug.-Sept., 1921; do., Basrah, Oct., 1921 to Jan., 1922; dir., wks., Bahamas, 1923; ast. D.P.W., Trinidad, May, 1928.

GORDON, HON. WESLEY ASHTON, P.C. (Can.), K.C., M.P.—B. 1884; ed. at Owen Sound Coll. and Osgoode Hall; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., July, 1930; min. of immigr. and colonization and min. of mines in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930; also min. of lab., Feb., 1932.

GORDON-HALL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER.—B. 1894; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; lieut., M.V.I., Mar., 1923; dist. offr. Kuala Langat, May, 1926; offr. cls. III., May, 1931; dist. offr., Krian, May, 1932; ag. sec., res., Selangor, July, 1933.

GORING, GEORGE INGHAM, A.R.T.C. (Glasgow), A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Allan Glen Schl.; ast. mech. engrnr., col. transport dept., Br. Guiana; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., transport and harbrs. dept., Nov., 1932-Aug., 1933; ch. mech. engrnr., Sept., 1933.
GORMLEY, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1904; ed. Dover County Schl. and Reading Univ.; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1927; ast. dist. offr., May, 1929.

GORSUCH, LEMLIE HAROLD.—B. 1897; ed. Ipswich Schl. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; B.A. (1920); war serv., May, 1916-Jan., 1919; lieut., Sept., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1920; attached to dist. office, Pekan; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1923; 4th ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ast. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cls. IV, dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1932; ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Sept., 1933.

GOSDEN, MINNIE, M.B., B.Sc. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—War serv. as V.A.D. nurse, 1914-17; chem. pathology astt., Hale chem. lab., London hosp., 1925; pathology astt., 1926; astt., bacteriological dept., 1927; astt., med. offr., Lingfield Epileptic Colony, 1928; bacteriologist, Cyprus, Feb., 1929.

GOSLING, WILLIAM RICHARD, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Sutton Valence and Cambridge Univ.; served in France, the Balkans and Turkey, 1915-1919; demob. with rank of major, 1919; astt. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, May, 1920.

GOSTELOW, CECIL, F.I.A. (Lond.).—B. 1891; ed. Fort St. Model High Schl., Sydney, N.S.W.; actuary, N.Z. nat. provident fund, 1921; astt. actuary, N.Z. govt. life insurance dept., 1922; actuary, govt. life insurance and friendly societies depts., 1923; also sec., govt. life insurance, 1925; govt. actuary, 1926.
GOTT, JOHN BURN.—B. 1890; ed. Lancing Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; sup't., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1922.

GOURLAY, WILLIAM NEWLANDS.—B. 1894; cadet, S.S., May, 1920; offr. cls. V., Feb., 1922; astt. dist. judge, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. IV., Feb., 1926; 3rd mag. Singapore, Jan., 1929; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1929; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1931; ag. astt. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, Nov., 1931; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1933.

GOULD, LIEUT.-COL. HON. SIR ALBERT JOHN, KT. BACH. (1908).—B. 1847; solr.; M.L.A., N. S. Wales, 1881-98; M.L.C., 1899-1901; min. for just. in Reid ministry, 1894-8; returned to the first senate of Commonwealth parlmt., 1901; pres. of the senate, Feb., 1907, to June, 1910; ret. from politics.

GOWERS, SIR WILLIAM FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), M.A., Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple).—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1898 (1st cls. class. tripods); entd. service of B.S.A. Co., Jan., 1899; astt. commnr., Matabeleland 1900; resigned, 1902; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1902; 2nd cls. res., 1906; 1st cls. res., 1910; ag. chief sec., Sept., 1912; lieut.-gov., N. Nigeria, June, 1921; gov. of Uganda, 1925-32; senr. crown agt. for the col., 1st July, 1932.

GRAEME, ALEXANDER WILLIAM SUTHERLAND, M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern, pupil Crewe, 1902-6; held various appts. on Eng. and Scottish rlys.; dep. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., 1915-24; ag. loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., May, 1917, Apr., 1919, Mar., 1922, Dec., 1923; loco. supt., F.M.S. rlys., Mar., 1924; ch. mech. engrnr., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1932.

GRAHAM, CAPT. BERNARD JAMES.—B. 1882; served S. African war, 1899-1902; Cape Garrison Arty., 1909-10; 2nd lieut., N. Rhodesia pol., June, 1913; lieut., ditto, Apr., 1915; temp. capt., Jan., 1917; temp. major and 2nd in commd., N. Rhodesia Pol. Service Batt., June, 1918; twice ment. in desps.; hon. capt., Aug., 1919; offr. commdg. town and dist. pol., C.I.D. and ch. emigrn. offr., N. Rhodesia and supt., Livingstone prison, July, 1920; offr. commdg. N. Rhodesia pol., supt., cent. prison and dist. commdt., volunteers, Mongu, Barotse, Apr., 1922; ditto, Livingstone, Jan., 1923; ditto, Fort Jameson, Dec., 1923; capt., N. Rhodesia pol., July, 1924; J.P. for N. Rhodesia; ag. commdt., N. Rhodesia Regt., 1933 and 1934.

GRAHAM, RT. HON. GEORGE PERRY, P.C. (1925), LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. High schls., Iroquois and Morristburg, Ontario; journalist; Morrisburg Herald, Ottawa Free Press, Brockville Recorder; elected to Ontario legislature for Brockville, 1902 and 1906; provcl. sec. of Ontario, 1906; resig. with the govt., 1906; leader of the oppn. in the Ontario legislature, 1907; sworn of privy coun., for Canada and min. of rlyws. and canals, 1907; elected to the H. of C. for Brockville, by acclamation, 1907; defeated at g., 1911; re-elec. for S. Renfrew, bye-elec., 1912; did not seek re-election at gen. elec., 1917; re-ele., Dec., 1921; min. of militia and defence, and naval service in King admtn., Dec., 1921; min. of rlyws. and canals, 1922; senator, Dec., 1926.

GRAHAM, JAMES MACAY.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Gram. Schl. and S. African Coll.; Transvaal civ. ser., 1903; astt. mag., Jota-neuburg, 1916; mag., Heidelberg, 1927; d. Lichtemberg, 1930; do., Durban, 1933; do., C. Town, 1934.
GRAHAM, J. W., M.C., M.A., M.D., Ch.I (Glas.), Gold Med. (S. S.), Thesis in Trop. Med.—B. 1896; mil'y. serv., 1914-19; med. in Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1925.

GRAHAM, SIR THOMAS LINKDOCK, K.B. BACH. (1920), K.C.—B. 1860; ed. at St. And' Coll., Grahamstown, and Clare Coll., Caz. (B.A.), barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1885; ad. of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, M.L.C., 1898; atty.-gen., May to Oct., 1898; col. ser., 1900-1902; atty.-gen., 1902; ag. prime min. J. to Aug., 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, July, 1904; judge pres., Eastern Dist. local div., 15th Apr., 1913; chrmn. pub. inquiry comm., June, 1918.

GRANNUM, SIR EDWARD ALLAN, KT. BACH (1926), C.M.G., 1915.—Passed Barbados, W. I. civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd clsk. regiar., off. Jan., Oct., 1885; 2nd clsk., Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1887; master-in-chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1889; honours graduate, diploma, Ontario Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountant, Barbados, W. I., to Aug., 1897; astt. act. Sierra Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1900; auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to Mar., 1901; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system of entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, 1901; local auditor, Cyprus, 1902; auditor, Mauritius, Apr., 1909; revr.-gen., Mauritius, 1912; ag.col. sec., on various occasions, 1912-1922; col. sec., Aug., 1923; admin. gr. on various occasions, 1923-30; ret., 1931.

GRANNUM, REGINALD CLIFTON, C.M.G. (1925).—B. 1872; 2nd clsk. to gov., Windward Is., Sept., 1891; ditto, St. Vincent, Apr., 1892; supvr. of c. G. Coast Col., Oct., 1893; astt. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1895; local audr., S. Leon, June 1896; J.P., S. Leon, 1897; ch. astt. G. Coast, Apr., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. of leg. couns., mem. hd. of educ., July, 1903; June, 1904, Dec., 1904, to Dec., 1905, June, 1906 to May, 1907; Oct., 1907, to Sept., 1910; admitted student of Gray's Inn, Aug., 1910; recvr.-gen., B. Guiana, Sept., 1908; mem. exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1908; astt. sec., July to Oct., 1913; deputy gov., Sept., Oct., 1913; treas. and mem., exec. and leg. ass., Kenya, Sept., 1922.
GRANT, D. K. S.—Astt. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Dec., 1912; conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.
