GRANT, EZZIEL AUGUSTUS.—Ent. Rtns., Honduras civ. serv. after compet. exam., copyist to survey dept., Nov., 1899; 3rd cls. clk. (ast. keeper), King's warehouse, Feb., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., P.W.D., Apr., 1907; on active serv. with B.W.I. Regt. during war, Apr., 1916 to July, 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., P.W.D., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; acct., P.W.D., Apr., 1920; ag. ch. clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., Apr., 1921; ag. ast. inspr., schls., during 1921 to 1923; ast. inspr., schls., provisionally, Apr., 1923; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1928; dist. comsrr., Orange Walk, Nov., 1928; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to May, 1931; ag. dist. comsrr., Belize and offl. admstr. and offl. recr. from Apr., 1931.
GRANT, PETER.—B. 1883; Hong Kong pol., Nov., 1906; inspr., ditto, Dec., 1923; ch. ditto, Mar., 1928; King's pol. med., 1933; ast. supt., pol., Feb., 1933.

GRANT, RICHARD WILLIAM, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Jones' Schi., Mon. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attd., col. sec's. office, Dec., 1920; offr., cls V, dist. offr., N. Tobal, Nov., 1923; offr., cls IV, Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Bentong, Dec., 1929; ast. advr., K. Tinggi, Mar., 1930; offr., cls III, Nov., 1932; ast. advr., Segamat, Oct., 1933.

GRANT, THOS. ST. JOHN.—B.A., Dublin Univ., 1896; clk. to C. C. and R. M., Komgha, Cape Colony, Dec., 1897; ditto, East London, June, 1898; ditto, Port Elizabeth, Jan., 1899; passed Cape civ. serv. law exam., Feb., 1899; clk. atty.-gen.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1899; clk. to R. M., Cape, Oct., 1899; regis. to judge pres. of high ct., Griqualand West, Sept., 1901; transf. to O.R.C. civ. serv. as ast. res. mag., Winburg, May, 1902; ditto, Bloemfontein, July, 1904; ag. res. mag., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1905, to Mar., 1906; res. mag., Hoopstad, 1907; mag., Bethulie dist., 1913; Parys, Feb., 1919; Newcastle, Sept., 1928; Queenstown, Oct., 1929; Kingwilliams Town, Aug., 1933.

GRANTHAM, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE HERDER, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; cadet, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1922; attd., C.S.O., 1925; 2nd pl. mag., Sept., 1929; acc. retrenchment comte., July, 1930; ast. to P.M.G. in addn., July, 1930; dist. offr., South, in addn., Dec., 1930; extra ast. col. sec., and sec. to harbr. advisory comte., 1931; 1st pol. mag. in addn., 1932; 2nd ast. col. sec. and d.p. clk. of couns., June, 1933.

GRANTHAM, SIDNEY HOWARD.—B. 1883; clk., W.O., Apr., 1901; ast. acct., army accts. dept., Apr., 1904; served S. Africa, 1904-07 and 1912-14; ast. commr., pol., Nigeria, May, 1915; senr. ditto, Apr., 1930; ast. inspr.-gen., pol., Jan., 1931; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., in 1932, 1933 and 1934.
GRAVER, GEORGE STANLEY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schi.; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923; ag. exec. engnr., May-Dec., 1933.

GRAY, CHAS. EDGAR.—B. 1904; cler. ast., offr. recr.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1919; 6th cls. offr., col. sec's. office, 1921; 5th cls. offr., 1923; 4th cls. offr., 1926; sec. to prisons comte., 1928; 2nd cls. clk., 1933; ag. prin. clk. and clk., exec. and leg. couns. in 1932 and 1933.
GRAY, H. W., O.B.E.—Office supt., lieut.-gov.'s off., E.A.P., May, 1908; clk. of the couns., Oct., 1908; ast. dist. commr., 1911; dist. commr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; ch. regis. of natives, Jan., 1920.

GRAY, JOHN.—B. 1874; ed. Glasgow Univ. and F.C. Training Coll., Glasgow; held several educational appts. in Scotland and England; served in S. African war; supt., Brandfort refugee camp, 1902-3; prin., Hoopstad schi., O.F.S., 1903; 1st cls. clk., educn. dept., O.F.S., 1904; senr. clk., educn. dept., 1912; ag. sec., educn. dept., Nov., 1918; prin. clk., educn. dept., 1919; sec., educn. dept., 1921.

GRAY, JOHN, B.A., M.D., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), L.M., Rot. Hosp. (Dub.), certif. L.S.T.M.—B. 1882; med. offr., gen. hosp., Singapore, Apr., 1909; grade II, F.M.S., Apr., 1910; sr. surgn., class III, Oct., 1913; ch. med. offr., Malacca, but contd. former appt., Jan., 1919; ch. med. offr., Singapore, but contd. former appt., Nov., 1920; ch. med. offr., Singapore, Nov., 1922; ag. prin. civil med. offr., S.S., and tempy. M.L.C., for various periods, 1923-31; state surgn., Kedah, Oct., 1931.
GRAY, JOHN MACFARLANE, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (I), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (Univ. Dub.).—B. 1898; health offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1933; ag. M.O.H., 1934.

GRAY, JOHN MILNER.—B. 1889; ed. Perse Schl. and King's Coll., Cambridge, M.A.; admitted solr., sup. ot., 1923; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1932; war serv. with B.E.F., France (twice wounded); ast. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 1920; mag., 1924; ag. solr.-gen., 1929; ag. puisane judge, 1933; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1934.

GRAY, R.—B. 1880; clk., treasy., 1902; clk., rev. dept., 1904; dep. recr., rev., Lichtenburg, 1905; clk., rev. dept., Jo'burg, 1908; Pretoria, 1912; prin. clk., 1921; survr., 1928; acctnt., 1929; recr., E. London, 1931; do., Pretoria, 1932; do., Durban, 1933.

GRAY, SIR REGINALD, KT. BACH. (1920), K.C. (1908).—B. 1851; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1875; compiled, in 1884, Bermuda laws from 1690-1883; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; compiled, in 1903, Bermuda laws from 1690-1902; counsel to statute law consolidation comte. since 1900; atty.-gen., Bermuda, 28th May, 1900; ag. chief just. in Oct. and Nov., 1911; ex-officio mem. of exec. coun.; M.L.A., 1895-1897, 1900-1906, and from 1907; ret.

GRAY, THEODORE GRANT, M.B., Bac. Surg.—B. 1884; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; ast. med. offr., 1911; med. supt., Nelson (N.Z.) mental hosp., 1920; med. supt., Auckland mental hosp., 1924; dep. inspr.-gen. mental hosp., N.Z., 1925; inspr.-gen., 1927.

GRAY, MAJOR WILLIAM BAIN.—B. 1886; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; Univ. of Edinburgh; M.A., 1st cls. hons., Gladstone Prize and Ph.D., and New Coll., Oxford, B.Litt.; some time Carnegie Research Fellow; lect., Tech. Coll., Brighton; examnr., Univ. of Edinburgh. Oxford and Cambridge joint bd. and Oxford local exams.; 2nd lieut., 6th Batt., the Royal Scots, Feb., 1912; mom., Aug., 1914; adjt., Jan.-Dec., 1915; capt., Aug., 1916; gen staff, Aldershot commnd., Dec., 1918-Apr., 1921; major, Army Educnl. Corps, Nov., 1920; major, R.A.R.O., Mar., 1923; commr., educn., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1924; (appt. altered to dir., Nov., 1927); mem., educn. commn., Nov., 1924; chmn., Georgetown public free liby., May, 1926; mem., ct. of policy and combined ct., June, 1927; ohmn diamond and gold industries comsn., Sept., 1927; mem. first exec. and leg couns. under reformed constitution, July, 1928; ag. col. sec. and gov.'s dep. on various occasions.

GREAVES, AUBREY VERNON, M.B. (Tor.), M.C.P. & S. (Ont.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—B. 1886; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Univ. of Toronto; Milne med. in trop. med. Univ. of L'pool; on active serv. as capt., Canadian A.M.C., 1918-19; govt. bacteriologist and asst. med. offr. of health and med. offr. in charge V.D. clinic and infant welfare clinic in addn., 1926; sent to Miami, Florida, to investigate epidemic of smallpox, 1926; ag. ch. med.-offr., Apr.-Dec., 1927; bacteriologist, Bahamas, 1928; asst. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1929; bacteriologist, 1931; lect. in forensic med. and toxicology, Univ. of Hong Kong.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM HERBERT, K.T. BACH. (1904), K.C. — B. 1857; B.A., Oxon., honours. juris., 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 1889; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. and of bd. of educn., 1888-1902; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; ch. just., Apr., 1902; ret.

GRECH, WYNDHAM, LL.D. (Malta), 1913.— Called to the bar (Grays Inn), 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.C., 1916; capt., R.A.F., 1918; sp. serv., Malta and Rome, 1917; attd., Italian Sup. Comd., 1918-19 (Chevalier, Crown of Italy); transf., unemployed list (R.A.F.), 1919; asst. legal adviser, Seychelles, 1921; ag. legal adviser and crown pros., 14th Mar., 1921 to 25th Sept., 1922 and in 1924; ag. ch. just., Dec., 1923 to Jan., 1924, Mar.-Oct., 1925 and Apr. to July, 1927.
GREEN, CLAUDE.—B. 1879; entd. Trinidad civ. serv., Mar., 1896; ch. clk. to gen. man. of rlyws., Mar., 1917.

GREEN, FREDERICK WALTER VICTOR.—B. 1891; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; 6th cls. clk., oust., 1914; asst. comrny., 1917; sec., excise bd., July, 1923; 3rd gte. comrny., July, 1925; 2nd gr. offr., dist. admstr., Jan., 1932; ag. ch. commissary and dist. commr., Georgetown and E. Bank, Demerara, May, 1934.

GREEN, GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster City Schl.; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., The Buffs; wounded in France; temp. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920; miny. of lab., Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1920; C.O., Dec., 1920 to June, 1922; apptd. cler. offr., C.O., 21st June, 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1928.
GREEN, GEORGE C.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

GREEN, GEORGE C., M.B.E. (1926)—B. 1897; clerical asst., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1912; 6th cls. clk., 1913; 3rd cls. clk., 1918; sec. to comttee, for considering disposal of colony lands, 1917; 2nd cls. clk., 1920; sec. to civ. serv. comn., 1923; sec., Br. Guiana section, Wembley, 1924; prin. clk., 1924; sec., rly. comn., 1925; liaison offr., parly. delegation to Br. Guiana, 1926; sec., Br. Guiana delegation to W. Indian confee., London, 1926; sec., civ. serv. comn., 1926-27; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk. exec. and leg. coun., various occasions, 1926-30; sec., Br. Guiana constitution comn., 1927; pvt. sec., O.A.G. in 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1930; asst. col. sec., 1931; prin. asst. col. sec., 1934.

GREEN, HAROLD.—B. 1877; ed. at Queen Mary's Gram. Schl., Hants.; entd. Royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1907; asst. supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1911; seconded for mil. serv., 1917-20; supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1920; J.P., 1922.

GREEN, MAURICE JOHN, V.D.—B. 1878; ed. Sea Point pub. schl. and S.A. Coll.; asst. clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1896; clk. of papers, 1897; comttee, elk., Cape leg. coun., 1904, and Ux. senate, 1910; gent. ush., black rod, 1916; clk. ass't, 1926; clk. of senate, 1930; sworn trs. (Eng. Dutch); sec. and shorthand writer var. govt. comms.; asst. sec. speaker's confee. on futur. const. senate; var. ass't, Anglo-Boer, in W.P.M.K. Great war, in Ger. S.W. Afr. as nav. trans. Offr., in Harwich light cruiser. squad. and we cabinet secretariat; comdr., cdmg. Cape Town base.

GREEN, RICHARD THOMAS BONFIELD, M.D., B.S. (Melb.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—B. 1871; ed. Melbourne Church of Eng. Gram. Schl. and Tritton Coll., Univ. of Melbourne; med. offr., F.M.S. June, 1925; pathologist II, inst. med. rec't, F.M.S., Mar., 1926; malaria reach. offr., I, med. resch., Jan., 1929; Milne Medal, Liv. Sch. T.M., Mar., 1930; rep. S.S. and F.M.S. at Int. Cong. on Malaria, Algiers, May, 1930; mem. of malaria comn., health organiz., L. of N., Dec., 1930; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Res., Apr., 1932 Feb., 1933; bacteriologist, do., 1934.

GREENBERG, LEOPOLD.—B. 1885; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, and S. Africar Coll., Cape Town; B.A., LL.B.; called to Transvaal bar, 1911; practised at Johannesburg, 1911-12; puisne judge, Transvaal Prov. divn., sup. ct., Nov., 1924.

GREENE, HON. COL. EDWARD, MACKENZIE, C.M.G. (1909), K.C.V.D.—Hon. colonel-in-arms served in Zulu war, 1878-1879 (medal, with clas.), S. African war, 1899-1902, in command of Natal Carbineers (Queen's medal, with three clasps, twice mentioned in despatches); mem. of Nat. legis. assem., 1893 to date of Union; m.t. of rlys. and harbours, 13th July, 1908; Nat. deleg. to S. African National Convention Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; represented Nat. at Impl. Confee. on defence, 1909; mem. of rlys. and harbours bd., Union of S. Africa, 1909-1916.

GREENE, MAURICE CHERRY.—B. 1871; ed. at Rathmines schl. and St. Stephen's Green schl., Dublin, and Dublin Univ.; R.A. 12th student, King's Inns, Dublin, 1910-1911; asst. offr., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; served with W.A.F.F., 1914-15; station mag., 1918; sta. mag., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1928.

GREENFIELD, LIEUT.-COL. HERSH, O.B.E., R. of O.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African War, 1901-02; joined R.A. as lieut., May, 1903; served in India, 1903-08; maj., May, 1908; attd. staff coll., 1910-12; did research work, N. and S. China, 1912-14; served in Great war, 1914-18 Tsingtau opera., may to July, 1915; D.A.D.V.S., 10th divn., Gal., Greater Macedonia, Serbia, Bulgaria, 1915-16; temp. lieut.-col., 1916; D.A.D.V.S., France and Belgium, 1917-18; ment. in despatches; A.D.V.S. (lieut. col. temp.) Rhine Army, 1919; served in Waziristan opera., 1920 as D.A.D.V.S.; D.D.V.S. (temp. lieut.-col.) Wazir Force, 1921; ment. in despatches; D.A.D.V.S., India and in B.C., 1921-24; exec. duty, Aldershot, 1925-28; var. offr., Barbados, Oct., 1928.

GREENHALGH, H., B.Sc., Hons. (Vic't).—B. 1888; agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1910; lecturer in agric. science to students of training coll., Antigua; asst. inspr. of schls., Barbados, 1912; lecturer in educ. and master of method, training inst., Compton Coll.; ag. chief inspr. of schls., Trinidad, Jan. to Oct., 1915.

GREENHILL, MARCUS ARTHUR.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Sep., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on military service, May, 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1923; staff offr., July, 1934.

GREENIDGE, CHARLES WILTON WOOD.—B. 1889; educ. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Nat. Sci. Tripos) 1910; LL.B., 1911; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1912; 1st cls. hon. criminal law; admitted to bar, Barbados, 1912; parochial treas., St. James', Barbados, 1915; mag., dist. D. St. Kitts, 1919; mag., dist. D. Barbados, 1923; judge, ct. of appl., Barbados, 1925; mag., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1927; ag. solr.-gen., Oct., 1928 to June, 1929; ag. puisne judge in 1929; ag. atty.-gen., Oct. to Nov., 1929; ag. regis., Oct.-Nov., 1930; ag. solr.-gen., Jan., 1931; ch. just., Br. Honduras, 1932.

GREENLAND, STANLEY ARTHUR.—Served with Natal Field Force, Rebellion, 1906; clk., govt., sec. dept., Papua, Mar., 1912; J.P., Sept., 1912; ag. ast. res. mag., Ioma, May, 1913; ag. ast. res. mag., Kairuku, Mar., 1915; inspr., dept. native affairs, Mar., 1917; ag. curator, intestate estates, regis.-gen., etc., Apr., 1917; ag. inspr., native affairs, 1918; ag. Papua govt. agt., Sydney, N.S.W., Dec., 1919; travelling inspr., native affairs, July, 1922; J.P. for N.S.W., Oct., 1923; ag. mem., land bd., Papua, Nov., 1924; ch. inspr., native affairs, Dec., 1924.
GREENSHIELDS, HON. ROBERT A. E., LL.D., D.C.L.—B. 1861; ed. Danville Acad. and McGill Univ., Canada; ag. ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1929; ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1933.

GREENWAY, PERCY JAMES, F.L.S.—B. 1897; ed. East Anglian Inst. of Agr., Chelmsford, R. Horticultural Soc., Wisley, Ripley, and R. Botanic Gardens, Kew; milv. serv., France and Italy, 1916-19; herbarium ast., R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1922-25; do., and ag. demonstr., systematic botany, Imp. Forestry Inst., Oxford, 1925-28; systematic botanist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

GREGG, JAMES REALI (B.A., Cantab.)—B. 1899; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Harvard and Cambridge Unv.; called to N. Ireland bar, 1923; mem., Gray's Inn (Lond.), and King's Inn (Dublin); chmn., ct. of referees under Min. of Lab., N. Ireland, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., St. Vincent, May, 1929; atty.-gen., Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just. on several occasions; govt. rep. on St. Vincent arrowroot bd.; attd. to C.O. for short period, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Jan., 1933.

GREGOROWSKI, REGINALD ADDINGTON.—2nd clk., govt. sec.'s office, O.F.S., Dec., 1895; priv. sec. to state pres., Mar., 1896; landdrost clk., Bethlehem, Dec., 1897; priv. sec. to state pres., July, 1898; landdrost clk., Wespen, Jan., 1899; prin., govt. schl., Reidsburg, O.R.C., Oct., 1900; astt. clk., educ. dept., Mar., 1901; ch. clk. and regis., educ. dept., July, 1901; prin. clk., col. sec.'s off., June, 1909; ch. clk. to admintr., O.F.S. prov., Aug., 1910; prov. sec., O.F.S., Oct., 1924.
GRELL, JESSIE MITCHINSON POTTER, M.B., B.S. Cantab., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1880; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, June, 1908; dist. med. offr., Aug., 1912.
GRENFELL-HICKS, J. B.—B. 1891; Rhodesian pol., 1911-16; lieut., Rhodesia Native Regt., 1916-19; Rhodesian adminstr., 1919-20; astt. supt., Kenya pol., Nov., 1920; staff offr., 1923; supt., 1926.

GRENIER, NORMAN ST. JOHN CHARLES.—B. 1879; clk., serv., Perak, 1894; ag. astt. audr., F.M.S., Apr., 1902; acct., audit dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1904; financi. astt., P.W.D., Perak, Jan., 1906; secy., san. bd., Selangor, Mar., 1911; regis., vehicles, Selangor, Mar., 1913; ag. rev. audr., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, 1919; ag. rev. audr., Perak, 1920 and 1921; ag. state treas., Perak, 1920; ag. astt. audr.-gen., F.M.S., 1921; acct., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1924.

GRETTON, RONALD HENRY.—B. 1897; B.A. (Br. Col. Canada); trop. African serv course, Oxford, 1928; cadet, Nigeria, 1929; seconded, Gambia, 1931; pvte. sec. to ag. gov., 1931 and 1933; astt. col. sec., 1932; ag. comsnr., 1934.

GRICE, NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1893; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; attd., Chinese sect., Perak, Feb., 1923; attd., cust. dept., Mar., 1923; attd., Chinese sect., Jan., 1925; astt. prot., Chinese, Selangor, Jan., 1925; astt. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Feb., 1927; astt. contra. rubber-S'pore, Mar., 1927; astt. dist. judge, S'pore, in addn., Aug., 1927; clm. III., Oct., 1929; 2nd mag., S'pore, Sept., 1933.

GRIER, Sir SELWYN MACREGOR, KT. Bach. (1934), C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1878; ed. at Marlboro' Coll. (classical scholar) and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (classical scholar); 2nd class tripos, 1900; 1st class French and English special, 1901; B.A. 1900; called to the bar, Nov., 1910; astt. res., Northern Nigeria, 1906; passed in Hausa, 1907; 3rd class res., Oct. 1908; in charge of Zaria prov., April, 1910; and from May to Nov., 1911; sec., native affrs., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, Aug., 1929; ag. gov. for various periods 1929-1934; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Windward Is., 1935.

GRIERSON, CHARLES HENRY ALEXANDER.—B. 1889; ed. privately and St. Paul's Schl.; served local forces end 2nd Batt. Loyal N. Lancs. Regt. in E. African campaign, Aug. 1914 to Jan., 1920; Turkana expdn., 1915; astt. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1916; adminis. offr., 1st grade, Apr., 1921; dep. prov. comsnr., Jan., 1926; prov. comsnr., Feb., 1930.

GRIFFIN, Sir CHARLES JAMES, KT. Bach. (1923), K.C., Ireland (1920), K.C., Gibraltar, (1914).—B. 1875; B.A. (hons.), Royal Univ., Ireland; 1st scholar in modern literature, exhib. bitioner; Chancellor's gold medallist; called to the Irish Bar, June, 1898; went Connaught circuit; apptd. Crown prosecutor, B.C.A. Protectorate (now Nyasaland), July, 1901; ag. chief judicial offr. and H.B.M. vice-consul, Nov., 1901 to June, 1902; ag. judge of High Court, Feb. to Oct., 1904 and Nov., 1905 to Feb., 1906; atty.-gen., Nov., 1905; judge of High Court, Feb., 1906, and member of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chairman of comsn. on trading and licencing, May to Sept., 1909 (recl. thanks of Prot. govt.); sole comsnr. for revision of Nyasa-land laws, 1913, and recl. thanks of Prot. govt.; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1914; King's proctor for prize ct. proceedings; K.C. for the colony, Dec., 1914; chief justice, Leeward Is., 1919; sole comsnr. for revision of laws of Antigua and Montserrat, 1921; ch. just., high ct., Uganda, 1921-32; sole comsnr. for revision of laws of Uganda, 1923, and recl. thanks of govt. in coun.; chmn., Bataka land comsn., 1924.

GRIFFIN, Nevill Alfred Malcolm.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's; pol. probr., S. Sttlmnts., 1908; Feb., 1911; astt. supt. pol., Penang, Mar., 1912; astt. supt. pol., Malacca and astt. prot., Chinese, July, 1912; seconded, Kedah, Oct., 1914; adjt., S.S. pol., Oct., 1916; supt. pol. (C.P.O.), Penang, Mar., 1923; C.P.O., Perak, July, 1931; dep. comsnnr., Selangor, 1932; ag. comsnnr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1933.
GRIFFITH, CARL.—Asst. auditor, Gold Coast, Jan., 1920.

GRIFFITH, GERALD VICT ALEXANDER, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dublin).—B. 1903; med. offr., med. dept., Hong Kong, 1930; med. offr. in ch. of officers' families, Victoria Gaol, infectious disease hosp. and pol. surg., Hong Kong, 1931.

GRIFFITH, SIR WM. BRADFORD, KT. BACH. (1898).—B. 1858; Gilchrist scholar, 1877; B.A. (Lond.), 1880; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1881; ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast Col., Apr. to Oct., 1884; dist. comsnnr., 1885; ag. puisne judge, Lagos, Jan., and Cape Coast, May, 1885; ag. Queen's advoc., 1886 and 1887; compiled ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1887; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888; R.M., St. Catherine, Feb., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., May to Dec., 1893; ch. just., G. Coast Col., May, 1895; admtd. govt. of Lagos, July to Aug., 1896; dep. for gov. G. Coast, Aug. to Nov., 1897; revised ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1898 and 1903; retired, 1911.
GRIFFITHS, J. A., F.R.C.V.S., F.Z.S.—Vety. offr., Nyasaland, 1913; ch. vety. offr., Nyasaland, 1919; dep. ch. vety. offr., Nigeria, 1929.

GRIFFITHS, JEFFREY ARTHUR, F.R.C.V.S.—B. 1884; Ed. Univ. of Liverpool; veterinary offr., Nyasaland, 7th June, 1913; attached Intell. Corps, Nyasaland Field Force, Aug., 1914 to July, 1917; seconded to rinderpest comsnnr., German E. Africa, July, 1917 to May, 1919; ag. rinderpest comsnnr., Aug., 1918 to May, 1919; chief vet. offr., 14th June, 1919; ment. in desps. (gallantry in action) South Africa, 1901; ment. in desps., services with Field Force, Nyasaland, 1915 and 1917; ag. dir. of agr., Oct. to Dec. 1920.
GRIFFITHS, JOHN HAROLD CROFTS.—B. 1896; ed., Leatherhead Schl.; supt., educn. dept., N. Prova., Nigeria, 5th Apr., 1922.

GRIFFITHS, W., A.M.I.E.E.—Imp. Post Office, 1899; R.N.A.S., 1915; twice ment. in desps.; transf., unemployed list, 1919; seconded, Kenya and Uganda as engnr. asst., 1920; asst. tel. engr., 1922; ag. tel. engr., 1923.

GRIGG, SIR EDWARD WILLIAM MACLAY, K.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.V.O. (1920), C.M.G. (1919), C.V.O. (1919), D.S.O. (1918), M.C.—B. 1879; ed. Winchester (schol.) and New Coll., Oxford (schol.); 2nd cl. moda., 1900, 3rd cl. lit. hum., 1902, Gaisford Greek Verse Prize, 1902; joined editorial staff "Times," 1903; asst. edr., "Outlook," 1905-6; rejoined editorial staff of "Times," 1908; joined Grenadier Guards, 1914; became G.S.O.I., Guards divn.; mil. sec. to Prince of Wales, Canada, Australia, N.Z., 1919; relinquished comsnn. with rank of lieut.-col., 1921; pvt. sec., Mr. Lloyd George, 1921-22; sec., Rhodes trustees, 1923; M.P., Oldham, 1923-25; gov. and comdrr.-in-ch., and high comsnnr. for transport, Kenya, 1925-31.

GRIMBLE, ARTHUR FRANCIS, M.A., F.R.A.I.—B. 1888; ed. Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, France, Germany; cadet, Gilbert and Ellioe Is., Mar., 1914; passed cadet, Mar., 1915; dep. comsnnr. for W. Pacifico, Aug., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Tarau Mar., 1916; dist. offr. Aug., 1916; cent. Gilbert Feb., 1917; S. Gilberts, Oct., 1918; sp. comsnnr. to settle native land disputes throughout co. and organise records, Jan., 1920; ag. res. comsnnr., Apr., to July, 1920 and Jan. to Feb., 1921; dist. offr., 1924; ag. res. comsnnr., Mar. to Sept., 1924; res. comsnnr., Gilbert and Kilico Is., 1925; adminstr., St. Vincent, July, 1933.

GRIMMER, WARD CHIPMAN HAZEN, K.C.LL.D.—B. 1858; admitted atty., 1880; barrister, 1881; K.C., 1902; elected to prov. legia., N. Bruna, 1903; re-elected, 1908, 1912; surr.-gen. N. Bruna, 1908; atty.-gen., 1911; puisne judge, appeal ct. of N. Bruna, Jan., 1914.

GRINDLEY-FERRIS, IVON, K.C., B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (Cantab.).—B. 1876; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; admitted barrister, Int. Temple, June, 1900; advoc., sup. ct., Transvaal Nov., 1902; K.C., 1922; ag. judge, high ct. S.W. Africa in 1924 and 1928, and as judge, Transvaal prov. divn. during 1927; judge pro tempore, native high ct., Natal, Mar., 1930; seconded to Natal prov. divn., sup. ct., May, 1930; judg. sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1933.

GRIST, DONALD HONEY, Camb. Univ. Dip. Agric.—B. 1891; ed. Huntingdon Grammar Sch., Camb. County Schl. and Camb. Univ. dipl. of Agr.; agric. instr., agric. dept., F.M.S., June, 1914; on duty in Ceylon, Feb., 1921; agric. instr., Sultan Idris Coll., Tanjong Malim, in addn., Dec., 1922; agric. economist, 1924; regisr.-gen., statistics, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1931; agric. economist and edr., S.S. and F.M.S., Sept., 1931.

GRIST, MAJOR H. G. R. OF O., R. Fusiliers—B. 1881; ed. Aldenham Schl., Herts.; 2nd Bn., R. Fusiliers, 1899; lieut., 1900; cap., 1903; R. of O., 1907; S. African War, Q.M. med.; capt., 14th (S.) bn., Northumberland Fusiliers, Nov., 1914; B.E.F., France, major, Oct., 1915; ag. lieut.-col., July-Oct., 1916; capt., Abbeville area, 1918; ment. in desps. ch. of pol. and offr. commdg. local forces, St. Vincent, Jan., 1924.
GROBLER, HON. P. G. W., M.P.—Rustenburg elec. divn. in Union of S. Africa parliment, since 1915; min. of lands, 34th June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; min. for native affrs., Mar., 1933.

GROOM, HON. SIR LITTLETON ERNEST, K.C.M.G. (1924), K.C., M.A., LL.M.—B. 1876; M.P. for Darling Downs, Queensland; elect., 1st hse. of rep., C. of Aust., 1901; re-elected, 1903; mem. of royal comsns. on Iron Box, 1902, and Navigation Bills, 1904-5; several times Crown prosecutor, and twice ag. judge of the ct., Queensland; min. for home affairs, C. of Aust., July, 1905; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; min. of external affairs, June, 1909, to Apr., 1910; min. of trade and customs, June, 1913-Sep., 1914; hon. min., Feb., 1917; vice-pres. exec. coun., Nov., 1917 to Mar., 1918; min. for war and rlws., Mar., 1918; ag. atty.-gen. from Apr., 1918; atty.-gen., Dec., 1921; atty.-gen., Priv. ministry, 1923; speaker, house of rep., Jan., 1926-29; re-el., M.H.R., Dec., 1931.

GROSSMITH, C. A.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., E. Lance Regt., 1916; lieut., 1918; served Mesopotamia and N. Persia, 1917-19; ment. in desps.; demob., 1919; attd., Br. mission to Czechoslovakia, 1919; temp. employed, cler. office, enemy debts, Berlin, 1921-23; enemy debts dept., London, 1924-26; cler. offr., C.O., 12th July, 1926; asst. prin., 1st Oct., 1934.

GUILLEMARD, Sir Laurence Nunn, G.C.M.G. (1927), K.C.B. (1910), K.C.M.G. (1923), C.B. (1905).—B. 1862; ed. Charterhouse, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; entered Treasury, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir W. Harcourt and Sir M. Hicks-Beach, 1892-1902; deputy chmn., board of inland rev., 1902; chmn., bd. of customs and excise, 1909; gov., S. Stlitlms., 7th Feb., 1920; ret., 1927.

GULLETT, Hon. Sir Henry Somer, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1878; served European War, 1915-18; dir., Australian War Museum and offi. historian, A.I.F. in Palestine; dir., Australian immigrn. bureau, Oct., 1920; M.H.R., C'wealth of Australia from 1925; min. for trade and cust., 1928-29; dep. leader of opposition, Nov., 1929; min. for trade and cust., Lyons' ministry, 1932.
GULLIVER, E. G.—Cust. asst., Kenya, July, 1916; collr., cust., Jan., 1926.

GULLOCH, WILLIAM SUTHERLAND.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1914; lieut., Scottish Rifles, 1916; lieut., Gold Coast Regt., 1918; capt., Gold Coast Regt., 1919; served France and Togoland; asst. commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1919; ag. commr., pol., Eastern prov., 1924-5; commr., pol., Cent. prov., 1926; ch. pol., Gibraltar, Nov., 1927.

GUMMER, WALLACE ALBERT.—B. 1880; royal observatory, Greenwich, 1895; ditto, Cape of Good Hope, Jan., 1900; trig. survey, Transvaal and O.R.C., Feb., 1903; surv., rev. survey dept., Selangor, Nov., 1907; asst. supt., rev. survey br., Jly., 1914; ditto, trig. br., Nov., 1914; supt., rev. surveys, Singapore, Aug., 1924; supt., surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1927; ditto, Kedah, Jly., 1929; supt., rev. surveys, Perak, 1932; senr. supt., 1933.

GUNARATNA, WALTER DANDRIS, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Galle kach., Jan., 1928; attd., Kandy kach., May, 1928; ag. pol. mag., Dumbara, Dec., 1928 to Jan., 1929; extra office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1929; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1930; pol. mag., Putalam, Oct., 1931; office asst., Badulla kach., Aug., 1932.

GUNASEKARA, SEPTIMUS THEODORUS, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.P.H. (Lond.), L.M.S. (Ceylon).—B. 1881; 1st house surg., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Sept., 1904; med. offr., Kandy, Jan., 1907; ag. 3rd surg., gen. hosp., Colombo, Oct., 1908; dir., ankylostomiasis campaign, Dikoya and Amella, Oct., 1917 to Mar., 1920; senr. offr., Apr., 1920; ag. senr. commr., Oct., 1922; asst. dir., senr. services, Oct., 1925.

GUNAWARDANA, DON CHARLES RAJAKABUNA, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1924; attd., Matara kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, Jaffna kach., Apr., 1926; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1927; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Sept., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura, Jan., 1930; do., Colombo, Jan., 1931; do., Batticaloa, Mar., 1931; office asst., Kandy kach., Dec., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1934.

GUNDY, JOHN ARNOLD, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Rea Sch. and Faraday House; engrn.-in-ch.'s office, G.F.O., Lond., 1920; asst. engrn., posts and tels., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; engrn., Mar., 1929; divnl. engrn., Apr., 1933.
GUNN, Hon. John.—B. 1885; M.P. for Adelaide 1915; leader, parly. Labour Party, 1918; premier and treas., S. Australia, Apr., 1924-26; mem., migration and development comm. of Australia, 1926.
GURNEY, HENRY LOVELL GOLDSWORTHY.—B. 1898; asst. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921; dist. offr., 1923; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927; asst. sec. comm., Jamaica, 1935.

GURUSINHA, KANKANAN TANATURO SUMATRIPALA.—B. 1877; apptd. cls. V Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1925; office asst., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1925; addnl. asst. commr., stamps, May, 1930; attd., Galle kach., Oct., 1933.

GUTHRIE, Hon. Hugh, P.C. (Can.), K.C.—B. 1866; ed. Coll. Inst. Guelph and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; el. to H. of C., Canada at g.e., 1900; re-el. at g.e., 1904, 1908, 1911, 1917, 1921, 1925 and 1926; solr. gen., Canada, 1917; min. of militia and defence, 1920; min. of national defence in Meighen admstrn., 1926; min. of just. and atty.-gen. in Bennett admstrn., 1930.

GUTSCHE, Hon. CLERMANS, B.A., LL.B., C.B.E., V.D.—B. 1877; ed. Dale Coll., King Williams Town and S. African Coll., Cape Town (gold med., lit. and philos.); on active serv., Anglo-Boer War (2 meda. and clasps); admitted to Cape bar, 1904; edr., Cape Town repts., 1908-10; and joint edr., Repts. of Cape Prov. Divn.; served in Great War, 1914-18; offr. in commd., Table Bay defences; Gen. Serv. and Victory med., twice ment. in desps., C.B.E. and V.D.; resumed professorship, law, S.A. Coll., 1919; first judge, high ct., S. West Africa, 1919; judge, eastern dists. local divn., sup. ct., Grahamstown, 1930.

GYBBON-MONYPENNY, RICHARD MONTAGU, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1927; attd., Badulla kach., Jan., 1928; office asst., Kurunegala kach., May, 1930; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1933; attd., rubber contriv.'s office, July, 1934.
HAARER, ALEC ERNEST, F.L.S.—B. 1894; ed. Birkenhead Inst., Battersea Gram. Sch. and Royal Horticultural Coll. (dipl. hort.); dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; agrl. lect., 1930.
HADDRILL, CYRIL F.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th Apr., 1920.

HAIG, RUPERT ALEXANDER.—B. 1904; ed. Glenalmond and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. (2nd cls. hon. schil., jurisprudence), 1925; called to bar, 1927; asst. dist. commr., Nyasaland Prot., 1928; ag. asst. atty.-gen., various periods, 1929-34; ag. town mag., 1931; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1933; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1934.

HALES, EVERETT OLIVE.—B. 1876; entd. N.Z. pub. trust office, 1894; offr. in charge, wills branch, 1914; inspr., 1919; local dep. pub. trustee, 1920; asst. pub. trustees, 1922; supt., N.Z. states advances office, 1931; pub. trustee, 1934.
HALFYARD, Hon. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Fogo, Newfoundland, 1913; min. of agric. and mines, 1917; M.E.C., 1917; col. sec., 1918.

HALIFAX, 3RD VISCOUNT (cr. 1866), Hon. EDWARD FREDERICK LINDLEY WOOD.—B. 1881; ed. Eton and Christ Church and All Souls, Oxford (M.A. and fellow); major, Yorkshire Dragons; M.P., Ripon divn., Yorks., 1910-25; under sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1921; min. of educ., Oct., 1922; created 1st baron Irwin, 1925; viceroy of India, 1926; pres. bl. of educ., 1931.
HALL, GUTHRIE.—B. 1897; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1915; lieut., 1917; served with Waziristan Field Force, 1919; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1921.

HALL, JULIAN DUDLEY, B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1910; passed cadet, July, 1913; supernum. offr., cls. V; asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, Johore, Feb., 1914; supernum. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1920;

HALL, THOMAS DONALD HORN, LL.B. (N.Z.)—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Sch., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; N.Z. dept. of agric., 1901; ast. law draftsman, 1923; clk. of house of reps., 1930.

HALLIDAY, L.G.—B. 1883; ed. Ho's Sch. and Liverpool Univ.; mily. serv., Mesopotamia 1916-1920; ch. engr., "Lord Milner," Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; in charge, reconditioning "Liamba," Dec., 1925; ch. engr., "Liamba," 1927.

HALLIER, CAPT. FRANK COLLINGS, M.C., R.O.—B. 1888; U.D.F. and Cape Corpsa, 1913-14; African Rebellion and German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; G.E.A., 1916-18; seconded as ast. off., civ. admin., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918; admin. off., 1st gde., Feb., 1922; j.p. prov. commr., 1933; prov. commr., 1935.
HALLIFAX, JOHN GORDON, C.S.L.L.—B. 1883; clk., law dept. (Cape), 1903; clk., pc serv. comm., 1913; ch. clk., ditto, 1927; prov. serv. inspr., 1930.

HALLINAN, MAJOR THOMAS JOHN, C.B.E. (1832), O.B.E. (1924), M.B., B.S. (London), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), I.R.C.P. (Lond.). D.I.M. (Dublin Univ.).—B. 1886; ed. Archbishop Trenchon's Sch. and Charing Cross Hosp. (examin. Pereira prize); commd., R.A.M.C., 1915; served, B.E.F., Egypt, 1914-16; Mesopotamia, 1916-20; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Amarah, 1917; D.A.D.M.S. (San.), Basrah, 1918-20; th. min. in despo.; seconded, govt. of Iraq, 1920; off., 1920; inspr.-gen., civ. and health services, 1922; ret., R. of O., 1925; suptg. med. off., chmn., cent. bd. of health, chinu., quarantine, bd., Jamaica, 1933; M.L.C., 1944.

HALL-JONES, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1910).—B. 1851; ed. at public schls.; arrived New Zealand, 1875; mem. of house of representatives for Timaru, 1881-82; min. of pub. wks. and marine, 1896; premier, treas., min. for educn. and labour, June, 1908; resig. premiership, and assumed portfolio of pub. wks. and rlys. in Ward ministry, Aug., 1906; high commr. in Lond., 1908-1912; M.L.C. since 1913.

HALLOCK, ANANDA RAJAH.—B. 1904; ed. Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1927; attd. Puttalam kach., Dec., 1927; attd., Kalutara kach., Nov., 1928; office asst., ditto, Oct., 1929; ditto, Jaffna kach., Feb., 1931; pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1932.

HALLOWES, BARABOON HENRY COLLINS, B.A.I.—B. 1884; ed. Corrig Sch., Kingstown Trinity Coll., Dublin; survr., valuation & landry. survey of Ireland, 1910; 2nd cls. surv., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; capt., R.F.A., 1919; 1st cls. surv., 1921.

HAM, GORDON LUPTON.—B. 1885; ed. Fox Edward's Sch. and Christ's Coll., Camb.: cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; lent to Keats Oct., 1913; cls. IV., S.S., Jan., 1918; cls. couns., Sept., 1919; 1st ast. col. sec., S.S.; clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; ast. off., assmrs., Dec., 1920; ag. sec. to high commr., Feb., 1921; ag. ast. adviser to govt. of Kelantan, Apr., 1921; ag. lat. ast. sec., R.S.S., Aug., 1924; ag. dep. treas. (B.), S.S., Dec., 1924; ag. treas. (A.), Mar., 1925; cls. II., Nov., 1925; to res., Perak, Nov., 1925; cls. IB., Jan., 1926; mun. commrs., Penang, May, 1926; Br. advr., Trengganu, Mar., 1931; off., I.A., dep. pres., munio. commr., Spore, Nov., 1932; under sec., S.S., June, 1933; ag. col. S.S., Dec., 1933.

HAM, HENRY SOMERSET MCKENZIE.—B. 1878; clk., recr. of rev., O.F.S., 1900; clk., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1901; ch. clk. and examt., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1904; astt. regar., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1912; ditto, Pretoria, 1928; ditto, Bloemfontein, Oct., 1928; regar., deeds, Pretoria, 1933; do., Cape Town, Feb., 1934.

HAM, LAURENCE PETER VAN ZYL.—Ck., rly. and cent. tel. office, 1898; clk., rea. mag., Hoopstad, June, 1902; A.R.M., Hoopstad, Aug., 1901; Jacobadal, Nov., 1903; Thaba 'Nchu, Feb., 1907; Winburg, Feb., 1910; D.A.R.M., Fouriesburg, Aug., 1911; Trompsburg, May, 1915; mag., Philippolos, Aug., 1919; Winburg, 1920; Wakkerstroom, May, 1920; ag. mag., Harrismith, Apr., 1925; inspr., pub. serv. comm., Feb., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1928; sec., dept. of mines and industries, Sept., 1928.

HAMBLIN, R. A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar., 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. astt., 1st Apr., 1925.

HAMILTON, HON. ATAM.—B. 1880; ed. tech. schl., Dunedin, N.Z.; Knox Coll. and Otago Univ.; mem., N.Z. H. of R., 1919-22 and since 1925; P.M.G., min. of tels., min. of lab. since 1931; min., internal affrs., 1931-33; min., employment, 1933.

HAMILTON, ANDREW TODD.—B. 1879; astt. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1910; on mily. serv., Dec., 1917 to Feb., 1919; astt. mast., cls. II, Jan., 1920; astt. mast., 1921; cls. I, Mar., 1926; headm., Ellis Kadoorie schl. for Indians, Feb., 1929; senr. mast., 1933.

HAMILTON, ARCHIBALD JOHNSTON.—B. 1892; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; ent service of govt. of Trinidad, Apr., 1911; mag., county of St. Patrick, Jan., 1927; do., St. George East, Jan., 1933; astt. mag., St. George West, Mar., 1934.

HAMILTON, ERIC WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's; exhibtn., Sid., Suss. Coll., Camb., 1906; scholar, 1907; B.A., 1909; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., 1911; J.P., 1914; astt. sany. dept., 1915; astt. impts. and expts. office in addn., 1916; astt. to sany. dept. for sp. serv., 1918; dist. offr., 1918; dist. offr., South, 1920 and 1922; 2nd pol. mag., 1922 and 1923; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1924, 1928 and 1931; 2nd astt. col. sec., 1926; dep. clk. of couns. in addn., 1927; ast. 1st pol. mag., 1929; extra assist. col. sec., 1930; ag. P.M.G., 1931; ag. S.I.E., 1932; 1st pol. mag., 1933 and 1934.

HAMILTON, PERCIVAL JOHN ALBERT.—Col. audit dept., Aug., 1915; astt. audr., Nyasaland, Sept., 1915; audr., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1922; audr., Leeward Is., Dec., 1923; audr., Fiji, Br. Solomons Is. Prot., Gilbert & Ellice Is. Col., May, 1928; sec. under to audit accounts of condominium govt., New Hebrides, Apr., July, 1929; astt. audr., Hong Kong, 1932.

HAMILTON, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM KT. BACH (1918).—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; clas. scholar, B.A., 1889(honours); M.A., 1892; student, Inner Temple, 1893; called to the bar, 1895; dist. comnr., Lagos, 1895 to 1897; regar., E. Africa Prot., 1897; town mag., 1899; astt. judge and admstr.-gen., 1900; judge of high court of E. Africa, Aug., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comnr., Oct., 1904; prin. judge, E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1905; chief justice, 1911; ret., 1920; parly. under sec. of state for the cols., 3rd Sept., 1931; reapptd., 9th Nov., 1931; resigned, 1932.
HAMILTON, HON. R. A. B. (Master of Belhaven).—Apptd. 2nd polit. offr., Aden, Sept., 1934. (In mily. serv. prior to joining polit. dept.)

HAMZAH, BIN ABDULLAH.—B. 1890; ed. Malay Coll., K Kangsar; cler. serv., F.M.S., June, 1908; sttlmt. offr., Selangor, May, 1909; ag. astt. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Apr., 1916; passed cadets' law exam., Dec., 1917; Mala; passed grade II, Jan., 1918; ditto, grade I, Jan., 1919; ditto, apl. cls., Jan., 1920; offr., cls. V, Oct., 1921; astt. offr. assignee, Perak, Jan., 1923; offr. cls. IV; dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1925; 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, June, 1929; dist. offr., K. Langat, Feb., 1930; offr. cls. III, mag., Seremban, Oct., 1930; dist. offr., U. Selangor, May, 1934.
HANCOCK, CYRIL GEORGE.—B. 1902; ed. Kingsland Grange, Shrewsbury, Bromagrove Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; astt. dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1929; dist. comnr., Jan., 1931.

HANCOCK, Capt. Otho Lewis, O.B.E. (1925)—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Exeter Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Aug., 1914; lieut., 1914; capt., 1915; wounded, 1915; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., 1916; staff offr., local forces and adjutant, volunteers, Barbados, 1917; gov., H.M. prison, Barbados, 1920; ag. senr. pol. mag., Aug. to Sept., 1922; comnr., Virgin Is., 1923; dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Aug., 1926; inspr., prisons, S. Sttlmts. and supr., Singapore prison, 1930; inspr., prisons, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

HANCOCK, WILLIAM JOHN, M.I.C.E., M.I.E.E.—B. 1865; ed. Dublin and Glasgow Univ.; G.S. & W. Rly. (Ireland workshops, Inchicore); Electric Lighting and Telephone Co. (Ireland); supt. of telephones, W. Australia, 1885; supt. of telegraphs, 1890; govt. electrical engnr., 1894; mem. of senate, Univ. of W. Aust.; pres., W. Aust. Inst. of Engineers, 1917-18; pres., Royal Soc. of W. Australia, 1918-19; hon. local sec., Inst. Elec. Engnr. (Lond.); liqut., A.A.M.C.; D.Sc., Univ.of W.Aust., 1921; Kelvin gold med., R. Soc'y. of W. Aust., 1924; hon. life mem., Inst. of Engnr.s of Aust.

HANDS, ANDREW CHARLES.—B. 1893; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; on war serv., Aug., 1914-Nov., 1919; col. audt. dept., Nov., 1921; astt. audr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1922; senr. astt. audr., Kenya, Oct., 1932.

HANDS, ARTHUR L. C., M.B.E.—B. 1880; clk. in P.O., St. Helena, Sept., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'off., Apr., 1898; regar. of births and deaths, 1904-07; ch. clk. and clk. to exec. coun., Jan., 1907; J.P., 1910; ag. crown prosecutor, clk. of the peace and pol. mag. during 1923.

HANDYSIDE, WILLIAM LUCAS, M.A. (Glas.), B.Sc. Econ. (Lond.), F.R.G.S., F.R. Econ. S.—B.1891; prizeman, homs. English, Glasgow Univ., 1913; 1st place language, English homs. schl., Glasgow Univ., 1914; astt. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, July, 1915; mast. of method, Hong Kong Univ. (in addn.), 1918-20; astt. mast., cls. II, Jan., 1920; lect. teachers classes, &c., tech. inst., 1917-20 and 1922-24; external examnr., Hong Kong Univ., geography, history of educn., 1922; head mast., Saiyingpun Schl., 1920; mast. of method(in addn.), Hong Kong Univ.: astt.mast., King's Coll., 1927; lect. on ethics and logic, Hong Kong Univ., 1927; astt. mast., Queen's Coll., 1928; lect. on polit. science, 1928; lect. on econ. hist. and statistics, 1929; headm., Yaumati schl., July, 1930; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 1931; lect., H.K. Univ., 1932-1933; headmr., Ellis Kadooni Sch., 1934.

HANKEY, Lieut.-Col. Sir, M.P.A., G.C.B. (1919); K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1912).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby; joined R.M.A., 1893; served in H.M.S. "Ramilles" flagship and 2nd flagship, Mediterranean, 1899-1901; naval intell. dept., 1902-6; granted temporary rank of Major whilst serving on a special comitee, 1906; intell. offr., Mediterranean, 1907; asst. sec., comitee of Imp. defence, 1908; apptd. sec., 1912; sec., war cabinet, from 1916; British sec., Peace Conf., 1919; comdr., Legion of Honour, 1917; commen- datore, Crown of Italy, 1918.

HANKINSON, Walter Crossfield, M.O. (1918).—B. 1894; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford (exhibitor); private, Royal Fusiliers, Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Yorks and Lancs. Regt., 9th Jan., 1915; lieut., 1st Apl., 1915; capt., 13th May, 1915; served in Egypt and France; demobilised, 28th Feb., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Feb., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1st Mar., 1923; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 22nd Jan., 1924 and to Mr. Amery, 20th Nov., 1924; ag. prin., 2nd Nov., 1925; rep. in Commonwealth of Australia of H.M.'s. govt. in U.K., 1931.

HANNAN, Gerald Hugh Crawford.—B. 1879; ed. Mercers' Schl., London; served in S. African War, 1900-02 (2 med., 5 clasps); 2nd cls. clk., exec. and leg. couns., Transvaal, Oct., 1902; 1st cls. clk., 1903; gentleman usher of black rod, leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907; clk., asst., prov. coun., Transvaal, 1910; clk. of prov. coun. and exec. comitee, 1917.

HARDING, Alfred J., C.M.G. (1930), C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; M.A., 1920; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comitee. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., 1909; sec. to Royal coman. on Mauritius, 1909; ass. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., 1910; ag. lat.clk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from 1st Apr., 1912; 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; sec., colonial blue-books comitee, 1917; ag. prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. African currency bd., 1926-1928; mem., Palestine currency bd., 1926-1928; dir., col. audit, 1st Oct., 1928.

HARDING, Sir Edward John, K.C.M.G. (1928), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comitee, 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to Dominions Royal Coman., Dec., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 10th Apl., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; on military service from 20th Nov., 1915, to 9th Apl., 1916; sec. to comitee. for stinted of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; ag. prin. clk., 1st Apl., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Dec., 1921; asst. under-sec. of state for dominions affairs, 30th July, 1925; permt. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 24th Jan., 1930.

HARDING, Sidney Walter.—B. 1895; I.D.S., (R.C.S.Ire.); house surg., Inc. Dent. Hosp., Dublin, 1925-29; demonstr., 1929-31: house surg., Middlesex Hosp., Lond., Mar. to Sept., 1931; dental surg., Falkland Is., Nov., 1931; served in Great War, 1914-19; B. Dublin Fusiliers, Leinster Regt. and R.A.F.; lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, 1932.
HARDING, William, B. Litt., LL.D.—B. 1898; matric., Malta Univ., 1912; B. Litt., 1915; LL.D., 1919; legal adviser to army, navy and air forces, 1929; judge, 1934.
HARDY, Thomas James.—B. 1900; ent. C.O., 3rd Mar., 1916; apptd. asst. clk. (afterwards cler. offr.), 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., higher grade, 5th Nov., 1928.

HARGREAVES, George McNhill, M.B., Ch.B., M.R.C.P. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.O.M.S. (Eng.).—B. 1901; Univ. of Edin. ad Univ. Coll., Lond.; asst. dist. med. offr., 1925; ag. gov't. bact. and pathologist, Jamaica, 1928; med., offr., Kenya, 1928; health offr., Hong Kong, 1934.

HARINGTON, Gen. Sir Charles, G.B.E. (1922), K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1917), D.S.O. (1909).—B. 1872; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and R.M. Coll., Sandhurst; ent. 2nd Batt., King's Liverpool Regt., 1892; served on staff, S. Africa, 1st-2nd 1900 (desp. twice, D.S.O.); European War, 1914-18 (desp., bt. lieut.-col. and c.l. comdr., Legion of Honour, maj.-gen.); instr., R.M. Coll., Sandhurst, gen. staff, W.O.; bgde. maj., 6th Infantry Brig.; G.S.O.L., B.G.C.S., Canadian Corps.; M.G.G.S. 2nd Army; dep. ch., Inf. gen. staff, 1918-20; G.O.C. Army of Black Sea, 1920-21; G.O.C. allied forces of occupation in Turkey, 1921-23; Northern command, 1st-2nd gen., 1927; G.O.C. in C., Western command, India, 1927-31; G.O.C. in C. Aldershot command, 1931; A.D.C. gen. to King since 1930; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Gibraltar, 1933.
HARKNESS, Edward Burns, C.M.G. (1922).—Entered treasy., New S. Wales, 1891; sec. to Premier's office, 1901-16; under sec., depts. of ch. sec. and public health, since 1916.
HARKNESS, J., L.R.C.P. & L.R.C.S. & L.R.F.P.S. (Edin. & Glas.), L.D.S. (Ed.), D.T.M. D.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1896; mil'y serv., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1927.
HARLEY, John A.—M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), certif., Lond. S.T.M. (with distinction), July, 1909; apptd. to W.A.M.S., Gambia, Apl., 1912; ag. prot. med. offr., Jan. to May, 1911.

HARMAN, Wm. Henry.—B. 1882; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apl., 1900; to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1906; transf'd. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902; staff offr., 8th Apr., 1930.

HARMER, John Daniel.—B. 1876; ed. Linlithgow and Trinity Old Stratford Schls., Edinburgh Univ., M.B., Ch.B., Edin., F.R.C.S., Edin.; war serv., 1914-17; med. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1917; surg. specialist, Apr., 1928; ag. D.D.M.S., 1934.

HARNETTY, Lieut. Edward.—B. 1887; ed. Brompton Oratory schl.; capt., Lincolnshire Yeomanry; lieut., Sierra Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., 1918-21; passed lower stand. exam. in Mandingo; asst. dist. comantr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1922; passed higher standard exam. in Mende; dist. comantr., May, 1928.

HARNEY, Egbert Ewart.—B. 1898; clk. to regiar-gen., Antigua, 1916; 3rd audit clk., 1st-2nd do., 1922; ch. clk., regiar's office, 1st-2nd comnr. for oaths, 1927; ag. regiar. and prov. marshal, Dominica, 1931.
HARPER, AUSTIN BYERS.—B. 1908; ed. Blundell's agr. dipl., Cedara, Natal, S. Africa; agr. dept., Nigeria, 1930; Jamaica constab., 1933.

HARPER, SIR CHARLES HENRY, K.B.E.(1930), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E.(1919).—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to bar, Inner Temple, May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, Jan., 1900; attld., Ashanti field force, June to Sept., 1900; asst. dist. commnr., Sept., 1901; ag. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1904; asst. col. sec., Feb., 1906; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Apr. to June, 1908; cantonment mag., Coomassie, Oct., 1908; prov. commnr., July, 1909; census offr., 1911; ag. ch. asst. and clk. of couns., May to June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912 and from May to Aug., 1914; attld., Togoland field force, Aug., 1914; sen. polit. offr., Togoland, Sept. to Dec., 1914; ch. asst. col. sec., Feb., 1915; ag. col. sec., in 1915, 1916 and 1917; ch. commnr., Ashanti, May, 1920; gov., St. Helena, Dec., 1924; assumed govt., Feb., 1925; ret., 1932.

HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900; conf. clk., Nov., 1912; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1922; sec., "Discovery" comttee, 1st June, 1928.

HARRAGIN, ALFRED ERNEST ALBERT, D.S.O., V.D.—B. 1877; sub.-inspr., constab., Trinidad, Feb., 1905; asst. supt., fire brigade, Port-of-Spain, Sept., 1910; sub. inspr., Tobago, divn., May, 1911; inspr., constab., 1912; senr. inspr., constab., Apr., 1934.

HARRAGIN, WALTER.—B. 1890; barrister-at-law; clk. of the peace and sub.-regir., San Fernando and Victoria, Trinidad, Mar., 1914; served with the army as temporary major; mag., Victoria, Sept., 1919; crown counsel, 1924; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to Oct., 1924 and from Jan., 1925 to Aug., 1926; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1927; ag. judge in 1927, 1929, 1930 and 1932; atty.-gen., Kenya, Oct., 1933.

HARRIS, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1917); C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); C.V.O. (1917); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1855; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Taunton student in com. law, Linc's Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Coms., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bndry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bndry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana bndry. arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comttee. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents office, 1908; ch. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; represented C.O. on advisory comttee. of comm. intell., B. of T., 1900-17; mem. of managing comttee. of Imp. Inst. 1909-16; gov. and c. in e., Newfoundland, Nov., 1917; assumed govt., Dec., 1917; ret., 1922; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial gvs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, DAVID JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., hoy clk., G.P.O., 1912; on mily. serv. (R.E. and R.A.F.), 8th Oct., 1915 to 27th Feb., 1919; tempy. employment clk., Miny. of Labour, 1st Oct., 1919 to 20th June, 1920; permanent ditto, 21st June, 1920 to 30th Sept., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Oct., 1922.

HARRIS, HENRY GLADSTONE BOLTON.—B. 1885; ed. Exeter Schl. and Cambourne Schl., mines; inspr. mines, F.M.S., 1910; ag. ast. warden, mines, Perak, 1914; ag. warden mines, Selangor, 1920; ag. warden mines, Perak, 1923; warden mines, N. Sembilan, 1927; warden, mines, Selangor, 1929; do., Perak, 1933; senr. warden, mines, F.M.S., 1934.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. serv. of Imp. Post Office, Jan., 1900; transf. to dept. of posts and tels., Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1902; resig., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia tel. serv., Jan., 1908; reasig., Jan., 1910; prov. postmr., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1910; ast. supt. posts and tels., F.M.S., June, 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian army res. of offrs., unattached, Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., 91st Punjabis, July, 1918; lieut., Mar., 1919; released from army serv., Oct., 1919; resumed duty, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1920; relinquished coman., I.A.R.O., Sept., 1920; contr., posts and tels., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; contr., P. and T., S.S., Jan., 1929; do., S'pore., June, 1931; ast. sec. for postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1932; dir., posts, P. and T. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1933.

HARRIS, PERCY GRAHAM.—B. 1894; ed. St. Bees Schl.; Law Soc.'s studentship, 1911; war serv. with King's (L'pool) Regt. and Nigeria, Regt., 1914-19; France, 1915-16; admtrve. offr., cl. IV, Nigeria, Jan., 1919; anthropological dipl. (Lond.), 1932; dep. res., Apr., 1933; admtrve. offr., cl. I, 1935.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland Prot., June, 1913; editor, govt. gaz., since 1914; clk., 1st grade, Oct., 1920; ag. ch. clk., secretariat, Nov. 1920 to June, 1921; ast. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. senr. ast. treas., in 1930 and 1931.

HARRIS, WILLIAM VICTOR, M.Sc., A.I.C.T.A., F.E.S.—B. 1904; B.Sc. (Agr.), Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1925; Cambridge, 1925-26; assoc., Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1927; ast. entomologist, dept. of agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

HARRISON, ERNEST, M.S.A. (Ivwa), B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A. (Hons.).—Lecturer in agr., schl. of agr., Grootfontein, Cape, 1904; prin., schl. of agr., Cedara, Natal, 1913; dep. dir. agr., Kenya, 1921; ng. dir. agr., Feb. to Nov., 1922 and Mar.-Oct., 1921; dir. agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

HARRISON, CAPT. HOBACE DURNFORD.—B. 1884; war serv., with D.L.I., 1914-19; France, 1915-18; twice ment. in despatches; headm. ast., govt. primary schls., Gold Coast, June, 1920; mast., training coll., Jan., 1921; inspr., schl., Apr., 1926; ag. ast. dir., educn. in 1932, 1933 and 1934.

HARRISON-JONES, CHARLES.—B. 1884; ed. Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1907; pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1909; ast. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1912; on military duty, Jan., 1915; dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1929; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1932; grade I, cl. I, 1933.
HARROWER, JOHN GORDON, M.B., Ch.B. (Hon.), D.Sc. (Edin.), L.M. (Glas.), F.R.C.S.E., F.R.S.E.—B. 1890; prof. of anatomy, King Edward VII Med. Coll., Singapore, Mar., 1922.

HART, BRIG.-GEN. HERRBERT, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1915), Croix-de-Guerre - French (1919), V.D. (1925).—Served in S. African War, 1902; lieut., N. Z. volunteers, 1907; capt. in territorial force, 1911; major, 1912; major in N.Z. exped force (main body), 1914; lieut.-col., 1915; col., 1916; temp. brig.-gen., 1917; in commd. territorial brig. 1920; R. of O., 1925; ret. list, 1930; admstr. Western Samoa, 1931; was A.D.C. to gov-gen., N. Z., 1920-24; five times men, in deeps.

HART, H. W.—B. 1897; on milly. service in France and Flanders, Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1919; service in corps of milly. accts., 1919-21; temp. clk., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1926.

HART, LAWRENCE HEREBARD.—B. 1891; ed. Lancing Coll., pol. probtnr., F.M.S. Nov., 1912; asst. comntr., pol., June, 1918; ditto, Selangor, Oct., 1920; ch. pol. offr., J. Bahu, Dec., 1922; 1st asst. comntr., pol., Perak, Dec., 1924; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Apr., 1928; 1st asst. comntr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1929; asst. comntr., pol., F.M.S. Rlys., June, 1930.

HART, RUPERT LANCELOT LEON, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; ed. Reading Schl. and R. Veterinary Coll., London; govt. vety. surg., N. Rhodesia, 1910-14; vety. offr., Uganda, 1914; served with E. African Forces in Great War, 1914-17 and on Western Front, 1917-19; senr. vety. offr., Uganda, 1920; ag. D.D.V.S. for several periods; ag. D.V.S., June, 1932.

HART, WILLIAM HUME, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.), grad., L.S.T.M.—B. 1885; ed. Sedbergh, Yorks and Dub. Univ. (Trin. Coll.); med. offr., grade III, F.M.S., May, 1912; grade II, Nov., 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Dec., 1915; temp. 2nd lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1916; capt., Feb., 1917; discharged, Sept., 1919; ag. medl. supdt., cent. mental hosp., Tanjong Rambutan, June, 1920; senr. med. offr., Pahang, but continues ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, Aug., 1926; senr. med. offr., Perak, Nov., 1927; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Feb., 1936; state med. and health offr., Perak, Jan., 1932.

HARTER, JEAN DENIS EDGARD.—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll. of Mauritius; asst. mast., R. Coll., 1901; asst. mast., Victoria Schl., Seychelles, Aug., 1902; asst. mast., King's Coll., 1910; headmast., Victoria Schl., July, 1920; ag. dir., educn. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1922; inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1924; sec. to bd. of educn.; local sec., Cambridge local exam.; ag. pvt. sec., clk. to gov. and clk. to cours., from Dec., 1924.

HARTLEY, BRIAN JOSEPH, N.D.A., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1907; ed. Loughborough Grammar Schl. and Midland Agrl. Coll.; C.O. agrl. course, Oxford and Trinidad, 1927-29; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1929.
HARTNELL, G. F. W.—Chief mechanical engrnr., Uganda rlyw., June, 1918, and from Apr., June, 1922; comntr., July, 1922; ag. regir.-gen., July-Sept., 1922.

HARTWELL, CHARLES HERBERT, B.A. (Canab.)—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1926; attd., Kandy kach., Jan., 1927; attd., secretariat, Oct., 1927; office asst., N. Eliya kach., May, 1928; asst. ch. sec., Aug., 1931; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1932; sec. to min. for lab., industry and commerce, Mar., 1933; addnl. dep. contr., lab., May, 1933; attd. to treasv. and sec., select. comttee on salaries and cadres commsn., June, 1933.
HARVEY, Cedric, B.Sc. (Agr.), A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1902; ed. Woodford Coll., Essex, Transvaal Univ. Coll. Pretoria; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., 1926; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1927.
HARVEY, Hon. Sir John Musgrave, K. Bach. (Sask.)—B. 1865; judge of the sup. ct., New S. Wales, 1913.

HARVEY, Hon. Herbert, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. and Toront. Univ.; called to bar (Ont.), 1889; called to Northwest Territories, 1893; regir., land tr., Alberta, 1896; dep. atty. genl., Northwest Territories, 1900; judge, supt. ct., N.W.T., 1902; judge, sup. ct., Alberta, 1907; ch. just., Alberta, 1910; chmn., bd. of govt., Alberta univ., 1912.
HARVEY, Reginald James.—B. 1901; Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920; 4th cls., hon., mod. hist.; St. George Sci., Jerusalem, 1923-26; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

HATCHER, C. M.—B. 1873; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11th Sept., 1893; transfd. to C.O., 1st Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902; staff c. first grade, July, 1913; deptl. astt., 26th Feb., 1920.

HATHORN, Alexander Anthony Roy, E. I.—B. 1882; ed. Hilton Coll., Lancing Coll. and Cam. Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Inner Ten., 1904; admitted advoc., Natal, 1904; K.C., 1912; judge, Natal prov. divn., sup. ct., S. Africa, 1917.

HAULTAIN, Hon. Sir Frederick William Gordon, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; ed. Montra High Schl., Collegiate Inst., Peterboro, Ont.; Toronto Univ.; B.A., 1877; LL.D., 1915; called to bar, Ont., 1882; K.C. (Dom.), 1902; K.C. (Sask.), 1907; mem., northwest coun., 1887-89; M.L.A., northwest, 1888-1905; leader, exec. comttee., Northwest Territories, 1891-97; prem. atty. genl. and comntr., of educn., N.W.T., 1897; M.L.A., Sask., 1905-12; ch. just., Sask., 1912; chan. univ. of Sask., 1917.

HAVENGA, Hon. Nicolaas Christiaan—Atty-at-law; mem., prov. coun. for Faure; O.F.S. and mem., exec. comttee. for O.F.S., 1910-15; mem., Union H. of A. for Faure; sec. to min. of finance, from June, 1924; del., Ottawa Econ. Conf., 1932; del., West Economic Conf., London, 1933.
HAWS, Richard Brunel, M.B., M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1893; med. cl., Kedah, June, 1923; prof. of medicine, K.; Edward VII Med. Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927.

HAWKEN, H. E. A.—B. 1879; ed. pub. subj., Ottawa Colleg. Inst.; ent. civ. serv. of Canada as jun. clk., dept. marine and fisheries, 1901; 2nd cls. clk., 1906; 1st cls. clk., 1910; ch. clk., 1917; ast. dep. min., Apr., 1920.
HAWKER, Charles Allan Symonds—Served European War 1914-17 (wounded); M.H.R., Commonwealth of Australia, 1929; min. for markets and repatriation, Lyons' ministry, 1931; resigned, Oct., 1932.

HAWKINS, Brian Charles Keith.—B. 1890; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.), hon., mod. hist., 1923; called Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; 2nd astt. coll. clk., June, 1927; astt. supt., impts. and exports, Nov., 1927; astt. head, sanp. dept., Oct., 1930; dist. offr., South, May, 1932; 3rd astt. col. sec., May, 1935.

HAWKINS, Gerald.—B. 1891; B.A.; B.L.; Univ.; on milly. serv., 1914-20 (capt.); cadet SS. Nov., 1920; astt. to land office, Penang, Jan., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; res. Pahang, Apr., 1926; dist. offr., Mar., 1927; cls. III, May, 1928; assessor, wild life cons., Aug., 1930; astt. advr., Segamat, May, 1931; offr., cls. II, May, 1933; astt., co-op. dept., Sept., 1934.

HAY, ALISTAIR WILLIAM.—B. 1899; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1921; offr. cls. V, ag. inspr. Chinese schls., Nov., 1924; passed cadet, June, 1925; 2nd astt. dir., educn. (Chinese), Sept., 1925; offr. cls. IV, Nov., 1928; 3rd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1932; offr. cls. III, astt. prot. Chinese, S'pore, Nov., 1933.

HAZLERIGG, GREY, O.B.E. (1920).—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., LL.B. (class tripso, 1900, law tripos, 1905); dist. commr., G. Coast, Dec., 1908; registr., sup. ct., Ceylon, Oct., 1910; called to Ceylon bar, 1914; sec., Ceylon Coun. of legal educn., 1914; served in Belgium and France with Rifle Brig., 1915; served in C.O. from May, 1916; sec. to gov. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1919-21; employed in C.O. since 1924; temp. sg. prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; astt. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; sg. astt. land offr. in addn., 1921 to 1922 and from 1922 to 1923; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924; treasy. solr. in addn., 1926-30; ag. dep. regisr. in addn., Apr., 1928 to Feb., 1929; pol. mag. in addn., July to Sept., 1929; registr., sup. ct., offr. admstr. and regisr., companies, in 1929 and 1932-34; ag. land offr., and regisr., marriages, Nov., 1930 to Dec., 1931; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1932.

HEAD, ERNEST WILFRED.—B. 1876; loco. foreman Ceylon rly., Dec., 1902; dist. loco. supt., Sept., 1905; astt. loco., carriage and wagon supt., Nov., 1912; supt., do., Dec., 1915; dep. gen. man., Oct., 1924; ag. gen. man. in 1930 and 1932; gen. man., Aug., 1932.

HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925; seconded for service with Br. economic mission to Australia, 10th May, 1928 to 3rd Mar., 1929; staff offr., 18th Dec., 1934.

HEARNE, HORACE HECTOR.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; astt. dist. commr., Uganda, Jan., 1916; 1st cls. mag., Aug., 1918; dist. mag., Jan., 1921; senr. mag., Nov., 1924; ag. puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and 1928-30; ag. chan. of diocese, Nov., 1929; mem., H.M.'s ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1930; chan. of diocese, 1932; puisne judge, Tanyika Territory, 1933.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; re-sig. with his govt., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint commn. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mily. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transfld., O.S.O., Mar., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.
HEATH, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1890; astt. gov't printer, Gold Coast, 1915; ag. gov't printer, 1920, 1921, 1923 and 1932.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A," 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905, 1912, and since 1916; prem. defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May,

HEATON, GEORGE HERBERT.—B.1896; ed. privately and Oxford Univ.; lieut., 1/1st Kent Regt. and K.F.C., 1915; H.M. Borstal instr., 1924; supt., prisons, Uganda, 1927; comsnr., prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

HEDDEN, GEORGE BRENTNALL, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1886; ed. Bedford Schl.; tel. constrn. supt. engrnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, May, 1909; supt.g.tel.engnr., Jan.,1914; astt. engrnr.-in-charge, Jan., 1921; P.M.G., July, 1928; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-16; dir., posts and tels. to C.E.F., 1915; engrnr.-in-ch., 1921; P.M.G., 1928.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1879; elk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903; prin. clk., East London, 1915; survr., Durban, 1916; ch. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918; dep. comsnr., cust. and excise, 1926; comsnr. cust. and excise, 1930; del. for Union govt. at Imp. Cust. Confce., 1921; cust. adviser to delegn. to Australian govt. (flour shipments), 1921; mem., delegn. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1922; rep. of Union govt. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923; advisory mem., Imp. Confce. 1926; del. for Union govt., Imp. Confce., 1930; adviser, Union govt., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932.

HEDELEY, HAROLD THOMAS, Incorporated Acct.—B. 1887; audit dept., S.S., Dec., 1921; i/c. external audit dept., K. Lumbur, Jan., 1922; astt. audr., grade A, Jan., 1923; ag. sr. astt. audr., Nov., 1928-Jly., 1929; audr., Penang, Aug., 1930; senr. astt. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1933.

HELFS, ERNEST HERBERT.—B.1888; joined Lord Milner's staff in Transvaal, 1905, and served with Inter-Col. coun. of the Transvaal and O.F.S. until July, 1908; clk., gov.'s office, Transvaal, July 1908, and gov.-gen.'s office, May, 1910; admstr.'s office, Cape Prov., Mar., 1913; senr. clk., 1920; prin. clk., 1928; munie. sec., Dar-es-Salaam, Dec., 1930.

HELPS, GEORGE SYDNEY.—B. 1886; bldg. inspr., P.W.D., S.S. Mar., 1914; war serv., Oct.,1914-Nov.,1917; astt. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb.,1918: sup't.,way and wks. depot, K.Lumpur, Dec., 1921; dist. engrnr., grade II, F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1923; dist. engrnr., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1927.

HEMANS, CAPT. CECIL RUPERT, R.N. (Ret.).—B.1883; ed. H.M.S."Britannia"; Boxer Rebellion, 1900; med. and clasp; Gen. Serv. Med. with Persian Gulf bar; war serv., 1914-18; ment. in despr.; placed on ret. list, 1922; prte. sec. to Sir Gordon Guggisberg, 1923; harbmr., Takoradi, Sept., 1927.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDERSON, GUY WILMOT McLINTOCK, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1897; ed. at Blundells, Wangani Collegiate Schl., New Zealand and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; served in Great War with R.F.A., for 34 years; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1923; prof. clk., prosecuting staff, G.P.O., London, 1929; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, 1932; ag. atty-gen. and temp. mem., exec. coun., in 1933 and 1934.

HENDERSON, JAMES ABBEY, M.B., Ch.E. (Edin.), B.Sc. (Pub. Health), D.T.M. and H. (Edin.), Offr., Order St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1889; lieut., R.A.M.C., T.F., 1914; senr. offr., R.E.F., D.A.D.M.S., Jerusalem, 1919; ret. with rank of major, 1921; senr. med. offr., Jerusalem, 1920; do., Palestine Rlys. and Haifa, 1928; senr. senr. med. offr., Jamaica, 1930; ag. supt. med. offr. and mem., leg. coun., on two occasions; surg. gen., regnr. gen. and immigr. agt. gen., Br. Guiana, 1933; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

HENDERSON, JOHN, M.A., D.Sc. (N.Z.), B.Sc. (Eng.), A.O.S.M., F.R.S.N.Z.—B. 1899; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ.; instr., Reefton schl. of mines, 1903; mining geologist, geol. survey, 1911; dir., 1928.

HENDERSON, RICHARD McNEILL, M.I.C.E., M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1886; ed. Allen Glen's Inst. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; senr. astt. engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1912; exec. engnr., 1920; astt. D.P.W., Hong Kong, Jan., 1928: J.P., 1929; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1933; D.P.W., 1932.
HENDERSON, W. W., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. (Vict.), capt., R.A.V.C. (R. of O.).—Vety. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1913; vety. offr., Nigeria, 1924; ch. vety. offr., Nigeria, 1927.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1915; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; M.C. and Bar; seconded to Garma as prin., new vocational schl. to organise tech. educn., 1929.

HENRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist), and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educn. serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; served Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1916-19; ment. in dep., 1919; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920; M.L.C., 1926; 3rd cls., Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1928; M.B.C., 1932.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, July, 1888; astt. to conservator, Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, June, 1893; Stutterheim, Aug., 1898; astt. conserv. forests, E. Conservancy, Oct., 1905; astt. conserv., W. Conservancy, July, 1907; prin. lecturer, forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, Oct., 1906; resig. lecturership Feb., 1908; chmn., bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, May, 1907; astt. conserv. forests, Midland Conservancy, Feb., 1908; conserv. forests, ditto, 2nd grade, Apr., 1912; conserv. forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's med.; tranf'd., Rhodesian forest serv., Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., Oct. 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 29th May, 1930.

HENNIKER HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1928).—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford (hona. hist., 1910); cadet, Fiji, 1922; attached to col. sec.'s off., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regnr.-gen.'s cf. Mar.-May, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., musk. instr., and instr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and instr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; clk., native dept., and ag. astt. native comm., July, 1906; apptd. ast. native commr., Mar., 1907. ag. chief pol. mag. and offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining bd., Apr., 1910; comntr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; nom. mem. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigrate and poor law commr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comm. co., Mauritius Vol. Force, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1925; col. sec., Cyprus, Sept., 1929; ag. gov., for various periods, 1929-32; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Falkland Is. and Dependencies, Jan., 1935.

HENRY, ROBERT NOEL, M.A., higher dipl. educn., T.C.D.—B. 1898; ed. Coleraine and Trinity Coll., Dublin; on staff of St. Columba's Coll., Dublin, 1924-28; war serv., 1916-19; asst. dir., educn., Cyprus, 1930; ag. dir., educn. for various periods, 1929-34.

HERBERT, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1887; ed. Latymer, Liverpool Inst. High Schol. and Jesus Coll., Oxford, B.A.; supt., schls., S. Prova., Nigeria, 1915; inspr. and schlmast., 1919; ag. asst. dir., S. Provs., 1927 and 1928; ag. dep. dir., S. Provs., 1929; ch. inspr., S. Provs., 1929; ag. asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., 1934.

HERDMAN, HON. SIR ALEXANDER LAWRENCE, KT. BACH. (1929).—B. 1863; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd. New Zealand parl., 1902; atty.-gen., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National ministry, 1915; resig., 1918, and apptd. judge of N.Z. sup. ct.

HEROLD, THOMAS BARRY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. clk. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; asst. master, June, 1902; comntr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1906; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn.), 1907-8 and 1908-9), land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chrmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. bank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912; man. dir., land and agric. bank, Oct., 1924-30; mem., land bank bd., 1930.

HERON, GEORGE, A.M.I.N.A., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1882; temp. surv., ships, Singapore, Nov., 1915; asst. surv., Jan., 1918; ag. ar. surv., ships, May, 1921; surv., ships, Singapore, Jan., 1922 and Aug., 1924; ar. surv., ships, Dec., 1923; ag. surv.-gen., ships, S.S. Apr., 1927 and Sept., 1931; dep. surv.-gen., ships, May, 1928; surv.-gen., ships, S.S., Nov., 1932.
HERRING, HERBERT RALPH.—B. 1905; ed. Ipswich and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A. (chem.) (hons.), B.A. (forestry); asst. conservator, forests, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amadernad Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comd. Boer forces of S.W. Div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-12; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., nat. party govt., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933; do., coalition govt., Mar., 1933.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B. 1890; asst. auditor, G. Const., Feb., 1914; ag. senr. asst. auditor, Jan. to Mar., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug. to Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Col. Audit Dept., July, 1920; 2nd cls. clk., Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, Nov., 1922; audr., Trinidad, Sept., 1926; audr. N. Rhodesia, 1930.

HEWSON, ROBERT WARRENE DALE.—B. 1880; ed. Repton Schl., Edinburgh Univ. and R. Colls., Edinburgh; L.R.C.P. & S. Edinburgh, L.R.F.P. & S. Glasgow; served, S. African war, Queen's med.; served, European war, capt., R.A.M.C.; med. supt., Jamaica lunatic asylum, Nov., 1926; joint author with Capt. R. M. Stewart, "Case of Acute Febrile Poliomyelitis," B.M.J., 1919.

HEYMANS, Dr. G. M. A.—B. 1870; M.D. (Brussels) 1895; personal physician to late Pres. Kruger, 1899-1904; consul for Belgium, Transvaal, 1908-29; vice-consul for Spain, Transvaal, 1907-29; chmn., med. bd., Pretoria, 1912-29; S. African trade commr., Milan, 1929; 1st sec., S. African legn., Rome, 1929-34; chargé d'affaires, Rome, 1933-34; min. plenip., Rome, Sept., 1934.

HEYWOOD, GRAHAM SCUDAMORE PERCIVAL.—B. 1903; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; B.A. and B.Sc., 1925; meteorological research asst., war dept., Leafield, 1928; professional asst., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1932.

HEYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 1898; ed. Devonport High Schl. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; lieut., 3rd Somerset L.I., July, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; offr. cls. V, Mar., 1924; fedl. examr. in Tamil, Sept., 1924 and July 1926; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1928; asst. emigrn. commr. for Malaya; asst. dir., so-opern., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; asst. contr., lab., Penang, Jan., 1931; offr. cls. III, Mar., 1933; collr., estate duty, F.M.S., offl. assignee and regir., companies, Apr., 1934.

HIBBERT, JOHN GROFFERT, M.C.—B. 1890; ed. Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hon. schl. mod. languages (French), 1912; pvt., 28th County of London Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.A.O.C., Nov., 1915; capt., May, 1916; major, May, 1918; D.A.D.O.S. 15th (Scottish) Divn.; ment. desps.; attd. mission to Lithuania, Oct., 1919; junr. examr., clearing office (enemy debts) B. of T., Sept., 1920; senr. examr., Jan., 1921; asst. claims clk., Jan., 1922; attd., Br. govt. agt., Mixed Arbitral Tribunal, July, 1928; temp. admstve. offr., D.O., Nov., 1929; C.O., Feb., 1930; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the Cols., Oct., 1930; asst. prin., 1st Oct. 1934.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1917; 1st grade admstve. offr., Apr., 1921; seconded as dep. lab. commr., 1930-31.

HIDE, EDGAR WALTER—B. 1893; ed. Munclpl. Secndy. Schl., Eastbourne; P.O., Gr. Britain, 1910; R.E., Sp. Res., France, 1914-19; supt., grade II, P.O., Penang, Nov., 1919; do., S'pore, Nov., 1920; do., Malacca, Dec., 1924; ag. contrlr., P. & T., Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1927; do., N. Sembilan and Malacca, July, 1930; do., Perak and Dindings, Feb., 1932.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramsgate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., May, 1905; asst. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, Oct., 1925.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTABLE, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in despa.; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.

HILL, ALEXANDER GEORGE GLENDON, B.A., Agr.B., A.R.C.Sc.I.—B. 1900; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1923; botanist, Nigeria, 1926; senr. geneticist, sugarcanes research sta., Mauritius, 1929; sent to Java to study cane breeding method, 1930; ag. dir., agr., Mauritius, Oct., 1930 to Mar., 1932.

HILL, GILBERT.—B. 1889; war serv., 1914-19; offr. in ch., wireless stations, Scapa Flow, 1919-20; wireless engr. operator, Gambia, Feb., 1923; tel. asst. dir., Somaliland, Oct., 1926; ag. dir., posts and tels. in 1931, 1932 and 1933.
