JENNINGS, PERCIVAL HENRY.—B. 1863; ed. Christ's Hosp.; col. audit dept., 1926; asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1927; asst. audr., Mauritius, 1931; ag. audr., 1932.

JEPSON, FRANK PRICE.—B. 1885; M.A., Pembroke Coll., Camb.; exhib., medallist, and dip. in agrl. (hons. in entomology), Wye Agrl. Coll., Kent, 1906; trop. disease reseach furl. student in med. entomology, Camb. Univ., 1907; assisted in investig. by L.G.B. on dise. as carry. of infection, 1908 and 1909; govt. entomologist, Fiji, 1909; offl. visits to Hawaii, 1911, Saraco, 1912, Java, 1913; asst. entomologist, Ceylon, 1919; ag. entomologist in adml., July, 1925 to Mar., 1924 and Oct. to Nov., 1925; del. to Imp. Agrl. Research Confer., Lond., 1927; on sp. serv., U.S.A. (termite investig.), 1928.

JERMYN, LANCELOT AMBROSE SCUDAMORE, B.A.(Oxon.), M.R.S.T.—B. 1886; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond and Keble Coll., Oxford; headmast., Malay Coll., K. Kangar, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; do., Northam rd. Eng. Schl., Penang, Jan., 1923; do., govt. Eng. Schl., B. Pahat, Johore, Feb., 1925 and Mar., 1931; do., Anderson Schl., Ipoh, July, 1930.
JERRAM, JAMES HAVELOCK.—B. 1879; ed. Thames High Schl. and Auckland Gram. Schl., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. state fire insurance office, 1906; asct., 1906; dep. gen. man., 1908; gen. man., 1925.

JESSE, WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Marlborough Coll.; served Indian Army R. of O., 1916-19; Afghan campaign, 1919; Calcutta Light Horse, 1921-22; asst. treas., Kenya, 1924; asst. treas., Uganda, Jan., 1925; asst. currency offr., Uganda, 1927-28 and 1933.

JEWELL, MORTON, M.B.E. (1931).—R. 1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899; conf. clk., 1911; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., establishments, 1st June, 1928.

JOBLING, GREGORY LIONEL.—B. 1899; ed. Bowen Schl., Brisbane; called to bar, Jan., 1926 (Gray's Inn); served German S.W. Africa, 1914-15; R.A.F., 1918-19; law dept., B.S.A. Co., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1915; asst. regar., high ct., N. Rhodesia; asst. offi. recr. in bankcy., asst. admin. gen., asst. regar., lands and deeds, asst. regar. births, deaths and marriages, asst. regar. companies, asst. custodian enemy property, asst. contr. local clearing office, asst. regar., high ct., etc. Uganda, June, 1927; crown coun., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1929; ag. solr.-gen. in 1933 and 1934.
JOHN, SAMUEL SPEDDING.—B. 1891; barrister-at-law, Four Courts, Dublin, 1914; war serv., 1914-20; admstr. serv., Nigeria, Jan., 1921; asst. judge, high ct., 1934.
JOHNS, BRIAN MAURICE, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), F.R.C.S. (Ed.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—B. 1901; med. offr., S.S., Mar., 1928; prof., clin. surgery., King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore, Aug., 1928.

JOHNS, ROBERT.—B. 1903; ed. Monmouthshire Agr. Inst., Usk, and Univ. Coll. of Wales, Aberystwyth; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; N.D.A., C.D.A., C.D.D., A.I.C.T.A.; stock inspir., Tanganyika, 1926; C.O. agril. research scholar, Trinidad, and Univ. of Reading, 1929-30; agrl. offr., Zanzibar, 1931.

JOHNSON, CHARLES WALTER TREVOR.—B. 1893; Fiji serv., 1910; col. sec's office, 1915; 1st cls. clk. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., 1920; ag. pyte. sec. to ag. gov. on three occasions; seconded for serv. in W. Pacific high comm. as dist. offr., Fanning Island, Feb.-Nov., 1922; ch. clk., secretariat, 1927; ag. prov. and dist. commr., June-Sept., 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1929; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1931; ag. 1st asst. col. sec. in 1932, 1933 and 1934.

JOHNSON, E. T.—B. 1875; ed. Sedbergh Schl. and Trinity Hall (Camb.); B.A., LL.B. (Law Tripos, 1902); res. mag., E. Africa Prot., 1915; senr. res. mag., Kenya, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, in 1924-25 and 1928-29; puisne judge, Zanzibar, 1929; ag. ch. just., 1932; judge, Nyasaland Prot., 1934.
JOHNSON, ELLEN M., M.B.E. (1930).—B. 1873; asst. clk., post office, Sept., 1914; ch. clk., Jan., 1924; ag. postmtr. in 1925 and 1930; asst. postmtr., June, 1926; ag. postmtr., Aug. to Sept., 1932.

JOHNSON, FREDERICK ENGLAND, I.S.O. (1932), A.F.I.A. (Australia).—B. 1878; served in S. African war, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); inspir. of native labourers, British residency, New Hebrides, 1908; in charge of acts. of British residency, 1909; ag. comdt. of British div. of native constab., 1908, 1910, 1912, and from 1914, in addition to other duties; is also a dep. commr. of the high commr.'s ct. for the W. Pacific, and ag. regar. of high commr.'s ct., in the New Hebrides; treas. and collr. of cust., Br. Solomon Is., Nov., 1919; offi. mem., advisory coun., 1927.

JOHNSON, CAPT. GEORGE BENJAMIN, M.R.S.T.—B. 1884; ed. Ryder's Schl., Devonport and Cheltenham Training Coll. (dipl. educn.); Leicestershire Regt. and gen. staff, 1915-19; res. of offrs., T.A.; ag. vice-couns., Le Havre, 1920; headmtr., Zanzibar educn. dept., 1920; ditto and inspir., schl., 1925; ch. inspir., schl., 1928; ag. dir. educn. for various periods, 1920-33; temp. mem., L.C., 1928, 1930, 1932-33; temp. mem., E.C., 1932-33.

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNYNGHAM.—B. 1875; 2nd clk., treasy., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; 2nd clk. admstr.'s office and clk. of legis. coun., Mar., 1896; clk. to admstr. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1901; treas. and comptlr., cust., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; ag. admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; offi. mem., exec. and legis. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis and offi. mem., Fed. exec. coun.; censor, St. Kitts during the war; is a J.P. for St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and admstr. gov't., Antigua, June, 1925; ag. commr., Montserrat, Feb., 1927.
JOHNSON, JOHN, M.I.M. AND C.E.—Asst. engnr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, 1913; attd., R.W.A.F.F., Cameroons Expedn., 1914-16; senr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927.

JOHNSON, JOHN COLEMAN DE GRAFT, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1884; ed. Wesleyan Coll. Schl., Cape Coast; 5th grade clk., Ashanti, 1907; 4th grade clk., police dept., 1912; asst. inspir. W. and M., 1914; asst. sec. for native affrs. Mar., 1920; mem., Accra town coun., Jan., 1928.

JOHNSON, WALTER BURFORD, L.R.C.P., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1885; med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1912; seconded for sp. investig. work under yellow fever comm., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1913 to May, 1914; temp. capt., gen. list, 1914-15; tssetse investigator, 1921; specialist, 1921; seconded for serv. on Tssetse fly comm.; dir., med. and sani. services, Nigeria, Mar., 1929.
JOHNSON, HON. SIR WILLIAM ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1862; speaker of the H. of R., C. of A., 1913-14 and 1917-23; ret. from politics.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM JOSEPH, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1892; serv. in I.E.F., E. Africa, 1914-17; ment. in despa., 1917; transf'd. to depot, 111th Mahars, India, 1917; offr. in charge, clearing house, office of field contr. of mil'y. accts., E.E.F., Cairo, 1918; major, ch. accts. offr., O.E.T. admstr., Palestine, 1919; treas., Palestine civ. admstr., July, 1920; dep. treas. on abolition of post of finan. sec., May, 1922; ag. treas. and mem., exec. and advisory couns. for various periods, 1923-28; mem., comttee. on pub. security forces, 1923; mem., comttee. on establishment of permanent and pensionable cadre for gen. clerical serv., 1925; mem., road bd., 1926; mem., rly. bd., 1928; currency offr., Jan.-June, 1928; treas., 1932.
JOHNSTON, H. LINDSAY.—Govt. statist, S. Australia, 1916.

JOHNSTON, Hon. Harold Featherston, K.C. (1930), B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1875; ed. Wanganui Coll., N.Z. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn (1897) and N.Z.(1898); many years legal practice; pres., Wellington dist. law soc., 1928; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1934; served in France with London Scottish (1916-18).

JOHNSTON, JOHN ROOK.—B. 1894; ed. St. Ninians, Moffat and St. Bees Schls. and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, 23rd Dec., 1917; seconded to 2/1st K.A.R., 23rd Dec., 1920; adminis. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Sept., 1923.

JOHNSTON, SIR REGINALD FLEMING, K.C.M.G. (1930), C.M.G. (1928), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; M.A. Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901), Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1894; prox. acc. Lord Rector's essay, Edin.; hons. in English lit., mod. hist. and constitutional law, Edin.; exhibr., Magd. Coll., Oxford, 1898; ment., hon. causa Stanhope Essay, 1898; B.A., 1898; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of couns., 1899; pvt. sec. to gov., 1900-01; ag. asst. col. sec., 1900-04; J.P., June, 1902; sec. to gov't., Weihiwei, 1904; dist. offr. and mag., ditto, 1906; seconded for serv. at tutor to Manchu Emperor (ex-Emperor of China), 1919-26; reed. from Emperor button of highest offl. grade and sable robe, 1922; warden, AA Summer Palace, Peking, 1924; brought Emperor into safety in legation quarter, Peking, Nov., 1924; sec., Br. China indemnity delegation, 1926; comsnr., Weihsiai, 1927-30.

JOHNSTON, WILLIAM.—B. 1890; asst. clk., G.P.O., May, 1910; offr., cust. and excise, Aug., 1911; dep. collr., cust., Mauritius, Dec., 1920; sea., harbr. bd., 1922-25; chmn., ditto, 1923-24; sec., tariff advisory bd., 1927-31; chmn., ditto, on various occasions, 1927-30; comsnr. of excise for tobacco in addn., 1927-31 and ag. supt., inland rev., May to July, 1929; ag. collr., cust. and harbrmr., M.L.C., on various occasions, 1923-30; collr.-gen., Jamaica, Jan., 1932; col. sec., treas. and collr., cust., Br. Honduras, 1935.

JOHNSTONE, ANGUS COLIN DUNCAN.—B. 1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and in Germany; passed Army entrance exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1906; higher grade staff, G. Cent. Kly., 1909-12; served, Balkan War, with Br. Red Cross Unit attd. Turkish Forces, 1912-13; lieut., 5th (Res.) Batt. West Yorks Regt. (T.), Nov., 1914 to Apr., 1915; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 10th Sept., 1913; lieut., W.A.F.F., and attd. Gold Coast Regt., Apr., 1915; seconded Br. Forces, Togoland, May, 1915; offr., cls. II., polit. serv., Jan., 1920; dist. comsnr., Ashanti Akim, Sept., 1922; prov. comsnr., Apr., 1928; comsnr., S. Prov., Aug., 1929; ag. ch. comsnr., N. Territories in addn. Dec., 1929 to Jan., 1930.
JOHNSTONE, JAMES MONTAGU CHOLMELEY.—Ent. prison dept. (England and Wales), Aug., 1921; dep. asst. supt., prisons, Trinidad, 1924; asst. supt., 1927.

JOHNSTONE, JOHN WILKIE.—B. 1884; solr., England, 1906; asst. polit. offr., G.E.A. (Tanganika Territory), Nov., 1918; ag. mag., Apr., 1919 to Feb., 1921; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1924; res. mag., Apr., 1929.

JOHNSTONE, SIR ROBERT STEWART, KT. BACH. (1915).—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science), 1882; LL.B., 1884; M.A., 1895; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1886; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut., 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary comsnr., 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. pleas., Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902; ch. just., Grenada, 1909; ret., 1914.

JOMARON, ADOLPHE CHARLES—B. 1893; ed. at University Coll. Schol. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (class. schol.)—B.A., 1920; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914; lieut., 1915; capt., 1917; ag. major, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920, attd. to asst. adviser's office, Muar, Johore; 2nd asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, Jan., 1922; offr., cls. V., June, 1922; asst. state audr., chmn., conservancy bd., Kgota Bharu and coroner, June, 1924; offr., cls. IV., June, 1925; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; sec., to rivers comsnrs. in addn., Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, June, 1930; 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1930; hon. A.D.C. to high comsnr., Mar., 1931; az. pub. trustee and offl. admin., F.M.S., May, 1931.

JONES, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1892; B.A., B.Sc., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. to Kegalla kach., Dec., 1914; Ratnapura kach., Apr., 1915; Kegalla kach., Jan., 1916; censor's off., Apr., 1916; on mil. duty, 1918; returned to censor's off., June, 1919; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1919; sec., retrenchment coman., Jan., 1922; addnl. asst. dir., edcn., Dec., 1923; dist. judge, N'Ellya, Apr., 1925; ast. gov't. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1925; ast. treas., Sept., 1928; ast. gov't. agt., Matale, Jan., 1929; dep. collr., cust., Sept., 1933.

JONES, GEORGE ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1889; appt., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907; ast. sec. to Oversea Prize Disposal Comtee., Mar., 1917; ag. sec. to ditto, Jan., 1914; promoted, under O.-in-C. of 1910, 2nd cls. ast. C.O., 14th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JONES, GWILYM ARTHUR.—Agrie. diploma Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. m agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1906; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua gram. schl., 1909; ast. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charge of agric. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ast. sec. to permanent exhibitn. comtee., 1909; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 1913; chemist, agric. dept., Dominica, Apr., 1915, and a director of the Victoria museum, 1916.

JONES, LLEWELYN A. F.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; junr. ast. sec., Feb., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., 1915; dist. comsnr., June, 1916; ag. senr. comsnr., Kenya, 1926 and 1928; prov. comsnr. (2nd grade), 1929.

JONES, MALCOLM LUDLOW, O.B.E. (1919).—Served at Bd. of Trade (Labour Dept.), 1877-1901; chief clk., Emigrants' Information Office, 1901; visited Canada on behalf of E.I.O., 1907; chief clk., Oversea Setttm. Office, 1919.

JONES, CAPT. NELSON, M.C. and Bar.—B. 1895; ed. Glasgow Univ.; comand., Cameron Highlands, 1915; dischyd. on account of wounds, Apr., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; ast. contr. lab., Penang, Mar., 1920; ditto, K. Lumpur, May, 1921; passed cadet, Dec., 1921; ast. dist. office, Klang to act as 1st cls. mag., 1923; 2nd mag., S'pore, July, 1924; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1926; ast. advr., Kukup, May, 1929; ag. senr. comsnr., Malaya, Madras, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III, Oct., 1931; mag., J. Bahru, Jan., 1933.

JONES, PERCY SYDNEY TWENTYFIVE.—B. 1876; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; White prize for classics; Archbishop's Greek testament prize; B.A., Cape, 1896; LL.B., Cape, 1898; called to bar, Aug., 1898; took silk, 1924; edited "Liquor Laws of Cape," and jointly with H. O. Buckle "Magistrates' Court Practice of S.A."; judge, Aug., 1926.

JONES, ROBERT NOBLE, C.B.R. (1928).—B. 1864; barrister and solr., N.Z., 1881; judg. native land ct. and dist. land regtr., 1903; cand. judge, native land ct., since 1919; was undersec., native dept., 1922-33 and native trustee, 1933.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.B.E. (1924); M.A. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B. Exam. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glasg.).—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. Schol.; med. offr., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; thank'd by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking outbreak of small pox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offr., dist. No. 2, St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY BOSCAWAY.—Ch. acct.'s offl., Beira and Mashonaland and Rhodesia Rly., Bulawayo, 1912-18; 2nd Rhodesia Regt., 1914-17; ast. native comsnr., and native comsnr., N Rhodesia, 1918-28; ast. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1928; dist. offr., Oct., 1929.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON.—B. 1888; B.A. (Manchester), 1st cls. hon. hist. (1908); cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1911; ag. ast. dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; ast. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1917.

JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANDREW, B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Mar., 1913; dist. commnr., Dec., 1913; dep. prov. commnr., Jan., 1924; prov. commnr., July, 1927; sec. native affrs., 1930; ag. col. sec., 1930, 1931 and 1932; gov't dep., Sept., 1932; ch. commnr., N. Territories, 1933.

JONES-BATEMAN, REGINALD.—B. 1894; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1919; attached to Kurunegala Kach., Dec., 1919; office asst. to supt. of census and dir. of statistics, Oct., 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Dec., 1926; asst. attm't offr., Aug., 1927.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Nottingham High Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford (class. exhib.).—B.A., Oxon., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Apr., 1916; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., offtig. as ast. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1916; ast. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1918; lieut., M.V.I., Dec., 1918; ast. dist. offr., Raub (Bentong), Feb., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ast. prot. of Chinese N. Sembilan, Apr., 1921; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1926; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Dec., 1930; mem., Br. deleg. to L. of N. Opium Confce., Bangkok, Nov., 1931; prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Nov., 1932; sec., Chinese affrs., S.S. and F.M.S.; offr., cls. IA., Oct., 1933.
JORDAN, A. H.—B. 1903; ent. C.O., 17th Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 14th Oct., 1921.

JOSEPHS, HECTOR ARCHIBALD, B.A., LL.M. (Trinity Hall, Camb.), K.C. (Jamaica, 1911, Br. Guiana).—Called to Bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1896; ag. ast. atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1907, 1908, 1909, 1910; ast. atty.-gen., 1912; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1914-24; temp. atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1921 to Apr., 1923; ag. puisne judge, Jamaica, 1924-25; referee of titles, Jamaica, 1924-25; non.-mem., leg. coun., Jamaica, 1924-25; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1925; ag. ch. just., 1932; chmn., several select and other comites, Jamaica and Br. Guiana.

KAUFMANN, HILARY PILKINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.).—B.1892; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; addt. to Jaffna kach., June, 1920; ag. office astt. to govt. agt. N. Prov., May, 1921; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1923; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1926; astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Sept., 1929; ohm., munici. conn., Kandy, Oct., 1931.

KAUFMANN, JOHN ERNEST.—Forester, Umtata, 1900; elk., conservator of forest's office, Umtata, 1901; senr. elk., ditto, 1902; astt. dist. forest offr., Umtata, 1903; transf'd. to Kokstad, 1912; dist. forest offr., Kokstad, 1912; transf'd. to Knyana, 1925; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, eastern conservancy, 1926; conserv. of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1927; ditto, Midland conservancy, 1929; ditto, Eastern conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., E. inspectorate, 1932.

KAUNTZ, W. H., B.A., M.D.; Ch.B. (Vic. Univ.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. London S.H. and T.M.; (1st place and med.), D.P.H. (Vic. Univ.)—W. African med. staff, Jan., 1914 to Jan., 1919; served with W. African Forces, Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1916; seconded for serv., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1916 to Jan., 1919; ment in desps.; pathologist and astt. bacteriologist, Kenya, Jan., 1919; dir., laboratory services, Kenya, Apr., 1919; dir., med. and san'y. services, Uganda, Nov., 1932.
KAY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1887; Hong Kong educn. dept., 1913; head, Queen's Coll., 1933; headmr., King's Coll., 1934.

KAY-MOUAT, JOHN RICHARD, M.A., Oxon and Hon. Causa Adelaide, M.Sc., M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Bristol.—B. 1881; formerly astt. demonstr., physiology, Oxford Univ.; demonstr., pathology, Bristol Univ. and pathologist to R. Infirmary, Bristol; temp. surg. lieut. to R. Naval Divn., Aug., 1914; ag. surg. lieut. comdr. for anti-malarial work, Sierra Leone, May, 1918; demob. to act temp. as prof. of pathology, Bristol Univ., April, 1919; astt. prin. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, Mar., 1921; ag. prin. in 1921, 1922 and 1926-29; prof. of physiology, Nov., 1921; lect. in pharmacology, June, 1923; del. to jubilee, Adelaide Univ., Aug., 1926.

KEAST, MAJOR SIDNEY BANKS, R.E. (R. of O.), M.C. and Bar, French Croix de Guerre.—B. 1884; ed. City of London Sch.; Sudan P.W.D., 1906-13; suptg. san'y. engr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1913; Cameroons E.F., 1915-16; B.E.F., 1916-19; senr. pub. health engr., 1924; astt. D.P.W., 1926; dep. D.P.W., 1928; D.P.W., 1932; lt. col. commdg. Gold Coast Defence Force, 1931.

KEATES, CAPT. E. H. O., M.C., F.R.G.S.—B. 1896; ed. St. Peter's Schl., York; on mil'y. serv. France and Belgium, 1915-18, Germany, 1919; adminstr. offr., N. Nigeria, 1921; astt. treas., Nigeria, 1925.

KEATINGE, WILLIAM MAYBURY, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin (Jellett prizeman); B.A., LL.D. (1900); L.L.D. (1903); solr., 1904; admstr.-gen., regis. of deeds, and sec., Waka couns., Zanzibar, Mar., 1915; joint custodian of enemy property and liquidator of enemy firms, Nov., 1915; offr. trustee, 1919; contr., local clearing office, Nov., 1920; registr.-gen., pub. trustee and om. recr., Kenya, June 1924.

KEEGAN, R. J.—B. 1901; ed. R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Indian Army; served, N.W. Frontier and Iraq; astt. to commdt., pol. (Br. divn.), New Hebrides, Sept., 1927; ag. acct., Jan. to June, 1928 and Mar. to Nov., 1930; ag. dist. agt., Efate and commdt., constab., Nov., 1929 to June, 1930.
KEEN, KENNETH.—B. 1907; ed. King's Schl., Rochester, Guy's Hosp. and King's Coll., London; cadet, Hong Kong, 1930; astt. sec., Chinese affrs., 1933.

KEET, JOHAN DIEDERIK MOHR.—Astt. dist. forest offr., working plans, eastern conservancy, Jan., 1908; astt. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1914; ag. dist. forest offr., Knyana, Nov., 1915; dist. forest offr., Knyana, Apr., 1917; conserv. of forests, Transvaal, Mar., 1923; ch. forest offr. (ch., divn. of forest management), 1932.

KEEVIL, L. G. M., H.I.R.S.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1895; ed. privately; served with R.A.S.C. (TF) and R.E. Slga. (TF) in France, German East Africa and Turkey, 1914-20; R.A.R. of O. and capt., Gold Coast Defence Force; engnr. astt., posts and tels. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; astt. engr., Gold Coast, 1929; tel. engnr., 1929; ag. astt. suptdg. tel. engr. in 1933 and 1934.

KEIR, ALEXANDER, M.A., U.D.A.—B. 1884; ed. Fordyce Acad. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A. (Aberdeen), 1905; diploma, agr., Cambridge Univ., 1913; educ. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., 1916; inspr., schls., Selangor, 1911; head, Malay training coll., Matang, 1913; inspr., schls., Perak, 1920; repd. British Malaya at Imp. conf. on educn., 1923; ag. ch. inspr., English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., in 1923, 1924, 1928 and 1931; inspr., schls., Perak, Sept., 1924; del. for British Malaya to Imp. Educn. Conf., June to July, 1927; inspr., schls., Perak, Apr., 1929.

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, C.B.E. (1933), O.B.E. (1927), Commdr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd lieut., 7th Lancs. Fusils., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., R.E.F., July, 1915; Bum-bashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan, Aug., 1916; Darfur Exped. and first inspr., Umkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. commr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; 1st cls. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory adminstr., Dec., 1919; 1st astt. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; astt. dist. commr., N. dut., 1st Oct., 1925; dep. dist. commr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, "Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," "The Pageant of Jerusalem," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922"; edr., "Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine."

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNERMAN.—B. 1889; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919; cadet, S.S., 1913; attd. res. councillor's office, Penang, Dec., 1913; attd. G.P.O., Singapore, Mar., 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernmy. offr., cl. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, Apr., 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernmy. offr., cl. IV., Nov.,
KENNAN, MYLES.—B. 1894; olk., Basutoland, 1913; war serv., 1915-18; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1926.
KENNAN, THOMAS BERETON, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Collet Court, Lond. and Worcester Cathedral Schl.; cler. ast., Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., pol., 1913; inspr., 1919; served in European War; comsnr., 1929.

KENNEDY, DONALD GILBERT.—B. 1898; ed. Waitaki Boys' High Schl. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; housemr., Dannevirke High Schl., N.Z., 1920; ast. mast., Grammar Schl., Suva, 1921; headmr., Banaban Schl., Ocean Is., 1922; headmr., Ellice Is. Schl., Vaitupu, 1923; headmr., King George V. Schl., Bairiki, Tarawa, 1931; ag. admstv. offr., Ellice Is. dist., 1932.

KENNEDY, F.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Apl., 1917; London matric., June, 1917; on mil. service from 17th Sept., 1917, to 19th March, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher gr., 1st June, 1928.

KENNEDY, HON. ROBERT, M.A., LL.M. (N.Z.)—B. 1887; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; admitted barrister and solr., 1909; examr. in law to N.Z. Univ.; pres., N.Z. law soc., 1924-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1929.

KENNEDY-SKIPTON, GEORGE STAC.—B. 1898; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st class schl., 1919; triple senr. mod., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; ast. head, sany. dept. and sec., sany. bd., 1924; ast. sec., Chinese affrs., 1926; ast. supt., impts. and expts., 1926; ast. treas., 1927; dep. estate duty comsnr., 1931; senr. ast. treas., 1933.

KENNIEF, VICTOR, B.E. (SYD.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (AUST.), M.L.M. AND CR. E.—B. 1895; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; ast. eng., P.W.D., N.S. Wales, 1916; dist. eng., P.W.D., Fiji, 1921; ag. ast. comsnr. of wks., 1927; ag. comsnr. of wks., chmn., cent. road bd., Apl. to Dec., 1927; mem., leg. coun., Fiji, May to Dec., 1927; ast. D.P.W., Cyprus, Mar., 1930; ag. D.P.W. on various occasions, 1930-33; engr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1934.

KENT, HENRY TUFFLEY MONEY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1886; ed. Elstow schl. City and Guilds Coll.; instr., tech. schl., F.M.S., Sept., 1914; ag. timber suptd., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1914; contr., timber supplies, F.M.S. rly., Jan., 1919; ditto, forest dept., S. Stllmtns, and F.M.S., Jan., 1923; sent to report on timber utilization problems in India and Burma, May, 1924; on spl. duty, Madison, U.S.A., Nov. to Dec., 1926; prin., tech. schl., F.M.S., Dec., 1930.
KENT, HON. JAS. M.—K.C., B.A.; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1904; mem. of exec. coun. and min. of just., 1907; judge, supreme ct., 1916.

KERR, ROBERT ASHFORD.—B.1870; ed. pub. sch., Swellendam; served Matabele war and rebellion and seriously wounded in latter; formerly mem., dep. ch., and ch. of com. prov. coun., Transvaal and pres. senate; mem., Union senate since 1921.

KERR-PEARSE, MAJOR B.A.T., C.M.G.(1929) (formerly Riffle Brigade).—B.1871; A.D.C. to gov.-gen. of Australia (Lord Northcote), 1905-07; priv. sec. to gov. of West Australia (Sir G. Strickland), 1909-12; priv. sec. to gov. of Tasmania (Rt. Hon. Sir W. Ellison-Macartney), 1913-15; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia (Rt. Hon. Sir R. Munro Ferguson), 1915-16; mil. sec., 1916.

KERSHAW, ARTHUR EDWIN POMEROY, M.C., A.I.M.M.—B. 1890; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1912; sec. for mily. serv., Sept., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.F.A. (S.R.), Feb., 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; inspr., mines, F.M.S., 1919; asst. warden, mines, Sept., 1921; senr. inspr., mines, B. Gajah, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Perak, Apr., 1931; warden, mines, Pahang, 1934.
KEY, WALTER JOHN SMITH, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Struct.E.—B. 1898; ed. Dundee High Schl., Dundee Tech. Coll. and St. Andrews Univ.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1923.

KIBBLE, EDMUND DAVID, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Highgate Schl. and City & Guilds (Engng.) Coll., S. Kensington; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Dec., 1907; ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; senr. ex. engrnr., 1923; attd., govt. town planning dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; state engrnr., Pahang, June, 1927; ch. hydraulic engrnr., Aug., 1930; state engrnr., Pahang, Jan., 1932.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, B.A., Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; seconded for mili. serv. 1915 to 1919; sec. to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; asst. agt., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; agt., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., census, Kedah, Oct., 1920; sec., sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. comte., Kedah, Oct., 1922; attd., rubber restn. off., K. Lumpur, Feb., 1925; chmn., assess. comte., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., May, 1927; offr., cls. II. Dec., 1929; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1929; ag. asst. advr., Muar, Sept., 1932; offr., cls. I.B., 1934.
KIDD, CAPT. W. R., M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

KIDGER, CECIL EDWARD.—B. 1875; ed. Craddock Pub. Schl.; ent. Cape civ. serv., 1894; Cape Garrison Arty.; S.A. War, 1899-1902; asst. mag., Queenstown, Middledrift, Capetown, 1908; ditto, Taung, v. Rhynadorp, Basedsorp, Pilgrims Rest, Witbank, Pietersburg, 1915-32; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1932.

KIDSON, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919), M.A., D.Sc., F.Inst.P.—B. 1882; ed. Nelson Coll. and Canterbury Coll., N.Z.; on staff, Australian C'wealth meteorological bureau, 1921-27; asst. dir., 1923-27; dir., meteorological services, N.Z., 1927.

KIDSON, NORMAN S. B.—B. 1885; served in Connaught Rangers, 1914-19; 2nd asst. mast., Boys' Gram. Schl., Fiji, Mar., 1920; clk. of peace, Rewa, Sept., 1920; ag. dist. commnr., Rewa and ag. prov. commnr., Naitasiri, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. commnr. and prov. commnr., Colo East, Apr., 1921; govt. sec., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Nov., 1922; on sp. duty as judl. commnr. for W. Pacific, Apr., 1924; has acted as offr.-in-charge at headqrs. on several occasions; ag. res. commnr. on various occasions, 1925-1932; offr. mem., advisory coun., 1927; judl. commnr. (temp. and provisional), Nov., 1928.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesan Schl., Rondebosch, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. of A., 1905; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; 2nd clk.-asst., 1917; elk.-asst. and acctnt., 1920; admitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch), sup. ct., of S. Africa, 1912; assessor apptd. by admistr., Cape exec. comtes. elections and elec. of senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-17; recd. vote of thanks of leg. assembly, S.W. Africa, for preparing standing rules and orders and assisting in inauguration of assembly, 1926; author of "The Old Cape House" and "The Romance of a Colonial Parliament."

KIMBER, GURTH.—B. 1906; ed. Perse Schl. and Cambridge; nat. sci. schl., Clare Coll., 1924-27; 1st cls. nat. sci. tripods, pt. I, 1926; 2nd cls., pt. II, 1927; B.A., 1927; apptd. after compt exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 19th Oct., 1928; temp. ag. prin., 18th Mar., 1932.

KINCAID, PERCY FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. Wynberg High Schl.; Cape govt. rly. serv., 1902; transf., col. sec.'s dept. (Cape), 1909; cl. clk., dept. of interior, 1925; commr. for immigr. and Asiatic affrs., 1933.

KING, CLEMENT HAMPDEN.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ag. 5th cls. clk., police dept., B. Guiana, June, 1893; gradual prom. to prin. clk.; dist. inspr. of police, 1901; count. inspr., Dec., 1906; ag. adjt., July, 1907 to Mar., 1908, Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909, and from Oct., 1911 to Apr., 1912; adjt. and staff offr., loca. forces, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1919; capt., B. Guiana Militia, Oct., 1914; major, Aug., 1919; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. and supt. of Georgetown fire brigade, July to Oct., 1914; ag. deputy inspr.-gen. on various occasions; dep. inspr.-gen. pol. affr., supt. fire brigade, Aug., 1919; commr. pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1921.

KING, SIR FREDERICK TRUSS, K.T., B.A. (1925), C.M.G. (1917), M.B., B.Sc.—Founder of New Zealand Plunket Soc'y, and famous for his work in connection with infant-life protection; dir. of divn. of child-welfare, N.Z. health dept., 1921; inspr.-gen., mental hosps., 1925-27.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD, I.S.O. (1932).—Ag. clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1895; ag. cia. regar.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O. and sub-secy. of schls., St. Kitts, 1906; ag. postmtr., St. Kitts, 1914; postmtr., May, 1915.

KING, JOHN EDMUND.—B. 1900; entered C.O., Sept., 1916; served with B.N.W.R. from Oct., 1916; served with B.N.W.R. from Sept. to 19th Dec., 1918; apptd. after compt exam., ast. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; cler. offr., Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 4th July, 1921.
KING, JOHN GEORGE MAYTON.—B. 1886; ed. Oundle Schl. and S. Eastern Agrl. Coll., Wisbech, Kent; dipl. agr.; C.O. Agrl. schol., Cambridge, 1929-30; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1931.
KING, LOUIS HERBERT, M.I.E.E.—B. 1896; clk., engnr. and manr., elec. wks., Dar es-Salam, 1919; clk., elec. engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921.

KING, PERCY WILLIAM.—B. 1891; ed. Seminary, Quebec and Munget Coll., Ireland; passed final exam., Law Soc.; ag. offr. recr., pub. trustee and crown soltn., Br. Guiana, in 1920; 1st clk., 1921; offr. recr., pub. trustee and crown soltn., 1923; ag. asst. col. sec. in 1932; ag. col. sec. in 1934.

KING, SYDNEY NORL.—B. 1897; ed. Dover Coll. and Bedford Schl.; served in R.F.A., 1916-1920 (comsn., Aug., 1916); cadet, S.S.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; ast. advr., Kukub, July, 1928; ag. 1st mag., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1928; dep. pres., tow. bd., in addn., Oct., 1928; dep. pub. proc., Johor, Nov., 1929; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; 2nd ast. sec. (A) S.S.S., Aug., 1933.

KING, STUART EDGAR.—B. 1898; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S.S., July, 1921; attd. Chinese prot., S'pore, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. V., Julr., 1924; ast. prot., Chinese and ast. contr., lab., S'pore, Apr., 1926; offr., cls. IV., ast. prot., Chinese, Penk, July, 1928; ditto, Malacca, Apr., 1931; offr., cls. III., Julr., 1933; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Sept., 1933.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. at Exeter Gram. Schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1904; passed in Cantonese, 1907; attched. to Punjab pol., 1907; passed in Hindustani, 1907; ag. asst. supt. of pol., 1908; J.P., 1908; ag.-supt., impts. and expts., Dec., 1909; asst. emigr. offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; passed in Punjabi, 1910; ag. asst.-supt., pol., 1911; mem., bd. of examrs., 1912; asst. supt., pol., 1912; ag. dep. supt., pol. and fire brig., 1913; dep. supt., pol., 1921; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1921 and June-July, 1925; dir., crim. intell., 1923; ag. capt. supt., pol., Mar. to Nov., 1929; dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Sept., 1930; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., Mar.-Oct., 1932.

KING, WILLIAM CYRIL CAMPBELL.—B. 1891; ed. Cranbrook schl.; probr., Ceylon pol., 1912; asst. supt., pol., 1914; on active serv., 1917-19; supt., pol., 1921; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., 1931-32; ch. commdt., pol. and inspr., prisons, Cyprus, Aug., 1933.

KING, RT. HON. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE, P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Univ., Ph.D., Harvard Univ.—B. 1874; appd. dep. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; regis. of boards of conciliation and investigation, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of labour, 1909; defeated at g.e., 1911; special investigator, Rockefeller Foundation, 1914; chosen leader of the opposition, H. of C., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Prince Co., P.E.I., 20th Oct., 1919; re-elec. for N. York, Ont., g.e., Dec., 1921; prime minister, Dominion of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921, also pres. of the coun. and sec. of state for external affairs; represented Canada at Imp. Confce., 1923; prime min., 1926-30; leader of opposition, 1930.

KINGDOM, FRANCIS WINDSOR PELHAM.—B. 1890; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate and Fitzwilliam Hall, Cambridge; col. audit dept., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, Feb., 1916; ag. sen. asst. audr. on various occasions; asst. censor, 1918-19; audr., Zanzibar Prot., Apr., 1933.

KINGDON, SIR DONALD, Kt. Bach. (1931), K.C., M.A., LL.B.—Ed. at Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., July, 1905; legal asst. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Jan., 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Mar. to July, 1912; atty.-gen., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ag. chief just., Apr., 1917, to July, 1918; atty.-gen., G. Coast, Sept., 1918; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1919; compiler of 1920 revd. edn., G. Coast laws, 1920; atty. gen., Nigeria, Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and legis. couns., compiler, revd. edn., Nigeria laws, 1923; ag. ch. sec. to govt., Oct., 1928 to Feb., 1929; ch. just., Sept., 1929.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Cheltenham Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high commr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. commr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun. June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; adminstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high commr., Cyprus, 13th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—Asst. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., ven. diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Mat. Hosp.; lieut., R.F.A.; ag. gov't. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; bacteriologist, Inst. Med. Research, F.M.S., Apr., 1927; ag. dir., ditto, Aug., 1927; dir., ditto, Oct., 1927; awarded N. Persian Forces memorial med. for research, Oct., 1927; del. L. of N. health organization laboratory confce., Copenhagen, May 1928; rep. C.O. at Internat. Cancer Confce., London, July, 1928; rep. F.M.S. at Leonard Wood memorial leprosy confce., Manila, Dec., 1930; author of many medical and scientific publications.
KINLOCH, JOHN BAXTER.—B. 1906; ed. Kirkcaldy High Schl. and Edin. and Oxford Univs.; B.Sc., forestry, Edin., 1927; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1927.

KINNEARD, GEORGE, M.D. (Univ. of Manitoba), M.C.P.S. (Sask.), Certif. L.S.T.M. and H.—B.1894; ed. Regina Collegiate Inst., 1st cls., certif., Regina Normal Schl., 1912; prin., Earl Grey and Govan. Pub. Schls. (Sask.), 1913-17; Leeward Is. med. serv., 1926; D.M.O., dist. D., Dominica; M.O., Bahamas gen. hosp., 1930; ag. M.O. and bacteriologist, Bahamas gen. hosp., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., 1932.
KINSMAN, CYRIL WILLIAMS.—B. 1887; offr. sec. to gov., Victoria and clk., execo. coun., 1934.
KIPPING, RUPERT HARRY, M.B., Ch.B. (Leeds).—B. 1904; late jun. house surg., Darlington Genl. Hosp.; locum house surg., Sheffield Roy. Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland, Feb. 1929.
KIRBY, A. C.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; 2nd grade rea., Nyasaland, Jan., 1926; 1st grade rea., Mar., 1925; ag. prov. commr., June, 1933.

KIRK, JAMES BALFOUR.—B. 1893; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edin Univ., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxford), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), F.Z.S. (Lond. and Scot.), Vans Dunlop scholarship bot. and zool. Edin. Univ., 1911, certif. (with distinc.), L.S.T.M., 1922; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1914-16; lieut., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917; capt., 1918; asst. pol. offr., Hai., 1918-19; civ. surg., Arabistan, 1919; asst. M.O., Baghdad, 1919-20; bacteriologist, Int. Pub. Health, 1920-21; demonstr., L.S.T.M., 1921; M.O.H., Mauritius, 1922; dir., med. and health dept., 1927; author of "Pub. Health Practice in the Tropics" (1931), "Hints on Equipment and Health for intending Residents in the Tropics," and several articles on med. and hygenic subjects.

KIRKHAM, G. H.—B. 1881; S. African constab., 1901; served S. African War, 1901-02 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adjt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. African Rebellion, 1914; capt., 1st S. African Infy. (Overseas Brig.), 1916-18; German W. Africa campaign, 1914-16; ment. in despr., France, 1916-17 (twice wounded); M.O., Apr., 1917; King's pol. med.; dep. commr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1918; commr., 1929.

KIRKPATRICK, THOMAS WINFRIED, M.A. (Cantab.), Dipl. Agr. (Cantab.), F.E.S.—B.1896; ed. Marlborough and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; mily. serv., 1915-19; entomologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1921; do., Kenya Colony, 1925; entomologist, Welcome Trop. Research Labs., Sudan, 1928; entomologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

KIRSOOPP, GEORGE DOUGAL.—Asst. collr. cust., E.A. Prot., Jan., 1914; collr., cust., Kenya Col., Jan., 1920; comptlr., cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; ag. treas. for various periods, 1924-31; treas. and comptlr., cust., Jan., 1932; comntr., cust., Kenya-Uganda, 1934.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONSBYER—Jun. olk., col. sec.'s off., Falkland Is., Jan., 1907.
KIRWAN, SIR JOHN WATERS, Kt. Bach. (1930).—B.1869; M.L.C., S. Prov., W. Australia since 1908; chmn. of comntr., 1923-26; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

KITCHING, A. E.—Ed. Oundle Schol. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; ast. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1919; dist. offr., June, 1926; ag. ast. sec., native affrs., Apr., 1929; ast. sec., native affrs., 1930; ag. sec., native affairs., Apr. to Dec., 1931; dep. prov. comntr., Apr., 1932; prov. comntr., 1934.

KITCHING, GEOFFREY CHARLES, O.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; ed. Oundle Schol. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; on mily. serv., 1919; ast. polit. offr., Iraq, 1920; miny. of int., Iraq, 1920-30; dep. dir., development, Palestine, 1931; govt. secy., St. Helena, 1932.

KITCHING, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1890; ed. Lancaster and Peterhouse, Camb.; survey prob., F.M.S., May, 1913; ast. supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, 1919; Perak, 1921; Selangor, 1924; supt., survey dept., F.M.S. and S.S., 1930; seconded to Trengganu as supt., surveys, 1928-31; officg. ast. survr. gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Dec., 1931 and Sept., 1932; ag. dep. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., June and Dec., 1932; supt., surveys, Johore, Mar., 1933.

KITTERMMASTER, SIR HAROLD BAXTER, K.R.E (1928), C.M.G. (1926), O.B.E. (1918).—Ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Ch., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1902-07; ast. dist. comntr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. comntr., 1915; offr. in charge, N.F.D., 1916; secy. to admntr., Somaliland, Aug., 1921; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 1926; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Br. Honduras, 1931; do., Nyassaland Prot., 1934.

KNAPMAN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—S.A. War; Transvaal civ. serv., 1900; ch. clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; office supt., secretariat, E.A.P., 1911; lieut., K.A.R., 1917-19; estab. offr., secretariat, Kenya, 1923; Queen's S.A. med., Br. War and Victory med.

KNIBBS, STANLEY GEORGE CURTHOYS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Sydney Gram. Schol., New South Wales; dipl. in all subjects, Hawkesbury Agrl. Coll., 1908; land survr., Fiji, 1911; land survr., Victoria and N.S.W., 1912; crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1913; ag. supk., pub. wks. in addn., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands during 1919; regiar. of land titles in addn., 1919; dep. comntr. for W. Pacific, 1919; dep. for natives in lands comntr., 1920 and 1921; ag. res. comntr. during 1920; comntr. of currency (temp.), 1920-24; qualified as civ. engrnr. to shires and municipalities of N.S.W., 1923; comntr. of lands and crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1924; chmn., mining bd., 1927; attended Imp. Survey Confce. and Internat. Geog. Confce., 1928.

KNIGHT, JOHN MARCUS, M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1894; ed. Grenada Grammar Schol.; 5th clk., treasry., Grenada, 1912; 2nd clk., G.P.O., 1913; served in the Great War, 1914-19; Egypt, 1916; Palestine, 1917; twice ment. in desps.; rev. and cust. offr., Grenada, 1919; 2nd clk., secretariat and clk., leg. coun., 1921; ch. clk., gov.'s o'fice, Windward Is. and elk., ex. coun., Grenada, 1925; sec. to govt. of Windward Is., 1928.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS.—Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wkn., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan., 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914.

KNIGHT-BRUCE, G. K.—B. 1891; ed. Radley Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; B.A. (2nd cls. hons., schol. of jurisprudence), 1913; ast. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ment. in desps., 1919; ag. dist. comntr., 1920; ag. res. mag., Mombassa, 1923; res. mag., Uganda, 1925; res. mag., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. judge, Zanzibar, 1926; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1927; ag. judge, Zanzibar, for various periods, 1927-34; ag. atty.-gen., 1930 and 1933.

KNOLLYS, LANCELOT ERskINE.—B. 1885; ed. Dover Coll. and S.E. Agric. Coll., Wye, Kent (sp. agric.); ast. supt., cotton cult., Uganda, 1929; ast. dist. comntr., 1914; 1st grade admin. edfr., 1925; senr. ast. sec., 1931; ast. ch. sec., Dec., 1932.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. schl. and Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; res. master, agric. schl., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; supt. of agric., Fiji, 1905; M.L.C., 1912; chmn., experiments comte., 1916; chmn., food supp., comte., 1917; ag. govt. entomologist (comte.), 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C. (temp.), June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, May, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., agr., Dec., 1924.

KNOWLES, JOHN WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1877; joined Trinidad govt. serv., 1898; warden, counties Nariva Mayo, June, 1922; warden, St. Andrew and St. David, Dec., 1925; do., St. Patrick-Victoria, May, 1933.

KOLLER, Thurstan William, A.S.A.A., C.A. (S.A.), ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; clk., O.P.S. civ. surv., July, 1900; senr. inspr., Union athl. dept., Feb., 1929; chf. inspr., rev., do., Sept., 1931; provl. audr., Cape Prov., Dec., 1932.

KOTZE, HON. SIR JOHN GILBERT, Kt. BACH. (1917), K.C., LL.B. (LOND.).—B. 1849; ed. South African Coll. and Lond. Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1874; sole judge of the high court of the Transvaal, 1877 to 1881; one of the comntr. under the convention of Pretoria to settle claims for losses and injuries suffered during the Boer War of 1880-81; chief justice of the Transvaal, 1881-1898; chmn. of bd. of examrs. in literature and science, Transval, 1890-1898; recd. the Portuguese honour of Knight Grand Cross of the Order of the Conception, 1896; atty.-gen. and member of exec. and legis. coun., S. Rhodesia, 1900; ag. admtr., S. Rhodesia, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, 1903, and judge pres., Eastern dista. et. 1904; chmn. of the coun. of Rhodes Univ. Coll.; examr. in law and jurisprudence to the Univ. of C. of G. H., 1903-1908; puisne judge, C. of G. H. Provl. Div., 1st Apr., 1913; judge, president, 14th June, 1920; judge of appeal, appellate div., June, 1922; ret., 1932.

KOTZE, PETER CANZIUS, B.A. (Cape), Dip. For. (Oxon.).—Amt. forest offr., King William's Town, 1911; ditto, Port Elizabeth, 1912; ditto, Concordia, 1914; forest offr., Storma River, 1918; ditto, Eshowe, 1920; ditto, Kokstad, 1927; ditto, Bloemfontein, 1929; conservator, forests, Midland conservancy, 1931; forestry inspr., Midland inspectorate, 1932.

KOTZE, SIR ROFT. NELSON, KT. (BACH.), B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schl. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.; asst. engr., and consulting engr., to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; govt. mining engr., Transvaal, Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn., Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., Nov., 1926.

KRAUSE, FREDERICK EDWARD TRAUGOTT.—B. 1868; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Amsterdam, London, Cambridge Univ.; B.A. (Hons.), Cape, 1888; LL.D., Amsterdam, 1893; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Cape, 1905; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa and high ct., Rhodesia; state prosecutor and ag. atty.-gen., Witwatersrand, 1896-1900; sp. commdt. and mil. govt., Witwatersrand, 1900; chmn., mining regs. comm., 1907-9; mem., Johannesburg town coun., 1905-9; M.L.A., Vrededorp, 1907-10; chmn., hosp. inquiry comm., Johannesburg; mem., prop. representation comm.; mem., Witwatersrand cent. schl. bd.; K.C. (Union of S. Africa), 1912; ag. judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., Apr., 1923; judge-pres., O.F.S. prov. divn., 1933.
KROGH, ANDRE LOUIS, M.D., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1887; govt. med. offr., Dec., 1920; dist. med. offr., Tobago, 1922.

KRUGER, JOHANNES JACOB.—B. 1888; on active serv., E. Africa and overseas, 1915-29; govt. printer, 1927; Union R. of O. with rank of major; mem., Union tender bd. and govt. rep., coun., Pretoria tech. coll.

KUIT, ALBERT.—S. Africa Repub. govt. and Z.A.S.M. rly. serv. till Br. annexation; served Jameson raid and Boer war on Boer side; finan. and estate agt., Amersfoort (T'vaal) town coun., J.P. and practically all public bodies, 1902-25; rly. commr. since 1925; pub. debt commr. since 1928; a commr. of oaths for Union.

KUSCHKE, HERMANN F. G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed., Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stofberg Gedenk Schl., 1916-24; supervising inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924; ch. inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regis., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., adminstr.'s office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several comm. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. adminstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. adminstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. adminstr. and col. sec., May to Nov., 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1915-20; adminstr., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.

LACEY, MAJOR ALFRED TRAVERS.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schl., Norwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripos; hons. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; dir., educn., Nyassaland Prot., 1930.

LAFLERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., O.B.E. (1935), L.L.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903; capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elem. schls., Malta, Aug., 1920.

LA FONTAINE, SYDNEY HUBERT, O.B.E. (1930), D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., 1910; district commnr., Kenya, Oct., 1918; 2nd grade prov. commnr., 1931; ag. ch. native commnr., 1933; prov. commnr., grade I, 1934.

LAFLACHE, Lieut.-Col. LEO RICHER, D.S.O.—B. 1888; lieut., 1914; lieut.-col., 1918; examr., bd. of commerce, 1919; mem., purchasing comm., Canada, 1920; mem., pension appl. ct., 1930; dep. min., national defence (Can.), 1932.

LAING, GORDON DACOME, B.Sc., M.B. Ch.B. (St. And.), D.P.H. (Johannesburg)—B. 1899; ed., Merchiston Schl., Pietermaritzburg, Madras Coll., St. Andrews, and St. Andrews' Univ.; senr. lect. in anatomy, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1924; asst. pathologist, S. African Inst. for Med. Research, 1927; asst. health offr. for Union of S. Africa, 1928; med. offr., health, Pretoria munic., Nov., 1929; asst. med. offr., health, Johannesburg, 1934; maj., sp. R. of O., Union Defence Force; published some papers on anatomical and anthropological subjects in the Journal of the S. African Assoc. for the Advancement of Science.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN.—B. 1890; ed., Forest Schl., Essex; asst. commissary, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; seconded as ch. clk., immigr. dept., Apr., 1913; mem., govt. standing bd. of examrs. in Hindi, etc., Nov., 1913; immigr. agt., Aug., 1920; capt. B. Guiana Arty. (Militia), Jan., 1921; senr. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1925; clk. to exec. coun. and leg. coun., July, 1928; prte. sec. to gov., Apr., 1929 to June, 1930; dist. commnr., Jan., 1932.

LAKE, FREDERICK JAMES.—B. 1894; 2nd divn. clk., G.P.O., 1912; B.E.F., France, 1915; K.A.R., German E. Africa, 1917; exec. offr., G.P.O. 1920; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika, 1921; asst. dist. offr., 1923; dist. offr., 1932.

LAKE, MORICE CHALLONER.—B. 1885; ed., Wellington Coll. and Sandhurst; commd., unattd. list (attd. 1st Battn. Somerset Light Infy.), Jan., 1904; T.A., 1905; regtl. duty, 1905-15; Aden, 1913; served, Aden Field Force, 1915-19; offr. in ch., signals, 1915-16; offr. commdg. signal schl., Lucknow, 1916-17; raised and commanded Aden (Arab) Labour Corps, 1917-18; raised and commanded 1st Yemen Infy., 1918-25; sp. duty, W.O. revising mily. rept., Aden Prot., 1926-27; reverted, mily. duty, India, 1927-28; adviser to C.O. and Air Miny. on raising levies, etc., in Aden, 1928; polit. offr., Aden, Nov., 1928; ag. 2nd asst. res. and Prot. sec., July to Nov., 1930; polit. secr., May, 1934.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1903; apptd. cler. offr., London telephone service, May, 1921; transf. to C.O., July, 1923.
LAMB, F. M.—Senior staff survr., E.A.P., May, 1907; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda Prot. Feb., 1910; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Jan., 1911; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1918.
LAMBERT, CHARLES DOUGLAS, M.I.Mech.E., M.I.Loco.E., M.I.Transport.—B. 1885; ch. mech. engrn., Kowloon Canton rly., 1910.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., cust., Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1920; R.I.C., Feb., 1920 to Feb., 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921 to 7th July, 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923; seconded as regisr. on staff of high commr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in Great Britain, 1928-32; returned to C.O., 12th Sept., 1932.

LAMBERT, EDGAR BUCHANAN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. Dulwich Coll., R. Milly Coll., Sandhurst and Crystal Palace Engng. Schl.; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt. 1909-11; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st cls. do., 1920.

LAMBERT, R. W.—Asst. auditor, E.A.P., Mar., 1912; asst. dist. comsnr., May, 1914; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. senr. nat. sec., 1926; ag. prin. asst. comsnr. for local govt., lands and stimlt., 1928.
LAMBERT, WILLIAM OSBORNE, A.M.I.N.A., M.I.Mar.E.—B. 1887; asst. govt. marine survr., Hong Kong, 1917; Hong Kong deleg. to shipping confce., Simla, 1931; govt. marine survr., 1932.

LAMBORN, WILLIAM ALFRED STEDWELL, O.B.E. (1930), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1877; ed. Oxford Preparatory Schl., King's Schl., Canterbury, and Middlesex Hosp.; asst. med. offr., St. Mary's Infirmary, London, 1899 to 1906; asst. med. offr., British Guiana, 1905; asst. med. offr., Oxford County Asylum, 1906; med. offr., Nigeria Bitumen Co., 1908; govt. entomologist, S. Nigeria, 1913; entomologist, Imp. Bureau, 1914; temp. med. offr., Tanganyika, 1917; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1919; asst. med. entomologist, F.M.S., 1920; med. entom., Nyasaland, Dec., 1921.
LAMBOURNE, NELSON THOMAS, M.A. (N.Z.).—B. 1877; ed. Mangere Schl. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; many years teaching ser., N.Z. instr., schls.; ch. inspir., primary schls., 1931; dir. educn., 1933.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat. 1880), SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903-7.

LAMONT, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1885; vice-prin., Schl. of Agr., Grootfontein, Middelburg (Cape), dep. of agr., South Africa; prin., schl. of agr., Elsenburg, Mulder's Vlei, 1920; asst. ch., divn. of agrl. educn. and extension, 1925; ch., divn. of economics and markets, 1926; mem., agrl. educn. comsnr., 1921 and fruit export enquiry comte., 1924; chmn., wine expt. comsnr., 1927; ag. under-sec. for agr., 1927; seconded for sp. duty, expt. of Union tobacco, 1928; chmn., wine comsnr., 1931; seconded for sp. duty as gen. man., co-op. maize agency, 1932; under-sec., agr., 1933; sec., dept. of commerce and industries, 1933.

LANCETOT, CHARLES, K.C.—B. 1864; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Laval Univ.; private tutor; called to the bar 1885; K.C., 1899; special law offr., Quebec, 1891; dep. atty.-gen., Quebec, 1905; mem. of comsnr. for revision of code of civil proceed., 1893-97; comsnr. for revision of statutes of Quebec, 1909-10; published "Traité théorique et pratique du droit criminel"; "Manuel du juge de paix"; "Annotated Criminal Code."

LANE, CHARLTON ADELBERT GUSTAVUS.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1911; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 1913; ag. dist. comsnr., 1918; attd., secretaries, 1918; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1919; senr. asst. sec., 1920; dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 1923; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; rea. mag., Kenya, 1929.

LANG, ERNEST PHILIP HENRY.—Ed. Monmouth; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1905; pvt. prac., Hong Kong, 1905-15; war serv., 1915-19 (capt.); asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, Sept., 1928; dep. regiar., sup. ct., Oct., 1928; ag. offl. recor. and regtr., trade marks and letters patent in addn., 1930; ag. regiar., admstr. and regiar., companies in 1931, 1932 and 1934.

LANG, HON. SIR FREDERIC WILLIAM, K.T. BACH. (1916).—B. 1852; mem. of H. of R., New Zealand, 1893-1905 and 1906-22; chmn. of comtees, Aug., 1912 to June, 1913; speaker from June, 1913 to 1922; M.L.C., 1924-31.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales; treas. in Storey and Dooley govt.; premier, N.S.W., 1925-27 and 1930-32.

LANGLEY, CARLETON GEORGE, K.C. (1933).—B. 1885; ed. City of London Schl.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1913; on mil. serv., Malta, France and Ireland, 1914-21; pvt. prac., 1921-30; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1931; comsnr., sup. ct., J.P.

LANKTREE, CHARLES JOSEPH DANE, Barrister-at-Law, Kings Inns, Dublin.—B. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-19; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1921; attd. Colombo kach., Nov., 1921; ag. office sec. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1922; asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1924; admstve. sec. to dir. of med. and sanitary serv., Oct., 1925; addnl. asst. dir., educn., Nov., 1926; admstve. sec. to dir., elec. undertakings, July, 1928; asst. stimlt. offr., Jan., 1932; ag. man., elec. undertakings, Apr., 1933.

LANDSDOWN, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., LL.B.—Cllk., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; ed. C.S.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty.-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s clk., Aliwal N., Feb., 1895; pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. clk., ch. to sol.-gen., Grahamstown, May, 1901; ag. ass't. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Oct., 1901; addnl. legal advcer., July, 1907; prof. asst., atty-gen.'s of. Capetown, Apr., 1912; law advcer., dep. of justice Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Cape Prov., Dec., 1926; senr. law adviser, 1927; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., Oct., 1931.

LARTER, LESLIE N. H., B.Sc., A.R.C.S., A.I.C.T.A.—Ed. Imp. Coll. (Lond.), scrol. and medallist, graduated (1st cls. hona.) 1929; col. agrl. schol., Fitzwilliam House, Camb. and Int. Coll. of Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1930-32; geneticist, Jamaica, Nov., 1932.

LASCELLES, SIR ALFRED GEORGE, K.T. BACH. (1913), B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxf.; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1857; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct., Papho, 1882; judge, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advcer., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. ct., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advcer., 1898; atty.-gen. of Cypr., 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LATCHFORD, Hon. Francis Robert, B.A., K.C.—B. 1856; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; eleo. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divn., ct., sup., Oct., 1923.
LATHAM, D. V., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1897; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926.

LATHAM, Rt. Hon. John Greig, P.C., C.M.G., K.C., M.A., LL.B., M.P.—B. 1877; ed. State Schls., Scotch Coll., Melbourne, and Melbourne Univ., called to bar, 1904; formerly lecturer, Melbourne Univ.; during European War was lieut.-commdr., Intelligence Branch; attended, with min. for navy, Imp. Confce., 1918; assist. sec., Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; Br. sec. to inter-allied comm., on Czechoslovak affairs at confce.; M.H.R. (C'wealth) for Kooyong (Vic.); atty.-gen., C. of A., Dec., 1925; rep. of C'wealth at Imp. Confce., 1928; leader of opposition H. of R., 1929; dep. leader, 1930; atty.-gen. and min. of external affrs. and industry in Lyons' ministry, 1932.

LAUCHLAN, Stuart, B.Sc. (Forestry).—B. 1891; ed. Royal High Schl., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; astt. conserv., forests, Nigeria, 1914; conserv., forests, 2nd grade, 1918; senr. conserv., forests, Dec., 1927; ag. dir., forests, Aug., 1933-Jan., 1934.
LAUBENCE, C. G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LAVILLE, Louis Victor Joseph.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford Schl., and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (schol.), 2nd cls. class. and hist. tripes, B.A., Camb.; cadet, S. Stltmtn., Dec., 1912; attd. to col. secy.'s office, Jan., 1913; passed cadet, Jan., 1916; supy. officer, cls. V, Jan., 1916; lent to govt. of Kelantan, June, 1917; offtg. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1918; supy. offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1919; offtg. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, July, 1920; offr. collr., income tax, Singapore, 3rd Apr., 1922; mag. and astt. regnr., sup. ct., Seremban, Dec., 1923; dep. pub. pros., Perak, July, 1925; sheriff and dep. regnr., sup. ct., Spore, Nov., 1928; 1st mag. and pres., town bd., Johore Bahru, Mar. and Oct., 1929; cls. II, Dec., 1929; offr. regnr., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1931; ag. legal advr. and judl. commnr., Kelantan, June, 1932; ag. dist. offr., P. Wellesley, Mar., 1933; offr., cls. IB, Nov., 1933.

LAW, Charles Ewan.—B. 1884; ed. High Schl., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prizeman, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1905; Burma bar, 1905; staff capt. (temp. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med. (India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. Ind. army res. of offrs.) comdg. 15th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., I.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem., legn. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili (L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston ct., on various occasions, 1925 to 1927; judge, Kingston ct., Feb., 1927; ag. puisne judge, on various occasions 1925, and 1927 to 1929; mem., standing comm. of res. mag., June, 1926; mem., comm. of enquiry into admnstr. of prisons, Sept., 1926; puisne judge, Uganda, Oct., 1930; ag. ch. just., from May, 1932 to May, 1933; chan. of diocese, Jan., 1933; mem., comm. of enquiry into admnstr. of just. in crim. matters in E. Africa, Jan., 1933; ch. just., Zanzibar, Mar., 1934; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa.

LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1888 astt. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1915; ag. inspr. of vernac. schls., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1920 and 1921; hon. sec., bd. of educn., in addn., 1920-22; inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, 1922; re-apptd. mem., bd. of exams., 1924; ag. sen. inspr., vernac. schls. for urban districts in addn., 1925; inspr., vernac. schls., 1927.

LAWLEY, Hon. Sir Arthur, G.C.S.I. (1911); G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to admininstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; dep.-admininstr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; administrator, Matabeleland, Dec., 1898; ag. administrator, Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high commnr., 1903; chmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.; gov. of Madras, 1905.
LAWLOR, John Henry, B.A.—B. 1880; astt. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Feb., 1908; astt. inspr., schls., Natal, May, 1910; inspr. of schls., Apr., 1912; ch. inspr., sohla., Aug., 1931.

LAWRANCE, Major Sir Arthur Salisbury, K.B.E. (1934), C.M.G. (1931), D.S.O.—Major, late reserve of officers; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with 2 clasps, ment. in desps.); served with 6th Batt., K.A.R., Somaliland, 1905-1911 (African General Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland 1908-1910," ment. in desps.); astt. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1911; dist. commnr., Somaliland, Sept., 1914; comdt., Somaliland Camel Corps, Oct., 1913; operns. against Mullah, 1913; ment. in desps.; A.G.S. med. with clasp; ag. consul., Harar, 1915 and 1919; operns. against Mullah, 1920; ment. in desps., D.S.O.; admstr. govt. on various occasions, 1921, 1925-32; sec. to govt., 1926; commnr. and commdr.-in-ch., June, 1932.

LAWRENCE, Major Charles Trevor, O.B.E. (1926)—B. 1879; ed. St. David's, Reigate and at Shrewsbury Schl.; N. Nigeria Regt., 1900-03; A.D.C. to Gov., S. Nigeria, 1906; junr. astt. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ag. astt. prov. commnr., Cent. Prov., 1912; pvtc. sec. to gov.-gen., 1914 and 1915; commanded Nigeria Land Cont., 1914-15; 1st astt. sec., S. Prova., 1916; attd., Nigeria Regt., 1917-19; sec., petroleum pool bd., 1918-19; ag. sec., S. Prova., 1919, 1920 and 1921; prin. astt. sec., 1921; commnr., Br. Empire Exhibn., 1923; ag. lient.-govr., S. Prova., 1927; staff grade, 1928; admstr. of col., 1930.

LAWSON, Hon. Sir Harry Sutherland Wrightman, K.C.M.G. (1933).—B. 1875; M.L.A. for Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; sat continuously in State Parlmt. from 1899; commr. of crown lands and survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; afterwards atty.-gen., solr. gen. and min. of public intrn., premier and attv.-gen. 1918; premier and min. of agr., 1920-24; now C'wealth senator for Victoria.
LAWSON, R. A.—Astt. traffic man., Uganda rly., Apr., 1919.

LEA, Leslie.—Ed. privately and Univ. Coll. Schl., Grey's Inn, 1922; clk. actg., fencing dept., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. clk. to commr. for lands, 1909; conveyancing clk., land dept., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; astt. regnr., crown lands, 1916; musketry instr., K.A.R., 1916 (reed. thanks G.O.O.L. of C.); regisr. of titles, 1919; dep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. regisr., crown lands, 1920; dep. regisr.-gen., titles and ch. controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; law offr., legal adviser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. audr., mem., bd. of comsrr., crown property, Sept., 1923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; comsrr. for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.
LEA, WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West Ham Tech. Inst.; dist. engrnr., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1914; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

LEACH, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; on mily. serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; addl. secretariat, June, 1921; office asst. to asst. gov't. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1922; asst. stltm. offr., Oct., 1923; office asst. to dir., med. and sanry. services, Apr., 1929; asst. stltm. offr., Jan., 1930; asst. gov't. agt., Badulla, Feb., 1931; do., Hambantota, Apr., 1932.
LEADBETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LEAVITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Sch., Bellaire, Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; M.Sc. For., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv. as student asst., 1903; apptd. forest asst., 1904, with successive appts. as ch. of boundaries, asst. ch. office of organization, ch., office of organization, dist. forester, and asst. forester; ch. forester, coman. of conservn. and bd. of rly. comsrrs., Canada, 1912; ch. fire inspr., bd. of rly. comsrrs., 1921; mem., soc. of American Foresters; successively, pres. and sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engrs.

LECHMERE-GUPPY, GARETH EVERARD.—B. 1874; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, May, 1895; seconded for special serv. to water authority and sewerage bd., 1904-1905; ag. inspr. of imigrts. in 1909, 1910, 1912 to 1913; statistical clk. and libr., col. secretariat, Trinidad, July, 1915; sec. income tax comtce., ests. comtce., tenders comtce., and civ. serv. comtce.; J.P. for the col. and Tobago; dep. sub-intendant crown lands, Dec., 1920; sub-intendant, crown lands, Jan., 1925.

LECHMERE-GUPPY, P. (Jnr.).—Supernumerary clk., audit off., Trinidad, 1889; 4th clk., sub-treas., San Fernando, Jan., 1892; landing waiter, customs, May, 1894; elk., col. treas., Mar., 1898; acted as warden, Tacarigua, in 1902, 1904 and 1905; sub-recvr., postmaster, collr. of cust. and harbr. mast., Tobago, Oct., 1917; collected fresh-water fishes of Trinidad for British Museum.
LECK, W. J., M.I.M.E.—B. 1889; ed. St. Bees Sch., Cumberland; colliery man., Nigeria, 1914.

LE CONTE, LOUIS.—B. 1881; mag., lesser dependencies, Mauritius, 1909; do., Mauritius and Rodrigues, Oct., 1913; ag. mast. and regisr., sup. ct., 1913-14; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law comsrr., 1914; war serv. in France and Macedonia, 1916-19; food contr., May, 1920; puisne judge, July, 1932; ag. ch. judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1933.

LEDEBOER, CLAUD MARIUS.—Served in S. African Constab., 1901-7 (S.A. war med.); Bech. Prot. police, from 1907; sub-inps. Bech. Prot. police, 1st Apr., 1914; asst. res. mag.; passed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam. and 2nd grade Sechuana exam.; 2nd lieut., gen. list, 1919; ag. res. mag., 1921; res. mag., 1921; passed Cape civ. serv. higher law exam., 1923; ag. asst. res. comsrr., 1930.

LEE, FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cl. English tripus, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripus part II, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926; temp. ag. prin., 26th Sept., 1930; seconded to Nyamaladz admstr., 2nd Apr., 1931; asst. pvt. sec. to S. of S. for the Cola, 4th Aug., 1933.

LEE, H. N., O.B.E. (Mily.), 1918.—Ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schol. and King's Coll. Londoon; articles and exams., Inst. of Chart. Acta, 1906-11; col. audit dept., 1913; asst. audr. E.A.P., Jan., 1914; E.A. Expedy. Force, 1914-19 (twice ment. in despatches); senr. asst. audr., Kenya, 1920; ag. audr., Kenya, 1923; temp. distr. Somaliland Prot., 1924-26 and in 1928; ag. audr., Kenya, 1926-27; prin. asst. audr., Jan., 1929; audr., Zanzibar Prot., Nov., 1928; audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1933.

LEE, HARRIE BERTIE, D.S.O., M.C., M.B., B.S. (hons.) Melbourne, D.T.M. and H., Eng.; certif. London S.T.M. (with distinct.).—B. 1888; ed. Sydney and Melbourne Univ.; four years serv. with A.I.F. in field as R.M.O.; field ambulance offr. and D.A.D.M.S., 1st Australian Divn., 1914-18; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; specialist, Nigeria, Aug., 1933.
LEE, JOHN HENRY BURKILL.—B. 1906; ed. Univ. Coll., Leicester; cadet, Hong Kong, or serv., Dec., 1928; asst. treas., Apr., 1931; ass. P.M.G., 1932; 1st clk. and pol. mag., Kowloon, Feb., 1934.
LEE, LIONEL JAMES, A.M.I.M.E.—Ed. R.N.E. Coll., Keyham; asst. engrnr., marine dept., Nigeria, 1913; prin. engrnr., 1927; supt. engr., June, 1933.
LEE, HON. SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), K.B. (1926).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.
LEEMBRUGGEN, EDWARD LESLIE.—B. 1896; regisr. clk., Western Pacific high comm., 1917; corres. clk., Gilbert and Ellie Is. Col., 1920; sec. to res. comsrr., 1922; sec. to gov't., 1924.

LEES, AUBREY TREVOR OSWALD.—B. 1899; ed. Repton and Royal Mily. Academy. Woolwich; ent. R. Arty., Jan., 1919; served with 2nd R.W. Fus., Ireland, June, 1920; and with Arab and Kurdish Levies, Iraq and Kurdistan, June, 1922; attd. air hd. qrs., 'Iraq (air staff intell. spec. serv., June, 1924; admintr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1925; pvt. sec. to Br. res. and ag. asst. sec., Nov., 1926; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. and ed. of gazette, Sept., 1927; temp. attd., C.O., 1928; admstr. offr., Palestine, 1929.

LEEESE, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1930).—Asst. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1906; transf. to treas. dept., 1907; acted as supervisor of customs, 1908, and as junior asst. sec. in 1911; served as finan. offr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; dep. treas., 1921; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1921 and June to Dec., 1923; treas., G. Coast, 1924; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Nov., 1925 and from Oct., 1926 to Mar., 1927; gov't. dep. on various occasions in 1926 and 1927; treas., Nigeria, Oct., 1928.

LEFEBVRE, PHILIP LLEWELLIN.—B. 1875; ed. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Brighton Coll., Sussex; clk., G.P.O., Cape Town, Feb., 1896; clk., atten.'gen's off., Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., Gic Grey, Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., East London, Sept., 1898; clk., sol.-gen's off., Grahamstown, Apr., 1899; elk., atty-gen's off. (treason branch), Cape Town, Sept., 1900; B.A., Cape Univ., 1901; clk. to atty-gen., O.R.C., June, 1901; see to law dept., Oct., 1904; ag. elk. to legis. coun. May-July, 1906; relieving mag., 1912; mag., Thaba'Nchu, May, 1917; mag., Ladybrand, Feb., 1925; mag., Grahamstown, Feb., 1929; joint editor of law-book, O.R.C., 1900-1906.

LEGGE, CHARLES ARTHUR LIONEL.—B. 1879; clerical astt., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut. B.G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk. cert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted capt., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. of pol., Grenada, from June, 1907.

LEGGE, CYRIL HENRY ETHELWOOD.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., June, 1901; clk. in surg. gens. office, cent. bd. of health, cust. and atty-gens. office; commissary, May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1914 to Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 2nd grade, Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 1st grade, Mar., 1921; passed final bar exams., 1912; called to bar, Lincolns Inn, July, 1922; passed gov't. Hindi exam.
LEICESTER, WILLIAM SAMUEL, B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B.1880; dist. surgn., Pahang, Sept., 1907; med. offr., Kuantan, Jan., 1919.

LEIGH-CLARE, HARRY JAMES LEIGH.—B. 1891; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; attd. Colombo kach., Mar., 1920; attd. office of food contrlr., Apr., 1920; ag. office astt. to govt. ast., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office astt. to govt. ast., Cent. Prov., Apr. and Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cust., Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Dec., 1926; astt. govt. ast., Mannar, Dec., 1927; landing survr., cust., Mar., 1928; addnl. astt. govt. ast., Matara, Dec., 1929; astt. govt. ast., Matara, Feb., 1930; admstve. secy., med. dept., Sept., 1932.

LEMAIRE, ERNEST JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1874; ed. Sherbrooke (Que.), Coll.; ent. privy coun. office for Canada, Jan., 1894; priv. sec. prime min., 1904-11; post office dept., 1912-23; clk. of privy coun. for Canada, Aug., 1923.

LEMBERGER, MAURICE.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LEMPRIERE, WILLIAM HENRY.—B. 1898; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; enlisted K.R.R.C., 1916; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1917; ag. capt., Schol. of Instrn., E.E.F., 1919-21; demob., 1921; col. audit dept., Oct., 1921; astt. audtr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1921; senr. astt. audtr., Kenya, 1933.

LENTON, HENRY JOHN.—Cape teleg. serv., Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, Jan., 1902; 1st cls. clk., ditto, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., ch. clk's office, Apr., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Dec., 1919; 2nd grade, ch. clk., Apr., 1921; astt. sec., Nov., 1923; ag. P.M.G., May to Oct., 1924 and from July, 1925; P.M.G. and chmn., civ. air bd., 1926-31.

LEONARD, HUGH GOODWIN RUSSELL, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1880; cadet, F.M.S., 1903; passed cadet, 1905; offr. astt. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1921; chmn., European unemployment comtee., Apr., 1922; attd., fedl. sect. for sp. duty, Sept., 1925; contr. rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; finan. commr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Aug., 1926; offr. cls. 1a, finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., June, 1927; Br. res., Pahang, Apr., 1930; M.F.C.

LE ROUX, JONATHAN JOHAN DU PREZ.—B. 1906; ed. B.H.S., Paarl, Univ. Capetown, Royal Coll., Edin. and Univ., Witwatersrand; L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Wit), 1933; med. offr., leper inst., Pretoria, Nov., 1927; med. sup't., Jan., 1933.

LESLEI, ALLAN WALLACE.—Ag. clk., Estcourt magistracy, Natal, 1889; 3rd cls. clk., Newcastle, Jan., 1890; clk. of ct. and Zulu interp. (2nd cls.), Alexandra, May, 1891; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1898; transf'd. to Newcastle, Jan., 1899; mag., Ndwandwe, July, 1900; Krantzkop, Apr., 1904; Richmond, Oct., 1906; ag. mag. for col. of Natal, Oct., 1906; astt. under-sec., Dec., 1907; ag. prin. under-sec., Apr. to Nov., 1909; ag. under-sec. for just., Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1911; chief clk., dept. of just., Apr., 1912; mag., Dundee, Nov., 1915; mag., Durban, May, 1920; judge, native high ct., Natal, Oct., 1920.
LESSELS, RAYMOND.—B. 1897; astt. engrnr., posts and tels. dept., Nigeria, Mar., 1924; divnl. engrnr., Apr., 1933; ag. engrnr.-in-ch., 1932 and 1934.

LESTER, A. K., M.B., B.S. (Bomb.), F.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.P.H., D.T.M. and H. (Edin.)—B. 1890; Indian med. serv., 1917-22; mily. serv., India and Persia, 1918-22; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1924; med. offr. in ch., soc. sociological investgn., Kahama, 1927-31; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

LETHAM, CHARLES KINGSLEY, B.Sc. (agri.)—B. 1901; ed. S. African Coll. Sch., Cape Town; B.Sc. (agri.), Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, Univ. of S. Africa, 1922; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LETHEM, GORDON JAMES, C.M.G. (1934)—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill Sch., Middlesex, and Edin. and Grenoble Unive.; M.A. and LL.B. (with distinction), Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; astt. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; res., Aug., 1924; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ag. sec., native affrs., Nov., 1927 to Sept., 1928; staff grade, Mar., 1928; sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1929; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Seychelles, 1933.
LEVINKIND, LEOPOLD.—B. 1892; ed. S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.Sc. (engnr.); corp., C.P. Riffe, S.W. Africa campaign; ent. dept., irrig., Union of S. Africa, 1917; hydrographic survr., 1934.

LEWRY, MAJOR A. W.—Ed. St. Paul's and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; M.A. (Cantab.); Middlesex Regt., European War, 1914-18; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; aide-de-camp and pte. sec., Gambia; pol. mag., Gambia, Dec., 1929; ag. legal adviser, and prov. mem., exec. and leg. couns., June-Nov., 1930; crown coun., Kenya, 1932.

LEWIN, CLAUS JOHN, M.C., B.Sc.—B. 1898; ed. Sexey's Sch., Univ. Coll., Reading and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; on war serv., 1915-17; astt. agr., Nigeria, Dec., 1922; junr. botanist, 1923; senr. botanist, 1925; ch. agriculturist, N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1930; ag. sec., agr., Dec., 1932; dir., agr., July, 1933.
