LEWIS, ALFRED DALE, B.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.I.M.E., M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1905-07; circle engrnr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America,"
LEWIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, Oct., 1922 to May, 1923; asst. govt. printer, Nov., 1923; govt. printer, Feb., 1933.

LEWIS, ERNEST HARRY, C.B.E., C.A. (S.A.).—Ed. King's Coll., Lond.; on war ser. (S.A.), 1899-1900; 1st grade clk., audit dept., Transvaal, 1902; acctnt., educ. dept., 1907; ch. clk. and acctnt., Transvaal prov. admin., 1910; ch. paymr. and acctnt., S. African pol., 1910; asst. finan. sec., defence dept., 1916; finan. under sec., 1919; finan. advr., S. African pol., 1929.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engrng.; jr. asst. engrnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rly., 1909; asst. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., Nov., 1923; ditto, Joho Bahrui, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; main- tenance and new wks. engrnr., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1932.

LEWIS, HON. SIR NEIL ELLIOTT, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1901), B.A.—B. 1858; atty.-gen. in Dobson-Hervy ministry, Tasmania, 1892-4; premier and atty.-gen. in Lewis ministry, 1900; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as min. without portfolio, Jan., 1901; again premier of Tasmania, 1909-1912; treas., and min. for mines, Apr., 1916.

LEWIS, WILLIAM EDWIN.—B.1886; ed. West Monmouthshire Grammar Schl.; traffic asst., Gold Coast rly., Aug., 1911; seconded for war serv., Cameroons, 1915; dist. traffic supt., Apr., 1921; traffic man., Feb., 1929.

LEWTHWAITE, RAYMOND, M.A., D.M., B.Ch. (Oxon), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1894; resch. student, Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; ag. pathologist I, Mar., 1928, June, 1929, and May, 1931; pathologist II, Jan., 1929; pathologist I, Dec., 1931.

LEY, MAJOR CUTHBERT HILLIAR, O.B.E. late R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; coman. in R. Engrns., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1907; army serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1899-1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C. to inspr.-gen. of fortifications and engrns., 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1903-05; topographical scen., gen. staff, 1906-07; survey of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Britain, 1912; served during war at Southampton in depts. of fortifications, miny. of munitions, and directorate of arty.; dir., Palestine survey, Dec., 1920.

LEY, HENRY COATES.—B. 1898; clk. to gov., Leeward Is., Nov. 1920; 1st clk., treas. and cust. and shipping master, Falkland Is., Apr., 1924; also postmtr., Apr., 1928; ag. treas. and collr., cust., man., savings bank, recr. of wrecks and regular., shipping, June 1928; ch. acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, Sept. 1930; ag. dep. treas., July-Oct., 1933.

LEYH, STANLEY GEORGE HOARE, Pay-Lient. R.N.R., O.B.E. (Mily.)—B. 1893; ed. East Ham Tech Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1898-12; Royal Navy, 1913-20; staff of comdr-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admty. war staff (allied naval coun.), 1918-19; staff, comdr.-in-chief, China station, 1919-20; "1914-15 Star," Brit. War Med., Victory Med.; librarian, col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Oct., 1920; office asst., col. sec.'s office, S'pore, Jan., 1922; offr.-in-ch., records revision, in addn., Feb., 1924; asst. supt., govt. monopolies dept., S'pore, Nov., 1929; ag. senr. asst., do., S.S., June, 1933.

LIESCHING, PERCIVALE, C.M.G. (1932)—B. 1896; ed. Bedford Schl., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1920; pvtc., 1st tan. H.A.C. 1914; 2nd lieut. Rifle Brig., Feb. 1915; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct., 1918; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Afrca, 1916; ment. in despatches, 1918; appd. urcr. reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 2nd July, 1920; pvtc. sec. to perm. under sec. for dominion affrs., 6th Aug., 1920; temp. ag. prin. 6th Jan., 1927; prin. 1st Apr., 1929; accom- panied U.K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1927, to signature of Pact of Paris, 1928 and to Imp. Economic Confce., Ottawa, 1932; sec. as sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in Canada, 1932 and as polit. sec. to H. Cr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, 1933; ag. high commr. to U.K., Aug.-Dec., 1933.

LIGHT, JOSEPH.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon serv., Nov., 1921; attld., Galie kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Kegalla kach., Feb., 1923; office att., asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag. Puttalam, Apr., 1925; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1926; ditto, Jaffna, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Jan., 1929; asst. attlt. offr., Jan., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1933; do., Kalutara, Apr., 1934.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk., sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar.-June, 1912; pol. commn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. sect., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1925; ag. commr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; clk., grade I, audit dept., July, 1927; ag. att., various occasions, 1929-34.
LILLEY, CYRIL CHARLES, O.B.E. (1932)—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; capt., Worcestershire Regt.; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1920.
LILLEY, RONALD, B.A.—B. 1906; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford; col. audt. dept., Apr., 1930; asst. audr., Tanganyika, Jun., 1930.

LINDON, NORMAN LINDTON—B. 1892; ed. Bristol Cathedral schl., and Christ's Hosp.; pol. probt., S.S., 1912; asst. supt. pol. Slough, Mar. 1917; dist. offr., Christmas Is., 1921; comdt., pol., depot, S'pore, 1929; dep. comdt., pol., F.M.S., May, 1929; comdt., pol., depot K. Lumpur, 1931; ch. pol. offr., Malacca, July, 1931.

LINDOW, ERIC DRAFIELD, M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., certif., L.S.T.S.M.—B. 1889; ed. Christ's Coll. Blackheath, Lond. Univ., King's Coll. and King's Coll. Hosp.; med. offr., hosp., S'pore, Apr., 1923; do., T.T.S. hosp., Dec., 1923, Oct., 1928 and Apr., 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1928 and Apr., 1929; ag. suppy. ch. med. offr., A. S'pore, June, 1931.

LINDSAY, JAMES.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; M.P., Ch.B., 1906; late res. physician, Grampian sanatorium for consumption; late clinical ass., Royal Victoria Dispensary for Consumption and diseases of the chest, Edin.; joined W.A.M., Apl., 1910.
LINDSAY, K. G.—Asst. dist. commr., Keppel June, 1921.

LINDSELL, HAROLD OFFLEY.—B. 1884; ed. at Bradfield Coll. (exhibition) and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Litt. Hum.), 1907; ass't res., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1909; dist. offr., 1911; res., 1925; res., Kano, 1929.

LINDSELL, ROGER EDWARD.—B. 1885; ed. at Uppingham schl. (scholar) and Keble Coll., Camb. (scholar); class. tripus, part I, cls. I, div. 2; called to bar, 1930; came Hong Kong, 1909; passed cadet, 1911;
LLOYD, EDGAR BRINLEY.—B., 1894; ed. Aberavon; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1919; ag. land astt., 1920; ag. regar., crown lands, documents and titles, 1921.

LLOYD, J. D.—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Tiverton, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; class. tripos, cls. II., div. I.; Blundell exhibtnr., and major scholar of Sid. Sus. Coll.; B.A., 1906; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrts., 1908 to 1909; passed cadet, 1910; ag. 1st astt. regar.-gen., 1910; J.P., 1911; astt. postmaster-gen., 1912 and 1914; 2nd astt. regar.-gen., 1913; ag. dep. regar., acctnt. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1913 and 1914; ag. sup., impts. and expts., ditto, 1916; seconded for mil. serv., 1917; comsn. in Chinese Labour Corps, 1918; offr. recvr. and regar. of trade marks and patents, 1919; custodian of enemy property, and contrl. of local clearing office, 1920; admstr. of Austrian and Bulgarian property in addn., 1920; census offr. in addn., 1921; custodian of enemy property, etc., and dep. regar., sup. ct., 1922-23; sup., impts. and expts., 1923; commended by S. of S. for repts. on opium smuggling, 1924; Hong Kong rep. to Bangkok Opium Confce., 1932.

LLOYD, T. I. K.—B. 1896; ed. Rossall Schl. and Caius Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1914); cadet, Royal Mil. Acad., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1916; served in Egypt and Palestine, ment. in desps., 1917; apptd. under re-construction scheme, astt. prin., miny. of health, July, 1920; att. to C.O. as astt. prin., Dec., 1921; ag. prin., D.O. 4th Sept., 1929; seconded for serv. assec. to Palestine comsn., 1929.

LLOYD, WILLIAM BUTLER.—B., 1880; ed. Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; M.A.; called to the bar, 1905; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mag., Rodriguez, 1916; mag. for occupied territories of German East Africa, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1919; pol. mag., S. Leone, June, 1922; puisme judge, Mar., 1924; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1924 to May, 1925, and from July, 1928; puisme judge, sup. ct., Nigeria, 1930; ag. ch. just. July-Oct., 1933.
LLOYD, WILLIAM HENRY.—B., 1882; ed. Hamyden Gunnery Schl.; astt. acctnt., open lines, Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, July, 1912; transf'd to admstr. ser., Apr., 1917; admstr. offr., cls. I., Dec., 1932.

LLOYD, HON. SIR WM. FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Owen's Coll., Manchester and Durham Coll. of Science, Newcastle-on-Tyne; bachelor of law, London Univ., 1894; D.C.L., Victoria Univ., Toronto, 1901; vice-principal, Bishop Field Coll., St. John's Newfdld., 1891; editor, "Evening Telegram" 1904; M.H.A., Trinity dist., 1904; leader of the opposition, H. of A., 1916; with Sir Edward (now Lord) Morris formed a National Govt., becoming attorney-general, 1917; ag. prime minister, 1917; prime minister, 1918.

LLOYD-BLOOD, CAPT. L.I.N., M.C., B.A.; Barrister-at-law.—B. 1896; ed. Tonbridge and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 5th R. Dublin Fusiliers, Jan., 1916; France, 1916-18; ment. in desps.; dep. regist., sup. ct., Kenya, Oct., 1920; registr., July, 1921; astt. atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Aug., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., in 1926, 1926, 1927, 1929 and 1932; solr.-gen., Palestine, 1932.
LOCK, FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. King's Schl., Peterboro and Quernmore Coll., Upper Norwood; temp. acck., Nyasaland, July, 1915; acct., July, 1918; astt. treas., Feb., 1923; senr.

LOCK, GEORGE ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Sidney-Sussex Coll., Camb., 1895 (schol.); senior optime, math., tripos, 1898; 1st cls. mech. sciences tripos, 1899; ent. C.A. as tech. ast., 1905; dep. head, wks. dept., 1913.

LOCKHART, CHARLES RAMSAY, C.B.E. (1930).—B. 1892; mil. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Gallipoli, Egypt, and with K.A.R. in E. Africa); twice ment. in desps.; astt. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; ag. senr. astt. treas., May, 1921; senr. astt. treas., Apr., 1923; dep. treas., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; treas., Apr., 1933.
LOCKHART, F. R., M.B., Ch.B. (Manch.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—B. 1897; mil. serv., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924.

LOCKHART, Sir JAMES HALDANE STEWART, K.C.M.G. (1908); C.M.G. (1888); F.R.G.S.; M.R.A.S.; LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.).—B. 1863; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; cadet Hong Kong 1878; attached to the C.O. for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; supt. opium revenue, Mar., 1883; astt. col. sec. and astt. auditor-gen., Aug., 1883; ag. regist. gen., 1884 and 1885; regist.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; mem. exec. coun., 1891; chmn., bd. of exams. in Chinese, 1891; ditto govern. body Queen’s Colli., dep. registr., sup. in prize, Nov., 1914; 1894; ag. col. sec., Apl., 1894; col. sec. and regist.-gen., Mar., 1898; sp. comsrr. to rept. on extension of bindy. of H. Kong, 1898; rep. of Gt. Br. to delimitate bindrys., for both of which recd. thanks of S. of S., 1899; rector of coll. of med. for Chinese, 1898-1902; comsrr. of Weihaiwei, Jan., 1902; assumed govt., May, 1902; ret., 1920.

LOCKHART, KENNETH SPENCER.—B. 1889; copyist, admstr.’s office, Dominica, 1908; 2nd clk., regist.r’s office, 1910; 2nd rev. offr. and harmbr., Nevis, 1913; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1917; is also sec. to quarantine bd., and clk. to cent. bd. of health; govt. trade agt. to enquire into and rep. on conditions re W.I. labourers at Santo Domingo, Nov., 1918; rev. offr., St. Kitts, May, 1920; ag. audit. clk., 1920; 2nd clk., treas. dept., July, 1921; capt., St. Kitts-nevis Defence Force; ag. 1st clk., treasy., May-Oct., 1922; audit clk., St. Kitts-nevis, June, 1922; income tax comsrr., 1924; prin., Oct., 1929.
LOCKIE, JAMES REACH.—B. 1904; ed. Campbeltown and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (forestry 1st cls. hona.); astt. conservtr., forests, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1926.

LOGAN, Sir EWEN REGINALD, Kt., Bach. (1928).—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., B.A., 1891; M.A., 1897; student, Inner Temp., Jan., 1894; called to bar, Nov., 1899; joined Middlex and N. London ses. and cent. crim. et., 1900; serv. in Boer war as 2nd lieut., 55th squrd., I.Y., 1901; Queen’s medal and four clasps; mines dept., Transvaal govt., Aug., 1901, to Apr., 1902; astt. res. mag., Transvaal, May, 1902 to May 1904; mag. E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1905; chief just., Seychelles, 1914; admstd. govt. from July, 1916 to Mar., 1917 and from May to Oct., 1918; drafted in conjunction with Alfred Gellé, Esq., barrister-at-law, code of civil procedure for Seychelles; recd. thanks of sec. of state; puisne judge, Co. Coast, June, 1920, with seniority from Mar., 1920; senr. puisne judge, Dec., 1922; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Nov., 1923 and Feb. to Mar., 1925; ch. just., Bahamas, Feb., 1925; chmn. bd. of educn., Aug., 1926; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, May, 1927; ret., June, 1931.

LOGAN, WILLIAM MARSTON, O.B.E. (1929).—B. 1889; ed. Bristol and Brasenose Coll., Oxon; (Hulmeexhibr., Bridgeman prize man, B.A.); astt. dist. comsrr., East Africa Prot., Mar., 1913; sec., cent. comttee, of supplies, 1914-15; hon. lieu. unattached list, 1915-17; ag. dist. comsrr., 1915 and Dec. to July, 1921; ag. senr. astt. sec., Sept. to Nov., 1920 and July, 1921 to June, 1924; 1st grade adjutve. offr., June, 1924; sen. astt. sec., Dec., 1924 to Mar., 1926; ag. astt. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1925 and in 1926; sec., local govt. comsrr., 1926; ag. astt. col. sec., 1977; prin. astt. comsrr. for local govt., lands and stiml., 1928; comsrr., local govt., lands, stiml. and mines, 1931.
LOLTGEN, W. G.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., 18th May, 1916 to 18th Feb., 1920; temp. clerk, O.S.O., Mar. 1920 to 27th June, 1922; aprted after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 27th June, 1922.

LONDON, GEORGE ERNEST.—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick, and Downing Coll., Camb. (schol.); lst in nat. sci. tripos, 1910; hist. triplos, 1911; F.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S. 1911; offr., cl. IV., 1914; on mily. serv., 1916-19; lieut., Glouc. Regt., offr., cl. IV., 1919; sec. to res., Palang, 1920; offr., cl. II., Nov., 1923; dep. contr., rubber expts. S.S. and F.M.S. and dep. supervisor, rubber, F.M.S., Nov., 1924; cl. II., Nov., 1928; ag. hans. comsrr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Jan., 1930; sec. to res., Perak, Sept., 1930; cl. IB., Apr., 1931; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; ag. Br. res., N. Sembanj, June, 1933; under sec. to govt., F.M.S., 1934; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1934.
LONEY, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1877; ed. Tinaru Pub. Schl.; ent. N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1895; supt., 1922; govt. printer, 1933.

LONG, CAPT. HERBERT EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Kendrick Schl., Reading; B.E.F., Egypt, Gallipoli, Salonika, Mar., 1915 to Feb., 1917; India and Aden, Mar., 1918 to April, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1921; H.B.M.’s vice-consul, Jigiga, Sept., 1927; consul., Oct., 1930.
LOOS, W.T.—B. 1885; aprtd. cl. III. Ceylon civ. serv., Mar., 1934; office astt., Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1934.
LORD, GEORGE ERIC LUDWIG, B.A.—B. 1897; ed. Felstead Schl. and Christ’s Coll., Camb.; col. audlt dept., June, 1930; astt. audr., Gold Coast, July, 1930.

LORD, Lieut.-Col. J. E. C., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D.—Ent. Tasmanian civ. serv., 1886; commanded 40th Batt. Tas. A.I.F. in European War from Feb., 1915 to Aug., 1919; commands 12th Inf. Brig., Australian Mil. Forces; A.D.C. to the gov. gen.; comsrr. of pol., Tasmania.
LOUDOUN, WILSON ANDERSON.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls, Chatham, New Brunswick; astt. audr.-gen., 1902; audr.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1907; now comptroller-general.

LOUW, ERIC H.—B. 1891; ed. Beaufort West High Schl. and Univ. of Stellenbosch, S. Africa; B.A. and LL.B. (Cape); called to bar, 1916 and practised at sup. ct., Grahamstown; el. M.P. for Beaufort West, 1924; first S. African comn. to U.S.A. and Canada, 1925; high comnrr. for U.S.A. and Canada, 1925; high comnrr. for U.S.A. and Canada, 1925; min. plenttary, Washington, Oct., 1929; doc., Rome, Nov., 1933; doc., Paris, 1934.
LOUW, JAN DANIEL, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll. and Univ., Stellenbosch; educ. serv., Cape Prov., 1912-1930; govt. translator, Pretoria, 1930.

LOUWRENS, HILLIGARD MULLER.—B. 1868; ed. Normal Coll., Cape Town, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and law classes, S.A. Coll., Cape Town; admitted as atty. and notary, sup. ct., Cape Town, 1892; pract. as atty., 1892-1903; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1906; pract. at sup. ct. bar, Cape Town, 1906-23; K.C., 1922; ag. waterct. jge., 1923; jge., sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope local divn., 1924.

LOWE, CAPT. HERBERT JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1914; capt., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1915; war serv., France, 1914-18; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1918; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923; ag. dep. ch. vety. offr., May to Dec., 1926; ag. dir., vety. serv., in 1932 and 1933; ag. dep. dir., do., in 1933.
LUCAS, HON. SIR EDWARD, KT. BACH.(1921).—M.L.C., S. Australia 1900-1918; agt. gen. for S. Australia in London, 1918-24.

LUCAS, FRANK ARCHIBALD WM.—B. 1881; ed. St. Augustine and Marest Bro. Schls., Johannesburg, S. African Coll. (now Univ., Cape Town), Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Cape); B.A. (hons.) Oxon.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa; K.C., 1924; mem., Transvaal provin. coun., 1914-17; mem., econ. and wage comsn., 1925; mem., native econ. comsn., 1930; chmn., Union wage bd., since 1926.

LUCETTE, EDWARD HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Apr., 1915; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1921; attd., Trincomalee kach., Mar., 1922; attd., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1922; pvt. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to the gov., July, 1924 to Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1926; ditto, Kandy, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1928; pol. mag., Colombo, Feb., 1929; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, July, 1931; asst. regtr., co-op. socs., Nov., 1931; asst. govt. agt., Kegalle, Oct., 1932.

LUCIE-SMITH, SIR ALFRED VAN W., KT. BACH.(1911).—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Temp., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900; ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 1908; pres., W. Indian ct. of appeal, May, 1920; ret., 1924.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1888; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1905-06; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc. and solr., S. Sttlnta., 1910-14; on mily. serv., 1914; twice ment. in desps.; ag. pres., dist. ct., Trinidad, 1920; asst. mag., Port of Spain, 1920-23; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Dec., 1925; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1926-27; ag. crown pros., Nov., 1926; puisne judge, Oct., 1927; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1927; puisne judge, Trinidad, 1929; chmn., oil and water bd., 1931; puisne judge, Kenya, 1931; mem. of appl. for Eastern Africa.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN DUDLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Berkhamstead schl.; entd. civ. serv. of Jamaica, 1909; 2nd cls. clk., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 1920; sec., Westmoreland hurricane relief comsn., 1912; sec. foot and mouth disease comsn., 1922; ag. priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Sydney Olivier) 1912; priv. sec. and A.D.C to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Leslie Probyn), 1920-23; prin. clk., col. secretariat, 1929; war serv., R.F.A. (France and Flanders), 1916-18; A.D.C. to C.R.A., 68th Div., 1918; W.O. staff, 1919; edr. of "The School Garden in Jamaica" and "Elementary Hygiene."

LUCKMAN, CYRIL HERBERT MORTON.—B. 1881; clk., cust., Johannesburg, 1903; 1st grade clk., Capetown, 1912; senr. clk., 1915; 2nd grade prin. clk., Pretoria, 1922; 1st grade ditto, 1926; accnt., Pretoria, 1930; ch. clk., 1933; ch. inspr., 1933.

LUDDINGTON, NORMAN JOHN, M. Sc., Durham Univ.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attd., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1911; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1915; Panadura, June, 1915; Negombo, Aug., 1915; Galle, Apr., 1916; Colombo, July, 1921; asst. comsnr., excise, July, 1923; asst. attltm. offr., Nov., 1926; offr., cls. I, grade II, 1933; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, July, 1933.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (CR. 1928), RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEATHLY LUGARD, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1895), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st comm., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880; medal; Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakhi; admntd. Uganda; went to Borgu in comd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apl., 1895, Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brd Charterland Co.; apptd. comsnr. and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1397; high comsnr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, Mar., 1912; gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913; assumed govt., Jan., 1914; retired, 1919; Br. mem., Permanent Mandates comsn. since 1922; author of "The Dual Mandate in Br. Tropical Africa" and other works.

LUGG, HARRY CAMP.—B.1882; ed. Greytown; clk. and Zulu interp., mag.'s office, Bulwer, Dec., 1899; Msinga, Jan., 1901; Greytown, Feb., 1902; clk., civ. comm., Zululand, Aug., 1903; native affrs., Natal, Nov., 1903; Zulu interp. and astt. regtr., native high ct., July, 1908; sworn Zulu interp., sup. ct., Aug., 1909; sen. clk. and astt. mag., Durban, Dec., 1916; mag., Ndwebwe, Dec., 1918; Ingwavuma, May, 1920; Mapumulo, Aug., 1921; 2nd grade mag., Richmond, Apr., 1923; 1st grade mag., Verulam July, 1928; senr. mag., Verulam, Aug., 1930; pres., native agt. ct., Natal and Transvaal, Oct., 1932; ch. native comsnr., Natal, May, 1933.

LUKE, SIR HARRY CHARLES, KT. Bach.(1933), C.M.G. (1926), Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1884; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mod. hist., B.A., 1906; M.A., 1910; B. Litt., 1919; priv. sec. to the gov. of Sierra Leone 1908, and A.D.C., 1909; ag. astt. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar. to July, 1909; priv. sec. to the gov. of Barbados, Feb., 1911; attached to C.O., May to July, 1911 (Coronation medal); priv. sec. to the high comsnr. of Cyprus (Sir H. Goold-Adams, G.C.M.G.), and clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1911; astt. sec. to gov., Cyprus, 1912; ag. comsnr., Paphos, 1917; comanr., Famagusta, 1918; sec., Cyprus defence comte., Aug., 1914; served in H.M.S. Doris on Syrian Coast, 1914-1915; political offr. to Admirals Lord Western-Wemyss and Christian and govt. sec., Mudros, Feb., 1915 to June, 1916; (liout.-comdr., and ag. comdr., R.N.V.R.); recd. thanks of admry.; Italian med. for mily. valour; political offr. to Admiral of the Fleet Sir J. de Robeck, Constantinople and Black Sea, Aug., 1919; Br. ch. comanr. in Georgia, Armenia and Azerbaijan, Apr. to Sept., 1920; asst. gov. of Jerusalem, Oct., 1920; mem. of comans. on the Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, and on the Jaffa disturbances, 1921, and finan. comanr. of the Patriarchate, 1921-24; col. sec., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ag. gov. on four occasions; ch. sec., Palestine, July, 1928; lieut. gov., Malta, Aug., 1930; passed in Turkish and modern Greek (hons.); author of "A Bibliography of Sierra Leone," 1910 and 1925; "Cyprus under the Turks," 1921; joint editor, "Handbook of Cyprus," and "Handbook of Palestine and Transjordan."

LUKE, S. E. V., B.A., 1927.—B. 1905; ed. St. George's, Harpenden and Oxford, Braeigirdle exhib., Wadham Coll., 1924; 2nd cls. med. hist., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. inspr., taxes (inland rev.), 1927; apptd. after further compet. exam., junr. clk., House of Commons, Feb., 1930; transf. to C.O. as asst. prin., Sept., 1930.

LUMLEY, JAMES MADDY, I.S.O. (1930).—B. 1878; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne; served R.H.A., 1895-1901; S. African war, 1899-1902; wounded; Queen's medal, four clasps; King's medal, two clasps; S. African constab., 1901-08; pub. pros., and just. of the peace, Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1904-08; asst. supt. of pol., E.A.P., 1909; served in E. Africa, 1914-18; King's pol. medal, 1919; supt., pol., Kenya, 1919; ag. asst. comanr., Kenya pol., various periods, 1920-24; asst. comanr., pol., 1925; dep. comanr., pol., 1926; ag. comanr., pol., 1927.
LUNNON, GEORGE JOSEPH.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler.offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LUPPRIAN, ERNEST VICTOR, M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Dip. L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. Bishop Currier Gram. Schl. and St. Mary's Coll., Madras; Baldwin High Schl. and Aldham Coll., Bangalore Univ., Madras; med. coll., Madras; St. Mary's hosp. med. schl.; L.S.T.M. and Hygiene; med. offr., S'pore, port health, Apr., 1923; do., Mlym. emigr. depots, India, July, 1923; med. offr., P. Wellesley, Mar., 1928 and Jan., 1931; ag. ch. med. offr., Malacca, June, 1932.

LUSCOMBE, LAURENCE GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Heles schl., Exeter; served in S. African war, 1901-02; clk., Swaziland adminstr., Sept., 1902; ast. acctnt., Dec., 1909; acctnt., Apr., 1910; finan. secy., 1918; finan. sec., Basutoland, Sept., 1927.

LYALL-GRANT, SIR ROBERT WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1934), M.A. (Aberdeen), L.L.B. (Edin.)—Vans Dunlop schol. in law, Edin. Univ.; called to Scottish bar, 1903; practised till 1909; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1909; ag. judge, high ct., in 1909, 1910, 1912 and 1913; judge of high ct., Aug., 1914, and mem., H.B.M. Ct. of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chmn., native rising comanr., 1915; chmn. of claims bd. for Nyasaland, under Br. prots. defense O. in C., 1916-19; judge advoc. to Nyasaland-Rhodesia Field Force, 1917-18; atty.-gen., Kenya, July, 1920; mem., exec. andleg. councils; puisme just., Ceylon, Feb., 1926; ch. just., Jamaica, 1932.

LYNCH, FRANCIS CHRISTOPHER CHISHOLM.—B. 1884; ed. at pub. schls., priv. tuition, McGill Univ.; assoc., Eng. Inst., Canada; assoc. mem., Town Planning Inst. of Canada; gen. engnr., 1902-05; apptd. to interior dept., 1906; ass't. supt., rly. lands br., 1911; supt., nat. resources intell. br., dept. of int. Can., Dec., 1911.
LYNDE, WALTER MIGHILLS, F.S.I. (Eng.)—B. 1883; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; quantity surv., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1910; in charge sp. wr., Kabete, 1918; contracts offr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1923; ag. D.P.W., 1926.

LYONS, Rt.-Hon. JOSEPH ALOYSIUS.—B. 1879; ed. state and convent schls.; M.H.A., Tasmania, 1909-29; treas., min. for educn. and min. for mines, 1914-16; premier, treas. and min. for rly., 1923-24; premier, treas. and min. for mines, 1924-27; premier and treas., 1927-28; el. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 1929; P.M.G. and min. for wks. and rlys., 1929; prime min. of the C'wealth, 1932.

MAARTENSZ, LEWIS MATTHEW.—B. 1876; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; addnl. crown counsel, Ceylon, Apr., 1903; crown counsel, July, 1906; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, Oct., 1910; ditto, Colombo, Apr., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Apr. to Oct., 1916, Apr.-Dec., 1920, Mar. to Nov., 1923 and from June, 1924; comanr. of assize, Apr. to June, 1925; ag. puisme just., July to Sept. and from Oct., 1925; dist. judge, Colombo, Jan., 1927; ag. puisme just., June, 1927.

MACASKIE, CAPT. CHARLES FRDERICK CRANINGHAM, barrister-at-law, F.R.G.S.—B. 1888; cadet, N. Borneo, Aug., 1910; 1st cls. mag., Mar., 1913; on mil. ser., 1916-19 (ment. in desp.); ast. govt. sec., Jan., 1920; legal advr. in addn., July, 1920; prot., lab., Mar., 1922; prot., lab. sec. for Chinese affrs. and legal advr., June, 1925; ret., July, 1929; judge, sess. ct., Aug., 1930; judge, high ct., Dec., 1930; O.A.G., Dec., 1930-Jan., 1931; res. and offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1931; seconded as govt. sec., Sarawak, Sept., 1932.

MACBRIEN, MAJ.-GEN. HOWDEN, C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1878; ed. Port Perry High Schl.; brig. gen. comdr., 12th infy. brig. during war; ch. of gen. staff, O.M.F.C.; ch. of staff, dept. of national defence, Canada; comanr., R. Canadian Mounted Pol., 1931.

MACCALL, THOMAS VERNON.—B. 1884; ed. Vicker's schl., Portsmouth, and Edin. Univ.; joined London Metro. Police, Feb., 1915; served in C.I.D., New Scotland Yard; resig. Mar., 1911; served in B. of T. labour exchanges, June, 1911 to Mar., 1914; ast. supt. of police, B. Homelands, Apr., 1914; J.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comanr., Orange Walk, July-Aug., 1914; visiting just. dist. prisons, 1914-19; dep. atty.-gen., Coronal sessions, sup. ct., Sep., 1914; ag. dist. comanr., Orange Walk, Feb.-Mar., 1915; comanr. of sup. ct., Apr., 1915; 2nd lieut., B. H. Volta., Dec., 1915, attld., Coroal; lieut., Jan., 1917; served in Cayo Exped. Force, on Guatemalan frontier, Apr. to July, 1916; capt., B.H.T.F., May, 1917; dep. atty.-gen., Coroal sessions, sup. ct., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. comanr., Coroal from Nov., 1918.

McCALLAN, E.A.—B. 1874; ed. Ontario agrl. coll.; clk., post office, Bermuda, 1903; clk., works dept., 1904; rev. offr., 1907; agric. astt., 1913; dir. of agric., Bermudas, 1920; del. to Washington 1920 and 1928; mem., immigr. lab. bd.

McCALLUM, ALEXANDER.—B. 1890; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh; G.E.A. campaign, 1916-18; trooper, Tanganyika Territory pol., 1918; astt. inspr., 1919; inspr., 1920; astt. supt., 1923; suplt., 1928; ag. dep. commdr., pol. and prisons, 1931.

McCALLUM, ANGUS, M.C.—Ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin. and Aberdeen Univ.; capt., B.E.F., France, Aug., 1915 to Aug., 1918, M.C. and bar. ment. in despa.; lieut., 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1918 to June, 1922; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1923.

McCARTER, HARRY BERTRAM ADAIR.—B. 1883; entd. civil serv., Cape Col., 1892; served S. African war in Fingoland native levies, 1901-02; law dept., Transvaal civil serv., 1902; clk., Swaziland adminstr., June, 1907; clk. and dep. astt. commnr., Apr., 1911; passed Cape Univ. civ. serv. lower law exam., 1911; astt. commnr., 1928.

McDONALD, F. H. E.—Aast. supt. pol., B. Honduras, July, 1919; ag. supt., pol., July, 1919 to July, 1920; supt., Belize fire brig., Oct., 1919; on mil. service in Egypt, Mesopotamia, and India, 1915-19 (ment. in desp.); Meritorious Ser. Medal, 1918; dist. comsrr., Br. Honduras, 1928.

MACDONALD, FARQUHAR VICTOR, A.M.Inst. C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Dingwall Acad.; water and hbr. wks., 1904; G.N.R., 1909 and 1917; Gordon Highlanders, 1914-17; asst. engr., Gold Coast rly., Jan., 1920; dist. engrnr., 1929; ch. engrnr., Nov., 1932.

MACDONALD, HON. HECTOR Y.—B. 1876; ed., common schls. of Nova Scotia, and Univ. of St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Antigonish, N.S. (B.A. 1900); studied law in the law schl. of Dalhousie Univ.; called to bar of N.S. 1903; practised in Sydney, N.S., removed to Regina, 1906; called to bar of Sask., May, 1906; law elk. in atty.-gen.'s dept.; city solr., Regina; K.C., 1913; justice, ct. of King's Bench, Sask., Mar., 1918.

MACDONALD, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1858; ed. coll. inst., Stratford, Ontario, Toronto Univ., Osgoode Hall, Toronto; called to Ontario bar, 1889; Brit. Columbia bar, 1897; K.C., 1905; benoher, Brit. Columbia Law Society, 1906-7; elec. to legis., Brit. Columbia, 1903; re-elec. 1907; leader of the opposition, 1903 to 1909; ch. just., ct. of appeal, Brit. Columbia, 1909.
MODONALD, JOHN, D.F.C., B.Sc. (hons.).—Mycologist, agrl. dept., Kenya, Sept., 1920.

MACDONALD, Hon. JOHN ALEXANDER, P.C. (Can.)—B. 1874; min. of pub. wks. and highways, P.E.I., 1923-25; dir., national serv. and hon. fuel and food control admstr. for P.E.I. during war; 1st el. to P.E.I. legis., 1908; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, 1925; min. without portfolio in Meighen cabinet, 1930.
McDONALD, J. H., M.B., Ch.B. (Aberd.)—B.1902; mil. serv., 1922-25; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926.

MACDONALD, MALCOLM, M.P. (Bassetlaw)—B. 1901; ed. Bedales Schl., Petersfield and Queen's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; partly under sec. of state for Dominion affrs., 3rd Sept., 1931; re-apptd., 9th Nov., 1931.
MACDONALD, Patrick Donald.—B. 1909; ed. Marlborough Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A. hon., 1931; cadet, Gilbert and Ellice Is., 1932; dep. comsrr. (temp. and provnl.), 1934.

MACDONALD, ROBERT ARTHUR SCRYMGOUR.—B. 1898; D.Sc., Edinburgh Univ.; M.R.C.V.S., Royal (Dick) Vety. Coll.; war. serv., Machine Gun Corps, 1917-18; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1922; vety. research offr., 1927; ag. ch. vety. offr., 1927; ag. dep. dir. animal health, 1932-33; dep. dir., animal health, July, 1933; publications, "Cattle Management, a Textbook for use in Native Schools in Africa" in collab. with Foll; "Notes on Rabies."

MACDONALD, RONALD, M.C. and 2 Bars.—B. 1889; apptd. to C. A. for Cola. on passing jr. appts. (intermediate) open civ. serv. exam., June, 1914; war service, France and Flanders, 1915-19; seconded from C. A. Office for tour of duty with audit dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1924; ast. audr., grade A, F.M.S., Aug., 1926; ag. ast. audr.-gen., F.M.S. and ast. audr. (commercial) in addn., Sept., 1926; ag. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Nov., 1930-May, 1931; ag. ast. audr.-gen., F.M.S., in addn. May, 1931-Jan., 1932.

MACDONALD, W. G. MACLEOD.—Imp. post office, 1899; transf'd. to combined post and tel. serv. of E. Africa and Uganda, 1908; dep. postmtr., 1910; ch. clk. and storekeeper, 1913; postmtr., 1913; in charge radio tele., Lake Victoria area, 1914; 1st cls. postmtr., 1918; ast. postmtr.-gen., 1922; ag. P.M.G., 1926; dep. P.M.G., 1931; dep. P.M.G., Kenya, Uganda-Tanganyika Territory post. and tel. serv., 1933.

MACDONALD, W. M., O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (England), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1870; ed. Liverpool coll., and St. Bart.'s Hosp., London; house physician, St. Bart.'s, 1896; ag. med. offr., Montserrat, 1897; ditto, Antigua, 1899; med. offr., Montserrat 1901; med. offr., burgher refugee camp, Klarkerdorp, Transvaal, 1902; med. offr., Antigua, 1904; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts, 1906; med. supt., hosp., etc., St. Johns, 1913; med. offr. of health, St. Johns, 1914; surgeon-lieut., Antigua defence force, 1914.

MACDONELL, SIR PHILIP JAMES KT. BACH. (1925), B.C.L., M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Clifton Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol.; 1st cls. hist. schls., 1894); of Gray's Inn, utter barrister; Bacon schol.; I. of C. prizeman, constitutional law; called to the bar, 26th Jan., 1900; pub. pros., Transvaal, Apr., 1906; ast. crown pros., Transvaal, Feb., 1907; legal adviser and pub. pros., N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1908; legal adviser and pub. pros., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1911; ag. judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Apr. to Oct., 1914, Apr. to July, 1916, June to Nov., 1917; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 13th Sept., 1918; chmn., Native Reserves Comm., 1924-25 (East Luangwa) and 1926-7 (Railway Dist.); ch. just., Trinidad, 1927; ch. just., Ceylon, 1930.

MACDONNELL, SIR MICHAEL FRANCIS JOSEPH, KT. Bach. (1929), M.A.—B. 1892; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb. (scholar), B.A., 1904; pres., Cambridge Union, 1904; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1908; ast. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; pol. mag. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Apr., 1913; legal adviser, Gambia, Apr., 1918; mem., exco. and leg. couns.; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Apr., 1920; mem., exco. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. just., circuit judge and judge of ct. of appeal on various occasions, 1919-23; ch. just., Palestine, 1927 and ex officio pres., full ct. of appl. of Egypt, 1930; compiler, revd. edn., Sierra Leone Laws, 1925; edr., law rept., of Sierra Leone, 1925; edr., law rept., Palestine, 1934.

McDOUGALL, ARCH.—B. 1889; ed. West of Scot. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; civ. engr., P. and W. Anderson, rly. contractors, 1906-11; ast. engr., Caledonian Rly., 1911-12; M.I.E.S., 1914; engr., way and works dept., Demerara Rly. Co., Br. Guiana, 1912-21; ch. engrnr., ways and wks., col. transport dept., 1922; gen. man., transport and harb'r. dept., 1933.
McDOUGALL, DAVID MERCER, M.A.—B. 1904; ed. United Coll., St. Andrews; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., Dec., 1928; 1st clk., magistracy, 1931; dist. offr., South, 1933-34; J.P., 1934.

McDOUGALL, JOHN HENRY GORDON.—B. 1889; ed. Marlborough Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; 3rd cls. hona., schl. of jurisprudence, 1911; B.A., 1911; ast. dist. comsrr., Uganda, Nov., 1911; ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; mag., Tanganyika Territory Aug., 1921; senr. mag., 1925; puisne judge, 1931; ag. ch. just., Mar.-Sept., 1934.

McELDERREY, SAMUEL BURNSIDE BOYD.—B. 1885; ed. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; math. schl., 1st cls. hons. maths. and experimental science, 1907; B.A., 1907; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., northern dist., New Territories, 1911; ag. dept. regis., sup. ct., 1912; J.P., 1912; ag. 1st asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. dept. regis., sup. ct., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., S. Dist., New Territories, 1913; offr. 3rd grade, 1915; temp. in C.O. 1916-1917, and 1919; released for mil. serv., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1920 to 1921; attd. to secretariat, League of Nations, opium section, 1922; represented Br. Eastern Colonies on opium question at internat. red cross conf., Bangkok, 1922; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1924; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1925; 1st pol. mag., 1925; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1926; rep. Hong Kong at C.O. Conf., May, 1927; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1929; ag. ch. sec., for various periods in 1929, 1930, 1931 and 1933; gov't. dep. on various occasions; ch. sec., Zanzibar, Dec., 1933; ag. Br. res., Feb.-Oct., 1934.

McELWAINE, PERCY ALEXANDER, B.A.(Hons.), LL.B. (1907), K.C.—B. 1884; ed., Campbell Coll., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to Irish bar (King's Inn) 1908; Alberta bar, 1913; temp. lieut., Royal Irish Rifles, B.E.F., 1916-19; stip. mag. and coroner, 2nd and 3rd dists., St. Lucia, 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Apr., 1923; senr. crown coun., Jan., 1926; ag. solr.-gen., Kenya and M.L.C., Oct., 1925 to Mar., 1926; atty.-gen., Fiji, 1927; called to Inner bar, N. Ireland, Feb., 1929; dep. pub. pros., Singapore, 1930; atty.-gen., Sept., 1933.

McEWEN, JOHN HARRY, F.S.I.—B. 1886; ed. Kimberley Pub. Schl. and Schl. of Mines, Kimberley; land survey dept., E.A.P., 1918; resigned, 1918; estates offr., Kenya and Uganda rly. and harbrs., 1926; prin. asst. to gen. man., Nigerian rlys., Feb., 1934.

MACFADDY, QUENTIN ALLISON ASHBY, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Berkhamsted schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford cls. (3rd hons. hist.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1927; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1930; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1931; J.P., 1931; ch. asst. do., Mar., 1933; dep. regis., sup. ct., May, 1933; 2nd pol. mag., 1934.

MCFALL, JOHN LYNE, Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1922).—B. 1888; educ. Coleraine Acad. Inst. and Queen's Coll., Galway; B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland, 1909; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1910; offr. cls. IV., Aug., 1920; attd. cust. dep., F.M.S., Aug., 1922; supervm. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; dep. pub. pros., Perak, Apr., 1923; prot., Chinese Negri Sembilan, Dec., 1924; asst. regis., sup. ct., Ipoh, Apr., 1925; asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; dep. pub. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Oct., 1925; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1927; asst. dist. judge, S'pore, Mar., 1928; dep. pub. pros., Penang, Sept., 1928; offr., cls. IB, dist. judge, S'pore, Dec., 1928; dist. offr., P.W., Nov., 1930; ag. judge, sup. ct., F.M.S., Jan., 1932.

MCGAVOCK, ALEXANDER DOUGLAS.—B. 1877; ed. Park Schl., Invercargill, N.Z.; N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1891; conservator, state forest serv., 1921; asst. dir., 1930; dir. and sec. of state forests, 1931.
MOGEAGH, WILLIAM RYDER, M.A.—B. 1901; ed. Winchester and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; dist. offr., Kenya, 1927; ag. asst. sec., secretariat, Apr., 1931 to June, 1932; admstve. offr., Zanzibar, 1934.

MCGILL, MAJOR HAROLD WIGMORE, M.D., M.C.V.D.—B. 1879; ed. Manitoba Univ. (M.D.); served as M.O. with 31st Batt. Infy. and 5th Canadian Field Ambulance during war; pres., Alta. Mil. Inst., 1928; first elec. to Alberta legis. assem. at g.e., 1930; resig. on apptnt. as dep. supt. gen., Indian affrs., 1932.

MCGILLIVRAY, JOHN WALKER, O.B.E. (1934), F.S.I.—B. 1884; 4th asst., trig. survey, Trinidad, 1901; 3rd asst., 1903; 2nd asst., 1904; 1st asst., 1906; qual. by exam. for diploma or sword land surv., 1905; survr., Trinidad rly. extensions, 1909; survr., roads and traces, P.W.D., 1912; topographical asst., survey dept., 1917; crown survr., 1920; survr. gen. and sub-intendant, crown lands, Oct., 1930.

MACGREGOR, ALASDAIR DUNCAN ATHOLL.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., M.A. (Edin.), class. hons.; B.A. (Oxon) class. hons.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1908; dist. commr., S. Nigeria; polit. dept., Mar., 1912; asst. sec., col. sec.'s office, S. Nigeria, Mar., 1913; statn. mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; crown coun., Mar., 1921; solr.-gen., Sept., 1923; atty.-gen., Trinidad, July, 1926; atty.-gen., Kenya, 1929; ch. just., sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1933.
MACGREGOR, CECIL JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. S.A. Coll. Schl., Capetown; mil. serv., 1916-19; dip. agr., Elsenberg Agr. Coll., Cape Prov., S.A., 1921; dist. agric. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1928.
MACGREGOR, ERNEST S.—B. 1867; pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1890; Out Island commr. (4th div.) and teacher, 1917.
MACGREGOR, MALCOLM, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1888; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1912; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1929.

MACGREGOR, CAPT. ROBERT BARK, M.B. Ch. B. (Edin.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.).—B. 1896; med. and health offr., Singapore, Jan., 1920; ag. prof., physiology, in addn., June, 1920-July, 1921; med. offr. i/c schl. and v. d. clinics, Jan., 1922; med. offr. i/o Tan Toek Seng's hosp., Apr., 1922; ch. med. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1927; ag. ch. med. offr., Penang, Jan., 1928; do., S'pore, June, 1930.

MCGUSTY, VICTOR WILLIAM TIGHE, M.B. Dublin and L.M.Rotunda Hosp., D.T.M. and H. Liverpool.—B. 1887; ed. Coleraine and Trinity Coll., Dublin; med. offr., Fiji, 1912; dist. commr., conjoint, 1914; gov't. commr. for sp. duty, 1931; sec. for Indian affrs. conjoint with the duties of insp. med. offr., 1932; ag. ch. med. offr., 1934.

MACHAN, JOHN WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Marconi Wireless Schl., Lond.; Marconi Internat. Marine Communication Co., Lond., Sept., 1913-Aug., 1914; operator, S'pore, Aug., 1915; asst. engrnr.-operrtr., S'pore, May, 1920 and Aug., 1926; supt., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1927; engrnr.-operrtr., Penang, Oct., 1929 and Mar., 1932; engrnr., wireless, P. Lebar, S'pore, July, 1932.

MCHARDY, W.—Ast. local auditor, E.A.P., 1909; ast. traffic man., Uganda rly., 1914; deputy traffic man., 1917; supt. (admstve.), Jan., 1924; London rep., Kenya and Uganda, rly. and harbrs., Oct., 1927.

MACHTIG, E. G. S., C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1889; educ. at St. Paul's and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (scholar) also at the Sorbonne, Paris; 1st cls. 2nd div., class tripes, Pt. I., 1911; B.A. (Cantab), 1911; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., O.C.O., 15th Oct., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Nov., 1917; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. ast. sec., D.O., 24th Jan., 1930.

MINTON, ANDREW ELPHINSTONE SINGLAIR, B.Sc., Ph.D. (Edin.)—B. 1903; ed. Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (1st cls. hons., botany); ast. plant pathologist to bd. of agr. for Scotland, 1925-27; Ph.D. (Edin.), 1927; geneticist to dept. of sci. and agr., Barbados, B.W.I., 1928.

MACKEY, JAMES MURDOCH, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.); D.P.H. (Lond.); certif., London S.T.M.—B. 1892; ed. Hutchison's Grammar Sch., Glasgow and Glasgow Univ.; M.O., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1920; served, R.A.M.S., 1915-19; ag. M.O.H., Nov., 1923; S.S.O., June, 1926; A.D.H.S., Gold Coast, Jan., 1930; transf. to Nigeria, Feb., 1932.
MACKEY, R., M.D., Ch.B., D.P.H. (Aberd.)—B. 1898; war serv., R.N.R., 1916-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1926; malaria research offr., 1931.

MACKEY, WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1893; offr., cust. and excise, Cyprus, 1913; ast. collr. and harbrmr., 1916; collr., cust., 1921; ag. ch. collr., cust. and excise, June to Oct., 1925; ag. comptr., cust. and excise, June to Oct., 1928 and June to Oct., 1931; ag. regtr., companies, ag. regtr., trade marks, ag. regtr., patents, in 1925, 1928 and 1931; ag. commr., Sept., to Oct., 1928; dep. comptr., cust. and excise, 1931.
MCKEAG, CAPT. V. M.—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927.
MCKEAN, J. D.—Ast. dist. commr., K.A.P., Jan., 1917; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926.

MCKECHNIE, ROBERT MCKECHNIE, M.I.Wech.—B. 1875; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Univ. Coll. (Lond.); loco. dept., L.S.W.R., 1896; engnr. inspr., C.A., 1905; ast. engnr., 1919; dep. head, engnr. inspr. dept., 1922.

MCKENZIE, A., M.B., B.S., L.M.S.A., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), Cert. Lond. S.H.T.M. (distinct. and Duncan Med.)—B. 1898; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-18; med. offr., Cook Is., 1922; do., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1925.

MCKENZIE, CAPT. ANGUS DURHAM.—B. 1888; cust. dept., Gold Coast, Dec., 1913; seconded for mil. serv., 1915; served in France, 1915-17 and with Gold Coast Regt., 1917-19; contrlr., enemy property and offr.-in-ch., treasy. and cust., Togoland, 1920; prov. collr., cust., Feb., 1924; contrlr., enemy property, Gold Coast, Apr., 1928; dep. comptr., cust., June, 1928; pres., Acora town coun., 1928-29; comptr., cust., Feb., 1933.
MCKENZIE, CHARLES JOHN, A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.)—B. 1878; ed. Otago Boys' High Schl.; ent. N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1897; ast. engnr.-in-ch., 1920; engr.-in-ch. and under-sec., 1932.
MACKENZIE, MAJOR WALTER JOHN EASTON, M.C., M.R.C.V.S., D.T.V.M.—B. 1889; ed. Royal (Dick) Vety. Coll. and Edinburgh Univ.; war serv., 1914-19; col. vet. surg., Hong Kong, 1920.

MCKERRON, PATRICK ALEXANDER BRUCE.—B. 1896; ed. Fettes Coll., Edin.; 2nd lieut. Gordon Highrs., 1914; capt., 1916; transf., I.A.R.O., 1917; capt., 20th Punjabis, 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; cadet, S.S., Feb., 1920; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; dist. offr., N. Tebal, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; Br. res., Brunei, May, 1928; attd. fed. sect., Oct., 1931; sec., retrenchment commn., F.M.S., Mar., 1932; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1932; supt., convict establt., Taiping, Oct., 1933; ag. ast. advr., Kedah, Apr., 1934.

MACKIE, GEORGE DOUGLAS, M.B.E. (1923).—Ag. 2nd clk., govt. office, St. Vincent, Dec., 1890, to Apr., 1891; 2nd clk. and cashier, treasy., Apr., 1891, to May, 1892, and from Jan., 1897, to Oct., 1898; clk., crown lands and survey office, May to Dec., 1892; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to Dec., 1896; 2nd clk. and acctnt., treasy., Grenada, Nov., 1898; ch. clk., Jan., 1903; ch. sub-collr. and revenue offr., treasy., St. Lucia, Jan., 1911; ag. treasur. on several occasions in 1911 to 1915; treas., Nov., 1915; ag. col. sec. in 1923, 1924 and 1926; admstd. govt., July to Sept., 1925.
MACKIE, JAMES RICHARD, B.Sc. (Agric.)—B. 1886; ed. Sexey's Schl., Bruton, Somerset and Univ. Coll., Reading; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1921; dep. ast. dir., agr., Nigeria, 1928; ast. dir., agr., 1929.

MCKINNEY, E. H., M.B.E. (1933).—B. 1877; ent. Bahamas civ. ser., 1898; 2nd clk., P.O., 1901; res. just., Exuma, 1904; Out Island commr. (2nd div.), 1909; commr. (1st div.), 1912; senr. commr., 1928; ag. stip. and circuit mag. in 1931, 1932 and 1934.

MACKINNON, WILLIAM LUNAN, M.A. (Aberdeen), 1911.—B. 1888; ast. audr., G. Coast, Nov., 1912; ag. senr. ast. audr., in 1918 and 1919; ag. ast. treas. (in addition to his own duties), May to Sept., 1919; ag. senr. ast. audr., Oct., 1919; ag. dep. audr. on various occasions, 1926-29; ag. audr., May-Oct., 1930; senr. ast. audr., Nigeria, Oct., 1930; prin. do., 1934.

MCKINSTY, SIDNEY ALEXANDER ROGER.—B. 1901; called to bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1930; supernumy. sub-inspr., pol., Leeward Is., 1929; sub-inspr., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1921; sub-inspr. and offr. instr., local forces, Antigua, 1924; sub-inspr., Montserrat, O.C.L., forces, offr. in ch., pris. and supt., fire brig., 1924; mag. and coroner, regist.-gen., regar., sup. ct., escheator gen., etc., Montserrat, 1928; dep. judge, Leeward Is., 1929; mem., exec. coun., Montserrat, 1929; chmn., prison justices, 1929; admstd. govt. of Montserrat, 1929; dist. commr., Belize, Br. Honduras and offr. admstr. and offr. recr., 1930; ag. atty.-gen. in 1930, 1931 and 1932; ag. col. sec. in 1932; ag. ch. just. in 1931, 1932 and 1934.
MACKLEY, GARNET HERCULES.—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. govt. rly. dept., 1900; ch. clk., 1928; ast. gen. man., 1931; gen. man., 1933.

MACKLIN, MAJOR RODERIC WILLIAM, M.O., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1891; serv. European War, 1914-18; with army of occupation, Germany, 1919; major, R.A. (R.O.); travelling commr., Gambia, Mar., 1920; do., South Bank prov., 1920; do., Macarthy Is. prov., 1924; commr., North Bank prov., 1930.

MCLACHLAN, HON. ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. Adelaide Univ.; called to bar, 1895; el. C'wealth senator for S. Australia, Nov., 1923; hon. min., Aug., 1926; ag. atty.-gen., 1926-27; plenipotentiary for Australia for signing of Kellogg Pact, 1928; vice-pres., exec. coun., Lyons ministry, 1932.

MACLACHLAN, JAMES BOYD, M.C., M.Sc., B.E., A.M.I.C.E. Chartered Civil Engnr.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway and Royal Univ. of Ireland; on active serv., Canadian F.A., 1915-19; M.C., 1917; mentd. in despatch, 1918; ast. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1919; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Apr., 1928; ag. sr. exec. engnr., June, 1929; ag. state engnr., Kelantan, May, 1934.
MACKLIN, MAJOR RODERIC WILLIAM, M.C., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1891; serv. with R. Artillery in the Gt. War, 1914-18; travelling consmr., Gambia, Mar., 1920.
MAGLADERY, THOMAS.—B. 1876; M.I.A. for Temiskaming, Ont., 1914-24; promoted in war serv. to capt.; dir., soldier settlmt. bd., 1931; dep. min., immigr. and colonization, 1934.

MACLAREN, JOHN SUTHERLAND.—B. 1900; Fettes Coll. and Edin. Univ.; M.A. (hons. class.), 1921; Fettes exhibn., Lanfine schol., Ritchie schol., 1st equal Gray Essay Prize, 1922; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; ag. 3rd and 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924; passed in Cantonese, Dec., 1925; ast. treas. and ast. assessor, 1926; ast. estate duty comsmr., 1927; 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1928; ast. dist. offr., N. and S. dists., New Territories, 1929; ast. treas., 1930; dist. offr., South, 1931; ast. treas. and dep. estate duty comsmr., 1933.

MACLAREN, COL. THE HON. MURRAY, C.A.M.C., C.M.G., LL.D., M.D., C.M., M.R.C.S., F.A.C.S., Commander of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, also of Order of Avis Portugal.—B. 1861; ed. Gramair Schol., St. John, N. B.; univ. of New Brunswick; Edinburgh univ.; Vienna; served overseas, 1914-19; int. el. to H. of C., Canada, 1921; re-el. at g. e., 1925 and 1926; min. of pensions and national health in Bennett admin., 1930.
MACLEAN, ADRIAN JOHN.—Ed. at Selwyn Coll., Camb., B.A., 1895; M.A., 1901; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1901; collr., Apr., 1905; recorder of titles, 1911; senr. comsmr., Kenya, May, 1920.

MACLEAN, F. H.—B. 1889; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to bd. of educn., May, 1908; transf. to C.O., Dec., 1911; on military serv., from 25th Apr., 1917, to 23rd Feb., 1919; cler.offr., higher grade, 24th Jan., 1922.

MACLEAN, GEORGE, O.B.E. (1934), M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.).—B. 1888; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Mar., 1918 to Mar., 1919; temp. capt., Mar., 1919 to June, 1920; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1921; sleeping sickness offr., Apr., 1926.

MACLEAN, SIMON JAMES, M.A., LL.B., Ph.D.—B. 1871; comsmr. of the Canadian govt. to report on rly. rate grievances, 1901; advr. to commr. of govt. to draft Railway Act, 1903; prof. of polit. econ., Toronto Univ., 1906-1908; apptd. a mem. of rly. comm., Canada, 1908; re-apptd., Nov., 1918; ast. chief comsmr., Aug., 1919.

MACLENNAN, FINLAY MALCOLM.—B. 1887; ed., Tarbutt Public Schol.; Sault Ste. Marie, Ont., High Schol.; ent. serv. of dept. of rlys. and canals, Can., as officeman, Sault Ste. Marie Canal, Sept., 1910; transferred, Port Nelson on Hudson Bay terminals work, as jur. engnr., 1912; ast. audr., dept., rlys. and canals, Ottawa, 1st Apr., 1915; audr., 1917; ch. audr., 1918; ast. comptlr., 1920; comprtr., 1930.

MACLENNAN, IAN MORRISON ROSS.—B. 1909; ed., Hymers Coll., Hull and Worcester Coll., Oxford; schol., 1928; 2nd cls. hons., mods., 1930; 1st cls. philo.-phy, politics, economics, 1932; Gladstone research student, St. Deiniol's liby., Hawarden, 1932-3; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. prin., C.O., 1933.

MACLENNAN, NORMAN MACPHERSON.—B. 1895; ed., Waitaki College (N.Z.) and Aberdeen Univ.; M.D., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.); war serv., 1915-18; res. med. offr., City Hosp., Aberdeen, 1922; bacteriologist, James Mackenzie Inst., St. Andrews and M.O.H., St. Andrews, 1923; ast. M.O.H. and tuberculosis offr., Woolwich, 1925; health offr., Kenya, 1927; senr. med. offr., Palestine, 1929; senr. health offr., N. Rhodesia, 1931; tuberculosis expert, Palestine, 1933.

McLEOD, DAVID WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Cent. Training Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; senr. ast., King's Coll., Seychelles, 1910; prin., King's Coll. and inspr., schls., Seychelles, 1913; on active serv., 1914-19; senr. ast., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Feb., 1920; prin., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Jan., 1922; prin., Free Schl., Penang, Dec., 1930; prin., Raffles Inst., S'pore, Feb., 1933.

MACLEOD, J. R. S.—B. 1887; ed. Scots Coll., Ballarat and Cambridge, B.A., LL.B., F.S.A., Scotland; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1921; lieut., Australian Citizens Forces, 1914; served overseas as 2/lieut., Australian Imp. Forces, 1915; capt., 1918 (wounded, desps. twice); temp. clk., F.O., 1921; ast. consnr., national savings comte., 1922; after compet. exam. apptd. ast. prin., Overseas Sttlmt. Dept., 1924.

MCLUCKIE, WILLIAM HARLEY, A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1895; ed. Greenock High Schol., Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; milly. serv., 1915-19; ast. engnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; exec. engnr., 1928; ag. dep. dir., pub. wks. for various periods, 1930-33; ag. dir., pub. wks. in 1933.
MACLUSKIE, HECTOR, C.D.A.—B. 1900; univ. dipl., agr. (Aberdeen); Imp. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1929-30; agrl. supt., dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1930.

MOHAMON, CECIL, O.B.E. (1934), M.C.—Ent. S. Rhodesia civ. serv., Nov., 1909; served S. African rebellion and German S.W. African campaign, 1914-15 with 1st Rhodesian regt.; E. Africa campaign with 8th S. African Inf., 1915-18; capt. and adjt., "1914-15" Star and M.C.; ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1918; dist. offr., June, 1926; dep. prov. comsmr., 1934.

MACMICHAEL, SIR HAROLD, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1927), D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1852; ed. King's Lynn and Bedford Schls. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); joined Sudan polit. serv., 1905; successively inspr. in provs. of Korodofan, Blue Nile and Khartoum; polit. and intell. offr., expedy. force, Darfur reoccupation, 1916 (three times ment. in desps., D.S.O.); subj. gov., Darfur prov.; ast. civ. sec., Sudan govt.; civ. sec., do., 1926; gov. and comdr-in-ch., Tanganyika Territory, 1934; Order of the Nile (2nd cls.), 1930; Burton Memorial Med. of the R. Asiatic Soc'y., 1928; Star of Ethiopia (2nd cls.), 1930; author of "The Tribes of Northern and Central Kordofan" (1912), "A History of the Arabs in the Sudan" (1922), "The Anglo-Egyptian Sudan" (1934).
MACMILLAN, HON. CHARLES EDWARD DE LA BARCA.—B. 1872; ed. Brighton pub. schl. and pvtly.; ent. N.Z. H. of R., 1923; min. of agr. and mines, 1932.
MCMILLAN, L. B.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schls., Charlottetown, Prince Edward Is.; messenger, P.E. Is. govt., 1890; clk., 1893; sec. for pub. wk., P.E. Is., 1900.
MCNAMARA, GEORGE, C.B.E. (1932).—B. 1881; entd. N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1896; aspt. of staff, 1916; 2nd ast. sec., 1920; 1st ast. sec., 1923; sec., 1926; dir.-gen., 1933.

MACNIE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER.—B. 1899; 2nd lieut., Seaforth Highlands, 1918; lieut., 1919-20; served, France, 1918; sub-instr., pol., B. Guiana, 1921; dist. instr., 1925; ag. detective instr., 1925; ag. pol. instr., 1926, 1928-31; seconded to col. secretariat as addnl. asst. col. sec., May, 1931; dist. commr., Jan., 1932.

McNULTY, THOS. SYDNEY.—B. 1870; ed. at Marist Coll., Sydney; clk., acta branch, wks. dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1896; ch. corresp. clk., ditto, 1897; mem. mun. coun., Claremont, 1901-3; sec. to goldfields water supply admin., 1903; under-sec. for agric. and industries, 1909.
McPHEE, SIR JOHN CAMERON, K.C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1878; premier of Tasmania since 1st June, 1928.

MACPHERSON, BARBARA.—Ed. Durness H.G. Schi. and Skerry’s Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after compet. exam. typist, Lond. tel. serv., 1912; transf. to C.O., May, 1918; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., Jan., 1923.
MACPHERSON, COLIN, R. OF O., K.A.R.—B. 1898; ed. Alleyn’s Coll., Dulwich; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1929.

MACPHERSON, JOHN STUART.—B. 1898; ed. Watson’s Coll. and Edin. Univ. (M.A., 1921); 2nd lieut., A. and S. Highlanders, Jan., 1918; France, Apr., 1918-Jan., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ag. 4th asst. sec. to govt. F.M.S., Aug., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; ag. asst. dist. offr., K. Salangor, Aug., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1923; offr., cls. V, Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Bentong, Oct., 1925; offr. cls. IV, dist. offr., Bentong, Mar., 1928; relief asst. sec. to govt. F.M.S., Feb., 1930; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1933; attcl. C.O., Sept., 1933.

McPHILLIPS, HON. ALBERT EDWARD.—B. 1861; ed. St. Boniface and Manitoba Colls.; barrister, K.C., 1900; elected to leg. assem., B.C., 1898; atty.-gen., 1903; subsequently pres. of coun.; judge, ct. of appeal, B.C., 1913.

McQUADE, JOHN HERBERT.—B. 1893; highest marks in U.K. in entrance exam. for cust. and excise, Mar., 1913; imp. cust. and excise, July, 1913; war serv., Flanders with R.A., 1917-18; asst., cust., Kenya, July, 1920; collr., cust., Aug., 1924; sec., tariff comte., 1930; dep. compt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930; census offr., 1931; sec., econ. advy. bd., 1931-33; compt., cust., Nov., 1934.

MACQUARRIE, EDMUND JEFFREY.—B. 1883; ed. Queen’s Coll., B. Guiana and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., L.B.B., 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1906; ag., stip. mag., B. Guiana, June to Nov., 1906, and July, 1907 to Mar., 1914; dormant commr. as stip. mag. and apptd. J.P. and commr. of oaths, Dec., 1907; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 1915; crown counsel, 1916; ag. chief regisr., Apr.-May, 1917; ag. senr. crown counsel, 1917; ag. pol. mag., Nov., 1917 to Apr., 1918; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1919; ag. ch. regisr., Apr. to Oct., 1919; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923, 1924 and 1927; puisne and circuit judge, Sierra Leone, 1931.
MACQUILLAN, CECIL JAMES, B.A., M.B., B.Ch. (T.C.Dublin). M.O., R.A.F. (res.) served U.K. and Irak, 1924-26; R.M.O., Kent and Canterbury hosp., 1926; medl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1927.
MACRAE, LACHLAN, M.A. (Aberdeen), M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1886; dir., educn., Ceylon, Jan., 1922; M.L.C.

McROBERTS, CAPT. B. A.K.—Natal mounted pol., 1903-08; Natal rebellion, 1906; Cautu operations, 1907; instr., veterinary pol., B.A.I., 1909; asst. instr., pol., Mar., 1910; asst. supt., Sept., 1911; capt., Gordon Highlanders, Aug., 1915; France, 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1917; attd., Highland Light Inf., Mesopotamia, Mar., 1917; seconded, polit. dept., dist. mag. and dep. mily. gov., Basrah, 1917-18; mily. mission to Siberian govt., 1919; supt., pol., Kenya Colony, May, 1920; mag., Zanzibar Prot., Jan., 1921; ag. asst. judge, in 1922 and 1924; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1925; pol. mag., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1926.

McTURK, WILLIAM HENRY, F.S.I.—B. 1883; survrs. asst., dept. lands and mines, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1901; land survr., 1904; 2nd clsc. offr. and govt. survr., 1906; J.P., 1908; land offr., Pomeroon dist., 1910; drawing office supt., Jan., 1919; ag. asst. commr., lands and mines, Apr. to Nov., 1920 and May to July, 1923; in char., dept. lands and mines, June to July, 1920; ch. clk., 1922; land offr. and survr., Gambia, Nov., 1924.

MAFFEY, SIR JOHN LOADER, K.C.B. (1934), K.C.M.G. (1931), K.C.V.O. (1921), C.S.I. (1920), C.I.E. (1916).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby and Christ Ch., Oxford; ent. I.C.S., 1899; transf. polit. dept., 1905; served with Mohmand Field Force, 1908 (med. and clasp); polit. agt., Khyber, 1909-12; dep. commr., Peshawar, 1914-15; dep. sec., foreign and polit. dept., govt. of India, 1915-16; ch. polit. offr., Afghanistan with N.W. Frontier Field Force, 1919; pvt. sec. to viceroy, 1916-20; ch. sec. to Duke of Connaught, 1921; ch. commr., N.W. Frontier prov., India, 1921-24; gov.-gen., Sudan, 1926; perm. under-sec. of state for the cols., Nov., 1933.
MAGILL, CHARLES VICTOR, O.B.E. (1934).—Ast. dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Nov., 1919; dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Apr., 1926.

MAGRATH, CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1860; ed., priv. tuition; irrigtn. and development work in S. Alberta from 1878; mem. for Lethbridge in N.W.T. asm. for several years, and finally mem. of prov. cabinet; el. to H.C., Can., 1908, for Medicine Hat; def., 1911; fuel contr., Can., 1917-20; chmn., Internat. Joint Coman., Can. sect., 1921.
MAHABIR, F.—B. 1888; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Trinidad, Apr., 1915.
MAHABIR, JULES.—B. 1891; called to bar, Gray’s Inn, July, 1916; mag., St. Patrick, Trinidad, Mar., 1934.

MAINGOT, ELLIOT FRANCIS.—B. 1891; ed. Ushaw Coll., Durham; served articles of clerkship in London and qualified as solr., 1914; crown solr., admstr. gen. and offr. receiver, Trinidad, Dec., 1922; pub. trustee, May, 1931.
MAJOR, ALFRED REGINALD SEYMOUR.—B. 1909; ed. St. John’s, Leatherhead; pol. prof. ationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1929; asst. supt., pol., Dec., 1931.
MALAN, HON. D. F., M.A., D.D.—M.L.A., Calvinia Divn., Union of S. Africa; min. of int. pub. health and educn., June, 1924 to Mar., 1933.

MALAN, Rt. Hon. François Stephanus, P.C. (1920).—B. 1871; ed. in S. Africa and Camb.; B.A. Science, Cape univ.; LL.B., Camb.; advocate of sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1895; M.L.A. for Malmsbury, 1900; re-elected, Feb., 1904; min. of agric., Cape Colony, 1908; min. of educn., Union of South Africa, 1910; attended Imp. conf., 1911; min. of mines, industries and educn., 1912; min. for agr., 1920; min. of mines and industries, 1921-24; senator, 1927.

MALAN, François Stephanus.—B. 1882; ed. Normal Coll., Capetown and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, B.A.; inspr. schls., Transvaal, 1916; prin., Normal Coll., Ermelo, Transvaal, 1919; sec., Transvaal educn. dept., 1924.

MALAN, Hendrick Jacobus, B.A. (Cape).—B. 1879; Cape civ. serv., 1900; war serv., capt., Pretoria Rgt., S.W.A., 1914-15; astt. mag., Johannesburg, 1918; mag. Britstown, 1919; Ladysmith, 1922; Johannesburg, 1927; Senekal, 1930; Potchefstroom, 1933.

MALAN, Wouter de Vos.—B. 1892; ed. Boys High Schl., Huguenot Coll., Wellington, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Teachers' Coll., Columbia Univ., New York; B.A. lit. and phil., 1911; B.A. (hons.) hist., 1916; Ph.D., Col. Univ., 1923; princ., Ceres High Schl., 1924-28; prof. educn., Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1929-30; prof. astt. to supt.-gen., educn., Cape Prov., 1930; supt.-gen., educn., Cape Prov., 1934.

MALCOLM, Harcourt Gladstone, C.B.E. (1935), O.B.E. (1918); K.C. (1910).—Priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson when admstr. of Bahamas, 1892, 1893, 1895; ditto to Sir W. F. Haynes-Smith, gov. of Bahamas, 1895; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, July, 1899; mem. of H. of A., Bahamas, 1900; deputy-speaker, 1901; re-elected, 1904 and 1911; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1905; Sept., 1906 to Feb., 1907; July to Nov., 1910; Apr. to Dee., 1911; May to July, and Aug. to Nov., 1913; ag. chief just., 1913 and 1927; speaker, H. of A., 1913; re-elected, 1913, 1919, 1927 and 1928; thanked by S. of S. for assistance to atty.-gen. in drafting bills; thanked by legis. for obtaining copies of early records; Bahamas del. to Canada-W. Indies confcs., at Ottawa, 1920 and 1925; Bahamas rep. during visit of Prince of Wales, and partly, ter-centenary celebrations at Bermuda Oct., 1920; Bahamas del. to W. Indian confce., London, 1926; prepared 1929 revised edition of the laws of Bahamas.

MALHERBE, Ernst Gideon.—B. 1895; ed. Stellenbosch Univ. and Columbia Univ.; B.A. Hons., M.A. (phil.) (Cape), M.A. and Ph.D. (educn.) Columbia; fellow, Teacher's College, Columbia Univ., 1923-24; teacher, Cape Town Training Coll.; lect., educn. psychology, Univ. of Stellenbosch; senr. lect., educn., Univ. of Cape Town; ch. investigator educn. sec., Carnegie Poor White Commn. of research, 1928-32; dir., National Bureau for Educn. and Social Research, 1929; author publications on educn. subjects.

MALHERBE, Joza François.—B.1880; clkr., dept. of just., Transvaal, 1900; clkr., commr. of pol., 1902; 1st gde. clkr. and A.R.M., dept. of just., 1908; pub. prov., Kimberley, 1912; senr. clkr., dep. of just., 1920; mag., Wynberg, 1921; addt. mag., Pretoria, 1925; mem., wage bd., 1926; pub. serv. inspr., 1930.
MALONE, Denis Keron.—B. 1903; ed. Cooper's Company Schl.; cler. offr., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921.

MANDERS, Richard Henry David, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1927; attd., secretariat, Feb., 1927; attd., Kandy kach., Oct., 1927; office astt., ditto Feb., 1929; ditto, Kandy kach., May, 1930; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Dec., 1931; astt. ch. sec., Apr., 1932; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1933; pol. mag., Galle, Nov., 1933.
MANGAN, William Edward.—Served in Bechuanaand Prot. pol., 1910-14; elk., 1914; ag. astt. res. mag., 1921; passed 2nd grade Sewana exam., 1917; ag. res. mag., 1922.

MANGIN, Thorleif Rattray Orde.—B. 1896; ed. Marlborough Coll.; sub-lieut., R.N.V.R. and attd. to Royal Naval Dvn., 1915; qualified, gunnery course, H.M.S. Excellent, 1916; apptd., to H.M.S. Repulse and served with Grand Fleet, 1916-1919; astt. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1919; dep. prov. commnr., 1934.

MANION, Hon. Robert James, M.C., M.D., C.M. (Toronto), L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P. and S. (Glasgow).—B. 1881; ed. Fort William and Port Arthur High Schls.; Trinity, Toronto, and Edinburgh Univ.; el. to H. of C., Canada, at g.e. 1917; min. of soldiers civ. re-estab., 1921; re-e.l., g.e., 1921 and 1925; P.M.G., in Meighen cabinet, 1926; re-e.l., g.e., 1926; min. of rly.s and canals in Bennett cabinet, 1930.

MANN, George Ernest, M.C., M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1896; ed. St. Olave's Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; astt. agrl. inspr., F.M.S., Sept., 1921; agrl. field offr., Nov., 1924; agrl. instr., Dec., 1926; vice-prin., schl. of agr., Malaya, Apr., 1931.

MANNING, Richard Joseph.—B. 1883; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; sub-inspr. of police, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; dist. inspr., Sept., 1912; ag. stip. mag., W. Coast, Demarara, June, 1919; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1920; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1920; res. mag., Jamaica, July, 1925; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Nov., 1927; ag. ch. regis. on various occasions, 1928-31; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N.Territories judiciary, July, 1930; 3rd puisne judge, Trinidad, 1932; higher certif. in Hindi, Mar., 1916; higher certif., Swahili, 1922; lower certif., Ga., 1928; compendium "Br. Guiana Police Manual," Dec., 1918.
MANNING, William.—B. 1892; on active serv., Sept., 1914 to Feb., 1919; inspr., pol., Zanzibar, Mar., 1919; supdt., 1924; ag. astt., commdt., on various occasions, 1925-32.

MANSERGH, N. S., M.B.E.—B. 1889; ed. Wellington Coll.; B.S.A. pol., 1910; Rhodesia Natl. Regt., 1917; lieut., 1917; demob., 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1919; opera. against Mad Mullah, 1920; astt. commnr., pol., Gold Coast, 1921; commr., pol., 1926; commr., pol. orim. investgn. dept., 1927.

MANTON, Cyril Cowan.—B. 1877; 2nd cls. clk., collectorate, Black River, Jamaica, 1897; 1st cls. treasy. clk., 1903; junr. astt. collr. taxes, 1912; astt. collr., Kingston, 1920; dep. stamp commnr., 1920.
MARCH, Harry Thomas.—B. 1881; ed. King's Coll., London; 2nd divn. clk., post office, 1900-07; acctnt., post office, Nigeria, Apr., 1907 to Jan., 1912; postmr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Feb., 1912.
MARCHANT, W. S.—Astt. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. commnr., Kenya, 1926.

MARCIU, HON. CHARLES, LL.D., Ottawa Univ.—B. 1860; elec. to H. of C. of Canada for Bonaventure, 1900; dep. speaker of H. of C., 1905-1908; re-elec. at gen. elec., 1908, 1911, 1917, and 1921; speaker of the H. of C., 1909; mem. of P.C. for Canada, 1911.
MARILLIER, ERIC STANLEY, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1890; olk., cust., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1912; collr., cust., Apr., 1915; senr. collr., cust., Oct., 1926; contr. cust., Mar., 1928; mem., leg. coun.

MARIETZ, G. J.—B. 1889; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1912; pres., sp. income tax appl., 1926; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1930; mem., delimitation comm., Mar., 1932.

MARKS, LIEUT. COMMDR. N. J. S., N.D., R.N.R.—B. 1893; ed. H.M.S. “Worcester”; served Royal Navy, 1914-20; (lieut. in commd. H.M.T.B. 070 on mine clearance service, 1918); demob., Mar., 1920; 2nd offr., lake steamers Uganda rly., Oct., 1920; ag. commd., lake steamers, 1924-28; asst. to port man., Mombasa, 1928; port. man., July, 1933.
MARLOW, SYDNEY RAYMOND.—B. 1896; mily. serv., R.W.A.F.F., 1916-18; treas., asst. 3rd grade, Nigeria, 1919; senr. asst. treas., 1933.

MARR, MAJOR THE HON. CHARLES WILLIAM CLANNAN, D.S.O., M.C., V.D., M.P.—B. 1880; served in European war with Australian and N.Z. wireless squadron in Mesopotamia (ment. in desps. four times); M.H.R., C’wealth of Australia since Dec., 1929; hon. min., 1925-27; ag. min. for defence, 1928; sec. to Canberra parly. com. from 1929; min. for home and territories, 1927-29; min. for health and works and sec. to cabinet, Lyons’ ministry, 1932.
MARRS, ROBERT, M.A. (Oxon.), C.I.E.—B. 1884; prin., Ceylon Univ. Coll., Sept., 1921.

MARDSDEN, ERNEST, D.SC., M.C. (1898).—B. 1889; ed. Queen Elizabeth Gram. Schl., Blackburn, and Univ. of Manchester; lect., Univ. of London, 1911; fellow, Univ. of Manchester, 1912; prof., Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z., 1915; asst. dir. of educ., N.Z., 1922; sec., dept. of scientific and indu. research, 1926; served in Great War.

MARDSDEN, HERBERT, B.SC. (Manch.), A.I.C.—B. 1887; ed. Hulls Hall, Manch. Univ.; asst. chem., inst. med. resch., F.M.S., Jan., 1914; chmn., do., Jan., 1921; chem., trade and cust. dept., F.M.S., Aug., 1928.

MARSH, EDWARD HOWARD, C.B., C.M.G., M.V.O.—B. 1872; ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; scholar, Trin. Coll., 1891; 1st cl. classical tripos, 1893; sen. chancellor’s medal for classics, 1895; 1st cl. with distinction classical tripos, Part II., and B.A., 1895; M.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Sept. 29th, 1896; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, July, 1900; to Mr. Lyttelton, Oct., 1903; 1st cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1905; priv. sec. to Mr. Winston Churchill, U.S. of S. for the Colonies, Jan., 1907; accompanied Mr. Churchill on his visit to E. Africa and Uganda, 1907-8; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Pres. of B. of T., 1908; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Home Sec., 1910; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as first Lord of the Admiralty, 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, June to Nov., 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Asquith, first lord of treasy., 16th Nov., 1915; returned to C.O. as 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as min. of munitions, July, 1917; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill, as sec. of state for war, Jan., 1919, as S. of S. for the Colonies, Feb., 1921 and as chancellor of exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; priv. sec. to the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 23rd Jan., 1924; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Chancellor of the Exchequer, Nov., 1925; pvt. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas as Lord Privy Seal, and afterwards as S. of S. for Dom. affrs., Dec., 1929; pvt. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas as S. of S. for Dom. affrs. and for the cols., 26th Aug., 1931.

MARSHALL, C. W.—Imp. serv., 1897-98; clk., atty.-gen.’s dept., Cape Town, 1898; examin. accts., 1899; clk., res. mag., King William’s Town and distr., stamps, 1900; sec., plague admn., 1903; 2nd cls. examin., control and audit dept., Cape, 1905; inland rev. dept., 1910; contr. and audr.-gen.’s dept., 1912; senr. clk., 1912; recr., Port Elizabeth, 1916; surr., rev., Pretoria, 1921; Cape Town, 1929; recr., rev., Pretoria, 1930; ditto, Johannesburg, 1932.

MARSHALL, EDWARD FOSTER, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attd. Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1913; Badulla kach., Mar., 1913; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1917; asst. gov’t. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1918; pol. mag., Gam-pola, May, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Galle, Sept., 1923; pol. mag., Matare, Dec., 1923; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Avissawella, Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Avissawella, Jan., 1928; ditto, Badulla, Feb., 1929; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1930; asst. attlm. offr., Nov., 1931; dep. tea contr., May, 1933.

MARSHALL, FREDERICK CHARLES.—B. 1880; ed. Repton and Camborne Sch. of Mines; attlm. offr., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1901; inspr., mines, Mar., 1902; mag., Dec., 1902; asst. warden, mines, Pahang, Jan., 1904; dep. supt., census, Oct., 1910; mag., Raub, Mar., 1912; collr., Raub, July, 1912; seconded for service in S. Nigeria Nov., 1913 to Mar., 1914; warden, mines, Kedah, June, 1918; warden, mines, F.M.S., warden mines, Johore, Feb., 1921; commr., under land enact., Oct., 1922; asst. registr., sup. ct., July, 1931; ag. 1st mag. in adrn., Dec., 1931.

MARSHALL, CAPT. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry.—B. 1893; exhibr. Queen’s Coll., Oxford, 1911; hona. moda. (maths.), 2nd cls. hons., 1912; 2nd lieut., Worc. Regt., Aug., 1914; Gallipoli, 1915 (wounded); Mesopotamia, 1916 (wounded); lieut., 9th Batt., Worc. Regt., France, 1917-18 (wounded and prisoner); capt., 1st Batt. Worc. Regt., F.A. (Oxon.), 1919; diploma of forestry (with distinc-tion—plaid head of the list), 1920; M.A., 1933; forestry commn., 1920-22; instr., forestry schl., Forest of Dean; asst. commr., national savings comm., 1922; dep. conservator, forests, Trinidad and Tobago, 1923; conservator, forests, 1927; mem., bd. of agr., 1923; conserv., forests, Gold Coast, 1934.

MARSHALL, REGINALD HENRY.—B. 1890; ed. Dulwich; active serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919; col. audit dept., May, 1919; audr., Ser-chelles, June, 1919; audr., Br. Honduras, Aug., 1924; C.A.D., Oct., 1927; dep. audr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930.

MARSLAND, HERBERT, B.SC. (Ag.)—B. 1894; ed. Ashton-under-Lyne Secondary Schl.; Coll. of Agr., Holmes Chapel, 1917; N.D.A., Harris Inst., Preston, 1921; N.D.D., W. of Scotland Coll. of Dairying, 1921; B.Sc. Univ. of Edinburgh, 1924; Imp. Coll. Troop Agr., Trinidad, 1924; inspr. agr., Sudan govt., 1925; cotton investigator, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

MARTIN, JOHN MILLER, B.A.—R. 1904; ed. Edinburgh Acad. (and) Oxford; class. school... Corpus Christi Coll., 1923-27; 1st cls. class. mods., 1924; B.A., 1927; apptd. after compet. exam. ast. prin., Doms. Office, 2nd Nov., 1927; seconded to Malayan civ. serv., 1st Dec., 1931; pvt. sec. to permit. under sec., D.O. (Sir Edward Harding), 21st Nov., 1934.
MARTIN, KENNETH SEFTON, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ast. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 1911; supervisor, cust., 2nd grade, 1911; 1st grade, 1919; senr. collr., 1929; ast. comptir., 1933.
MARTIN, LEWIS VERNON.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 17th Mar., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Jan., 1935.

MARTIN, PETER HENRY, A.F.C., M.A., B.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—B. 1896; pathologist, Inst., Med. Resch., F.M.S., Sept., 1928; bacteriologist, Dec., 1931; ag. dir., Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Apr., 1933.

MARTINDALE, MILES HAY, Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1893; ast. dist. offr., 1919; pvt. sec. to gov., 1920; ast. sec., S.P. secret., 1923; ag. dist. offr., 1924-27; dist. offr., 1928; ag. crown coun. and ag. ch. regisr., 1933; ast. judge, high ct., Prot., 1934.

MARTYN, ELDERD BRIDGEMAN, B.A., A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1903; ed. Marlborough Coll., Oriel Coll., Oxford and Imp. Col. Sci. & Tech.; C.O. scholarship courses in mycology, Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, and Imp. Col. Trop. Agr., Trinidad; botanist mycologist, dept. agr., Br. Guiana, 1928.

MARWICK, ALLAN GRAHAM, O.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; served in Natal civ. serv., 1895 to 1899; served Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1901; sub. native comsnr., Mbabane, Swaziland, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1907; ast. comsnr., ditto, Mar., 1907; ast. comsnr., Hlatikulu, Swaziland, Aug., 1916; mem. Swaziland-Portuguese bndry. comsnr., 1920; ast. comsnr., Mbabane and ag. dep. res. comsnr., Oct., 1932; dep. res. comsnr., 1933.

MASON, GERALD BOVELL.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; matric., London Univ., 1890; joined London hosp., 1890; passed prelim. scientific, 1892; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896, certif., London Sch. of Trop. Med., 1909; D.P.H., Cambridge, 1917; med. offr., No. 4 dist., Antigua, 1903; ag. comsnr., Virgin Is., 1904; surg.-lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla and dep. judge of sup. et., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905 to Sept., 1906; med. offr., No. 6 dist., News, 1906; med. offr., dist. "D", and mag. dist. "F", Dominica, 1907; med. offr., St. Vincent, 1909-12; temp. capt., R.A.M.C.; med. offr. in charge of troops, Cambridge, 1916-19; founder and hon. sec., W. Indian Club, 1897-8; founder and hon. sec., West India Red Cross Soc., 1921-1924; dir., med. and health dept., Bermuda, Aug., 1924.
MASSEY, T. H., O.B.E. (1934), M.C., L.R.C.P.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Jan., 1913; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Oct., 1923.

MASTEN, HON. CORNELIUS ARTHUR.—B. 1857, Quebec; educ. parish dist. schl., La Colle; La Colle Academy; Victoria Univ., Coburg, Ont. (graduated with hon., May 1879); mathematical master, high schl., Orillia, Ont., 1879-1880; entered law soc. of Upper Canada, Oct., 1880; called to the bar, 1883; K.C. (Ont.), and bencher of the law soc. of Upper Canada, 1908; comsnr. to enquire into the Insurance situation in Ont. with a view to improvements in the law; pres., Alumni Assoc. of Toronto Univ.; chmn., exec. comtee. of the speaker's patriotic league; author of "Master Company Law in Canada"; justice of the sup. ct. of Ontario, Nov., 1915.

MASTERS, HON. ROBERT, M.L.C.—B. 1879; ed. at Brunnerston, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. house of reps., 1919-26; mem. and dep. leader of leg. coun., and mem. of exec. coun. without portfolio, 1930; min. of educn. and min. of industries and commerce, 1931; mem., N.Z. delegation to World Economic Conf., 1933.

MASTERTON-SMITH, SIR JAMES EDWARD, K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1915).—B. 1878; ed. Harrow (schol.) and Hertford Coll., Oxford (schol.); ent. home civ. serv. (cls. L), admly., 1901; priv. sec. to 2nd sea lord, 1904-8; priv. sec. to perm. sec., 1908-10; priv. sec. to successive 1st lords of the admly., 1910-17; ast. sec., min. of munitions, Sept., 1917 to Jan., 1919; ast. sec.
