(addnl.), W.O. and Air Min., 1919-20; joint perm. sec., miny. of labour, 1920; perm. under-secr. of state for the colonies, 30th Aug., 1921; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; mem. of the offi. side of the National Whitley Coun.; ret., 1925; Offr. of the Belgian Order of the Crown.

MASTERTON-SMITH, PERCIVAL FRANK, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Wadham Coll., Oxford (hons. mod. hist.); B.A., 1921; R. Fusiliers, 1914-16; 60th Rifles, 1916-19; M.C., 1917; capt., res. of Offrs., 60th Rifles; adminis. offr., Nigeria, 25th Jan., 1922.
MATER, EDWARD JOHN.—Ed. Foyle Coll. and Taunton Schl.; ast. treas., Uganda, July 1920; senr. ast. treas., 1931.

MATHER, NORMAN FREDERICK HUGH.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; served in Rifle Brig., Dec., 1915-Sept., 1919; ast. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Mar., 1921; regisr., war serv. land grant scheme, S. Feb., 1922; sec. to res., Pahang, Dec., 1923; mag., Seremban, July, 1925; offr., cl. III, Nov., 1925; chmn., sany. bd., Seremban, Aug., 1926; cl. II, Nov., 1930; ast. advr., Segamat, May, 1928; dist. offr., Larut and regisr., titles, Perak, Mar., 1932; chmn., govt. rice mill in addn., May, 1933.

MATHERS, FREDERICK FRANCIS, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. schls. in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Dalhousie law schl. and Harvard law schl.; called to the bar, 1892; dep. prov. sec. and clk. of exec. coun., N.S., 1892; also regisr. of joint stock companies, 1909; now deputy atty.-gen.

MATHESON, CAPT. C. L. T., M.C.—Commissioned in Royal Engineers, 1909; ast. dir. of surv., G. Coast, 1914; Togoland campaign, 1914; Camerons, 1914-15; France with Field Coy. R.E., 1916-17; W.O., 1918; dep. dir. topographical surveys, G. Coast, 1920.

MATHIEWS, ARNOLD BURNETT.—B. 1898; ed. Shrewsbury (schl.) and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (cl. schl.); sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1917-19; 2nd cls., Pt. I, classical tripes, 1920; B.A., 1920; 2nd cls., anthropological tripes, 1922; ast. dist. offr., N.P. Nigeria, Jan., 1923; ast. sec., N.P. secretariat, Feb., 1925; ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Aug., 1927; clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Apr.-Sept., 1928; attd., C.O., July, 1929.

MATHIESON, HON. JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C.—B. 1863; ed. Harrington Gram. Schl., Prince of Wales' Coll., Taught Schl., Desable Gram. Schl., P.E.I., read law with Maclean and Macdonald; called to the P.E.I. bar, 1893; K.C., 1906; el. to legislature for King's county, 1900; re-el., 1904, 1908 and 1912; leader of the opposition, 1903; premier and atty.-gen., 2nd Dec., 1911; ch. just., P.E.I., 13th June, 1917.

MATTHEWS, ERNEST LEWIS, K.C., C.M.G. (1914).—Called to the bar, Inner Temple; law adviser to Transvaal govt., June, 1902; law adviser to govt. of Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., 1926.

MATTHEWS, LESLIE GARD.—Ed. Plymouth. Oxford Univ. O.T.C., 1915; O.A.S., France, 1916 and 1917, Lano. Fusiliers; Mesopotamia, 1917-21; attd., Indian Army and Iraq Levies; Arab rebellion, Iraq, 1920 (med. and clasp); astt. treas., Somaliland, June, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1923; astt. commr., pol., Nigeria, 1926.
MATTHEWS, HON. ROBERT CHARLES.—B. 1871; ed. McMaster and Harvard Univ.; lst ol. to H. of C., Canada, at gen. elec., 1926; re-elec., 1930; min., natl. rev. in Bennett cabinet, 1933.

MAUDE, HENRY EVANS.—B. 1906; ed. Highgate Schi. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1929; M.A., 1933; cadet, Gilbert and Ellioce Ia., 1929; dep. commr., 1931; ag. native lands commr., 1931; adm. offr., 1932; ag. sec. to govt., Mar.-June, 1933.

MAULE, JOHN PATRICK.—B. 1906; ed. Berkhamsted schi. and Sidney Sussex coll., Cambridge; M.A., 1932; dipl., agr., 1929; A.I.C.T.A. (Trinidad), 1930; supt., agr., Nigeria, 1930; man., govt. stock farm, Cyprus, 1934.

MAVOR, WILLIAM DAVID.—B. 1881; ed. Heriot Watt Coll. (Edin.), foreman, photo-litho secn., F.M.S., Oct., 1910; mily. serv., Highland F. Amb., Sept., 1915; demob., Mar., 1919; astt. supt., photo-litho secn., Jan., 1921; ch. photo-litho, surv. dept., F.M.S., July, 1923.

MAVROGORDATO, COL. ARTHUR STEPHEN, O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1886; ed. at Oakham Schi., Rutland; clk., pol. headqrs., Cyprus, Nov., 1903; inspr., mil. pol., Cyprus, May, 1906; in ch., pol. detachment, Kyrenia, 1907-1909; gov., prison and dep. coroner; in command of Papho dist. several occasions; detailed for sp. duty in Larnaca dist. in connection with Limassol riots, May-June, 1912; astt. commr., pol., Sierra Leone, June, 1913; J.P. and dep. coroner, 1913; ag. commr., pol., also sheriff, prison visiting just., and govt. nominee on city coun. of Freetown, and performing duties of marshal of the Admy. prize et al., on various occasions, 1916-18; ag. harbrmr. and detaining offr. under mil. authorities, in 1916, 1917, 1918 and 1919; ag. dist. commr. in headqrs. dist., S. Leone, Feb.-Mar., 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Palestine, Dec., 1922; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, Apr., 1924; commdt., ditto, Apr., 1926; rep. Palestine govt. at internat. pol. conf., Antwerp, Sept., 1930; inspr.-gen., constab., Trinidad, July, 1931; mem., leg. coun.; awarded King's Pol. med., 1934.

MAXWELL, COL. GEOFFREY ARCHIBALD PRENTICE, C.M.G. (1934), D.S.O., M.V.O., M.C., Officer Ordre de Leopold, Officer Legion d'Honneur, Belgian Croix de Guerre, French Croix de Guerre, M.Inst.T.—B. 1885; R.M.A., Woolwich, 1902; R.E., 1904; Nigeria Barakanorly., 1908-11; survey, E.A.P., 1912-14; European War, 1914-18 (ment. seven times in desps.); inter-allied comm. of control, rly., occupied territory, 1919; communications sec., sup. economic coun., 1919-20; gen. man., rly., Tanganyika territory, 1920.

MAXWEILL, SIR WM. GEORGE, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1915); Royal Humane Society's med. (1882).—B. 1871; ed. at Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp.; jun. offr., Perak, F.M.S., Mar., 1891 astt. mag., Kinta, Apr., 1892; regnr. of cts., Kinta, Perak, Feb., 1899; dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1905; dep. pub. pros., Singapore, 1906; sol.-gen., S. Settments, Aug., 1906; British adviser to govt. of Kedah, July, 1909; ag. col. sec., S. Stiltns., 1914; ag. sec. to high commr., Malay States and Brunei, 1915; ag. British res., Perak, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; ag. sec. to high commr., Malay States and Brunei, June, 1917; vice-chmn., food control comtee., S.S. and F.M.S.; vice-chmn., shipping control comtee., S.S.; pres., rubber comm., 1918; pres., Singapore housing comm.; pres., Singapore centenary comtee.; general adviser to govt., Johore, June, 1918; ag. col. sec., S. Stiltns., Dec., 1918; Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1919; ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ret., 1926; publications, "Perak Land Laws (past and present)," "The Laws of Perak, 1877-1903"; "The Laws of the S. Stiltns., 1827-1907," "In Malay Forests."

MAXWELL-ANDERSON, SIR MAXWELL HENDRY—Kt. Bach. (1934), C.B.E. (mil.) (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.).—Ent. Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; recd. thanks of Admy. for series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; inst., R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; promoted capt., Apr., 1919; govt. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, May, 1919; ag. chief just. and col. sec. on several occasions; ch. just. Fiji and judl. commr., W. Pacific, 1929; ag. high commr., W. Pacific, June to Aug., 1930.

MAY, HENRY O'HARA, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Univ. Dubl.), D.P.H. (Irel.)—B. 1878; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Apr., 1907; med. offr., health, Nov., 1911; junr. sanry. offr., Aug., 1916; dep. dir., sanry. serv., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1922; dep. dir., health serv., Gold Coast, June, 1926.

MAY, THOMAS WILLIAM VIVIAN, A.R.I.B.A., M.I.Struct.E., chrted. archt.—B. 1889; astt. prin. archt's dept. of H.M.O.W., 1902-14; war serv., Aug., 1914-Aug., 1919; astt. archt., S'pore Harbr. Bd., Nov., 1919; astt. archt., P.W.D., May, 1924; ag. govt. archt., B.S., Sept., 1925 and May, 1929; astt. archt., S'pore, July, 1933.

MAYBIN, JOHN ALEXANDER.—B. 1889; M.A. (Edin.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1914; attached to the Badulla kach., Jan., 1914; ag. office astt. to gort. agt., July, 1914, and Jan., 1915; addtl. dist. judge and pol. mag., Badulla in addn. June, 1915; pol. mag., Panadura, March, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; ag. pol. mag. Kanur, July, 1919; 2nd finan. astt. to chmn. mun. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1920; finan. astt., Colombo Municipality, Nov., 1920; addtl. astt. col. sec., Jan., 1922; landing surv., Colombo cust., Mar., 1923; 3rd astt. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 2nd astt. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Mar., 1925; astt. registr., co-op. socs., Apr., 1928; dep. registr., co-op. socs., Sept., 1932; ch. sec., Nigeria, 1934.

MAYHEW, ARTHUR INNES, C.L.E.—B. 1878; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford (schl.); B.A., 1901; Berlin and Jena Unive.; ent. Indian educnl. serv., 1903; educnl. advisor, Hyderabad State, 1910; dir., pub. instrn., Cent. Provs., 1913; ag. educnl. commr. to govt. of India, 1921; mem. Eton Coll. staff, 1922-29; joint sec. to advisory comtee. on educn. in the C.O., 1929; author of "The Education of India," "Christianity and the Government of India."

MAYLE, NORMAN LESLIE.—B. 1899; appnt. after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., Oct., 1917; sler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; on mil serv. from Sept., 1917 to Sept., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923; ast. prin., 16th Nov., 1928; ag. pvt. sec. to parly. U.S. of S. for the Cola., 2nd Feb., 1932; pvt. sec., do., 18th Apr., 1932.
MAYNARD, PERCY.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam, cler. offr., C.O., 15th Oct., 1920.

MEAD, JOHN PHILLIPS.—B. 1886; dip. forestry (Oxon); ed. Charterhouse Sch., Royal Indian Engng. Coll. (Coopers Hill) and Exeter Coll., Oxford; ast. cons. forests and ag. dep. cons. forests, Aug., 1907-May, 1916; on milly serv. 1916-19; inspr., forests Kordofan prov., Soudan, Aug., 1918; conserv., forests, Sarawak, 1919; temp. forestry advr. to govt., Fiji, Nov., 1926; ast. cons. forests, F.M.S., Jan., 1928; dep. cons. forests, F.M.S., March, 1928; dir., forestry, F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1930.

MEADE, CHARLES RICHARD.—Copyist, treasy. and post office dept., Montserrat, Oct., 1907; 6th clk., Apr., 1912; 4th clk., Nov., 1912; govt. offr., Sandy Point, St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1915; elk., treasy., Virgin Is., May, 1917; clk., exec. council; prin., Apr., 1927; ag. postmr., regis. and provost marshal, Virgin Is. circuit, Mar. to July, 1928; prin., treasy., Dominica, Dec., 1928; ag. treasy., 1931.

MEADE, CAPT. JOSEPH MICHAEL, B.A. (T.C.D.), Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns, Dublin)—B. 1889; European mast., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ast. mast., King Edward VII schl., Taiping, Feb., 1921; ast. inspr., schls., Perak, Jan., 1923, Sept., 1926 and Apr., 1929; inspr., schls., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1926; ast. inspr., schls., S'pore, Oct., 1930; ag. inspr., schls., Malacca, Jan., 1931; senr. educn. offr. (B), Nov., 1931.
MEADEN, BERTRAM GRANT, M.I.C.E.—B. 1879; irrign. engnr., Ceylon, Sept., 1905; dep. dir., irrign., Nov., 1928; dir., irrign., Nov., 1931.
MEADEN, HENRY, M.B.E. (1931).—B. 1882; ast. warden, Tobago, 1919; ast. warden, St. Patrick, 1922; mag. warden, Tobago, 1st Apr., 1926.
MEARNS, JOHN WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Engng.), Glasgow, A.M.I.Struct.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B.1894; ast. engnr., sea defences, Br. Guiana, 1920; do. pub. wks. dept., 1922; dist. engnr., 1925.

MEDWAY, F. W.—Imp. serv. (acctnt.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O.), 1901-2; elk., G.P.O., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1902; clk., treasy., O.F.S., May, 1908; senr. clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; senr. clk., inland rev. dept., Dec., 1916; prin. clk., Apr., 1917; ch. clk., April, 1928; recr., Pietermaritzburg, Apr., 1933.

MEEHAN, JOHN FRANCIS.—G.W.R., 1903-14; ent. traffic dept., Gold Coast govt. rly., Mar., 1914; seconded Togoland milly. rly., traffic offr., 1918-19; ast. traffic supt., Tanganyika rlys., Aug., 1920; traffic supt., April, 1928.
MEEK, A. L., L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., D.P.H. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.).—B. 1889; R.A.M.C., Aug., 1918 to May, 1920; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1922; senr. health offr., Apr., 1930.

MEGARRY, THOMAS, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Academical Inst., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; I.A. (1st cls. hons., classics and mental and moral philosophy); cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1922; passed cadet, Dec., 1924; ast. to sec., Chinese affrs., 1925; ast. head, sany. dept., Mar., 1928; 3rd ast. col. sec., June, 1928; sec., currency comttee, in addn., 1930; mag. and dist. offr., N. district, New Territories, Mar., 1931; head sany. dept. and pres., sany. bd., Jan., 1933; dist. offr., N. dist., New Territories, Jan., 1934.

MEGSON, JOHN.—B. 1895; apptd. after compet. exam. elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv.; temporarily assigned to Board of Agric., July, 1914; Home Office, Aug., 1914; on military service, Nov., 1915 to Mar., 1919; Home Office, Mar.-Apr., 1919; assigned to C.O., Apl., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 9th Jan., 1923; staff offr., 13th Oct., 1930.

MEIGHEN, Rt. Hon. ARTHUR, P.C., K.C.—B. 1874; ed. St. Mary's Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ. (B.A., 1896; Ist al. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., 1908; re-el., 1911, 1913 and 1917; defeated, 1921; el. by-el., 1922; re-el., 1925; solr.-gen., 1913; re-el. accl., 1913; p.c. (Can.), 1916; sec. of state and min. of mines (Can.), 1917; later, same year, min. of interior and supt. gen., Ind. affrs.; prime min. of Canada, 1920; p.c. (Eng.), 1920; prime min., Can., 1926; def., 1927; m.m., senate, 1932; min. without portfolio in Bennett cabinet and govt. leader in senate, 1932.

MELLISH, HON. HUMPHREY.—B. 1862; ed. Mount Mellick Sch., Prince of Wales Coll., Charlottetown, 1876-79 (holder of Govt. scholarship for County of Queen's P.E.I., 1876-78; medallist, classics and maths., 1879); Dalhousie Univ. (B.A., 1882, LL.B.); matriculant, Univ. of Lond., hons. div., 1883; called to N.S. bar, 1890; K.C., 1904; practised law, 1891-1918; pres., N.S. Barristers' Soc., 1912-13; agt., Canadian min. of just. in N.S., 1903-04; judge, sup. ct., N.S., 1918, also surrogate, and dep. local judge in adm'y., for N.S. dist., 1920; local judge in adm'y., 1921.

MELVILLE, THOMAS ALEXANDER.—B. 1880; G.P.O., (Lond.), Dec., 1895; supt., mails, G.P.O., S'pore, Oct., 1905; supt., money order branch and savings bank, but ag. acctnt., G.P.O., S'pore, Jan., 1915; acctnt., post and tel. dept., F.M.S., July, 1917, but conf. ag. acctnt., S'pore; ag. dir., posts and tels., F.M.S., Apr., Dec., 1921 and Oct., 1923; acct., posts and tels., F.M.S., Apr., 1923; ag. sec. for postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., in addn., Apr., 1926; offi. mem., coopern. bd., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; ast. sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., continuing to act as sec., ditto, Oct., Nov., 1926; ret., 1930; apptd. treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Leeward Is., June, 1934; mem. and execo. and leg. couns., Antigua, execo. and leg. couns., Leeward Is.; chmn., income tax comsrs.

MENENDEZ, SIR M. RAYMOND, KT. BACH. (1906).—Ed. Emman. Coll., Camb.; LL.B., Camb., 1890; barrister-at-law, Inner Tem.; ag. stip. and circuit mag., etc., and judge of com. pleas, Bahamas, 1892-4; dist. comsrr., Lagos, Aug., 1894; ag. Queen's advocate, Lagos, Dec., 1896, to Aug., 1897; ch. judicial offr., Niger Coast Protectorate, Aug., 1897; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1899; ag. ch. just. on several occasions; recd. thanks of gov., for spec. services in reorganising native courts of prot., Jan., 1903; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1905.

MENTZ, GERHARDUS DANIEL POTGIETER.—B. 1875; elk., dept. of just., 1902; senr. elk., dept. of just., Pretoria, 1919; addnl. mag., Potchefstroom, 1920; mag., Klarksdorp, 1924; pub. serv. inspr., 1928; sec., pub. serv. comsrr., 1930.

MERCIERA, PROF. SIR ARTURO, KT. Bach. (1926), M.A., LL.D.—B. 1878; matric., Malta Univ., 1894; M.A., 1897; LL.D., 1901; el. for coun. of govt., 1907-10; ast. crown advoc., 1915; prof. of law, 1915; crown advoc., 1919; judge, 1921; ch. just., 1924; ex. in law, 1921.

MEREWETHER, SIR EDWARD MARSH, K.C.M.G.(1916); K.C.V.O.(1907); C.V.O.(1903); C.M.G.(1902).—B.1858; ed. Harrow; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1880; confirmed, Jan., 1883; Malay interp. with H.M.S. "Pegasus" to Tenom in connection with the "Nisero" case, June, 1884; dist. offr., Dindings, 1886; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1890; supt. of census, 1891; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1891; collr. of land rev., and in charge of treasy, Malacca, July, 1892; inspr. of prisons, S.S., 1893; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., May, 1897; Br. res., Selangor, Dec., 1901; lient.-gov. and ch. sec. to govt., Malta, Aug., 1902; gov., Sierra Leone, 1911; gov., Leeward Islands, Jan., 1916; ret.

MERRICK, H. F. C.—B. 1889; ed. Battersea Gram. Schl. and London Univ.; asst. audr., S. Nigeria, 1913; ditto, Sierra Leone, 1914; audr., Malta, 1920-22; clk., col. audit dept., 1922; audr., Gibraltar, May, 1927.

MERRICK, JOHN EDWARD SIEMFRED, O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1888; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1911; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 1912; 3rd asst. sec., 1913; 2nd asst. sec., 1916; senr. asst. sec., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1919; asst. col. sec. (2nd grade), Kenya, 1926; ag. asst. col. sec. (1st grade) in 1925 and 1926; prin. asst. col. sec., 1927; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1928-32; dep. ch. sec., Uganda, 1932; ag. ch. sec. in 1933 and 1934.
MESSERVY, E., M.R.C.V.S., D.T.V.M.—ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey and R. Vety. Coll., Lond.; D.T.V.M. (Univ. Edin.), 1932; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1929.

MESSERVY, GEORGE FORSHAW.—B. 1889; ed. Minto House Schl. and Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; ag. survr.'s asst., lands and mines, Br. Guiana, 1910; 5th cls. offr., do., 1911; 4th cls. offr., 1915; land offr. and dist. commissary, taxation, Demerara River, 1916; ag. ch. clk., lands and mines, 1926; supt., lands and mines, 1930; ag. sec. and acct., do., 1931, 1932, and 1933.

METCALFE, CHARLES CAMPBELL, M.B.E. (1931). M.C.—B. 1880; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey; clk., W.O., 1901; olk., treasy., Nyassaland, 1911; K.A.R., 1914-22; ag. chief transport offr., Nyassaland, Apr., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ch. transport offr., Feb., 1923.

METHVEN, STUARTON COLLARD.—Herts. Yeomanry, 1898-99; served S. African War, 1899-1902; clk., cust., Fiji, Nov., 1912; clk., res. comsnr.'s dept., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Coly., Nov., 1913; served Great War, 1916-18; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific, Oct., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Dec., 1918; dist. offr., July, 1926; offr. in ch., constaby., Ocean Is., 1928; do. and supt., grol., 1931.
METIVIER, CAPT. HARRY VINCENT MERCER, O.B.E.—Govt. vet. surg. examr. of animals, Trinidad, Dec., 1920.

METZGEN, MONRAD SIGFRID, M.B.E.(1932).—B. 1893; temp. clk. to keeper of prisons, B. Hond., Mar., 1910; transf'd. to treas. dept., Aug., 1910; temp. asst. keeper of King's warehouses, Sept., 1910; 3rd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1911; 2nd grade ditto, Jan., 1918; 2nd lieut., B. Hond. Territorial Force, June, 1918; internal rev. offr., Mar., 1920; seconded to income tax dept., June, 1921; prin. offr., income tax, Aug., 1922; offr. of cust., Nov., 1922; sec. to select comte. to enquire into cost of living to civ. servants, Mar., 1923; ag. audr. in 1923 and 1924; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, June-July, 1923; ch. income tax collr., Jan., 1924; joint compiler, Br. Hond. Handbook.

MEYER, LEOPOLD DONNELLY.—B. 1889; survey cadet, Queensland, 1909-11; survey br. state forests dept., May, 1911; lic. land survr., Victoria, Oct., 1913; survr., Kedah, Apr., 1914; mily. serv., 1917-19; demob., 1919; asst. survr., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, Apr., 1923; do., Pahang, 1924; supt., surveys, Trengganu, Sept., 1927; ag. supt., surveys, Kelantan, May, 1930; senr. asst. supt., surveys, Kinta, contd. as ag. supt., rev. surveys, Aug., 1933.

MIDDEBROOK, STANLEY MUSGRAVE.—B. 1898; ed. Bradford Gram. Schl. and St. Catharine's Coll., Camb. (open exhibn., mod. history); war serv., 1917-19; cadet, S.S. Apr., 1921; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; asst. prot., Chinese, Nov., 1924; attd., govt. monops. dept., S'pore, Nov., 1927; offr., cls. IV, Apr., 1928; asst. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang; asst. prot., Chinese, Muar, Apr., 1931; asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., F.M.S., July, 1932; sec., uniform licences and fees comte. (road and rail Transports, Malaya), Aug., 1932; offr. cls. III, Apr., 1933; asst. prot., Chinese, S'pore; asst. dir., educn., Chinese schls., F.M.S. Aug., 1934.

MIDDLETON, H. HALE, B.A. (1907).—B. 1885; ed. Warwick Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (hons. jurisprudence); asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1910; survey instr., Feb., 1914; dist. offr., Sept., 1915; res., Jan., 1924; ag. sec., N. Prova. on various occasions, 1926-29; staff grade, May, 1929; senr. res., 1929.
MIDDLETON, I. C., M.B. Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.)—B. 1890; mily. serv., 1917-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1929.

MIDDLETON, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1931). K.B.E. (1924). C.M.G. (1916).—B. 1870; ed. Sedburgh and Univ. of Edin.; M.A.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1901; ag. dist. comsnr., Warri, 1901; asst. sec., 1902; sen. asst. sec., 1904; officiated as sec. to govt. and divisional comsnr., 1904-5; dist. comsnr., 2nd grade, 1906; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and asst. prot. comsnr., 1906-7; senr. asst. col. sec., 1907; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1915; ag. col. sec., 1918-9-10-11-12-13; col. sec., 1913; admst. govt., 1914, 1916 and 1919; gov., Falkland Is., 1920; gov., Gambia, 1927; gov., Newfoundland, Sept., 1928; ret., 1932.
MIDDLETON, HON. WM. EDWARD.—B. 1860; ed. Toronto Coll., Inst. and Toronto Univ.; admitted solr., 1884; called to the bar, 1881; K.C., 1908; judge of high ct., Ontario, 1910.

MIFSUD, EDWARD R., C.M.G. (1932). O.B.E. (1927), M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1875; clk., Malta cir. serv. after compet. exam., 1892; clk., audit office, 1892; secretariat, 1901; ch. clk., lient. gov.'s office, 1913; dep. asst. sec. to govt. and clk. of couns., 1920; clk. of exec., nominated, and privy couns. and private sec. to gov., Oct., 1921; sec., Maltese Imp. Govt., June, 1927; sec. to govt., 1932.

MIFSUD, HON. SIR UGO P., KT. BACH (1927), LL.D. (1910), B. Litt. (1906), M.I.A.—B. 1888; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; called to bar, 1904; el. to rep. first dist. in Maltese parlmt., Oct., 1921, in June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. of industry and commerce, 28th Oct., 1921; prime min., Aug., 1924; leader of House, 1921-23 and from 1924-27; leader of opposition and head of national party, Aug., 1927 and June 1932; prime min., 1932-1933.

MILES, GEORGE COCKBURN, B.A., (Oxon.)—B. 1898; on. mil. serv., Mar., 1917; cadet, Ceylon cir. serv., May, 1921; attd., Jaffna kach., June, 1921; pol. mag., Puttalam, Aug., 1922; offr. asst., Galle kach., Apr., 1924; ditto, Anuradhapura
MILLAR, WILLIAM, B.E. (Irel.), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; mily. serv., 1916-19 (ment. in desps.), Gold Coast, 1920; irrigu. engrnr., Imga, 1922; anti-malarial engrnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1932.

MILLARD, ALFRED SUTTON, B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.) ; D.P.H.—Med. offr., S. Sttlmts., May, 1907; ag. sen. med. offr., Singapore, 23rd Jan. to 17th June, 1909; med. offr., grade II., F.M.S., 6th July, 1909; health offr., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; temp. med. offr., Quilting, Basuto-land, Sept., 1916; med. offr. of health, Cyprus, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. med. offr., June to Oct., 1921; ag. ch. med. offr., May to Sept., 1927; title altered to asst. dir. of health, May, 1928; ag. dir. health, June to Sept. and in Oct., 1929.

MILLARD, THOMAS, C.M.G. (1931).—B. 1884; ed. King's Coll., London; paymaster, 6th K.A.R., Mar., 1905; A.G.S. medal and clasp, 1908-10; asst. treas., Somaliland Prot., June, 1910; ag. treas. in 1910, 1911 and 1912; asst. treas., Cyprus, 1913; on mily. serv., B.E.F., France from Sept., 1915; capt. and adjt., 20th R. Fusiliers; wounded, Apr., 1917; resumed duty, Nov., 1921; ag. treas., June to Dec., 1922 and June to Nov., 1924; title altered to dep. treas., July, 1927; col. treas., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1928; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; rep. Br. Guiana at C.O. confce., 1930; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1933; col. sec., 1934.

MILLER, ALBERT EDWARD, M.C.—B.1892; H.M. Forces, Aug., 1914; serv. in Malta Sept., 1914 to Jan., 1915 and France, 1915-19; 2nd lieut., 1917; wounded, Feb., 1918; inspr., live-stock, Gold Coast, May, 1921.

MILLER, GEORGE LAWN.—B. 1889; asst. acctnt., F.M.S. Rlys., Dec., 1919; asst. sec. finance, bd. and estabmt., various periods, 1920, 1921, 1924 1927 and 1929; sec. to P. Sham. advay. bd., Jan., 1929; ag. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., Dec., 1930; sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., Jan., 1932.

MILLER, JAMES INNES, M.A. (Edin.), F.R.S.S.—B. 1892; ed. Fettes Coll. (schol.) and Edin. Univ.(exhbr.); James Welsh Bursar, 1911; 1st cls. hons., class., 1914; 1st cls. hons. hist., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., June, 1915; on serv. Suvala Bay and Mesopotamia, 1915-18; cadet, Malayan civ. serv. (S.S.), Oct., 1919; dep. collr. and regis. of deeds, Penang, Dec., 1919; mag., May, 1920; agt., food contr., Penang, Oct., 1920; secy. to food contr., Singapore, Feb., 1921; attd. col. secy.'s office in addn., June, 1921; passed cadet, May, 1922; mag. and 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 1923; cls. IV, May, 1924; dist. offr., Bukit Merlajam, Sept., 1924; sec., rubber restr., Johore, 1924; recr., Alsagoff concession in addn., Dec., 1924; mag. and asst. regisr., Johore in addn., 1924; inspr., prisons, Johore, in addn., 1926; 3rd mag., S'pore, Apr., 1929; cls. III, May, 1929; 2nd mag., S'pore, May, 1929; ag. regisr.-gen., statistics S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1929; on sp. duty, N.E.I., Oct., Nov., 1931; mem. and sec., S.S. trade consnr., 1933-34; sup't., rubber control, S'pore and Penang and regisr., textile quotas S.S. and F.M.S. in addn.; offr. cls. II, May, 1234.

MILLER, RALPH W. R., B.A.(Camb.), A.I.C., Dip. Agric. Camb.—B.1892; ed. Darlington and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd lieut., S. Lancashire Regt., 1915; sp brig., R.E., 1917-18; asst. analyst, Kenya, 1919; chem. offr., Kenya, 1923; senr. agrl. offr., Tanganyika, 1925; dir., agr., Barbados, 1929.

MILLINGTON, EDWARD TURNER.—B. 1881; ed. Victoria Univ., Manchester (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1904; extra off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov. and asst. collr. of cust., Jaffna, Jan., 1906; pol. mag., Avissawala, Apr., 1906; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1906; Cent. Prov., Aug., 1908; ag. dist. judge, etc., Nuwara Eliya-Hatton, Jan., 1910; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Feb., 1913; ditto, Matale, Dec., 1917; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, Apr., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, Aug., 1922; ag. regisr.-gen., July, 1923; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1924; ag. govt. agt., S. Prov., Dec., 1924; ag. govt. agt., Uva, Feb., 1925; dist. judge, Jaffna, Nov., 1925; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1926; addnl. govt. agt., W. Prov., Nov., 1928; ag. govt. agt., Jan., 1929; govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, June, 1929.

MILLINGTON, WM. MILNES, B.A. (Oxon.).—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1907; ols. V., Mar., 1911; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1917; supernmy. offr., cls. III., Jan., 1919; dist. offr., B. Padang, Sept., 1920; offr. cls. II., Dec., 1923; dist. offr., Uluselangor, Jan., 1924; chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. I., B., July, 1927; sec., res., Perak, ag. res. councillor, Malacca, May, 1931; under sec., cls. IA, S.S., Jan., 1932; res. coun., Malacca, Aug., 1933.

MILLS, ERIC, C.B.E. (1934), O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919, B.A. (Cantab.), F.S.S.—B. 1892; schol. of Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, 1910; Cowell school, 1913-14; 1st cls. maths. tripoe, pt. I., 1912; 2nd cls. maths. tripoe, pt. II., 1913; 2nd lieut., Bedford Regt., 1914; gas poisoned, 1915; transferred to Egypt, 1916; staff capt., gen. list, 1918; served in occupied enemy territory admstn.; ment. in desps., 1918; major, mily. gov., Gaza, 1919; asst. gov., Samaria dist., Palestine civil admstn., July, 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine gov't., and ag. prin., Nov., 1921; 1st asst. sec. to Palestine gov't., Oct., 1925; asst. ch. sec., Nov., 1926 and Jan., 1933; seconded as supt. census, May, 1931 to Dec., 1932; ag. ch. sec. on many occasions; consnr., migrn. and statistics, 1934.

MILLS, HENRY ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. East London Coll.; 4th cls. clk., C.A.'s office, 1907; on mily. serv., 1914-19; 3rd cls. clk., 1918; exec. offr., 1920; seconded for duty in Hong Kong, Aug., 1924; asct. post office, Hong Kong, Oct., 1926; senr. clerical and acctg. staff, 1927; attd., sup. ct. for sp. duty, 1927; asct., treasy., 1930; ch. acct., post office, 1933.

MILLS, JOHN VIVIAN GOTTLIER, B.A. Oxon. (Merton Coll.), Mods. and Lit. Hum., Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919.—B.1887; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1911; sec. to Chinese Riots Comsn., 1912; passed cadet, May, 1914; on sp. duty at Chinese emigrn. camp, Port Swettenham, Aug. to Sept., 1914; offr., cl. V., Nov., 1914; asst. protr., Chinese, Selangor and N. Sembilan, Feb., 1915; tempy. attd. office, consnr., trade and cust., Aug., 1918; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; agt. to food contrlr., F.M.S. in addn., Jan., 1919; offtg. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Nov., 1919; offr., cls. III., Oct., 1923; registr., sup. ct., Penang Dec., 1924; asst. offr. assignee, Penang, in addn., Apr. to July and Oct. to Nov., 1925; cls. I.B., Apr., 1928; solr.-genl. S.S., Apr., 1928; temp. puisne judge, Sept. to Nov., 1930 and Jan to Feb., 1931; ag. attv.-gen., Apr., 1932; puisne judge, S.S., Jan., 1933.

MILNE, Geoffrey, M.Sc. (Leeds), F.I.C.—B. 1898; ed. Hymers Coll., Hull and Leeds Univ.; milly. serv., 1917-19; asst., later lecturer, Leeds Univ., Sept., 1923; soil chemist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1928.
MILNE, T. M.—B. 1890; dep. regiser. gen. and dep. regiser. and dep. marshal, sup. ct. and regiser., friendly societies, Trinidad, Sept., 1931; asst. crown solr., 1932.

MILSTEAD, Herbert Ernest George.—B. 1886; temp. asst. acct., P.W.D., Uganda, May-July, 1921; asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1921; visiting just., Nairobi prison, 1922; sec., cent. tender bd., 1923 and 1927; ag. senr. asst. treas., in 1924 and 1927; seconded to secretariat as estab. offr., June, 1925 to Apr., 1926; ag. acct., treas., May to July, 1927; asst. currency offr., July, 1927; ch. storekeeper, P.W.D., Nov., 1929.

MILTON, Sir W. H., K.C.M.G. (1903), K.C.V.O. (1910), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, Apr., 1878; 3rd class clk., 1st Jan., 1879; 2nd class elk., Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., Apr., 1883; sec. to the tender bd., 1881; clk. to exec. coun., 1885; ch. elk. and acctnt., col. sec.'s office, 1891; sec. to prime min., Mar., 1894; detached for serv. in Rhodesia as ch. sec. and sec. serv., Oct., 1897; ag. admr. Rhod., July, 1897, to Nov., 1898; apptd. adminstr. Mashonaland and senr. adminstr. S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1898; adminstr., S. Rhodesia, 20th Dec., 1902; resig., 1914.

MINTER, K. S.—B. 1894; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. elk., and assigned to G.P.O., Sept., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to H.M. stationery office, Aug., 1914; to C.O., Oct., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.
MITCHELL, Edward Thomas.—B. 1900; on milly. ser., May, 1918 to Jan., 1919; temp. elk., W.O., Feb., 1919 to Aug., 1926; apptd. to C.O. as cler. offr. after compet. exam. (Southborough), Aug., 1926.

MITCHELL, Hon. Sir James, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1917); M.L.A., W. Australia.—B. 1866; entd. ser. of W. Aust. Bank; 16 years man. of Northam branch; entd. parlmt., 1905; hon. min. and mem. of exec. coun., 1906; min. of agric., 1907; min. for lands in addition to agric., 1909, and again in 1916; premier, 1919-24 and since Apr., 1930.

MITCHELL, Philip Euen, C.M.G. (1933), M.C.—Ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; asst. res., Nyasaland, 1912; lieut., K.A.R., 1915; capt., 1917; adjt., 1917-18; men. in despe., A.D.C. and pyte. sec. to ag. govt., Nyasaland, 1918-19; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; asst. sec., native affrs., 1926; prov. comsnr., 1928; sec. for native affrs., 1928; ch. sec., 1934.

MOFFAT, James.—B. 1876; exam. offr., Bloemfontein, 1903; inspr., cust., Pretoria, 1912; ch. inspr., Johannesburg, 1917; asst. investigating offr., N. York, 1923; do., London, 1926; collr., cust., Johannesburg, 1933; do., Cape Town, 1934.

MOIR, John Innes.—B. 1897; ed. Gordon's Coll. and Univ., Aberdeen; M.B., Ch.B., 1921; D.P.H., 1923; surg.-sub. lieut., R.N.V.R., European War; lieut. R.A.M.C., 1921; house surg., Sick Children's Hosp., Aberdeen, 1921; asst. M.O. City Hosp., Aberdeen, 1921-23; M.O. for Falkland Is. Coy., 1923-27; P.M.O., Falkland Is. govt., Feb., 1929; capt., Falkland Is. Def. Force, 1929; major and O.C. from May, 1930; prin. med. offr., B. Hond., 1934.

MOIR, Stedman Esdaile.—Clk., P.O., St. Kitts, Mar., 1904; rev. offr., Nevis, Apr., 1906; clk. to mag., Dist. "C," St. Kitts, 1913; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; on active service, 28th London Regt., 1916-17; cotton impr., St. Kitts, ag. audit elk., May to Sept., 1922; cashier, treasy. dept., St. Kitts, Oct., 1922; ag. treas., Dominica, 1928; warden, Northern dist. and mag., dist. C., 1928.

MOIR, William John, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen), D.P.H. (London), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.)—B. 1893; health offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ditto, Kinta, Nov., 1925; ag. sr. health offr., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1928; ag. health offr., Johore, June, 1929; ag. prin. med. offr., Aug., 1932.

MOLLARD, Percival Malcolm.—B. 1906; ed. Portsmouth and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. theol. tripus, 1928; B.A., 1928; M.A., 1932; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; offr. examnr., Swahili, 1932; edr., govt. vernacular monthly paper, 1932.

MOLLOY, Paul Augustus, A.M.L.C.E., chtd. civ. engnr.—B. 1887; ed. King's Coll Schl., Wimbledon and King's Coll., Lond.; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Madras, 1910-13; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1913; asst. engnr., irrig'n., F.M.S., May, 1915; exec. engnr., antimalarial br., Jan., 1922; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Nov., 1922; ag. sr. exec. engnr., in 1927 and 1931; senr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., S.S. and F.M.S., 1934.

MOLTENO, Hon. Sir James Tennant, Kt. Bach. (1911), B.A., LL.B., M.L.A.—B. 1865; ed. at Diocesan Coll., Randebosch, and Trm. for native aff., Aug., 1896; transf'd to Rdcd. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temp.; entd. Cape parlmt., 1890; speaker of Cape H. of A.; speaker of H. of A., Union of South Africa, 1910.

MONTAGNAC, Louis Ivan de.—B. 1855; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1911; cts. of cts., parish of St. Andrew, Jamaica, Mar., 1921; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. regiser., sup. ct., Oct., 1924; regiser., sup. ct., July, 1926.

MONTAGU, James Drogo.—B. 1878; served Cape Mounted Pol., 1903; Johannesburg Mounted Pol., 1903-8; R.S.A. pol., 1908-9; S. Nigeria pol., 1909-18; local comdt., Cyprus milly., pol., 24th Jan., 1920; ag. comsnr., on various occasions in 1922-29 and 1932-33; ag. dep. ch. commtd., 1916; June-Oct., 1925 and from Nov., 1926 to Feb., 1927; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1926; ag. ch. commdt., pol. and impr., prisons, July-Sept., 1928 and July to Sept., 1930; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf, July-Sept., 1930; comsnr., 1934.
MONTAGUE, Cyril John.—B. 1893; asst. audr., Nigeria, July, 1919; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1932.
MONTANARO GAUCI, Anthony J., L.L.D.—B. 1898; matric. Malta Univ., 1915; L.L.D., 1922; el. for leg. assessor, 1927; dep. speaker, leg. assessor, 1927; speaker, do., 1929; judge, 1930.
MONTGOMERY, H. R.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., May, 1908; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1914; ag. senr. comsnr., Kenya, 1926; senr. comsnr., 2nd grade, 1929; 1st prov. comsnr., 1932.
MOOD, H. L.—Asst. dist. comsnr., K. Afr. Prot., Feb., 1915; res. 2nd grade, N. asseiland, Nov., 1925; res. 1st grade, Mar., 1926; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

MOODY, SYDNEY, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1889; ed. King's Sch., Pontefract, Leeds Univ. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (Hist. Schol.), 2nd cls. hist. finals, 1914; prte., Pub. Schls. Batt., R. Fusiliers, 1914; 2nd lieut., K. York. Regt., France, 1915; lieut., King's Liverpool Regt., Egypt, 1916; lieut., Herefordshire Regt., Palestine, 1917-1918; held various appts. in O.E.T.A. (S.) Palestine, Dec., 1918- June, 1920; dist. offr., Palestine, July, 1920; addt. to Middle East Dept., C.O., Feb., 1923 to Feb., 1924; ass't sec., 1924; ag. ass't ch. sec. and ch. sec. on various occasions, 1925-32; ass't ch. sec., 1934.

MOONESINGHE, NALIN, B.A. (Oxon.).—Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1924; addt., Anuradhapura kach., June, 1924; do., Batticaloa kach., Jan., 1925; do., Badulla kach., June, 1925; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1926; ditto, Puttalam, May, 1927; ditto, Jaffna, Aug., 1929; office asst., Galle kach., Nov., 1930; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1931; pol. mag., Jaffna, July, 1933.

MOOR, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1891; obtained higher standard certif. in Forestry at Imperial Research Inst. and Coll., Dehra-Dun, India, 1914, with medals for forest engineering and sylviculture; probationer, Indian provincial forest service, 1914; passed exams. in Indian land revenue systems, forest law and accounts, and Hindustani by the higher standard, 1915; permanent extra ass't conservator, 1916; transfd. to Trinidad as deputy conserv., forests, Jan., 1918; ass't conserv., forests, Gold Coast, Jan., 1923; prin. forest offr., Apr., 1926.

MOOR, RICHARD, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1898; ed. Ermysted's Schol., Skipton and Hertford Col., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, S.S., July, 1921; ass't. aspt., govt. monops., S'pore., Mar., 1924; offr., ols. V, July, 1924; offr., ols. IV, July, 1928; sec. to res., Pahang, Nov., 1929; ag. solr.-gen., S.S., Sept., 1931 and Apr., 1932; dist. judge, S'pore., Penang and Malacca, Oct., 1932; ag. regist., sup. ot., Penang and offl. assignee in addn., Nov., 1932; offr., ols. III, July, 1933.
MOORE, ERNEST STANLEY.—B. 1890; imp. post office, 1909; war serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919; ass't. surv., posts and teles., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; surv., do., 1926; divnl. surv., do., 1933.

MOORE, HENRY MONCK-MASON, C.M.G. (1930), B.A., Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; addt., col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1910; addt., Colomba kach., May, 1911; addtl. pol. mag., Colomba, Negombo and Avissawela, Aug., 1912; ditto, Colomba, and addtl. mun. mag., Colomba, Nov., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Central Prov., Jan., 1913; office asst. to prin. coll., customs, and extra land surv., July, 1913; 4th ass't. col. sec., Feb., 1914; 4th ass't. col. sec., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, May, 1916; addtl. ass't. col. sec., July, 1917; 3rd ass't col. sec., Dec., 1919; 4th ditto, Jan., 1920; priv. sec. to offr. admag. govt., Mar.-Sept., 1920, in addn.; ass't. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1921; col. ols., Bermuda, Jan., 1922; prin. ass't. sec., Nigeria, Jan., 1924; ag. dep. ch. sec. and ag. ch. sec. on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. ch. sec. to govt., in 1926 and 1927; dep. ch. sec., Aug., 1927; govt.'s dep., Sept. to Oct., 1927; col. sec., Kenya, 1929; ag. gov., in 1930, 1931 and 1933; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Sierra Leone, 1934.

MOORE, HON. SIR NEWTON JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1870; mayor of Bunbury, W. Aust., for some years, M.L.A. for Bunbury, 1904; min. of lands and agric., W. Aust., Aug., 1906; premier Aug., 1906; resign, Sept., 1910; major comdg. 18th Austn. Light Horse; agent-gen. for W. Australia, 1911-1918; mem. of comtee. for stintm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; on mil. ser. as G.O.C., Austn. Imperial Forces in U.K., 1915-17; M.P. (U.), St. George's, Hanover Sq., 1918; M.P. (Co.U.), N. Islington, from 1918.

MOORE, WILFRED HENRY, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; addt., Kalutara kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Kandy kach., July, 1926; office asst., N'Eliya kach., Jan., 1927; office asst., Ratnapura kach., May, 1928; do., Kandy kach., May, 1931; ass't. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Dec., 1931.

MOORE, WM. BROWNLOW ASHE, L.R.C.P.I., L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (Rotundas), 1912, D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), 1927.—House surg. to Meath hosp. and co. Dublin infirmary, Dublin, 1903; ass't M.O.H., sanitary dept., Hong Kong, 1906; med. offr., med. dept., 1906; surgeon-lieut., H.K.V.R., 1915; temp. capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-19; sp. plague offr., Mesopotamian Exped. Force, 1918; med. offr. in charge, civil hosp. and Asylums, H. Kong, 1920; med. offr. in charge, Kowloon and New Territories, 1922; med. offr. in charge, Victoria hosp. for women and children, 1925; dep. dir., med. and sanry. serv., 1928; ag. dir., 1928, 1929 and 1931.

MOORHOUSE-WEDDON, W. C.—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich and Trin. Coll., Dublin; cadet, B.N. Borneo, 1905; ass't. dist. offr., 1906; offr.-in-charge Tenom-Tawau expedn., 1906; dist. offr., 1908; ag. P.M.G. and supt. of telegraphs, 1917; ag. offr.-in-charge, West Coast residency, 1917; examr. in Malay, 1917; on special service in Ceylon and F.M.S., 1918; postmr.-gen. and supt. of telegraphs, B.N. Borneo, 1918; ag. res., interior, B.N. Borneo, 1920; ag. food contr., 1920; res., Kudat, 1921; sessions, 1922; ag. res., Sandakan and mem., leg. coun., 1922; res., East Coast, 1923.

MOORMAN, ALEXANDER WILLIAM GEORGE.—B. 1887; nine yrs. Met. pol., New Scotland Yard; served in Italy, Corfu, and Salonika, 1914-18; supt. pol. in ch., C.I.D., Nigeria, 1919-21; ass't. comanr., pol. in ch., C.I.D., Nigeria, 1921-24; ass't. dir., C.I.D., Gold Coast, June, 1924; ag. dir., C.I.D., 1925 and 1930; title changed to comanr., pol., C.I.D., June, 1928.
MORETON, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1905; ed. Northwich Grammar Schol. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, Feb., 1928.

MORGAN, HYWEL GLYN.—B. 1899; ed. Univ. Coll. (Wales); B.A.; LL.B. (Wales); called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1924; practised as barrister in London and on S. Wales circuit, 1924-26; lieut., the Welch Regt., Regular Army R. of O.; war serv. with Machine Gun Corps, India; crown coun., Gold Coast, Oct., 1926; ass't. atty.-gen., Nyassaland Prot., Mar., 1932; ag. atty.-gen. in 1932, 1933 and 1934; ag. judge, Oct.-Nov., 1933.

MORGAN, W., B.A. (1908).—Ed. Merchant Taylors Schol., London (Exhibn.), and Jesus Coll., Oxford; Pitt Clud exhibn. in classics, 1904-1908; Meyrick classical exhibn. at Jesus Coll., 1904-1908; 2nd cls. class. hon. mods., 1906; 3rd ols. Lit. Hum., 1908; ass't. res., N. Nigeria, 1910; station mag., Sept., 1918; res., July, 1926; ag. sec., native affrs., Sept., 1928; ag. sec., N. Provs., Apr., 1930; senr. res., Aug., 1932.
MORICE, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1891; ed. Ampleforth Coll., York; Br. P.O., Jan., 1912; war serv., 1914-19; lieut., R.E., Sept., 1919;

MORRISON, CHARLES RHODWAY, I.S. (1925).—B. 1873; ed. Wesleyan (Boys) H.I. Schl., Freetown; 2nd clk., dist. ct., Karru Sierra Leone, 1897; clk., Karene Exp. Farm, 1898 (W.A. med. and clas); 1st clk., 1st postal agt., 1902-06; ch. clk., 1908; finan. 1911; passed higher stand., Mende and Tenema and lower stand., Susu, 1912; office ast., 1912; offr. in charge, Sumbuya dist., 1921; dep. ast. mast., circuit ct. on several occasions, 1915-16.

MORT, ERIC LAIDLEY, O.B.E. (1935).—B.A.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl., and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. aspt., edn. dept., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1916, and 1917-18; sup. educn., Nigeria, Jan., 1920.

MORTEN, FREDERICK JOSEPH—B. 1888; ed. Lancing Coll., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1911; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1912; ag. dist. mag. and ast. dist. judge, Singapore, Mar., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, Jan., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Sept., 1915; attached to col. sec., Jan., 1916; ag. ast. to res., Malacca, and agent to for contrir., in addition, Jan., 1920; ast. treas. and collr. of stamps, Malacca, in addn., June, 1920; ag. sheriff and registr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1924; ag. 1st ast., sec. B, and clk. of courts, Nov., 1924; ag. under sec., S.S., Feb., 1925; 1st ast., sec. B, and clk. of couns., Feb., 1925; ag. 1st ast., sec. A, Dec., 1928; ag. under sec., S.S., Oct., 1929 and Dec., 1930; ag. dist. ocr., P.W., Dec., 1929; cls. II, Dec., 1929; ag. educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. IA, Apr., 1932.
MORTIMER, PETER.—B. 1875; cls. V. Col. civ. serv., June, 1929; extra office ast., Jaffna kach., June, 1929.

MORTON, JOHN, M.R.C.V.S. (1927)—B. 1901; ed. Keil Schl. and Glasgow Vet. Sch.; Fraser research schol. at Rowett Research Inst., Aberdeen, 1927-28; Scottish bd. of agr. research schol., 1928-29; vetry. offr., Falkland Is., 1929; lieut., Falkland Is. Defence Force, 1929; vetry. offr., Nigeria, 1933.

MOSELEY, FRANCIS ARNOLD.—B. 1853; ed. Perth, W. Australia and Brasenose Coll., Oxon.; B.A. 1904; called to bar, Middle Temp., May, 1905; judge's associate and clk. of arraigns, W. Aust., Oct., 1905; mily. clerk, Australia, 1915-16; France, 1916-19; Ireland, 1920-22; M.C., 1919; ast. registr., titles, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. registr., titles, 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; ag. solr.-gen. for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1927-32; ast. land offr., Mar., 1926; crown coun., 1926; ast. gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1934; ag. judge, Nov., 1933 to Dec., 1934.
MOSELEY, HENRY DOYLE.—B. 1884; jnr. clk., sup. ct., W. Aust., Oct., 1910; judges assoc. and clk. of arraigns, Aug., 1910.

MOUBRAY, GEORGE ALEXANDER DE CHAZAL DE.—B. 1888; ed. Loretto Schl., Manchester Univ. (civ. engnr.), Zurich Polytechnicum and Univ. (pure science); cadet, F.M.S., 1917; offr. cls. V, Jan., 1916; do., cls. IV, Dec., 1917; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., Aug., 1918; regis. titles and town planning admstr., Sect. 1 in addn., July, 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1929; 1st mag., K. Lumpur, Aug., 1930; offr. cls. I.B. dist. offr., L. Persia, Oct., 1933; do., Kinta, Feb., 1934.

MOULDER, HUGH ARTHUR WILLIAM.—B. 1879; ent. Fiji civ. serv., 1910; acctnt. and clk. Gilbert and Ellico Is., Dec., 1915; seconded to serv. in Fiji, Apr., 1921; ag. audtr., Aug., 1921 to Apr., 1922; ag. treas., Apr., 1924; ag. res. commnr., Sept. to Oct., 1926; treas. and collr., cust., 1925; col. treas., Br. Honduras, Feb., 1928; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; dir. pub. offrs. guarantee fund.

MOUNTAIN, HABOLD GRIFFITHS, A. Inst., M.M.—B. 1887; ed. at King's Schol., Worcester, and Schol. of Mines, Camborne (1st cls. dip.); served R.E., tunnelling coys. and engg. stores dept., 1916-19; inspr., mines, Cyprus, Oct., 1925; ag. water engrnr., July-Oct., 1930; inspr., lab. and regisr., trade unions, Jan., 1933; sec. for mines, Gold Coast, 1934.
MUHOMORE, ALFRED.—B. 1893; Br. postal serv., 1912-1920; acct., posts and tels., Tanganyika territory, 1920; asst. treas., 1923; senr. asst. treas., 1931.

MUDIE, NORMAN DAVID, M.A. (Edin.), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple—B. 1884; cadet S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1907; passed cadet, Jan., 1911; offr., cls. V., June, 1911; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Malacca in addition, Aug., 1917; offr. assignee of debtor's estates for Malacca, Oct., Dec., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1918; supernymy, offr., cls. III., Nov., 1919; stamp and probate offr., Singapore, Apr., 1922; regisr. and sheriff, sup. ct., Penang, 1924; dep. pub. pros. in addn. to dep. regisr. and asst. offr. assignee, Penang, Dec., 1924; dist. judge, Malacca, May, 1925; offr., cls. IB., Oct., 1927; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Nov., 1928; judge, F.M.S., June, 1932; puisne judge, sup. ct., S.S., Feb., 1932.

MULHALL, JOHN ARCHIBALD.—B. 1899; on mily. serv., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. Kalutara kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Puttalam kach., Nov., 1922; extra office asst., Badulla kach., Oct., 1923; office asst., ditto, Jan., 1924; office assst., Galle kach., June, 1925; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., May, 1927; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1928; asst. col. sec., May, 1928; sec. retrenchment commn., Apr., 1932; asst. ch. sec., Oct., 1933.

MULLIN, JOHN, F.S.I.—B. 1878; ed. England and Wales; assayer, surveyor and mining engr.; mem., instit. of mining and metallurgy, Eng.; cert. sworn land surv., B. Guiana, 1906; 3rd cls. offr. and surv., B. Guiana, 1908; warden, mining dist. and J.P., 1910; 1st cls. clk., dept. of lands and mines, 1911; ag. chief clk., 1911-12, 1913-14; 1st lieut., B. Guiana local forces, 1915; capt., 1918; seconded to govt. secretariat as conf. clk., 1916; chmn., canals polder, 1917-25; ch. clk., lands and mines dep., 1918; ch. clk. and ag. asst. D.P.W., 1919-20; asst. commn., lands and mines, 1922; mem. and sec., bd. of colonisation and development, 1926; ag. commn., lands and mines, chmn., local gov't. bd. and mem., town council on various occasions; commn., land and mines, 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1929.

MULOCK, Rt. Hon. Sir WILLIAM, P.C. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1902), K.C., LL.D., M.A.—B. 1843; graduated at Toronto Univ., 1863; gold medal in modern languages; called to the Ontario bar, 1868; vice-chanc. of Toronto Univ. in 1881; sat in parlmt. 1882 to 1905, representing N. York; postmr.-gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; ch. just., exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1905; ch. just., Ontario, 1923.
MUNRO, THOMAS AMBROSE VESPER.—B. 1883; habntr., Bahamas, 1st Sept., 1913; ag. marshal, admv. side, sup. ct., 1919.
MURDOCH, JAMES WILSON, M.B., Ch.B. (Aberdeen).—B. 1900; 2nd asst. med. supt., ment. hosp., T. Rambutan, F.M.S., Jan., 1928; med. supt., do., July, 1931.

MURISON, Sir JAMES WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1919), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1872; ed. privately and at Trin. Hall, Camb.; senr. class. scholr., 1891; 1st div., 2nd cls. class. tripso., 1894; 3rd cls. law tripso., Part II., 1895; Latham English prize, 1895; B.A., LL.B., 1895; called to Bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1896; devilled and practised at the Chancery Bar, 1896-1902; regisr. E.A.P., Oct., 1902; town mag., Mombasa, Jan., 1904; 2nd asst. judge, H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Apr., 1904; sen. asst. judge, Mar., 1905; ch. judge, Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1914; mem., of ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1905-1910, and from 1914 upon transfer of Zanzibar to C.O.; judge, sup. ct. of H.H. Sultan of Zanzibar from 1908; arbitrator in dispute between Zanzibar and E.A.P. govt., as to H.H. mainland properties, 1911; recd. thanks of S. of S. for conversion of applied Indian Acts into Zanzibar Decrees, 1917; judge prize offr. Zanzibar from 1914; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, 1903, and in Swahili, 1904; examr. in Swahili to the Zanzibar Gov't.; joint edr., Zanzibar Law Repts., 1869-1918; atty.-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1919; ag. col. sec., June, 1920 to May, 1921; ch. just., S. Sttlmts., July, 1928; ret., 1933; commn. to Palestine to enquire into Jewish-Arab riots, 1934; edited the Revised Laws of the S. Sttlmts., 1926; author of "Swahili Translations," 1911.
MURPHY, Hon. DENIS, B.A.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; B.A. 1892; called to the bar, 1896; elec. to provincial legis. British Columbia, 1900; judge of sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1909.

MURPHY, John Howard Blackwood.—B. 1895; Schol. of Physic, Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1913; R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915; India Office, Apr., 1919; A.D.C., Jubaland, Dec., 1919; dist. commn., Gosha, 1920; ditto, Choresh, 1922; ditto, Kitui, Ukambani, 1923; resigned, private affairs, 1926; re-appld., commn., Somaliland, 1927; admn. offr., Kenya, 1930.
MURPHY, Joseph.—B. 1892; ed. Stokane Schol., Sligo; R.I.C., 1913-22; Jamaica constab., Apr., 1922; sub-inspr., Apr., 1926; 3rd cls. inspr., Apr., 1931; King's pol. med., 1926.

MURPHY, Hon. Thomas Grow, P.C. (Can.).—B. 1883; ed. at pub. and high schools of Ont.; grad. of Man. Coll. Pharmacy; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, g.e., Oct., 1925; re-elec., g.e., 1930; min. of interior in Bennett admnstr., Aug. 1st, 1930; re-elec. by accl. after assuming office, Aug., 1930.
MURPHY, Timothy.—B. 1882; joined Hong Kong pol., Dec., 1903; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 1912; inspr., Aug., 1922; King's pol. med., 1924 C.D.I., Nov., 1926; asst. supt., pol., July, 1931.

MURPHY, William Lindsay.—B.A., Trin. Coll., Dublin; B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1910; attd., Kandy kach., Dec., 1910; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1911; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1912; office asst. to govt., Prov. of Uva, May, 1913; asst. land settmtn. offr., July, 1913; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1915; addnl. pol. mag. and addnl. mun. mag., Colombo, Apr., 1915; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Oct., 1915; ditto, Hambantota, Nov., 1917; ditto, Matara, Oct., 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Galle, Jan., 1921; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, May, 1921; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Sept., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1923; temp. employed, C.O., 1926; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1927; 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Feb., 1927; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., May, 1928; comptlr., lab., Sept.,

MURRAY, ARTHUR EVELYN FRANCIS, B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll.; asst. dist. commnr., S. Nigeria, 1912; cls. I, grade I, admstve. serv., 1928; sec. to commn. of inquiry into disturbances in S.E. Provs., 1930; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1930.
MURRAY, HON. SIR GEORGE JOHN ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1917), B.A., LL.M.—B. 1863; judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1912; chief just., Jan., 1916 and lieut.-gov., Apr., 1916.

MURRAY, JOHN.—B. 1871; ed. Tokomairiro dist. high schl. and Otago boys' high schl.; joined N.Z. stamp duties dept., 1890; asst. commnr., stamp duties and asst. regtr., companies, Dunedin, 1909; ditto, Christchurch, 1920; dep. commnr., stamp duties, 1923; commnr., 1932.

MURRAY, SIR J. H. P., K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—M.A., Oxon.; ed. at Sydney gram. schl. and won Knox prize, Fairfax prize, senr. univ. prize, and John West medal; went to Brighton Coll., Germany, and Univ. Coll., London; entd. Oxford Univ. and gained foundation scholarship to Magdalen Coll., 1880; 1st cls. class. mods., 1882; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1884; M.A., 1885; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1886; practised as a barrister in Australia until apptd. crown prosecutor, N.S. Wales; acted as dist. ct. judge on several occasions; commanded N.S.W. Irish Rifles, 1898; served in S. African war, 1900-1901; recd. commn. of major in Impl. army in recognition of his services, 1902; chief judicial offr. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Papua, Sept., 1904; ag. administrator, 8th Apr., 1907; and lieut.-gov., in addition to duties of judge, 1908.

MURRAY, MENZIES.—B. 1890; ed. Stellenbosch and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (hons. Stellenbosch), 1909; B.A., LL.B. (T.C.D.), 1914; LL.B. (S.A.), 1920; war serv., 1914-19; capt., R.W. Fus., M.C.; advoc., 1921; ch. clk., patent office, Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1930; regtr., patents and regtr., companies, June 1932.
MURRAY, ROBERT EVERITT KING.—B. 1902; olk., grade II., Basutoland, 1924; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1930.

MURRAY, WILLIAM ANSLEY, R.A., M.R., Ch.B. (Edinburgh), D.P.H. (Cape Town).—B. 1874; attd. to Dutch Reformed Ch. Mission in Brit. Cent. Africa Prot., 1901-12; co-operated with sleeping sickness commn. of the Royal Sooy., 1911-12; capt., S. Africans med. corps, and was associated with Major Park-Ross in conducting the Sleeping Sickness Investigation Bureau, 1916-18; govt. pathologist, Durban, 1919; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, 1923; mem., statistical coun., Union of S. Africa; health research offr., Carnegie commn. of investigtn., poor white problem in S.A., 1929-31; senr. asst. health offr., Union, 1932.

MURRAY-JARDINE, ROBERT WILLIAM GORDON.—Ed. Marlborough Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; asst. dist. commnr., Uganda, 1912; govt. commdr., 4th Batt. K.A.R., 1917-18; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1919; dist. mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ret. without taking up appt., 1920; dist. mag., Entebbe, Uganda, 1921; senr. mag., 1930.

MURTLAND, CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1884; ed. Harrogate, York and Dublin; sub.-inspr. of police, B. Guiana, 1908; dist.-inspr., 1912; ag. county inspr., 1910-1911, 1914-1916; capt., B. Guiana militia, 1915; county inspr., 1920; detective inspr., 1927; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., Sept., 1928 to May, 1929 and Apr.-Nov., 1930.

MUSK, HAROLD.—B. 1899; ed. Gainsboro' and Knutsford; mily. serv., R.N.V.R., 1917-19; Kev training, 1924; Agr. Coll., Wye, Kent, 1925; Impl. Coll., Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1926; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.
MYCOCK, CHARLES.—B. 1890; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Jan., 1915; asst. mast., Wantsi Eng. Schi., 1930; headmast., Yaumati, 1934.

MYERS, HON. SIR MICHAEL, K.C.M.G. (1930), K.C. (1922), LL.B. (1897).—B. 1873; ed. Thorndon schl., Wellington Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); in legal practice many years; ch. just., N.Z., 1929.

NAIRAC, GEORGE EDOUARD, K.C.—B. 1876; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, 1898; asst. addnl. subst. procureur and advoc.-gen., 1903-5; mem. for Port Louis in coun. of govt., 1911-25; nominated mem., 1926; K.C., 1926; procureur and advoc.-gen., July, 1927; mem., exec. coll. and coun. of govt.; ag. col. sec., July, 1928 to Mar., 1929; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1929 to Oct., 1931; edr., Mauritius Law Repts. since 1906 and of the Law Repts. Digest, 1902-25.

NAISH, RICHARD BRYANT.—B. 1891; R.A. (Oxon.), M.R. San. I.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Galle knck., Dec., 1914; addnl. pol. mag., Galle in addn., Apr., 1915; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1916; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1918; asst. contr. of estate supplies, May, 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Feb., 1920; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, Sept., 1920; asst. finan. asst. to chmn. mun. coun., Colombo, Jan., 1921; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Ratmalana, Mar., 1923; ditto, Karunegala, Sept., 1923; asst. contr., rev., Mar., 1928; sec., unit. commn. addn., July, 1928; addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Sept., 1931; dep. contr., lab., Oct., 1931; asst. atty.-gen.'s office, May, 1934.

NANKIVELL, HOWARD.—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hosp.; olk., col. secretariat, Jamaica, 1911; asst., 1912; 2nd ols. air mech., R.F.C., 1916; 2nd hel., R.F.C., 1916; lieut., tech., R.A.F., 1917-19; 2nd ols. clk., col. secretariat, Jamaica, 1917; ditto, 1920; sec., salaries regrading commn., 1922; sec., disturbances commn., 1922; sec. adviser, finan. comte., 1924; sec. civ. serv. comte., 1925; priv. clk., secretariat, 1925; sec., prelim. W. Ind. confce., 1926; asst. for temp. duty, C.O., 1926; sec., salaries commn., Jamaica, 1928; ag. clk., privy commn., 1928; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1929; ag. col. sec., on various occasions, 1929-31; dep. col. sec. Jan., 1930; sec., intercol. commn. on trade relations with Canada, 1930; sec., Trinidad air bd., 1930; ag. gov.'s dep., Jan., 1932 and Aug., 1934.
NAPIER, SIR WALTER JOHN, KT. BACH. (1909), D.C.L. (Oxon.).—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; atty.-gen., S. Stittltns., 1907-1909; mem., W. African lands comte., 1912.

NASH, HERBERT FREDERICK WILLIAM.—R. 1901; entered C.O., 26th Nov., 1917; app't. after compet. exam., asst. clk. and assigned to C.O., 29th Dec., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 17th Sept., 1929.
NASH, WALTER.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll., London; conf. clk. to gov.-gen., Nigeria, Jun., 1913; admstve. offr., 1918; res., 1932.

NATHAN, LT.-COL. SIR MATTHEW, G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1899).—R.N.: entd. R.E., 1880; capt., 1889; maj., 1888; lt.-col., 1907; served in Nile expdn., 1885; Luahai expdn. (medal with clasp), 1889; sec. col. def. comtee., 1895; admnstr. govt. of S. Leone in 1899; gov., Gold Coast, Oct., 1900; gov., Hong Kong, 21st Sept., 1903; gov., Natal, 1st May, 1907; sec. to G.P.O., London, 1910; chmn. of bd. of inland revenue, 1911; under sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1914; permanent sec. to ministry of pensions, 1916; gov., Queensland, 1920-25.
NATTELE, HAROLD RUNDLE FITZ.—B. 1875; med. offr., Basutoland, 1904; prin. med. offr., 1928.

NATTRASS, ROLAND MARSHALL, B.Sc. (Agr.), Lond., Ph.D., Lond., D.I.C., Dip. Agr., Wya.—B. 1895; ed. Nottingham High Sch.; astt. to cotton specialist, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; advisory mycologist, Western Prov.; Bristol Univ., Long Ashton Research Station, 1924; senr. mycologist, miny. of agr., Egypt, 1928; mycologist, Cyprus, 1931.

NATTRISS, ERNEST ALFRED, O.B.E. (1934).—B. 1882; ent. C.A. shipping agents, 1898; apptd. to O.A. office, 1912; dep. head, shipping dept., 1922; apptd. shipping offr. to the "Discovery Committee," 1927; head, shipping dept., May, 1933.

NAVARATNAM, SARAVANAMUTHU SAMUEL, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1894; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Matara Kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Ratnapura Kach., May, 1922; extra offr. ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1923; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1924; ditto, Chilaw, Feb., 1926; astt. comntr., excise, Aug., 1927; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1930; astt. attlm. offr., July, 1931; sec. to min. of lab., industry and commerce, Oct., 1931; dep. contr. lab., Aug., 1933; astt. land comntr., Dec., 1933.

NEALE, HAROLD BERNARD.—Second clk. to astt. comntr., Southern div., Bech. Prot., June, 1909; 2nd clk. to astt. comntr., Northern Prot.; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1911; J.P., 1912; ag. astt. res. mag., 1913; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; granted local rank of capt.; res. mag., 1921; ag. govt. sec., 1933; govt. sec., 1934.

NEAVE, JOHN RICHARD, M.C.—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; attd., res. office, Malacca, Nov., 1919; astt. advr., Kota Tinggi, Feb., 1925; res., Labuan, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1930; ag. comntr., lands, Trengganu, July, 1931; 2nd astt. sec. (A), S.S., Dec., 1932; ag. astt. treas. and collr., land rev., Malacca, Aug., 1933.
NEILL, J. H.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ditto, Uganda, July, 1920.

NEIL, WILLIAM HARDLEY EDWIN, F.R.G.S.—B. 1892; ed. Cathedral Sch., Winchester, Taunton's, Southampton and Hartley Univ. Coll., Extn., Southampton; war ser., Sept., 1914-Sept., 1919; survey probtnr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; astt. supt., trig. surveys, Dec., 1923; ditto, topo. br., Dec., 1925; ditto, rev. surveys, Pahang, Jan., 1931; astt. supt., surveys, N. Kedah, Aug., 1932.

NEILL, JAMES SCOTT, B.A., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1889; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; clas. schol., 1911; 1st sen. mod. hist. and pol. sci., 1912; Victoria prizeman, Kings Inns., Dublin; cadet, Fiji, 1914; clk., leg. and exec. coun., Oct., 1915; dist. comntr., 1916; regtr. sup. ct., and offr. recr., 1922; ag. atty. gen. and mem. exec. and leg. coun., July, 1922 to July, 1923; ag. prin. astt. col. sec. and agt. gen., imigrn., Jan., 1924 and Sept., 1924; mem., bd. examrs., Fijian language and ag. editor, Indian newspaper "Radjut"; seconded sp. duty under Tongan govt. and apptd. mem., privy coun. of Tonga, Aug.-Sept., 1924; comntr. of assize, circuit ct., June, 1925; ct. of review, income tax, Nov., 1925; Br. agt. and cons.-gen., Tonga, Jan., 1927; is also a dep. comntr. for W. Pacific and a judl. comntr. for matrimonial cases; seconded for sp. duty, Ocean Is., Dec., 1930 to Mar., 1931.

NEILSON, HARRY ROSS, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Mod. Schol. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., Uganda, 1912; temp. capt., Uganda med. staff, 1914; health offr., 1921; senr. do., 1924; ag. D.D.S.S. for various periods, 1926-32.
NEISH, DONALD GORDON.—B. 1905; ed. Bedford Schol.; 2nd lt., Jamaica local forces, 1928; lt., 1930; machine gun offr., 1931-33; sub-inspr. Jamaica constab., 1933.
NEILL, L.—B. 1880; ed. Blundells Schol.; astt. loco. supt., Nigerian plys., 1914; astt. supt., line, Mar., 1931.

NETHERSOLE, JOHN MAPLETOFT, C.B.E. (1933).—B. 1863; clk. to admstr. gen. and trustee in bankruptcy, Jamaica, 1880; 1st cls. clk., 1893; admstr. gen. and trustee in bankruptcy, Jamaica, 1899; custodian of enemy property, 1915; contr., local clearing office, 1920; mem., income tax assmnt. comtee., 1920; chmn., inc. tax assmnt. comtee., 1922; res. mag., 1933.

NETTLETON, GERALD ENRAGHT.—Clk., res. comntr.'s office, 1914; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1917; sp. J.P., 1917; ag. astt. res. mag., 1920; passed 1st gr. Secowana exam., 1920; passed civ. serv. lower law exam., 1922; ag. res. mag., 1923; res. mag. and hon. iner. Bech. Prot. pol., Apr., 1924.

NETTLETON, SIR SYDNEY CHARLES KING FARLOW, K.T., BACH. (1924).—Ed. Harrow and Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A. class hona., 1887; M.A., 1889; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1889; went S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; puisne judge, high ct., Uganda, 1912; ag. chief just., 1913; judge of ct. of appeal for E. Africa, 1912-14; puisne judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, Feb., 1915; ag. chief just., S. Leone, May to Aug., 1919, and of G. Coast, June-Oct., 1921; senr. puisne judge, G. Coast, Mar., 1920; ch. just., Bahamas, Nov., 1922; pres., reformatory and indus. schls. comntr., 1923; ch. just., Cyprus, Dec., 1924; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1927; ret., 1931; author of "Some Gold Coast Judgments, 1915-17"; "The Masai Case, 1913"; "The Law Affecting a Company Prospectus"; partly, candidate, Sheffield (Attercliffe), 1909-10; S. Hackney, 1910.

NEWBOLT, CAPT. A. F.—B. 1893; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; B.A.; on active serv., Oxfordshire Light Infy., Aug., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Flanders, wounded, Apr., 1916; capt., 1915; adjt., 1918-19; astt. priv. sec. (appts.) to S. of S., Lord Milner, May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. J. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; Lord Passfield, June, 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

NEWBOULT, ALEXANDER THEODORE, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Kingswood and Exeter Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; attd. Perak sect., Dec., 1920; offr., cls. V, Aug., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Aug., 1927; sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Dec., 1927; offr., cls. III, Aug., 1932; ag. comntr., lands and mines, Trengganu, Oct., 1933.

NEWDEGATE, SIR FRANCIS ALEXANDER NEWDIGATE, G.C.M.G. (1925), R.C.M.G. (1917), J.P., D.L.—B. 1862; ed. Eton and R.M.C., Sandhurst; in Coldstream Guards, 1883-86; gov. of Rugby schl.; alderman of the Warwickshire county coun.; M.P. for Nuneaton division of Warwickshire, 1892-1906; M.P. for Tamworth division of Warwickshire, 1909; gov., Tasmania, 30th Mar., 1917; gov., W. Australia, 1920; ret., 1924.

NEWHOUSE, EDWARD, P.A.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Friend's Schl., Lancaster; astt. engr., waterwks., Hong Kong, 1912; exec. engr., drainage, 1920; do., roads, 1927; J.E., 1931; exec. engr., waterwks., 1932; ag. A.D.P.W., 1932; astt. D.P.W., 1932.
NEWILL, COMMOR. JOSEPH BERNARD, D.S.O., R.N. (ret.)—B. 1887; harbor., Malacca, 1922; dep. harbor., Hong Kong, 1926.

NEWMAN, WILLIAM AUGUSTIN, M.B.E.—B. 1873; ed. Sydney High Schl.; ent. New South Wales pub. serv., 1891; served with Australian forces, S. African war (Queen's med., 5 clasps); on staff of first prime min. of C'wealth of Aust., Jan., 1901; senr. elk., dept. external affrs., 1902; oh. elk., C'wealth electoral admstrn., 1903; C'wealth electoral electoral offr. for Victoria, 1906; oh. elk., home affrs., 1909; offr. sec. to gov. gen., C. of A., and sec. to fedrl. exec. coun., 1913; sec., royal comm., on navy and defence admstrn., 1916; ch. elk., Australian defence dept., 1918; admstr., Island of Nauru, 1927.

NEWNHAM, HUBERT ERNEST.—B. 1886; ed. St. John's Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; office astt. to govt. agt., Uva, Nov., 1911; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1913; landing survr., Colombo customs, Oct., 1913; ag. commr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1920; astt. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1920; chmn., munic. coun., Colombo, Aug., 1924; commr., local govt. and pres., local govt. bd., 1930, and sec. to min. for local admstrn., July, 1931.
NEWTON, ALEXANDER STAFFORD, L.D.S. (Liv.), R.A.M.C.—B. 1896; mily. serv., 1914-19; dental surg., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1925.

NEWTON, SIR FRANCIS JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1892), C.V.O. (1911)—B. 1857; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1880; M.A., 1890; barrister, Inner Temple; lieut., 3rd batt. Hampshire regt., and extra A.D.C. to Sir H. Robinson, gov. Cape, 1881; priv. sec. to Sir Thomas Scanlen, prime minister of Cape, 1883; and to Sir H. Robinson, 1884-86; sec. to Mauritius roy. comm., 1886; ag. admtr. and oh. mag., Br. Bechuanaland, 1888; col. sec. and rec-gen., 1889; ag. admtr. and ch. mag., 1892; res. commr., Bechuanaland Protectorate, Dec., 1895-7; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1898; ditto, Barbados, 1901; treas., S. Rhodesia, 1903; ret., 1919; col. sec., 1st S. Rhodesian ministry, 1st Oct., 1923; high commr. for S. Rhodesia in London; ret., 1930.

NEWTON, ISAAC, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Clin. astt., Charing Cross hosp., 1921; 2nd health offr. at port, Hong Kong, 1925; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hosp., 1926; med. offr. in ch., Kowloon hosp., 1927; M.O.-in-ch., govt., civ. hosp., 1930; in ch., Univ. surg. clinic in aldtn., 1932; med. offr. in ch., Victoria hosp., 1932.
NEWTON, T. C., B.A.—B. 1882; ed. St. Peter's Schl., York and Queen's Coll., Oxford; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; res., Dec., 1925; admtr. offr., staff grade, 1935.

NGATA, HON. SIR APIRANA TURUPA, KT. BACH. (1927). M.A., LL.B.—B. 1874; ed. Waimatatini Native Schl., Te Aute Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; mem., house of reps., N.Z., since 1905; mem., exec. coun., repg. Maori race, 1909-12; min., native affrs., min., Cook Is., and mem., exec. coun., representing Maori race, 1928.

NICHOLAS, HARRY THEODORE, M.I. Struct. E.—B. 1882; ed. Bond's Gram. Schl., Plymouth; engr. constrn., waterworks, Singapore, Jan., 1904; astt. engr., P.W.D., Perak, Aug., 1899; exec. engrnr., Jan., 1919; senr. exec. engrnr., Dec., 1926; state engr., Selangor, July, 1927; ag. dep. D.P.W. F.M.S., Jan., 1930-July, 1931.

NICHOLLS, HON. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1927)—B. 1868; LL.B., Tasmania; called to bar, 1892; atty-gen. and min. of educn., Tasmania, 1903-4; puisne judge, Tasmania, Jan., 1909; chief justice, 1914; also lieu.-gov., 1917.

NICHOLLS, LUCUS.—B. 1884; L.S.A. Land., M.D.C., B.A., Cantab.; pathologist to seamen's hosp. soc'y., and bacteriologist to post-graduate schl. of clinical med., 1907-8; stationed in W. Indies, 1909-12, as bacteriologist to govt. of St. Lucia, and surg. to Victoria hosp.; in E. Africa, 1913-15; dir., bact. institute, Ceylon, Oct., 1915; ag. med. supt. lun. asylum, in addition to his own duties, May, 1916 to Feb., 1917.

NICHOLS, JAMES EDWARD.—B. 1882; astt. engrnr., Lagos rly., Feb., 1907; astt. engrnr., Gold Coast rlys. (open lines), May, 1911; offr. in charge, Togoland Mily. rlys., 1916-17; ch. engrnr., G. Coast rlys., Jan., 1920.

NICHOLS, RAYMOND, A.M.Inst.T., F.R.G.S.—B.1894; ed. Harvey Grammar Schl., Folkstone and privately; war serv., E. Kent Yeomanry (R.E.K.M.R.), 2/4th "The Buffs" (East Kent Regt.) and R.F.C., 1914-19; motor instr., P.W.D., (Gold Coast, June, 1920; ag. transport offr., Feb., 1922; transf. motor transport dept., Aug., 1922; dep. ch. mech. transport offr., Nov., 1922; ch. mech. transport offr., 1922 and 1924; ch. transport offr., Oct., 1924.

NICHOLSON, WILLIAM EWART, B.A.—B. 1890; ed., Leeds Grammar Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; instr. arts and crafts, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. insp., S. Provs., 1933; N. Provs., 1934; prim., Higher Coll., Katsina, 1934.
NICOL, ANDREW, A.M.Inst.C.R., A.M.I. Struct.E.—B. 1898; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Robert Gordon's Tech. Coll., Aberdeen; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921.

NICOLL, MAJOR FREDERICK ALAN BENSON, O.B.E., R. of O. (1921).—B. 1883; ed. St. Edward's Schl., Oxford; imp. Yeo S. African War, 1900-02; Natal mtd. pol., 1903-09; Natal native rebellion, 1906; dist. supt., pol., Uganda, 1916; R. Sussex Regt., 1914-20 (France, Belgium and Palestine, army of occupation, Germany, and plebiscite troops, Danzig); ment. in deep.; O.B.E. (mil. div.); Uganda pol., 1924; dep. commr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1924; ag. commr., pol. and prisons, Apr.-Nov., 1930; commr., pol., Oct., 1933.

NIGHTINGALE, L. M.—B. 1875; ed. Wimbledon Collegiate Schl. and Soham Gram. Schl., Camb.; astt. to Mr. W. H. Thomas, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., of Westminster, 1892 to 1901; contractors' engrnr., Bermuda harbour wks., 1901-1906; 3rd cls. offr., lands and mines dept., E. Guiana, 1908; sworn land survr.; dist. engrnr., West Coast, Demerara, Jan., 1915; inspnr., dist., 1926; dist. commr., Esseguibo, 1932.

NIHILL, J. H. B., M.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pts. I and II; pres., Union Soc'y., 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; 2nd lieut., Munster Fusiliers, 1914-18; investigating offr., Min. of Lab., joint industrial couns. divn., 1919; priv. sec. to Sir Wm. Edge, junr., Lord of Treas., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1921; ag. ast. crown solr., 1924; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ag. ast. atty. gen., 1926; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; legal sec. to high commnr., Iraq, 1927-31; ag. Br. cons., Baghdad in admn., 1928 and 1931; mem., Br. deleg. to coun. of L. of N., 1931; sovr.-gen., Uganda, 1934; ag. atty. gen., June, 1934.
NIXON, FRANK ASHLEY.—B. 1879; Transvaal civ. serv., 1902; Union civ. serv., 1910; oh. clk., dept. of mines and industries, 1919; under-sec., dept. of mines and industries, 1924.
NIXON, R., M.B., Ch.B. (Lpool), D.T.M. (Lpool), D.P.H. (Lpool); Tempy, lieut., capt. and major, R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1921; senr. sanitation offr., 1928.

NOAD, JOHN ELICOTT MONCHIEFF.—Ed. R. Naval Coll., Osborne and Dartmouth and Training Cruiser, H.M.S. "Cornwall"; midshipman, R.N., Aug., 1910; H.M.S. "Prince of Wales," Flagship, Atlantic Fleet; resgd., 1911; Edinburgh Univ., Oct., 1911 to Aug., 1914; sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., Aug., 1914 to Nov., 1918; interned in Holland from fall of Antwerp to armistice; while in Holland passed for A.M.I.C.E., Feb., 1917; ast. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, Mar., 1920; ast. engr., Kenya and Uganda rly., Apr., 1924; senr. ast. engr., P.W.D., 1930; exec. engr., 1934; "1914" Star, War and Victory meda.

NOALL, W. E.—B.1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., June, 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Jan., 1911; conf. clk., 1st Mar., 1916; minor staff clk., 8th Sept., 1917; sec. to visual instr. comte., 1st Jan., 1908.

NOBBS, CAPT. HOWARD.—B. 1891; ed. University Coll., and Inst. of Educn., Lond.; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1913 (hons. in chem.); M.Sc. (Lond.), 1919; A.I.C. (1918); F.I.C. (1923); 2nd lieut., Northamptonshire Regt., Apr. 1914; lieut., 1915, capt., 1916; war serv. in France, 1st battn., Northamptonshire Regt. 1914-15 and with R.E., 1916-18; chem. mast., R. Coll., Mauritius, 1928; prin., Queen's Coll. Br. Guiana, 1931.
NOBLE, G. S. P., M.F., Ch.B. (Glas.), Certif. Lond. S.H.T.M. (distinc.).—B. 1897; mil. serv., 1916-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1923.

NOBLE, PETER, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Elgin and Aberdeen, Scotland; ast. civ. engr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1906; ag. dist. engr., in 1907 and 1909; dist. engr., Demerara, B. Guiana, Aug., 1910; ag. in addition as chief draughtsman, 1911, and as ast. col. civ. engr., in 1912; col. engr. and survr.-gen., Dominica, Aug., 1912; nom. mem. Roseau Town Board, offl. mem. legis. coun. and road board; on war service as mem. commrs. of valuation (min. of munitions); recd. thanks of sec. of state for work in connection with roads of Dominica, 1916; dist. engr., N. dist., Palestine, Aug., 1922.

NOOTT, P. GORDON.—B. 1880; entered army (Royal Welsh Fus.), 1898; served in Boer war, 1899-00 (Queen's medal with clasp); Tugela heights, relief of Ladysmith, Cape Colony, Orange Free State and Transvaal; seconded for serv., N. Nigeria regt., Dec., 1904; served with Dakka Kerri expdn., N. Nigeria, 1908; ret. on pension, Oct., 1913; ch. warder, Belize prison, B. Honduras, May, 1914; comen. 2nd lieut., B. Honduras Terr. Force, 1914; lieut., 1916 served on Cayo expdn., B. Honduras, 1916; instructor to 2nd B. Honduras contingent, 1916; capt., 1918; supt. of prisons, Grenada, Jan., 1920.
NORRIS, C. G.—B. 1890; ed. St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; cadet, Solomon Islands, Jan., 1915.
NORRIS, J. A.—B. 1872; ed. Ballygun and Williamstown, Ireland; acct. to treas., Victoria, Mar., 1914; audr.-gen., Mar., 1919.

NORRIS, JOHN WILSON A.C.G.I., A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1890; ed. Lond. Univ. Imp. Coll. Sc. and Technology; active service B.K.F. (France) 1914-19, major R.E., mentioned in despatches; joined C.A. as engr. inspr., 1919; ast. engr., 1920; visited Gold Coast 1924; services loaned to consult. engr. reporting on Takoradi Harbour; seconded for duty as ast. engr. Gold Coast harbours staff, Takoradi 1924; rejoined C.A. 1926; dep. to oh. insp. engr., 1929.

NORTH, ROLAND ARTHUR CHARLES, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. Blundell's and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. with Samoa govt., 1921; studied Japanese in Japan, 1922; 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923; ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1924 and 1930; sec. for Chinese affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1927 and 1931; extra ast. col. sec., Feb., 1931; 2nd ast. col. sec. and dep. clk., couns., June, 1931 and Jan., 1934; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., couns., June, 1933 and Apr. and June, 1934.

NORTHCOTE, GEOFFREY ALEXANDER STAFFORD, C.M.G. (1931).—Ed. at Blundell's Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (honours); and Newte and Huish exhib.; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1904; ag. collr. and ag. dist. comsnr., S. Kavirondo, 1904-06; polit. offr., Kisii Patrol, 1906; polit. offr., sleeping sickness comsn., 1908-09; dist. comsnr., 1909; in charge, Kikuyu Prov., 1911; ag. prov. comsnr., May to Oct., 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Oct., 1919 to July, 1920; ast. col. sec., Apr., 1921; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1921, Aug. to Sept., 1922 and Mar. to Sept., 1923; gov.'s dep., July to Aug., 1923; mem., legis. coun., 1923; ag. col. sec. in 1925 and 1926; ch. sec., N. Rhodesia, Feb., 1927; mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1927; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1930; ag. gov., 1930, 1931 and 1932; gov. and commdr-in-ch., Br. Guiana, 1934.

NORTHCOTE, MAJOR LEONARD AUGUSTUS STAFFORD.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd lieut., Royal Fusiliers, 1900; res. of offrs., Feb., 1914; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Salonika and Palestine, 1914-19; local commdt. of pol. and ast. comsnr., Cyprus, 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek; ag. comsnr., for various periods 1924-27 and 1929-34.

NORTHCOTE, R. C.—Ed. Blundells and S. African Schl. of Mines and Technology, Johannesburg; Rhodesian admtn., B.S.A. pol., 1914-16; Rhodesian Native Regt., 1917-19; German E. Africa campaign (ment. in desp., mily. med.); ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; dist. offr., Feb., 1932.

NORTHCROFT, LESLIE ARTHUR, B.A. (Can- tab).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to secretariat, Jan., 1924; ditto, Puttalam kach., Jan., 1925; ditto, N'Eliva kach., July, 1925; office ast., Jaffna kach., May, 1926; temp. attd., C.O. Sept., 1928 to Mar., 1929; addnl. ast. col. treas., Apr., 1929; ag. ast. gov't agt., Matara, Oct., 1929; ast. sttlmt. offr., Nov., 1929.

NORTHNEY, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD; G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918). C.B.—B. 1868; served with Hazara expdn., 1891; Miranzai expdn., 1891; Isazai expdn., 1892. In S. African war, 1899-02; and in the war of 1914-18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyasaland Rhodesia field force, 1915-18; lieut.-col. (brevet col.), 1915; King's R.R.C., 1911; gov. E. Africa Prot. and high comsnr., Zanzibar Prot., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922.

NORTH-HUNT, HAROLD.—B. 1894; ed. Ipswich Schol. and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge (exhbr.); war serv., 1914-20; (ment. in desps.); cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; 2nd asst. dist. offr., July, 1920; asst. dist. offr., Jan., 1921; 2nd asst. adviser, B. Pahat, Johore, Apr., 1922; offr., cls. IV, Aug., 1925; dist. offr., Lipis, June, 1930; offr., cls. III, Aug., 1930; ag. asst. advr., supt. lands and regiar., titles, Kelantan, Aug., 1933.
