NORTHMORE, HON. SIR JOHN ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1932), LL.B.—B. 1865; judge, sup. ct. W. Australia, 1914; ch. just., 1931; lieut-gov., 1932.

NORTON, CUTHBERT HUGH.—B. 1878; ed. pte. schls. and Weymouth Coll.; ent. govt. ser., Br. Guiana, 1913; asst. comsyr., taxation, 1914; 3rd gde., do., 1917; 2nd gde. do., 1920; 1st gde. do., 1927; seconded as census comsyr., Sept., 1930 to Dec., 1931; 1st gde. offr., dist. adminstr., 1932; ch. comsyr. and dist. comsyr., Georgetown and East Bank, Demerara dist., 1934.

NOETON, HENRY JOHN STEPHEN, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1896; cler. asst., Gibraltar, 1913; crane and wharf clk., 1914; treas. clk., 1914; passed compet. exam., 1917; clk-in-charge, impts. and expts., and clk. to food contr., 1914 to 1920; dep. asst. shipping master, 1921; asst. shipping master, 1922; sec. and treas., sailors' home, 1920; clk-in-charge port dept. and ag. Trinity agt., in 1924-26; asst. sec., col. sec's. office, 1927; pvt. sec. and interpreter to gov., 1929.

NORVAL, WILLEM DIXON.—B. 1893; ed. Wepener, O.F.S.; clk., dept. of lands, 1913; clk., dept. of educn. (Union), 1919; pvt. sec. to min. of interior, educn. and pub. health, 1924; senr. clerk, dept. of agr., 1926; mining comsyr., Namaqualand, 1929; do., Barberton, 1930; do., Barberton and Pilgrims Rest, 1931; under-secr. for lab., 1932.
NORWOOD, LEONARD ROBERT OSWALD.—B. 1896; ed. Lincoln Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 2nd Nov., 1921.

NOSWORTHY, RICHARD, C.M.G. (1926).—Ed. Christ's Hosp.; ent. Jamaica civ. service, 1880; held appts. in various depts.; 1st cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1905; sec., bd. of supervision for poor relief, 1905-09; supervisor of rev. and valuation comsyr., 1907-18; ag. collr. gen. and contr., widow's and orphan's fund; ohmn., marine bd. on several occasions; asst. col. sec., 1918; ag. col. sec., 1921; Island treas. and man., govt. savings bank, 1921; ohmn., agrl. loan bd., ohmn., currency comsyr., man., civ. service guarantee assn.; sent to America to conclude purchase of Jamaica govt. rly. bonds, 1912, and as mem. of banana comsyr., 1915; censor, 1915-19; ag. admstr., Dominica, 1922.

NOSWORTHY, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1867; ed. privately and at Boys High Schl., Christchurch, N.Z.; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1908-28; jun. whip for reform party and later sen. whip; sen. whip for Nat. govt.; min. of agr., 1919-26; min. of immigr., 1920-28; min. of finance and stamp duties, 1925-26; P.M.G. and min. of tels., 1926; min. of external affrs., 1926-28.

NOTT, CHARLES ROBERT HARLEY, M.A.—B. 1904; ed. Marlborough and Christ's Coll., Camb.; cadet, Fiji and W. Pacific services, Mar., 1926; col. sec's office, Fiji, Apr., 1926; passed cadet, Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. couns., Mar., 1928; ag. A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., 1930; ch. clk., col. sec's office, Jan., 1931; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., in 1932 and 1934.

NOWELL, WILLIAM, C.B.E., D.I.C., F.L.S.—B. 1880; ed. munici. tech. schl., Halifax; R. Coll. of Sci., Lond.; on milly. serv., S. Africa, 1900; ast. supr., agr., Barbados, 1911; mycologist and agrl. lecturer, Imp. Dept. of Agr. for W. India, 1913; ast. dir., agr., Trinidad, 1920; mem., agrl. relief comttee.; original mem., bd. of man., govt. agrl. bank; mem., advisory bd., Emp. Cotton Growing Corp., cotton research stn.; dir., sec. and agr., Br. Guiana, 1926; dir., E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1926.
NUNAN, P. F., M.B., Bsc.-Surg., M.D.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1924; do., Kenya, 1925.

NUNN, GEOFFREY NOEL NAPIER.—Ed. Bradford and Magdalen Coll., Camb.; B.A. (thons.); cadet, Gold Coast, July, 1929; ast. dist. comsyr., 1930; seconded, Gambia, Dec., 1931; ag. comsyr., 1932; 2nd lieut., R.A.R.O., July, 1929.

NUNN, MAJOR REGINALD LEWIS, D.S.O., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.W.E.—B.1892; ed. Brighton Coll.; waterwks. engrnr., Trinidad and Tobago, Apr., 1921; res. engrnr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1921; ast. dir., P.W.D., Nigeria, Feb., 1927; dep. col. engrnr., P.W.D., S.S., Aug., 1930; dep. D.P.W.-S.S., Jan., 1932; seconded for aerodrome planning, July-Nov., 1934.

NUTMAN, FREDERICK JOHN, A.R.C.S., M.Sc., D.I.C.—B. 1904; ed. Newton Abbot and R. Coll. of Sci., Lond.; timber mycologist, forest products research lab., Oxford, 1926; plant physiologist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

OAKLEY, PHILIP DOUGLAS, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Lpool), B. B.S.—med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; senr. med. offr., July, 1926; ast. dir., med. serv., Aug., 1928; dep. dir., Feb., 1932; dir., med. and sanry. services, Sierra Leone, 1933.

O'BRIEN, ARTHUR JOHN RUSHTON, C.M.G. (1929), M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H., B.H. (hons.), (Durham), Certif. L.S.T.M.—B.1885; joined W.A.M.S., 1911; served in Great War, 1915-19; awarded M.C. and bar, twice ment. in desp., and recvd. thanks of S. of S. for services in Cameroons; surg. specialist, Gold Coast, 1921; R.M.O., Gold Coast hospital, 1923; sec. to C.A.M.S. comttee., Dec., 1929; visited West Indies at request of S. of S., 1930; assumed duty as sec., C.A.M.S., comttee., June, 1930.

O'BRIEN, BRYAN JUSTIN.—B. 1902; ed. Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hons. mods.; 2nd cls. lit. hum.; ast. sec., col. sec's office, Cyprus, Apr., 1927, ag. clk., exec. couns., for various periods, 1927-31; clk., exec. couns., Aug., 1931; ag. ch. ast., sec. for various periods.

O'BRIEN, LT.-COL. Sir C. R. M., K.C.M.G. (1920); C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1869; ed. Felsted, on the Continent, and Royal Milly. Coll. (passed out with hon.); entrd. Army as 2nd lieut., 1878; lieut., 1880; capt., 1887; major, 1897; brev.-lt.-col., 1902; 2nd in command of 1st E. Lancs. regt., Oct., 1902; served in India, 1880-95; ret'd. on pension in order to remain under C.O., Mar., 1903; served in S. African War, Jan., 1900, to end of campaign; pres. of mil. tribunal, 1900-01; S. African War med. and 3 clasps, King's med. and 2 clasps (twice ment. in desps.); senr. dep. comsnr. Transvaal town pol., J.P. for the Witwatersrand, and in charge of mtd. pol., May, 1901; in charge of martial law arrangements, Johannesburg and Rand, and personal asst. to comsnr., 1901-02; dep. comsnr., headqrs., Transvaal town pol., 1905-08; dep. comnrr., Transvaal town pol.; retrenched, 1908; col. sec., Gambia, Jan., 1910; ag. gov., Gambia, in 1910, 1911 and 1912; gov., Seychelles, Nov., 1912; gov., Barbados, June, 1918; assumed govt., Sept., 1918; ret., Sept., 1926; selected by S. of S. as sp. coman. to rept. on constitution and organisation of Freetown municipality, Sierra Leone, May to July, 1926.

O'CONNOR, ALEXANDER JAMES, Dipl. Forestry (Cape).—Clk., conserv. of forest's office, W. conservancy, 1903; forester, Uitvlugt, W. conservancy, 1905; asst. dist. forest, offr., Tekai, 1908; seconded to gen. coun., Transkei, 1908; asst. dist. forest offr., Knyasa, 1909; ditto, working plans, Midland conservancy, 1910; ditto, Woodbush, Transvaal conservancy, 1910; dist. forest offr., Woodbush, 1918; ditto, Pietersburg, 1924; ag. conserv., forests, Natal conservancy, 1926; 2nd grade conserv. of forests, Natal conservancy, 1927; prin. forest offr. (forestry inspr.). Transvaal and O.F.S., 1932.

O'CONNOR, CHARLES ANTHONY.—B. 1883; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; supt., Reduit gardens and plantations, forests and gardens dept., Mauritius, Sept., 1904; ch. overseer, dept. of agr., Aug., 1913; enlisted, 2nd Artists' Rifles, Aug., 1917; read. coman., Royal Munster Fus., Sept., 1918; inspr. of plantations, Zanzibar Prot., Aug., 1919; agr. instr. and inspr., co-op. credit societies, Mauritius, Jan., 1923.

O'CONNOR, HUGH JOSEPH, F.Z.S.—B. 1887; Lieut., Gambia Artillery (V.), 1910; mem. of navigation and pilotage board, Gambia, 1914; asst. comsnr. of police, G. Coast, Jan., 1916; ag. staff instructor, Mar., 1916, and June, 1918; ag. comsnr. of police for various periods in 1917, 1921 and 1922; O.C. Togoland mil. police, and O.C. Togoland prisons, 19th Aug., 1919; capt., G. Coast Rifles, 1922; comsnr. of police, Jan., 1923.
O'DONOUGHE, LAURENCE.—B. 1895; ed. Newcastle Schl., Co. Tipperary; R.I.C., 1917-22; staff sergt.-major, Jamaica constab., Feb., 1922; sub-inspr., Feb., 1931; 3rd cls. inspr., Nov., 1932.

OFARRELL, EDWARD JOSEPH.—Ed. Belvedere Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; solr., supt. ct., Ireland, 1905; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Kenya, 1924; dep. offr. recr. for Coast Prov. and ex-officio agt. at Mombasa for pub. trustee, Kenya, 1930.
OGG, ROBERT CUTHBERT, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1901; rel. med. offr., Basutoland, 1927; med. offr., 1929.
OGILVIE, J.—B. 1886; ed. Fordyce Acad., Banffshire and Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; war serv., 1914-19; asst. engnr., Nigerian rly., 1919; divnl. engnr., 1928.

O'GRADY, WILLIAM MICHAEL, B.L.—B. 1886; ed. Wesley Coll., Dublin, admitted solr., Ireland, 1908; mil. serv., Egypt, 1916; Salonika, 1916-17; Palestine, 1917-18; Egypt, 1919; called to bar, Ireland, 1921; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. puisane judge, high ct., on various occasions, 1927, 1932 and 1934.

OKE, HARRIS RENDELL, M.C.—B. 1891; ed. Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's, Newfoundland; studt.-at-law, 1909-14; The Royal Newfoundland Regt., 1914-15; The Royal Scots, 1915-20; served France, 1916-18 (M.C. and ment. in desps., 1918); served N. Russian Relief Force, 1919 (bar to M.C.); admstive, offr., Nigeria, 1920; seconded to admstive, serv., the Gambia, 1932; ag. col. sec., May-Aug. and Dec., 1933; ag. gov., Jan.-Apr., 1934; senr. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1934; col. sec., 1935.
OLDFIELD, H. G.—Tempy. asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919.

OLIPHANT, JOHN NINIAN, M.B.E.—B. 1887; ed. Glenalmond Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford, B.A. and dipl., forestry, 1909; Imp. forest service, India, 1909-23; dep. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Dec., 1923; conservator, forests, 1924; dep. dir., forestry, F.M.S. and S.S., 1929.

OLIVER, GEO. A.—B. 1898; ent. G.P.O., Dec., 1914; apptd. asst. clk., Apr., 1916; wireless operator, R.N.V.R., Jan., 1917 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O., June, 1923; accompanied U.K. delegation to Imp. Economic Conf., Ottawa, 1932.
OLIVER, WALTER REGINALD BROOK, D.Sc., F.R. Soc. N.Z.—B. 1883; ed. Tauranga High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. cust. dept., 1900; Dom. museum staff, 1920; dir., 1928.

OLIVIER, 1ST BARON (created 1924), RT. HON. SIR SYDNEY OLIVIER, P.C. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1907), C.M.G. (1898), B.A.—B. 1869; ed. at Tonbridge schl.; open exhibnr., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 2nd class classical mods., 1878; 2nd class in final classical schl., 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for cols., 17th Apr., 1882; honourably mentioned for Cobden prize essay, Oxford, 1883; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890, to Apr., 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to the Earl of Selborne, Sept., 1896; sec. to the Roy. coman. to inquire into condition of W. Indies, Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to Washington on sp. serv. in connect. with W. Indian reciprocity negotiations, 1898; col. sec., Jamaica, 1900-1904; ag. gov., 1900, 1902, and 1904; returned to C.O. as a prin. clk., Oct., 1904; gov. of Jamaica, Apr., 1907; assumed govt., May, 1907; represented West Indian Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; perm. sec., bd. of agriculture and fisheries, 1913; mem. of comtese. for settm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; asst. comptr. and audr., exchequer and audit dept., 1917; S. of S. for India, 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924.

O'MAY, LILY STEWART, M.B., Ch.B. (St. Andrews, 1909).—B. 1883; med. offr., Warren's hosp., K. Kangsar, June, 1913; grade II, May, 1914; med. offr., girls' schls., Penang, P.W. and Dinding, and i/c women and children's clinic, maternity hosp., Penang, Feb., 1926; i/c women and children's disp., Kandang Kerbau, Singapore, Oct., 1929; med. offr., Malacca, June, 1930; seconded for serv. under govt., Kedah, May, 1931.

O'MORCHOE, THE (CAPT. ARTHUR DONALD MACMURROGH).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined Leinster Regt. (Royal Meaths), Aug., 1914; served in the great war; regt. signalling offr., 1915; asst. adjt., 1916-17; A.D.C. to maj. gen. Hon. E. J. Montague Stuart-Wortley and camp commdt., 65th divn., 1917-18; provost marshal's dept., 1918-22; junr. pol. offr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; pub. pros., 1924-25; supt., pol., Feb., 1926; ag. supt., C.I.D., Nov., 1925 to May, 1926; adjt., 1928; ag. asst. commdt., 1930; A.D.C. to Br. res., Aug., 1930; pvtc. sec., May, 1931; commr., pol., Gold Coast, 1934.

ONRAET, RENE HENRY DE SOLMINIHAC.—B. 1887; pol. prob., S. Stiltna, Dec., 1907; passed final exam. in Hokkien, July, 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1911; asst. supt. of pol., July, 1912; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Jan., 1919; asst supt of pol., Singapore, Mar., 1919; supt., pol. and dir. crim. intell., S.S., Mar., 1922; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1925, and June 1931; dir. crim. intell., S.S., Oct., 1926; dep. commr., pol., Perak, F.M.S., Oct., 1931; ag. inspr. gen., pol., S.S., Mar., 1932; ch. pol. offr., Perak, Oct., 1933.
OORLOOF, CEDRIC JAMES, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1930; attd., secretariat, Oct., 1930; attd., Kalutara kach., July, 1931; office asst., Hambantota Kach., Oct., 1932.

O'REILLY, JAMES GALLUS.—B. 1883; ed. Hughes' Acad., Belfast; R.I.C., May, 1902; inspr., pol., F.M.S., Dec., 1910; inspr., wghts. and mars., Aug., 1911-Dec., 1912; supvr., cust., F.M.S., Sept., 1921; supt., cust. prev. br., F.M.S., Oct., 1923; supvr., ext. duties, F.M.S., Prai and S'pore, Nov., 1925 and Jan., 1927; ch. supt., prev. br., cust. dept., F.M.S., Sept., 1927; asst. supt., govt. monom., S'pore, June, 1930; dep. commr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Feb., 1932; dist., Perak, Nov., 1932.

ORMSBY-GORE, RT. HON. WILLIAM GEORGE ARTHUR, P.C. (1927).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., Shropshire Yeomanry; intell. officer Arab bureau, 1916-17; asst. sec., War Cabinet, 1917-18; asst. polit. offr., Palestine, 1918; British mem., Permanent Mandates Com., League of Nations, 1921-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; accompanied O.O. mission to British West Indies, 1921-22; partly, under sec. of state for Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 1924 and since Nov., 1924; chmn., partly, comm. to E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and rept. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926.

ORR, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1928), C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1870; ed. Bath Coll. and Woolwich Acad.; 1st comm., Royal Artillery, Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 1904; ret., Sept., 1908, served with Chitral relief force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian frontier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expedit. force, 1897-99 (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. African war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. res., 1904; 1st cls. res., 1908; ag. sec. to adminstr., N. Nigeria, May to Sept., 1907; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; adminstr., govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to Aug., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill health, Feb., 1917; attd. W.O., Int. branch, 1918-19; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., or several occasions; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 1926-1931; ret.

OSBORNE, MAJOR D. R.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide, S. Australia, Adelaide Univ. (mental and moral phil.) and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., law; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1909; practised at bar until 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; ct. martial offr., Nov., 1918 to May, 1920; in Ireland as A.L.O. until Apr., 1922; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, June, 1923; ag. ch. just., Aug. to Sept., 1923 and from July to Nov., 1924; ag. reg.-gen. in addn. June to July, 1924.

OSTLER, HON. HENRY HUBERT, K.C. (1925), LL.B. (1876); ed. Christ's Hosp. and Victoria Univ.; assoc. to New Zealand ch. just., 1903-06; edr., N.Z. Law Repts., 1906-11; last asst., crown law office and crown pros., 1910; ag. solr.-gen., 1913; in private practice, 1915-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1926; examr. in law for N.Z. Univ. for many years; fellow N.Z. Univ. senate, 1916-20.

O'SULLIVAN, TIMOTHY ALOPHUS, M.A., M.R.S.T.—B. 1894; ed. King's Hosp. and Trinity coll., Dublin; asst. mast., col. for blind, Worcester, St. Christopher's, Eastbourne, Ranelagh etc., Athlone, 1913-15; Inns Ct. O.T.C. and R.A., 1915-19; asst. mast., Raffles inst., Singapore, 1919; inspr., schls., Pahang, Feb., 1922; senr. educn. offr. (A.), S.S., 1931; inspr., schls., Malacca, Nov., 1924; do., Selangor, Nov., 1924; senr. educn. offr. (A.), S.S., 1931; inspr., schls., Penang, Nov., 1931.

OSWELL, HENRY THEODORE WARREN.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; war serv., 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., April, 1920; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., class V., Apr., 1923; asst. dist. offr., T. Malim, Feb., 1925; asst. contrlr., lab., Seremban, Aug., 1925; offr., class IV., Apr., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Nov., 1929; prot., lab., Kedah, Mar., 1930; offr., class III., Apr., 1932.

OTWAY, J. H.—Clk., treasy., Grenada, 1887; 2nd audit clk., 1899; audit clk., St. Vincent, 1901; audit clk., St. Lucia, Jan., 1916; sub coll. and dist. offr., Soufriere; J.P. for the cable censor, St. Vincent, 1918; ch. clk., post office, and clk. of couns., St. Vincent, 1918; ag. ch. clk. and pvtc. sec. to gov., Grenada, 1925; col. treas., contr., cust. and ch. of excise, St. Vincent, 1923; ag. admtr. in 1932 and 1933.

OWEN, ALBERT EDWARD.—Aust. acct. N. Rhodesia, Oct., 1915; attd., land dept., Oct., 1915 to May, 1922; treasy., May, 1922; ch. clk., dept., agr., Jan., 1927; treasy., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. treas., in 1928, 1929 and 1931; contr., stores and transport, Dec., 1932.

OWEN, A. H., B.A. (Camb.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Camb.), Cert. of S.T.M. (Lond.)—Med. offr., Uganda, Oct., 1912; sp. serv. (venereal diseases), Uganda, 1912; active serv., E.A. Campaign, and temp. capt., U.M.S., Jan., 1915 to May, 1918; med. in desps.; senr. med. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1921; senr. san. offr., Tanganika Territory, 1922; dist. dir., san. serv., 1928; dir., med. and san. services, 1932.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1887; ed. Highgate Schl. (Eng.); entd. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd clk., col. sec., office, Aug., 1915; asst. sec., local defence com., 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; sec. to educn. comm., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec. West Indian Court of Appeal Com., 1916; priv. sec. to Sir S.W. Knaggs in 1910, 1912 and 1915; to Sir G. R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J. R. Chancellor, 1916, 1920 and 1921; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; ch. and conf. clk., col. secretariat, and clk. of legis. coun., May, 1920; priv. sec. to Sir T. A. V. Best, 1921 and to Sir S. Wilson, 1922-24; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec. for several periods since 1921; ast. col. sec., clk. to exec. coun., combined et. and ot. of policy, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1925-30; col. sec., Barbados, Sept. 1931; ag. gov. in 1932 and 1933.

OWEN, GEORGE HUMPHREY RICHARD ST. JOHN.—Cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; 2nd grade admstive. offr., July, 1922; seconded to C.O. as ast. prin., Feb. to Aug., 1929; dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1931; ag. ast. admstr.-gen., 1933.

OWEN, HAROLD ALBERT.—B. 1892; entd. crown atty.'s office, Dec., 1911; seconded as 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, July, 1916; sec. to comm. of enquiry into condition of St. John's fire brigade, Nov., 1916; returned to crown atty.'s office, Sept., 1919.

OWEN, JOHN HENRY, D.S.C. (1917).—Younger brother, Trinity House, London; master mariner; ed. Univ. Coll. Schl., London and H.M.S. "Worcester"; served in war, 1914-18; lieut., naval reserve; port mine-sweeping offr., Jamaica, Oct., 1918; harbmr. and naval agt., Kingston, Jamaica, Jan., 1919; harbmr., Malacca, 1927.
OWEN, WILFRED HERBERT, B.Sc.Tech., A.R.I.B.A., Chart. Arch.—B. 1896; ed. St. John's, Cheetham, Manchester, Manchester Grammar Schl. and Manchester Univ.; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925.

PAGE, RT. HON. EARLE CHRISTMAS GRAFTON, P.C., M.B., Ch. M. (Syd.).—B. 1880; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ. (George Allen Maths. Scholarship and John Harris Scholarship for anatomy and physiology); served in European War with A.I.F. as surg. specialist to No. 3 Australian casualty clearing station, France, and No. 3 Australian gen. hosp., Egypt; mayor of S. Grafton, N.S.W., 1918; pres., N. Coast Development League, 1918; el. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Cowper, Dec., 1919; treas., C. of A., 1923-29; leader of country party in Fedl. parl., 1930.

PAGE, CAPT. JOHN CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); 2nd lieut., Buffs, Aug., 1914; on active serv. in France, 1916-19; M.C., Oct., 1916; passed progress exam. in Mendi; ast. dist. commr., S. Leone, Aug., 1916; ag. dist. commr., Moyamba dist., 1922; dist. commr., May, 1928.

PAGE, SIDNEY MAYNARD.—Transvaal constab., Oct., 1900; clk. mgtg. off., July, 1902; ast. mgtg., Mar., 1913; mgtg., Mar., 1918; pub. serv. inspr., Nov., 1924; addnl. mag., Pretoria, May, 1928; mag., Pieterburg, Sept., 1928; ditto, Durban, Feb., 1930; do., Johannesburg, Mar., 1933.

PAILTHORPE, WULFRED ARTHUR BOYTON.—B. 1875; ed. King Edward VI Schl., Chelmsford, and Grav's Coll., Essex; serv., S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's med. and 6 clasps, King's med. and 2 clasps); registrn. clk., E.A.P., 1904; regir. of docs., 1910; collr. under Stamp Act, 1910; ex-officio agt. for admstr.-gen., 1911; ag. prin. regist. of docs., crown lands, etc., 1915-19; ch. controlling rev. authority, 1918; dep. regist.-gen. of titles, Kenya, 1919; prin. regist. of docs., crown lands and land titles, 1920.

PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ast. mast., St. George's Schl., Windsor Castle, 1913; ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., 6th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926; res. mag., 1929.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser.; apptd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O., 8th Mar., 1920; second ast. acctnt., 5th Nov., 1928.

PALMER, SIR HERBERT RICHMOND, K.C.M.G. (1933), C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ed. Oundle Schl. and Trinity Hall, Camb. (class. schol.), M.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1904; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; commr., native rev., N. Nigeria, 1911; rev. mission to Anglo-Egyptian Sudan, 1912; supervisor, native rev., Nigeria, 1914; ag. res., Kano Prov., Nigeria, 1915-16; res., Bornu Prov., Nigeria, 1917; visited Anglo-Egyptian Sudan from Bornu via Wadai and Darfur in connection with educ. proposals, 1918; ag. lieut.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May-Dec., 1921; ag. res., Sokoto Prov., Apr. to May, 1922; lieut.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1925; gov., Gambia, 1930; gov. and commr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 1933.

PALMOUR, FREDERICK JOHN CLIFFORD, P.A.S.I.—B. 1884; ed. Daventry Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxon.; survr., Can. Pac. Rly., 1911; valuer, valn. dept., I.R., 1913; R.E., ord. surv. office, Southampton, 1918; land agent, lands branch, W.O., 1920; ex. offr., land dept., Nigeria, 1928; land offr. and survr., Gambia, 1934.
PAMELLONE, ARMAND JOSEPH, M.B., Ch.B. Edin.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 1st Feb., 1906; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1913.

PANES, JOHN NELSON.—B. 1888; ed. Dean Close Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (M.A. hons.); mast., King Edward VI's Schl., Norwich, 1914-22; ast. mast., King's Coll., Lagos, Nigeria, 1922; supt., educn., S.P., Nigeria, 1926; ag. ch. instr., educn., 1930-31; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, 1931.

PANET, LIEUT.-COL. CHARLES LOUIS.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; ent. civ. serv., Canada, 1889; sec., Canadian defence comte., 1898; priv. sec. to late Sir F. W. Borden, min. of militia and defence, 1904-12; sec., dept. of militias and defence from 1907; pres., bd. of enquiry, re claims of applicants for the fenian raid volunteer bounty, 1912-15; pres. of pensions and claims bd.; ment. in dep. for serv. rendered in Can., 1914-1918.
PAPAFIO, M.K.Q.—Ed. Accra Grammar Schl. and in England; 6th grade teacher, 1911; 5th grade do., 1912; 2nd els. do., 1919; 1st cls. do., 1920; headmistress, 1921.

PAPATEIROU, ANTONI, M.B.E. (1937).—B. 1878; clk., land registn. and survey dept., Cyprus, Oct., 1903; instr., land registn. and survey dept., Apr., 1919; ast. regir.-gen., Mar., 1927; title altered to ast. dir., land registn. and surveys, July, 1927; ag. dir., land registn. and surveys for various periods, 1928-34.

PARHAM, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, F.R.I.B.A., F.S.I., Chartered Archt. and Quantity Surv., mem., Town Planning Inst.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll., London; temp. architectural ast., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1915; 1st ast. archt., 1918; ag. ch. archt. in 1919, 1923, 1925, 1927 and 1929; ast., town planning divn., Miny. of Health, 1926; ast. commr., income tax, Ceylon, 1934.

PARK, ALEXANDER DALLAS, C.M.G. (1932), F.I.A.N.Z., A.R.A.N.Z.—B. 1882; ed. Waitaki Pub. Schl., Waimate High Schl. and privately; N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1900-18; agr. dept., 1919; inspr., pub. serv. commr's. office, 1920; sec. of marine, 1923; asst. pub. serv. commr., 1923; asst. sec., treas., 1925; sec. 1930 also financial adviser, 1931; served with N.Z. forces in S. Africa.
PARK, CAPT. EDWARD NIGEL, O.B.E. (1933), M.C.—B. 1889; ed. Aldenham Sch.; Somaliland Camel Corps, 1917-20; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

PARKE, JOSEPH.—B. 1888; R.I.C., July, 1908-May, 1913; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. asst. commr., pol., Nov., 1923; asst. commr., detect. br., Perak, Nov., 1924; dir. crim. intell. br., K. Lumpur, Nov., 1927; do., F.M.S., June, 1932; dep. commr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1932.

PARKE, CAPT. L. S.—Comsn., 7th Batt., Hampshire Regt., 1909; Coldstream Guards, 1911; on war ser., Uganda and India, 1913-18; selected by W.O. for serv. with Yemen Infy., Aden, 1919-20; cadet, Zanzibar pol., 1923-25; asst. finan. advr. and comdt., pol., govt. of Bahrain, Persian Gulf, 1926-32 (twice acted as advr. to govt.); 1st grade inspr., Mauritius pol., 1933.

PARKER, CECIL JOSEPH, M.C., M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; asst. engnr., Peru, 1910; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1911; lieut., R.E. (Field Co.), 1915-19; served in France and Italy; ment. in desps., 1917; M.C., 1918; war and victory meda.; ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1920; sen. ex. engnr., Apr., 1927; state engnr., Perak, Jan., 1930; ag. dep. D.P.W., S.S., Mar.-Sept., 1933.

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Is.; dist. postmr., Bethesda, 1901; schlmr., govt. boys' reformatory schl., 1903; elk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and elk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of weights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

PARKER, LANGLEY—B. 1879; elk., cust., Johannesburg, 1902; survr., Cape Town, 1915; served R.F.A. in Great War; cust. investigation offr. for Union in New York, 1926; collr. cust. and shipping mast., Port Elizabeth, 1930; do., Durban, 1933.
PARKHILL, ROBERT ARCHDALE—El. to H. of R., C'wealth of Australia, 1927; min. for home affrs. and transport, Lyons' ministry, 1932.

PARKINSON, SIR ARTHUR CHARLES COMO, K.O.M.G. (1935), C.M.G. (1931), O.B.E., M.A. (1910).—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; class. demy., Magdalen, 1903-7; 1st cls. moda., 1905; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after compet. exam., 1st cls. elk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transf. as 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec., man. comm. of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comm. apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Mr. Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916; 1st cls. elk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th May, 1915 to 26th May, 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1925; order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. III, 1929; asst. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

PARMELEE, JAMES GRANNIS, O.B.E.—B.1875; inspr., iron and steel bounties, Sydney, Nova Scotia; trade and commerce, Canada, 1902; elk., dept. T. and C., Apr., 1911; on mily. serv., 1914-19 (twice ment. in desps.); head elk., Apr., 1919; elk. ch., Jan., 1921; asst. dep. min., T. and C., Apr., 1923; on sp. duty to U.K., 1928; dep. min., trade and commerce, Nov., 1931.

PARNALL, JOHN.—Solr.; asst. aud., E. Africa Prot., 1915; asst. aud. in charge, Zanzibar, Nov., 1915; ag. treas., Zanzibar, Sept. 1919 to July, 1920; regiar. and offi. assignee, H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar; regiar. of patents, designs, and trade marks, Feb., 1920; ag. admstr.-gen. and registr. of docs., Apr. to Oct., 1922; asst. admstr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1923; ag. admstr.-gen., Oct., 1923 to Jan., 1924; admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, registr. of docs. and land offr., Zanzibar, May, 1924; ag. atty.-gen., June-Dec., 1929; offi. assignee, registr. patents, designs and trade marks, Jan., 1934.

PARR, HON. SIR CHRISTOPHER JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1869; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; barrister-at-law; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1914-29; min. of educn., 1920-26; min. of health, 1920-25; min. of just., 1923-26; P.M.G. and min. of tele., 1925-26; high commr. for N.Z. in England, 1926-29 and 1934; M.L.C. and leader of leg. coun., 1931-33.
PARRY, J. H. B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—Tempy. comm. in Indian med. serv., Mar., 1915 to Mar. 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 'Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., 1928.
PARRY, REGINALD FRANCIS SCARLETT.—El., St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. inspr., S. Prov., 1934.
PARSONS, HON. HERBERT ANGAS, LL.B.—Judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1921.

PARTIDGE, GEORGE JAMES, B.A. (1915), M.A. (1921), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1927).—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hospital and Cambridge; open exhib. (maths.), and Essex County Scholar, Queens' Coll., 1912-14; 2nd cls. maths. tripods, part I., 1913, B.A. (Wals.), 1915; temp. comm., 1914-18; on active serv. in France and German E. Africa; 1914-15 Star; ment. in desp.; asst. polit. offr., 1st grade, provisional admin., German E. Africa, Dec. 1918; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Aug. to Dec., 1919; asst. sec., Oct., 1919; ag. senr. asst. sec., on various occasions, 1921-23; elk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1922 to Sept., 1923; senr. asst. sec., Aug., 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., in 1927, 1928 and 1929; asst. ch. sec., Apr., 1930; ag. dep. ch. sec. for various periods, 1930-33; 1st asst. ch. sec., 1934.
PASCHALIS, NEOPTOLMOS, K.C. (1931).—B. 1880; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1922; solr.-gen., Cyprus, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen. for various periods, 1927-34.

PASKIN, JESSE JOHN, B.A., M.C.—B. 1897; ed., King Edwards Schl., Stourbridge and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., maths. tripods, pt. I., 1913; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Worcestershire Regt., T.F. and Machine Gun Corps); Croix de Guerre, 1918; transf. to T.F. res. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, junr. higher divn. comm. of transport, Jan., 1920; asst. prin., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921; asst. pte. sec. to S. of S., 10th Dec., 1928; prin., 4th Sept., 1929.

PASSFIELD, 1ST BARON (Cr. 1929) Rt. Hon. SIDNEY JAMES WEBB, P.C., 1924, LL.B. (Lond.), Hon. Doc., Polt. Economy (Munich), etc.—B. 1859; ed. London and abroad; ent. W.O. by open compet., 1878; survr. of taxes, 1879; C.O., cls. I by open compet., 1881; resigned, 1881; sometime hon. lect. on polit. economy at City of London Coll. and Working Men's Coll. and subsequently hon. prof., public adminstr., London School of Economics, 1912-27; M.P.

PEARCE, PHILIP GEORGE.—B. 1888; ed. Mt. Albert Sch. and Coromandel dist. High Sch., N.Z.; joined N.Z. valuation dept., 1904; justice dept., 1919; lands and survey dept., 1921; mines dept., 1921; inspr., pub. serv. comsnr.'s office, 1926; under-seo., native dept. and native trustee, 1933.

PEARSON, COLIN BATEMAN, M.A. Cantab.—B. 1889; capt., R.A.R. of O., Durham L.I.; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; European mag., Tonga, Sept., 1928; ag. land st. judge, 1930; pol. mag., Gold Coast, Oct., 1932; ag. circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, May to Sept., 1934.

PEARSON, JAMES RAE, C.I.E.—B. 1871; scho., St. Paul's Schl. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; ent. I. C. S., 1892; held various Indian appts., 1892-1926; ret., I. C. S., Aug., 1926; sec. for Indian affrs., Fiji, 1927.
PEASE, J. W. K.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1926.

PEDLER, FREDERICK JOHNSON, B.A.—B. 1908; ed. Watford Grammar Schl. and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; major schol., 1927-30; Goldsmith's exhib., 1929; cls. I. divn. 1, hist. tripes, pts. I. and II; B.A., 1930; Schuldham plate prizewinner; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930; seconded to prov. admstrn., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1934.

PEDLOW, JOSEPH HOWARD, B.A. (N.U.I.). Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple).—B. 1889; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway; cadet, S.S. Dec., 1912; Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1913; at Amoy, to study Hokkien, Sep., 1913; Chinese Prot., S'pore, Mar., 1916; treasy. for custodian enemy property and war loan work, May, 1916; collr. of war tax, S'pore, in addition, Mar. to June, 1918; pol. mag., S'pore, Aug., 1919; supermyn. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, Sept., 1920; asst. to atty.-gen., S'pore, Dec., 1920; offr. asst. prot. of Chinese, S'pore, June, 1921; pol. mag., Penang, Oct., 1921; mem., rent assessment bd., Penang, and munic. comsnr., George Town, Nov., 1921 and Oct., 1924; cls. III., Nov., 1924; prot. Chinese, Penang, Dec., 1924; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; dep. pres., munic. comsnrs., George Town, Penang, (cls. IB.) in addn., Oct., 1926; registr., sup. ct., in addn., Mar., 1927; mem., Mohammedan and Hindu endowments bd., Penang, Oct., 1928; offr., cls. II., Dec., 1929; stamp and probate offr., S'pore, Dec., 1930; ag. solr. gen., S.S., 1931-34; dist. judge, S'pore, 1933; offr., cls. I.B., Dec., 1933; solr. gen., S.S., Nov., 1934.
PEDRAZA, R.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEL, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1931), K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1926), B.A., 1896, M.A., 1931—B. 1875; ed. Queens' Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. tripes, 1896; cadet, S. Sttltns., Oct., 1897; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ditto, Tampin, F.M.S. June, 1908; seconded as pres., mun. comsnrs., Penang, Jan., 1911; offr., Class III., but remained seconded, Jan., 1912; ag. res. councillor, Penang, in addn., Feb. to Oct., 1917; pres., mun. comsnrs., Singapore, July, 1918; offr., Class II., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; joint passage contrlr., Malaya, in addn., Feb., 1919; seconded as food contrlr., Malaya and chmn., shipping cte., May, 1919; chmn., European unemployment comte in addn., Feb., 1921; contr., lab., F.M.S. and S. Sttltns., Feb., 1922; Hr. adviser, Kedah, Oct., 1922; res. coun., Penang, July, 1925; ch. sec., F.M.S., June, 1926; offr. administering govt., S.S. and k. comsnr., Malay States, May to June, 1927; sp. duty, London, Mar., 1928; gov. and commr-in-ch., Hong Kong, Mar., 1930; Kt. of Grace Order of St. John of Jerusalem; hon. fellow Queens Coll., Camb., 1932.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT.—B. 1882; clk., ad. office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th clk., P.O., 1907; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd clk., treasy. dept., 1911; ch. clk., P.O., 1913; ch. clk. and ass. examg. of customs, 1914; compt. of cust., Apr., 1914; ag. revr-gen., treas. and temp. mem., exec. coun., on various occasions, 1925-28; ag. postm., Sept., 1928; has held numerous other appts.; postm., Sept., 1928.

PEGG, HUGH HANDLEY.—B. 1889; ed. Ironville Schl., Derbyshire and ptly.; asst. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; asst. engnr., Jan., 1921; mem., Kowloon communicat. comte., 1924; dep. head, rds. office, Jan., 1924; exec. engr., Dec., 1932.
PELLISSIER, S. H., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Grey Univ. Coll.; vice prin., Boeshof, 1924; prin., Ficksburg, 1917; dir., educn., O.F.S., Dec., 1926.
PELLY, RUSSELL STRELE.—B. 1910; ed. Mercer's Schl. and Edin. and Oxford Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry Edn.), 1931; asst. conservator (forests), Br. Hond., Oct., 1932.
PEMBLETON, EDGAR STANLEY, B.A. (Oxon)—B. 1888; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1911; res., 1928; admvte. offr., staff grade, 1935.

PENGILLEY, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp. (exhib.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); lieut., 23rd Batt., Royal Fusiliers, 1917-19; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1921; passed fin. July, 1922; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Feb., 1924; temp. attd. for crit. in C.O. as asst. prin., Nov., 1925-May, 1927; asst. collr., land rev. K. Lumpur and dep. recvr. titles, Selangor, June, 1926; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1928; dist. offr., P. Puteh, Kelantan, Aug., 1928; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1929; ag. ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, June, 1931; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1933.
PENNINGTON, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Newbury Gram. Schl. and Kt. Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1925.

PENTZ, HERBERT FREDERICK, M.C.—B. 1886; clk., col. soc.'s office, Cape, 1906; c.e., Cape admtrn., 1910; ch. clk., ditto, 1928; serv. inspr., 1930; served in S. African int. brg. in Great War (overseas); under sec. for pct. wks., Aug., 1931; prov. sec., Transvaal, Apr., 1934.

PEPPYS, WALTER EVELYN.—B. 1888; ed. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1916; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. V., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Jelebu, Apr., 1912; do., Pasir Puteh, June, 1915 and Jan., 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., Jan., 1918; offr., class III., Nov., 1920; fed. sect. sp. duty, Aug., 1921; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1925; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1925; offr. in 18th com-nr., lands and mines, Johore, Jan., 1926; offr., cls. IA, under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1930; asst., govt. monopolies, S.S., Mar., 1932 and comsnr., trade and cust., F.M.S. in addn., Apr., 1932; mem., S.S. trade comsnr., in addn., Feb., 1933; comsnr., cust. and excise, S.S. and F.M.S., 1934.

PERCY, H. VAN (formerly Baumgartner)—B. 1883; ed. Bedford Schl.; clk., land mts. dept., O.R.C., Oct., 1902; clk., and typist audit dept., Dec., 1902; clk., audit dept., July, 1904; examr. of accts., audit dept., Jan., 1908; jnr. astt. treas., G. Coast, Jan., 1914; astt. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. ch. astt. treas., Nov., 1919; ag. dep. treas. on several occasions, 1921-28; dep. treas., 1929; ag. treas., in 1930, 1932 and 1934.

PERDUE, CECIL GRAHAM.—B. 1891; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; police probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; passed in Chinese, 1915; attached to Punjab police, 1916-16; passed in Urdu, 1916; temp. 2nd lieut., Indian army reserve, 1916-19; J.P.; astt. aspt., pol., 1920; astt. census offr., 1921; dir., criminal intelligence, 1930; aspt., pol., 1930.

PERERA, BENJAMIN FRANKLIN.—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; addt., Colombo kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., Apr., 1926; office astt. Puttalam kach., Oct., 1927; pol. mag., Kalutara, Apr., 1928; office astt., Kandy kach., Nov., 1928; addn. pol. mag., Colombo, Aug., 1930; addn. astt. govt. agt., Matara, Aug., 1930.

PERERA, CONRAD BONIFACE PETER, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; addt., Badulla kach., Mar., 1927; addt. Anuradhapura kach., Oct., 1927; office astt., Puttalam kach., Apr., 1928; ditto, Ratnapura kach., May, 1930; addn. astt. govt. ast., Colombo, June, 1933; addn. pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1933; astt. stimt. offr., May, 1934.
PERERA, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1885; ols. V., Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1930; extra office astt., Ratnapura kach., Nov., 1930; office astt., Kalutara kach., Jan., 1934.
PERERA, HENRY SAMUEL, M.A. (Lond.), Barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn.—B. 1885; divn. inspr., schlr., Ceylon, Dec., 1922; prin., training coll., Sept., 1932.

PERERA, THEODORE DUNCAN, LL.B. (Lond.).—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attchd. to Kurunegala kach., Aug., 1919; Jaffna kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Avisawella, July, 1921; ag. pol. mag., W. Prov., Feb., 1923; astt. comsrr. of stamps, July, 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Oct., 1926; astt. comsrr. stamps, Nov., 1926; astt. col. sec., Jan., 1929; astt. to income tax adviser, Sept., 1929; sec. to min. of agr. and lands, July, 1931; astt. comsrr., income tax, Feb., 1932.

PEREZ, JOSEPH LEON MATHEU, LL.B.—B. 1896; ed. St. George's Coll., Weybridge, Surrey; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; dep. regisr.-gen., Trinidad, Oct., 1927; ag. regisr. gen., regisr., sup. ct. and marshal in 1928 and 1929; ag. solr.-gen., July to Nov., 1929; ag. astt. mag., St. George West, Nov., 1929 to May, 1930; mag. St. George West, June, 1930; do., Victoria, 1932; astt. law offr., 1934.

PERIES, HERMAN ERIC, B.A. (Cantab), B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1906; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1929; addt. secretariat, July, 1929; addt., Puttalam kach., Jan., 1930; ag. office astt., Kegalla kach., Jan., 1931; addt., rubber contr. office, July, 1934.

PERKINS, LEICESTER GRANT.—B. 1890; ed. privately and Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk., post office, Barbados, Jan., 1909; 5th clk., audit office, Mar., 1911; 2nd clk., registn. office, Nov., 1913; clk. to master in chancery, May, 1914; 4th clk., savings bank, Jan., 1915; 2nd-lieut., Imperial Army, Mar., 1917, served during war in Egypt and Palestine with 2nd B.W.I. Regt.; lat.-lieut., Oct., 1918; 1st clk., Barbados savings bank, Aug., 1919; 2nd grade super. of cus., Nigeria, Feb., 1920.

PERLEY, Rt. Hon. Sir GEORGE HALSAY, P.C. (1931), G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1857; ed. Ottawa gram. schl., St. Paul's schl., Concord, N.H., and Harvard Univ.; B.A., 1878; elec. to H. of C., Canada (Argenteuil), 1904; re-elec., 1908 and 1911; chief conservative whip, 1911; mem. of privy coun. for Canada and min. without portfolio in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ag. high comsrr. in London, 1914; min. of overseas mil. forces, 1916; high comsrr., 1917; joint comsrr. and plenipotentiary of Canada to sign Peace Treaties, 1919; Canadian del. to League of Nations Assembly, 1921; resigned high comsrrship, 1922; re-ele. to H. of C., 1925; sec. of state in Meighen Cabinet, 1926; re-ele., g.e., 1926; min. without portfolio in Bennett admnstr., 1930.

PERRY, SIR ALLAN, KT. BACH. (1904); M.D. (Durham); D.P.H.(Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S.A. Lond.—B.1860; prin. civ. med.offr.and inspr.-gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Br. med. assoc.; del. for Ceylon to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909; ret., 1915.

PERRY, B. C. G.—B.1886; apptd.after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 9th Apr., 1906; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; regir., C.O., 7th Sept., 1931.

PERRY, GEORGE McREDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb. to Mar., 1894; ag. astt. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to expedn. to Idanai mountains; sen. astt. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.
PERSSE, CAPT. EDMUND MATURIN, M.C. (1918), F.R.G.S., R. of O., W. Yorks. Regt.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Mod. Schl.; astt. dist. comsrr., Uganda, Oct., 1919; dist. comsrr., Apr., 1925.

PETERKIN, EDMUND MURRAY.—B. 1886; agri. astt., dept. agr., Barbados, 1909; agri. instr., dept. sci. and agr., Br. Guiana, 1913; hort. astt., bot. gdns., 1917; clk. and sec., bd. agr., 1918; ag. astt. bot. and mycologist, 1922-23; agri. surv., 1928; investigated rice conditions, Br. Honduras, 1933.
PETERS, BERNARD R., M.B.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ast. engr., E.A.P., 1914-15; exec. engr., Tanganyika, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1926 and 1927; D.P.W., Nyasaland, Aug., 1928.

PETRIDES, PHILIP BERTIE.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Brussels; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1906; joined Midland Circ. and Birmingham and cent. crim. court sess., 1906; legal adviser and crown prosecutor, Seychelles, Jan., 1916; ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. chief just., July, 1916 to Mar., 1917, May, 1918 to Oct., 1918, and from Dec., 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. empld., C.O., Feb. to Apr., 1920; ch. just., Seychelles, June, 1920; admstd. gov't., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1924; ag. judge, May to Dec., 1925; puisne judge, Nigeria, Sept., 1926; ch. just., Mauritius, May, 1930.

PETRIE, GEORGE MELVIN.—Joined Bech. Prot. pol., June, 1910; treasv. clk., 1922; acct., 1924; ag. finan. sec., master and regisr., deeds, 1922-23 and 1926; ag. finan. sec., mast. and regisr., deeds, 1927; acctnt., Basutoland, Nov., 1927.
PETTMAN, P. A. H.—B. 1896; acct., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1925.

PHEAR, HAROLD WILLIAM, M.M.—B. 1891; ed., Chord Sch.; war serv., 1914-19; prob. inspr., pol., Singapore, Dec., 1919; det. inspr., pol., Aug., 1921; asst. supvr., cust., F.M.S. and ag. supvr., ext. duties, Singapore, Aug., 1923; supt., cust., prev. br., Selangor, Nov., 1923; do., N. Sembilan, Apr., 1924; dep. commr., trade and cust., N. Sembilan, Jan., 1931; head, prev. ser., Singapore, Feb., 1932; J.P., Singapore, Mar., 1932.

PHILIPS, JAMES ERASMUS TRACY, Capt., The Rifle Brigade (Reg. Army R. of O.), M.C. (1916), Ordre de la Couronne of Belgium (1921), M.A., R. Litt., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1888; ed., Marlborough Coll., Oxford and Durham Univ.; (pres., Union and pres., U.B.C.); lieut. 1912; asst. dist. commr. and addnl. dist. mag., Uganda, 1912, asst. intell. offr., E. Africa Expedy. Force, from Aug., 1914, att. K.A.R.; commanding 2,000 armed levies and scouts from Sept., 1914 (wounded and ment. in desps.); admin. Kifumbiro (Bukoba) area of occupied territory, Sept., 1914, under martial law; polit. offr. on staff of Major-Gen. J. M. Stewart, C.B., Bukoba Expedy. Forces, June, 1915 (ment. in desps.); ch. polit. offr., Uganda area and N.W. Provs., Tanganyika during oper., Jan., 1916; ch. intell. offr. (G.S.O.3), E.A.E.F., to Tabora, from Aug., 1916; M.C., 1916; in charge of polit. mission in Br. and Belgian areas of conquered territory, Nov., 1916; empl. intell. duties, W.O., June to Aug., 1917; attd. Admy. War Staff, intell. div., Aug. to Oct., 1917; Arab bureau, G.H.Q., Cairo, 1917-18; employed by F.O. to Abyssinia, 1918; on sp. duty in Sudan, 1918; raised two companies Somalis and Abyssinians for K.A.R. in French and Abyssinian territory, Apr., 1918; ag. dist. commr., Kigezi, Br. Ruanda, and dist. mag., Kabale, Feb., 1919; expedn. on foot across Africa from E. to W. via Lake Kivu through Belgian, French and Portuguese territories; Br. relief commr., S. Russia, under the L. of N., 1922; mem., Br. comttee. for Moslem refugees, Turkey, 1922; "Times" war corresp. in Greco-Turkish war, 1922; liaison offr. with French troops and with Greek forces of occupation in Turkey, 1923; mem. Greco-Bulgarian frontier enquiry, 1923; seconded to Sudan govt. as asst. dir. of intell. Khartoum, 1924; commr., Tembura dist., French frontier, 1925; dist. commr., Kigezi, 1928 (Ruanda); publications, "Pan-Islam in Africa," "Cape to Cairo developments," "Ruanda" (1919); "The Nabing" (1919); "A Kinya-Ruanda Vocabulary" (1920); "Ethiopianism" (1921); "The Turkish Emigration from Thrace" (1922); "The Religion of the Niam-Niam (Zande) People" (1926); "U.S.S.R. or Russia Revisited" (1933).
PHILLIPS, C. H., L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.).—Tempy. lieut., R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915 (General List); med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—B. 1877; elk. to auditor, B. Honduras, Oct., 1892; 5th elk., treas., customs and P.O. depts., June, 1893; 4th elk., Feb., 1894; 3rd elk., treas. and cust. dept., May, 1897; 2nd elk., June, 1898; 2nd elk., C.S.O., Sept., 1900; ch. elk. and elk. of couns., Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to gov. Sir E. Swayne, 1909-10; to gov. Collett, 1911-13; to ag. gov., Walter, 1916; ag. commr. of currency, Feb., 1911; ag. treas., collr. of cust., 1911-14 and 1917-20; ag. col. sec., 1920; Br. Honduras del. to Canada- W. Indies Confec., Ottawa, June, 1920; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1921; commr. and judge, Turks and Caicos Is., June, 1923.

PHILLIPS, TREVOR STANLEY, B.A.—Ed., Llandovery Coll., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept. N. Nigeria, Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; prtn., higher coll., Kataina, Dec., 1931.

PHILLIPSON, SYDNEY, M.A. (Manchester).—R. 1892; on mily. serv., May, 1915; cadet Ceylon civil serv., Nov., 1919; attchd. to Kandy kach., Dec., 1919; pol. mag., Matare, Sept., 1922; office asst., Kurunegala kach., July, 1924; asst. contr. rev., custodian, enemy property and contr., local clearing office, in addn., Jan., 1925; sec., salaries comttee., Nov., 1926; sec., land comn. in addn., July, 1927 to March, 1928; asst. sec., sp. comm. on constitution in addn., Nov., 1927 to Jan., 1928; addnl. asst. col. treas., Nov., 1929 and June, 1930; contr., estab., July, 1931; contr., supplies and finance, July, 1932; contr., estab., Feb., 1934.
PHILP, DAVID.—B. 1893; asst. acct., F.M.S. plys., Dec., 1919; ag. constr. acct., Mar., 1921; ag. dep. acct., Oct., 1930 and Apr., 1932; ag. asst. sec. to gen. man., June, 1931.
PICKERING, SIR GEORGE HUNTLEY, Kt. Bach. (1932).—Town mag., B. A. P., 1910; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1915; puisne ju-gt., 1917; ag. ch. just., Apr. to Nov., 1927; ch. just., Zanzibar, 1928; ret., 1932.

PICKWORTH, CECIL HENRY.—B. 1890; war serv., in France, 1914-19; attd. experimental dept. of tanks, miny. of munitions, 1917-19; res. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1921; res. engr., Singapore municipality, 1924; elec. engr.-in-c., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1927.
PIDDUCK, H. B.—Ed. Harper Adams coll., Univ. of Wales; Shropshire Yeomanry, 1914-19; science mast., Dominica Grammar Sch., 1922; headmast., 1923; agr. offr., 1929.

PIENAAR, ANDRIES GUSTAF ERLAND.—Ch. of ct., Lichtenburg, July, 1902; ag. pub. prov. outor., Feb., 1903; pub. prosecutor, June, 1916; ch. elk. and ag. asst. res. mag., Nov., 1916; asstl. asst. res. mag., Krugersdorp, Sept., 1916; res. mag. and native commr., Waterberg, Nov., 1919; res. mag., Ermelo, Apr., 1912; senr. mag., Ge- miston, May, 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Sept., 1921; mag., Louis Trichardt dept. of just., Aug., 1924; sec. for int., Dec., 1924; chmn., pub. serv. comm., Aug., 1926; chmn., Union tender and supp. bd., June, 1934.

PIENAAR, MAJOR FILIPPUS FOURIE, D.T.D., O.B.E.—Civ. serv., S. African Republic; dep. engnr., miner's phthisis bd., Witwatersrand; accredited rep. of Union of S. Africa to League of Nations; cons. gen. for Union of S. Africa, Laurens Marques, 1934.

PIERIS, PAULUS EDWARD, C.M.G. (1933), M.A., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1895, L.L.M., D.Litt. (Cantab.)—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1896; asst. to govt. agt., Colombo, 1898; pol. mag., Panadura, 1899; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1899; asst. to govt. agt., Galle, 1901; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Mar., 1903; office asst. to govt. agt., Galle, June, 1903; asst. commr., St. Louis exhib., Feb., 1904; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1905; ditte, Kalutara, Mar., 1906; ag. regar.-gen., Dec., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, May, 1913; commr. of requests, Colombo, Nov., 1912; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Nov., 1913; dist. judge, Galle, June, 1914; ditte, Jaffna, Dec., 1915; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Mar., 1919; ditto, Kandy, Apr., 1920; dist. judge, Kandy, July, 1921; pub. trustee, Oct., 1928.
PIERS, FRANCIS A.—B. 1882; sub-instr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1910; instr., 1923.

PIERS, HAROLD HENRY ROSS.—Cler. ass't, Peddie, Cape Prov., 1895; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1895; R.M. clk., Peddie, 1896; on active serv., served Boer War, 1899-1900; senr. clk., comsnr., Kimberley, 1902; first clk., Calvinia, 1903; Albany, 1907; asst. mag., Albany, 1907; 3rd grade mag., Keiskama Hoek, 1914; ditto, Hopefield, 1919; 2nd grade mag., Molteno, 1920; 1st grade mag., Greytown, 1927; senr. grade mag., Windhoek, S.W.A., June, 1929; sp. "B" mag., E. London, Mar., 1933.

PIGGOTT, Sir FRANCIS TAYLOR, K.T. BACH. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitration, 1893-4; proc. gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, Apr., 1905; ret., 1912; read, thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Naturalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PILBEAM, S. A.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Jan., 1915; passed asst. clk.'s exam., July, 1916; on military service from 18th July, 1917, to 10th Mar., 1919; assigned to C.O. as asst. clk., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

PILLING, HENRY GUY, C.M.G. (1932).—B. 1886; ed. King's Schl., Ely, and Keble Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1911; cadet, Fiji, 1907; passed cadet, 1910; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., 1914; mem., Indian Franchise Coman., 1920; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. sec., W. Pacific high coman., Mar., 1924; sec., W. Pacific high coman., 1926; asst. to high coman., W. Pacific, 1929; col. sec., Br. Honduras, Dec., 1929; ag. gov. in 1930 and 1931; dep. col. sec., Kenya, 1933.

PINDER, RICHARD FRANCIS (R. of O.).—B. 1898; ed. St. Bees, Cumberland; served, European War, Flanders, Germany, 1916-19; asst. audr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920; ag. audr., Nov.-Dec., 1920; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1929; ag. audr., Feb.-May, 1930; sen. asst. audr., Uganda, Aug., 1931; ag. audr., July-Dec., 1932; audr., Fiji, Solomon Is. Prot., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Col., Aug., 1934.

PINKERTON, WILLIAM JAMES DUNLOP, A.M.I.C.E., Chartered Civil Engrn.—B. 1890; asst. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1914; exec. engrn., Ulu Selangor, July, 1923; sr. exec. engrn., in addn., Jan., 1930; offst. as sr. exec. engrn., Kinta, in addn., Nov.-Dec., 1930; senr. drainage and irrig. engrn. Malay States, Jan., 1932; irrig. engrn., Kelantan, Jan., 1932.

PINNEY, CAPT. CHAS. ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1883; ed. Essendon Grammar Schl.; draughtsman, lands dept., Papua, 1906; on war serv., A.I.F., 1914-20; wounded; transf'd. R. of O., C.M.F., 1920; ch. draughtsman, Papua, 1920; staff surv. in addn., 1924; ag. coman., lands, etc., 1927; coman., lands, dir., mines, dir., agr., dep. chmn., land bd., mem. exec. and leg. couns., July, 1930; admstr., Norfolk Is., July, 1932.

PIRIE, D. A.—B. 1886; clk. Burgher camps, Transvaal, 1903; treasy., 1903; prin. clk. treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1920; sec. mily. pensions bd., treasy., 1920; ch. clk., grade II, 1923; ch. clk., grade I, 1932.

PIROW, HANS, D.Sc., M.Inst.M.M., D.I.C., vice-pres., Geol. Soc. of S.A.; mem., coun., Chem. Met. and Min. Soc., and S.A. Assoc. for the Adv. of Science.—B. 1892; ed. S. Africa and on continent; grad., S.A. and London; various offl. capacities on Witwatersrand gold mines, 1911-1919; govt. research schol., 1919-20; asst. instr., mines, 1920; dominion sci. schol., London, 1924; G.M.E., Dec., 1926; is also chmn., mining leases bd. and elec. control bd., assessor mem., research grant bd. and statistical coun.; ag. sec., mines and industries, 1927.

PIROW, OSWALD.—B. 1890; ed. South Africa, Germany and England; admitted barrister, 1913; K.C., 1924; ent. parl., of the Union of S. Africa, 1926; min. of justice, June, 1929; min., rlys. and harbors and min. defence, Mar., 1933; World Econ. Conf., London, 1933.

PITCAIRN, ANDREW.—B. 1894; ed. Edinburgh Coll. of Agr.; on war serv., 1915-19 (France, Mesopotamia and Palestine); lieut., R.F.A.; ment. in deeds; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1921; asst. dir., agr., Cyprus, Mar., 1929; ag. dir., agr., on various occasions, 1930-33; ag. dir., animal hu-bandry, June to Sept., 1930, and June-Sept., 1932.

PITMAN, CHARLES ROBERT SENHOUSE, D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916).—B. 1890; ed. Royal Naval Schl., Blundell's and R.M.C., Sandhurst; att'd. R. Warwickshire Regt.; 27th Punjabis; capt., 1915; war serv., Egypt, 1914-15; France, 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916-18 (wounded, deeps twice, M.C., D.S.O.); Palestine, 1918-21; gen. staff, 1919-21; game warden, Uganda, 1925; seconded to N. Rhodesia for sp. duty, 1931-33.

PITT, WALTER JAMES, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge (modern and medieval language tripos); ent. Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; passed Bar final exam., Dec., 1920; headm., Cape Coast Govt. Schl., 1910; instr. of schl., 1912; prov. instr. of schl., 1914; att'd. to polit. serv., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; asst. dist. coman., July, 1917; dist. coman., Jan., 1921; ag. prov. coman., W. Prov., Ashanti, Feb.-Nov., 1920.

PITTMAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1878; ed. S. African Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A. (Univ. S.A.), M.A. (Oxon); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1903; admitted to Cape (1904) and Transvaal (1905) bars; dean of faculty of law, Univ. S.A., 1918-25; K.C., 1924; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa eastern dists. local divn., 1925; mem. delimitation comm., July, 1927 and Mar., 1932.

PLACE, ALAN WHITLEY.—B. 1888; native affrs. dept., S. Rhodesia, 1910-19; mily. serv., 2nd Rhodesia Regt., 1915-16, 4th K. A. R., 1916-24; cadet, Uganda, 1924; asst. sec., 1927; temp. serving in D.O. and C.O., 1928-29; senr. asst. sec., Uganda, 1934.

PLANT, GEORGE FREDERIC, C.B.E. (1927).—B. 1877; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Brasenose Coll. (scholar, 1896); 1st cl. class. mods., 1898; B.A., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1901; off. asst., Kurunegala, 1902; off. asst., Galle, 1904; pol. mag., Avissawella, 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Apr., 1906; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1906; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, May, 1909; temporarily employed in O.O. London, May, 1915, to Dec., 1918; sec., oversea settlement comte., Jan., 1919; permanently transferred to Home civ. serv., Sept., 1919; mem., oversea settlement delegtn. to Canada, 1924.

PLATTS, SHELLEY ARDEN.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl.; passed army entrance exam.; "King's India cadetship," 1917; gazetted 2/9th Gurka Rifles; 3rd Afghan War, 1919; attd. 2/11th Gurka Rifles and 4/7th Burma Rifles, Burma, 1921; cadet, admstive service, Tanganyika Territory, May, 1926; asst. dist. offr., July, 1928.

PLOWMAN, CLIFFORD HENRY FITZHERBERT, O.B.E (1928).—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Ely and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A. (clars. tripods); asst. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; pol. offr., Turkana Expdn., 1915 (A.G.S Med. with clasp); ag. offr. in charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1919-20; seconded to F.O. as H.B.M. Vice-Consul, Harar, Abyssinia, 1920; H.B.M. Consul on transfer Harar Consulate to C.O., 1922; transf., Somaliland, 1922; H.B.M. chargé d'affaires, Addis Ababa, Dec., 1925 to Jan., 1926; mem., mission to Abyssinia for coronation of Emperor, 1930; 2nd cls., Star of Ethiopia; asst. comsrr. and pol. offr., Br. Somaliland-Ethiopia bdy. comn., 1931-33; sec., govt. of Somaliland, 1933; ag. comsrr., Mar.-Oct., 1934; has passed E. Africa Prot. law exam. and in Swahili, Boran Galla and Amharic languages.

PLYMOUTH, 2nd Earl of (or. 1905) and 15th Baron Windsor (or. 1539) IVOR MILES WINDSOR-CLIVE.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; D.L., Worcestershire; lord-lieut., Glamorganshire, 1923; capt., hon. corps of gentlemen-at-arms, 1925; M.P., Ludlow divn., Shropshire, 1922-23; parly. under-sec. of state for Dominion affrs., Jan., 1929; kt. of grace of St. John of Jerusalem; parly. sec., min. of transport, Sept., 1931; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 30th Sept., 1932.

POLE-EVANS, ILLTYD BULLER, C.M.G. (1931), M.A. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (Wales), F.L.S.—B. 1879; ed. Cowbridge Gram. Schol. and Univs. of Wales and Cambridge; mycologist, Transvaal, 1905; rep. Transvaal govt. at 4th Internat. Botanical Congress, Brussels, 1910; ch., divn. of mycology and plant pathology, 1911; ch., divn. of botany and plant pathology, 1912; sp. mission to P. E. Africa, coconut palm disease, 1913; dir. botanical survey of S. Africa, 1918; pres., S. African Assocn. for Advancement of Science, 1920; ch., divn. of botany, horticulture and entomology, 1927; ch., divn. of plant industry, 1929; author of many scientific papers.

POLLARD, ARNOLD.—B. 1893; ed. Heles Schl., Exeter; active serv., R.N.A.S. and R.N.R., 1915-19; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, May, 1924; ag. senr. audr. on several occasions in Tanganyika and Kenya; audr., Windward Is., Jan., 1926; senr. asst. audr., Uganda, Jan., 1929; ag. audr., Apr., 1929; audr., Trinidad, June, 1931.
POOLEY, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 22nd Oct., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, Aug., 1933.

POPE, GEORGE WILLIAM, L.I.M.R.C.P., and S.I. (Ireland), D.P.H., R.C.P. and S.I.—B. 1891; med. offr., Kenya, 1920; 2nd health offr., port med. dept., Hong Kong, 1924; ag. asst. bacteriologist, 1924; asst. health offr., 1925; health offr., 1926.

POPE, LAWRENCE HENRY.—B. 1896; ed. Uppingham; served European War, 1915-19 (France and N. Russia); asst. P.S. to gov., S. Australia, Sir W. E. E. A. Weigall, K.C.M.G., 1920; P.S. to gov., Tasmania, Sir W. L. Allardyce, G.C.M.G., 1921; col. audit dept., 1923; asst. audr., Fiji, 1923; ag. audr., 1924-28; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

POPK, STEAD.—Br. post office, Feb., 1903; dist. survr., post and tels., Gold Coast, Dec., 1913; on activeserv., Togoland Field Force, Aug., 1914; and later in charge posts and tels., Togo, to May, 1915; Imp. army serv., Sept., 1915-19; lieut., R.E. and R.A.F.; dist. survr., Gold Coast, June, 1919; div. survr., Jan., 1927; P.M.G., Nyasaland, Mar., 1930.

POPHAM, HENRY BRADSHAW, M.B.E. (1918)—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; comsrr., 13th LightInfy., 1900; served, S.AfricanWar (2 med.); seconded, G. Coast regt., W.A.F.F., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec. 'office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. to ag. gov., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., July to Aug., 1913; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1913; dist. comsrr., Jan., 1914; seconded to Togoland polit. admtn., Nov., 1914; senr. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1920 to Feb., 1921; dep. prov. comsrr. and ag. comsrr., W.Prov., Feb., 1921; prov. comsrr., Apr., 1922; ret'd. (invalided) May, 1923; comsrr., Cyprus, Oct., 1925; admstr., Dominica, 1933; mem., exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1933.
POPLEWELL, GEOFFREY DOUGLAS, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1904; cadet, provincial adminstr., Tanganyika Territory, April, 1927; asst. dist. offr., May, 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1931-32.

PORTELLY, JOHN, M.D., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H.(Eng.), Certif. Lond. Schl. of Hygiene and T.M.—B. 1889; ed. govt. secondary schl. and Univ., Malta; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, S.S., Mar., 1922; med. offr., S. Kedah, and asst. supt. prisons, in addn., Jan., 1925; med. offr., in ch. gen. hosp., J. Bahru, Apr., 1927; port health offr., S'pore, Feb., 1929; ag. senr. health offr., Penang, Apr., 1929; offl. rep. on L. of N. Interchange Tour for port health offrs. in the East, June, 1930; ag. senr. health offr., Johore, Sept., 1932; regir.-gen., births and deaths, Johore, Oct., 1932; ag. state med. and health offr., N. Semban, June, 1933.

POSTMUS, JOHANNES.—B. 1877; ed. in S. Africa and at Sneek, Holland; gen. man., Netherlands Bank of S.A., 1920-26; dep. gov., S. African Reserve Bank, 1927-31; gov., S. African Reserve Bank, Jan., 1932; World Econ. Conf., London, 1933.

POTTER, JOHN WAGNER.—B. 1873; ed. at Uppingham Schl. (class. schol.); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Crown Agents offr., Oct., 1896; asst. registr., inscribed stock, Mar., 1905; asst. head, gen. stores dept., Jan., 1913; ditto, gen. dept., Jan., 1920; ditto, pay dept., Jan., 1924; dep. head, pay dept., Feb., 1934.

POTTINGER, JOSEPH HECTOR, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Lpool).—B. 1894; on war serv., 1915-18; med. offr., W. African med. staff, Gold Coast, 1928; asst. gov't. med. offr. of health, Br. Guiana, 1932.
POTTS, HUGH CUTHBERT MILLER.—R.1907; ed. Durham Schl. and Lincoln Coli., Oxford; B.A. Law; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, July, 1930; asst. dist. offr., 1932.

POTTS, JOHN WILFRED.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1912-15; clk., 1915; ag. ast. mag., 1920; ag. res. mag., 1929 and 1930; passed civ. ser. lower law exam., 1919; passed 2nd grade Seawana exam., 1922; sub. inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1924; inspr., Mar., 1927; res. mag., Apr., 1929.

POUGNET, EDOUARD DESBARRIÈRES, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., Mauritius, Jan., 1888; asst. water authority, Moh., 1898; inspr. of water wks., July, 1903; govt. survr., Moh., 1911; supt. of pub. wks. and survr. of roads, St. Kitts, Apr., 1914; in service of French govt., in France during the war.

POULIER, REGINALD SYDNEY VERNON.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; att. Galle coll., Aug., 1919; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1922; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Oct., 1924; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1926; commr., requests, Kandy, Oct., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1928; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1931.

POULTON, ARTHUR STANLEY.—B. 1900; ed. R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; R.A., 1918; Iraq campaign, 1920; Somaliland Camel Corps (K.A.R.), 1927; commr., 3rd grade, Br. Somaliland, 1931; asst. dist. offr., 1932.
POULTON, WIFRED FRANK.—B. 1888; ed. Bishop's Stafford Coll. and Royal Vety. Coll., London; vety. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1914; war serv. in E. Africa, 1914-17; dir., vety. services, Uganda, Feb., 1925.

POUSSETTE, HENRY RIVINGTON.—B. 1872; ed. Sarnia Pub. Sch., Upper Can. Coll., Royal Mily. Coll., Kingston, and Sch. of Prac. Science, Toronto; capt., S. African Constab., 1901, and thereafter in same corps throughout S. African war; dist. commr., and pub. pros., S. Africa; ent. serv. dept. of trade and commerce, Can., as trade commr., Durban, S. Africa, 1909; trade commr., Buenos Aires, 1910; enlisted for active serv., 1914; returned Can., 1919; inspr. trade commr., serv., sp. trade commr., India, 1919; dir., comm. intelligence serv., Ottawa, 1921; trade commr., Trinidad, 1924.

POWELL, BASIL WESTRAK BARNARD.—B. 1881; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. and Highfield Coll., Southampton; senr. engr., P. and T. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., Penang, Mar., 1913; ditto, K. Lumpur, July, 1928; ditto, N. Sembilan, Dec., 1928; mag., Seremban, July, 1934.
POWELL, HARRY CRESSWELL, A.M.Inst.T.—B. 1881; mily. serv., France, 1915-19; traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rly., Jan., 1921; dir., do., Oct., 1930.

POWER, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; Indian Army, 1917; seconded, I.C.S. as polit. res., Perim Is., 1922-23; col. audit. dept., Apr., 1924; asst. audr., Gold Coast, June, 1924; ag. dep. audr., July, 1928; audr., Windward Is., Feb., 1929.
POWER, G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1914; asst. dir., educn., Gold Coast, May, 1929; dir., ditto, Feb., 1933.
POWERS, HON. SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G., (1929).—Justice of the high ct. of Australia, 1913; ret.

POYNTON, ARTHUR HILTON.—B. 1905; schol. of Marlborough Coll. (1918-23) and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (1923-7); 1st cls. hona., mods., 1925, 2nd cls. hona., lit. hum., 1927; M.A., 1933; apptd. asst. prim., dept. of scientific and industrial research after compet. exam., 31st Oct., 1927; transf. to C.O., 1st Oct., 1929; joint sec., Col. Development Advisory Comte., 1st Apr. to 30th Sept., 1930; sec., do., 1st April, 1932; pte. sec. to parly. under sec. for cols. (Sir R. Hamilton), 18th April, 1932 and to (Lord Plymouth), 30th Sept., 1932; seconded to Sierra Leone admtrn., Apr., 1933-35,

POYSER, KENNETH ELLISTON, D.S.O., K.C. (Barbados).—B. 1882; ed. Shrewsbury and Merton Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1906; served, European War, 1914-20, Yorks. L.I., and Loyal N. Lanc. Regt. (despatched, D.S.O., 1917); puiane judge, Leeward Islands, Sept., 1920; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1926; atty.-gen., Uganda, 1928; puiane judge, Ceylon, 1933.

PRAIN, Lt.-Col. Sir David, Kt. Bach. (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram. schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3, and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1898; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1896-1905; director, botanical survey of India, and supt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1905; trustees of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1905; ret., 1922.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR, B.A.(Cantab.).—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class, tripso), B.A., 1905; baccalaureat (B.L. 1st part) Lyons Univ., France, 1908; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. res., 1911; ag. supt., schl., July, 1912 to Apr., 1913; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Apr., 1921; promoted cls. I, grade I, admstve serv., Feb., 1928.

PRASAD, MURARI, U.B.—B. 1888; Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; addtl. office ast. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office ast. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917; ditto, Panadure, Apr., 1919; office ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, May, 1921; pol. mag.; Kalutara, May, 1922; ditto, Gampola, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1924; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1926; dist. judge, Matale, June, 1927; commr., stamps, July, 1932; dist. judge, Kurunegalla, Oct., 1933.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verendrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1885; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puiane judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910; ch. just., Dec., 1929.
PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Apptd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; ast. regis. and deputy clk. of arraigns, 1911.

PRETEROE, EDWARD OWEN, M.C., B.A., LL.B.—B. 1896; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921; admstve. offr., 1922; ast. commr., lands, 1924; ag. commr., lands, 1924; ast. sec., S. Provs., 1924; pol. mag., 1924; ag. ch. registr., sup. ct. on various occasions; crown ct., 1930; ag. solr. gen. on various occasions.

PRETORIUS, Jan Lodewyk.—B. 1878; ed. at Grey Coll., Bloemfontein; joined O.F.S. repub. civ. serv., Mar., 1887; ast. mag., Edenburg, 1902; Vrede, 1904; Lindley, 1908; det. ast. mag., Dewetsdorp, Feb., 1910 to Oct., 1913; Reitz, Nov., 1913; mag., Reitz, 1918; Hoopstad, Feb., 1919; Boshof, Oct., 1919; Frankfort, Oct., 1923; Senekal, June, 1929; Vereeniging, Feb., 1930; Lichtenburg, Mar., 1933; Port Elizabeth, Feb., 1934.

PRETTY, ERIC ERNEST FALK.—B. 1891; ed. Harrow, and Demy of Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ag. colr. of land revenue, Johore Bahru, Sept., 1917; offr., cls. V., S.S., Dec., 1917; 2nd lieut. and asst. adjt., J.V.R., May, 1918; supernumy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; Br. res., Brunei, Mar., 1923; offr., Cls. III., Oct., 1926; dist. offr., Krian, June, 1928; ag. sec., high commnr., July, 1931; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931.

PRICE, CHARLES ROY.—B. 1893; ed. Wellington Schol., Somersetand University Coll., London; B.A. (war), 1919; unattached list, Territorial Force, Jan. to Aug., 1915; Royal Garrison Arty., Sept., 1915 to Dec., 1918; served in France, June, 1916 to Sept., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prim., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; pvt. sec. to perm. under sec. for dom. affrs., 6th July, 1927 (ag. from 6th June, 1927); temp. ag. prin., D.O., 22nd July, 1927; pvt. sec. to partly. under sec. for dom. affrs., Jan., 1928; asst. sec., Imp. wireless and cable confce., Jan., July, 1928; ag. prin., D.O., 15th Sept., 1928; U.K. delegn. to L. of N. assembly, 1928 and 1932; U.K. delegn. to London naval confce., 1930; attd. to sec., Imp. confce., 1930; U.K. delegn. disarmament confce., Geneva, Feb.-Dec., 1932.

PRICE, HERBERT HENRY.—2nd clk to asst. commnr., Northern Div., Bech. Prot., 1910; 2nd clk., res. commnr.'s office, 1st May, 1912; passed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam., 1910; ag. ass't. res. mag., 1911-12; registr. of brands, 1913; ch. clk. and registr., 1921; passed 3rd grade Seewana exam., 1927.

PRICE, STANLEY LEWIS, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Lampeter Coll., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jur. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; ag. prin., higher coll., Katsina, 1934.

PRICE-JONES, CAPT. WILLIAM.—Served during Great War in France; awarded M.C., 1917; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1919; dist. commnr., Apr., 1924; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1926; pol. mag., 1928.

PRICHARD, HERBERT WILLIAM, K.C. (1923), B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1873; ed. at Keble Coll., Oxford (open class. scholar, 1892); called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1902; practised in London, 1902-1910; stip. mag. Trinidad, Aug., 1910; puisne judge, Mauritius, Feb., 1915; 1st puisne judge, Oct., 1916; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., Oct., 1916 to July, 1917; chrmn. of commn. on Royal College, 1916-1917; chrmn. sugar sales commn., Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1920 and Feb. to Apr., 1921; ag. procureur and advoc. gen., April, 1921; confirmed in appt., Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. from Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925; puisne judge, S. Sttlms., and judge, F.M.S., June, 1927.

PRING, HARRY.—B.1883; ed. Watford Schol. and King's Coll., London; Imp. serv., 1898-1903; col. sec's office, Capetown, 1903; dept. of interior, Pretoria, 1910; ch. clk., 1926; under sec., 1927; pub. serv. commnr., 1931.

PRINGLE, LEONARD DUNGAN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B.1905; med. offr., F.M.S., 1931; Hong Kong, 1932; ag. radiologist, Jan.-Aug., 1933; M.O. in ch. of officers' families, Victoria Gaol, infant dist. hosp., Victoria mortuary and remand house, 1934.

PRIOR, A. C. VINCENT.—B. 1881; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1903; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Jan., 1915; ag. ch. just., Jan. to June, 1915; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 1918; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Gold Coast, 1925; ag. atty.-gen., May to Nov., 1926; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1927; ag. ch. just., June to July, 1928 and June to Oct., 1929; atty.-gen., Nigeria, Oct., 1929.
PRITCHARD, NEIL.—B. 1911; ed. Liverpool Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford (hist. schol.: 1929-32); hist. schol., 1st cls.; B.A., 1952; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., D.O., Oct., 1933.

PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in France and Germany, called to the bar. Mid. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The Jurisdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," and other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1893; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. col. sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; gov. secretary, S. Nigeria, Apr., 1901; gov., Sierra Leone, July, 1904; gov., Barbados, Nov., 1910; gov., Jamaica, May, 1918; ret., 1924.
PROFTING, HON. W. B., C.M.G. (1918).—M.L.C., Tasmania; atty.-gen. and min. for rly.s., Apr., 1916; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

PROUT, SIR WILLIAM THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1928), KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1905), O.B.E., M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—Asst. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1886; surg. supt., immigrant st.p. "Dundee," 1887; asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. chief surg., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895; t.l.e altered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and correms. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect. schl. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory med. and sanitary comttee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; senr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; author of articles "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm Climates" (Paragon); Fil. Volouloides (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. Leone" (Brit. Med Journal, 1902); and other papers.

PRYER, JOHN HAWKES.—B. 1894; ed. Bedford Harpur Trust; mech. transport, B.E.F., France, 1918-19; cadet pilot, R.A.F.; asst. storekeeper, Gold Coast rly., Sept., 1920; ch. do., Aug., 1929; ch. do., Mar., 1932.

PUCKRIDGE, GEOFFREY MARTIN.—B. 1895; ed. Exeter Schol.; 2nd lieut., Devon Rext., T.F., Dec., 1913; capt., Dec., 1914; served in France with 6th West Yorks regt.; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, July, 1917; seconded to Inter Allied Aeronautical Comn. of Control, Germany, 1920; resigned commn., Sept., 1921; asst. dist. commn., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. commn., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1928.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schol.; apprentice with Messrs. Rowe, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1901-1905; engineering asst., dock engrn.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hesle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916; D.P.W., Palestine, 1927.

PUGHE, S. R., M.B.E. (1926).—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; confd. clk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 1st Mar., 1916; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1922; on service in Sierra Leone, Oct., 1926 to Feb., 1927; sec., Discovery comte., Jan.-June, 1928.

PULLICINO, SIR PHILIP, Kt. Bach. (1934), B.Litt., LL.D.—B. 1885; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1900; B.Litt., 1903; LL.D., 1907; advoc. for poor, 1918; judge, 1928; pub. pros. and treasy. coun., 1929.

PULLON, BERTRAM.—B. 1882; asst. printer, N. Rhodesia, 1921; ag. govt. printer, 1924-25; asst. govt. printer, 1925; ag. govt. printer for various periods during 1927-29, 1931 and 1932; govt. printer, 1932.

PURCELL, HUBERT KENNEDY.—B. 1884; entd. Crown Agents office, Oct., 1901; asst. cashier, July, 1914; head of pay dept., Dec., 1920; dep. head of pay and checking dept., Nov., 1921; dep. head, pay dept., 1924; dep. head, gen. dept. and dep. ch. clk., 1930; dep. head, gen. stores dept., Jan., 1933; head, pay dept., May, 1933; head, gen. dept. and ch. clk., Nov., 1933.

PURCELL, VICTOR WILLIAM WILLIAMS SAUNDERS.—B. 1896; ed. Bancroft's Schol. and Trinity Coll., Camb.; B.E.F., France, 1915-18; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., K. Lumbur; passed cadet, July, 1924; offr., cls. V, July, 1923; inspnr., Chinese schls., S.S., and asst. dir., educn., June, 1924; asst. prot., Chinese, Penang, July, 1925; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1926; offr., cls. IV, July, 1927, dep. regisr. and asst. offr. assignee, Penang; 2nd asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S., Aug., 1930; offr., cls. III, July, 1932; ag. prot., Chinese, Penang, Nov., 1932.

PURLL, FREDERICK JOHN.—B. 1891; conf. shorthand writer, B. of T., Aug., 1909; conf. clk. to gen. man., lab. exchange dept., B. of T., Oct., 1909; conf. shorthand writer to S. of S., H.O., Jan., 1911; cler. offr., 21st Jan., 1925; accompanied the Rt. Hon. L. S. Amery, M.P.
