(S. of S. for Dominion Affairs) on his tour of the Dominions July, 1927-Feb., 1928; transferred to C.O., June, 1928; accompanied Br. economic mission to Australia, Aug., 1928-Feb., 1929; seconded for serv. with Newfoundland R. Comsn., Mar.-Oct., 1933.

PURVES, ALEXANDER BRUCE, M.I.C.E.—B. 1890; ed. Kirkcaldy and George Watson's Coll., Edin.; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1914; asst. engrnr., Tytam Tuk scheme, 1915; asst. water engrnr., 1916; engnr., Shing Mun Valley scheme, in addn., 1923-29; dep. water engrnr., 1925; exec. engrnr., in ch., waterwks., 1930.

PUTTICK, LEONARD JAMES.—B. 1885; ed. at High Schl. and Huguenot Coll., Wellington and S. African Coll., Cape Town; served Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; draughtsman, Swaziland, 1910; clk. of lands, 1916; ch. clk., 1919; ag. regist. and mast., sp. ct. and sheriff, 1919; sec., Swaziland-Portuguese bdry. comsn., 1922; sec., undeveloped minerals comsn., 1927; ag. dept. asst. comsnr., 1925 and 1930; beacon inspnr., 1927; ag. gov't. sec. in 1927 and 1928; asst. gov't. sec., 1928; regist. and mast., sp. ct., sheriff, regist., births, marriages and deaths and comsnr., protocols, 1932.

QUIGLEY, EUGENE PATRICK, M.C.—B. 1889; served six years, R.I.C.; joined army, 1914; 2nd Lieut., R. Dublin Fusiliers, 1915; served in France, Egypt and Palestine; organised pol., C.I.D., Palestine, 1919; D.C.F., grade I, 1921; dist. supt., pol., Apr., 1926; dep. comdt., C.I.D., 1930.

RAE, SIR JAMES STANLEY, Kt. Bach. (1933)—B. 1881; articled and conf. clk. to Sir W. R. Davies, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; called to the bar, Bahamas, Apr., 1904; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; ag. coroner for the Is. of New Providence from 1904, confirmed in 1911; J.P., 1910; ag. atty.-gen. Jan., 1910; ag. stip. and circ. mag., 1910-11, and on other occasions; dist. comsnr., Belize, B. Honduras, 1914; offr. rec. in bankruptcy, offr. admstr. and J.P. for the col., 1914; regist.-gen., Nov., 1915, in conjunction with duties as dist. comsnr.; dir. of P.O.G. fund, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1916; press censor during war; pvt., B. Hond. Territorial Force; attd. to staff as mily. censor; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Apr., 1919; cross of Chevalier of the Order of the Crown, Belgium, for relief services during war, May, 1919; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, June to Oct., 1919; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, Sept., 1920; ag. ch. just., June, 1921 to Feb., 1922 and from Sept., 1922 to June, 1923; ch. just., St. Vincent, June, 1923; ag. admstr., June, 1926 to Feb., 1927; ch. just., Grenada, 1927; revised and consolidated laws, 1926; ch. just., Leeward Is., Apr., 1931.
RAE, NORMAN.—B. 1892; entd. Crown Agent's office, May, 1911; asst. head of dept., May, 1919; dep. head, stores dept., Oct., 1932.

RAEBURN, COLIN, O.B.E. (1934), D.Sc., F.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Hamilton Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; lieut., Cameron Highlanders, 1915-19; Salonika, 1915-18; geologist, Scottish Spitzbergen syndicate, 1919; geologist, geol. survey, Nigeria, 1920-29; asst. dir., 1929; author of "Alluvial Prospecting."

RAFFLES, MAJOR STAMFORD CECIL, O.B.E. (Mily.), 1919.—B. 1885; ed. All Saints Schol., Bloxham, Royal Naval Academy, Gosport, and in Germany; 2nd lieut., 4th Suffolk regt., 1903; lieut., 1903; transf., K.S.L.I., 1906; resig. comsnr., 1908; supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, 1913; seconded for mily. serv., Jan., 1915; lieut., 3rd R.W.F., Mar., 1915; capt., Sept., 1915; staff offr., 3rd cls., R.F.C., 1917; staff offr., 2nd cls., R.A.F., 1918; tempy. major, 1918; transf., unemployed list, R.A.F., Apr., 1919; resumed duty as supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, and supvr., cust., Kinta, Dec., 1919; supvr., cust., Selangor, Dec., 1920; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Jan., 1921; dep. comsnr., excise, F.M.S. in addn., Apr.-July, 1929; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., Selangor and Pahang, June, 1931; dep. comsnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Apr., 1932; ag. comsnr., do., in addn., Dec., 1932; senr. dep. comsnr., cust. and excise, F.M.S., 1934.

RAIKES, H. R.—Ed. Tonbridge Schol., Dulwich Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (Williams exhib.), Abbot Schol., 1911, final hon. schol. of nat. science (chem.), class I, 1914; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1919; served European War, 1st Batt. The Buffs, R.F.C. and R.A.F., 1914-1919 (A.F.C.); fellow, Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1919-27; sub-rector, 1924-27; demonstr. and treas., Balliol and Trinity Coll. laboratory, 1920-27; ch. instr., Oxford Univ. Air Squadron, 1925-27; wing commander, R.A.F.O. since 1926; prin., Univ. of Witwatersrand, Johannesburg, 1928; publns.—papers in Jnl.s of Chem. Soc'y. and Faraday Soc'y.
RAINSFORD, HUME FREDERICK.—B. 1902; ed. Dublin Univ.; B.A. and B.A.I. hons., civ. engrngr., 1925; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

RAINSFORD, Capt. Richard Frederick.—B. 1888; Natal pol., 1904-07, served Zulu Rebellion, 1906 (med.); B.S.A.P., 1907-08; inspr. E.A. Pol., 1910; asst. supt., 1914; commanded No. 1 Column, Turkhana Punitive Expdn., 1915 (desps., A.G.S. med.); capt., 5th K.A.R., 1916; adjt. and staff offr. to O.C. troops, Jubaland, 1917-19 (desps.); "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory med.; supdt., Kenya pol., 1923.

RAJA MUSA, Bin Raja Haji Bot, Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—B. 1897; Malay probr., Oct., 1910; Malay asst., grade III, July, 1914; do., grade II, July, 1917; Malay offr., grade I, July, 1920; asst. dist. offr., U. Langat, Nov., 1921; offr. cls. V Jan., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Jan., 1928; offr. cls. III, Jan., 1933; ag. reg., sup. ct. K. Lumpur, Apr., 1933.

RAJA UDA, Bin Raja Muhamad.—B. 1894; Malay probr., Oct., 1910; Malay asst., C.S.O., grade III, Sept., 1913; do., grade II, July, 1917; Malay offr., grade I, July, 1920; offr. cls. V, asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Jan., 1924; offr. cls. IV, Jan., 1928; dep. chmn., sany. bd., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1930; offr. cls. III, Jan., 1933; dist. offr., K. Selangor, Mar., 1933.

RALSTON, James.—B. 1890; M.A. (Glas.), 1911; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1914; seconded for mil. serv., 1918-19; headmast., Indian Schl., Hong Kong, 1925; 2nd mast., King's Coll., 1928; headmast., Ellis Kadoorie Schl., 1929 and 1933; ditto, Wansai Schl., 1930; external examr. in method, Hong Kong Univ., 1930; ag. inspr., schl., 1931; off. J.P., 1932; headmr., Cent. Br. Schl., 1934.

RAMBAUT, Arthur Edmund, B.A.—B. 1883; ed. Magd. Coll. Schl. and B.N.C., Oxford; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1905; 4th cls. bot., 1908; dipl. forestry, 1909; asst. con., forests, F.M.S., Nov., 1909; on mil. dutv., 2nd lieut. North Fus. (T.F.), 1915; lieut., 1916; temp. capt. 1916; served in France; intel. corps., 1917 (France, Belgium, Germany); rtnd. to F.M.S., Aug., 1919; dep. con., forests, Perak, South, Nov., 1926; ag. per. asst. to con., forests, Dec., 1927 and Aug., 1928; conserv., foresta, Jan., 1930.

RANASINHA, Arthur Godwin, B.A. (Lond.)—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; attd. to Kegalla Kach., Mar., 1921; attd., Jaffna kach., Oct., 1922; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Mar., 1923; ditto, Balapitiya, Nov., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, June, 1926; dist. judge Avissawella, Apr., 1928; ditto, Badulla, Jan., 1930; sec. to min. for agr. and lands, June, 1933.

RANKINE, Sir Richard Sims Donkin, K.C.M.G. (1932), C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1875; clk. treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir George O'Brien, Sept., 1897 to 1901; acted clk. to ex. coun. Sept., 1899 to Mar., 1900: apptd. acot. native taxes, treas., 1901; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson, July, 1902; priv. sec. to Sir E. im Thurn, Oct., 1904; conf. sec., Jan., 1905; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific; clk. of exec. coun.; ag. sec., W. Pacific high comm., June, 1905 to June, 1906, and from Nov., 1907 to Dec., 1908; asst. col. sec. and asst. recvr-gen. Jan., 1909; recvr-gen. and commr. of stamps, pres. of marine bd., mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1910; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1913 to Nov., 1914; gov't. dep., in June, 1914; commr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. agt.-gen. of immigrn. (conjoint), Mar., 1915; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1915 to May, 1916; gov't. deputy, Aug., Oct., Dec., 1915; ag. col. sec. on various occasions, 1917-19; on sp. mission to India as personal rep. of the gov't. in connection with emigr. of Indians to Fiji, Oct., 1919 to June, 1920; ch. sec., Nyamalal Prot., July, 1920; ag. gt. on various occasions, 1921-24 and in 1926; ch. sec., Uganda, Aug., 1927; admstd. govt., June to Oct., 1928; Br. res., Zanzibar, 1929.

RANSOM, Hon. Ethelbert Alfred.—B. 1868; ed. lower Hutt state schl., N.Z.; mer. N.Z., H. of R. since 1922; min. of pub. wks., 1929-30; min. of lands and commr., state forests, 1930; min. same offices, coalition min'y., 1931.

RANSOM, Herbert Charles, O.B.E. (1920), M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1881; entd. crown agt.'s of May, 1901; asst. cashier, Mar., 1914; ag. head of appts. dept. and asst. sec., Jan., 1917; head of miscellaneous dept. Apr., 1919; dep. head, gen. dept., 14th Nov., 1921; sec., Dead Sea mineral reeves comte., 1923; sec., Palestine currency td., 1926-30; ch. clk. and head, gen. dept., Feb., 1930; head, finance dept., Nov., 1933.
RANSOME, John Edward Geoffrey.—B. 1891; ed. Bradfield Coll.; 3rd K.A.R., Oct., 1914; Mar., 1923; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Mar., 1925.

RAYMAN, Lazarus.—B. 1889; ed. Manchest. Gram. Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; ed. F.M.S., 1913; Brit. advr.'s office, Keilah; asst. dist. offr., Pekan, 1914-16; asst. dist. offr., Bentong, on various occasions, 1916-19; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1917 and 1919-21; on sp. duty; collr. of stamp duty, Mar., 1922; offr. cls. III, Nov., 1925; collr., estate duty, F.M.S., Oct., 1926; 2nd asst. sec. to gov't., Jan., 1927; ag. commr., lands and mines, Trengganu, Dec., 1929; cls. II, Nov., 1930; ag. finan. commr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Dec., 1930; ag. treas., S.S., Mar., 1933; ag. dep. treas., S.S., Nov., 1933; ag. finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., May, 1944.

RAYMOND, Leonard William.—B. 1862; ed. Horwell Gr. Schl. and Dunbeved Coll., Launceston; Royal Coll. Sci.; B.A. (Lond.), B.Sc. (Lond. 1912, hons. 1915), A.R.C.S. (1915), A.I.C. (1918); chem. offr., R.E., 1916; ch. bactgt. with forces, Mesopotamia, 1917-19; asst. gov't. chem., agrl. dept., Zanzibar, 1925; ag. gov't. chem., 1926; ag. ast. dir., agr. on various occasions, 1926-32; del., Imp. agrl. conf., 1927; gov't. chemist, 1931.
READ, Alexander Llewellyn.—B. 1877; pub. serv., S. Australia, 1891; ch. exec. office, 1920; under sec., sec. to premier and clk. exec. coun., June, 1931.

READ, Sir Herbert James, K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B. (1914), C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1885; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. math. mods. and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to M. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to M. (afterwards Sir. E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; clk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., Apr., 1899; British delegate at African liquor conf., Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on boundy. comm. at Berlin for the settm. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor conf., Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. mem. comtce. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1910; ch. of deptltn. comtce. to enquire into W. Afr. med. staff, 1909; visited E. Africa, 1911-12; sen. mem., C.O. delegation at Paris Peace Conf., 1919; asst. under-sec. of state, March, 1926; regar. of the Order of Saint Michael and Saint George; gov', Mauritius, 1924 to 1930; ba... mem., managing comtee., Imp. Inst. of Entomology, 1931; hon. mem., managing comtee., Imp. Mycological Inst., 1932.

REAKES, CHARLES JOHN, C.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.V.S., Eng., Doctor of Vet. Science, Univ. of Melbourne.—B. 1885; vet. offr., N.Z. dept. of agr., 1897; asst. ch. vet., 1901; dir., live-stock divn., and dir. vet. services and remounts, N.Z. Defence Forces with rank of lieut.-col. (since col.), 1909; dir.-gen., agr. dept., 1918.
REAY, CAPT., GEORGE HENRY NOEL, A.M.I.E.E., D.F.H.—B. 1889; asst. teleg. engrn., F.M.S., Jan., 1914; war serv., 1915-19; elec. inspr., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; elec. engrn., P.W.D., Singapore, July, 1922.
REDELINGHUYSEN, JOHANNES NICOLAAS.—Cape post and tel. serv., 1909; sec.'s office, Cape Town, 1909; senr. clk., 1920; ch. clk., 1924; asst. under sec., 1926; under sec. and asst. P.M.G., 1927.
REDFERN, PERCY WILLIAM.—B. 1890; loco. acctnt., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1919; asst. acctnt., Jan., 1925; finol. offr., July, 1928; officg. sr. asst. acctnt., F.M.S. rly., Aug., 1933.

REECE, C. MANSEL.—B. 1894; ed. Rossall and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; war serv., 1914-18 (ment. in desps.); called to bar Inner Temple, 1921; pvt. practice, Shanghai, 1921-26; mag., Zanzibar, 1927; ag. puisane judge. Apr.-July, 1929; crown coun., Uganda, 30; ag. solr-gen. in 1930, 1931, 1932 and 1933; atty.-gen., Grenada, Nov., 1933; K.C., 1934; comsnr. to revise laws of Grenada.

REECE, GERALD.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby Sch.; served in France with Sherwood Foresters, 1915-18; solr., sup. ct., England, 1921; admstve. cadet, Kenya, 1925; dist. offr., 1927; ag. consul for S. Abyssinia, 1932.

REED, E. A.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1918; temp. paper keeper, C.O. lib., 22nd Nov., 1920-15th Aug., 1924; estab. paper keeper (library attendant) after qual. exam., 16th Aug., 1924; cler. offr., 31st Oct., 1928.

REED, EDWARD BOYS, P.A.S.I., F.R.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. S. Savior's, Eastbourne, Hurst and County Tech. Coll., Sussex; land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1906; 1st grade survr., 1913; on mily. serv., capt. R.G.A., 1915-19; prin. land survr., 1922; supt., surveys, 1928; supt., crown lands and surveys, 1932; J.P., 1932.

REED, HON. JOHN RANKEN, C.B.E. (1919)—B. 1864; ed. Gram. Schl., Auckland, N.Z., Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1921; pres., prisons bd., 1928; is judge advoc.gen., N.Z. Defence Forces, with rank of colonel.

REED, THOMAS BOUCHER.—B. 1892; ed. St. Stanislaus Coll. and Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana joined lands and mines dept., Br. Guiana, 1908; successively 5th, 4th, 3rd and 2nd cls. offr., P.W.D., 1910-24; senr. clk., P.W.D., 1929; sec., standing unemployment relief comtee., 1933; sec. flood relief finance comtee., 1934.

REES-DAVIES, STIR WM., KT. BACH. (1913).—B. 1863; ed. Eton and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1885; called to bar (Inner Temp.), 1887; went South Wales circuit; J.P. and dep. lieut. Pembrokeshire; J.P. for Haverfordwest; M.P. for Pembrokeshire, 1892-8; priv. sec. to the late Rt. Hon. Sir W. V. Harcourt when chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5, and afterwards from 1895-8; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1897; administered the govt., 1901; ag. ch. just., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, Oct., 1902; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1906; K.C., 1908; ag. ch. just., 1909-10; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1912; chmn. of gen. mily. serv. tribunal, 1918; chmn., pub. serv. comm., Hong Kong, 1919; chmn., exec. comtee. on the visit of H.R.H. The Prince of Wales, 1922; ret., 1924.

REEVE, GEORGE WALKER, B.A. (Alta., Canada).—B. 1887; on mil. serv., 1914-19; admstve. branch, A.I.P., Air Miny., 1918-22; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, Mar., 1922; lectr., Hong Kong Univ., 1925-33; 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 1931 and 1933; headmnr., Wansai schl., 1934.
REEVES, CHARLES JAMES.—B. 1875; entd. office consulting engrns. to W. African govt. rlys., 1895; apptd. to Crown Agents office, 1911; asst. head, appts. dept., 1919; dep. head, do., 1933.

REGNARD, MAJOR LOUIS FERDINAND.—B. 1888; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology and McGill Univ. (B.Sc.); 2nd lieut., R.K., Nov., 1914; war serv., France, Belgium and Palestine, 1914-20; major, R.E., 1918-20; regular army B. of O.; asst. D.P.W. and surveys, Mauritius, 1921; D.P.W., 1926; M.L.C.
REID, C. B. B., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—Capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.
REID, FREDERICK MURRAY.—B. 1875; inspr., schls., Basutoland, Oct., 1914; senr. inspr., schls., 1927; dir., educn., 1934.
REID, HAROLD WALLACE, B.Sc.—B. 1897; dep. inspr., mines, Trinidad, Sept., 1927.
REIDY, CAPT. MICHEL JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1888; ed. Royal Veterinary Coll., Dublin; Fiji, 1922; asst. col. vet. surg., Hong Kong, 1924.

REILLY, Sir BERNARD RAWDON, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.I.E. (1926), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1882; ed. Bedford; joined Indian Army, 1902; ent. Indian polit. dept., 1908; served in India and Aden in various appmts.; officiated as polit. res., Aden, 1926 and 1926, and as res. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, 1930 and 1931; res. and commdr.-in-ch., Mar., 1931; ch. comsnr., Aden, Apr., 1932.

REILLY, ROBERT O'MALLEY, O.B.E. (1935).—Sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. police, 1904; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; ag. asst. res. mag., 1912; inspr., Apr., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; res. mag., 1921; passed 3rd grade Secwana exam., 1922; asst. res. comsnr., Oct., 1931; ag. res. comsnr., April to Oct., 1932.
REIMERS, EDMUND, M.B.E. (1934).—B. 1881; archivist and librarian, Ceylon, Oct., 1921.
REITZ, DENNY.—B. 1882; M.P. for Bloemfontein, 1920; M.P. for Port Elizabeth, 1920-27; M.P. for Barberton, 1929; min. of lands, Smuts cabinet, 1921-24; min. of lands, coalition cab., 1933.
REJAS, FELIX.—B. 1856; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, Feb., 1887; warden, July, 1912.

REID, JAMES STUART WEBSLEY, M.A. (Edin.).—B. 1899; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1921; attclh. land office, Kajang, Nov., 1921; do., dist. office, L. Perak, Mar., 1922; do., U. Perak as financ. ast., Sept., 1922; passed cadet, July, 1923; priv. sec. to ch. sec. to govt. F.M.S., Jan., 1924; offr. cls. V., Oct., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Apr., 1925; do., Sitianwan, Sept., 1926; offr. cls. IV., Oct., 1928; 2nd ast. advr., Muar, Apr., 1931; ag. dist. offr., K. Bahru and P. Puteh, and treas., Kelantan, May, 1932; offr. cls. III., Oct., 1933.

RENDLE, HILARY CAMERON RUSSEL.—B. 1899; ed. Christ's Coll., N. Zealand, Cantby Coll., Univ. of N.Z. and New Coll., Oxford; cadet F.M.S., Nov., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1923; offr., cls. V Nov., 1924; asst. sec. to res., Perak, Jan., 1925; priv. sec. to chief sec., July, 1925; asst. dist. offr., B. Pahang, Feb., 1927; offr. cls. IV, 2nd asst. advr., Muar, Nov., 1928; asst. dist. offr., L. Perak, Jan., 1932; offr. cls. III, 2nd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1933.

RENNIE, ALFRED BAULIE, LL.B., B.B., 1896; ed. King's Coll., Lond.; lieut., B.W.I.R.; war serv., 1916-19; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1922; clk. of cts., Jamaica, May, 1929; res. mag., Jan., 1933; ag. judge, Kingston et al., Jan.-Dec., 1933.

RENNIE, GILBERT MCCALL, M.C., M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1895; on mil'y. serv., July, 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Anuradhapura Kachcheri, June, 1920; attd. to N'Elia Kachcheri, Nov., 1920; office asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Nov., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Dec., 1923; ditto, Chilaw, Mar., 1924; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1925; office asst. to dir., med. and sany. services, Apr., 1926; sec. min. for health, July, 1931; contr. finance and supply, Sept., 1933.

RENWICK, HENRY LANGDON.—B. 1898; London Rifle Brig., Apr., 1915; attd. gen. staff, W.O., 1919; ch. clk. to H.M.T.C. in E. Africa, Dec., 1919; asst. imigrn. and embarkation offr., Mombasa, July, 1920; asst. cust. dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1920; imigrn. offr., Aug., 1921; ag. asst. compt., cust., for various periods, 1923-34; ag. asst. treas., May, 1932-May, 1933; aviation control offr., Aug., 1932; ag. compt., cust., Feb.-Aug., 1934.
RETIEF, D. J., B.A.—B. 1882; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; prin., Boshof, 1913; prin., Winburg, 1921; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1927.

REX, MARCUS.—B. 1886; ed. Highgate and Trin. Coll., Camb. (exhbr.); B.A., 1908; cadet, F.M.S., 1910; asst. sec. to res., Perak, 1912 and 1915; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangar, 1913; sup't, chandu monopoly, Sel., N.S. and Pahang, 1917; dist. offr., Raub, 1919; mag., K. Lumpur, June, 1921, sec., retrenchment comm., in addn., Feb., 1922; dep. contr. of rubber expts., in addn., Oct., 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1922; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1927; officg. comanr., trade and cust., Johore, offr., cls. IB, Aug., 1930; finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Jan., 1932; offr., cls. IA, June, 1932; contr., rubber, Malaya, Mar., 1934.

REY, CHARLES FERNAND, C.M.G. (1932)—B. 1877; W. Africa, 1900; joined B. of T. 1900; sec. to China Tariff comm., 1902; to Imp. Inst. Advisory Comtee. and Imp. Inst. Trustees, 1903; to All Red Route Comtee., 1904; Roumanian Treaty Negotiations, 1905; mission to Constantinople and Sofia, 1905; Swiss Treaty negotiations, 1906; gen. man., lab. exchange and unemployment insurance, 1909; asst. gen. sec., min'y. of munitions, 1916; dir. employment dept., B. of T., 1917; dir.-gen., national lab. supply, min'y. of national service, 1918; chmn. and Br. rep., Inter-Allied comm., Rotterdam, for food supply to Germany; seconded for serv. in Abyssinia, 1919; further journeys to Abyssinia, 1922-3, 1925-6, 1926-7; unemployment grants comm., 1920-29; asst. res. comanr., Bechuanaland Prot., Oct., 1929; res. comanr., June, 1930; F.R.G.S., commdr., Order of the Star of Ethiopia; author of "Unconquered Abyssinia as it is to-day," 1923; "In the country of the Blue Nile," 1927; "The Romance of the Portuguese in Abyssinia," 1929; "The Lady or the Leopard," 1929; contributions to the Royal Geographical Jnl. and the Jnl. of the African Society.
REYNOLDS, A. E.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1900; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918.
RHODES, ALEX LEOPOLD.—B. 1884; gen. foreman, govt. printing and stationery dept., Trinidad 1921; typography instr., bd. of instr. training, 1922; mem., bd. indu. training, 1925; govt. printer, 1925.

RHODES, HON. E. N., P.C. (Can.), K.C. B.A., LL.B., D.C.L.—B. 1877; ed. Amherst Acad., Acadia Univ. and Dalhousie Univ.; apptd. C. Nova Scotia, May, 1916; 1st el. to H. of C. Canada at g.e., 1908; el. dep. speaker, H. of C., Feb., 1916; speaker, 1917; re-el. Mar., 1918; el. to legis., N.S., 1923; prov. premier and prov. sec., 1925; re-el. at g.e., 1928; joined Dominion cabinet of Bennett admstr., as min. of fisheries, Aug. 1930; min. of finance, 1932.
RHODES, BRIG.-GEN. SIR GODFREY DEAN, K.C. Bach. (1934), C.B.E., D.S.O., R.R.(ret.), M.E.I.C., M.Inst.T., A.M.I.C.E.—Ch. engnr., Kenya and Uganda Rly., 1920; gen. man., 1928.

RHODES, HON. SIR ROBERT HEATON, K.C.V.O. (1927), K.B.E. (1920).—B. 1851; B.A. Oxford; entd. New Zealand parlmt., 1899; served in S. African war (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); lt.-col., 1st Regt., Canterbury Rifles Yeomanry Cavalry; postmgr.-gen., min. of telegraphs, min. of pub. health, 1912-1915; min. of defence, 1920-26; commr., state forests, 1922; leader of leg. coun. and mem., exec. coun. without portfolio, 1926; dep. leader, leg. coun., 1927; ret. from office, 1928 and from leg. coun., 1932; re-apptd., M.L.C., 1934.

RHODES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Oxon.).—B. 1884; ed. Bromsgrove Sch., Cambridge Univ. and St. Thomas' Hosp.; sen. govt. pathologist, Cape Town, and offr.-in-charge, govt. vaccine inst., Cape Town.
RICE, HARRY PATRICK, O.B.E. (1934).—R. 1886; S.A. constabl., 1906-15; K.A.R., 1916-17; asst. supt. pol., Kenya, Nov., 1919; supt., 1924; dep. commdt., pol. (C.I.D.), Palestine, 1925.
RICE, J. A., B.A., LL.B., (T.C.D.)—B. 1904; ed. Clongowes; admitted solr. sup. ct., I.F.S.; asst. comanr., lands, lands dept., Gold Coast, 1927.
RICH, HON. SIR GEO. EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1932).—Senr. puiane just. of the high court of Australia, Apr., 1913.
RICHARDS, ARTHUR COURTNEY.—B. 1896; 2nd asst. govt. printer, Ceylon, Sept., 1920; asst. govt. printer, Apr., 1923; govt. printer, Apr., 1932.

RICHARDS, ARTHUR FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1933), B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1885; ed. Clifton Coll. and Christ Ch., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; offr., cls. V, May, 1913; asst. dist. offr., Krian, June, 1913; mag., cent. ct., Kota Bharu, and state audr., Aug., 1915; adviser, land offr., Kedah, and ag. supt. of mines, June, 1916; offr., cls. IV., Jan. 1918; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1920; 1st asst. col. sec., S. Stiltna, and ag. clk. of couns., in addn., Apr., 1921; gen. sec., S. retrenchment comm., in addn., Jan., 1922; on sp. duty, London, May, 1923; sec. open comm., Br. Malaya in addn., Nov., 1923; chmn., exec. comtee., Br. Malaya, Br. Empire Exhibn., in addn., 1924; offr., cls. IA, under-mt. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1927; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Nov., 1929; gov., N. Borneo, Mar., 1930; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Gambia, Nov., 1930.

RICHARDS, DAVID MORGAN, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1892; ed. Christ Ch. Cathedral Schl., Oxford, St. David's Coll. Schl., Lampeter, S. Wales and non-collegiate, Oxford; war. serv., 1917-19; mast., educ. dept., F.M.S., Apr., 1921; ditto, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; ag. head mast., Central Br. Schl., 1922 and 1932; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., 1934.

RICHARDS, FRANCIS OTHIENI.—B. 1882; vol. 6th cls. clk., postal dept., Br. Guiana, 1905; cler. ast., genl. reg. office, 1907; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1910; 5th cls., 1911; 4th cls., 1916; 3rd cls., 1924; 2nd cls., 1925; 1st cls., 1926; ch. clk., cust., 1927.
RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.D.—Crown sol., S. Australia, 1916; formerly parliamentary draftsman; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1925; judge, ditto, 1927.
RICHARDS, P. E.—B. 1901; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 20th Dec., 1928.

RICHARDSON, ALEXANDER STEWART, B.Sc. (Agr.).—B. 1897; ed. Burgh and County High Schl., Peebles; mily. serv., R. Arty., 1916-19; B.Sc., Edinburgh Univ., 1923; dist. agril. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1924; senr. agril. offr., 1930.

RICHARDSON, CECILY.—B. 1889; ed. The Laurels, Rugby and Royal Holloway Coll.; B.A., London, 1912; ast. mistress, govt. girls' schl., Penang, 1914; ast. mistress, Raffles girls' schl., Singapore, 1915; ag. prin., 1920 and 1924; prin., St. George's girls' schl., Penang, Sept., 1928.

RICHARDSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR GEORGE SPAFFORD, K.B.E. (1925), C.B. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1867; enlisted in Royal Artillery, Aug., 1886; attached to N.Z. Forces as instructor in gunnery with rank of master gunner, Aug., 1891; capt., N.Z. militia and dir. of artillery services (N.Z.), Apr., 1907; transf. to N.Z. staff corps, Jan., 1911; major, 1912; lieut.-col., 1915; col., 1919, and brig.-gen. with seniority as from Dec., 1916; N.Z. representative at War Office, London, and attached Impl. gen. staff, Dec., 1913; attached to Admity. staff, Aug., 1914; attached Royal Naval Divn. as ast. adjutant and qtrmr., Nov., 1914; active service at Antwerp with Royal Naval Divn.; subsequently served as A.A. and Q.M.G. on Gallipoli; selected as D.A. and Q.M.G. of an Army Corps at Salonika with temporary rank of brig.-gen.; comdt., N.Z.E.F. in U.K., Mar., 1916; G.O. in charge admtn., N.Z. Forces, May, 1919; admtr., W. Samoa, 1923-28; also admtr., Tokelau Is., 1926-28; rep. N.Z. before mandates committee, L. of N., 1928; Legion of Honour (Croix de Chevalier) conferred by Pres. of the French Republic, 1916; ment. in despatches (3).

RICHARDSON, ROBERT BOOTH.—B. 1902; ed. Lancing Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A.; 2nd cls. juris-prudence, 1923; called to bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1927; cadet, Tanganyika Territory adminstr., May, 1924; ast. sec., Dec., 1924; attd. as ast. prin., C.O., Feb. to Aug., 1927; ag. crown coun. in 1929 and 1932; ag. ast. admtr. gen. and regis., companies, 1929; ag. res. mag., 1934; sec., Tanganyika soil erosion and gold mining industry comitcees.

RICHES, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1885; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915 to Mar., 1919; 2nd lieut., London Scottish, Sept., 1917; apptd. to army audit staff, Eastern command, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 17th July, 1922.

RIDDELL, WALTER ALEXANDER, M.A., PH.D.—Supt., trades and lab. dept. of pub. wks., Ontario govt., 1916; dep. min. of lab., Ontario, 1919; ch. of agril. serv., Internat. Lab. Office, L. of N. Jan., 1920; Dominion of Canada advisory offr. to L. of N., Geneva, 1925.

RIDDELL, HON. WILLIAM RENWICK.—B. 1852; ed. Cobourg, Ont., Coll. Inst. and Victoria Univ., Toronto; lecturer in maths., Normal Schl., Ottawa, 4 years; called to bar, 1883; Q.C., 1897; bencher, Law Soc., Upper Can., since 1892; judge, high ct., Ont., King's Bench divn., 1906; judge, appellate divn. (second divt. et.), 1913; hon. mem., American Bar Assoc. and of Bar Assocs. of several states; F.R. Hist. Soc., England; F.R.S.C., Can.; LL.D., Toronto, Yale, McMaster, Lafayette, Rochester, Wesleyan, North Western Univ.; L.H.D., Syracuse Univ.; J.U.D., Trinity Coll. (Toronto); author of several legal and hist. works; has contrib. extensively to hist., scientific and literary jnl.

RIDGWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.B. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—B. 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-sec., foreign dept., India, 1881-5; commr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895-1903; chmn. of comm. sent to S. Africa on Transvaal constitution, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comitce. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn., st. of dirs., Br. N. Borneo Co., 1910.
RIDGWAY, T. R. J.—Ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923; supt., pol., 1935.

RIGBY, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Bury Gram. Schl.; served in France, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1918 and June, 1918-Nov., 1919; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1920; attchd., Chinese Prot., Pernang, Dec., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1923; ast. supt. govt. monops., S'pore and ast. contrlr., rubber, Mar., 1926; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1927; 3rd mag., S'pore, June, 1930; inspr., prisons, in addn., Sept., 1931; relief ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1932, offr., cls. III; ast. Malayan estabmt. offr., Sept., 1933.

RIGNELL, JOHN HERBERT.—B. 1886; Scots Guards, 1904-25; mily. prov., staff corps, 1911; warrant offr., cl. I., 1920-25; physical training instr., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1915-19; physical training instr., Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, Sept., 1925.
RIMINGTON, CAPT. G.B.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

RINFERT, HON. THIBAUDEAU.—B. 1879; ed. St. Mary's Coll. (B.A., 1897), McGill Univ. (B.C.L., 1900); called to Quebec bar, 1901; K.C., 1912; min. of roads in Quebec Govt., 1910-22; prof., munici. law and law of public utilities, McGill Univ.; judge, sup. ct., Canada, Sept., 1924.
RING, JULIUS, B.Sc. (Eng.) (Birm.), F.S.I., Chem. Surveyor, A.M.I.Struc.E. — B. 1892; engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1925; ast. assessor, rates, 1928; 1st ast. do., 1933.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1895; lieut., R.A.V.C., Jan., 1917; serv. in Salonics, Macedonia, and with Army of Black Sea; capt., Jan., 1918; demob., Jan., 1921; vety. offr., Gold Coast, May, 1921.

RISLEY, SIR JOHN SHUCKBURGH, K.C.M.G. (1922), K.C. (1921), C.B. (1912)—B. 1867; ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitioner); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inns of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L., 1894; author of "The Founding of St. Stephen's Golf Club," 1893; "The Law of War," 1897; "Notes and Echoes," 1902; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901; legal adviser, 19th May, 1911.
RITCHIE, Capt. A.T.A., M.C., B.A. (Oxon) — Game warden, Kenya, 1923.
RITCHIE, J.D.—Clerk, agric. dept., Kenya, Aug., 1912; head clerk and acct., cust. dept., Sept., 1922.
RITCHIE, T.M.—B. 1896; foreman of wks., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 1926; inspr. of wks., 1928; mast., Accra Tech. Sch., 1930.

ROBERTS, ARTHUR HALLAM, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1888; ed. Aldenham schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1914; cadet, Fiji, Jan., 1915; priv. sec. to gov., Mar., 1915; ag. 1st cls. clk., col. sec's office and clk. to exec. and leg. coun., Jan., 1916; passed cadet, June, 1916; priv. sec., Oct., 1916; 2nd grade dist. commr., Oct., 1916; ag. crown solr., July, 1917; assigned dist. of Savu Savu, Aug., 1917; act. serv., June, 1918; dist. commr., Navua, Dec., 1919; resigned, Oct., 1920; el. mem., leg. coun., 1923-26; regisr., sup. ct., taxing master, curator, pub. trustee and off. recr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1927; ag. atty. gen. in 1931 and 1932; ch. pol. mag., Aug., 1931.
ROBERTS, GEORGE.—B. 1886; ent. Admy., 1902; seconded as exec. engnr., Falkland Is., 1925; col. engnr., 1928; mem., leg. coun., 1929; title of office altered to dir., pub. wks., 1931.

ROBERTS, GEORGE F.C.B.—B. 1881; ed. Univ. Coll., London; telegraphist, G.P.O., Sept., 1897; 2nd div. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Apr., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1905; off. ast. to gov't. agt., Uva Prov., Apr., 1907; pol. mag., Panadure, Jan., 1909; Jaffna, Dec., 1909; ast. gov't. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, and commr. of requests and pol. mag., Nuwara Eliya-Hatton, May, 1912; pol. mag., Galle, Mar., 1915; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1916; tea commr., Nov., 1918; contr., estate supplies, in addn. to his own duties, May, 1919; commr., requests, Colombo Nov., 1921; rubber contr., Oct., 1922; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Nov., 1925; ditto, Negombo, Nov., 1926; registr.-gen. and registr., motor cars, Nov., 1928; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, June, 1931.

ROBERTS, JOHN EDWIN THEOPHILUS.—Ed. Mico Coll., Jamaica; head teacher, St. Mary's Sch., St. Kitts; head teacher, Road Town Wes. Sch., Virgin Is.; clk. to commr., Virgin Is., Mar., 1917; ag. educn. dist. offr., Oct., 1919; Feb., 1920; educn. dist. offr., Mar., 1920; clk. to mag., and bailiff, sup. ct., Apr., 1921; commr. to admin. oaths, July, 1921.

ROBERTS, JOHN HOLLAND, B.A., M.I.M.E.—B. 1886; ed. Tonbridge Sch., 1900-04 and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (B.A. mech. sciences); ast. engnr., P.W.D., Perak, 1912; ast. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1914; exec. engnr., 1921; senr. exec. engnr., 1930; ast. state engnr., Perak, 1933.
ROBERTS, M.A.W., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Trinity Coll., Dublin), F.R.C.S. (Ireland).—Med. offr., Kenya, July, 1929.

ROBERTS, PERCY HAMER.—Prison offr., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1901; served penal settlmt, Massaruni, and at Essequibo and Georgetown county prisons; clerical ast., Georgetown and Essequibo prisons; gaoler, G. Coast, Mar., 1911; keeper, James Fort prison, June, 1911.

ROBERTS, THOMAS WEBB.—B. 1889; ed. Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; pol. mag., Matara, Aug., 1903; Panadure, Sept., 1906; dist. judge, Chilaw, Feb., 1909; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, May, 1912; commr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1914; commr. of excise, Mar., 1917; excise commr., Aug., 1920; dist. judge, Galle, Sept., 1927.
ROBERTS, W. P.—B. 1893; Out Island commr., grade IV, Bahamas, 1923; grade III, 1926; grade II, 1927.

ROBERTS, WILLIAM RODICK.—B. 1889; war serv., three years, nine months, R.N. and R.N.A.S.; gaol tech. instr., prisons dept., G. Coast, Jan., 1921; mast., Accra tech. sch., Oct., 1923; seconded to prisons dept., Oct. to Nov., 1923; housemast., Apr., 1926.

ROBERTS-WRAY, KENNETH OWEN.—B. 1890; R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich, 1917-18; commr. I. Arty., Jan., 1918; served in France, 1918; 1920; Merton Coll., Oxford (latcs. jurisprudence, 1922); M.A.; certif. of hon., bar final exam., 1923; called to bar, Jan., 1924; S. Eastern circ.; professional legal clk., Miny. of Health, Oct., 1926; ag. ast. ch. clk., Jan., 1929; 2nd ass't. legal adviser, D.O. and C.O., 1st July, 1933; part author of "The Law of Collisions & Land."
ROBERTSON, COLIN BROWN.—B. 1863; ed. Leven higher grade; architect, P.W.D., H.Kong, 1920.

ROBERTSON, IAN DONALD, A.M.I.C.E.—Chartered Civil Engnr.—B. 1886; ast. exec., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jly., 1914; ast. exec., P.W.D., irrigation br., Aug., 1916, Aug., 1917 and Nov., 1921; dir., wks., Kelantan, Jan., 1923; exec. engnr., Jan., 1923; sr. exec. engnr., P.W.D., S.S. and F.M.S., 1933.

ROBERTSON, THOMAS ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Glenalmond M.B., Ch.B., Edin.; former house surgeon and physician, Swansea hosp.; Victoria Park hosp. for chest diseases; surgeon, S. African Field Force, 1901-2 (medals, three clasps); ast. commr. and gen. med. of Salt Cay, Turks Is., 1906; dist. commr. and med. offr., Caicos Is., 1905; gen. med. of Grand Turk, 1909.

ROBERTSON, Sir W. C. F., K.C.M.G. (Sir), C.M.G. (1919).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitor, 1886 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1889; inspr., educn. Coast, 1898; dir., educn., 1899; ag. commr., educn., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901; ag. nat. affairs, June to Nov., 1905; prov. commr., Nov., 1905; sec. for nat. affairs, May, 1906; ag. coll. sec., July to Sept., 1908; ed. nat. coll. sec., Jan., 1909; ag. coll. sec., 1910, 1911, 1912, 1913; deputy gov., Jan., 1910; dep. gov., Dec., 1912, and April to May, 1913; dep. gov., May to Aug., 1914; dep. gov., Aug. to Sept., 1914; coll. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1915; heat. and chief sec. to gov't., Malta, May, 1917; adm'tg. gov't., Malta, for various periods, 1917-19; gov't. dep., in Oct., 1920; lieut. gov't. by comm. under new constitution, Apr., 1921; gov't. for various periods in 1920 and 1922; LL.D., Malta Univ., 1925; gov't., Barbados, 1925; attended C.O. confees., 1927 and 1930, ret., 1932.
ROBERTSON, WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Palmerston Dist. High Sch., N.Z.; N.Z. dept. of educn. and survey, 1892; inspr., 1919; ast. under-seer., 1925; under-seer., 1931.

ROBIN, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ALFRED WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1916), C.B. (1900).—B. 1861; ed. privately and at Otago high schl.; N. Zealand volr. offr.; comd. squadn., N. Zealand mtd. rifles, Queen Victoria's diamond jub., 1897; comd. 1st N. Zealand contngt., S. Africa, 1899-1901; comdt., Otago mil. dis., 1902-07 (brev. col.); chief of gen. staff and 1st mil. mem. of coun. of defense, 1907-11 (col.); adjt.and qtrmr.-gen., 1911-12; represented N. Zealand on Imperial gen. staff, War Off., 1912-13; comdt. and G.O.C., N. Zealand mil. forces, 1914-19; maj.-gen., 1916.

ROBINSON, CAPT. ARTHUR GORDON, B.So. (Eng.), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. St. Asaph gram. schl., N. Wales and Birmingham Univ.; asst. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1912; seconded, Kodah, July, 1912; B.E.F., France, Mar., 1917-Jan., 1919; capt., commanding Indian lab., lieut., R.E.; ex. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., June, 1921; sen. ex. engrnr., pub. works dept., F.M.S., July, 1925; ch. hydraulic engrnr., 1930; asst. advr., drainage and irrig., Malay States, Jan., 1932.

ROBINSON, AUBREY CHARLES.—B. 1875; ed. Lancing Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1901; practised at the bar, Jamaica, 1901 to 1911; commnr., and judge of grand ct., Cayman Is., Jan., 1913; compd. revised edition of Laws of Cayman Is. (published deferred during the war); regsr.-gen., Bahamas, Jan., 1918; perf. duties of S. and C. mag. in July and Dec., 1918 and from Mar., 1919; regsr.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919; ag. regsr., sup't, ct. and marshal, June to Sept., 1920; res. mag., Jamaica, Sept., 1923; regsr., sub. ct. and regsr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1928; 2nd puisne judge, Aug., 1931.
ROBINSON, GEORGE GILMOUR.—B. 1894; ed. Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxon., B.A., M.A.; war serv., 1914-19 (France); called to bar, Inner Temple, 1924; practised, 1924-30; res. mag., Kenya, 1930.
ROBINSON, INNES LOVELL.—B. 1904; ed. Marlborough and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (2nd cls. hon. classics); cadet, Tanganyika, 1927; asst. dist. offr., 1929; ag. dist. offr., 1932.

ROBINSON, MONTGOMERY BLAIR.—B. 1878; B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1901; LL.B., Univ. of Cape of Good Hope, 1909; clk. law dept., Cape, 1903; mag., 1921; ch. clk., dept. of just., 1929; addnl. mag., Pretoria, June, 1931.

ROBINSON, S.—B. 1893; ed. Barnsley Gram. Schl. and Univ. of Sheffield; B.A., 1914; 1st cls. hon. mod. langs.; M.A., 1919; on milly. serv., 1914-19; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, junr. clk., bd. of cust. and excise, Sept., 1919; asst. prin., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd to C.O. as ast. priv. sec. (appts.) to successive secs. of Lord's 23-24; ast. pvtc. sec. to Lord's past-field, Sept., 1929; prin., 1st Oct., 1930.

ROBINSON, Lieut.-COL. SIR THOMAS BILBE, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1910), K.B.E. (1918).—B. 1853; formerly major in command of 1st Queenslanders, and senior offr. in Cent. Queensland; man. and dir. of various public companies; J.P., Queensland, 1887; pres. of Rockhampton chamber of commerce; Queensland commnr. for Franco-British exhibn.; agt.-gen. for Queensland, 1910; mem. of comte, for settm't. of ex-serv. men within the Empire, 1917.

ROCHE, Hon. WILLIAM JAMES, C.M.G. (1934), M.D., LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. pub. schl., Lucan, Ontario, and high schl., London, Ontario, Trinity med. coll., Toronto; grad. M.D., with 1st cl. hon., Western Univ., London, Ontario, 1883; 1st grad. in medicine of that Univ.; LL.D., 1911; removed 1883 to Minnedosa, Man., where he is a practising physician; territorial rep. for Manitoba med. coun., 1885-1901; has held highest offices in I.O.O.F., Manitoba; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1896; re-elec. g.e. 1900, 1904, 1908 and 1911; conservative whip for the West, 1901-1911; mem. of the privy coun. for Canada, and sec. of state in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; min. of the interior, 1912; chmn., civ. serv. comm., 1917.

ROCK, ALFRED MILTON.—B. 1888; ag. 3rd outdoor offr. and harmbr., treasry., Dominica, 1913-16; clk. and interpreter to mag., dist. "E," Mar., 1915; clk., mag., regist. and provost marshall, "A," commnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Montserrat, Sept., 1915; clk. to mag., dist. "A" and "B," dep. coroner, Antigua, Nov., 1920; senr. clk., regist.'s office, clk., sup. ct. law lib., "A" commnr. of oaths, dep. coroner, Dominica, May, 1927; prin. for duty as regist. and provost marshal, regist. of titles, admr. of estates and regist.-gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Dominica, Apr., 1929.

ROCK, JOHN CHRISTOPHER WILBERFORCE.—B. 1882; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados and Jesus Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1905; off. asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., June, 1906; Sabaragamuwa, Jan., 1908; pol. mag., Panadure, Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Nov., 1911; dep. commnr. of excise, May, 1921; dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1925; ditto, Galle, Mar., 1926; dep. commnr., stamps, July, 1927; dist. judge, Jaffna, Feb., 1928; regist. gen. and regist., motor cars, Sept., 1930; excise commnr., June, 1931; regist.-gen., etc., June, 1933; offr. cls. I, grade I, 1934.

RODD, A. J.—B. 1886; apptd., after exam., asst. library attendant, C.O., 11th Dec., 1905; library attendant, 18th Feb., 1911; supplementary clk., col. audit dept., Apr., 1914; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug., 1914 to 1919; regist., 1st Apr., 1928.

RODEN, SIR ROBERT BLAIR, KT. BACH. (1918).—B. 1860; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; clk., public library, Antigua, 1880; 2nd clk. to the gov., Leeward Is., 1881; acted on several occasions as ch. clk., and as clk. to the legis. and exec. couns., and as a dist. mag., Antigua; also acted as priv. sec. to different govts., Leeward Is., in 1883, 1890, 1895 and 1896; mag., Nevis, 1890; mem. St. Kitts and Nevis legis. couns., 1891; mem., fed. leg. coun., Leeward Is., 1898-9; mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1903; visiting justice, Glendairy pris., 1904; ch. just., St. Vincent, 1912; adminstr. govt. of St. Vincent from Oct., 1912 to Feb., 1913, and for short periods in Apr., Aug., and Dec., 1913; also from Mar. to Nov., 1914; ch. just., Br. Honduras, Feb., 1915; chmn. of food control comte., Jan., 1918; ret., 1921.

RODGER, ROBERT, A.M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Mech.E., A.M.Inst.M. & Cy.E., M.I.Struc.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1893; ed. Wishaw High Schl., Wishaw Tech. Schl. and Glasgow Tech. Coll. (affiltd.); on active serv. with Lanarkshire Yeo., (Lowld. Mtd. Bde., Scot., T.F.) 1914-20; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Palestine, 1924; road engrnr., P.W.D., Gold Coast, on Tarmet' Scheme 1926; road engrnr., P.W.D., Nigeria, on road development schemes, 1930; road engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1930; ag. ast. dir., pub. wks., in 1931 and 1932.

RODRIGO, EDMUND.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attd., Kurunegala Kachcheri, Mar., 1913; office ast. to govt. agt., W. Prov., May, 1915; mun. mag., Colombo, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Puttalum, July, 1917; office ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1918; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Apr., 1920; ditto, Matara, May 1922 asst. comsnr., excise, Sept., 1924; dep. comsnr., excise, Sept., 1927; contr., finance and supplies, July, 1931; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, July, 1932; agt. regt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1933.

RODWELL, Sir CECIL HUNTER, G.C.M.G., (1934), K.C.M.G., (1919), C.M.G. (1909).—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; serv. on staff of high comsnr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904; gov., Fiji, and high comsnr., W. Pacific, 1918; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., B. Guiana, 1925; gov. and commdr.-in-oh., Southern Rhodesia, Sept., 1928; ret., Dec., 1934.

ROE, ROBERT JESTIN, M.R.C.V.S. (Williams Memorial Prize), D.V.S.M.—B. 1897; ed. Drogheda and R. Vety. Coll., Ireland; war serv., 1915-16; vety. surg., Cyprus, June, 1921; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Jan., 1923; del. to Imp. Agric. Research Conf., 1927; ag. dir., animal husbandry, July-Oct., 1928; title changed to ch. vety. offr., Jan., 1930; rep. govt. Internat. Dairy Cong., Copenhagen, 1931.

ROGERS, FRANCIS MERVYN.—B.1890; student, R. Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1915; milv. serv., 1916-19; sub-foreman, Kew, 1919-20; head gardener, Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; supt. plantations, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, 1927.
ROGERS, WALTER ERNEST.—B. 1864; ent. civ. serv., S. Australia, 1882; 2nd clk., audit dept., 1912; audr.-gen., S. Aust., 1923.

ROGERSON, WALTER JOHN LANCASHIRE, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attd. Kandy kach., Jan., 1913; addtl. office asst., Anuradhapura, June, 1914; office asst. to govt. agt., Badulla, Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Matale, Jan., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., Kandy, May, 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Jan., 1921; ditto, Kandy, Feb., 1921; dist. judge Kegalla, Sept., 1922; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Nov., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., N.W.P., Feb., 1930; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1931; addnl. govt. agt., W. ROLSTON, CECIL MICHELL.—M.D., C.M., Univ. of Manitoba, Canada, 1895; R.C.P., Edin., F.R.C.S., Edin.; L.R.F.P., and S., Glasgow, 1895; mem. Coll. of Phys. and Surgeons, 1903; F.R.I.; pub. health, London; late civil surg., attached to R.A.M.C. during S. African war, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal, 2 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps); apptd. to Leeward Is., med. serv., 1903; med. offr., dist. Nevis, 1914; ag. mag., Nevis, May to June, 1915; med. offr., dist. 6, Nevis, Sept., 1915.

ROOKE, CHARLES EUSTACE, M.Inst.T., F.R.S.S.—B. 1892; ed. Dover Coll.; asst. traffic man., Uganda Rly., Oct., 1914; served with Indian Expdy. Force, 1915-17; 2nd lieut., Indian Army R. of O.; asst. traffic man., F.M.S. Rly., Oct., 1919; gen. man., rly., Cyprus, Oct., 1929; traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 1935.

ROOTHAM, J. ST. J.—B. 1910; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge (class scholarship); 1st cls. class. tripso, Pts. I and II; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin. min. of agr. and fisheries, 1933; transf. to C.O., July, 1934.

ROOTS, NEVILLE, M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Tonbridge Schl. and City and Guilds Engng. Co.; S. Kensington; held various engng. assts. in U.K., 1897-1904; inspgr. engnr., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1908; asst. dir., P.W.D., 1914; ag. dir., Apr., 1920 to Sept., 1921; dep. dir., Apr., 1921; C.A. office, 1907; seconded to Falkland Is., Aug., 1912; ag. priv. sec. to adminstr. and clk. to coun., Aug., 1913; chf. clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1914; ag. local audr., Nov., 1914; retnd. to C.A. office, 1915; served European war (M.G.C.), France, 1916-1919; 1st cls. clk., Nyasaland, Jan., 1920; ag. ch. clk., July to Dec., 1921; ed., Gazette, Oct., 1921 to Dec., 1922; ag. 2nd adm. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. coun., in 1925 and 1926; organising sec., Nyasaland Vol. Res., Sept., 1926; ag. ch. transport offr., Dec., 1926 to May, 1927; office supt., secretariat, Apr., 1927; sec., govt. tender bd., Mar., 1928; clk., exec. and leg. coun., Mar., 1928; ag. staff offr., Nyasaland Vol. Res., Apr. to Nov., 1928; ag. senr. asst. sec., May, 1929 to June, 1930; ag. senr. asst. sec., May, 1929 to June, 1930; supr., census, 1930; asst. sec., Apr., 1931.

ROSE, ALAN EDWARD PERCYAL, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.).—B. 1899; ed. Aldenham Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; temp. 2nd lieut. R.F. Brig., B.E.F., 1918-19; 2nd cls. hon., law tr., Cambridge, 1921; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., Jan., 1923; mem., S.E. circuit; ch. pol. mag., Fiji, July, 1923; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1930; June, 1931; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, 1931; ag. solr. gen. in 1931 and 1932.

ROSE, HON. HUGH EDWARD.—B. 1869; ed. as Jarvis St. Collegiate Inst., Univ. of Toronto; B.A., 1891; LL.B., 1892), and Osgoode Hall; mem. of the firm of Faassen, Cowan, Chadwick and Rose; called to the bar, Ontario, 1894; K.C., 1916; judge of the sup. ct., Ontario, Dec., 1916; ch. just., high ct., Ont., 1930; examr. in law for some years for University of Toronto and the Law Society of Upper Canada.

ROSEDALE, JOHN LEWIS, Ph.D. (Bend.), D.Sc. (Aberdeen), F.I.C.—B. 1889; prof. of biochemistry, King Edward VII Medical Coll., Singapore, Apr., 1927; offr. deleg. to 8th cong. of Far Eastern Asso. of Trop. Med., Bangal., Dec., 1930.

ROSEDALE, WILLIAM OBEL PELLY.—B. 1891; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, Aug., 1914; dist. offr., 1924; asst. sec. and clk., exec. and leg. coun., 1927; ag. prin. asst. sec., 1930-31; adm. odr., class I, Oct., 1930.

ROSENOW, JACOBUS DANIEL MALAN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. Boys' Public Schl., Riversdale and Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; Cap. ser., audit office, Capetown, 1903; attr. gen. office, Capetown, 1907, various mag. offices for 12 years; clk., office of dir. prisons, 1922; prof. asst. att. gen., Natal, 1923; regist., high ct., S.W. Africa, 1926; sen. prof. asst. atty. gen., Natal, 1927; solr. gen., Grahamstown, Mar., 1928.

ROSS, CHARLES CLAYE, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1901; ed. Charterhouse and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1925; pvt. practice, 1927-34; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Aug., 1934; ag. ch. just., Aug., 1934.
ROSS, DONALD GILLON.—B. 1903; sub-insp. pol. Jamaica, 1928; 3rd cls. inspnr., Mar., 1929.

ROSS, EDWARD ANDREW, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Leigh Gram. Schl. and Manchester Univ.; active serv., 1914-19; relinquished comm., 1919; with rank of capt.; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1919; passed cadet, Dec., 1921; supernumerary offr., cls. V. May, 1923; asst. contrlr., lab., Penang, Mar., 1925; offr., cls. IV, May, 1927; attd. for sp. duty to office of offl. assignee and pub. trustee, regis. of companies and collr., est. duty, F.M.S., May, 1927; asst. contrlr., rubber, and asst. dist. judge, S'pore, Oct., 1927; 2nd asst. advr., B. Pahat and mag., Klang, Jan., 1928; asst. advr., Kukup, Sept., 1928; contrlr., lab., Johore, Jan., 1930; offr., cls. III, May, 1932; ag. dep. contr., lab., Malaya, Jan., 1934.

ROSS, GEORGE ARCHIBALD PARK, M.D., Ch.M. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.)—diploma, trop. diseases, Liverpool.—B. 1879; S. African War, Lovat's Scouts, 1900-02; Anglo-Portuguese bndry. coman., Zambesia, 1904-5; native rebellion, Natal, 1906; trop. diseases investig., 1906-10; govt. pathologist, Natal, 1911; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, statud. in Natal, 1918; offr. in ch., sleeping sickness bureau, Durban and sanitation offr., Natal, 1914-18; senr. asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, Natal, 1918; col., S.A.M.C. and A.D.M.S., No. 3 mily. dist., Union S. Africa.

ROSS, J. G. H.—Asst. dist. commnr., R.A.F., Sept., 1919; ag. junr. asst. sec., Kenya, Nov., 1919 to Oct., 1919; ag. clk. of exec. and leg. couns., Dec., 1919 to Mar., 1920; editor, offl. Gazette, Feb., 1920 to Mar., 1920.
ROSS, JAMES SANDFORD.—B. 1884; ed. Merchant Taylors Schl.; mily. serv., 1914-19; N.W. Front campaign, 1915; Salonika Field Force, 1916-19; asst. treas., Kenya, 1919.

ROSS, OSWALD F.—ed. Malvern Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; capt., N. Staffs. Regt., European War; served Dardanelles expdn., 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916; passed A.T.S. course, 1920; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1920.
ROSS-CLUNIS, ROBERT CHATTAN.—B. 1905; ed. South Africas and Westminster; 2nd lieut., N. Rhodesia pol., 1931; lieut., 1933; local commdt., pol., Cyprus, 1934.

ROUSE, HAROLD STUART, C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Truro Coll. and Christ's Hosp.; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; engnr., drainage wks. office, Deo., 1913; transf. to gen. wks. office, P.W.D., Apr., 1915; offr. in charge, G.W.O., May, 1924; sec., water emergency comtee., May to Aug., 1929; exec. engnr., roads office, P.W.D., Nov., 1931; do., drainage office, 1934; O.C., N.V.F., Feb., 1934.

ROWAN, WILLIAM ALFRED.—B. and educ. ROWE, CHARLES HAWKSWELL.—B. 1885; asst. acct., constrn., F.M.S. Rly., Feb., 1912; divisiol. acct., June, 1913-Jly., 1914; acct. and audr., constrn., head office, Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1920; stores supl., M.S.M.S. Rlys., Nov., 1926.
ROWE, ERIC GEORGE.—B. 1904; ed. Chatham House Schl., Ramsgate and St. Edmund Hall, Oxford; B.A. 1926; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1930.

ROWELL, THOMAS HUBERT.—Cik., loco. dept., I.M.R., Pretoria, Oct., 1900; acotg. brch., ch. loco. supt.'s office, Jan., 1903; acotg. offr., ch. mech. engrnr.'s dept., July, 1908; inspr., audit dept., S.A.R. and Harbrs., Oct., 1910; inspr., asst. contr. and audr. gen.'s dept., Union of S. ROWNEY, LESLIE CHARLES, M.A. (Oxon), Dip. For. (Oxon).—B. 1895; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; on active serv., Salonika and Egypt, 3rd Batt. Middlesex Regt., France, pilot, R.A.F.; asst. commvr., forests, Gold Coast, Aug., 1921; utilisation offr., Oct., 1933.

RUDD, VIVIAN HUGH THOMAS HERMAN.—B. 1882; clk., just. dept., Outdooshoorn, 1900; senr. clk., Pretoria, Apr., 1912; asst. acct., lands dept., Pretoria, Oct., 1919; 2nd gr. ch. clk., Sept., 1929; lat gr. ch. clk., Aug., 1932; under-secr. for lands, May, 1934.

RUNDLE, GEN. SIR HENRY MACLEOD LESLIE, R.A.—B. 1856; G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1914); G.C.V.O. (1911); K.C.B., 1898; K.C.M.G., 1901; D.S.O., 1897; ed. Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; entd. RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELEY, I.S.O. (1931).—Cik., commr. of rlys. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to commnr. of rlys., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901; sec., W. Australian London Agency, 3rd Nov., 1919.

RUSHTON, HERBERT HARVEY, C.B.E. (1934).—B. 1876; acot., electric light dept., Gibraltar, 1897; asst. sec. and asst. acot., munic. authority, 1902; ch. acot. and asst. sec., 1903; sec. and finan. offr., 1908 (also man., pub. electricity and water supplies, mem., comtee. on exchange and currency); asst. ch. acot., Uganda Rly., E. Africa Prot., 1915; lieut., E.A. Forces, 1915; capt., 1916; ag. dep. ch. acot., Uganda Rly., 1916 to June, 919 (also govt. rep., Nairobi munic. coun., 1918, mem., gen. manager's advisory comtee., 1919); dep. treas., Kenya-Colony, 1920 and 1921; ag. treas., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. couns. (also chmn., currency and other comtees); col. treas., Fiji, 1922 (with charge of group of depts., cust., port and marine, transport, posts, telegraphs and telephones, govt. stores, and returned soldiers' dairy stiml.); mem., exec. and leg. couns.; comsmr. of currency, mem., educn., mining and other bds.; chmn., many comtees; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1929; gov't. dep. for four periods; treas., Kenya, Mar., 1930.

RUSSELL, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ANDREW HAMILTON.—K.C.B. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1868; held comsmr. as lieut. for 5 yrs. in Imperial Army; vol. offr., New Zealand, 1900; hon. A.D.C. to H.E. the Gov. of N.Z., 1911; brig.-gen., 1914; temp. major-gen., 1915; major-gen. in command of N.Z. Div., 1916.

RUSSELL, LAURENCE ARNOLD.—B. 1888; ed. Rossall Schol. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; probationer, N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1912; asst. native comsmr., 1914; war serv., 1915-19; native comsmr., 1918; asst. mag., 1926; ag. prov. comsmr., for short periods 1929 and 1933; prov. comsmr., 1934.
RUSSELL, WILLIAM, M.C.—M.D.—B. 1888; ed. Edin. Univ.; Union of S. Africa mental hosp. serv., 1913; comsmr., mental hygiene, 1932.

RUSSELLI, SIR WILLIAM ALISON, KT. BACH. (1928), K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., LL.B., honours, 1897; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1900; equity bar, 1900-6; apptd. Crown advoc., pub. proscr., admstr.-gen., prin. reg. of docum., reg. of companies, Uganda Prot., May, 1906; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Jan., 1909; ag. chief sec. to govt., Aug., 1909, to Mar., 1910; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Mar. to May, 1910; ag. chief sec. to govt., June, 1910 to May, 1911; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., June, 1911 to June, 1912; King's advoc., Cyprus, July, 1912; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1912; joint comsmr. under Reprint of Statutes Law, 1913; edition pubd., Feb., 1914; K.C., Cyprus, 1924; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; ret. and re-apptd. as legal adv. to govt. of Malta, 1933; published, "Legislative and other Forms" (2nd edn., 1928) and "Handbook for Magistrates" (2nd edn., 1928).
RUSSSELL, WILLIAM HAYWOOTH.—B. 1894; clk., Mankaiana, Swaziland, Apr., 1915; clk., treasy., 1920; asst. acct. and asst. collr., inc. tax, 1922; acct., 1928; ag. finan. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1931.

RUSSSELL, W. J., A.I.M.M.—Ed. Royal Coll. of Science, Ireland and Cornwall Cent. Schl. of Mining; asst. inspr., mines, Nigeria, 1914; inspr., mines, 1919; ag. dep. ch. inspr. in 1923, 1925, 1926 and 1928; dep. ch. inspr., mines, 1929; ag. ch. inspr., mines, 1930-33; ch. inspr., mines, Jan., 1934.

RUTHERFOORD, JOHN WINNE CORBIE B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1888; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Merton Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsmr., S. Nigeria, 1911; seconded as asst. recr., 1914; asst. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Lagos, 1926; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1926; cls. I, grade I, admstr. serv., 1928.

RUTHERFORD, CAPT. WILLIAM, M.C. (1916), F.R.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Brighton Coll.; 2nd lieut., W. Yorks Regt., 1914; lieut., 1915; seconded, Flying Corps, 1916; capt., 1917; retd. from army, 1919; asst. dist. comsmr., G. Coast, Sept., 1921.

RYRIE, MAJ.-GEN. HON. SIR GRANVILLE DE LAUNE, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1916), V.D.—B. 1865; ed. King's Schl., Parramatta; mem., state parl., N.S.W., 3 years, mem., house of reps., C. of A., 1911-27; asst. min. for defence, 1919-22; commanded 3rd Light Horse Regt., N.S.W., for 7 yrs. with rank of lieut.-col.; served S. Africa, 1900-01 (severely wounded (Queen's med. 4 clasps)); European War, 1914-18; commanded 2nd Light Horse Brig., A.I.F., Egypt; commanded Australians divn., Syria; G.O.C., Australian Forces, Egypt (wounded twice, five times ment. in desig.); maj.-gen. commdg. 1st Cav. Divn., N.S.W., 1923-27; high comsmr. for Australia, 1927-32.
SABINE, NEVILLE WARDE, B.A.—B. 1910; ed. Chorlton-cum-Hardy and Manchester Grammar Schls. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; ed. audit dept., Feb., 1934; asst. audr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1934.

SAINT, SYDNEY JOHN, B.Sc., A.I.C.—B. 1877; ed. Beaumarister Grammar Schol., Reading Univ.; B.Sc., 1920; served with R.A.F., 1916-19; Salter research fellow, 1920-22; lect. in agrl. chemstry, Leeds Univ., 1922-26; agrl. chemist, Barbados, 1927.

SALE, GILBERT NOEL, B.Sc. (Forest Edin.)—B. 1897; ed. King Alfred's Schl., Wantage, Edinburg Univ. and Imp. Forestry Ins., Oxford; army serv., 1916-19; lieut.-R.A. SALOMONE, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. St. Ignatius Coll. and Malta Univ.; elec. for fu divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-elec., Jan., 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for industry and commerce, Aug., 1927.

SAMARAKOON, EDWIN RICHARD DE ALWYN—B. 1877; cls. V. Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1927; extra office asst., Badulla kach., Oct., 1927; office asst. to contr., rev., Feb., 1928; offr. to land comsmr., July, 1931; attld. Ratmalana kach., Nov., 1933; attld. treas., Feb., 1934.

SAMPSON, BASIL FEHRSSEN, M.C.—B. 1888; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown; M.B., B.S. Lond. Univ. (Guy's Hosp.), 1917; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1915; temp. surg., R.N. (H.M.S. Benbow), 1915; tempy. capt., S.A.M.C., France, 1916; M.C., 1918; astt. bacteriologist, Guy's Hosp.; senr. govt. pathologist, Union of S. Africa health dept., Durban, 1931.

SAMUEL, Rt. Hon. Sir Herbert Louis, P.C. (1908), G.B.E. (1920), M.A.—B. 1870; ed. University Coll., Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P., Cleveland Divn., N. SANDERS, JOHN OWEN, M.I.T., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1892; ed. Elstow Sohl. and Manchester Univ.; mil. ser., 1915-20; 2nd lieut., 1917; ag. capt., 1918; astt., Crewe loco. works, L.M.S.R., 1922; works man., F.M.S. Rly., 1924; run. aspt., 1927; traffic man., Nov., 1930; transportation man., 1932.
SANDERSON, I., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.). D.T.M., D.T.H. (Liv.)—B. 1894; milly. serv., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.

SANDFORD, FOLLIOTT HUGH BLAKELOCK.—B. 1889; ed. Rugby and Magdalen Coll., Oxford, (demy and Shepperd exhib.) M.A.; barrister-at-law; astt. dist. comsnr., Uganda, 1913; dist. comsnr., 1923; senr. dist. offr., 1933.

SANDFORD, GEORGE RITCHIE, O.B.E. (1924), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple, 1924.—B. 1892; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Queens' Coll., Camb., B.A. and wrangler, 1914; astt. dist. comsnr., E. SANSON, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Plymouth and Munnamed Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; pol. prob., 1905; registr. of criminals, F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; astt. comsnr. of pol., Ipoh, 1911; adjt., F.M.S. pol., 1913; comanr. of pol., Johore, 1921; aspt., pol., S'pore but cont. former appt., Nov., 1924; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Nov., 1930; comanr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1931.

SARAVANAMUTTU, P.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., local divn., Aug., 1919; attd., Colombo, kach., Aug., 1919; attd. to Matale Kach., June, 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala, June, 1922; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1923; addnl. ast. dir., educn., Dec., 1925; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1929; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1932; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, Apr., 1933; rubber contr., May, 1934.
SARGEANT, CAPT. R. G., M.B.E. (1928), A.I.N.A., F.R.G.S.—Port. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1917; port capt., 1925.

SAUL, SAUL ISAAC.—B. 1887; ed. privately; shorthand writer, off. of dir-gen. of educn., Simla, Mar., 1906; ag. confd.; shorthand writer to the Viceroy, July, 1912; coun. reporter, Imp. leg. coun., Simla, Feb., 1913; on staff of Viceroy, Aug., 1914; coun. reporter Burma and per. astt. to lieut.-gov. of Bengal, June, 1916; European coun. reporter, S.S. & F.M.S., May, 1921; do., S.S., May, 1922.

SAUNDERS, SIR CHARLES JAMES RENAULT, K.C.M.G., 1906, C.M.G., 1895.—B. 1857; held various appointments in Natal civ. ser., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (desps.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bndry. between Amatongaland and Zululand, 1889 (desps.); variously employed under Zululand Govt., 1883-95 (desps.), when he was apptd. spec. comsnr. for Trans-Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. comsnr. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag. and civ. comsnr. for prov. of Zululand on its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Coman., 1902, and comsnr. for native affairs, 1905; ret., 1909.

SAUNDERS, PHILLIP HENRY NORMAN.—B. 1890; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; successively 6th, 5th, 4th, 3rd and 2nd cls. clk., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1911-24; sec., bd. of industrial training, 1930; acctg. clk., P.W.D., 1931; sec., advisory comte. on sea defences, 1932; sec., sea defence bd., 1933.

SAUNDERS-JONES, DAVID WILLOUGHBY.—B. 1899; ed. Christ Coll., Brecon; Indian Army, 1917-23; on service in Waziristan, Egypt and Palestine; wounded in Palestine, 1919; astt. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924; ag. ptve. sec., Br. res. in 1926 and from Aug., 1930-May, 1931; ag. prov. comsnr., Pemba, June, 1930 and July, 1932-Feb., 1933.

SAVAGE, GEORGE.—B. 1887; supt. of regt., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1914; supt. of mails, ditto, Jan., 1915; supt. mails, Penang, but cont. ag. senr. supt. mails, July, 1917; supt., grade II., Jan., 1919; supt., grade I., Jan., 1920; supt., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1920; contr., P. and T., S’pore, Oct., 1926; senr. acct., G.P.O., S’pore, contd. to act as contr., P. and T., Jan., 1929; contr., P. and T., F.M.S., Aug., 1929; ag. P.M.G., Sept., 1929; P.M.G., Johore, July, 1930.

SAVARY, JOHN LEWIS HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1884; col. schol., Trinidad, 1901; Gray’s Inn, 1902; prizeman in evidence, procedure and criminal law exam., 1904; barrister-at-law, Nov., 1905; practised at bar, Trinidad, 1906-24; retainer from Trinidad govt. as junr. coun. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1916-Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen., in 1921, 1922 and 1923; register and marshal and registrar gen. and prin. registr., West Indian ct. of appeal, Jan., 1925; ag. puisne judge in 1925 and 1926; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1928; ag. ch. just. in 1932 and 1934.

SAVI, VICTOR GEORGE.—B. 1884; pol. prob., S.S., Nov., 1904; asst. supt. pol., Jan., 1908; do., Penang, Oct., 1917; dir., crim. intell., Jan., 1920; dep. comsnr., pol., Perak, Mar., 1922; supt., pol., S’pore, Oct., 1931.
SAVILE, ARTHUR HUGH, Dip. Agr. (Wye), A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1906; ed. Clifton Coll., Wye Agr. Coll. and C.O. Sch., course Oxford and Trinidad; dist. agric. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1928.

SAWARD, CAPT. SIDNEY CARMION, M.C. (1916), M.M. (1916).—B. 1889; fellow of surveyors’ inst.; served with 509 (1-1) Lond. Field Coy., R.E., 6th divn.; home serv., Aug. to Dec., 1914; France and Germany, Dec., 1914 to June, 1919; Ireland, June to Aug., 1919; commdg., cadre, 12th Field Co., R.E.; command., Sept., 1915; lieut., June, 1916; capt., Oct., 1918; ag. major, Dec., 1918 to Jan., 1919; ment. in desps., Mar., 1919; survr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; field supervisor Apr., 1924; ag. dep. survr.-gen. on various occasions, 1925-32; ag. survr.-gen., Feb.-June, 1927; asst. survr.-gen., Apr., 1929.

SAWTELL, WILLIAM GEORGE.—B. 1894; ed. at King Edward VI’s Sch., Bath, and H.M.S. “Worcester”; H.M. Transport Serv., 1915-17; Indian govt. tel. ship, 1917-18; Indian Army R. of O., 1918-22; wharf-mast. and pilot, Tanganyika Territory, 1922; marine offr., 1932.

SAXTON, SIDNEY WARING.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Schl., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., July, 1905; paymr.-lieut.-comdr., Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter, lent to C.O. and apptd. asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptmt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Apr., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Accra, and ag. trav. comsnr. for Accra-Akwapim and Birrim dists., Oct., 1919; invalidated, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part); barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1925; promoted pay commdr., ret. list, 1927; dep. prov. comsnr., 1930.

SAYER, GROFFERT ROBLEY, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Highgate schl. (foundationer) and Queen’s Coll., Oxford; open exhibr. in classics and Fitzgerald exhibr.; 2nd cls. class. mods.; 3rd cls. lit. hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; asst. dist. offr. (South), 1912 to 1913; ag. asst. supt. pol., 1913 to 1914; ag. dep. offr. recvr., 1914 to 1915; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1914 and 1915, in addn. to other duties; priv. sec. to gov., in addn. 1915; attd. to crown solr.’s off., 1915; passed final exam. in Hakka, July, 1915; ag. chief asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1915; on milly. serv., 1915-19; dep. regar. and appraisr., Hong Kong, 1920; head sany. dept., 1920; passed in Mandarin, May, 1921; attd. to treas., 1924; asst. treas. and comsnr. of estate duties, Apr. 1925; supt., impta. and expts. dept., June, 1927; P.M.G., Apr., 1928; head, sany. dept., Oct., 1928; offr., cls. I., 1934.

SAYERS, ELBERT FRANK.—B. 1889; ed. King’s Coll. Schl., Wimbledon, St. John’s Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Temne, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, K. Africa, 1918; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1915; dist. comsnr., Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, FREDERICK RICHARDSON.—B.A., B.Ch., B.A.O., M.D., D.P.H. (Dublin Univ.), B. 1880; ed. Rathmines Schl., Dublin, The Meath Hosp., Dublin, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; res. med. offr., Monkstown Hosp., Dublin, 1909-12; med. offr., S. S., Mar., 1912; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, Apr., 1912; post health offr., Penang, and med. offr. in ch. of leper asylum, P. Jerejak, Penang, Sept., 1913; asst. censor, Penang, Sept., 1914, to Apr., 1916; lecturer in hygiene, King Edward VII. medical schl., S’pore, 1919, 1923, 1926 and 1928; sect. health offr., Penang, Feb., 1924; cls. R., Jan., 1925; mem., Br. delegation to internat. conf., Paris, 1926; senr. health offr., Penang, Feb., 1927; ch. health offr., S’pore, May, 1927; regar. gen., births and deaths, S.S., Jan., 1931; in addn.

SAYERS, GERALD FLEMING.—B. 1893; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A. (hons.) 1914; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; ag. senr. asst. sec., Nov., 1919 to July, 1920; asst. sec., Tanganyika territory, July, 1920; ag. senr. asst. sec., for various periods 1920-24; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1923; attd. to C.O., Aug., 1924 to Sept., 1925 and fox July to Sept., 1930; clk., leg. coun., 1926-32; ag. asst. ch. sec., on various occasions, 1927-1931; asst. ch. sec., 1932; asst. ch. sec., Nyamwezi Prot., 1933; ag. ch. sec., Feb., 1934; edr., Handbook of Tanganyika Territory, 1930.

SAYLE, LIEUT. COMMR., ARTHUR REGINALD WILLIS, R.N.R., Barrister-at-Law (Gray’s Inn), Ed. Merchant Taylors Schl. and cadet H.M.S. “Worcester”; Royal Navy, May, 1914; R.C., 1920; admtrve. offr., S. Nigeria, May, 1922; station mag., Ibadan, S. Nigeria, Aug., 1922; pol. mag., Oct., 1923; crown coun., 1930; ag. solr. gen. in 1932 and 1933.

SCADDAN, HON. JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), M.L.A.—B. 1876; leader of Parly. Lab. party in W. Aust. since Aug., 1910; elected for Ivanhoe, June, 1904; premier of W. Australia, 1911-1916, min. for rly., mines and industry, 1916.

SCAMMELL, ERNEST HENRY.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organizing sec. Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; asst. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hosps. comm., Canada, 1916; asst. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-establish., Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts. regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; sec., pensions divn., dept. of pensions and national health, 1931; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHARFF, JOHN WILLIAM, B.A., M.D., B.A.O., B.Ch., D.P.H. (T.C.D.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1895; surgn. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., July, 1915; health offr., S.S., Nov., 1920; lect. in biology, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine in addn., May, 1921; hon. sec. for Malaya of the Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Aug., 1921; health offr., Malacca, June, 1925; sr. health offr., Penang, May, 1928; offl. deleg. to 8th cong. of Far Eastn. Assoc. of Trop. Med., Bangkok, Dec., 1930.

SCHMIDT, CARL FRIEDRICH, B.A.—Civ. serv. law clk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; control and audit office, July, 1893; asst. examnr. of accts., Jan., 1894; examr., May, 1898; ch. examr., July, 1907; inspr. of accts., Jan., 1909; prin. clk., Apr., 1912; sec., educn. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920; sec. for interior, Sept., 1926; contr. and audr.-gen., 1929.
SCHNEIDER, SIR GUALTERUS STEWART, Kt.Bach. (1828), K.C.—B. 1864; ag. puisne just., Ceylon, Jan., 1921; comnr. of assize, Nov., 1921; puisne just., Dec., 1921; ag. ch. just., Sept.-Dec., 1926; ret.

SCHOCH, AUGUST ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Berne, Switzerland; graduated, Leiden, LL.D. and called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Union pub. serv., 1911; priv. sec. to min. of justice, 1913-16; professional ass't. to atty.-gen. (Transvaal), 1916-24; solr.-gen., Cape Eastern Dist., Aug., 1924; law adviser and Dutch draughtsman, dept. of just., Pretoria, Sept., 1925; senr. law adviser, Oct., 1931.

SCHOFIELD, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. of Liverpool, M.A.; non-coll. student, Oxford, 1908-1911, M.A.; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1911; attached to secretariat for Chinese affairs, 1914; asst. cable censor, in addition to other duties, 1914 to 1915; passed cadet, Feb., 1915; ag. 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915; ag. 2nd ditto on various occasions, 1915-23; passed law exam., 1916; ag. dep. regir. and appraiser, in addn. to other duties, 1919; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., in 1919, 1920 and 1921; dist. offr., South, in addn., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1923; mag., Hong Kong, 1926; mag., Kowloon, 1927; asst. supt., impts. and expts., 1929-30; dist. offr., South, 1930; 1st pol. mag., 1931.
SCHOKMAN, CLIVE CONRADE.—B. 1897; 4th asupt., prisons, Ceylon, Jan., 1924; 1st do., Apr., 1928; ag. inspr.-gen., prisons, Dec., 1931.

SCHOLEFIELD, GUY HARDY, D.Sc. (Lond.), O.B.E. (1919). Ed. Tokomairiro High Schl. and Univ. of N.Z. (Bowen and Macmillan Brown prizeman) and Univ. of London; ch. librarian, gen. assembly liby. and contrlr., dominion archives, N.Z., 1926.

SCHOLEFIELD, WILLIAM.—Dep. clk., res. mag's. ct., Jamaica, Mar., 1913; clk., res. mag's. ct., Feb., 1917; asst. comnr. and mag., Turks and Caicos Islands, July, 1919; ag. comnr., ditto, Sept. to Oct., 1920; ag. res. mag., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1922-26.

SCHUMANN, THOROD E. WERNER, B.A., M.Sc., Ph.D.—B. 1896; ed. Stellenbosch, S.A., Gottingen, Germany and Yale, U.S.A.; lect. in physics and maths, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown, 1918-19; and at Stellenbosch, 1920-21; dir., fuel research inst. of S. Africa, June, 1931, reagd., Feb., 1933; ch. meteorologist, Union of S.A., Sept., 1933.

SCICLUNA, HANNIBAL P., M.B.E. (1935), L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regir., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of govt. emigr. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; att'd., ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of the Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; libr., Malta public library, July, 1923.
SCOTLAND, ERICA ELEANOR.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, EUSTACE LINDSAY, C.M.G. (1933), O.B.E. (1918), M.C. (1917).—B. 1885; ed. Bradford, and Univ. Coll., Oxford, B.A., 1908; asst. dist. comnr., Uganda, Dec., 1908; dist. comnr., June, 1918; seconded for milly. serv., 1914-19; dep. asst. dir. transport, 1916; dep. dir., milly. lab., E.A.E.F., 1917; lab. comnr., 1921; asst. sec. for nativ. affrs., Jan., 1922; asst. ch. sec., Jan., 1924; dep. ch. sec., Mar., 1930; ag. ch. sec., May, 1930; ch. sec., June, 1932; ag. gov., in 1932 and 1934.

SCOTT, HENRY HAROLD, M.D., London Univ.; F.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; D.T.M. & H. (Camb.); Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosp.; Univ. Colls. London and Copenhagen; prosecutor, 1894; asst. demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1895; asst. house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; res. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray depts., mil. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; res. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willesden Dist. Coun., 1910; govt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1915-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charge No. XIII, mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, Cambridge hosp., Aldershot, 1916; govt. pathologist, dir., serum inst. Hong Kong, 1919; lect. on path. and med. jurisprudence, Hong Kong Univ., 1919; Milner research fellowship, L.S.H. and T.M., 1925; lect., trop. disease, Westminster hosp. med. schl., 1926; examr., D.T.M. and H. Eng., 1927; med. sec., col. med. research comte, 1928.

SCOTT, HUBERT SEPTIMUS, C.M.G. (1935).—B.1873; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; M.A.; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examr., 1908; sec. to educn. dept., Transvaal, 1911; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1924; dir., educn., Kenya, 1928.

SCOTT, SIE JOHN, K.B.E. (1932).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1901; prin. astt. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to gvt., Nigeria, Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1924; admstd. gvt. for various periods, 1924-29; col. sec., S.S., 1929; admstd. gvt., Oct., 1929 to Feb., 1930 and from Oct., 1930 to June, 1931.
SCOTT, MARGARET.—Ed. Sherborne Schl. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Achimota, 1925.

SCOTT, R. R., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.)—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in despr., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924; dep. dir., sanry. serv., 1932.

SCOTT, WALTER RICHARDSON.—B. 1899; ed. Durham, 1922; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1922; passed final exam. in Chinese, 1924; astt. supt. pol., 1924; J.P., 1925; astt. Indian pol. and passed in Urdu, 1926; passed Punjabi, 1928; pte. sec. to O.A.G., in addn., 1928; ditto to gov. in addn., Jan. to Mar., 1929; hon. A.D.C., to gov., 1929; ag. dir., crim. intell., 1932-33; supt. pol., 1933.

SCRIVENER, RUPERT FREDERICK.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schl. of Mines, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; commd. serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, I.A.; astt. ch. engr., Palestine rly., 1920; engr., ways and wks., Palestine rlys., 1924.
SCRIVENOR, T. V.—B. 1908; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1930; apptd. temp. astt. prin., C.O., Oct., 1930 prior to serv. as an admstr. offr., N. Rhodesia.

SCULLIN, RT. HON. JAMES HENRY, P.C.—El. to Commonwealth H. of R. for Corangamite, Victoria, 1910; defeated, gen. el., 1913; M.H.R. for Yarra, Victoria, since 1922; prime min. of the C'wealth of Australia, 1929-31.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 1886; clk. to res. commr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. astt. to res. commr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 1915, to Dec., 1922; ag. astt. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., constab., Aug., 1922; dep. commr., W.
