Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in oh. of accts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. astt. to res. commr., on various occasions, 1923-31; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., pol. (Br. divn.) Apr., 1925.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cls. hist. tripes, pt. I, 1914 and pt. II, 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme astt. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; astt. prin., C.O., 1922; pte. sec. to parly. under sec. of State, D.O., 23rd Sept., 1929; prin., D.O., 1st Oct., 1930.

SEDWICK, NATHANIEL ASHER.—B. 1890; ed. at Univ. Coll. Schl., London, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb.: B.A., London and M.A., Camb.; cadet, S.S., Dec., 1913; dep. to supt., gvt. monopolies dept., Jan., 1914; in charge of gvt. opium factory, May to Aug., 1914; passed cadet (Malay), Nov., 1917; astt., col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1917; col. of war tax, Singapore, July, 1918; supernumy off., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; 1st mag. and pres., town bd., Johore Bahru, Oct., 1930; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1930; astt. treas., Penang, Nov., 1932; ag. finan. advr. and audr.-gen., Johore, May, 1934.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1885; ed. St. John's (R.C.) Coll., Wonesh, Surrey; Artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. and Victory Meds.); col. audit dept., July, 1921; astt. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; ag. senr. ast. audr., July-Sept., 1926; astt. audr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. ast. audr., June, 1927 to July, 1928 and Oct., 1928 to Sept., 1929; audr., Seychelles and Somaliland, July, 1930; also ast. sec. to gov., Seychelles, 1931-33 and to O.A.G., July-Sept., 1933.

SEELE, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1891; ed. Macalesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv. Cleasby Regt. in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; astt. to air min., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as astt. prin., 9th Jan., 1921; pte. sec. to parly. under sec. of state for the Colonies, (Mr. Wm. Lunn, M.P.), 10th June 1929; prin., 17th Sept., 1929.

SEELY, MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1928), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1878; lieut.-commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (desp., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercrombie div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Liskeard div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French gov't) for saving life at sea, 1891; parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comttee. to inquire into organisation of crownagents'office, 1909; under sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; jointly parly. sec. to ministry of munition, 1918; under sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.
SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMANUEL.—B. 1885; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, Mar., 1902; astt. clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1916; astt. censor, 1914; censor, Apr., 1918; man. sec., agric. inst., Jan., 1923.

SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, M.I.C.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; astt. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., Sept., 1917; 1st grade dist. offr., June, 1917; gen. astt. to D.P.W., Jan., 1925.

SEIGNORET, G. B.—Ck. to prem., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treas., 1895; astt. clk., regist'r's off., 1895; ag. postmtr., 1895; clk., govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treas., 1914; postmtr., 1925.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1906).—THE Rt. H. Wm. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1839; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-83; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1886-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cola, June, 1895; chmn. Pacific cable comttee., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comttee., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high commr. for S. Africa, and gen. and comm.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.
SELBY, JOHN ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.)—B. 1900; venereal diseases offr., med. dep., Hong Kong, 1930.

SELDON, ARTHUR ARNOLD.—B. 1889; ed. Charterhouse and Oriel Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hons.); asst. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; capt., K.A.R., 1917-18; sec., famine comm., Nairobi, 1919; personal asst. to ch. native commnr., 1920; 1st grade admstv. offr., 1925; H.M.'s ag. consul. S. Abyssinia, 1928.

SELLS, MARTIN PERBONNET, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E. M.I.Loco. E.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. asst. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorks Rly.; temp. major R.E., 1917; supt., light rly., France, 1917-1919 (twice ment. in desps.); dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1919; ag. ch. mech. engnr., Tanganyika Terry. rly., 1920; ch. mech. engnr., Gold Coast rly., 1922; ch. mech. engnr., Nigerian Rly., 1926.

SELWYN-CLARKE, PERCY SELWYN, M.C., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.F.H. (Eng.) with distinc., D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), barrister-at-law.—B. 1893; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp. and Lond. Univ.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1919; med. offr., health, W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Nov., 1919; senr. san. offr., W.A.M.S., Oct., 1924; awarded Langley Memorial Prize, 1924; dep. dir., san. serv., May to Aug., 1927; del. for Gold Coast to Yellow Fever conf., Dakar, Senegal, 1928; visited U.S.A. and Canada at invitation of Rockefeller Foundation, 1928; asst. dir., san. serv., June, 1929; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1929; awarded Medaille d'Argent des Epidemies for work in connection with outbreak of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1929; ch. health offr., F.M.S., 1930; asst. dir., med. serv., Gold Coast, Jan., 1932; dep.-dir., health serv., 1933.

SEMPLE, J. M., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., T.C.D., L.M. (Rotunda)—Sandhurst, 1915; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926; ag. D.D.S.S., Feb. to Aug., 1928; and Nov., 1928 to Aug., 1929; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1934.

SENEVIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1895; on milv. serv., Nov., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to B'calon Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Nov., 1921; office asst., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1923; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1926; asst. govt. agt., Ratnapura and addnl. asst. govt. agt., Kegalle, Jan., 1929; dist. judge, N. Eliva, Jan., 1930; ditto, Anuradhapura, Sept., 1930; do., Ratnapura, Apr., 1931; asst. sttlm. offr., Oct., 1931.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; attd., Matara kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Jaffna kach., July, 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1926; ditto, Pt. Pedro, May, 1927; asst. sttlm. offr., Apr., 1929.

SENNETT, Cedric William Arthur.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S.S., 1914; asst. regisr., impts. and expts., 1917; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, 1920; asst. dist. offr., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Nov., 1923; dep. regisr., sup. ct. and asst. off. assignee, Penang, Jan., 1925; dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1926 (offr., cls. III.); 2nd mag., asst. dist. judge and coroner, Penang, Nov., 1926; collr., land rev., Penang, Apr., 1927 and Mar., 1930; off. assignee and asst. regisr., coys., Penang, July, 1929; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; dist. offr., L. Perak, Apr., 1933; 1st mag., Ipoh, Oct., 1933.

SERTSIOS, B. D., O.B.E. (1931).—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909; asst. king's advoc., June, 1924; title changed to asst. atty. gen., May, 1925; ag. solr. gen., Apr. to May, 1927; puisane judge, Oct., 1927; ag. ch. just., July-Sept., 1934.

SETON, CLAUS RAMSAY WILMOT, M.C. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk, Laleham, Margate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on milv. serv., 1914-19; wounded, 1918; twice ment. in desps.; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1928; pres. dist. ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1919; judl. adviser, Transjordan, Oct., 1926; pres. dist. ct., Haifa, 1931; puisane judge, Jamaica, 1934; publication "Legislation of Trans-Jordan, 1918-1930."

SEVERN, Sir CLAUS, K.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (Cantab.), LL.D., Hong Kong (1920).—B. 1869; temp. clk. in librarian's dept., Foreign Office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; passed in Malay and law, 1898; asst. dist. offr., Selangor, June, 1897; asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; asst. sec. to res. gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; sec. to high commnr., Malay States, Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; admstn. govt. on various occasions, 1912-13, 1916-19, 1921-22; ret., 1926.

SEVIGNY, Hon. Albert, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., city of Quebec; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.O. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, Mar., 1921.

SEWELL, CHARLES YULE BLACKWOOD.—B. 1868; govt. roads dept., New Zealand, 1906; survr., rev. survey dept., Perak, Oct., 1908; survr., 1st grade, Apr., 1909; asst. supt., surveys, Kedah, Aug., 1917; supt., rev. surveys, N. Sembilan, Jan., 1926; ditto, Selangor, July, 1929; senr. supt., surveys, Spore, May, 1933.

SEYMOUR, ALFRED WALLACE, C.M.G. (1934).—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schl. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apr., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; asst. govt. agt. Kegalla, Mar., 1920; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1921; registr. gen., Dec., 1924; col. sec., Fiji, Dec., 1926; O.A.G., Fiji and high commnr., W. Pacific for various periods, 1927-33; gov.'s dep. for various periods, 1930-32; col. sec., Trinidad, 1935.

SEYMOUR-HALL, CHARLES CYRIL WILLIAM, F.R.S.A., F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc., F.R.E.S., M.R.A.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ment. in desp., 1919; capt. H.M.A.Z. 31, 1919; demob., 1919; asst. dist. commnr., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. res., 1923-4; ag. asst. admstr.-gen., 1926-27; ag. asst. sec., pvt. sec. to Br. res., clk. of couns. and edir., offr. gaz., 1928; ag. prov. comnr. (with seat on leg. coun.) 1929, 1930, 1931, 1932 and 1934; assisted in revision of Madan's Swahili Dictionary, 1931.

SHACKLES, DEREK HOLMES.—B. 1900; ed. R.N.C., Osborne and Grahams Schi., Holt; solr.; asst. comnr., lands, Gold Coast, 1929; dep. regisr., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1933; ag. regisr., 1933; reviser, high ct., Uganda, 1934.

SHARP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. Coll., Schi. and King's Coll., London; compt. and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; acct., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct., posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on mily. serv., London Rifle Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Contgt., 1914-18; ch. acct., P. and T., Nigeria, 1920; ag. dep. P.M.G. in 1928-30; ret., Oct., 1930; appd. col. postmr., Barbados, July, 1931 and in 1934.

SHARPE, SIR ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897);—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, Feb., 1894; ag. comnr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, May, 1894 to May, 1895; given rank of dep. comnr., Apl., 1895; and again acted as comnr. and consul-gen., Apl., 1896 to July, 1897; comnr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infl., N. of Zambesi, July, 1897; Jubilee medal, 1897; comnr., commdr-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., Jan., 1902; Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyasaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; rep. E. African Prot. at Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.
SHARPE, H. B.—Asst. dist. comnr., Kenya Colony, Apl., 1919; ag. dist. comnr., 1926.

SHAW, BARNABAS JAMES DOLD.—B. 1877; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1897; senr. clk., Pretoria, 1915; prin. clk., 1920; ch. clk., Cape Town, 1928; collr., cust. and S.M., R. London, 1932; do., Johannesburg, 1934; do., Port Elizabeth, 1934.

SHAW, FREDERIC LLOYD, B.A. (Hons.) Lond.—B. 1892; ag. headmast., English Coll., Johore Bahru, Feb., 1921; headmast., govt. English Schi., Muar, June, 1922; lat. asst. mast., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Nov., 1925; asst. mast., Raffles Inst., Jan., 1926; inspr., schls., Selangor, Apr., 1926; asst. inspr., schls., Singapore and Labuan, Mar., 1927 and Dec., 1928; headmast., Victoria Inst. K. Lumpur, Aug., 1929.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BURGH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton Schi., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schi., Kingstown; asst. dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, May, 1921; dist. comnr., May, 1928.

SHAW, JOHN VALENTINE WISTAN.—B. 1894; ed. Repton Schi.; service with R.E., 1914-19; asst. dist. comnr., Gold Coast, 1921; dist. comnr., 1925; asst. col. sec., 1928; seconded for duty at C.O., Mar.-Sept., 1930.
SHAW, ROBERT PHILLIP, A.R.I.B.A. (Chartered Architect).—B. 1888; ed. Bathgate Acad. and Heriot Watt Coll., Edinburgh; architect, P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1920.

SHAW, SIR WALTER SIDNEY, KT. BACH.(1921).—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908; to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admtd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911 to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., B. Honduras, 1912; puisne justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916; ch. just., S. Stilms., May, 1921; ret., 1926.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. Christian Schls., Dublin Univ. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; pte., Artists Rifles, Dec., 1916; lieu. R.F.A. Sept., 1917; demob., Mar., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; sec., rubber restrn., Oct., 1922; supernumerary offr., cls. V., Mar., 1923; dep. contrlr., rubber exp'ta., Johore, in addn., Aug., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Mar., 1927; dist. offr., Temerloh, Jan., 1930; offr., cls. III., Mar., 1932; dist. offr., K. Piah, Nov., 1933.

SHELLEY, MALCOLM BOND, C.M.G. (1934)—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; cadet, F.M.S., 1902; capt., M.S.V.R., 1915; offr. assignee and pub. trustee, 1923; chmn., trade statistics comte., Aug., 1926; contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1926; treas., S.S., 1929; ag. dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S. and pres., Raffles Coll., Dec., 1929; Br. res., Pemba, June, 1932; ag. ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1933.
SHELLISH, ALEXANDER JOHN.—B. 1881; jun. clk., postal dept., Cyprus, 1902; asst. island postmr., 1921; postmr.-gen., 1934.

SHELSWELL-WHITE, GEOFFREY HENRY, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schi.; comnd. with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia, 1916-18; asst. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal asst. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. civ. comnr., 1918; Iraq rebellion, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res., ag. int. asst. sec., secretariat, clk. to prot. coun. and cd., offr. gaz. at various periods; higher standard Swahili, 1925; pres., Pemba Swahili exam. bd., 1929; dist. regisr., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; ag. res. mag., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; called to bar, 1930; pvt. sec. to Sultan, 1930; asst. sec., secretariat, 1930; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1931; edir., Off. Gaz., 1931-33; trades licenses comnr., 1934; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1934; author of "A Guide to Zanzibar."
SHELTON, C. F., M.D., B.S., D.T.M & H., M.R.C.P.—B. 1888; civil surgeon, Serbian Red Cross, 1913; surgeon lieut., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1921; junr. med. specialist, 1934.

SHELTON, JAMES, F.I.C.—B. 1886; asst. rech. and analytical chem. in the sc. and tech. dept., Imp. Inst., G. Britain, Mar., 1899; 2nd asst. govt. analyst, Singapore, Feb., 1915; chmn., geol. dept., F.M.S., Feb., 1917; in charge of geol. dept., June, 1917; on sp. duty at inst. Med. Resch., Jan., 1923; in charge, trade and cust. lab'y., 1930 and July, 1933; ag. ch. chem., Inst. Med. Resch., F.M.S., Mar., 1931.
SHELTON, LAWRENCE HUGH, M.A. (Cantab)—B. 1891; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Tewksbury Hereford Schi. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1915; cls. I. admin. serv., 1932.

SHEPHERD, CHARLES HUBERT ENGLERET, M.C.—B.E.F., 1915-18; asst. to sup't., pol. wks., 1922; sub-inspr., Leeward Is. pol., 1924; ag. inspr., pol. and offr.-in-ch., prison discipline on several occasions, Dominica and St. Kitts-Nerv.; ag. ch. keeper, prisons, Leeward Is. in addn. to pol. duties, 1928; clk., P.W.D., Apr., 1929; seconded inspr., roads and water supplies, Antigua, Nov., 1931; sec., road bd., 1932.

SHEPPARD, CHARLES HENRY, O.B.E. (1932).—B. 1897; European War, 1914-1918; ment. in despatches; Kurdish gendarmerie, 1919-21; inspecting offr., Iraq pol., 1922-31; Iraq gallantry medal, 1932; asst. commdt., pol., Somaliland, 1931.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 1918 to 1920; cler. offr., Ist Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, SIR JOSEPH, K.T. Bach. (1932).—B. 1882; ed. Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to bar, Ireland, 1907; went Connaught circuit; judicial clk. (title subsequently changed to asst. to atty.-gen.), Nyasaland Prot., 1908-13; ag. atty.-gen. on different occasions, 1909-13; ag. judge, high ct., Dec., 1909 to Jan., 1910; res. mag., Kenya, 1913-20; ag. puisne judge, Kenya, and mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1919-20; puisne judge, Kenya and mem. ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1920; ag. ch. just., Kenya, 1928-29; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1929; ch. just., Kenya, 1934.
SHERINGHAM, ANTHONY ILEX.—B. 1890; ed. Brighton Coll.; Ceylon pol., 1909-31; on active serv., 1917-18 (lieut., 8th Hussars); comsrr., pol. and gov. of prisons, Zanzibar, Apr., 1931.
SHERLOCK, DAVID THOMAS JOSEPH, K.C. (1922), B.A.T.C.D., M.B.E. (Mil.).—B. 1881; judl. comsrr. and offl. mem., leg. coun., N. Borneo, Dec., 1920; ch. just., Feb., 1926.

SHERWELL, ARNOLD GUY.—B. 1891; ed. St. Edward's Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A. (schl. of jurisprudence, hons. cls. III); called to bar (Inner Temple), 1914; asst. res., Nyasaland, 1914; mily. serv. in Africa, ment. in despatches, 1915; practised at bar, 1920-25; dist. judge, Sudan, 1925; asst. advc. gen. and legal advr. to Sudan govt., rly. and steamers, 1928; advc. Palestine, Apr., 1929; relieving pres. of dist. ots., 1930; pres., dist. ct., 1935.
SHIELD, CHARLES GEOFFREY.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. secy. to gov., and clk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.
SHIELDS, W. P.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

SHIELS, DR. T. DRUMMOND, M.C., M.P.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.B., Ch.B.); served European War as offr. R. Scots.; in command with rank as capt. one of trench mortar batteries of 9th (Scottish) Divn. (severely wounded); was a mem., Edinburgh town coun.; M.P., East Edinburgh since 1924; mem. ap. coman., Ceylon constitution, 1927; partly, under sec., India Office, June, 1924; partly, under sec. for cols., 1929-31.

SHILL, A. T.—B. 1895; on active serv. with Rifle Brig. and Dorset Regt., 1915-17; wounded; Imp. cust. and excise dept., 1919; also dep. superint., mercantile marine, reor. of wreck, reg. shipping, Lascar transfer offr., etc. asst. recr. gen., Gambia, Aug., 1928; ag. asst. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1929 and from July, 1931; contr. food and fuel, May, 1931; supervisor, cust. and excise dept., Mauritius, Apr., 1932.

SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff clk., 1911; reglar. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922; staff offr., promotions, 19th Nov., 1928.

SHIRLEY, EVELYN PHILIP SWALLIS, O.B.E.—B. 1900; ed. St. Columbus Coll. Rathfarnham, Dublin and R. Mily. Coll. Camberley; joined R. Irish Fusiliers, Dec., 1918; N. Persia and Iraq, 1920; seconded, K.A.R., Jan., 1923; 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika and Somaliland Camel Corps; 3rd grade comanr., Somaliland, Sept., 1929.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newburch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; priv. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. clk., store dept., I.O., Oct., 1900; transf. to judl. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to permitt. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; asst. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. asst. under sec. of state C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924; dep. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

SHUTE, GEOFFREY GAY, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Schl., Eastbourne, Uppingham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. offr., Nigeria, 1915; cls. I, admstr. serv., 1930; priv. asst. sec., S. Prova., 1931; sec., S. Prova., 1933.

SIDEBOOTHAM, JOHN BIDDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhibr., open class exhibr., 1911, schol., 1912); Ist clc., theolog. tripus., pt. I., 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1915; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf. to C.O. as asst. prin. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922; sec., managing comte., bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1925; sec., East African guaranteed loan advisory comte., 1927; pvt. sec. to partly. under-sec. of state for dom. affrs., 15th Sept., 1928; pvt. sec., permitt. under sec. for the cols., 8th July, 1929, prnt., 24th Jan., 1930.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; county inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbandment of that force, July, 1922; dep. inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., June to Oct., 1925.

SIKES, HOWARD LECKY, B.A., B.E. (hons. and ext.), R.U.I., M.I.C.E., F.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Bootham Schl., York; asst. engnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1907; res. engnr., Mombasa water wks., 1911; engnr. in ch., Magadi water wks. constr., 1913; res. engnr., Magadi rly., 1915; lieut., E.A. Pioneers, 1915; capt., 1916; govt. hydraulic engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Jan., 1919; dep. dir., pub. wks., Kenya, 1921; D.P.W. and offl. mem., leg. coun., 1923.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. comanr., Kenya, 1926.

SIMMONS, BENJAMIN ROBERT—Clk. in conservator of forest's office, E. conservancy, Transvaal, 1898; ag. dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1902; dist. forest offr., Stutterheim, 1906; ditto, George, 1914; 2nd grade conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1929; priv. forest offr. (forestry inspr.), Natal and Transkei, 1932.

SIMMONS, GEORGE STANLEY, M.I.Mech.E., Chartered Mech. Engr.—B. 1892; ed. Battersea Polytechnic; asst. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorke rly.; loco. supt., Togoland Mily. rly., Apr., 1917; asst. loco. supt., Gold Coast rly., May, 1920;

SIMMONS, Hon. WM. CHAS.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1895; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1906; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resig., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct., Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMMS, Henry, M.V.O. (1917), A.M.I.C.E. (1913).—B. 1881; sany. engrnr., Gold Coast, 1908; sany. engrnr., Sierra Leone, 1912; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., Jamaica, 1925.
SIMPSON, JOHN.—Imp. tel. serv., Jan., 1892; transf'd, post office, Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1893; survr., posts and tels., Mar., 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Aug., 1921.
SIMS, JAMES HUGH.—B. 1878; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; asst. commr., 1918.

SINCLAIR, Hon. John Kewn.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine comm. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.K.L, 1908; el. to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admin., 29th Dec., 1921; senator, June, 1930.
SINCLAIR, Hon. Sir J. R., KT. Bach (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and 1918-32; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comm., 1912.

SINCLAIR, ST. CLAIR OVERBECK, M.A., F.I.C., D.Sc.—Clt., atty-gen.'s office, 1893; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1894; analyst, 1897; transf'd, dept. of agr., 1907; transf'd, dept. of int., 1911; govt. analyst, Cape Town, 1918; transf'd, dept. of agr., Apr., 1923; chief of divn. of chemistry, Mar., 1926.

SISNETT, Sir Herbert Kortright McDonnell, KT. Bach (1927).—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Temp., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; ag. res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to Sir A. Henning, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. gov't., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, do., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, do., Manchester, Apl. to June, 1904, do., St. James and Trelawny in 1904, 1905 and 1906; reglar.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. cor. of Belize, Apl., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie mutiny, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ag. atty.-gen., 1919-20; seconded, puisne judge, Jamaica, 1920; 1st puisne judge, 1921; cu. just., Br. Hond., 1922; compiled Br. Hond. sup. ct. r. 1926; apptd. arbitrator by govt. of U.S.A. and Guatemala in case of P.W. Shufeldt v. the govt. of Guatemala.

SITTAMPALAM, CATHIVALEU, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temp.), B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1922; attd., Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office asst., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926; pol. mag., Matara, Apr., 1928; asst. Puttalam, Oct., 1929; asst. govt. agt., Kandy kach., Nov., 1929; dist. judge, Kegalle, Jun., 1930; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1932; Batticaloa, May, 1934.
SKAN, D. A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. B.T.H. (Liv.).—B. 1899; war serv., R.N.V.R., 1918-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1926.

SKEENE, DONALD TRENCH, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1883; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., F.M.S., grade III, May, 1918; med. offr., grade II, Dec., 1912; war serv., 1915-19; med. offr., Batu Gajah, Sept., 1920; senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1921 and Feb., 1928; state med. and health offr., Sembilan, Jan., 1932; ag. state surg., Kempas, June, 1933.

SKEETE, CECIL CARRINGTON, B.A. (Cantab.)—B. 1900; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; Jesus Coll., Cambridge; 3rd class hon. nat. sci. tripos pt. I., 1922; dipl. course, Schl. of Agr., Cambridge, 1922-23; asst. dir., agr., Kew, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., agr., in 1924 and 1925; asst. dir., sci. and agr., Sept., 1925; ag. sci. and agr., on various occasions, 1926-29.

SKELTON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D.—B. 1878; ed. Orangeville and Cornell, B.A.; Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Calif.; counsellor, dept. of external affrs., 1922-24; under-sec. of state for external affrs., Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. Paul's Schl. and Wren's; asst. dist. commr., East Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in coll. sec.'s office, 1912-13; dist. commr., G. Coast, 1912; col. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec., coll. gov', 1914; ag. dist. commr., Ashanti, 1915; dist. commr., Oct., 1918; ag. pol. mag., 1922; dep. prov. commr., July, 1927; prov. commr., Sept., 1928; ag. sec., native affrs., Aug., 1930 and Apr. to Oct., 1932; ag. dep. sec., June-Sept., 1930.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schl.; 4th elk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-instr. schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apl., 1915; o.c. col. corp., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. mpr. of col. Leeward Is., Apl., 1918 to Feb., 1920; clk. to commr., elk. of couns. and educnl. dist. off., Montserrat, Feb., Oct., 1920; 1st elk., col. sec.'s office, elk. of couns., Antigua and subj. post printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. ast. col. sec. and ag. elk. of couns., Leeward Is., various occasions 1921-24; bumar, Achimota col. Gold Coast, 1927; ag. prin., Prince of Wales Coll., July to Sept., 1929; astl. col. sec., Sept., 1931; ag. clk. coun., May to June, 1932.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—B.1890; Gloucestershire R.F.A., Apr., 1908 to July, 1910; B.S.A. Pol., Aug., 1910 to Aug., 1913; E.A.P. Police, Sept., 1913; astl. supt., Apr., 1915; staff offr. to inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Prot., May, 1920 to Feb., 1922; supt., pol., Nyasaland, Nov., 1922; ag. ch. comsnr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, May, 1923 to Jan., 1924; astl. commdt., pol., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924; ag. commdt., pol. and gov., prisons on various occasions, 1925-33.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL, M.B.E. (1933).—B.1893; ed. Bedford Modern Sch.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1920-26; ch. clk. to admr., and clk. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk. couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; comsnr., income tax, May, 1927; pte. sec. to Sir Eustace Fiennes, Nov., 1927 to June, 1929; ag. treas., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., Dominica, June to Sept., 1929; O.A.G., Montserrat, Feb., 1930; ag. treas. and offl. mem., exec. and leg. couns., May to Sept., 1930; treas., Antigua and fedl. treas., Mar., 1931; offl. mem., fedl. exec. coun. and gen. leg. coun.; mem., exec. and leg. coun., Antigua; dep. treas. and comsnr., income tax, Trinidad, Mar., 1934.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Highrs.; certif. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant offr., S. Africa and St. Helena, Nov., 1899 to Oct., 1902 (Queen's med. and clas); inspr. of pol. gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1903; served in various offl. capacities in St. Helena, Antigua, Montserrat and Dominica, 1906-14; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Islands; ag. mag. Dist. F., May-Aug., 1914; capt. (2nd in command), Dominica Defence Force, Aug., 1914; comdg. local forces, Dominica, Sept., 1914; in command of 3rd (Leeward Is.) contgt., B.W.I.R., Barbados, June-July, 1917; also paying offr. and offr.-in-ch. records and dispersal, B.W.I. Regt., Dominica, Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1920; inspr. pol., offr. instr. to local forces and in ch. prison, fire brig. and weights and measures depts., Montserrat, Oct., 1920; ag. in charge of P.W.D., and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1923; re-transf. to Antigua as ch. keeper of prisons, supt., St. John's Training Schl. and supt., pauper cemetery, Leeward Is., Nov., 1924; dep. coroner, 1926.

SLACK, KENNETH HUGH.—B. 1885; ag. clk., mag. dist., "C," St. Kitts, in 1905, 1906 and 1907; clk. and inspr. wks., P.W.D., Jan., 1909; rev. offr., Dieppe Bay, Apr., 1910; govt. offr. and harbrmr., Sandy Point, July, 1914; 3rd rev. offr., Apr., 1915; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1916; 2nd rev. offr., Jan., 1917; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1919; dep. harbrmr., Apr., 1920; ag. cashwr., 1920; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1922; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force.
SLADE-HAWKINS, W.—Astl. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, G.C.M.G. (1933), K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. astl. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd astl. P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd astl. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyalatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd astl. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apl., 1906; additional astl. col. sec. and clk., legis. coun., Apl., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd astl. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. astl. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. astl. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on spec. duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. gov'r., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and comd-r-in-chief, Sierra Leone, Apr., 1922; gov. and comd-r-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927; capt. gen. and gov.-in-ch., Jamaica, 1932; resigned, 1934.

SLEEP, CAPT. ARTHUR, B.Sc. (Manchester).—B. 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; attd. land office, Kelantan, Feb., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; astl. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; astl. dist. offr., Mar., 1925; dist. offr., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1931; 2nd astl. sec. to govt., F.M.S., May, 1931; ag. astl. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, May, 1934.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9) ; capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; astl. comsnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1896; ag. res. comsnt., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SLOMAN, ARTHUR ERIC PENROSE, B.A.—B. 1890; ed. Bradfield Coll., Berks., Queen's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. of Neuchatel, Switzerland; served in Great War, 1915-19; lieut., R.F.A.; mem., Br. pol. mission to Greece, 1920-27; inspr., secondary schls., Cyprus, 1934.

SMALL, ALEXANDER SYM., M.A., B.Sc. (Glas.).—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; offr., cls. V, sec. to res., N. Sembilan, Mar., 1914; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1923; town planning admstr., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1926; offr., cls. Ia, finan. comsnr. and audr.-gen., Johore, June, 1927; offr., cls. la, finan. advr. and treas., F.M.S., Dec., 1930; treas., S.S., June, 1932.
SMALL, WILLIAM, M.B.E. (Mily.), ment. in desps.—B. 1886; ed. Univ. of St. Andrews; D.Sc.; botanist, Uganda, 1913; mycologist, 1920; mycologist, Ceylon, 1926; dir. of agr., Nyasaland, 1930.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical astl., house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical astl., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examr., physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr. govt. civil hosp., 1923; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Nov., 1927 to Jan., 1928; ag. dep. dir., med. and sanitary services, Feb., 1928 to Feb., 1929; senr. med. offr. and in ch., Kowloon hosp., Jan., 1931.

SMART, Capt. Archibald Guelph Holdsworth, M.B.E. (Mil.), M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1882; med. offr., 1/4 quarantine stn., Singapore, May, 1912; attd., govt. lab., Singapore, July, 1913; lent to Kedah for anti-cholera work, Jan.–Apr., 1914; asst. health offr., Singapore, June, 1914; on war serv., R.A.M.C., 1915–18; med. offr., S. Kedah, Oct., 1920; health offr., Kedah, Nov., 1921; sr. health offr., Penang, May, 1927; ditto, Perak, May, 1928; State med. and health offr., Selangor and regt.-gen., births and deaths, F.M.S., Jan., 1932; mem., S.S. and F.M.S. med. coun., Feb., 1932.

SMART, L. M.—B. 1889; asst. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., Mar., 1927; gen. man., Gold Coast rly. and hbr. authority, Takoradi hbr., Oct., 1933.

SMART, V. IRVING.—B. 1875; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Toronto; graduated from Queen's Univ.; engaged on surveys for the Dom. govt., 1897–98; ent. rly. serv., 1900; prof., rly. engng. and oper., McGill Univ., Montreal, 1907–14; sp. engrnr., Canadian National Rlys., 1923–28; gen. supt., trans., Western region, C.N.R., 1928–30; dep. min. of rlys. and canals, Dominion of Canada; also govt. dir., Canadian National Rlys., 1930.

SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PERCY.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921; asst. audr., N. Rhodesia, May, 1925; ditto, Tanganyika Territory, 1934.
SMIT, DOUGLAS LAING.—B. 1885; Cape civ. serv., 1903; pub. provr., 1912; mag., 1927; under sec. for justice, Pretoria, 1933.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May–Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores comm., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. clk., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., May–Oct., 1924; sec. for lands, Feb., 1934.

SMIT, JACOBSUS STEPHANUS.—B. 1878; D.T.D. (Dekoraties Trouwe Dienst), Anglo-Boer War; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; prte. sec. to Gen. Smuts as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; to Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1907 and to Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1908; asst. mag., Transvaal, 1909; asst. mag., Union civ. serv., 1910; resig., 1915; el. mem. for Klipdrift, Union of S. Africa legis. assem., 1920, 1921 and 1924; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925; admstr., Transvaal, 1929–34; chmn., diamond control bd., 1934.

SMITH, Lieut. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Sch., Bushey; sub. lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; asg. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914–15; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915–17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salaam, Mar., 1919; harmr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, Feb., 1926.

SMITH, ALLAN CHALMERS, B.A., M.C.—B. 1893; ed. Warwick Acad., Bermuda, Rossall Sohl, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1920; war serv., France and Italy, 1915–18 (three times ment. in despatches); pol. mag., W. dist., Bermuda, Jan., 1928; gen. comm., dist. and chmn., licensing authorities, Oct., 1931; pol. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1935.
SMITH, A. H. W., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.M. (Univ. of Lond.)—Med. supt., St. Ann's lunatic asylum, Trinidad, Nov., 1934.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler. ast., post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave and pension comm., 1920; sec., deptl. comm. comte., 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency commrs., July, 1923; transf. to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; acctnt. sup. ct., Oct., 1925; ch. acct., G.P.O., Dec., 1932.

SMITH, CHARLES JOHNSTON, C.B.E., M.B., F.R.C.S. (Edin.)—B. 1880; med. offr., F.M.S., Oct., 1911; med. offr., grade III, Aug., 1912; surgn., native hosps., S.S., Aug., 1914; 2nd surgn., Singapore, May, 1917.
SMITH, CLIVE ALEXANDER.—B. 1916; ed. Malton Grammar Sch.; R. Navy, 1919; gun inspr., Jamaica constab., Dec., 1924; 3rd cls. inspr., May, 1930.

SMITH, CUTHBERT, Dip. in Forestry (Oxon.)—B. 1890; ast. conserv., forests, grade II, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; ast. conserv., forests (new scheme), Jan., 1919; dep. conserv., forests, Jan., 1930; ast. conserv., forests, Johore, May, 1930; conserv., forests, Johore, 1934.

SMITH, HON. DAVID STANLEY, LL.M.—B. 1888; ed. Southland Boys' High Sch., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); on serv. with N.Z. exped. force, 1917–18; barrister, solic., and notary pub. till apptd. judge, N.Z., sup. ct., 1928; examt. in law, univ. of N.Z., 1920–27; appointee of U.S.A. as national mem., perm. comm. to settle disputes between U.S.A. and Peru; chmn., nat. affs. comm. (N.Z.), 1934; author of "Adoption of Children in N.Z." in Journal of Comparative Legislation, Oct., 1921.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., Dec., 1913; on mil. serv., Dec., 1913 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, EDWARD PAUL.—B. 1884; Colo. mounted pol., June, 1901; clk. to regr., dep. Com. Town, 1903; clk. to high sheriff, C.T., 1907; 2nd gr. clk., pub. ser. comm., 1913; as mil. serv., Great War, 1915–18 (wounded); 1st gr. clk., ditto, 1919; 1st gr. clk., treas., 1920; senr. clk., P.S.C., 1922; prin. clk., ditto, 1927; ch. clk., ditto, 1930; pub. ser. inspr., 1931.
SMITH, EMILIE HAMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1912.
SMITH, ERNEST ALLEN.—B. 1896; war serv., Leeward Is., 1927; senr. ast. audr., N. Rhodesia, 1932; ag. audr., 1934.

SMITH, ERNEST JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. E. London Tech. Coll.; mil. serv., Gold Coast Volunteers, 1914–18; ast. sect., constr., 1st Coast rly., 1920; ch. acct., 1922; dep. ch. constr., root., Nigerian rly., 1929; ast. ch. acct., 1931; ch. acct., 1933.

SMITH, FRANCIS EDWARD VINER, B.Sc. (Hons.)—Ed. Colston's Sch. and Univ., Bristol, 1921; dep. sci. and indust. research, 1921; ast. ast. mycologist, min. of agr. and fisheries, Eng., 1924; govt. microbiologist, Jamaica, 1924; Jamaica del., Impl. Mycological Conf., and Impl. Meteorological Conf., London, 1924.

SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, July, 1891; draftsman survey off., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1895 to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., Apr., 1902; comsrr. of crown lands, June, 1904; survr., crown lands, 1904; ag. supt., wks., June, 1906 to Jan., 1907; supervisor, govt. cotton ginney, Oct., 1912; col. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supt., pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevis, Sept., 1920.

SMITH, JOHN, O.B.E. (1933).—B. 1883; ed. New Vety. Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.); vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reapp't, Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921-May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun., ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.; dir., animal health, Nov., 1929.

SMITH, JOHN NOEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks, Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as asst. res., N. Nigeria, Nov., 1910; attd., Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, Mar., 1915; station mag., N. Prova., Mar., 1919; dist. offr., Jan., 1921; ag. senr. res., Sept., 1923.
SMITH, L. J. D.—B. 1893; ed. Radstock Grammar Sch.; U.K. cust., 1914-16; mily. serv., 1917-19; supt., cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1919.

SMITH, LEO ANDREW, A.R.S.M., Assoc. Inst. M.M.—B. 1886; inspr., mines, Perak, June, 1916; asst. warden, mines, June, 1925; sr. inspr., mines, Larut, Feb., 1931; asst. warden, mines, Selangor, Aug., 1932; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1933; ag. warden, mines, Perak, Sept., 1933.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. regisy. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; app'td. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923; higher grade cler. offr., 1st Mar., 1930.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEA.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comsrr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs postmr., cashier and regsr. of shipping) comsrr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regsr. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. asst. comsrr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.
SMITH, M.G., M.A.—B. 1904; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Dec., 1926.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, Oct., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. asst. postmstr.-gen., 1914; ag. postmstr.-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; recd. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 6th cls., 1920; supt., imptd. and expts., 1921; attd., Geneva opium confere., 1923; head of sany. dept., 1924; P.M.G., Oct., 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec. and dep. clk. of couns., 1930; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1931; dir., educn., 1933; sec., Chinese affrs., and mem., exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1934.

SMITH, P. C., M.I.Munic.E. (Lond.).—B. 1879; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Keites and Fosbrooks, archs. and survr., Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as asst.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contra., Lond.; asst. to Mr. C. F. Wike, M.I.C.E., city engrnr., Sheffield, 1902; town engrnr., Rainy River, Ont., Can., 1910; town engrnr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engrnr., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1915.

SMITH, MAJOR P. P. O., M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1881; ed. London Univ.; mily. serv., France, with R.E.'s; awarded Croix de Guerre, ment. in desps.; Reg. Army, R. of O., 1915-19; asst. engrnr., 1st grade, Lagos harbr. wks., 1920; senr. asst. engrnr., harbr. dept., Nigeria, 1923; port engrnr., ditto, 1934.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Ramsgate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.

SMITH, ROBERT EDMUND, B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1881; asst. mast., Outram Rd.Schl., Singapore, Aug., 1906; ditto, Raffles Inst., Nov., 1907, July, 1912, and Oct., 1913; headmast., Victoria Bridge Schl., Singapore, Mar., 1922.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., mast. olk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher grade cler. offr., 29th Oct., 1930.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, C.B.E. (1929), F.R. Aus. H.S.—B. 1865; ed. Grafton Superior Pub. Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N. S. Wales, June, 1912; asst. under sec., educn., dept., N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educn., N.S.W., 1923.

SMITH, STEPHEN JOHN.—B. 1887; N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1903; internal affrs. dept., 1913; sec., Samoan admin., 1915; ch. elk., external affrs. dept., 1920-30; ch. elk., Cook Is. dept., 1920-28; sec., 1928.

SMITH, WALTER JOHN.—B. 1884; asst. supt. wks., P.W.D., S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1908; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1909; ex. engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1917; ex. engrnr., grade III, July, 1908; senr. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; do., Kuala Lumpur Town, Apr., 1925; do., Taiping, Dec., 1928; do., Kinta, June, 1929; do., waterwks., Selangor, Mar., 1932; state engrnr., N. Sembilan, Nov., 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM FORGAN, M.L.A. (Queensland).—B. and ed. in Scotland; M.L.A., Queensland since 1915; successively min. without portfolio, sec. for pub. wks. and sec. for agr. and stock; premier and treas., June, 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM HARDEN, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (T.C.D.), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (R.C.P. and S.I.), D.T.M. (Calcutta).—ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; surg. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; med. offr., Zanzibar, Nov., 1925; ag. D.D.S.S., on various occasions, 1930-33; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1935.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb.; Rustatschol., 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls., class. tripes, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Dec., 1903; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., Jan., 1915; wounded, Mar., 1917; relinquished comsn. owing to wounds, and returned to col. audit dept., Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, Dec., 1923; audr., Kenya, 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, Australia, 1899-1903; obmn., cent. bd. of health and coroner, 1903-29; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Eben scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; doctor in law tripes, 1894; barrister, Cape Col. state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in cmd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during latter part of the campaign; mem. of comm. of Hist Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1905; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confce. at Paris, 1919; prime min., min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1924; M.P. for Standerton, 1924; leader, opposition, 1924-33; pres., Br. Assn. for Advancement of Science, 1931; min. of just. and dep. prim. min., coalition govt., 1933; leader, S. African deleg., World Economic Confce., 1933.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CHAMPTON, KT, B.A. (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911; ret., 1929.

SNELGAR, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1890; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Kings Coll., London; clk. of educn., 1906; elk., C.A., 1910; clk., last dept., E.A.P., 1912; ag. office supt., 1917 and 1919; land ast., 1919; sec., land tenure comm., 1920; warden of mines, 1920; regsr. of titles, 1922; asst. land offr., 1922.

SNELL, EDWARD WALTER.—B. 1879; post office, Kingwilliamstown, Mar., 1900; ed. G.P.O., Cape Town, Nov., 1900; clk., Natal branch, July, 1903; 1st cls. clk., Apt., 1912; income tax office, Pretoria, Sept., 1914; clk., income tax office, Cape Town, July, 1919; senior clk., rev. office, Johannesburg, Nov., 1918; prin. clk., ditto, Jan., 1921; ch. clk., income tax, head office, inland rev. dept., Pretoria, Aug., 1921; asst. commr., inland rev., Sept., 1929.
SNYMAN, NICOLAAS.—Prof. ast. to succ. gen., O.F.S., 1920; survr.gen., O.F.S., 1931; ditto, Natal, May, 1934.

SOLLIS, CLIFFORD GEORGE, B.A.—B. 1896; ed. Bristol Grammar Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; ast. mast., Bukit Zahrah Schl., Johor Bahru, Dec., 1920; asst. inspr., schl., Petaling, Kedah, Dec., 1930; inspr., schl., Selangor, Aug., 1931.

SOLOMON, NEVILLE STAFFORD VICTOR.—B. 1874; clk., G.P.O., Bahamas, 1891; tel. dept., 1895; treas. and cust., 1895; supervisor, cust., 1906; finan. ast., treas. and cust. dept., Nassa, 1907; supervisor, 1st gde., cust. dept., 1910; collr., 1917; adm. reporting offr., various ports, Nigeria, during great war, 1914-18; ret. on pension, 1922; asst. postmtr. (temp.), Bahamas Jan., 1924; resigned, Nov., 1925; recr. gen. and treas., and mem. exec. coun., Bahamas, 1925; mem., leg. coun., Feb., 1931; chmn., licensing authority, Mar., 1931; has held various ag. appts.
SOLOMON, SAUL.—B. 1875; ed. Bedford Grammar Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; M.A.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1900; K.C., Union of S. Africa, 1919; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAR.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colombo Kach., Dec., 1922; office astt. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office astt., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th astt. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th astt. col. sec., May, 1928; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1929; addnl. astt. contr., rev., Nov., 1929; astt. govt. agt., Kurunegala, July, 1931; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1932; sec., min. for lab., industry and commerce, June, 1933; dep. contr., lab., May, 1934.

SOMERS, CECIL GEORGE.—B. 1896; served in Dardanelles and E. Mediterranean with H.M. Transports, 1914-18; govt. pilot, Kenya, 1924; astt. port offr., Zanzibar, 1929; ag. port offr., Aug., 1929 to Mar., 1930 and Apr. to Dec., 1932.

SOPER, JOHN ROBERT PHILPOTT, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); headmaster, agrl. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agrl. offr., 1926; agrl. offr., 1928.

SOUTH, FRANCIS WILTON, M.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1886; ed. Marlborough Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; mycologist and lect. in agrl. sci., Imp. dept. of agr. for W. Indies, Mar., 1909; chg. agrl. inspr., agrl. dept., F.M.S., May, 1913; chg. agrl. field offr., Nov., 1924; ag. sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., for various periods 1927-31; rep. of Malaya at mycological confce. in Lond., Sept., 1929; ag. dir., agr., S.S. and F.M.S. for various periods, 1929 and 1931-32.

SOUTHBOROUGH, 1ST BARON (creat. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; ass't law clk., B. of T., 1885-1888; ass't solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec., dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. Deleg. to Internat. rly. congress in London, 1896; land in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comtees of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic coman., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions coman., 1906; mem. of comans. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal coman. on electoral reform, 1909; regar. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development coman., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, SIR WILFRID THOMAS, K.B.E. (1933), C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwickschl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct. 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1905; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr., customs, Colombo, July, 1909; astt. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to govt., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. astt. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port coman., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1923 and exec. coun., 1925; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., in 1927, 1928, 1929, 1930, 1932 and 1934.
SPAFFORD, H. H., B.A.—B. 1899; inspr., schls., Gold Coast, Jan., 1928.
SPARROW, HERBERT ROBERT, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1889; astt. elec. engnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; elec. engnr., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1927; ag. dep. ch. elec. engnr. and inspr. in addnl., various periods, 1928-31.

SPARROW, WILLIAM LABARTE.—B. 1904; pol. probationer, Nov., 1924; astt. aupt., pol., Hong Kong, 1927; seconded to Weihaiei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928; passed in Mandarin and Cantonese; attd. Punjab pol., 1931-2; passed in Urdu, 1932.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNEY, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripus, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripus, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comanr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.
SPEIRS, R. C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.T.M. (Liv.)—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territ., Apr., 1925.

SPICER, ROY GODFREY BULLEN, C.M.G. (1934), M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Colet Court and St. Paul's; probationary astt. supt. pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; astt. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war surv., 1915-19; wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt. grade I., 1923; comanr. pol., Kenya, June, 1925; comdt., pol., Palestine, 1930.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GARTH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. astt. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1903-06; astt. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1906-12; dist. engnr., grade III, 1912-14; dist. engnr., grade II, 1914; on active surv., 1915-19; dist. engnr., grade I, Ipoth, Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engnr., Ipoth, Jan., 1921; senr. dist. engnr., J. Bahru, Mar., 1927; ditto, K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; engnr., ways and wks., June, 1929.

SPOWERS, JAMES HOWARD.—B. 1889; authorised survr., Queensland, Oct., 1911; survr., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; astt. supt., surveys, Jan., 1919; astt. supt., trig. surveys, F.M.S., Nov., 1922; astt. supt., rev. surveys, Sept., 1924; ag. sup't., surveys, Johore, Jan. 1931; do., Kedah, Oct., 1932; supt., surveys, F.M.S., and S.S., June, 1933.

STACPOOLE, GEORGE WENTWORTH.—Asst. commr., lands survey dept., Gold Coast, 1925; transf'd. to lands dept., 1927; ag. dep. commr., July, 1928-Apr., 1929; ag. commr., lands, in 1932 and 1933; commr., lands, 1934.

STAFFORD, FRANK EDMUND.—B. 1895; ed. R. Grammar Schl., Guildford; Queen's Regt., 1914-19; joined Iraq civ. adminstr., Feb., 1919; ag. asst. rev. sec., Oct., 1919; supervising offr., high commr., Oct., 1920; asst. to finan. adviser to high commr., June, 1921; dep. fin. sec. to high commr., Jan., 1923; ag. finan. sec., Apr., 1923; finan. sec., June, 1923.

STAINES, CAPT. EDMUND ALFRED.—B. 1882; P. and T. dept., G. Britain, 1899; Br. sea post offr., U.K.-U.S.A., 1907; asst. asst. P. and T., F.M.S., Sept., 1911; mil. service, 1915-18; ag. asst. P. and T., F.M.S., 1921-1925; contrlr., P. and T., Perak and Dindings, June, 1927; ditto, Selangor and Pahang, June, 1928; do., Penang and P.W., May, 1931.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, C.M.G.M. (1930), K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov. gen., 1913; resident commr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high commr. for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, July, 1927; high commr. for the U.K. in the Union of S. Africa, 1931; gov. and commr.-in-ch., S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1934.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.E. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921.—Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expedit., 1901, (med. and clasp); ag. trav. commr., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. commr., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. eng., Chatham, 1905; resig. commr., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. commr., S. Leone, 1910; prov. commr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, on various occasions, 1918-20 and 1922-23; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.

STANTON, SIR AMBROSE THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1934), C.M.G. (1920), M.D., F.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. and H.—B. 1875; sen. house surg. (to Sir Patrick Manson), hosp. for trop. diseases, 1905; asst., Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1907; bacteriologist, do., 1908; dir., gov. laboratories, F.M.S., 1920; ch. med. adviser, C.O., 1926; chmn., col. advisory med. comtee. and managing comtee., bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1926; author of publications on "Beriberi," "Malayan Culicidæ" and "Meliodiosis."
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf'd. to C.O., Mar., 1923.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922; higher grade cler. offr., 17th Feb., 1930.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES KNIGHT K. BACH. (1926).—Mem., N.Z. parl., since 1919; speaker since 1923.

STEAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS, C.B.E. (1925), O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwxt., Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbrmr. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prev. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; regis. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower stand. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. case censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; case censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, Jan.-Mar., 1920; refugee commr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; regis. of patents, Aug., 1921; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; insp. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Francaise, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. commr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; regis. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt. f. port of Larnaca, June, 1922; regis. of companies, July, 1922; regis. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; regis. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir., excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. regis-gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; regis. trade marks and patents, Apr., 1925; ag. regis. companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STEAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; asst. gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of immigr. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.
STEED, L. F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Nov., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July, 1922.

STEELE, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; adm. to the bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1899; adm. Grenada bar, July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and regis.-gen., F., E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and leg. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1919; and July-Oct., 1920; pol. mag., Grenada, Oct., 1930; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1931.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.R.E. (1919), M.C., King's Police Med.—Served in African war, 1900-02; joined B.S.A. p.t., Rhodesia, 1903; comm., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Afr., 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in E. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in despatches; ch. commr., pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENS, JOHN EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1868; ed. St. Olave's Schl., York and Royal Univ. of Ireland; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1892; mag., H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar, Jan., 1912; and mag. of the ct. of H.H. the Sultan of Zanzibar, Feb., 1912; ag. asst. judge, Aug., 1915 to Aug., 1916 and from Feb. to Aug., 1918; 3rd ass. judge, Kenya, Feb., 1923; editor-in-ch. of "The Manual of Naval Law and Court-martial procedure" (4th edtn.); asst. editor of 11th edtn. of "Addison on Contracts"; author of works on "Demurrage," "Freight," "Charter party," etc.; contributor to the Encyclopaedia of the Laws of England, Journal of Comparative Legislation, etc.

STEPHENS, CAPT. JOHN HENRY, M.C. (1918).—B. 1887; 4th Canadian Mounted Rifles, Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., 5th Batt. East Lancs. Regt., Sept., 1916; served France and Flanders, 1916-18; asst. treas., Gold Coast, May, 1921; ag. dep. treas., Sept.-Oct., 1932 and Apr.-Sept., 1934.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD, O.B.E. (1935).—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cl., class. mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service 28th Aug., 1914 to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; appld. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923; attld. secretariat, Imp. Economic Confce., 1923; prin., 12th Dec., 1924; U.K. delegation to L. of N. Assembly, 1925; attld. secretariat (economic sec.), Imp. Confce., 1926; jnt. sec., confce. on opera. of Dom legislation, 1929; admtrve. asst. sec., Imp. Confce., 1930.

STERLING, HERBERT HARRY, LL.B.—B. 1886; ed. Christchurch (N.Z.) Boys High Schl., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. rlys. dept., 1901; mem., rlys. bd., 1924; ret., 1926; gen. man., rlys., 1928; chmn., rlys. bd., 1931.
STEVENS, BERTRAM SYDNEY BURNSDALE.—B. 1889; treas., N.S.W., 1929-30; premier and treas., 1932.

STEVENS, GROUSE ALEXANDER, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Schl., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schol. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cls. hon., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD.—B. 1891; M.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn. May, 1915; att. Batticaloa Kacheheri, Oct., 1915; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avissawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb. 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1932.
STEVENSON, NEIL STUART.—B. 1902; ed. Lincoln Schl. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1924; asst. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1924; conservator, Jan., 1930.
STEVENTON, L. E.—B. 1897; ed. County Schl., Wolverton and Elstow Schl., Bedford; on milly. serv. in E. Africa, Egypt, Salonika and Asia Minor; asst. divnl. supt., Nigerian rly., 1928.
STEWARD, PHILIP FRANCIS, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1925; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1926; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1933; ag. puisne judge, Mar., 1934.
STEWART, AUGUSTUS GEORGE, M.D., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922.
STEWART, CHARLES DUNCAN, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1889; supt., meteorological br., survey dept., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1929.

STEWART, DONALD JAMES.—B. 1876; ed. ptyvly. and at Whitgift Schl.; mines dept., Transvaal, 1901; senr. clk., 1903; ag. dist. registr., 1906; prin. clk., 1908; ag. asst. registr., 1910; asst. registr., 1921; registr. and Rand townships registr., 1923; served in S. African War, 1899-1901, and the Great War.
STEWART, DOUGLAS ALEXANDER.—B. 1876; ent. Cape civ. ser., 1896; lower law exam., 1901; mag. successively at Rietfontein, Britstown, Hoopstad, Smithfield, Lydenburg, Pine-town, Kroonstad.

STEWART, DOUGLAS ROY, C.M.G. (1929).—B. 1886; ed. Gordon's coll., Aberdeen; clk., native dept., Fiji, Apl., 1905; cadet, July, 1906; passed cadet, Dec., 1909; stip. mag. of colony and ag. stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and gov't. commnr., Colo West, May, 1910; ag. asst. col. sec. on several occasions, 1910-1916; ag. clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1911 and July, 1913; priv. sec. to admtrve., June, 1912; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1912; mem., bd. of examiners, Fijian language, Dec., 1913; asst. native commnr. and gov't. comsnr., Rewa Prov., May, 1914; asst. sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1916; lieut., Fiji defence force, July, 1917; hon. A.D.C. to ag. gov., June, 1918; asst. col. sec., 1917; ag. sec. for native affairs, mem. of leg. coun., bd. of health, etc., 1920; prin. asst. col. sec. and editor, native newspaper "Na Mata," May, 1921; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., May, 1920 and Apr., 1921; ag. agt., gen. imigrn. and editor "Rajdut" in 1922 and 1923; ag. col. sec. etc., Sept., 1922 and Jan., 1924 to Apr., 1926; gov't. dep., June and Sept., 1924, Jan., May and Aug., 1925; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific H.C. and ag. asst. high commr. and gov't. dep., Sept. Oct., 1925; gov't. dep., Jan., 1926; col. sec., Barbados, Aug., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns. and exec. comte. also ch., bd. of dir., sugar industry agrl. bank; ag. gov., Apr.-July, 1927, Feb., 1929 and June-Sept., 1930; rep. col. at standing W. Indian Confce., 1929 and at Inter-col. Trade confce., Trinidad, June, 1931; admtrve., St. Christopher and Nevis, July, 1931.

STEWART, HON. HUGH ALEXANDER, K.C.—B. 1871; ed. pub. schl., Brockville High Schl., Osgoode Hall, Toronto; mem., pub. utilities commn., 1907 to 1913; 1st el. to H. of C., Canada, 1921; re-el., g.e., 1925, 1926, and 1930; min. of pub. wks. in Bennett cabinet, 1930.

STEWART, CAPT. JOHN LIVINGSTONE, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory med.; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Gold Coast, Dec., 1923; prin. vety. offr., 1930.

STEWART, CAPT. MALCOLM JOHN.—B. 1893; 2nd lieut., Lovat Scouts Yeomanry, 1914; Indian Army, 1916-22; capt., 1919; imp. Schl. of Instrn., Zeitoun, Cairo, 1919-21; coy. offr., coy. commdr., 53rd Sikhs F.F.; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1923; ag. senr. asst. treas., 1930, 1931, 1932, 1933; ag. treas. and ch. of cust., Somaliland, Sept., 1933-Mar., 1934.
STEWART, NEIL, M.M. — B. 1893; Royal Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19 (M.M.); asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1919; supt., 1927.

STEWART, MAJ.-GEN. SIR ROBERT MACGREGOR, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1868; Afghan war, 1878-9; Soudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887-97; late commdr., R.A., southn. dist., Portsmth.; gov. of Bermuda, 1904-07.

STEWART, ROBERT STROTHER, M.A., B.C.L., B.Litt., Durham Univ., Gladstone prizeman.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., 1905; 2nd lieut. the Tynemouth R.G.A. (T.F.), 1913; A.D.C. to gen. offr., comdg. Tyne garrison, 1914-15; intelligence offr., Tyne garrison, 1915-17; ment. for valuable services; asst. competent mily. authority, Tyne garrison, 1917-19; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1919; major, R.A., T.F., R. of O., 1919-27; M.P. for Stockton-on-Tees, 1923-24; mag., county of Victoria, Trinidad, Apr., 1927; temp. asst. legal adviser, C.O., July to Dec., 1929; legal adviser to gov. of Malta, Jan., 1930; mem., privy and nominated couns. and coun. of health; examr. in English lit. and hist., Univ. of Malta; chmn., comte. to enquire into motor transport, 1930; dep. gov., July-Aug., 1932; chmn., traffic control bd., 1930-32; chmn., trade union council claims bd., 1932; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1933.

STEWART, WILLIAM. — B. 1881; ed. Waimate Dist. High Schl. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1899; commr., crown lands and ch. survr., Christchurch, 1926; valuer-gen., 1934.

STEWART, HON. WILLIAM DOWNIE, LL.B. — B. 1878; ed. Otago High Schl. and Otago Univ.; ent. N.Z. parl., 1914; served in France with N.Z. Rifle Brig.; min. of intern. affrs., 1921-23; min. of cust., 1921-28; min. of industries and commerce, 1923-26; atty.-gen., Jan.-May, 1926; min. of finance and min. of stamp duties, 1926-28; also atty-gen., min. of just., and min. of defence, Nov.-Dec., 1928; joined coalition miny., as min. of finance, cust., and stamp duties, and atty-gen., 1931; ret. from miny., 1933; negotiated N.Z.-Canada trade treaty, 1932; mem., N.Z. delegation at Ottawa confce., 1932.

STEYN, DANIEL, B.A. (Cape Town), LL.B. (Univ. of S. Africa). — B. 1893; senr. mast., Boshoff Schl. and Gymnasium, Paarl, 1917; judge's regir., O.F.S., 1920; pvt. sec. to prime min., 1924; Union consul-gen., Lourenco Marques, Sept., 1929; accredited rep., Union of S. Africa to l. of N., Mar., 1934.
STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN. — B. 1885; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1925), M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S. — B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalene Coll. Camb.; Holmes exhibnr., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; M.A. 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; on sp. serv. to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; scient. sec. to W.I. agric. confce., 1907 and 1908; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn., bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem., agric. sch. comrn., 1909; mem., banana comrn., 1910; on sp. serv. to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec., tobacco comte., 1911; comrn. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; dir., agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem., coun. of govt., 1913; regir., co-operative credit societies, July, 1913; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1914; mem. of irrigation comte., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; dir., agr., regir., co-op. socie. ties and vice-pres., agric. soc., Ceylon, 1916; chmn., exec. comte., Ceylon rubber research scheme, 1916; del. to Imp. entomological confce., London, 1920; mem., coun., Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921; M.L.C., 1921; del. to Imp. botanical confce., London, 1924; mem., bd. of management tea research inst., 1925; visited tea dist., N. India with Ceylon delegation, 1925; asst. agr. adviser to S. of S., Mar., 1929; editor "Tropical Agriculturist" and author of several repts., etc., on trop. agr.

STOCKS, AUTHOR HURSON. — B. 1889; ed. Heath Gram. Schl., Halifax and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comrn., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1914; priv. secy. to ag. gov., 1916-17; dist. comrn., Aug., 1922; senr. dist. comrn., Jan., 1931; prov. comrn., 1932.

STOKES, GEOFFREY. — B. 1880; ed. Bedford Grammar Schl.; probationer, N. Rhodesia, May, 1909; asst. native comrn. and J.P., Oct., 1910; nat. comrn., Dec., 1912; ag. asst. max., Feb., 1917; asst. mag., Feb., 1924; ag. dist. comrn., Apr., 1924; prov. comrn., Dec., 1933.

STONE, ARNOLD ALFRED PRICE DUNBAR, O.B.E. (1929), M.I.C.E., F.S.I. — B. 1877; eng. draughtsman, Sierra Leone, 1912; ag. assi. D.P.W., Nov., 1916 to June, 1919; ag. col. eng., Gambia, Jan., 1920; dist. eng., Dec., 1920; ag. D.P.W., May to Oct., 1922 and May to Oct., 1924; asst. D.P.W., 1923; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1925; mem., leg. coun., 1925-31; ag. gen. man. rly. in July, 1927.

STONE, ERNEST RICHARD, B.A., M.B., R. Chir. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.F. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Liverpool). — B. 1883; asst. med. supt., central mental hosp., Tanjong Pagar, Jan., 1921; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Singapore, April, 1923; ditto, mental hosp., Jan., 1925.

STONE, ROBERT GEORGE. — Asst. paymaster, 1st King's African Rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1906; asst. dist. comrn., K.A.P., Apr., 1911; dist. comrn., Apr., 1914; ag. senr. comrn., 1927 and 1928; senr. comrn., 2nd grade, 1929.

STONEHAVEN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), Rt. Hon. Sir John Lawrence Baird, Bart., P.C (1922), G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. (1915), J.P., D.L. — B. 1874; ed. Eton and Carus Church, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, 1896; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvt. sec. to Sir U. Garstin, under-sec., pub. wks., Kev., 1900-02; ag. agt. and oons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; polit. offr. Br. E. Africa and Abyssinia frontier survey, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in Somaliland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, deeps. C.M.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Ares, 1906-8; served as intelligence offr., European war, 1914-15 (deeps., D.S.O.); M.P., Regv., 1910-22; Ayr Burghs, 1922-25; parly. mem., Ar Bd., 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; parly. under sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of transp. port, 1922-24; 1st comrn. of wks., 1924; gen. gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Australia, 1925-31.

STOODLEY, EDWIN ALAN. — B. 1890; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; col. audit dept., Mar., 1915; asst. audr., Nigeria, May, 1915 (seconded to E. Africa, mily. audit, 1916-19, asst. audr., Somaliland, 1919); dep. audr., Gold Coast, July, 1933; ag. audr., May, 1934.

STOKE, GEORGE BERESFORD, F.R.G.S., F.R.Econ.S., F.R.A.I. — B. 1897; ed. Bath; paymr. cadet, R.N., Jan., 1914; asst. naval sec., Malta Dockyard, 1915; asst. sec., rear admr. Sir D. R. L. Nicholson, 1917; do. to Adm. Sir L. Bayley, 1918; transf., emergency list of offers, R.N., 1919; cadet, Sarawak civ. serv., 1920; asst. sec., 1924; dist. offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. clk. of leg. coun., 1931; olk., exec. coun. in addn., 1932; asst. treas., Mauritius, 1933; author of various papers on anthropological subjects.

STOREY, HAROLD HAYDON, M.A., Ph.D. (Cantab.). — B. 1894; ed. Cambridge Univ.; mily. surv., 1914-19; mycologist, dept. of agr., Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1922; plant pathologist, E. African agric. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1928.

STORM, WILLIAM THORBURN. — B. 1881; Imp. post office, 1896-97; asst., Cape of Good Hope P.O., June, 1901; survr.'s staff, N.distr., Dec., 1901; engnr. in ch.'s staff, Nov., 1902; postmr., Feb., 1904; postmr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1909-10; prov. postmr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1912; survr., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; divnl. survr., 1916; senr. survr., Jan., 1920; ag. dep., P.M.G., various occasions between Jan., 1918 and July 1920; P.M.G., Tanganyika Territory, 8th Oct., 1920; mem. legis. coun., July, 1926; P.M.G., N. Rhodesia, 1931; M.L.C., 1932-33.

STORRS, SIR RONALD, K.C.M.G. (1929), Kr. Bach. (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919). — B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. class, tripus, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust. admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir B.
