WILSON, CECIL SAMUEL, B.A., M.B., Ch. B. (Dub. Univ.)—B. 1897; med. offr., F.M.S., Jly., 1925; surg., F.M.S., Aug., 1928; surg., N. Sembilan, Apr., 1931; do., Perak, July, 1932 and Nov., 1933; do., Selangor, Aug., 1933.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES, Barrister-at-Law (King's Inns).—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (sir., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1913; attd. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Penang, Dec., 1914; passed cadet, Jan., 1915; on milly. serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., July, 1915; France, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. V, May, 1916; lieut., July, 1916; capt., Feb., 1919; demob., Mar., 1919; ment. in dep., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, June, 1919; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., Sept. to Nov., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., M.V.I., Perak, Apr., 1921; emigrn. agt. in India, Aug., 1922; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1925; accomp. contr., lab. to Delhi, Feb. to Mar., 1926 and to Simla, Aug. to Sept., 1927; state treas., Perak, Oct., 1928; chmn. sany. bd., Larut and Matang in addn., Oct., 1928; 2nd mag., S'pore, Jan., 1929; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1930; ag. regiar., sup. ct., S'pore, Jan., 1931; ag. offr., assignee and regiar., cov., S.S., Dec., 1932; offr., cls. I B., 1933; ag. contr., lab., Malaya, Jan., 1934.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and clk., Br. Solomon Is., June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, Jan., 1915 and Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., Apr., 1919 and Dec., 1924; acctnt. and 1st clk., treas. and cust., Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, Apr., 1923; dep. commr. for W. Pacific, July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, Dec., 1923; ag. govt. sec., Mar., 1925; ag. res. commr., Dec., 1925.
WILSON, D. E., M.B., B.Ch. (Aberd.).—B. 1902; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, July, 1928.
WILSON, DONALD BAGSTER, M.A., B.Ch. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.).—B. 1901; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1929.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed. Amersham Gram. Schi.; clk. acctnt. gen's. dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.

WILSON, FRANK KERSHAW, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1891; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1914; attd. col. acct., Dec., 1914; attd., censor's office, S'pore, Jr., 1915; supt., posts and tels. and ast. sup. lands, K. Bahru, Aug., 1917; sheriff and dep. regist., sup. ct., S'pore, Oct., 1925; chmn., rubber assessment comtee., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1926; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1926; 2nd mag., Penang, Aug., 1929; ag. regiar., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1931; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1931; mag., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1933.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schi., Bath; apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; clk., ch. sec's. off., 1904; ag. ast. sec., ch. sec's. off., Jan. to July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sep., 1919; ag. commr. in 1923, 1925, 1927 and 1929; ch. collr., cust., and excise, July, 1924 (now styled compt'r., cust. and inland rev.); regr., Br. ships, regr., Cyprus ships, imp., fisheries; mem., leg. coun., 1925-31; regiar., partnerships, Apr., 1929.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1877; ed. Chigwell Schi., Essex; ast. dist. commr., G. Coast, Aug., 1914; dist. commr., Jan., 1921.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—Temp. clk., R.A.M.C., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919; senr. med. offr., 1927.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1909), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1878; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1893; called to bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1898; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S for cols., June, 1895; legal ast. in the C.O., 1877; went to S. Africa as legal ast. on high commr's staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., in 1903, 1904 and 1905; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (rep. O.R.C. on rive. course); ret.; was sec. to the Royal Colonial Institute.
WILSON, J., M.A.—B. 1903; mast., econ. dept., N.T., Gold Coast, Sept., 1929; imp. schls., Apr., 1931.

WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN WOLSEY, M.C., V.D.—B. 1876; man., Tobago farm, 1895; inspr., Trinidad constab., 1895; inspr., 1907; senr. inspr., 1916; served with Imp. Army, 1914-19; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., 1929; ag. commr., local forces on several occasions.

WILSON, LIEUT.-COL. Rt. Hon. Sir LESLIE ORME, G.C.S.I. (1920), G.C.I.E., (1920), F.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1900), etc.—ed. St. Paul's Schi.; 2nd lieut., R.M.L.I., 1896; lieut., 1896; capt., 1901; served S. Africa, 1901 (severely wounded), depts., Queen's med., clasps, D.S.O.; A.D.C. to Gov., N.W. P., temp. lieut.-col., R.M. and served through Goliath operas., 1914-15 (depl.); served in France (severely wounded), 1915-16; M.P. (Reading) 1913-22; S. Portsmouth, 1922-23; partly ast. to war cabinet, 1918; chmn., national marine bd., 1919; partly sec. to miny. of shipping, 1919; partly sec. to treasury, and ch. unionist wh.p., 1923; Gov., Bombay, 1923-28; Gov., Queensland, 1932.

WILSON, CAPT. LOUIE WEIR.—B. 1894; ed. Gigglewick Schi., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin; Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, July, 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. commr., 1921; dist. commr., Aug., 1926.

WILSON, Mark.—B. 1896; ed. Mountjoy Schol. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; first schol. (hist. and pol. sci.), 1921; B.A. (senr. modr. and gold medallist), 1921; LL.B., 1922; served with R.A.F., 1918-9; called to bar, King's Inns, Dublin (1st cls. hon. at final examn.), 1924; cadet, admin. serv., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; mag., Uganda, 1926; ag. admtr. genl. and offl. recr. on various occasions, 1926-9; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1933-4; chan., Uganda diocese, 1934; mem., ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1934.
WILSON, Percival Douglas, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Swansea Gram. Schol.; aast. engrg., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1913; exec. engrg., 1924.

WILSON, Reginald Charles, D.Sc., F.G.S., Assoc. Inst. M.M.—B. 1888; ed. Scotch Coll. and Melbourne Univ.; capt., Royal Welsh Fusiliers and R.E., France, 1915-18; geologist, Portuguese E. Africa, 1911-14; geologist, geol. survey, Nigeria, 1919-27; dir., 1927; publins. on E. Africa and Nigerian Geology.

WILSON, Ronald Eric.—B. 1898; ed. Faversham and Sandhurst; cadet, F.M.S., Sept. 1921; attchd. dist. off., K. Selangor, Jan., 1922; do., cust. dept., Aug., 1922; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Larut, May, 1923; passed cadet, Dec. 1923; ag. ast. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1924; offr. cls. V., Sept., 1924; dist. offr., Jelebu, Jan., 1925; 4th ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1926; offr. cls. IV., sec. to res., Pahang, Sept., 1928; ast. dist. offr., K. Kangar, Oct., 1930; do., Kuantan, Oct., 1932; offr., cls. III., Sept., 1933; sec. to res. N. Sembilan, May, 1934.

WILSON, Brig.-Gen. Sir Samuel Herbert, G.C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B. (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comte., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff cffr., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comte., 19th Nov., 1918; gov., Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, 1st Sepk., 1924; assumed gov't., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the cols., 5th Aug., 1925; ret., 1933.
WILSON, William Glassford Walker, M.I.L.—B. 1894; ed. Kilmarnock Acad.; war serv., 1914-19; ast. loco. supdt., Nigerian rly., 1923; wks. man., 1927; ag. ch. mech. engrg., 1929.

WILTSHIRE, Eric.—B. 1893; ed. Monmouth Grammar Schol.; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; ast. engrg., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; dist. engrg., Nov., 1929; ag. ast. dir., P.W.D., 1927, 1928, 1930 and 1931.

WILTSHIRE, H. G., M.A., M.B., B.Chir. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; exhibr. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (coll. prize); L.S.T.M. certif. with distinc. (2nd place); war serv., 1914-19; France, Gallipoli, Egypt, Palestine (1914 Star with clasp); bacteriological specialist, R.A.M.C., 1916-19; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1924; do., Zanzibar, 1930; pathologist, 1932.
WINER, Cyril Legh, C.M.G., (1928), M.V.O. (1930).—Priv. sec. to govs. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSON, Victor Hantot, B.Sc., A.K.C., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1887; ed. Tiffins Schol., Kingston-on-Thames and King's Coll., London (1st cls. hon., elect. engrg.); 2nd cls. engr., G.P.O., engrg. dept., Oct., 1909; ast. engrg., July, 1911; supdt., posts and tels., Malacca, Sept., 1913; war serv., Aug., 1917-Apr., 1919; supdt., posts and tels., S.S. & F.M.S., Oct., 1926; senr. engrg., posts and tels., Penang, July, 1928; ditto, F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1930; ast. engrg.-in-ch., P. and T., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1930; engrg.-in-ch., do., 1933.

WINSTEADT, Richard Olaf, C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; passed cadet, 1904; class V., Jan., 1910; sec. to comteee, for Malay studies in addn., Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Dec., 1913; federal examr. in Malay in addn., Dec., 1913; class III., ast. dir., educn. S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addn.; cls. II., Nov., 1919; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stiltna., and F.M.S., June, 1920; seconded to act as prin., Raffles' Coll., Spore, June, 1921; offg. as sec. to high commr., in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addn., Mar., 1923; dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. IA, July, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comteee, in addn., July, 1924; chmn., tech., educn. comteee, in addn., Nov., 1925; rep. F.M.S., govt. at C.O. confce., Apr., to July, 1927; chmn., comteee, med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., Aug., 1928; chmn., Malayan delegation to 4th Pacific serv. cong., Batavia, May, 1929; rep. F.M.S. at C.O. confce., June, 1930; gen. advr., Johore, Oct., 1931; author of an "English Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawa and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.
WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd clk., post office, Dominica, 1920; ast. clk., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. clk., Sept., 1924; ag. audit clk., Jan., Apr., 1927.

WISE, Kenrick Stanton.—B. 1881; ed. Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C., Lond. Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and regiar.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, Aug., 1919.

WISE, Wilfred, M.Inst.C.E., B.Sc. (Hons., Lond.), M.I.Mun.Cy.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ed. privtly, and City and Guilds of London Cent. Tech. Coll., Univ. of Lond.; ast. engr., P.W.D., Fiji, May, 1921; ag. comteee, wks., Aug., 1923; comteee, wks., Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Tonga, May-July, 1930; chmn., cent. road bd., M.L.C.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schol. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comteee, Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917 to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comtee., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comtee., May, 1917; priv. sec. to parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Mr. Amery), 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; accompanied parly. U.S. of S. for the Cols. (Hon. E. F. L. Wood) on visit to W. Indies, Dec., 1921-Feb., 1922; rep. U.K. govt. at W. Indian confee., Ottawa, June-July, 1925; ast. sec., 18th Sept., 1927; D.O., 1928.
WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, O.B.E. (1932), M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., elec. and wireless dept., Zanzibar, Dec., 1911; 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WOODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Josceline Heneage, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890)—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comsnr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; commd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.O. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, R.E., O.B.E., M.D., D.P.H.—B. 1883; ed. pub. schls. and Toronto Univ.; on mil. serv., Canada, France and England, 1914-19; prov. dist. med. offr., Fort William and Port Arthur in 1912 and again in 1919; exec. sec., Canadian Tuberculosis Assoc., 1921; dep. min., dept. of pensions and national health, Dom. of Canada, 1933.

WOODEMAN, Guy Stanley.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. comsnr. of excise attached to the office of the excise comsnr., Apr., 1913; ast. comsnr. of excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; attd., ch. censor's office, Sept., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. comsnr., excise, Nov., 1926; comsnr., excise, Sept., 1927; govt. agt., S.P., Nov., 1930; prin. collr., cust., Nov., 1931.

WOHLMANN, Ward George, I.S.O. (1934).—B. 1872; ed. N.Z. state schls.; N.Z. permanent arty., 1894-95; N.Z. pol., 1895; sergt., 1906; senr. sergt., 1914; sub-inspr., 1918; inspr., 1921; aspt., 1926; comsnr., 1930; was comsnr. of pol., Samoa, 1920-22.

WOLFE, Edward Dudley Corscadden, C.M.G. (1928)—Tonbridgeschl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langa., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langa., triplos. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Speciai (1897); B.A. (1897); M.A. (1925); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; sec., P.W.D. enquiry comsnr., 1901; registr., land ct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1902; ag. ass't registr., gen., 1902; registr., land ct., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and ass't. supt. of pol., 1903-5; seconded to Transvaal govt. serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1907; ag. inspr. of schla., 1907; ag. registr., gen., 1907; ast. regisr.-gen., 1907; ag. inspr. of schla., 1907 to 1909; head of sanitary dept., 1909; postmtr.-gen., 1913; ag. col. treas., 1914-16; 1st pol. mag., 1917; ag. col. treas., and custodian of enemy property, 1917 and 1918; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1918; inspr.-gen., pol., 1920; ag. col. sec., 1921; M.L., 1924; attended internat. pol. confee., New York, 1925.

WOLFE, Henry, M.Sc. (Agric.), Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. asst., dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; ag. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa camp., 1916-17; dep. dir. agr. (plant industry), Keny., 1921; ag. dir., agr., 1933.

WOLFE, PHINIKER ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Acad.; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1916; ag. office supt., 1917; land ast., 1919; rec. titles, doc. and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOOD, Bernard Reader.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schl., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb.; M.A. dipl. in forestry; ast. conserv. Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conserv., 1918; conserv. of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925; reverted to Indian forest serv., Oct., 1927; conserv., forests, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1928; mem., bd. of examiners Hindi and Urdu, Apr., 1928; ag. off. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1928; chmn., local govt. bd., 1929-30; offl. mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1930; do., leg. coun., Jan., 1931; J.P., Aug., 1932.
WOOD, Francis William.—B. 1887; ed. Queen Elizabeth Gram. Schl., Wakefield; 2nd cls. survr., P.W.D., Kong Kong, 1914; 1st cls. dist., 1921.

WOOD, Harry Edwin.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Owen Coll. Manchester. (B.Sc., 1902, M.Sc., 1905); ast. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1905; ch. ast., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. ast., Union observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.

WOOD, Capt. John Calder, M.C., F.R.G.S., R.O.F., R.N., Qual. Land Surveyor, S. Africa—B. 1885; ed. Robt. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; ast., R. Observatory, Cape of Good Hope; 1st qualified land survr., S. Africa 1909; cartographer and trig. survr., F.M.S., 1911; war serv. France and England, 1915-19; offr.-in-ch., cartographer, framework travers., Gold Coast survey, 1925; dep. survr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926; surv.-gen., 1927.

WOOD, Leslie William.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; 1st cls. math. tripos., pt. I.; 2nd cls. math. tripos., pt. 2; B.A., 1912; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1914-20; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ast. of couns. for various periods, 1922 and 1923; senr. ast. col. sec., 1930; ag. prin. ast. col. sec., in 1931 and 1932.

WOOD, Capt. Reginald Cecil Murray—B. 1889; E. African Expd. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; cov. commr., 1922; ast. treas., Kenya, 1923; estab. offr., secretariat, Mar., 1928.

WOODHOUSE, John Leslie.—B. 1892; ed. Merchant Taylor's schl.; ast. transport offr. with rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Com., Nov., 1914; ment. in desig. for service in German E. Africa, Nov., 1916; ast. dist. commr., Uganda, Dec., 1916; 2nd grade admin. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1920; ast. offr., June, 1926.

WOODMAN, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. pvt.e schl., Winchester Coll. and St. Mary's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.S.A.; attd. to R.A.M.C. at Fort Pitt Hosp., Chatham, during S. African War; London Schl. of Trop. Med. exam., 1909; med. offr. and radiologist and dist. comsnnr., Br. Honduras, 1910-15; med. offr., hosp., gaol, etc., Hong Kong; seconded for serv. in war, 1918-19; capt., R.A.M.C. (T.), in charge of sanitary company, Salonica and Constantinople; in charge, Belize hosp., Jan. to June, 1928; Bahamas med. offr., Out Islands, June to Sept., 1928; ag. res. surg. and radiologist, Bahamas, Sept., 1928-Aug., 1929; ag. dir. med. services, Aug. to Nov., 1929.

WOODS, SIR WILFRID WENTWORTH, K.C.M.G., (1935), K.T. BACH. (1930), C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schl., mod. hist., 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Sept., 1901; asst. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; asst. col. auditor, Ceylon, Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions; col. auditor, Aug., 1915; temp. employed at W.O., Dec., 1917 to 1919; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; col. treas., Feb., 1922; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Oct., 1926; finan. sec., July, 1931; ret., 1934.

WOODWARD, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M. Inst.C.E.—B. 1892; ed. Nuneaton Gram. Schl. and Birmingham Univ.; served GT. War, 1915-19; engnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1921; ag. exec. engnr., waterwks., 1930 and 1932-4.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL, O.B.E. (1934), M.C.—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., June, 1921; ag. office asst. to govt.; agt. N.C. Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th asst. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd asst. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addnl. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1926; asst. govt. agt., N'Elia, May, 1927; asst. col. sec. and clk., leg. coun., Sept., 1928; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Nov., 1930; sec. to gov., July, 1931.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Kemper, July, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; asst. dir. of educn. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1926; asst. dir. educn. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., in 1927 and 1930; 1st mag., Ipoh, Nov., 1931, offr. cls. II; regir., sup. ct., K. Lumpur, May, 1932; D.P.P., Spore, Mar., 1934.

WORLLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Schl.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in despatches, Ireland, 1919; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1925; seconded to Seychelles, Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1926; on sp. duty, Seychelles, Oct.-Nov., 1929; audr., Br. Guiana, May, 1932.
WORRELLI, CECIL THEOPHILUS WILFRED EVERARD.—B. 1894; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1916; mag., St. Patrick, Trinidad, Jan., 1932; do., St. George East, Jan., 1934.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEADBURN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1910; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1910; attd., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914; asst. censor, Dec., 1914; on mily. duty, Feb., 1915; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1921; asst. stintm. offr., Jan., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, May, 1924; asst. govt. agt., Matara, Jan., 1929; cls. I, grade II, 1931; ag. govt. agt., W.P., Apr., 1932.

WORSLEY, RICHARD ROBERT LE GEYT, Ph.D. (Lond.), D.I.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc.—B.1899; ed. St. Paul's and R. Coll. of Sci.; R.E. O.C.B., 1918-1919; sub. dir., chemy. sect., miny. of agr., Egypt, 1926; biochemist, E. African agrl. research stn., Amani, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCKLYN, O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, govt. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. socy., 1906; jnr. asst. chem., Jamaica, 1902; asst. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmast., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; comsrr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food comsrr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comitee on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyassaland, Dec., 1920; dir., agr., Trinidad, 1930; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."

WRIGHT, MAJOR ANDREW BARKWORTH, C.B.E. (1932), M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918), R. of O.—B. 1893; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (schol.); served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; asst. sec., ch. sec's. office, Cyprus, June, 1922; asst. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high comsrr., and ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1927-34; ch. asst. sec., col. sec's. office, Jan., 1930; ag. comsrr., Mar., June, 1932; ag. treas., Aug.-Nov., 1934; ag. Br. del. of Evkaf, 1934.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALAN, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Edmund's Schl., Canterbury; open exhibnr., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1900; lieut., 3rd Bn. Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cls. hons. schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; passed cadet, 1914; 2nd grad. dist. comsrr., 1915; dist. comsrr., Nadi, 1915; do., Colo East, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 5th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. comsrr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. comsrr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1927; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1928; nom. mem., leg. coun., 1929; 1st asst. col. sec., Jan., 1931; sec. for native affrs., Mar., 1932; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. col. sec. in 1932, 1933 and 1934; gov't. dep. on several occasions, 1932-34.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.; B. 1889; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1902; M.A., 1916; asst. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. govt.analyst and prof.of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, OWEN FRANKLIN.—B. 1887; R.I.C. depot, 1908; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Nov., 1908; 3rd cls. inspr., June, 1911; 2nd cls. inspr., Aug., 1919; 1st cls. inspr., June, 1924; inspr.-gen., pol., Nov., 1932.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, Capt. Rodney George, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Sch., stud., engnr. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E. (S.R.), 1909; engr. on survey and constrn. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served, Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in deaps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E. Londonderry and Enniskillen Divs., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; supt. pub. wks., Antigua, July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, Aug., 1922; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W. in 1927, 1929 and 1931; dep. D.P.W., N. Rhodesia, Nov., 1931; ag. D.P.W. in 1933 and 1934.

WUPPERMAN, Rudolf Carl, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidad, Oct., 1909; asst. res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., May, 1923; dist. med. offr., 1929.

WYATT, Albert William.—B. 1900; ed. Leamington Coll. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; lieut., Hampshire Regt., 1919-26; 2nd K.A.R., 1923-26; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1926; asst. dist. offr., Oct., 1928; ag. dist. offr., Bihamamulo, June, 1930.

WYNNE, Mervyn Llewelyn.—B. 1891; ed. Corrig Sch., Ireland, Ellesmere Coll., Shropshire, and in Hanover, Munich and Edinburgh; Malayan pol. serv., 1911; asst. comanr., pol., Selangor, 1915; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1916; served in France 1917-18; comdt., pol. depot, Kuala Lumpur, 1920; comanr., pol., Trengganu, 1923; dep. comanr., pol., and ag. dir., crim. intellig., F.M.S., 1925; comanr., pol., Johore, 1929; dir., crim. intellig., S.S., June, 1931; dir., sp. br., S.S., June, 1931 and Nov., 1933; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Mar., 1933; dir., sp. branch, S.S., Nov., 1933.

WYNNE-JONES, Edward Irvine.—B. 1895; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; ag. dist. offr., South, June, 1922; attd. col. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1926; attd., col. secretariat, July, 1928; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1928; extra asst. col. sec., Nov., 1928; 2nd asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., in 1929; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. couns., 1929; dist. offr., North, 1930; 2nd pol. mag., Feb., 1932; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1934.

YATES, Geo. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schls.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins. from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Robt. Borden; asst. deputy minister of railways and canals, 1920.

YATES, Joseph Mervyn St. John, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. Forces, 1915-19; regar.-gen., Bahamas, Feb., 1920; ag. stip. mag., Mar., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; seconded for serv. as ag. asst. to atty.-gen., Jamaica, Sept., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923 and 1924; ag. judge, Kingston et al., Nov., 1924 to Oct. 1925; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1925; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1926; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1928; ch. just. on various occasions, 1934.
YATES, William Stewart, B.A.—B. 1895; ed. Coatham Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; cadet, Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1928; ass. dist. offr., Apr., 1930; ag. res. mag. and dist. regis., Sept., 1932.
YOUNG, Alfred Ernest, M.R.C.S., Eng. L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, Sir Alfred Karney, Kt., Bach (1923), K.O., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxf., called to the bar, In. Temp., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstd. of the govt., 1895; ag. regis., 1895-7; dist. commr., Orange Walk, 1898; regis. gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1910; admnstd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911, and in Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1915 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1921; chief just., Feb., 1923; ret. June, 1929.

YOUNG, Capt. Sir Arthur Henderson, G.C.M.G. (1916), K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1908), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Innings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; comdt., mil. pol., Kyrenia, Aug., 1878; ass. comanr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comanr., Poole, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dist. of survey and prim. forest offr., 1892; mem. leg. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admnstd. the govt. Jan. to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1895; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec. S. Stlntles, June, 1903; admnstd. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Stlntles, Aug., 1911; ass. admnstd. govt., Sept., 1911; also high commr. for Malaya States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, Hon. Sir Frederick William, Kt. Bach (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1878; comanr. of crown lands and immigr., 1913-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-16; mem. of comitee, for stintln. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, Henry Alfred, K.C.—Ed. Eton Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1901; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 2nd Jan., 1906; asst. atty.-gen., 1913; senr. res. mag., 1917; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions; priv. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1922; puisne and circuit judge, Sierra Leone, 1930.

YOUNG, Major Sir Herbert Winthrop, K.C.M.G. (1934), Kt. Bach (1932), C.M.G. (1927), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R.M. Acad., Woolwich; comand., R. Garrison Art., 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; maj., 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb. 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir., local resourc., Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in despatches); gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejaz operations, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in despatches); 3rd class Order of Naba); pres. local resource hd., Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to Col. Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to Col. Middle East Dept., as asst. sec., Mar., 1921; Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchill to Cairo conf., Apr., 1921; visited Palestine and Iraq on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty, Jan., 1922 and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1925 and 1926; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1927; couns. to high comnr., Iraq, Sept., 1929; ag. high comnr., on four occasions; envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary, Baghdad, Oct., 1932; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Nyasaland Prot., 1932; do., Northern Rhodesia, Mar., 1934.

YOUNG, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—Mem., N.Z., parl., since 1911; min. of health, 1926-28; min. of industries and commerce, 1928; min. of health, min. of immigr., in coalition min., 1931; min., internal affs., 1933.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univs.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919; asst. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, JOHN MOULD.—B. 1875; Cape civ. serv., June, 1893; clk., G.P.O., July, 1894; elk., native affrs. dept., Cape Town, Jan., 1896; asst. res. mag., Tsomo, Oct., 1896; ag. asst. res. mag., Kokstad, Mar., 1898; elk. and asst. res. mag., Tsomo, June, 1898; clk., P.W.D., Cape Town, Feb., 1899; senr. elk., Umtata, May, 1900; elk. and asst. res. mag., Idutywa, Oct., 1900; res. mag., Flagstaff, June, 1906; 1st gde. res. mag., Lusikisiki, Sept., 1920; senr. gde. mag., Umtata, Apr., 1923; sp. C gde. mag., Umtata, Sept., 1928; pres., native appl. ct., Cape and O.F.S., Jan., 1929; ch. native comnr., Natal, July, 1931.

YOUNG, SIR MARK AITCHISON, K.C.M.G. (1934), G.M.G. (1931).—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and elk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd asst. sec., col., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., July, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. asst. col. sec. and elk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1928; ag. gov. in 1929 and 1930; ch. sec., Palestine, 1930; admtd. govt. for various periods, 1930-32; gov. and commdr.-in-ch., Barbados, June, 1933.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhibr., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; clin. asst., children and ear depts., St. Thomas's hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.O., Zanzibar, 1927, 1930 and 1933.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1892; med. offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1921; med. offr., K. Lipis, Nov., 1922; ditto, Taiping, Oct., 1924; ditto, Larut, May, 1925; ditto, B. Gajah, Mar., 1927; ditto, B. Pahang, May, 1928; ag. sr. med. offr., Pahang, Nov., 1928; med. offr., Larut, Aug., 1929; ag. ch. med. offr., soc. hyg., F.M.S., May, 1930; ag. State med. and health offr., Pahang, Jan., 1932; ag. dep. do., Selangor, Jan., 1933.
YOUNG, WILLIAM ARTHUR, B.Sc., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.T.M. and H.—B. 1890; prof. of bacteriology, Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, Mar., 1927.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E.(1919); C.M.G.(1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; elk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. ast. govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. act. N.W. dist., 1895; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comnr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admtr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. gov'r., Leeward Is., 1903; admtr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. gov'r., Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; gov'r., Falkland Is., Dec., 1914; assumed govt., May, 1915; ret., 1920.
YOUNGMAN, WILLIAM, Ph.D., D.Sc.(Lond.).—B. 1880; Indian agrl. serv., 1918-30; dir., agr., Ceylon, 1931.
YU WAN, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; inspr. vernacular schls., Hong Kong, 1922; mem., bd. of exams., 1923.

ZAMMIT, HON. PROFESSOR SIR THEMISTOCLES, KT. BACH. (1930), C.M.G. (1911), M.D. (hon.), D.Litt. (Oxon.).—B. 1864; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1882; grad. in medicine, 1889; govt. analyst, 1891-1920; curator, Valletta Museum, 1903; prof. of chemistry, Malta Univ., 1905-20; mem. Mediterranean fever comnr., 1904-07; rector, Malta Univ., July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. coun., 1920-21; hon. fellow, Royal Anthropological Inst., 1919; local sec. (Malta) of Soc'y of Antiquaries, 1921; medallist (Mary Kingsley), Liverpool Schl. of Trop. Med.; delegate of Malta govt. at Imp. Educn. Conf., London, June to July, 1923.
ZIAL, MUSTAFA FUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; inspr., Cyprus mily. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.
